D12.en: At 18, he worked as a junior waiter and doubled as a busboy during busy shifts.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他做初级服务员，忙时还兼做勤杂工。

D2.en: At 18, he absorbed everything shown, so his sifu accelerated his training.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他吸收所学的一切，师父因此加快了他的训练。

D9.en: At 18, he knew working out in public would attract more interest.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他知道在公共场合锻炼能吸引更多关注。

D7.en: At 13-14, he would never acknowledge defeat or admit his gang lost fairly.
D7.zh: 13到14岁时，他从不认输或承认自己的帮派输得公平。

D6.en: At 12-13, he became leader of a school gang with anti-British sympathies.
D6.zh: 12到13岁时，他成为有反英倾向的学校帮派头目。

D4.en: At 25, he refused an approach, saying the essence of his kung fu was efficiency.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他拒绝了一种方法，说他的功夫精髓是效率。

D3.en: At 18-22, he began to find his feet as a philosophy student at UW.
D3.zh: 18到22岁间，他开始在华盛顿大学找到哲学学生的感觉。

D11.en: As a small child, he imitated Errol Flynn in Robin Hood.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他模仿罗宾汉中的埃罗尔·弗林。

D14.en: At 18, he was the natural choice to impress a girl Bruce wanted.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他是布鲁斯想追的女孩面前的最佳人选。

D5.en: At 13-14, he would never admit defeat or that his gang lost fairly.
D5.zh: 13到14岁时，他从不认输，也不承认自己的帮派被打败。

D13a.en: At 24, he told Linda's parents he wanted to marry her and that he was Chinese.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他告诉琳达的父母他想娶她，而且他是中国人。

D1.en: As a child, he kept fighting until his opponent gave up.
D1.zh: 小时候，他坚持战斗直到对手放弃。

D17.en: As a child, he helped a blind man cross the street.
D17.zh: 小时候，他帮一个盲人过马路。

D18.en: At 18-19, he got a job that required getting up before daylight.
D18.zh: 18到19岁时，他找到一份需要天亮前起床的工作。

D15.en: He practiced blocking and striking a wooden dummy's limbs and trunk.
D15.zh: 他练习格挡和击打木人桩的四肢和躯干。

D16.en: He could recite whole scenes of dialogue as a teenager.
D16.zh: 他十几岁时能背诵整段对话。

D19.en: As a child, he rearranged furniture to confuse the cleaner.
D19.zh: 小时候，他重新摆放家具来迷惑清洁工。

D20.en: At 12-13, he became the leader of a school gang.
D20.zh: 12到13岁时，他成了学校帮派的头目。

M4.en: At 12-13, he developed strong anti-British sympathies.
M4.zh: 12到13岁时，他产生了强烈的反英情绪。

M1.en: He never admitted defeat, fighting until his opponent gave up.
M1.zh: 他从不认输，一直打到对手放弃。

D10.en: At 18, he felt Bruce tense up and knew he prevented a fight.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他感到布鲁斯紧张起来，知道自己阻止了一场打斗。

D20X.en: As a child to young adult, he returned to the USA.
D20X.zh: 从幼年到青年，他回到了美国。

M5.en: As a child to young adult, he was involved in street fights.
M5.zh: 从幼年到青年，他参与了街头斗殴。

D5.en: At 18, she defied her parents' objections and insisted on entering the film industry.
D5.zh: 18岁时，她不顾父母反对，坚持要进入电影圈。

D7.en: At 18, she refused to give up her curiosity about filmmaking despite her parents' resistance.
D7.zh: 18岁时，她不肯放弃对拍电影的好奇心，违抗父母意愿。

D13a.en: At 18, she got her mother to accompany her to meet the director, who promised to fake kiss scenes.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她让母亲陪她去见导演，导演答应借位拍吻戏。

D13b.en: At 18, her mother signed her first film contract for her, with a fee of NT$10,000.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，母亲代她签了第一张电影合约，片酬新台币一万元。

D9.en: Between ages 17 and 25, she once had six films shooting simultaneously and went two weeks without sleep.
D9.zh: 17到25岁间，她同时拍六部戏，忙得两个礼拜没上床。

D1.en: At 25, she left the film world and went to California to study and enjoy freedom.
D1.zh: 25岁时，她离开电影世界，到加州进修并享受自由。

D2.en: At 25-26, she filmed 'Love Massacre' directed by Tan Jiaming in the US.
D2.zh: 25到26岁间，她在美国拍了谭家明导演的《爱杀》。

D3.en: At 25-26, she worked on 'Love Massacre', her first film with an art director (Zhang Shuping).
D3.zh: 25到26岁间，她拍了《爱杀》，这是她第一次有美术指导（张叔平）。

D4.en: At 18, on her first day of shooting, she had her hair cut short and cried until her eyes swelled.
D4.zh: 18岁时，拍摄第一天她就被剪短头发，哭得眼睛都肿了。

D6.en: At 17, she walked on Ximending street with two classmates, Li Wenyun and Yuan Hailun.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她和同学李文韵、袁海伦走在西门町街头。

D8.en: At 17, she refused a role on the phone, insisting she could only play a student.
D8.zh: 17岁时，她在电话里拒绝了一个角色，坚持只能演学生。

D10.en: At 18, on her first day of filming, she cried when her hair was cut above her ears.
D10.zh: 18岁时，拍摄第一天，头发被剪到耳朵以上，她哭肿了眼睛。

D12.en: At 17, after failing college entrance exams, she attended night classes at a cram school.
D12.zh: 17岁时，没考上大学，晚上去台北车站对面的补习班上课。

D15.en: At 17, she was thin and small, weighing under 100 pounds.
D15.zh: 17岁时，她又瘦又小，体重不到一百磅。

D16.en: At 17, she refused a role on the phone, insisting she could only play a student.
D16.zh: 17岁时，她在电话里拒绝了一个角色，坚持只能演学生。

D17.en: At 18, she heard her mother say from her sickbed, 'I hope you will always be this happy.'
D17.zh: 18岁时，她听到躺在床上的母亲说：‘希望你以后都这么快乐。’

D18.en: At 18, after signing a contract, she went shopping at a high-end boutique in Ximending.
D18.zh: 18岁时，签完约后，她去西门町逛委托行。

D20.en: From 17 to 25, she was part of the 'Two Chins and Two Lins' era in film.
D20.zh: 17到25岁间，她身处'二秦二林'的电影时代。

D20X.en: At 25, she left the film world and went to California to study and enjoy freedom.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，她离开电影界，去加州进修并享受自由。

M1.en: At 17, she felt anxious and lost about college entrance exams.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她对大专联考感到焦虑和迷惘。

M2.en: At 18, her family opposed her film career; her mother was bedridden for three days, so she gave up the idea.
M2.zh: 18岁时，家人反对她拍电影，母亲为此卧病三日，她只好放弃。

M4.en: At 18, she was curious about filmmaking and refused to give up.
M4.zh: 18岁时，她对拍电影充满好奇，怎么也不肯放弃。

M5.en: At 17, she refused a role on the phone, insisting she could only play a student.
M5.zh: 17岁时，她在电话里拒绝演出，说自己只能演学生。

M6.en: At 17, she felt destiny had arranged for her to enter the film path.
M6.zh: 17岁时，她感觉命运安排她走上电影这条路。

D14.en: At 17, she played a teenage girl in 'The Outside Window'.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她饰演了《窗外》的少女。

D3.en: At 16, she was captivated by French in Vietnam and dreamed of mastering it.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她在越南被法语迷住，梦想学会它。

D4.en: At 17, she wore an apron and sold tangyuan in Hong Kong for a photo shoot.
D4.zh: 17岁时，她穿上围裙在香港卖汤圆作秀。

D14.en: At 21, she insisted on performing for isolated troops, saying she wasn't afraid.
D14.zh: 21岁时，她坚定要求去小岛慰劳孤寂兵力，说不怕风浪。

D2.en: In her late teens to mid-20s, she studied French at school and with a tutor, practicing tapes for hours.
D2.zh: 17到25岁间，她按时上法语课并请家教，反复听录音带苦读。

D12.en: At 14-15, she faced malicious rumors about pregnancy and abortion, having no normal girlhood.
D12.zh: 14到15岁时，她遭遇怀孕堕胎等恶毒谣言，没有少女时代。

M1.en: At 21, she won three Japanese music awards for the single 'Airport'.
M1.zh: 21岁时，她凭单曲《空港》获日本唱片大赏新人赏等三奖。

D9.en: At 18, she switched to Life Records and toured Southeast Asia for a year.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她跳槽到丽风唱片，在东南亚巡回演唱一年。

D18.en: At 17, she won first place in a Hong Kong charity sale, becoming the youngest 'Baihua Oil Queen'.
D18.zh: 17岁时，她在香港工展会义卖得第一名，成为最年轻的白花油义卖皇后。

D1.en: At 20-21, after her first Japanese single failed, she succeeded with 'Airport' in 1974.
D1.zh: 20到21岁间，她首张日语唱片失败，后凭《空港》大热。

D6.en: As a small child, she had a group of close friends in Luzhou, about a dozen boys and girls.
D6.zh: 幼年时，她在芦洲有一批死党好友，十来个男女。

D5.en: At 21, she insisted on performing for isolated troops, saying, 'I'm not afraid of the wind and waves.'
D5.zh: 21岁时，她坚持要为孤岛上的官兵演出，说：‘我不怕风浪。’

D13a.en: At about 16, she made the painful decision to drop out of school to sing.
D13a.zh: 16岁左右，她痛苦地决定休学唱歌。

D7.en: As a small child, she silently joined her brothers when they were punished by kneeling.
D7.zh: 幼年时，她默默地和兄弟一起罚跪。

D10.en: At 6-11, she cried bitterly over classmates' teasing but endured silently.
D10.zh: 6到11岁时，她因同学捉弄哭得很伤心，但默默忍受。

D16.en: At 10-11, she represented her class in many speech and recitation contests.
D16.zh: 10到11岁时，她代表班级参加多次演讲和朗诵比赛。

D20.en: At 10-11, she played the lead role in a class play.
D20.zh: 10到11岁时，她在班级话剧中演女主角。

M2.en: At 4-5, her father proudly said, 'That's my daughter! That's the Deng family's daughter!'
M2.zh: 4到5岁时，她父亲自豪地说：‘那是我女儿！那是我们邓家的女儿！’

M4.en: At 11, she won first place in a Huangmei opera singing competition.
M4.zh: 11岁时，她参加黄梅调歌曲比赛获得冠军。

D13b.en: At 12, she was admitted to the hard-to-enter private Jinling Girls' High School.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，她考上了很难进的私立金陵女中。

D17.en: At 17, she went to Hong Kong and raised funds with a sweet smile.
D17.zh: 17岁时，她专程赴港，用甜甜的笑容募捐善款。

M7.en: At 16, she was recommended to perform at a charity gala for disabled children in Singapore.
M7.zh: 16岁时，她被推荐赴新加坡参加伤残儿童慈善晚会义演。

D9.en: At 18, he went with Wang Wei to rehearse a play at Shanghai Women's Commercial College.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他和王为一去给上海女子商业学院排戏。

D4.en: As a teenager, he handled lighting, sets, directing, and tried various roles himself.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，灯光、布景、导演都自己搞，各种角色也都尝试过。

D2.en: At 25-30, he spent his prison years recalling and re-creating every role he had performed.
D2.zh: 25到30岁间，他用回忆打发牢狱生涯，回忆演过的每部戏、每个角色，再表演再创造。

D3.en: At 14, he said he had been a veteran fan of Peking opera since childhood.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他说他从小就看京戏，是个老资格了。

D14.en: At 14, he watched a full performance of Dou Erdun at Shanghai Grand Theater.
D14.zh: 14岁那年，他在上海黄金大戏院看了全部《窦尔敦》。

D15.en: At 8, he began learning martial arts and studied various skills.
D15.zh: 8岁时，他学拳术，十八般武艺都学了点。

D12.en: As a small child, he accompanied his father grinding ink and writing, and went to Shandong with his elder brother.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他陪父亲磨墨写字，还跟大哥回山东老家。

D11.en: At 21, he had a sudden idea and kicked off his leather shoes.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他灵机一动，将脚上的皮鞋踢掉。

D6.en: At 18, he often participated in activities of the Left-Wing Drama League.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他经常参加左翼剧联的活动。

D7.en: At 25, he went on a hunger strike to protest, and guards force-fed him milk through the anus.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他闹绝食以示反抗，狱卒从肛门打牛奶整治他。

M1.en: At 21, he thought it was his time to shine and worked extra hard.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他觉得正是大显身手的时候，所以格外卖力。

D16.en: As a teenager, he gave patriotic speeches on the streets with the left-wing theater league.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，他跟随左翼剧联走上街头做爱国演讲。

D12.en: At 2, she was carried by her mother to a tram for the first time to see a doctor.
D12.zh: 2岁时，她被母亲抱着第一次搭乘电车去看医生。

D17.en: At 17, she felt love when meeting eyes with a child in her mother's arms.
D17.zh: 17岁时，她与母亲怀中的孩子目光相遇，心中爱意油然而生。

D3.en: At 22, she read the script 'Three Modern Women' and was moved by its realism.
D3.zh: 22岁时，她读完《三个摩登女性》剧本，被其反映现实所打动。

D13a.en: At 20, she considered breaking her contract with Tianyi to join Mingxing.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，她考虑毁约离开天一公司去明星公司。

D14.en: At 16, she wept while acting in mourning clothes, recalling her father's funeral.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她穿着孝衣拍戏时悲从中来，想起为父亲送葬的场景。

D18.en: At 20, she gave her savings to Da Min for his urgent need.
D18.zh: 20岁时，她把省下的三百多元钱给了达民急用。

D4.en: At 23, she rushed out in a storm and wept over Wang Guilin, mourning her father.
D4.zh: 23岁时，她冒雨扑倒在王桂林身上，放声恸哭悼念亡父。

D9.en: At 20, she resolved to support Zhu Shilin's directorial debut.
D9.zh: 20岁时，她决心全力支持朱石麟导演他的第一部戏。

D10.en: At 20, she trembled, paced, collapsed, and wept bitterly after hearing insults that reminded her of past humiliation.
D10.zh: 20岁时，她听到辱骂想起受辱经历，颤抖、乱走、瘫坐、苦笑流泪。

D11.en: At 16, she entered the Mingxing studio gate and went to the No. 2 glass studio as instructed.
D11.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，按通知到二号玻璃棚。

D15.en: At 19-20, she lay on thick ice in a torn jacket, holding a baby, for an hour.
D15.zh: 19到20岁时，她穿着破夹衫抱瘦女婴扑倒在厚冰上坚持一小时。

D16.en: At 25, she said, "Now that I'm dying, people must think I'm afraid of punishment."
D16.zh: 25岁时，她说：“我现在一死，人们一定以为我是畏罪。”

D19.en: At 16, she entered the Mingxing studio gate and went to the No. 2 glass studio as instructed.
D19.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，按通知到二号玻璃棚。

D20.en: At 16, she entered the Mingxing studio gate and went to the No. 2 glass studio as instructed.
D20.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，按通知到二号玻璃棚。

D20X.en: At 22, she politely refused a young director, fearing his inexperience.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，她婉拒了一个初出茅庐的青年导演，担心他拍不出好作品。

M1.en: At 24, she studied her role hard late into the night, reading the script alone under a lamp.
M1.zh: 24岁时，她下苦功研究角色，常深夜独自在灯下读剧本。

M2.en: At 24, she said, "I eat vegetarian to repay my elders' kindness, as filial piety."
M2.zh: 24岁时，她说：“我吃素是为了报答长辈养育之恩，是孝心。”

M3.en: At 16, she entered the Mingxing studio gate and went to the No. 2 glass studio as instructed.
M3.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，按通知到二号玻璃棚。

M4.en: At 17, she resolved to dedicate herself to art and film for the suffering and justice.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她暗下决心要为受苦的人们和正义献身给艺术和电影。

M5.en: At 23, she said she just wanted to make films properly, not be used for ads or tabloid attacks.
M5.zh: 23岁时，她说只想正正派派拍戏，不愿意别人拿她做广告或让小报记者造谣攻击。

M6.en: At 24, she wished for a god to bless her to live peacefully and happily.
M6.zh: 24岁时，她希望有神保佑她能平平安安、快快乐乐过下去。

M7.en: At 16, she entered the Mingxing studio gate and went to the glass studio for filming.
M7.zh: 16岁时，她走进明星公司大门，到拍戏的二号玻璃棚去。

M8.en: At 25, she quoted a proverb: 'When a bird is about to die, its song is sad; when a person is about to die, their words are kind.'
M8.zh: 25岁时，她引用了一句谚语：'鸟之将死，其鸣也悲，人之将死，其言也善。'

D6.en: At 20, she went to Guangdong with the location crew and was entertained by Sir Robert Ho Tung in Hong Kong.
D6.zh: 20岁时，她随外景队南下广东，在香港受到何东爵士盛情款待。

D2.en: At 24, she went to workers' quarters with Nie Er to prepare for a role, and dressed plainly on set to become the character.
D2.zh: 24岁时，她为演好角色和聂耳去工人居住区，在摄影棚穿淡蓝布旗袍，不化妆，默默坐在角落，成了影片中的韦明。

D8.en: At 22, she proactively asked director Bu Wancang if she could play Zhou Shuzhen.
D8.zh: 22岁时，她主动去找卜万苍问：“你看我来演周淑贞，合适吗？”

D13b.en: As a small child, she attended a traditional school for four years, then dropped out to farm.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，她在家乡读过四年私塾，然后辍学务农。

D12.en: At 11, she started selling rice with women in market towns, walking over ten miles of mountain paths.
D12.zh: 11岁时，她开始跟妇女们在集镇间贩米，要走十几里山路。

D18.en: From age 7 to 11, she herded cattle, cut grass, worked as a child bride, studied for half a year, and sold rice.
D18.zh: 7到11岁间，她放牛、割草、当童养媳、读书半年、贩米挣钱。

D2.en: At 14-15, she practiced vocal and physical skills daily from dawn, training in all seasons without pause.
D2.zh: 14到15岁时，她每天天不亮喊嗓子，练四功五法，夏练三伏冬练三九。

D6.en: At 10-12, her singing in the market attracted buyers, selling her group's rice quickly and at good prices.
D6.zh: 10到12岁时，她在集市唱歌吸引买米人，自己和妇女们的米卖得快又好价。

D3.en: At about 13, she learned a difficult aria after hearing it a few times, while others needed many tries.
D3.zh: 13岁左右，她听几遍就会唱别人学十几遍都学不会的难唱段。

D4.en: At 7, she listened, savored, pondered, and imitated folk songs, feeling fresh and amazed.
D4.zh: 7岁时，她倾听、品味、琢磨、模仿民歌，感到新鲜惊奇。

D11.en: At about 13, she improvised a song with her own lyrics set to a familiar folk tune.
D11.zh: 13岁左右，她自编歌词，套用熟悉的桐城民歌曲调唱出来。

D14.en: At 10-12, her singing in the market attracted buyers, selling her group's rice quickly and at good prices.
D14.zh: 10到12岁时，她在集市唱歌吸引买米人，自己和妇女们的米卖得快又好价。

D5.en: At 18, she was detained by a local militia leader, forced to be his concubine, abused, and attempted suicide by swallowing gold.
D5.zh: 18岁时，她被当地自卫队长扣押逼做小老婆，受凌辱毒打，吞金自杀。

D13a.en: At 8, she was sent as a child bride but ran back home.
D13a.zh: 8岁时，她被送去当童养媳，但跑回来了。

D13b.en: At 8, she was sent as a child bride.
D13b.zh: 8岁时，她被送去当童养媳。

D1.en: She practiced a difficult move for a week until she got it right.
D1.zh: 她苦练一个星期，终于完成了那个动作。

M1.en: At 8-10, after being stumped in duets, she decided to learn.
M1.zh: 8到10岁时，对歌卡壳后，她决定学。

D10.en: At 18, after a beating, she considered suicide but couldn't go through with it.
D10.zh: 18岁时，挨打后她想吞金戒指自杀，但没下决心。

D15.en: At 23, she played basketball aggressively on the court.
D15.zh: 23岁时，她在篮球场上横冲直撞。

M4.en: At 22, she vowed to become a qualified people's actor and join the Party.
M4.zh: 22岁时，她立志做合格人民演员，争取入党。

M7.en: At 21, she performed modern plays to support political campaigns.
M7.zh: 21岁时，她排演现代戏配合土改等运动。

D12.en: At 16, she decided to learn a skill to share her father's burden as the eldest daughter.
D12.zh: 16岁时，她决定学一门技艺为父亲分忧，担起长女的责任。

D3.en: At 24, she felt pressure but excitement, realizing her past films lacked real meaning.
D3.zh: 24岁时，她感到压力但更兴奋，意识到过去演的电影缺乏真正意义。

D11.en: At 16, while daydreaming, she looked out the window and got the inspiration to use 'Butterfly' as her name.
D11.zh: 16岁时，她苦思冥想时望向窗外，灵感闪现，决定用蝴蝶做名字。

D14.en: At 25-26, she accepted the challenge and, with guidance and practice, created iconic screen roles.
D14.zh: 25到26岁间，她接受挑战，在指导和揣摩下创造了经典银幕形象。

D2.en: At 18, she worked diligently on films, cooperating well with directors and spending almost every day under studio lights.
D2.zh: 18岁时，她认真拍戏，与导演配合默契，几乎天天在水银灯下度过。

D4.en: At 16, she and a friend hailed a taxi to the outskirts and asked the driver to teach them to drive.
D4.zh: 16岁时，她和好友拦下出租车去郊外，请求司机教她们开车。

D5.en: At 16, she and a friend hailed a taxi to the outskirts and asked the driver to teach them to drive.
D5.zh: 16岁时，她和好友拦下出租车去郊外，请求司机教她们开车。

D9.en: At 16, she and a friend hailed a taxi to the outskirts and asked the driver to teach them to drive.
D9.zh: 16岁时，她和好友拦下出租车去郊外，请求司机教她们开车。

M2.en: At 16, she resolved to achieve something to repay her parents.
M2.zh: 16岁时，她决心学出名堂来报效父母。

D1.en: At 17, during a scene, she wiped the lens instead of lifting the glasses, causing a retake.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她拍戏时擦镜框而非抬起眼镜，导致重拍。

D6.en: She and Pan Yousheng were inseparable at social events after returning from Europe.
D6.zh: 她与潘有声在回国后的应酬中形影不离。

D13a.en: She wisely left Tianyi to join Mingxing.
D13a.zh: 她明智地退出天一加盟明星。

M1.en: At 18, she set a goal to catch up with stars like Zhang Zhiyun, Yang Nai-mei, and Xuan Jinglin.
M1.zh: 18岁时，她立志要赶上张织云、杨耐梅、宣景琳等红星。

D15.en: She hung in the air with a wire around her waist while in costume.
D15.zh: 她身着戏装、腰挂铁丝悬在空中。

D17.en: She saved Zheng Chaofan's life when his wire broke, finding sudden strength.
D17.zh: 她救了郑超凡一命，危急中力量倍增。

D18.en: At 20, she opened a trading company named Hudie Company on Sichuan Road in Shanghai under the name of Hu Shaogong.
D18.zh: 20岁时，她以胡少贡的名义在上海四川路上开了一家商贸公司，取名胡蝶公司。

D10.en: She could laugh off the 'Xuedie contract case' and the Sept 18 dance scandal.
D10.zh: 她对雪蝶解约案和九一八跳舞风波一笑置之。

D7.en: She decided Lin Xuehuai was despicable and her love for him was gone.
D7.zh: 她认定林雪怀人品恶劣，对他已无爱意。

D16.en: She wrote a strongly worded letter to Lin Xuehuai on the 14th.
D16.zh: 她于14日给林雪怀写了封措辞强硬的信。

M7.en: She felt proud to star in the film, which cleared her name from the dance scandal.
M7.zh: 她为主演这部影片自豪，洗刷了跳舞风波的不白之冤。

D2.en: He practiced for five or six straight hours with barely a chance to breathe.
D2.zh: 他连续练习五六个小时，几乎没机会喘气。

D13b.en: At age 10, his parents let him go to opera school.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，父母让他去了戏曲学校。

D9.en: He directed his first film, The Fearless Hyena, at age 25.
D9.zh: 他25岁时导演了第一部电影《笑拳怪招》。

D14.en: He performed live as a youngster to exercise his skills.
D14.zh: 他小时候上台表演来锻炼技艺。

D4.en: At 22, he performed all five animal kung fu styles and used the staff accurately.
D4.zh: 22岁时，他表演了五种动物功夫并精准使用棍法。

D15.en: At 21, he prided himself on becoming a kung fu master by exhausting himself daily.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他自豪地通过每天累垮自己来成为功夫大师。

M1.en: At 17, he knew his destiny was more than a crowd extra or stunt performer.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他知道自己的命运不只是群众演员或特技替身。

D12.en: As a teenager, he worked for years at Shaw Brothers as a junior stuntman.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他在邵氏兄弟公司做了多年初级特技演员。

D1.en: After a failure in his early 20s, he was depressed but knew stunt work was always available.
D1.zh: 20岁出头失败后，他沮丧但知道总有特技工作可做。

D6.en: At 16, he went to a disco and showed off for girls.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他去迪斯科舞厅向女孩们炫耀。

D17.en: At 24, he cared for others' needs despite his own adversity.
D17.zh: 24岁时，他即使自己面临困境也愿意照顾他人的需求。

D5.en: At 12-13, his master forbade him from hanging around the shower entrance.
D5.zh: 12到13岁时，师父禁止他在淋浴入口闲逛。

D3.en: At 12, he studied multiple martial arts and sports.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他学习了多种武术和运动。

D7.en: At 17, he vied for cinema work against his parents' wishes.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他争取在电影界工作的机会，尽管父母完全反对。

D13a.en: At 17, he decided to leave school, believing he could do better.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他决定离开学校，相信自己能做得更好。

D11.en: At 7, he played alone, imagining himself as General Kwan Kung or the Monkey King.
D11.zh: 7岁时，他独自玩耍，想象自己是关公或孙悟空。

M6.en: At 7, he felt the Shaolin Temple was paradise.
M6.zh: 7岁时，他觉得少林寺是天堂。

M2.en: At 20, he decided to return to Hong Kong to succeed for his mother.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他决定回香港成功，以不辜负母亲的信任。

D8.en: He kicked out his foot and snapped a comic away across the polished floor.
D8.zh: 他一脚踢出，把漫画书踢飞，滑过光滑的木地板。

D10.en: He stood with his mouth open, feeling a sweet, pleasant pain rising from his belly.
D10.zh: 他张着嘴站在那里，感到一种从肚子升到喉咙的甜丝丝的愉快疼痛。

M5.en: He told the crowd he would take them all on and could afford to lose.
M5.zh: 他告诉人群，他要挑战他们所有人，而且输得起。

M7.en: He worked hard, doing double shifts when available.
M7.zh: 他工作努力，有双班就上。

M4.en: He signed a contract with Lo and didn't want to abandon Madame.
M4.zh: 他和罗签了合同，不想抛弃夫人。

D20X.en: He turned his back on Lo and ran to follow his friend.
D20X.zh: 他转身离开罗，跑去跟朋友走。

D5.en: At 22, she knew her first solo concert could make or break her career.
D5.zh: 22岁时，她知道第一次个人演唱会要么成功要么完蛋。

D12.en: At age 4.5, she started singing on stage in lounges and clubs.
D12.zh: 四岁半时，她开始在酒廊和歌厅登台唱歌。

D6.en: At 17, she entered a talent contest, signed up by her sister.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她参加新秀大赛，是姐姐帮她报的名。

D11.en: She loved the stage because it let her perform freely.
D11.zh: 她喜欢舞台，因为可以尽情发挥表演。

D7.en: As a child, she preferred playing with uncles and cousins over school.
D7.zh: 小时候，她更喜欢跟舅父、表哥和哥哥们玩耍。

M4.en: She said she would not fear dying for justice.
M4.zh: 她说为正义牺牲，她不怕死。

D10.en: At 24, she had a bad temper and mood swings, nearing a breakdown.
D10.zh: 24岁时，她脾气很坏，喜怒无常，濒临精神崩溃。

D9.en: At 22, she held her first solo concert with a suicide-squad resolve.
D9.zh: 22岁时，她举办第一次个人演唱会，下了敢死队般的决心。

D2.en: Even for songs she disliked, she sang her best without a sour face.
D2.zh: 就算点到不喜爱的歌，她也尽力唱好，不摆苦脸。

M1.en: At 19, she won first place in the 1982 Hong Kong New Talent Contest.
M1.zh: 19岁时，她获得1982年香港新秀比赛第一名。

D13a.en: At 14-15, she dropped out of school because she couldn't stand it.
D13a.zh: 14到15岁时，她因不堪忍受学校而退学。

D4.en: At about 19, she cut her hair short for the album 'Bad Girl'.
D4.zh: 大约19岁时，她为专辑《坏女孩》剪了短发。

D1.en: At 24, a strong wind nearly uprooted her.
D1.zh: 24岁时，一阵顶头风险些将她连根摧折。

D3.en: At 24-25, she was drawn to a book about Zhou Enlai.
D3.zh: 24到25岁时，她被一本关于周恩来的书吸引。

M6.en: At 24-25, she said she wouldn't fear dying for justice.
M6.zh: 24到25岁时，她说为正义牺牲不会怕死。

D20.en: At 14-15, as vice president, she organized a fan club gathering.
D20.zh: 14到15岁时，她作为副会长组织了歌迷会聚会。

D15.en: At 18, she trained in martial arts at Beijing Sport University.
D15.zh: 18岁时，她在北京体育学院接受武术训练。

D16.en: At 24-25, she said she wouldn't fear dying for justice.
D16.zh: 24到25岁时，她说为正义牺牲不会怕死。

D19.en: At 24-25, she gritted her teeth and got through it, and she succeeded.
D19.zh: 24到25岁时，她咬紧牙关挺过去了，然后她成功了。

D20X.en: At 24-25, she gritted her teeth and got through it, then succeeded.
D20X.zh: 24到25岁时，她咬紧牙关挺过去了，然后成功了。

M2.en: In her 20s, she used her songs to express the awakening of a modern woman.
M2.zh: 20多岁时，她用歌声表达时代女性的觉醒。

M3.en: At 24-25, she admired Zhou Enlai immensely.
M3.zh: 24到25岁时，她对周恩来佩服得五体投地。

M5.en: At 24, she said artists are people too and have the right to protect their dignity.
M5.zh: 24岁时，她说艺人也是人，有保护自己尊严的权力。

M7.en: At 24-25, she said she wouldn't fear dying for justice.
M7.zh: 24到25岁时，她说为正义牺牲不会怕死。

M8.en: At 24-25, she said passionately she wouldn't fear dying for justice.
M8.zh: 24到25岁时，她慷慨激昂地说为正义牺牲不会怕死。

D13b.en: At 20-22, she worked as a contracted singer for Hong Kong's Huaxing Entertainment.
D13b.zh: 20到22岁时，她在香港华星娱乐公司以合约歌星身份效力。

D17.en: In her 20s, she would push her sisters aside and say 'let me handle it'.
D17.zh: 20多岁时，她会把姐妹推到后面说我来。

D12.en: At 9-10, she dropped out after third grade and worked with her foster mother as a day laborer.
D12.zh: 她9到10岁时辍学，跟着养母去打零工。

D2.en: At 11, she secretly watched others play piano and asked to try the keys.
D2.zh: 她11岁时悄悄看别人弹琴，还伸手试键盘。

D14.en: At 12, she was chosen to lead the patriotic song 'National Light' in an anti-Japanese show.
D14.zh: 她12岁时被选为抗日歌舞剧终场歌曲《民族之光》的领唱。

D6.en: At 15, she greeted a friend while holding a doll and mimicking a puppet voice.
D6.zh: 她15岁时拿着布娃娃模仿木偶声音欢迎朋友。

D1.en: At 11, she practiced tirelessly, inspired by a dancer who fell and got up thousands of times.
D1.zh: 她11岁时刻苦练功，以黎莉莉跌倒爬起千遍为榜样。

M1.en: At 14, she ran to radio stations daily and practiced singing day and night for a competition.
M1.zh: 她14岁时每天跑电台卖力演唱，回家后昼夜练唱。

M7.en: At 12, she was chosen to lead the patriotic song 'National Light' in an anti-Japanese show.
M7.zh: 她12岁时被选为抗日歌舞剧终场歌曲《民族之光》的领唱。

D18.en: At 11, she gave most of her money to her foster mother and saved a little.
D18.zh: 她11岁时把大部分钱交给养母，只留一点攒起来。

D5.en: At 12, she substituted for the lead actress in the play 'Special Express' at the last minute.
D5.zh: 她12岁时临时顶替主演上台演出《特别快车》。

D10.en: At 17, she feared being trapped by a moment of sadness and felt suffocated.
D10.zh: 她17岁时害怕抓住刹那的思绪而尝尽悲痛，感到窒息。

D9.en: At 16, she took leave without permission to perform in Hangzhou.
D9.zh: 16岁时，她未经批准请假去杭州演出。

D7.en: At 16, she defied her studio's refusal of leave and went to Hangzhou.
D7.zh: 16岁时，她不顾公司不准假，执意去了杭州。

M4.en: At 13, she sang a patriotic line with such force that the audience erupted in cheers.
M4.zh: 13岁时，她唱到“与敌人周旋于沙场之上”时特别带劲，观众沸腾。

D15.en: At 17, she walked onto the deck to gaze at the sea during her first ocean voyage.
D15.zh: 17岁时，她第一次海上旅行，走到甲板上凭栏远望。

D16.en: At 12, she struggled with Mandarin retroflex sounds, confusing 'shan' and 'san'.
D16.zh: 12岁时，她分不清翘舌音，把“山”念成“三”。

D20.en: At 16, she toured West Lake with a companion and later recorded a song they created.
D20.zh: 16岁时，她与同伴游览西湖，并合唱了即兴创作的《扁舟情侣》并录成唱片。

M6.en: At 13, she decided on a path toward singing.
M6.zh: 13岁时，她为自己决定了一条通向歌唱的道路。

D3.en: At 15, filming 'Children of Troubled Times' sparked her desire for a film career.
D3.zh: 15岁时，通过拍摄《风云儿女》，她萌发了对电影艺术的渴望。

D4.en: As a teenager, she begged to learn piano and finished all basic exercises in under two years.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，她恳求学琴，两年内弹完拜伊尔全部基本练习曲。

D8.en: At 15, she was promoted to lead actress after hard work.
D8.zh: 15岁时，她因辛苦工作被擢升为第一女主角。

D11.en: At 13, she wrote diary entries that secretly revealed rippling emotions.
D11.zh: 13岁时，她在日记中写下看似普通的句子，却暗藏涟漪般的情意。

D13a.en: At 18, she finally nodded and agreed to try a period film, saying she wanted to expand her acting range.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她终于点了点头，说愿意试一试古装片，这对开拓戏路子有好处。

M2.en: At 17, she could not abandon the foster mother who raised her.
M2.zh: 17岁时，她抛不下养她长大成人的养母。

M8.en: At 17, she said love for family and country is a kind of faith.
M8.zh: 17岁时，她说家国之爱是一种信仰。

D13b.en: A boss 8 years older took her as his goddaughter; the news spread and became fact.
D13b.zh: 一个大她八岁的老板认她做干女儿，消息传出去后成了事实。

D17.en: At about 10, she washed her foster mother's face, cooked porridge, and worked to support her, giving her all earnings and care.
D17.zh: 大约10岁时，她帮养母洗脸、熬粥，自己不舍得吃，出去干活挣钱都交给养母，端茶倒水、洗衣擦身，无一不做。

D12.en: He was the first to arrive at his teacher's deathbed, wept bitterly, and set up a memorial hall.
D12.zh: 他第一个赶到恩师临终前，痛哭并设立灵堂抄经。

D13b.en: At age 10, he was sent to a theater troupe by his master.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他被主人送到戏班学戏。

D2.en: He worked harder than Mei Lanfang and created a unique singing style despite lacking a golden voice.
D2.zh: 他比梅兰芳更刻苦，凭自身条件创出独特声腔。

D14.en: He eagerly watched new films, befriended movie stars, and said he would have acted in films if not for his family.
D14.zh: 他抢先看新电影，结交影星，曾说要不是叶家早去拍电影了。

D6.en: He danced, skated, swam, rode horses, shot, and rode trendy bicycles and motorcycles.
D6.zh: 他跳舞、溜冰、游泳、骑马、射击，还骑名牌自行车和摩托车。

D1.en: At 19, he read his teacher's letter urging him to quit gambling and decided to stop playing mahjong.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他读了老师劝他戒赌的信，决定不再打牌。

D4.en: As a teenager, he made wooden props, painted masks and scenery, and built a crystal radio.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他做木刀枪、画脸谱布景，还装矿石收音机。

D15.en: In his teens and twenties, he danced, skated, swam, rode horses, shot, and rode bicycles and motorcycles.
D15.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，他跳舞、溜冰、游泳、骑马、射击，还骑自行车和摩托车。

M1.en: At 23, he was the only one among the Four Great Dan who aspired to surpass Mei Lanfang.
M1.zh: 23岁时，四大名旦中只有他雄心万丈想追赶梅兰芳。

D5.en: At 20, he turned professional as a xiaoyou, debuting with 'Catching and Releasing Cao' at a tanghui.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他以票友下海，在堂会上唱了第一出戏《捉放曹》。

D10.en: At 19, he set strict rules for himself: no second woman and no female disciples.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他对自己要求极端严格，立下不传女弟子、一生无二色的规矩。

D16.en: In the 1950s, he explained that his unique playing came from practicing violin and using staccato bowing.
D16.zh: 1950年代，他说自己演奏与众不同是因为常练小提琴，用上了断奏弓法。

D19.en: He performed meticulously and valued stage coordination.
D19.zh: 他演戏一丝不苟，非常讲究舞台配合。

M7.en: He was always enthusiastic about charity performances and attended if in Beijing.
M7.zh: 他演义务戏一向热心，人在北京就一定参加。

D17.en: At age 20, Cheng Yanqiu personally attended to meals daily without interruption.
D17.zh: 20岁时程砚秋每日亲自照料饮食，从未间断

D11.en: At age 20, he wrote a five-character poem 'Remembering Master Yinggong'.
D11.zh: 20岁时作五言诗《忆瘿公师》

M8.en: At age 20, Cheng Yanqiu stopped performing for over a month, wrote a poem recalling his teacher Luo Yinggong.
M8.zh: 程砚秋20岁时辍演月余，作诗怀念老师罗瘿公。

D2.en: He studied vocal music with teacher Shen Xueyong and continued voice training at the art college.
D2.zh: 他跟申学庸老师学声乐，在艺专继续练声乐。

D3.en: He experienced snow for the first time and borrowed banned books from the library at UIUC.
D3.zh: 他在伊大第一次经历下雪，从图书馆借了禁书回来读。

D16.en: At 12-15, he experienced his first cultural shock.
D16.zh: 12到15岁间，他经历了第一次文化震撼。

D12.en: He was slapped by a teacher at school for poor exam results at age 10.
D12.zh: 他十岁时因考试不佳在学校被老师打耳光。

D14.en: He first felt fate chose him when he stood on stage under the spotlight at 18.
D14.zh: 他十八岁第一次站上舞台，感觉是戏剧选择了他。

D9.en: At 20, he possibly directed Taiwan's first experimental circular theater, 'The Story of Tajis', with the audience in the center.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他可能执导了台湾第一个实验性圆形剧场《泰吉丝的故事》，观众席设在中间。

D11.en: At 20, he wrote a short story 'The Distorted Crane Burner' about filming 'Lazy Saturday Afternoon'.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他写了一万多字的短篇小说《走了样的焚鹤人》，讲述拍摄《星期六下午的懒散》的经历。

D7.en: He defied his father by leaving the table and locking himself in his room.
D7.zh: 他把筷子一放，走回房间锁上门，第一次犯上。

D6.en: In high school, he joined the choir.
D6.zh: 高中时他参加了合唱团。

D1.en: He broke a crane kite while test-flying it, then remade the prop in April the next year to finish the film.
D1.zh: 他试飞鹤形风筝时摔断了鹤脖子，隔年四月重做道具才完成片子。

D4.en: At 19, he used a camera to shoot an 18-minute black-and-white short film.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他用一台摄影机拍了一部18分钟的黑白短片。

D15.en: At 19-20, he learned ballet for over a month and performed in a one-act ballet.
D15.zh: 19到20岁时，他学了一个多月芭蕾，还上台演了一出独幕芭蕾舞剧。

M1.en: He graduated from NYU and won awards, considered an excellent student.
M1.zh: 他从纽约大学毕业并拿过奖，算是优秀生。

M6.en: At 18-22, he felt he was better at film and theater, and managed to create something.
M6.zh: 18到22岁时，他觉得自己拍电影、做戏剧比较容易上手，也就做出了一些东西。

D12.en: At age 3-5, he took a bus alone and transferred, scaring his parents.
D12.zh: 他三到五岁时自己坐公共汽车还倒车，把父母吓坏了。

D2.en: As a child, he often got perfect scores and was a top student.
D2.zh: 他小时候好多个双百，学习成绩特别好。

D3.en: He learned classical music while learning to play the trumpet.
D3.zh: 他学吹号时做古典音乐，接受了音乐训练。

D4.en: He learned to play the trumpet and performed classical music.
D4.zh: 他学吹号时演奏古典音乐。

D6.en: His father would drink, tap a stick, sing and dance.
D6.zh: 他爸喝酒后拿棍敲打，唱歌跳舞。

D18.en: His parents lost their jobs, and he couldn't enter university.
D18.zh: 他父母下岗，自己进不了大学。

D11.en: At 25, he wrote the script for the event before the performance.
D11.zh: 他25岁时为这个活动在演出前写了剧本。

D14.en: On May 10, 1986, he stepped onto the stage, marking the start of Chinese rock history.
D14.zh: 1986年5月10日他踏上舞台，中国摇滚乐的历史从那天算起。

D9.en: He felt challenged singing on a socialist stage and kept trying to break through.
D9.zh: 他每次唱都觉得有挑战性，在社会主义舞台上必须试，每次都做点突破。

D7.en: He was told not to sing songs with the word 'red' at a Shenzhen performance.
D7.zh: 他在深圳演出时被告知不能唱带‘红’字的歌。

D3.en: In 1974 at age 24, he bought a Seagull 4 twin-lens reflex camera.
D3.zh: 1974年24岁时，他买了一台海鸥4型双镜头反光相机。

D4.en: At 24-26, he trained himself harshly because he was poor and followed photography books closely.
D4.zh: 24到26岁间，他因为穷而苛刻地自我训练，照着书上的话练。

D14.en: At 18-21, he brought tools and paint to the village and painted portraits on all doors without being asked.
D14.zh: 18到21岁间，他自带工具和颜料，把村里所有的门都画上主席像，没人要求。

D7.en: At 18-21, he painted portraits on all village doors without anyone's permission.
D7.zh: 18到21岁间，他没征得同意就把村里所有的门画上主席像。

D9.en: At 21-28, as the workshop propaganda officer, he had more chances to leave the production line.
D9.zh: 21到28岁间，他作为车间宣传员，有更多机会溜出生产第一线。

D6.en: At 24-28, he was lent to organize exhibitions and learned art basics from skilled colleagues.
D6.zh: 24到28岁间，他被工会借出去办展览，从能人那里学到美学常识。

D19.en: At 24-28, he placed negatives under some photos on white cardboard and drew clear cutting lines.
D19.zh: 24到28岁间，他在白卡纸上的照片下面放了底片，裁切线画得很清楚。

D12.en: At 16, he began making himself useful by copying big-character posters for others.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他通过给别人抄大字报开始让自己工具化。

D18.en: At 21, he was assigned as an auxiliary worker in the factory.
D18.zh: 21岁时，他被分到工厂做辅助工。

D13b.en: At 13, he told his father he would not go to university but to technical school.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他告诉父亲不打算上大学，要考中技。

M1.en: At 21-24, his biggest dream was to become a propaganda officer in the labor union.
M1.zh: 21到24岁间，他最大的理想就是去工会做个宣传干事。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote a nursery rhyme, submitted it to a newspaper, and saw it published a week later.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他写了一首儿歌寄给报社，一周后看到它被刊登出来。

D5.en: He traveled alone to Italy and Greece after his companion went to Morocco.
D5.zh: 他独自去了意大利和希腊，因为同伴去了摩洛哥。

D10.en: At about 26, he couldn't help telling his teacher he wanted to disband the dance company.
D10.zh: 大约26岁时，他忍不住跟老师说他想解散舞团。

D13a.en: He waited until Dr. Graham died to become a professional dancer.
D13a.zh: 他等到葛兰姆医生去世后，才成为职业舞者。

D7.en: He hated the US occupation, led campus protests, and fled from police.
D7.zh: 他憎恨美国占领，策动校园示威，被警方搜缉后开始逃亡。

M1.en: He became a teaching assistant and then teacher through determination and hard work at age 22-24.
M1.zh: 他22到24岁间，靠决心和努力很快成为助教、教师。

M7.en: His mother was a pianist who sacrificed her art for marriage.
M7.zh: 他母亲是为婚姻牺牲艺术的钢琴家

D4.en: He learned movement breakdown from teacher Cao Junlin, a key choreography lesson at age 23.
D4.zh: 他23岁时，曹骏麟老师为他拆解示范起霸动作，那是一堂重要的编舞启蒙课。

M3.en: He was captivated by St. Denis on stage at age 17.
M3.zh: 他17岁时，被舞台上的圣·丹尼丝吸引。

D16.en: He insisted on using all Chinese modern composers' music, surprising Teacher Shi at age 23.
D16.zh: 他23岁时说，全部要用中国现代作曲家的音乐，史老师喜出望外。

M4.en: He insisted on using all Chinese modern composers' music at age 23.
M4.zh: 他23岁时坚持全部要用中国现代作曲家的音乐。

D1.en: At about 20, homeless and aimless, he learned drumming from Sado islanders to find an outlet.
D1.zh: 20岁左右，他无家可归，跟佐渡岛民学打鼓来发泄。

D13b.en: He was a university freshman who loved Chinese literature.
D13b.zh: 他是个热爱中国文学的大一学生。

D19.en: He woke at midnight and saw his mother kneeling at a low table, pondering household accounts.
D19.zh: 他半夜醒来，隔着蚊帐看见母亲跪在日式矮桌前，对着家用账本沉思。

M2.en: He believed his father's integrity as a scholar-official was impossible without his mother's full support.
M2.zh: 他认为父亲为官清廉，没有母亲全心全意支持是办不到的。

D14.en: At 14, he praised Chaplin's clever acting as Billy.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他称赞查理·卓别林扮演比利的表演非常聪明。

D12.en: At 9, he was sent off to Norwood Schools with his brother Sydney.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他和哥哥西德尼被送到诺伍德学校。

D6.en: At 22, he attended one of Chaplin's tennis afternoons escorted by Durant.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他在杜兰特的陪同下参加了卓别林的一个网球下午。

D5.en: At 18, he obtained a trial booking without pay in a small theater off the Mile End Road.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他在迈尔恩德路附近的一家小剧院获得了一次无薪试演。

D13b.en: At 21, he signed a new contract on 19 September 1910, though his old one wasn't due until March 1911.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他在1910年9月19日签了一份新合同，尽管旧合同到1911年3月才到期。

D18.en: At 15, he said he made half a crown on the afternoon tea table, totaling £3-2-6.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他说他在下午茶桌上赚了半克朗，总共3英镑2先令6便士。

D7.en: At 19, he claimed and received National Assistance in 1965, to the press's delight.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他在1965年申请并获得了国家补助，让媒体津津乐道。

D2.en: At 24, he said he would save like a son of a gun.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他说他会拼命存钱。

D9.en: At 25, he finished a six-reel picture with Marie Dressler and hogged the whole picture.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他和玛丽·德雷斯勒完成了一部六本影片，并独揽了整部片子。

D4.en: At age 20-21, he threw the first Keystone pie, hitting Fatty Arbuckle's face.
D4.zh: 20到21岁时，他扔了第一块启斯东派，正中胖亚巴克尔的脸。

D15.en: At 15, he marched in a fancy dress ball procession playing the bugle, then blew the bugle for Scotsmen in kilts on deck.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他穿着盛装在游行队伍中吹号，然后为穿苏格兰裙的人在甲板上吹号。

D1.en: At 25, he spent half a week's salary on an opera cloak he didn't need.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他花了一半周薪买了一件不需要的歌剧斗篷。

D10.en: At 14, he cried when he heard he was late for the matinee.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他听说自己赶不上日场演出就哭了。

D8.en: He used his will and comic example to make her his leading lady.
D8.zh: 他凭意志和喜剧榜样把她变成了他的女主角。

D6.en: At 14, she went to the beach with a boyfriend, wearing a skimpy bathing suit for the first time.
D6.zh: 14岁时，她第一次穿着暴露的泳衣和男友去海滩。

D10.en: At 12, she was obviously depressed after an unhappy stay with the Martin-Monroe family.
D10.zh: 12岁时，她在马丁-门罗家过得不开心，明显抑郁了。

D13b.en: At 18, she followed their example and joined the church.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，她效仿他们加入了教会。

D4.en: At 23, she returned to Kelley's studio and signed a release form as 'Mona Monroe'.
D4.zh: 23岁时，她回到凯利的工作室，以“莫娜·梦露”的名义签了一份授权书。

D2.en: At 19, she was scrupulous in attending fashion modeling classes during the first few weeks.
D2.zh: 19岁时，头几周她一丝不苟地参加时装模特课程。

D13a.en: At 19, Dougherty cut off her allotment after she wrote him about a divorce.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，她写信给多尔蒂谈离婚，他生气地断了她的津贴。

D5.en: At 23-24, she sometimes offered her body as well as time and attention to a man who might help her.
D5.zh: 23到24岁时，她有时会向可能帮助她的男人献出身体、时间和关注。

M1.en: At 25, she scored a triumph in Clash by Night, playing a tough, feisty working-class girl.
M1.zh: 25岁时，她在《夜间冲突》中大获成功，扮演一个强悍泼辣的工人阶级女孩。

D3.en: At 14, she stared at the ocean, full of gold, lavender, blue, and foaming white.
D3.zh: 14岁时，她凝视着大海，海面满是金色、淡紫、蓝色和泡沫般的白色。

D14.en: At 23, she signed a release form as 'Mona Monroe' at Kelley's studio.
D14.zh: 23岁时，她在凯利的工作室以'莫娜·梦露'的名义签署了一份释放表格。

D11.en: As a teenager, she wrote articles for the school newspaper, including one on the ideal dream girl.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，她为校报撰稿，包括一篇关于理想梦中女孩的文章。

D18.en: At 18, she worked 10 hours a day at Radioplane Co.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她每天在雷迪欧普兰公司工作10小时。

D9.en: At 25, she talked a man into becoming her press agent.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她说服了一个人成为她的公关代理人。

D17.en: At 17, she loved to babysit, bathing, cleaning, playing, and reading to children.
D17.zh: 17岁时，她喜欢当保姆，给孩子们洗澡、洗衣服、玩耍、读书。

D7.en: At 23, disappointed with the tour, she left early and returned home, angering Cowan.
D7.zh: 23岁时，她对巡演感到失望，提前离开回家，惹恼了考恩。

M3.en: As a young teen, she wrote an essay on Abraham Lincoln, her hero.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，她写了一篇关于她英雄亚伯拉罕·林肯的文章。

D15.en: At 12, she received athletic awards from Los Angeles City Schools.
D15.zh: 12岁时，她获得了洛杉矶市立学校的体育奖项。

M6.en: At 18, she joined the church, following others' example.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她效仿他人加入了教会。

D1.en: At 22, she reacted to adversity by refusing to dwell on it and taking immediate positive action.
D1.zh: 22岁时，她面对逆境不纠结，立即采取积极行动。

D8.en: She demanded he replicate the makeup she applied as a model.
D8.zh: 她要求他复制她作为模特时化的妆。

M4.en: She refused to go on his yacht unless his wife came along.
M4.zh: 她拒绝和他一起坐游艇去卡塔利娜，除非他妻子也去。

D16.en: She cited the dialogue.
D16.zh: 她引用了那段对话。

M5.en: At 20, she said, 'I know who you are.'
M5.zh: 20岁时，她说：‘我知道你是谁。’

D12.en: At 13-14, she took care of her family, cooking for her father and making shoes for her brother.
D12.zh: 13到14岁时，她当家了，每天给父亲烧菜送饭，还会给哥哥做布鞋。

D11.en: As a child, she read martial arts novels and went to learn martial arts at a school in Beijing.
D11.zh: 她看了武侠小说，跑到北平四民武术社拜师习武。

D15.en: At 4-7, she went out to gather firewood despite illness and felt better in nature.
D15.zh: 4到7岁时，她支撑着软弱的身子出门，背着满筐柴草回家后不再咳嗽发烧。

D18.en: At 11, after her mother died and family broke up, she joined progressive theater activities.
D18.zh: 11岁时，母亡家破，她参加了许多进步的演剧活动。

D6.en: At 22, she was moved by students staying overnight after a play and joined them to talk.
D6.zh: 22岁时，她深受感动，与同仁们走到大学师生中间畅谈。

D14.en: At 18, she played multiple leading roles in plays like Yuli Li in 'Nightlight Cup' and Jin Guniang in 'Black Hell'.
D14.zh: 18岁时，她饰演了《夜光杯》中的郁丽丽、《黑地狱》中的金姑娘等多个女主角。

D3.en: At 8-14, she read martial arts novels, joined a martial arts school, and practiced breathing exercises at night.
D3.zh: 8到14岁时，她看武侠小说，拜师学武，晚上练吐纳法。

M1.en: At 17, she rose to fame and replaced Hu Die as the top actress.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她一鸣惊人，由她代替胡蝶坐上了第一把交椅。

D2.en: At 18, she practiced singing repeatedly until she mastered the local opera style.
D2.zh: 18岁时，她不怕人笑，到处找人教，一句一句学，终于练好了这段唱。

D5.en: At 11, she joined a film studio and started her acting career in a silent film.
D5.zh: 11岁时，她考入联华影业公司演员养成所，在无声影片中扮演小丫头。

D1.en: At 9-10, she studied day and night to catch up on schoolwork.
D1.zh: 9到10岁时，她起早贪黑地念书写字，一个学期赶上了功课。

D9.en: At 17, she immersed herself in rehearsals, working tirelessly from morning to night.
D9.zh: 17岁时，她一头埋进排练，从早到晚奔忙在排练场上。

D7.en: At 16-17, she decided to give land to poor tenant farmers for free.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，她擅自决定把土地白给了特穷的佃农。

D8.en: At 16-17, she decided to give land to poor tenant farmers for free.
D8.zh: 16到17岁时，她擅自决定把土地白给了特穷的佃农。

D4.en: At 18, she bought cigarettes and practiced smoking in front of a mirror for a role.
D4.zh: 18岁时，她整包买香烟对着镜子练习抽烟。

M7.en: At 18, she said that leaving one's colleagues and nation is like a lone sail leaving its course, risking shipwreck.
M7.zh: 她18岁时说，离开同仁和民族利益擅自出走，会如孤帆触礁沉没。

D20.en: At 17, she served as a standing committee member of the newly formed Film Workers Association.
D20.zh: 17岁时，她是新成立的电影工作者协会的常务委员。

D10.en: At 18, she felt angry but then accepted that everyone has their own path.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她听了很气恼，但冷静后不强人所难。

M4.en: At 12-13, she joined leftist drama groups and performed in plays.
M4.zh: 12到13岁时，她参加左翼戏剧团体并演出话剧。

D13b.en: At 18, she reported the letter to her superiors immediately.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，她接信后马上禀告了沈浮和陈白尘。

D16.en: At 18, she performed a patriotic speech as a character facing execution.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她饰演临刑前痛斥汉奸的角色。

D20X.en: At 18, she was annoyed but accepted that others had different aspirations.
D20X.zh: 18岁时，她很气恼但冷静后接受了人各有志。

M2.en: At 18, she said we should be loyal to the country and honor our ancestors.
M2.zh: 18岁时，她说应该忠于国家、对祖先尽孝。

M5.en: At 18, she was determined not to be tricked into doing something dishonorable.
M5.zh: 18岁时，她决不上当做那种事。

M6.en: At 18, she loved film and longed to return to the screen.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她酷爱电影，渴望重返银幕。

D13a.en: At 21, she was told to be a pillar of the troupe, not a fixed actor.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她被要求不当固定演员，而是做剧社台柱子。

D7.en: He refused to sign any option, leaving Kramer with a one-picture deal.
D7.zh: 他拒绝签署任何期权，只给了克莱默一部电影的合约。

D5.en: After Byrnes broke his nose, he went on that night.
D5.zh: 伯恩斯打断了他的鼻子后，他当晚继续演出。

D6.en: He was extremely reserved and polite to his elders.
D6.zh: 他对长辈非常拘谨和礼貌。

D9.en: He marshaled his younger sister to join him in antics for attention.
D9.zh: 他带领妹妹一起搞怪以吸引注意。

D15.en: In gym, he walked away with a string of Es.
D15.zh: 体育课上，他拿到一连串的E。

D11.en: He loved Shakespeare but changed his lines.
D11.zh: 他热爱莎士比亚但会改台词。

D8.en: He insisted Kanter sit in back of the limo with him.
D8.zh: 他坚持让坎特和他一起坐在豪华轿车后排。

D13b.en: During three years at Libertyville High, he earned only six credits out of a possible fifteen.
D13b.zh: 在自由村高中的三年里，他只修了十五个学分中的六个。

D14.en: He realized what a terrific actor he could be after seeing Bud's incredible performance.
D14.zh: 他看到巴德的精彩表演后，意识到自己可以成为多棒的演员。

D2.en: He decided to take acting classes with Richard Boleslavsky at the American Laboratory Theatre.
D2.zh: 他决定在美国实验剧院跟理查德·波列斯拉夫斯基上表演课。

D4.en: He moved into the Birmingham Veteran's Hospital, sharing a ward with thirty-one paraplegic men.
D4.zh: 他搬进了伯明翰退伍军人医院，与三十一名截瘫患者同住一个病房。

D10.en: He cried throughout a showing of The Yearling and during a screening of The Wizard of Oz.
D10.zh: 他在看《鹿苑长春》和《绿野仙踪》时从头哭到尾。

D17.en: He dug up a buried pet and brought it back inside; his father reburied it, but he dug it up again.
D17.zh: 他挖出埋掉的宠物带回屋里；父亲重新埋了，他又挖了出来。

D18.en: He worked running an elevator at Best's department store for about a week.
D18.zh: 他在贝斯特百货公司开电梯，只干了一周左右。

D1.en: He withdrew from a show to join the cast of Maxwell Anderson's Truckline Cafe.
D1.zh: 他退出了一个演出，加入了麦克斯韦尔·安德森的《卡车线咖啡馆》剧组。

D13a.en: At 25, he said he had to go back to the States to do his third year.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他说他必须回美国读第三年。

M4.en: At 22, he sympathized with the cause of establishing a Jewish state to welcome Holocaust survivors.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他同情建立犹太国家以欢迎大屠杀幸存者的事业。

M7.en: At 23-25, he tried to spare others the loneliness he knew as a boy.
M7.zh: 23到25岁间，他试图让别人免受他童年时的孤独。

D12.en: She walked 2.5 miles to the theater to save carfare.
D12.zh: 她步行2.5英里到剧院以省下车费。

D2.en: She receives instruction excellently and follows orders closely, like wax in my hands.
D2.zh: 她接受指导极好，严格遵守指令，像蜡一样在我手中。

D9.en: She was told she and Stiller were to leave for Hollywood the next day.
D9.zh: 她被告知她和斯蒂勒第二天要动身去好莱坞。

D18.en: At 14, she earned seven crowns a week soaping customers' faces in a barbershop.
D18.zh: 14岁时，她在理发店给顾客脸上抹肥皂，每周挣七克朗。

D14.en: In 1919-1920, she was on stage in Cappy Ricks at the Lincoln Theatre, Decatur, Illinois.
D14.zh: 1919到1920年间，她在伊利诺伊州迪凯特林肯剧院出演《卡皮·里克斯》。

M4.en: At 12, she would join clusters at street corners or study photos outside movie houses.
M4.zh: 12岁时，她会加入街角的群体或研究电影院外的照片。

D6.en: At 17-18, she was the accepted center of their little group at the café where they lunched.
D6.zh: 17到18岁时，她是他们午餐咖啡馆小团体公认的中心。

D7.en: At 21, when they wanted her to do The Flesh and the Devil, she simply stayed at home, causing a scandal.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他们想让她演《肉体与魔鬼》，她干脆待在家里，引起轩然大波。

D11.en: At 12, she watched the coming and going of clouds, which was her geography.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她观察云朵的来去，那是她的地理课。

M1.en: At 12, she dreamed of being at their little place by Lake Årsta.
M1.zh: 12岁时，她只想在奥斯塔湖旁的小地方该有多好。

M2.en: At 12, she was the youngest of three children, and the family got on well together.
M2.zh: 12岁时，她是三个孩子中最小的，一家人相处融洽。

M5.en: At 12, she felt too grown up to spend her time glued to a school bench.
M5.zh: 12岁时，她觉得自己长大了，不该整天黏在学校板凳上。

M7.en: At 12, she would join clusters at street corners.
M7.zh: 12岁时，她会加入街角的群体。

M8.en: At 12, she watched clouds come and go; that was her geography.
M8.zh: 12岁时，她看云彩来去，那就是她的地理。

D17.en: At 21, she talked baby talk to him.
D17.zh: 21岁时，她用儿语跟他说话。

D10.en: At 21, she said, 'I get so nervous from playing these parts that I quite lose my head.'
D10.zh: 21岁时，她说：“演这些角色让我紧张得昏了头。”

D8.en: At 15, she told a friend to stay away from her girl friends.
D8.zh: 15岁时，她告诉朋友离她的女性朋友远点。

D14.en: At 22, she first appeared on television in a live half-hour drama.
D14.zh: 22岁时，她首次在电视上出演完整的半小时直播剧。

D12.en: At 24-25, she was left with two babies and no husband.
D12.zh: 24到25岁时，她独自带着两个孩子，没有丈夫。

D6.en: At 13, she and other students risked safety by presenting private dance recitals.
D6.zh: 13岁时，她和几个学生冒着危险举办私人舞蹈演出。

D10.en: At 5-6, she often wept in secret, for if she cried in front of others she was scolded.
D10.zh: 5到6岁时，她常偷偷哭，因为在别人面前哭会被骂。

D2.en: At 10, her mother enrolled her in ballet class at the Arnhem Conservatory.
D2.zh: 10岁时，她母亲给她报了阿纳姆音乐舞蹈学院的芭蕾班。

D13a.en: At 23, she announced the termination of her engagement to James Hanson.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，她宣布解除与詹姆斯·汉森的婚约。

D3.en: At 22, she learned that publicity must always be subordinated to professionalism.
D3.zh: 22岁时，她学到宣传必须始终服从于职业操守。

D4.en: At 22-23, she herself suggested the broad leather belt that cinched her waist.
D4.zh: 22到23岁时，她亲自建议用宽皮带勒紧细腰。

M4.en: At 21, she reactivated feelings from wartime fear and privation.
M4.zh: 21岁时，她重新唤起了战时恐惧和匮乏的感受。

D1.en: At 21, she sounded out Balcon's staff for opportunities.
D1.zh: 21岁时，她试探巴尔康的员工有没有机会。

D9.en: At 21, she attracted the attention of casting director John Redway.
D9.zh: 21岁时，她引起了选角导演约翰·雷德威的注意。

D18.en: At 13, she gave private dance recitals to raise money for resistance workers.
D18.zh: 13岁时，她举办私人舞蹈演出为抵抗运动工作者筹款。

M1.en: At 23, she decided it would be unfair to marry Jimmy while tied to her work.
M1.zh: 23岁时，她认为在热爱工作的情况下嫁给吉米不公平。

M3.en: At 11, she considered Fonteyn an early role model in style and ballet.
M3.zh: 11岁时，她视芳婷为个人风格和芭蕾的早期榜样。

D5.en: At 23, she asked Paramount to buy a play for her before it was staged.
D5.zh: 23岁时，她请派拉蒙为她买下一部尚未上演的剧本。

D16.en: At 22, she made small talk in French.
D16.zh: 22岁时，她用法语闲聊。

D15.en: As a child, she was thrown by a pony, dislocating her collarbone, and became uncomfortable with horses.
D15.zh: 幼年时，她被小马摔下来，锁骨脱臼，从此对马感到不适。

D1.en: At 21, she went to the theater to wish Nikolas and the cast well.
D1.zh: 21岁时，她去剧院祝尼古拉斯和演员们顺利。

D7.en: At age 4-10, she chose to wear her elder brother Tom's castoffs.
D7.zh: 4到10岁时，她选择穿哥哥Tom的旧衣服。

D15.en: Learned golf at age 5; competed in state championships as a teen.
D15.zh: 5岁学高尔夫，青少年时参加州锦标赛。

D10.en: At 23-32, she said her nerves are terrible and she gobbles food when watched.
D10.zh: 23到32岁间，她说自己神经紧张，被人看着就狼吞虎咽。

D13a.en: At 21, she admitted she was wrong and her father was right: theater was not for her.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她承认自己错了，父亲是对的：戏剧不适合她。

D5.en: At 24, she impulsively decided to return to the stage immediately.
D5.zh: 24岁时，她一时冲动决定立即重返舞台。

D13b.en: As a small child, she was spanked, plunged into cold showers, and forbidden candies.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，她被打屁股、被泼冷水、被禁止吃糖果。

D8.en: At 25, she told someone, 'You're too tall! He's a little minister!'
D8.zh: 25岁时，她对某人说：“你太高了！他是个小牧师！”

D18.en: At 25, she argued she always got seasick, so why waste money being sick in first-class?
D18.zh: 25岁时，她争辩说自己总是晕船，何必浪费钱在头等舱里晕？

D20.en: At 24, she bombarded the director with suggestions; he often let her take over.
D20.zh: 24岁时，她不断向导演提建议；他常常让她接手。

M4.en: She donated the $60 from the performance to buy a phonograph for the Navajos.
M4.zh: 她把演出赚的60美元捐给纳瓦霍人买了留声机。

M1.en: At 18-19, she told her family she hoped she'd die so she wouldn't have to go back to school.
M1.zh: 18到19岁时，她告诉家人她希望自己死掉，这样就不用回学校了。

D17.en: At 13, she collected herself, cut him down, and tried to revive him.
D17.zh: 13岁时，她镇定下来，把他放下来，试图救活他。

D12.en: At 10, he was given a horse on condition that he care for it himself.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他得到一匹马，条件是他自己照顾它。

D3.en: At 23, he sat at Googie's studying people for hours.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他坐在古吉餐厅花几个小时观察人。

D11.en: At 24, he made up poems and things, his ideas on what it might be like to die.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他编了诗和东西，那是他对死亡可能的想象。

D2.en: At 22, he practiced dancing to achieve greater physical flexibility for acting.
D2.zh: 22岁时，他练习舞蹈以提高表演的身体灵活性。

D6.en: At 18-19, he volunteered at registration, ushered at performances, and took tickets at dances.
D6.zh: 18到19岁时，他自愿帮忙注册、在演出中引座、在舞会上收票。

D5.en: At 23, he said: 'I got a red 53 MG... My sex pours itself into fast curves, broadslides and broodings, drags, etc.'
D5.zh: 23岁时，他说：'我搞了一辆红色53 MG……我的性欲倾注在急弯、侧滑、沉思、飙车等之中。'

D4.en: Before age 10, his uncle taught him to drive and service the tractor.
D4.zh: 10岁前，他叔叔教他开拖拉机并维修它。

D10.en: At 23, he said: 'I’m sad most of the time. Awful lonely too... I hope you’re dying, BECAUSE I AM.'
D10.zh: 23岁时，他说：'我大部分时间都难过。也非常孤独……我希望你快死了，因为我就要死了。'

D14.en: At 22, a reviewer praised his 'magnetic performance that brought a routine melodrama alive.'
D14.zh: 22岁时，一位评论家称赞他'磁性的表演让一部常规情节剧活了起来。'

D16.en: At 14, he challenged a boy to a fistfight, leading to a temporary suspension from school.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他向一个男孩挑战拳击，导致被暂时停学。

M1.en: At 21, he joined the Actors Studio after months of auditioning.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他经过数月试镜后加入了演员工作室。

D1.en: He gave someone ten dollars to fix their things and try again.
D1.zh: 他给了某人十美元，让他们修好东西再试一次。

D13a.en: At 19, he moved in with another student from City College.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他搬去与城市学院的另一名学生合住。

D9.en: He and his trio conspired to bring the amateur's older brother to the fight.
D9.zh: 他和他的三人组密谋把业余选手的哥哥带到比赛现场。

D7.en: At 21, he asked agents why they ignore actors until others notice them.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他质问经纪人为什么总等别人注意到演员才关注他们。

D20.en: At 19, he lost his temper and knocked out two sporting heroes, then was expelled from Sigma Nu.
D20.zh: 19岁时，他发脾气打倒了两位体育英雄，随后被Sigma Nu开除。

D15.en: At 24, he entered himself and his Porsche in two local races.
D15.zh: 24岁时，他给自己和保时捷报名了两场本地比赛。

D18.en: At 19, he took the bus to his first movie location in Griffith Park.
D18.zh: 19岁时，他坐公交车去了格里菲斯公园的第一个电影拍摄地。

D20X.en: At 19, he was expelled from Sigma Nu.
D20X.zh: 19岁时，他被西格玛努兄弟会开除。

D13b.en: At 23, his agent held off an offer because the script was weak and his part ill-defined.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，他的经纪人推迟了一个邀约，因为剧本薄弱且他的角色不明确。

D8.en: He was the only regular customer who dared to ask about her missing limb.
D8.zh: 他是唯一敢问她缺失肢体的常客。

M5.en: He lost his temper and knocked two sporting heroes to the floor.
M5.zh: 他发脾气，把两位体育偶像打倒在地。

M2.en: He planned to switch to law studies as a concession to his father.
M2.zh: 他计划转学法律作为对父亲的让步。

D19.en: He saved trivial pay stubs and financial records to document his activities.
D19.zh: 他保存琐碎的工资单和财务记录来记录自己的活动。

D17.en: He courted her, and for weeks that winter many believed she shared his bed.
D17.zh: 他追求她，那个冬天有好几周大家都认为她和他同床。

M8.en: He joined the broader search for truth and understanding of human behavior.
M8.zh: 他参与了更广泛的对真理和人类行为的探索。

M3.en: At 18, he began emulating James DeWeerd to climb socially and intellectually.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他开始模仿詹姆斯·德维尔德以提升社交和智力地位。

M4.en: He read about liberalism and egalitarianism on pages 14-15.
M4.zh: 他读了第14到15页关于自由主义和平均主义的内容。

D12.en: At 9, he attended a state-run facility costing one pound a year.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他上了一所每年只花一英镑的公立学校。

D13a.en: At 9, his strict mother disappeared from his life.
D13a.zh: 9岁时，他严厉的母亲从他的生活中消失了。

D6.en: At 18, he began to frequent the National Vaudeville Artists Club.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他开始常去国家杂耍艺人俱乐部。

D4.en: As a teenager, he performed in Pender's team of comic acrobats.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他在彭德的滑稽杂技团表演。

D19.en: At 10-14, he amassed a collection of items he kept as proud relics.
D19.zh: 10到14岁时，他收集了一批物品，并作为骄傲的纪念品保留了一生。

D2.en: At 25, he worked hard to make a success of the role.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他努力演好了这个角色。

D3.en: At 8, he encountered an electrician helping in the school lab.
D3.zh: 8岁时，他遇到了一位在学校实验室帮忙的电工。

D14.en: At 16, he made his Stateside debut as a stilt-walker in Good Times.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在《好时光》中踩高跷首次在美国登台。

M3.en: As a teenager and young adult, he studied others' dress, manners, gestures, and accents.
M3.zh: 十几岁到二十出头时，他研究别人的穿着、举止、手势和口音。

D9.en: He performed in Pender's Knockabout Comedians as a teenager.
D9.zh: 他十几岁时在彭德的滑稽杂技团表演。

D15.en: He performed with Pender's Knockabout Comedians as a teenager.
D15.zh: 他十几岁时在潘德的滑稽杂技团表演。

D1.en: Bad reviews motivated him to eradicate flaws in his stage technique and personal style.
D1.zh: 差评激励他消除舞台技巧和个人风格的缺陷。

D5.en: He was keen to stay in New York with some members at age 18.
D5.zh: 他18岁时和几个成员很想留在纽约。

D7.en: He was suddenly expelled from Fairfield at age 14.
D7.zh: 他14岁时突然被费尔菲尔德学校开除。

D18.en: He hawked hand-painted neckties on Sixth Avenue at age 18-19.
D18.zh: 他18到19岁时在第六大道叫卖手绘领带。

D20.en: He was part of Pender's Knockabout Comedians as a teenager.
D20.zh: 他十几岁时是彭德滑稽杂技团的成员。

M1.en: At 23, he was spotted by Ziegfeld and chosen to star in the national tour.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他被齐格飞看中，选为全国巡演的主角。

M7.en: At age 10, Archie refused tip money from soldiers out of patriotism.
M7.zh: 10岁时，阿奇出于爱国拒绝收士兵小费。

D2.en: He started with good grades to prove he could succeed.
D2.zh: 他一开始成绩不错，证明自己只要想做就能做到。

D15.en: At 14, he handled boats with skill that delighted his father.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他驾船技术娴熟，让父亲很高兴。

D9.en: At 14, he became a leader of the Seneca Point Gang.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他成了塞内卡角帮的头儿。

D14.en: He made his Broadway debut on January 2, 1922 in Drifting.
D14.zh: 1922年1月2日，他在《漂流》中首次登上百老汇舞台。

D16.en: At 21-22, he performed in a comedy that lasted a week; a critic called his performance 'inadequate'.
D16.zh: 21到22岁时，他演了一部只演了一周的喜剧，一位评论家称他的表演'不够格'。

D7.en: He publicly announced his intention to marry Helen in the New York Times.
D7.zh: 他在《纽约时报》上公开宣布要娶海伦。

D4.en: At 13-14, he built a makeshift clubhouse in the woods where friends spent the night.
D4.zh: 13到14岁时，他在树林里搭了个临时俱乐部，朋友们在那里过夜。

D13a.en: At 18, he refused to work in a shipyard and chose the sea instead.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝去船厂工作，选择了出海。

D18.en: At 19, he worked as a bond salesman for his father's Wall Street firm.
D18.zh: 19岁时，他在父亲所在的华尔街公司做债券销售员。

D19.en: As a child, he learned chess from his father, which became his lifelong passion.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他跟父亲学会了国际象棋，这成了他一生的爱好。

M1.en: From 22 to 35, he hoped each play would succeed but was often disappointed.
M1.zh: 22到35岁间，他期望每部戏都能成功，但常常失望。

D11.en: At 20, he tried writing and came up with a plot full of blood and death.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他尝试写作，构思了一个充满血腥和死亡的情节。

D10.en: At 20, he was terrified when another actor spun on him in a rage scene.
D10.zh: 20岁时，一个演员在愤怒场景中转身把他吓坏了。

D8.en: At 24, he accused her of stealing his scene after the show.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他在演出后指责她抢了他的戏。

D2.en: At 24, she was back in trim and looking for work when her child was three months old.
D2.zh: 24岁时，她在孩子三个月大时恢复身材并开始找工作。

D3.en: At 19-20, she pestered her uncle for introductions to film friends.
D3.zh: 19到20岁时，她缠着叔叔介绍电影界的朋友。

D13a.en: At 21, she eagerly took on the role of housewife for her husband.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她热切地扮演家庭主妇角色，为丈夫做饭和打理家务。

D12.en: As a small child, her father died around 1907.
D12.zh: 幼年时，她的父亲大约在1907年去世。

D14.en: At 12, she played violin for her first audience.
D14.zh: 12岁时，她拉小提琴给第一批观众听。

D7.en: At 24, she pleaded for tragic or virginal roles but was kept on by Barnowsky.
D7.zh: 24岁时，她恳求演悲剧或处女角色，但巴诺夫斯基留住了她。

D6.en: At 21-22, she stayed in a production through April 1923.
D6.zh: 21到22岁时，她在那部剧里一直待到1923年4月底。

D17.en: At 13, she wore a black armband and only black or grey dresses.
D17.zh: 13岁时，她戴上黑袖章，只穿黑色或灰色裙子。

D8.en: At 16, she took the front seat in the first row.
D8.zh: 16岁时，她坐到了第一排的前座。

D4.en: She pestered Uncle Willi for years for introductions to his film friends.
D4.zh: 她多年来一直缠着威利叔叔，要他介绍电影界的朋友给她认识。

D1.en: She was determined to find someone who agreed she was born to be a film star.
D1.zh: 她决心找到那个认为她天生就是电影明星的人。

D5.en: She crashed wild after-parties given by wealthy playgoers at popular restaurants.
D5.zh: 她闯入富有的戏迷在热门餐厅举办的疯狂庆功派对。

D15.en: She had been smoking heavily for five years.
D15.zh: 她已经大量吸烟五年了。

D16.en: She rehearsed daily at the Grosses Schauspielhaus when an actress withdrew from a revue.
D16.zh: 她每天在大剧院排练时，一名女演员退出了演出。

D11.en: She named herself Marlene as a child.
D11.zh: 她小时候给自己取名玛琳。

D13b.en: She was confirmed at fifteen, the usual age for a Lutheran girl.
D13b.zh: 她十五岁时接受了坚信礼，这是路德教女孩的常规年龄。

M3.en: At 21, she held hands with Henny Porten, hoping star quality would rub off.
M3.zh: 21岁时，她握着亨尼·波滕的手，希望明星气质能传染给自己。

D10.en: She was confirmed at fifteen, which seemed shockingly tardy to the Catholic cook.
D10.zh: 她十五岁受坚信礼，在天主教厨师看来晚得惊人。

D19.en: As a girl during WWI, she sang to soldiers at train stations and hospitals.
D19.zh: 她十几岁时在火车站和医院为士兵唱歌。

M1.en: At 24, she felt ambition stirred by news.
M1.zh: 24岁时，她因一则新闻感到雄心被激发。

M4.en: As a teen, she sang to soldiers at train stations and hospitals during WWI.
M4.zh: 十几岁时，她在火车站和医院为士兵唱歌。

M7.en: As a girl, she conducted potato and charcoal drives and sang to soldiers at stations and hospitals.
M7.zh: 她十几岁时参与土豆和煤炭募捐，在火车站和医院为士兵唱歌。

M6.en: She was confirmed at fifteen, as was typical for Lutheran girls.
M6.zh: 她15岁时接受了坚信礼，这是路德教女孩的惯例。

M8.en: She was confirmed at fifteen, as was typical for Lutheran girls.
M8.zh: 她15岁时接受了坚信礼，这是路德教女孩的惯例。

D6.en: He attended the theater but couldn't fully understand German.
D6.zh: 他去看戏，但德语让他无法完全理解。

D11.en: He signed a magazine pointer as 'Hitch' in June 1919.
D11.zh: 1919年6月，他以'希区'署名发表了一篇杂志短文。

D13a.en: He briefly served as an altar boy at age 6-7.
D13a.zh: 他6到7岁时短暂当过祭坛侍童。

D2.en: His parents failed to enroll him in school at age 7.
D2.zh: 他7岁时父母没让他按时入学。

D3.en: He frequented art galleries and museums, entranced by French moderns.
D3.zh: 他常去美术馆和博物馆，对法国现代派着迷。

D14.en: He first saw Tallulah Bankhead in her 1923 London debut.
D14.zh: 1923年他首次看到塔卢拉·班克黑德在伦敦首演。

D4.en: He sketched title cards as training for art direction.
D4.zh: 他画标题卡作为美术指导的训练。

D1.en: At 25, so much went wrong that the experience prepared him for all future disasters; he never tired of telling the anecdotes.
D1.zh: 25岁时，出了太多差错，那次经历让他为未来所有灾难做好准备；他从不厌倦讲述那些轶事。

D9.en: At 23, he directed a film on assignment from the production chief.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他受制片主管指派导演了一部电影。

M1.en: At 21-22, he got a copy of The Sorrows of Satan and read it all.
M1.zh: 21到22岁间，他拿到一本《撒旦的悲哀》并从头读到尾。

M4.en: At 25, he was adamant: this was the stage he needed and he was determined to use it.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他坚持认为这就是他需要的舞台，并决心使用它。

D20.en: As a young man, he emerged as a leader who attracted collaborators and united people.
D20.zh: 年轻时，他展现出领导力，吸引合作者并团结众人。

D7.en: At 25, he was disliked by Graham Cutts, who complained to Balcon.
D7.zh: 25岁时，格雷厄姆·卡茨不喜欢他，并向巴尔孔告状。

D10.en: At 25, amid frenzy, he was a calm center of confidence.
D10.zh: 25岁时，在疯狂中他保持平静自信。

D8.en: At 22, he would build a set and tell the director where to shoot.
D8.zh: 22岁时，他会搭好布景并告诉导演从哪拍。

D16.en: At 7, he astonished his family by reciting train schedules from memory.
D16.zh: 7岁时，他凭记忆背诵火车时刻表，让家人惊讶。

D19.en: At 8, he made a huge wall chart of daily positions of British ships.
D19.zh: 8岁时，他制作了一张大墙图，标注每天英国船只的位置。

D13b.en: He started at Saint Ignatius on a Wednesday.
D13b.zh: 他在一个星期三开始了在圣依纳爵的学习。

D3.en: He taught himself filmmaking by reading theory books and filling them with notes.
D3.zh: 他通过阅读电影理论书籍并做满笔记自学电影制作。

D2.en: He played a record continuously until his sister broke it in anger.
D2.zh: 他反复播放一张唱片，直到他妹妹生气地把它弄坏。

D5.en: He boldly resigned from Look to become a full-time independent filmmaker.
D5.zh: 他大胆地从《Look》杂志辞职，成为全职独立电影人。

D6.en: He completed a film extension of a magazine feature at his friend's suggestion.
D6.zh: 他在朋友建议下完成了一部杂志特辑的电影扩展版。

D9.en: He took each friend aside and gave them directions privately.
D9.zh: 他把每个朋友拉到一边，私下给他们指示。

D14.en: He played the drums in a swing band in high school.
D14.zh: 他在高中时在一个摇摆乐队打鼓。

D11.en: He wrote, scripted, and drew storyboards for a short love story.
D11.zh: 他为一个短篇爱情故事写作、编剧并绘制分镜。

M1.en: He wanted to direct, photograph, and edit his own films.
M1.zh: 他想导演、摄影并剪辑自己的电影。

D20.en: He shared a love of film with his friend Alexander Singer.
D20.zh: 他和朋友亚历山大·辛格分享了对电影的热爱。

D4.en: At 23, he stopped the dishwashing machines because the noise interrupted his run-throughs.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他关掉了洗碗机，因为噪音打断了他的排练。

D2.en: As a child, he was made to go to church on Sunday to hear his father preach.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他被迫每周日去教堂听父亲布道。

D1.en: He describes panicking when a violin string loosens during his playing, ruining his hopes.
D1.zh: 他描述自己拉小提琴时琴弦松了会惊慌，毁掉所有希望。

D3.en: As a teenager, he returned to the gallery week after week, following each production with score in hand.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他每周都回到楼座，手里拿着乐谱看每一场演出。

D11.en: At 24, he used a puppet theater conceit in The Death of Punch to portray the struggle against despair.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他在《潘趣之死》中用木偶剧的构思来描绘对抗绝望的斗争。

D10.en: Before 23, he had a violent temper and unbalanced temperament, sometimes hard and unfeeling, other times soft and helpless.
D10.zh: 23岁前，他脾气暴躁、性情不稳，有时冷酷无情，有时又柔软无助如小孩。

D9.en: At 19, he started his job as the leader of the dramatic section of a youth club.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他开始担任一个青年俱乐部戏剧部的负责人。

M6.en: At 17, his decision dated from seeing Olof Molander's production of A Dream Play in 1935.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他的决定源于1935年看了奥洛夫·莫兰德导演的《一出梦的戏剧》。

D5.en: At 16, he went to Germany for the first time on an exchange visit in the summer of 1934.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他于1934年夏天首次去德国进行交流访问。

D13a.en: At 24, he decided to forsake the Royal Opera for film.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他决定放弃皇家歌剧院投身电影。

D12.en: He was born with a caul, a Sunday child, a sign of future success.
D12.zh: 他出生时带着胎膜，是周日之子，预示未来成功。

D14.en: He bid to perform as a soloist during a national radio concert.
D14.zh: 他争取在国家广播音乐会上独奏。

D15.en: At 17-18, he says, 'I've grown so tall I can't keep my balance any more.'
D15.zh: 17到18岁时，他说：“我长太高了，平衡不了。”

D18.en: At 10, he swapped half his lead soldiers for a precious contraption.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他用一半铅兵换了个宝贝玩意儿。

M1.en: He bid to perform as a soloist during a national radio concert.
M1.zh: 他争取在全国广播音乐会上独奏。

M4.en: At 16, he was heavily indoctrinated about the might of the Nazi cause.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他被大肆灌输纳粹事业的强大与正义。

D7.en: He hated first nights and hid in the projectionist's booth.
D7.zh: 他讨厌首演之夜，躲在放映室里。

M3.en: He was convinced by Olof Molander's production that the dream-play could be staged.
M3.zh: 他被奥洛夫·莫兰德的制作说服，认为这部梦幻剧可以上演。

D13b.en: He was trapped into marriage by his girlfriend's pregnancy.
D13b.zh: 他因女友怀孕而被迫结婚。

D16.en: He addressed the public directly via a page-long column in the program.
D16.zh: 他通过在节目单中写一页专栏直接向公众讲话。

D6.en: He enjoys observing the diverse human types in moviedom.
D6.zh: 他喜欢观察电影界形形色色的人。

D10.en: He tortures himself, repeating that he should go back to Milan.
D10.zh: 他折磨自己，反复说应该回米兰。

D11.en: He and Maccari broadcast a radio show from under a napkin.
D11.zh: 他和马卡里在餐巾下广播了一档节目。

D13b.en: He joins a fascist youth group and wears the required uniform.
D13b.zh: 他加入法西斯青年团，穿上了规定的制服。

D5.en: He is swept up in a mass roundup and forced onto a German truck.
D5.zh: 他在一次大规模搜捕中被抓，并被推上德国卡车。

D18.en: He set up the Funny-Face Shop, drawing caricatures of Allied soldiers for money.
D18.zh: 他开了个搞笑脸铺子，靠给盟军士兵画漫画赚钱。

D13a.en: He could go to Rome, but must give his word that he would enroll in law school and live with his aunt.
D13a.zh: 他可以去罗马，但必须保证会注册法学院并和姨妈同住。

D2.en: He published his first article in Marc'Aurelio, entitled 'È permesso?'.
D2.zh: 他在《马尔克·奥雷利奥》上发表了第一篇文章，题为《可以进来吗？》。

D1.en: He returned to Rimini and finished high school there.
D1.zh: 他回到里米尼，在那里读完了高中。

D20.en: The credits list him as co-screenwriter with Amidei and Rossellini.
D20.zh: 片头字幕将他列为与阿米迪和罗西里尼合作的联合编剧。

D12.en: At 16, he couldn't pay his bill and ran out; she told him to eat regardless.
D12.zh: 16岁时他付不起账单跑掉，她让他照吃。

D16.en: His radio script 'Il cerino' was broadcast during intermission on Caleidoscopio.
D16.zh: 他的广播剧本《火柴》在《万花筒》节目的幕间休息时播出。

D15.en: At 16-18, he stole a chicken with friends.
D15.zh: 16到18岁时，他和朋友偷了一只鸡。

D17.en: At 16, he was told by a woman to eat there whether he had money or not.
D17.zh: 16岁时，他被一个女人告知不管有没有钱都得在那里吃饭。

D20X.en: He returned to Rimini and finished high school there.
D20X.zh: 他回到里米尼并在那里完成了高中学业。

M2.en: At 17-18, his father insisted he become a lawyer, while his mother wanted him to be a doctor.
M2.zh: 17到18岁时，他父亲坚持要他当律师，而他母亲想让他当医生。

M4.en: He acted on his resolve to become a journalist and went to Florence, where he was hired by 420.
M4.zh: 他下定决心成为记者，去了佛罗伦萨，并被420雇佣。

M6.en: At 17, he acted on his resolve to become a journalist and went to Florence.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他下定决心要当记者，去了佛罗伦萨。

D8.en: He threatens to dig a hole in the ground for the camera, making the angle even lower.
D8.zh: 他威胁要在地上挖个洞放摄像机，让角度更低。

D11.en: He showed his first feature-length movie, Firelight, at the Phoenix Little Theater.
D11.zh: 他在凤凰城小剧院放映了他的第一部长片《火光》。

D15.en: In a school footrace, he came second last, just ahead of a slower handicapped boy.
D15.zh: 在学校赛跑中，他跑了倒数第二，只比一个更慢的残疾男孩快一点。

D14.en: At 21, he was announced as the youngest filmmaker ever signed by a major studio.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他被宣布为有史以来与大型制片厂签约的最年轻电影人。

D4.en: He worked his way through three years of college by projecting classroom films.
D4.zh: 他通过放映课堂电影来支付三年大学的费用。

D6.en: At a retrospective of USC graduate films, he got to know the more social film students.
D6.zh: 在南加州大学毕业生电影回顾展上，他认识了更善于社交的电影学生。

D2.en: He spent summers with an uncle near Los Angeles to work at Universal Studios.
D2.zh: 他夏天和住在洛杉矶附近的叔叔一起，以便在环球影城工作。

D9.en: He spent summers with an uncle near Los Angeles to work at Universal Studios.
D9.zh: 他夏天和住在洛杉矶附近的叔叔一起，以便在环球影城工作。

D13b.en: He graduated from Saratoga High School.
D13b.zh: 他从萨拉托加高中毕业。

D10.en: Panicked, he roused others and fled after snapping some flash pictures.
D10.zh: 他惊慌失措，叫醒其他人，用闪光灯拍了几张照片后逃跑了。

D3.en: As a child, he found movies emotionally overwhelming, unlike television, especially Disney cartoons.
D3.zh: 他小时候觉得电影比电视更令人震撼，尤其是迪士尼动画。

D18.en: He worked in the cafeteria to earn pocket money.
D18.zh: 他在食堂打工赚零花钱。

D19.en: He broke down the film and hung shots on his cutting rack, taping descriptions.
D19.zh: 他把胶片拆开，把镜头挂在自己做的剪辑架上，贴上描述。

D13a.en: At 13, he was bar mitzvahed in an Orthodox synagogue.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他在正统犹太教堂举行了成人礼。

D5.en: He stuck tape to the tip of his nose and his forehead, praying it would tilt.
D5.zh: 他把胶带贴在鼻尖和额头上，祈祷鼻子能变翘。

D1.en: He was always the last picked for games; nobody wanted jerky little Steven.
D1.zh: 他玩游戏总是最后一个被选，没人想要别扭的小史蒂文。

D12.en: At 18-20, he worked in the college cafeteria to earn money for a 16mm camera.
D12.zh: 18到20岁间，他在大学食堂打工赚钱买16毫米摄影机。

M4.en: Being treated differently because of his religion scared and angered him.
M4.zh: 因宗教被区别对待让他害怕又愤怒。

D16.en: At 25, he said auteur theory believers will have coronaries early.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他说相信作者论的人会早发心脏病。

D17.en: As a small child, he lived with three screaming sisters and a mother playing concert piano.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他和三个哭闹的妹妹以及弹奏音乐会的母亲住在一起。

D20X.en: At 23, he told Sheinberg he quit TV because it was too tough.
D20X.zh: 23岁时，他对辛伯格说他不做电视了，因为太难了。

M2.en: As a child, he read comics, saw many movies, and watched too much TV.
M2.zh: 小时候，他看漫画、看很多电影、看太多电视。

M3.en: As a child, he read comics, saw many movies, and watched too much TV.
M3.zh: 小时候，他看漫画、看很多电影、看太多电视。

M5.en: At 23, he told Sheinberg he quit TV because it was too tough.
M5.zh: 23岁时，他对辛伯格说他不做电视了，因为太难了。

M7.en: At 23, he quit TV, telling Sid Sheinberg it was too tough.
M7.zh: 23岁时，他辞去电视工作，告诉希德·辛伯格这太难了。

M8.en: As a child in the 1950s, he read comics, saw many movies, and watched too much TV.
M8.zh: 1950年代小时候，他看漫画、看很多电影、看太多电视。

D13b.en: As an undergraduate at age 23, he married fellow student Laraine Marie Brennan.
D13b.zh: 他23岁读本科时与同学Laraine Marie Brennan结婚。

D4.en: He made his first real film, What's a Nice Girl Like You Doing in a Place Like This?, in 1963 at age 21.
D4.zh: 他21岁时拍了第一部真正的电影《你这样的好女孩在这里做什么？》。

D9.en: At age 21 in 1963, he made his first film, What's a Nice Girl Like You Doing in a Place Like This?.
D9.zh: 21岁时在1963年拍摄了首部电影《她在这么个地方干什么？》。

D15.en: At 19, he became a professional prizefighter known as The Bronx Bull.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他成为职业拳击手，绰号‘布朗克斯公牛’。

D13a.en: At 22, he announced his intentions to his parents.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他向父母宣布了自己的打算。

D1.en: In 1965, he made a 65-minute feature, but the screening was disastrous.
D1.zh: 1965年，他制作了一部65分钟的长片，但放映效果很糟糕。

D20.en: In 1966, he began writing with Mardik Martin.
D20.zh: 1966年，他和马迪克·马丁开始一起写作。

D8.en: At 20-25, he said if he wasn't first, no one would be first.
D8.zh: 20到25岁时，他说如果他不是第一，没人会是第一。

D11.en: At 17, he co-wrote a play, Marching Song, about John Brown and freeing slaves.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他与人合写了一部关于约翰·布朗和解放奴隶的戏剧《进行曲》。

D14.en: At age 11-12, he directed and performed in Richard III, Julius Caesar, and Androcles and the Lion.
D14.zh: 11到12岁时，他导演并演出了《理查三世》《尤利乌斯·恺撒》和《安德罗克勒斯与狮子》。

D2.en: At age 21, he would drive himself to exhaustion to avoid disappointing others.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他会把自己逼到筋疲力尽以免让人失望。

D7.en: At 16, Bergman said he saw no point in college, as he could teach professors.
D7.zh: 16岁时，伯格曼认为上大学是浪费时间，他能教教授。

D18.en: At 19, Orson wired Skipper for cash frequently.
D18.zh: 19岁时，奥森频繁发电报向斯基珀要钱。

D5.en: At 16, he was hired for a pivotal role at the Gate theatre.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他被Gate剧院聘为关键角色。

D4.en: At 11-15, Orson supervised building a larger marionette theater.
D4.zh: 11到15岁间，奥森监督建造了一个更大的木偶剧院。

D3.en: He saw magic as a metaphor of both power and delusion.
D3.zh: 他把魔术视为权力和幻觉的隐喻。

D16.en: He concocted subtly different voices for different stages of the character's long life.
D16.zh: 他为角色漫长人生的不同阶段设计了细微不同的声音。

D13a.en: He continued to pose as a would-be painter.
D13a.zh: 他继续假装自己是个想当画家的人。

M1.en: He scored a hit as Mercutio in Katharine Cornell's production of Romeo and Juliet.
M1.zh: 他在凯瑟琳·康奈尔制作的《罗密欧与朱丽叶》中饰演墨丘西奥，大获成功。

D1.en: He was very upset to have lost; Dadda Bernstein wrote him a note of consolation.
D1.zh: 他对失败感到非常沮丧；达达·伯恩斯坦给他写了一封安慰信。

M4.en: Scenting something new and dangerous, he was immediately recharged.
M4.zh: 嗅到新鲜而危险的气息，他立刻重新振作起来。

D8.en: At 19, he found that few people could deny him anything when he was in this vein.
D8.zh: 19岁时，他发现他这样的时候很少有人能拒绝他。

D15.en: At 19, he plunges into the duel scene with a fine fury of swordmanship.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他带着精湛的剑术狂怒地投入决斗场景。

D17.en: At 8, he worried constantly about her growing weaker.
D17.zh: 8岁时，他不断担心她越来越虚弱。

M6.en: At 16, he was fanatically devoted to the idea of the theatre.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他狂热地献身于戏剧理念。

D19.en: At 22, his version of the text was heavily cut and rearranged; he continued cutting and rearranging during rehearsals.
D19.zh: 22岁时，他的文本版本被大量删减和重新编排；排练期间他继续删改，直到首演之夜才停止。

D9.en: At 25, he suggested solutions to a director, which were adopted.
D9.zh: 25岁时他给导演提建议并被采纳。

D13a.en: At 18, he earned his elementary school teaching diploma but found the career unappealing.
D13a.zh: 18岁时他获得小学教师资格，但觉得这职业没吸引力。

D18.en: At 18, he took up a job in insurance due to his eloquence and empathy.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他因口才和同理心从事保险工作。

D2.en: At 25, he spent six months studying before returning to the stage after his debut in Reggio.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他学习六个月后重返舞台，此前在雷焦首次登台。

D3.en: At 19, he requested an audition to build a musical foundation.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他请求试音以打下音乐基础。

D1.en: He looked forward to winning the Peri gold medal next time.
D1.zh: 他期待下次赢得佩里金奖章。

D8.en: He gave the start signal for the quartet with an imperious gesture.
D8.zh: 他用专横的手势给四重奏发出了开始信号。

D12.en: He had no money for singing lessons because his family was poor.
D12.zh: 他家里穷，没钱上唱歌课。

D13b.en: He and his father went to talk with the best teacher in Modena, who accepted him for free.
D13b.zh: 他和父亲去找摩德纳最好的老师，老师免费收了他。

D14.en: He wore a hairy gorilla head mask in public.
D14.zh: 他戴着毛茸茸的大猩猩头套出门。

D10.en: He was shattered by a high-profile failure.
D10.zh: 他被一次高调失败击垮了。

D7.en: He refused to do interviews, but label executives forced him.
D7.zh: 他拒绝接受采访，但唱片公司高管强迫了他。

D11.en: As a teenager, he drew pictures of animals and monsters during lessons.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他在上课时画动物和怪兽的图画。

D5.en: At 25, he arranged for his plastic surgeon to give him a third nose job.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他安排他的整形医生给他做了第三次鼻整形手术。

D8.en: At 20, he won an argument and said, "Guys, let's just try it. What's your problem?"
D8.zh: 20岁时，他赢了争论并说：“伙计们，我们就试试吧。你们有什么问题？”

M3.en: At 18-19, he endlessly practiced after studying Fred Astaire.
M3.zh: 18到19岁时，他不断研究弗雷德·阿斯泰尔并反复练习。

M5.en: At 21, he felt he deserved to be on the cover of Rolling Stone and asked his publicist to arrange it.
M5.zh: 21岁时，他觉得自己应该登上《滚石》封面，并让公关去安排。

M6.en: He went door-to-door even at the peak of his fame.
M6.zh: 他在名气巅峰时仍挨家挨户上门。

M7.en: His family group competed against more experienced student groups.
M7.zh: 他家的组合与更有经验的学生团体竞争。

D18.en: At 17, Conrad earned a high school scholarship.
D18.zh: 17岁时，康拉德获得了高中奖学金。

D13b.en: He married Katherine by a justice of the peace on 5 November 1949.
D13b.zh: 他于1949年11月5日与凯瑟琳在治安法官主持下结婚。

D1.en: At 24, he strongly believed in the song and had disagreements with Quincy about it.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他非常相信这首歌，并与昆西对此有强烈分歧。

D20X.en: At 25, he said this would be his last tour with the guys, and he was serious.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他说这将是他和兄弟们最后一次巡演，他是认真的。

D12.en: At 10, she led a blind man through the streets for 50 cents a week.
D12.zh: 10岁时，她领着一个盲人走街串巷，每周赚50美分。

D11.en: At 11, she imagined herself as a glamorous woman named Sassafrassa.
D11.zh: 11岁时，她想象自己是个叫萨萨弗拉莎的迷人女人。

D13a.en: At 17-18, she quit her job, returned home, and enrolled as a bleached blonde at Jamestown High.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁间，她辞掉工作，坐伊利铁路回家，染成金发去詹姆斯敦高中上十年级。

D5.en: At 21, she was the 13th girl chosen as a backup when only 12 were needed.
D5.zh: 21岁时，她是第13个被选中的替补，而公司只需要12个。

D2.en: At 9, she was sent to study singing, piano, and dancing at the Chautauqua Institute.
D2.zh: 9岁时，她被送到肖托夸学院学习唱歌、钢琴和舞蹈。

D9.en: At 25, she was the standout in 'Hey Diddle Diddle', as in her earlier hit 'Within the Law'.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她在《嘿，迪德尔迪德尔》中表现出色，就像之前在《法律之内》一样。

D10.en: As a small child, she was extremely tense, nervous, sensitive, and filled with anxiety.
D10.zh: 幼年时，她极度紧张、神经质、敏感，充满焦虑和恐惧。

D14.en: At 12, she auditioned for and got a role in a local Masonic Club musical revue.
D14.zh: 12岁时，她试镜并获得了当地共济会俱乐部音乐剧的一个角色。

D3.en: At 9, she began reading magazines about theater personalities.
D3.zh: 9岁时，她开始看关于戏剧人物的杂志。

D18.en: At 18, she returned to New York and got a job from Hattie Carnegie to fund auditions.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她回到纽约，哈蒂·卡内基给了她一份工作，资助她继续试镜。

D17.en: At 12, she ran with two pails of water to help a tramp after a train derailment.
D17.zh: 12岁时，火车脱轨后，她提着两桶水跑去帮助一个流浪汉。

D16.en: At 18, she bluntly told another actress she could use her help, which was appreciated.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她直率地告诉另一位女演员她可以帮忙，对方很感激。

M1.en: At 18, she moved an audience to laughter and appreciation, like her hero Julius Tannen.
M1.zh: 18岁时，她让观众发笑并赞赏，就像她的偶像朱利叶斯·坦南一样。

M6.en: At 18, she again believed there was a place for her in show business.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她再次相信演艺界有她的位置。

M2.en: She registered to vote in the primary election in Los Angeles.
M2.zh: 她在洛杉矶登记参加初选投票。

D14.en: At 19, he occasionally performed at venues like the Purple Onion and the Bastille.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他偶尔在紫洋葱俱乐部和巴斯蒂尔等场所表演。

D4.en: He backed Williams on harmonica, following his unpredictable guitar work.
D4.zh: 他用口琴为威廉姆斯伴奏，跟上了他难以预测的吉他演奏。

D11.en: He wrote his first song in New York, 'Song to Woody.'
D11.zh: 他在纽约写了第一首歌《给伍迪的歌》。

D18.en: He got a regular gig at the Purple Onion, earning five dollars a night.
D18.zh: 他在紫洋葱餐厅得到一份固定演出，每晚赚五美元。

D2.en: He could command his extraordinary focus in the studio at will.
D2.zh: 他几乎能随意掌控自己在录音室里的非凡专注力。

D5.en: He obtained a prescription for methamphetamine, Desbutal, and Nembutal in February 1966.
D5.zh: 1966年2月，他拿到一份处方，包括甲基苯丙胺、德斯布塔尔和戊巴比妥。

D13b.en: He enrolled at the University of Minnesota in the Twin Cities in fall 1959.
D13b.zh: 1959年秋天，他进入双子城的明尼苏达大学。

D9.en: He invited Bloomfield to Woodstock the weekend before the session.
D9.zh: 他在录音前的周末邀请布卢姆菲尔德去伍德斯托克。

D7.en: At 22, he gave a speech that seemed to be made up of the first thoughts that popped into his head.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他发表了一篇演讲，内容似乎是他脑子里冒出的第一个念头。

D16.en: At 22, he got into a friendly but heated discussion with Dylan about the role of politics in folk music.
D16.zh: 22岁时，他和迪伦进行了一场友好但激烈的讨论，关于政治在民谣音乐中的作用。

M3.en: At 19-20, he met Woody Guthrie, sang to him, and felt intense hero-worship.
M3.zh: 19到20岁时，他见到了伍迪·格思里，为他唱歌，并对他充满崇拜。

D8.en: At 19, he mimicked admired artists and appropriated melodies and lyrics.
D8.zh: 19岁时，他模仿崇拜的艺术家，挪用旋律和歌词。

M1.en: He staged his first important solo concert at the Town Hall in New York on April 12, 1963.
M1.zh: 1963年4月12日，他在纽约市政厅举办了首次重要的个人演唱会。

D19.en: He learned everything he could about music from older, more experienced artists.
D19.zh: 他向更年长、更有经验的艺术家学习一切关于音乐的知识。

M2.en: At age 20, he proved he had fulfilled a deal with his father.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他证明自己履行了与父亲的约定。

M4.en: He wrote the first of what would be a steady flow of protest songs for two years.
M4.zh: 他写了第一首抗议歌曲，此后两年内持续创作此类歌曲。

M5.en: At 17, something still bothered him 25 years later.
M5.zh: 17岁时的事，25年后仍让他耿耿于怀。

M6.en: At 19, he said he was following in Woody Guthrie's footsteps.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他说他在追随伍迪·格思里的脚步。

M7.en: At 16-17, he went with the daughters of miners, farmers, and workers in Hibbing.
M7.zh: 16到17岁时，他和希宾的矿工、农民和工人的女儿们一起出去。

M8.en: He was following in Woody Guthrie's footsteps.
M8.zh: 他追随伍迪·格思里的脚步。

D15.en: He fought one of the most memorable Golden Gloves matches in Memphis history in 1952.
D15.zh: 1952年，他参加了孟菲斯历史上最难忘的金手套比赛之一。

D9.en: He needed someone from home besides Lamar, so he called Alan Fortas first.
D9.zh: 他需要除了拉马尔之外的家乡人，他第一个打电话给艾伦·福塔斯。

D2.en: In one night, he and his team got five nearly perfect sides.
D2.zh: 一夜之间，他和团队得到了五个近乎完美的面。

D14.en: He appeared on the Grand Ole Opry on October 2, 1954, and neither customers nor fellow performers wanted him to quit.
D14.zh: 1954年10月2日，他出现在大奥普里，顾客和同行都不希望他离开。

D8.en: He got into a fight with another usher he thought was a stool pigeon and was fired by the manager.
D8.zh: 他和另一个他认为告密的引座员打架，被经理开除了。

D5.en: He broke the strings on his guitar and smashed it on the floor.
D5.zh: 他弄断了吉他弦，然后把吉他摔在地上。

D10.en: Elvis came out mad, and after the Colonel left, he cussed and fumed.
D10.zh: 埃尔维斯出来时很生气，上校走后，他咒骂发怒。

D3.en: At age 19, he attended monthly All-Night Singings sponsored by the Blackwood Brothers.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他参加了黑木兄弟赞助的每月通宵歌唱会。

M4.en: At 21, he said he read his Bible and it was no made-up story.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他说他读圣经，这不是编造的故事。

D18.en: As a small child, he always had to climb the fence.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他总得爬篱笆。
