D11.en: At 21, he hung around Elvis and the Louvins, singing hymns and playing piano.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他和埃尔维斯及卢文一家一起唱圣歌弹钢琴。

M1.en: At 21, he wanted to be a movie star, a serious actor like Brando or Dean.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他想当电影明星，像白兰度或迪恩那样的严肃演员。

M3.en: At 21, he wanted to go to Hollywood and become the next James Dean.
M3.zh: 21岁时，他想去好莱坞成为下一个詹姆斯·迪恩。

D13b.en: At 22, he was happy to serve and would go whenever called.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他乐于服役，随叫随到。

D16.en: He sang with other troupe members between shows.
D16.zh: 他在演出间隙与其他剧团成员一起唱歌。

M2.en: At 20, he wanted to be big to do something for his folks.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他想出人头地，为家人做点事。

M7.en: At 20, he wanted to do big nice things especially for his mother.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他特别想为母亲做大事好事。

D3.en: At 7, he begged a senior artist for guidance, showing a secretly copied manuscript.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他苦求前辈指教，拿出暗中抄得的论画手稿。

D4.en: At 11, he secretly took his father's seal collection and carved over ten copies in a month.
D4.zh: 11岁时，他偷取父亲藏品，花一个月临刻了十多方篆印。

D2.en: From age 20 to 30, he carried a treasured rubbing everywhere and copied it for ten years.
D2.zh: 20到30岁间，他把心爱的拓片随身携带，临写了十年。

D13a.en: At 21, he resolutely quit his clerk job and returned home from Yangzhou.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他毅然辞去录事职务，从扬州回到家乡。

D12.en: At 17, after his family's businesses failed, he obeyed his father and continued studying at the academy.
D12.zh: 17岁时，家道中落，他遵父命继续就读书院。

D13b.en: As a child, he obediently studied whatever calligraphy style his elders assigned.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，长辈让他学什么字帖，他就学什么。

D7.en: At 7, he and his brother detained a senior artist and begged for instruction, showing a secretly copied manuscript.
D7.zh: 7岁时，他和弟弟苦留一位前辈，拿出暗中抄得的《逸甫论画》求教。

D11.en: At 16, he coined the studio name 'Chamber of Longing for Beauty' and had it carved as a seal.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他起了室名'思美人室'，并刻了印章。

M1.en: At 13, he ranked among the top in the county child examination.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他参加童子试，名列前茅。

D15.en: At 15, he learned martial arts from a clansman and enjoyed sword fighting and horse riding.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他跟族人学拳术，喜欢击剑、骑马。

D6.en: He visited collector Jin Dejian in Hangzhou and they exchanged views on famous paintings and calligraphy.
D6.zh: 他途经杭州，拜访收藏家金德鉴，一起观赏所藏书画名迹并交换意见。

D9.en: At age 23, he heard that Guo Tisheng organized a Go club with over ten members.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他听说过惕生组织了一个弈社，有十余人参加。

D3.en: He made various musical instruments from bamboo, wood, snakeskin, and horsetail.
D3.zh: 他用竹筒、木板、蛇皮、马尾等材料制作了二胡、三弦、琵琶、箫、笛等乐器。

D11.en: He became interested in drawing and drew people, flowers, birds, and animals from life.
D11.zh: 他对画画产生兴趣，画了钓鱼老头、花卉、草木、飞禽走兽等。

D2.en: He studied books and practiced calligraphy daily when free from chores.
D2.zh: 他抽空温习书本，每天临摹字帖写一页半页。

D12.en: He stopped school after less than a year to help with household chores.
D12.zh: 他读了不到一年书就停学在家帮忙做事。

D13b.en: He married Chen Chunjun at his parents' order.
D13b.zh: 他奉父母之命与陈春君结婚。

D4.en: He made various musical instruments from bamboo, wood, snakeskin, and horsetail.
D4.zh: 他用竹筒、木板、蛇皮、马尾等材料制作了二胡、三弦、琵琶、箫、笛等乐器。

D9.en: He organized and led the band in folk entertainment.
D9.zh: 他成为民间娱乐中乐队的组织者和领导者。

D18.en: He went up the mountain to cut firewood and sell it to help the family.
D18.zh: 他上山打柴卖钱贴补家用。

D6.en: He lived in Fayuan Temple, sold paintings and seals, and met several artists.
D6.zh: 他借住法源寺，卖画刻印，结识了陈师曾、姚芒父等人。

D7.en: He refused to go see an official, and his mother praised his spirit.
D7.zh: 他拒绝去看官，母亲夸他有志气。

D5.en: At 25, his train passed through gunfire at Huangcun and Wanzhuang.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他坐的火车在黄村、万庄穿过交战区，从弹雨中冲过。

D5.en: At 24, he said something he'd rather open a vein than say.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他说了宁愿割腕也不愿说的话。

D13a.en: At age 17, he eloped against her family's outcry, probably at her urging.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他可能在她的催促下私奔，不顾她家人的反对。

D14.en: He performed with his father and uncle in the Will Mastin Trio from age 3.
D14.zh: 他从3岁起就和父亲、叔叔一起在威尔·马斯汀三重奏演出。

D6.en: In the 1940s, he kissed Billie Holiday the way she ought to be kissed.
D6.zh: 1940年代，他像应该的那样吻了比莉·哈乐黛。

D11.en: At 16, he decided he would prefer to be a sportswriter.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他决定自己更想当体育记者。

D18.en: At 17-18, he borrowed money from her to get a stack of arrangements.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他向她借钱，拿到了一叠改编曲。

D10.en: At 24, he forgot the lyrics and froze on stage.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他忘了歌词，在台上僵住了。

D3.en: At 19, he used the microphone as his instrument.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他把麦克风当作自己的乐器。

D9.en: At 23, he dreamed of singing with Benny Goodman, Count Basie, or Tommy Dorsey.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他梦想与班尼·古德曼、贝西伯爵或汤米·多尔西同台演唱。

M4.en: At 19, he wanted to be a singer.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他想当歌手。

M3.en: At 18-20, he walked around in a yachting cap imitating Bing Crosby.
M3.zh: 18到20岁时，他戴着游艇帽模仿宾·克罗斯比。

D15.en: At 18-20, he had to get his voice down into his chest.
D15.zh: 18到20岁时，他得把声音沉到胸腔里。

D1.en: At 22-23, he was badly injured in a car wreck, became addicted to painkillers, lost all his money, and his marriage fell apart.
D1.zh: 22到23岁间，他因车祸重伤，对止痛药上瘾，失去所有钱财，婚姻破裂。

D13b.en: At 23, he joined Dorsey and held third chair in the trombone section.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，他加入了多尔西乐队，担任长号声部的第三席位。

D19.en: He had a lifelong obsession with cleanliness, cleaning ashtrays and lining up bottles.
D19.zh: 他一生痴迷于整洁，会清理烟灰缸并把酒瓶排整齐。

M6.en: At 19, he said, 'We're going to get married. This is my destiny.'
M6.zh: 19岁时，他说：‘我们要结婚。这是我的命运。’

D14.en: At 15, she performed Cavalleria Rusticana at the Olympia Theater.
D14.zh: 15岁时，她在奥林匹亚剧院演出了《乡村骑士》。

D12.en: At 13, she left New York with her mother and sister and traveled to Greece.
D12.zh: 13岁时，她和母亲、姐姐离开纽约去了希腊。

D10.en: As a teenager, she whirled a cat by its tail and flung it over a fire escape.
D10.zh: 十几岁时，她抓着猫尾巴抡了一圈，把它扔到了防火梯外面。

D4.en: As a small child, she was sent home with scrapes and shock.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她被送回家，只有擦伤和惊吓。

D3.en: As a teenager, she read music by bringing it close to her face while holding her glasses.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她拿着眼镜把乐谱凑近脸看。

M1.en: At 16, she came second out of thirteen candidates with marks of 1-2 and 1.
M1.zh: 16岁时，她在13名考生中得了第二名，成绩是1-2和1。

D6.en: At 17-18, she sent him a postcard at the Albanian front.
D6.zh: 17到18岁时，她在阿尔巴尼亚前线给他寄了一张明信片。

D13b.en: At 21, she swore the oath of allegiance before the U.S. consul and applied for a passport.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，她在美国领事面前宣誓效忠并申请了护照。

D2.en: At 17, she had an examination record in the advanced singing class of de Hidalgo.
D2.zh: 17岁时，她在德伊达尔戈的高级声乐班有考试记录。

D1.en: At 22-23, she faced rejections that left bitterness and renewed determination.
D1.zh: 22到23岁时，她遭遇多次拒绝，留下苦涩和更坚定的决心。

D7.en: At 12-13, Alexander tried to upset and irritate her, possibly provoking an explosion.
D7.zh: 12到13岁时，亚历山大故意惹她生气、激怒她，可能想引发她爆发。

D9.en: At 23, she signed something that later caused trouble.
D9.zh: 23岁时，她签了字，后来引发了麻烦。

D18.en: At 17-18, her earnings were the family's only income.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，她挣的钱是家里唯一的收入。

D8.en: At 20, she told Niko to get up so she could use the phone.
D8.zh: 20岁时，她对尼科说：起来，让我打几个电话。

D13a.en: At 15, she left the National Conservatory for the Athens Conservatory.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，她离开国立音乐学院，去了雅典音乐学院跟德伊达尔戈学习。

D17.en: At 19, she gave food to conductor Sotos Vassiliadis after her first concert in Thessaloniki.
D17.zh: 19岁时，她在塞萨洛尼基首场音乐会后把食物给了指挥索托斯·瓦西利亚迪斯。

D11.en: She was given a chance to escape to worlds of romantic imagination.
D11.zh: 她得到了一个逃往浪漫想象世界的机会。

D15.en: At 14, she made Mary practice falling repeatedly.
D15.zh: 14岁时，她让玛丽一遍又一遍地练习摔倒。

D16.en: At 14-15, she would pick up her part and sing it note for note.
D16.zh: 14到15岁时，她会拿起自己的部分，几乎立刻一个音符不差地唱出来。

M6.en: She saw her musical talent as a destiny and a duty, not just a means to earn a living.
M6.zh: 她认为自己的音乐天赋不仅是谋生手段，更是一种使命和责任。

M2.en: She went to America to be with her father, hoping to find affection at last.
M2.zh: 她去美国与父亲团聚，希望最终能得到一些关爱。

D20.en: She registered as an American citizen at the U.S. consulate general.
D20.zh: 她在美国总领事馆登记为美国公民。

M4.en: She may have seen the advantages of maintaining a good relationship with the Casa d'Italia.
M4.zh: 她可能看到了与意大利之家保持良好关系的好处。

M5.en: She took to heart that Kambanis made more of a hit than she did.
M5.zh: 她因坎巴尼斯比她更受欢迎而耿耿于怀。

M7.en: She looked down on those who did not work as single-mindedly as she did.
M7.zh: 她看不起那些不像她一样专心工作的人。

D19.en: At 20, she used a system where each person paid their own share.
D19.zh: 20岁时，她使用了一个每人各自付账的系统。

D7.en: At 20, he was caught by his brother and scolded, then escorted back to Sichuan by train.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他被二哥抓住训斥，当天被押上火车返回四川。

D5.en: At 20, he was caught by his brother and scolded, then escorted back to Sichuan by train.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他被二哥抓住训斥，当天被押上火车返回四川。

D3.en: As a teenager, he studied painting and calligraphy, emulating ancient masters.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他潜心绘画和篆籀，临摹古代大师作品。

D11.en: At 19-20, he decided to paint a handscroll based on Liu Yong's poetic mood, not realistic sketching.
D11.zh: 19到20岁时，他决定按柳永词意画手卷，不搞写生。

D13a.en: At 19, he defied family opposition and sailed from Shanghai to Japan to study dyeing and weaving at Kyoto Fair School.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他违抗家中反对，从上海坐海轮赴日本，进京都公平学校学习染织技术。

D9.en: At about 25, he planned and forged a landscape painting in the style of Shitao to sell to a collector.
D9.zh: 约25岁时，他筹划造一幅假石涛山水，找掮客兜售给程霖生。

D14.en: At 25, he attended a gathering and was asked to paint, compose poems, and collaborate with seniors.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他第一次参加该会，被要求绘画、咏诗、题字，并与前辈合作。

D4.en: He forged Shi Tao paintings that fooled top collectors in Japan and China.
D4.zh: 他伪造的石涛画作骗过了日本和中国的大收藏家。

D2.en: He practiced tracing ancient rubbings and composing couplets from calligraphy models.
D2.zh: 他用双钩拓写历代碑板，并集字作联语。

D19.en: He studied the rules of poetry from the book Shixue Hanying.
D19.zh: 他常读《诗学涵英》，学习写诗规则。

D15.en: He often swam across the Jialing River with classmates in hot weather.
D15.zh: 天热时他常与同学横游嘉陵江。

D16.en: Zhang Daqian had a friend Yu Feian who influenced his art indirectly.
D16.zh: 张大千早年有朋友于非庵，间接影响其艺术。

D20.en: He collaborated and socialized daily with Yu Feian in Beijing.
D20.zh: 他在北平时与于非庵日夕合作游宴。

M1.en: He earned praise from teachers for his calligraphy and Chinese essays.
M1.zh: 他的写字和国文常得老师夸奖。

M3.en: Zhang Daqian had a friend, Yu Feian, who indirectly influenced his art as a young man.
M3.zh: 张大千早年有个朋友于非庵，间接影响了他的艺术。

M6.en: He wished to be forgotten by people, seeking liberation.
M6.zh: 他自求解脱，只希望人们把他忘记。

D12.en: He resolved to commit suicide rather than marry a woman he did not love.
D12.zh: 他决心自杀，不愿与不喜欢的女人结婚。

D13b.en: At 20, he became a student of calligraphy masters Zeng Nongran and Li Mei'an.
D13b.zh: 20岁时，他拜曾农髯和李梅庵为师学书法。

D10.en: In 1918 at age 20, he heard the news and was deeply grieved, rushing from Japan to Shanghai to mourn.
D10.zh: 1918年他20岁时，闻讯十分哀伤，特从日本赶赴上海吊祭。

M2.en: In 1917 at age 19, he sailed from Shanghai to Japan to study dyeing at Kyoto Fair School.
M2.zh: 1917年他19岁时，从上海坐海轮赴日本，进京都公平学校学习染织技术。

M4.en: At 20-25, he proposed swapping his Shi Tao painting for one he admired.
M4.zh: 20到25岁间，他提议用自己的石涛画换对方那幅石涛。

M5.en: In 1916 at age 17, he said everyone knew he was Kang's adviser, so Kang would protect him to save face.
M5.zh: 1916年他17岁时，说谁都知道他是康东家的师爷，康东家为顾面子会保他。

M7.en: As a child to age 25, he hung the national flag at Huan'gan Temple and Baimai Garden.
M7.zh: 幼年到25岁间，他在环鞏菴和百梅园升挂了国旗。

M8.en: In 1920 at age 21, he said he was very interested in Buddhism.
M8.zh: 1920年他21岁时，说他对佛学很有兴趣。

D17.en: In 1916 at age 18, he felt sympathy and often pleaded for mercy for someone.
D17.zh: 1916年他18岁时，心生同情，常为之求情讨饶。

D7.en: At 24, he defied authority by personally adding Pan Yuliang's name to the admission list, declaring talent over background.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他违抗权威，亲自在榜上添上潘玉良的名字，宣称以才取人。

D13a.en: At 15, he blew out the candle and left to ask his father to dissolve his unhappy marriage.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他吹灭蜡烛，出门请父亲帮他解除不幸的婚约。

D9.en: At 25, he led over 170 students on a sketching trip to West Lake in Hangzhou.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他率领170余名学生赴杭州西湖写生。

D3.en: At 23, he traveled to Japan to study art, visiting numerous exhibitions over several months.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他赴日本考察美术，参观了多个展览会。

D14.en: At 10, he performed calligraphy in public, writing a couplet from the Xuanmi Pagoda Stele.
D14.zh: 10岁时，他在游艺会上当众书写《玄秘塔碑》对联。

D4.en: At 24, he thought about uniting more painters to fight against dark forces.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他思考团结更多画家与黑暗势力抗衡。

D20.en: At 17, he co-founded the Shanghai Academy of Fine Arts and served as its principal.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他与乌始光等创办上海图画美术院，任校长。

D2.en: At 23, he published a book on pencil sketching through the Commercial Press.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他编写的《画学真诠第一集铅笔画写生》由商务印书馆出版。

D18.en: At 15, with his uncle and cousin's support, he opened a drawing school in Changzhou for clan children.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他在姑父和表兄支持下，在常州开办图画传习所，教授族中兄妹。

D6.en: At 23, he was elected a special member of the newly formed art group Tianma Hui at the Shanghai Academy of Fine Arts.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他被推选为新成立的美术团体天马会的特别会员。

D12.en: At age 4, he was handed over to the care of his Lennon relatives.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他被交给列侬亲戚照顾。

D6.en: At 15-16, he became a firm friend of Rod Davis.
D6.zh: 15到16岁时，他与罗德·戴维斯成为密友。

D2.en: As a teenager, he was good at English, art, and French, but awful at arithmetic and made little effort.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他擅长英语、美术和法语，但算术很差，而且不怎么努力。

D5.en: At 25, a firecracker exploded near him with the snap-snap-snap of a real revolver.
D5.zh: 25岁时，一个鞭炮在他附近爆炸，发出像真左轮手枪一样的啪啪声。

D7.en: At 17-20, he would pocket the contents of the collection tin he had taken through the streets.
D7.zh: 17到20岁时，他会把沿街募捐罐里的钱装进自己口袋。

D14.en: At 16, he gave his first-ever public performance with the Quarry Men at a street carnival.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他随采石工乐队在街头嘉年华首次公开演出。

D13a.en: He decided not to rock any boats with Mimi until he was successful.
D13a.zh: 他决定在成功之前不和咪咪惹麻烦。

D10.en: He choked up at the mention of his mother's death two years before Hamburg.
D10.zh: 他提到母亲两年前去世时哽咽了。

D1.en: At 18, he and his band approached promoters for work as a nonpercussive trio.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他和他的乐队找推广人找工作，作为非打击乐三重奏。

M1.en: The Quarry Men entered several skiffle contests but failed to win or place.
M1.zh: 采石工人乐队参加了几个噪音爵士乐比赛，但都没赢或获得名次。

M6.en: He said they would go to America, be very big, very famous, and make lots of money.
M6.zh: 他说他们会去美国，变得非常大、非常有名，赚很多钱。

M3.en: They were top English literature students as much as would-be Elvises.
M3.zh: 他们既是顶尖的英语文学学生，也是未来的猫王们。

D15.en: The two guitarists and banjo player took off their shoes and climbed into the empty tub.
D15.zh: 两个吉他手和班卓琴手脱掉鞋子，爬进空浴缸里。

D17.en: He cycled daily to the fishmonger to buy hake for his pets.
D17.zh: 他每天骑车去鱼贩那里给宠物买鳕鱼。

D13b.en: He left Quarry Bank for art college.
D13b.zh: 他离开奎里班克去了艺术学院。

M2.en: At age 21, he thought there would be no illegitimate baby to besmirch the family name.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他认为不会有私生子玷污家族名声。

D20X.en: At 22, Spielberg and a friend decided to keep writing as just the two of them.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，斯皮尔伯格和朋友决定只由两人继续写作。

D12.en: At 12, he understood his father's hardship and refused to pay for a room change.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他深知父亲挣钱艰难，不肯加钱换房。

D3.en: As a child, he watched his teacher draw with soot and ink, fascinated.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他常看缪老师用煤烟和墨作画，看得入迷。

M1.en: At 12, he passed a competitive exam to enter Wuxi Normal School.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他在数百人竞争中考取无锡师范初中。

D2.en: At 11-13, he boarded at Eshan, using money his family saved by selling rice and pigs.
D2.zh: 11到13岁间，他住校鹅山，家里粜稻卖猪凑学费。

D13a.en: At 11, he decided he wanted to enter Wuxi Normal School instead of being a village teacher.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他不愿当农村小学教员，想进省立无锡师范。

D5.en: At 17, he resolutely transferred to Hangzhou Art Academy, plunging into art.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他毅然转入杭州艺专，投身艺术苦海。

D7.en: At 17, he defied his father's opposition to pursue art, despite causing parental grief.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他违抗父亲反对，执意学画，不顾父母悲伤。

D4.en: At 10-11, he led classmates to steal ginkgo nuts on a stormy night.
D4.zh: 10到11岁时，他串通同学在暴雨夜偷白果。

D14.en: At 23, he had an inspiration to make a red robe and wear it like a scholar.
D14.zh: 23岁时，他突发灵感，想做一件大红袍披在身上。

M4.en: At 17, he fell in love with art after encountering Hangzhou Art Academy.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他接触杭州艺专，疯狂爱上美术。

M3.en: He regarded Lu Xun as his spiritual pillar.
M3.zh: 他把鲁迅当作精神支柱。

M6.en: He resolved to forge his own path and paint oils that the Chinese people would love.
M6.zh: 他下决心走自己的路，要画出中国人民喜爱的油画来。

M7.en: He had a heart concerned for the nation and its people.
M7.zh: 他有一颗忧国忧民的心。

D6.en: He and some classmates from occupied areas ran a small food shop after class to earn money.
D6.zh: 他和几个沦陷区同学课余开小食品店挣钱。

D1.en: He borrowed money from a female classmate to make a big red robe.
D1.zh: 他向一位女同学借钱要做件大红袍。

D10.en: He suddenly became agitated, saying he was baring his soul about the suffering nation.
D10.zh: 他突然激动起来，说赤膊给你看苦难的民族和家园。

D11.en: He first used the pen name 'Wu Tucha', later changed to 'Tu' for his paintings.
D11.zh: 他始用笔名‘吴荼茶’，后改为‘荼’作为画作签名。

D20X.en: He let his children choose one painting each as souvenirs and donated the rest.
D20X.zh: 他让孩子们各选一幅画作纪念，其余全部捐赠。

D13b.en: At 15, he finished junior normal school and applied to an industrial school to save the nation.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，他读完师范初中，报考高级工业学校电机科，以期工业救国。

D9.en: At 23, he graduated from National Art College and became a teaching assistant at Chongqing University.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他毕业于国立艺专，在重庆大学建筑系任助教。

D16.en: At 21-23, he once fantasized about pursuing literature, following in Lu Xun's footsteps.
D16.zh: 21到23岁间，他曾幻想从事文学，步鲁迅的后尘。

M2.en: He wanted to depict his hometown's suffering乡亲 and his father's back in a small boat.
M2.zh: 他想表现家乡苦难的乡亲们，以及小篷船里父亲的背影。

D19.en: In his 20s, he recorded in his diary how the air at the alley entrance changed minute by minute from dawn to night.
D19.zh: 20多岁时，他在日记中记述了从清晨到黑夜巷口的空气如何在分分秒秒间递变。

M8.en: At about 77, he prayed to God to protect him from becoming a woman.
M8.zh: 约77岁时，他祈求上帝保佑他不变成女人。

D3.en: At 7-8, he began learning to paint by copying the Mustard Seed Garden Manual.
D3.zh: 七八岁时，他照着《芥子园画谱》开始学画。

D4.en: At 7-8, he helped his grandfather sharpen chisels and watched him carve stone.
D4.zh: 七八岁时，他帮祖父磨凿子、递榔头，看祖父刻石碑。

D12.en: He was born in 1900 into a poor peasant family in Meixian, Guangdong; his grandfather and father were stonemasons.
D12.zh: 他1900年生于广东梅县贫苦农家，祖父和父亲都是石匠。

D2.en: At 25, he devoted all his energy to art and deeply studied various painting schools.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他将全部精神奉献给艺术，深入研究各绘画流派。

D9.en: At 24, he co-organized the 'Hopus Society' (later 'Overseas Art Movement Society') with Lin Wenzheng.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他与林文铮共同组织了“霍普斯会”（后改名为“海外艺术运动社”）。

D5.en: At 19, he left home with simple luggage.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他带着简单行装离开家乡。

D6.en: At 22, through a friend's introduction, he became friends with a French girl.
D6.zh: 22岁时，经朋友介绍，他和一位法兰西女郎做了朋友。

D13a.en: At 20-23, after arriving in Paris, he wrote home to call off his arranged marriage.
D13a.zh: 20到23岁间，到了巴黎后，他写信回家退掉了这门亲事。

D1.en: At 25, his wife Laura died with their newborn in a Paris hospital due to illness after childbirth.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他的妻子罗拉在巴黎一家医院因分娩后染病，与新生儿一同去世。

D20.en: At 24, he co-organized the 'Huo Pu Si Hui' with Lin Wenzheng.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他与林文铮共同组织了“霍普斯会”。

D10.en: At 25, in extreme grief, he used his grandfather's stone-carving skill to chisel a stone tablet day and night.
D10.zh: 25岁时，在悲恸至极之中，他用祖父传给他的刻石技艺，没日没夜地凿刻一块石碑。

D18.en: Between ages 19-24, he visited Brazil four times (1978, 1979, 1981, 1983) to visit relatives.
D18.zh: 19到24岁间，他于1978年、1979年、1981年、1983年先后四次去巴西探亲。

D12.en: As a small child, he helped his grandmother and mother make matchboxes for piecework wages.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他帮祖母和母亲糊火柴盒挣计件工资。

D3.en: At age 7-10, his grandfather's large paint box sparked his interest in painting.
D3.zh: 7到10岁时，祖父的大画箱激发了他对绘画的兴趣。

D2.en: At 20, he spent days at the museum sketching and copying oil paintings, then went to a free studio for quick sketches.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他每天带一个面包去博物馆，从上午十点工作到下午三点，对着古代雕刻练习素描或临摹油画，之后去自由画院画速写。

M4.en: At 17, he joined patriotic students in street demonstrations and fund-raising against imperialism.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他和其他爱国学生一起上街举行反帝示威游行和募捐活动。

D6.en: As a teenager, he joined the school band and performed in cities like Changzhou and Wuxi.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他参加了学校乐队，到过常州、无锡等城市公演。

D4.en: At 20, he spent days at the museum sketching and copying oil paintings, then went to a free studio for quick sketches.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他每天带一个面包去博物馆，从上午十点工作到下午三点，对着古代雕刻练习素描或临摹油画，之后去自由画院画速写。

D10.en: At 22, he fell ill from long-term hunger and overwork; the doctor said it was malnutrition.
D10.zh: 22岁时，由于长期饥饿劳累，他病倒了，医生说是营养不良。

D11.en: At 22, he painted "Napkin Study" in his bedroom and wrote in French: "Today, I am well!"
D11.zh: 22岁时，他在卧室画了《餐巾习作》，并用法文写上：“今天，我病好了！”

D1.en: At 23, he completed all required courses in just one year and qualified for the year-end oil painting exam.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他仅用一年学完全部必修课，获得了参加学年末油画专业课评奖考试的资格。

D5.en: At 23, he completed a two-week professional oil painting exam in just one week.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他仅用一周时间就完成了规定两周画完的专业油画考试。

D20.en: At 22, he was elected as the art minister of the Nanguo Painting Society.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他被推举为南国画会美术部长。

D12.en: At 12-13, his mother died and father fell ill, forcing him to face self-reliance.
D12.zh: 12到13岁时，母亲去世父亲病倒，他不得不面对自立谋生。

D18.en: At 16-17, he earned 2-3 yuan per charcoal portrait, barely scraping by.
D18.zh: 16到17岁时，他画炭像每张得两三块钱，勉强糊口。

D4.en: At 13-14, he volunteered to repair a photo studio backdrop and painted a new one.
D4.zh: 13到14岁时，他自荐修补照相馆布景，又画了张新布景。

D5.en: At 16, he fought back against a thug, pinning his hand to a pole until he dropped his knife.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他反抗恶徒，把对方的手按在电线杆上磨出血，刀掉在地上。

D6.en: At 16-17, he often helped others when he had charcoal portrait work.
D6.zh: 16到17岁时，他有炭像画时常常接济别人。

D2.en: From 17 to 23, he taught himself Western painting, including素描, oil, watercolor, and sculpture.
D2.zh: 17到23岁间，他自学西画，素描、油画、水粉、雕塑都学得很认真。

D3.en: From 17 to 23, he frequently visited a bookstore selling Western art books and reproductions.
D3.zh: 17到23岁间，他常去一家专卖外国艺术书籍的书店。

M4.en: At 21, he witnessed British-led Indian police suppress unarmed protesters on Nanjing Road.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他在南京路上亲眼看到英国巡捕房派出的印度巡捕血腥镇压手无寸铁的群众。

D14.en: At 22-23, he created his first oil painting, 'The Rickshaw Puller's Family'.
D14.zh: 22到23岁时，他创作了处女作油画《黄包车夫的家庭》。

D9.en: From 24 to 26, he taught at the Art Department of Nanjing Central University.
D9.zh: 24到26岁时，他在南京中大艺术系任教两年。

D19.en: He treasured this oil painting and kept it intact for decades.
D19.zh: 他十分珍惜这件油画，将它完好地保存了几十年。

D7.en: He refused to learn traditional Chinese ink painting and calligraphy, instead adopting Western sketching and oil painting.
D7.zh: 他拒绝学习传统的中国水墨画和书法，而是代之以西方的素描和水彩油画。

D2.en: He practiced martial arts by punching walls, trees, stones, and sandbags daily, causing his fingers to swell and joints to harden.
D2.zh: 他练武术时天天打墙壁、打树、打石头、打沙袋，手指肿了，关节硬了。

D11.en: He said everything he was interested in as a youth eventually converged into a force serving his becoming an artist.
D11.zh: 他说年少时感兴趣的东西最终汇流成一股力量，全为他成为艺术家服务。

D6.en: At 16, he was selected for the city propaganda team, essentially a troupe, avoiding the send-down program.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他入选了市宣传队，基本上像一个剧团，没有上山下乡。

D15.en: In the 1970s, he was a young man who practiced martial arts and loved painting.
D15.zh: 1970年代，他是一个好练武术、爱画画的青年。

D16.en: From his teens to his 20s, he often argued with his father about this.
D16.zh: 少年到青年时期，他常为此和父亲争论。

D12.en: As a college student, he had wedding funds saved by his mother.
D12.zh: 大学时期，他母亲为他存好了结婚用款。

D18.en: Every summer he traveled using his own savings plus the wedding money his mother saved.
D18.zh: 每个暑假他都用自己工作存的钱和母亲存的结婚用款去旅行。

M1.en: From elementary school through university, he consistently performed excellently and was appreciated by all teachers.
M1.zh: 从小学到大学，他学习一贯优秀，所有老师都欣赏他。

M4.en: He used Marxism-Leninism to reflect on social issues and contradictions in China.
M4.zh: 他用马列主义反思中国的社会问题和矛盾。

M7.en: He wanted to travel all over China to get to know the country while he was still there.
M7.zh: 他想趁还在中国时走遍每一个地方，好好认识中国。

D13a.en: His violin teacher gave him an ultimatum: choose between martial arts and violin.
D13a.zh: 他的提琴老师给他下了最后通牒，要他在武术和小提琴中选一个。

D20.en: He was selected for the city propaganda team, basically a troupe.
D20.zh: 他入选了市宣传队，基本上像一个剧团。

D10.en: Seeing the Boston Symphony Orchestra perform in China shocked and hurt him.
D10.zh: 见到波士顿交响乐团到中国演奏，让他很惊讶也很受伤。

D14.en: He made martial arts films and also played the violin.
D14.zh: 他拍过武打片，也拉过小提琴。

D17.en: While he ran, his mother washed clothes in the river.
D17.zh: 他跑步的时候妈妈在河上洗衣服。

D19.en: He strictly and meticulously made every plan or task.
D19.zh: 他严格周密地做每一个计划或是任务。

D8.en: He always questioned his father.
D8.zh: 他总是质疑父亲。

D9.en: He was selected for the city propaganda team, basically a troupe.
D9.zh: 他入选了市宣传队，基本上像一个剧团。

D4.en: He studied painting and stage design under Chen Yiting, and was responsible for designing and painting stage sets.
D4.zh: 他跟随陈逸亭学习绘画及舞台设计，并负责设计和绘制舞台布景。

D12.en: At 23, he let his daughters mark red slips for those unable to pay, and he often covered their fees and medicine costs.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他让女儿们用红纸标记交不起挂号费的人，他不仅不收钱，还常倒贴药费。

D15.en: At 18, he walked over 600 li to Hangzhou, taking vehicles for less than a third of the journey.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他步行六百多里路到杭州，乘车搭船不到三分之一。

D3.en: At 11-12, he drew figures and flowers on paper, and copied illustrations from novels and temple decorations.
D3.zh: 11到12岁时，他在纸片上画小人花鸟，临摹小说插图和祠庙门窗上的画。

D18.en: At 7, he went herding cattle with a farmhand for nearly ten days.
D18.zh: 7岁时，他跟帮工去放牛快十天了。

D4.en: At 14-15, he made clay seals by kneading fine clay, drying, carving, and firing them.
D4.zh: 14到15岁时，他找来粘土做成印坯，晒干刻章后煅烧坚实。

D2.en: As a teenager, he stayed up late every night writing, painting, and carving until he used up a lamp of oil.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他每夜在小油灯下写字作诗画画刻章，不点完一灯油不上床。

D7.en: At 11-12, he was beaten for drawing but never cried, just rubbed his palm or pressed it with an inkstone.
D7.zh: 11到12岁时，他因画画被打肿手心，只用另一只手揉或用砚台按，从不落泪。

D5.en: At 22, a bayonet cut his face, but he remained fearless and wiped the blood with his hand.
D5.zh: 22岁时，刺刀划破他的脸，他毫不畏惧，只用手抹了一把血。

D1.en: At 18, teachers persuaded his father to let him take the exam for a free school, saying it was very competitive.
D1.zh: 18岁时，老师劝说他父亲让他考省立第一师范，说考上后免学费膳宿，但很难考。

M1.en: At 18, he wrote passionately about his concern for the nation and his ambition to revive Chinese painting.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他奋笔疾书，写下忧国忧民的真情和振兴民族绘画的抱负。

D9.en: At 18-22, he gladly brought his art supplies to draw and write for classmates and workers until they were satisfied.
D9.zh: 18到22岁间，他总笑着带上画具给同学或工友写画，直到对方满意。

D6.en: At 22, he was a radical student who helped younger classmate Zhao Pingfu borrow progressive books through student leaders.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他思想激进，帮低年级同学赵平复通过学生干部借进步书籍。

D13a.en: At 22, he obeyed his father's letter summoning him home and married without preparation.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他遵父命回乡完婚，毫无准备。

M7.en: At 22, he declared that every individual is responsible for the rise and fall of the nation.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他宣称国家兴亡，匹夫有责。

D14.en: At 24, when pleased with a painting, he would recite poetry and give it to any student who asked.
D14.zh: 24岁时，画得满意他就吟诗，并把画贴在墙上，学生要画他就送，从不让人空手。

D3.en: At 25, he noticed French blotting paper and wondered if it resembled Chinese rice paper.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他注意到法国吸墨纸，思考其是否类似宣纸。

D4.en: At 17-18, he bought a large box of pigments with his father's money instead of necessities.
D4.zh: 17到18岁时，他用父亲给的钱买了一箱颜料，而不是日用品。

D7.en: At age 25, Dong Jingzhao returned to Paris for Chu Teh-Chun, defying her father.
D7.zh: 25岁时，董景昭为朱德群违抗父命回到巴黎。

D12.en: At 13-14, Zhu Qing began worrying about family livelihood, pawning items to cover basic expenses.
D12.zh: 十三四岁时，朱晴开始为生计担忧，典当物品维持家用。

D9.en: At age 18, his PE teacher admired his talent and urged him to apply to a sports school in Shanghai.
D9.zh: 18岁时，体育老师欣赏他的天赋，动员他报考上海体育专科学校。

D6.en: At age 16, he often went to taverns with a friend to drink huadiao wine.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他常与朋友去小酒馆喝花雕酒。

D5.en: At 25, he boldly tried to transplant the uncanny workmanship into oil painting.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他大胆尝试将这种鬼斧神工移植到油画中。

D1.en: At 25, he refused to give up his love for Dong Jingzhao despite being married.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他认定与董景昭的爱才是此生不可错过的情愫，尽管已有妻子。

M4.en: At 19-20, he studied painting for his love of art, not for knowledge or to finish school.
M4.zh: 19到20岁时，他为了热爱的绘画艺术而学习，不是为了知识或完成学业。

D10.en: At 19, he felt grief and humiliation boiling in his blood, his chest nearly bursting.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他感觉悲愤屈辱的血在心中沸腾，胸腔憋闷得几乎要炸开。

D8.en: At 25, he issued an ultimatum in a letter: if she didn't return to Paris, he would go to the US to find her.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他在信中发出最后通牒：你如果再不回巴黎，我就马上到美国去找你。

M2.en: At 25, he waited as Dong Jingzhao defied her father to return to Paris for him.
M2.zh: 25岁时，他等待董景昭违抗父命回到巴黎。

M5.en: He issued an ultimatum: if she didn't return to Paris, he would go to the US to find her.
M5.zh: 他发出最后通牒：如果她再不回巴黎，他就马上去美国找她。

D15.en: As a teenager, he was obsessed with basketball, playing after class and even under the moonlight until silence fell.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他迷上了篮球，课后打两小时，有月亮时晚上继续打到万籁寂静。

D18.en: As a small child, he was welcome to eat melons for free as long as he spat out the seeds.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他白吃瓜受欢迎，只要把瓜子吐出来就行。

D16.en: Between ages 15 and 25, he learned some French at Hangzhou Art School and audited classes in Chongqing during the war.
D16.zh: 15到25岁间，他在杭州艺专学过一点法语，抗战时在重庆旁听中央大学的课。

M1.en: He never slackened in training his basic painting skills.
M1.zh: 他从未懈怠过绘画基本功的训练。

M3.en: He revered Cézanne as his idol.
M3.zh: 他尊崇塞尚为偶像。

M7.en: From age 15 to 35, he never slacked in training his basic painting skills.
M7.zh: 15到35岁间，他从未懈怠过绘画基本功的训练。

D12.en: He was born in pre-Communist China.
D12.zh: 他出生在共产主义之前的中国。

D3.en: He spent hours in the Lion Forest with other young family members studying under a tutor.
D3.zh: 他和别的幼年家人在狮子林花很多时间跟老师学习。

D4.en: At 13, he prepared his mother's opium pipe.
D4.zh: 13岁时，他给母亲准备鸦片烟枪。

D6.en: He played billiards with friends and became skilled.
D6.zh: 他和朋友打台球，打得很好。

D15.en: He played billiards with friends and became skilled.
D15.zh: 他和朋友打台球，打得很好。

D2.en: He studied at Penn, MIT, and Harvard.
D2.zh: 他在宾夕法尼亚大学、麻省理工和哈佛学习。

D13b.en: He came to the United States in 1935.
D13b.zh: 他1935年来到美国。

D9.en: At 25, he enrolled at Harvard GSD for a master's.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他进入哈佛设计学院读硕士。

D7.en: At 18, he gave up architecture and transferred to MIT for engineering.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他放弃建筑，转到麻省理工学工程。

D13a.en: At 17, he agreed to take Oxford exams but only if he could choose his school.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他同意考牛津，但前提是能自己选学校。

M1.en: He won a joint MIT-Harvard weekend sketch competition.
M1.zh: 他赢得了麻省理工和哈佛联合举办的周末素描比赛。

D11.en: As a small child, he believed he could hear bamboo growing.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他相信自己能听到竹子生长的声音。

D5.en: At 21, he traveled west hoping to meet the great man.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他西行希望能见到那位伟人。

D14.en: As a teenager, he frequented Shanghai pool halls and movie theaters.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他常去上海台球厅和电影院。

D12.en: At 22-26, he was an activist in 'going deep into life' at the Central Academy of Fine Arts, marking his travels on a map.
D12.zh: 22到26岁间，他在中央美术学院是‘深入生活’的积极分子，在地图上用红线标出去过的地方。

D6.en: At 24-25, he attended a symposium at the art academy where the guests were 'the odd ones' and they were 'the plural ones'.
D6.zh: 24到25岁时，他参加美院座谈会，觉得来宾是‘异数的’，而他们是‘复数的’。

D3.en: At 22-26, he had a map of China with red lines marking places he had visited, and the lines grew denser.
D3.zh: 22到26岁间，他有一张中国地图，上面用红线标着去过的地方，红线越来越密。

D4.en: At 22-26, he carried a sketchbook to capture lively scenes, then used the material to create profound works.
D4.zh: 22到26岁间，他随身带速写本画下生动的东西，再根据素材创作主题鲜明的作品。

D15.en: As a teenager to young adult, he dreaded the task of thinning millet seedlings, which meant squatting all day in the field.
D15.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，他最怕蹲在地里薅箍子（间谷苗），蹲着走一天。

D18.en: As a teenager to young adult, he carried a heavy braid in his bag to the commune, sold it, and gave the money to the woman.
D18.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，他把一条大辫子装在书包里带到公社卖掉，把钱交给她。

D19.en: He aimed to match the level of 'PLA Literature and Art' using stencil cutting, while picking lice and copying pompous texts.
D19.zh: 他的目标是用蜡纸刻印技术达到《解放军文艺》的水平，一边捏虱子一边刻蜡纸抄写豪迈篇章。

M1.en: At 22-26, he was determined to become a distinguished artist.
M1.zh: 22到26岁间，他一心要成为一个有出息的艺术家。

M4.en: In his teens to mid-20s, he felt outrage that artists ignored Mao's clear theory.
M4.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，他激动又愤慨：毛主席说得这么清楚，美术工作者怎么搞的！

M7.en: At 22-26, he believed they could convey people's grievances to the chairman through art.
M7.zh: 22到26岁间，他相信通过他们可以把不满直接反映给主席。

D2.en: As a teenager, he learned piano and violin, finding joy in the hard work.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他学钢琴和小提琴，虽苦却乐在其中。

D6.en: From age 14 to 22, he was a member of the Tsinghua Art Club and art editor of the yearbook.
D6.zh: 14到22岁间，他是清华艺术俱乐部成员和校刊艺术编辑。

D9.en: At 18, he was a minor leader among Tsinghua students in the May Fourth Movement.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他是五四运动中清华学生的小领袖之一。

D3.en: As a teenager, he learned piano and violin, finding joy in the hard work.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他学钢琴和小提琴，虽苦却乐在其中。

D4.en: At 23-26, he researched architectural history and classical decoration in the library.
D4.zh: 23到26岁时，他在图书馆研究建筑史和古典装饰。

D15.en: As a small child, he learned to dive from a retired naval officer at the beach.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他在海边跟退休海军军官学潜泳。

D5.en: At 22, he was pinned under his motorcycle after a car hit it.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他被汽车撞翻的摩托车压在下面。

D8.en: At 20-21, he tried to control her activities out of responsibility, and she fought back.
D8.zh: 20到21岁时，他因觉得对她有责任而试图控制她的活动，她坚决反击。

M4.en: At about 25, he died fulfilling his mission in his chosen profession.
M4.zh: 约25岁时，他死得其所，完成了自己选择的专业的使命。

D13a.en: At 5-6, he insisted on waiting for his mother to wipe him after pooping.
D13a.zh: 五六岁时，他拉完屎还撅着屁股非等娘来擦不可。

D11.en: At 18-22, he co-translated Wells' "The Outline of History" into Chinese, published by Commercial Press.
D11.zh: 18到22岁时，他与同学合译威尔斯的《世界史纲》，由商务印书馆出版。

D1.en: At 5-6, he vowed never to be careless again.
D1.zh: 5到6岁时，他发誓再也不敢马马虎虎了。

M7.en: At 18, he was a student leader in the May Fourth Movement, a core member of the Patriotic Ten-Man Group and volunteer corps.
M7.zh: 他18岁时在五四运动中担任清华学生的小领袖之一，是‘爱国十人团’和义勇军的中坚分子。

D18.en: At 22, he co-translated H.G. Wells' "The Outline of History" with Wu Wenzao and Xu Zongsou.
D18.zh: 22岁时，他与吴文藻、徐宗漱等合译威尔斯的《世界史纲》。

D3.en: As a small child, he found antiques and paintings strange and fun, secretly playing with them and doodling with a brush.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他觉得古董和书画古怪有趣，偷偷玩古董，还用毛笔乱涂乱抹。

D11.en: As a small child, he found antiques and paintings strange and fun, secretly playing with them and doodling with a brush.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他觉得古董和书画古怪有趣，偷偷玩古董，还用毛笔乱涂乱抹。

D4.en: At 19, he hastily packed and left West China Union University, determined to depart immediately.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他匆忙收拾行李，毅然离开了华西协合大学。

D2.en: At 22, he invited experts to teach, organized sketching, and wrote a long art theory outline, working late nights.
D2.zh: 22岁时，他请专家讲课，组织画素描，还熬夜写了长篇艺术论提纲。

D13a.en: At 15, he resented his mother for forbidding him to go to Chengdu and for arranging a marriage.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他因不能去成都和被迫成亲而对母亲非常不满。

D9.en: At 22, he lived with villagers, ate with them, explained party policies, and worked alongside them.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他和老乡同吃同住，讲解政策，一起劳动。

D16.en: At 22, he prepared thoroughly for debates, spoke passionately with gestures for over an hour, clear and logical.
D16.zh: 22岁时，他每次讨论会充分准备，发言充满激情，一讲一个多小时，条理清楚。

D5.en: At 19, he hastily packed and left West China Union University, determined to depart immediately.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他匆忙收拾行李，毅然离开了华西协合大学。

D17.en: At 19, he anxiously cared for a sick friend, more distressed than if he were ill himself.
D17.zh: 19岁时，他比对自己生病还痛苦，格外细心地照顾志瑞。

D1.en: At 19, he traveled from Sichuan to Shaanxi and asked a staff member to help him join a youth cadre training class.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他从四川到陕西，请求工作人员帮助他进入青年干部训练班。

D20.en: He was elected director of the student council.
D20.zh: 他被选为学生会的主任。

M4.en: At 19, he decided to leave immediately, packed his bags, and resolutely departed from West China Union University.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他决计立即出发，收拾行李毅然离开了华西协合大学。

M7.en: He proposed to the Yan'an literary and art committee to reform the 'Western scene'.
M7.zh: 他向边区文协美术工作委员会提出了改造“西洋景”的建议。

M1.en: He regretted not having formal schooling and studied hard to supplement his knowledge.
M1.zh: 他为自己只读过私塾没有正规中学学历感到遗憾，抓紧时间读书补充知识。

M3.en: He admired Lu Xun and often said he wanted to achieve in art like him.
M3.zh: 他对鲁迅十分崇拜，常说要像鲁迅一样在艺术上搞出成就。

D10.en: He became upset and silent, distracted during lectures.
D10.zh: 他心烦意乱，变得沉默寡言，听讲时有些神不守舍。

D8.en: He insisted that stage design should serve the plot and atmosphere, and his design was finally accepted.
D8.zh: 他坚持布景应配合剧情烘托气氛，最后他的设计思想被同意了。

D6.en: He fell in love with Min Lisheng and they often walked along the Yan River talking about life.
D6.zh: 他和闵力生相爱了，经常在延河边散步谈理想和人生。

M2.en: He came from Sichuan to Yan'an and was from a big landlord family.
M2.zh: 他从四川到延安，又是大地主家庭出身。

M5.en: At 24, he carried the burden of being a suspected spy but remained unnegative.
M5.zh: 24岁时，他背着特嫌分子的包袱，但毫不消极。

M6.en: At 21-26, he emphasized sketching as key to practice, life observation, and material accumulation.
M6.zh: 21到26岁间，他特别重视画速写，作为练习基本功、熟悉生活和积累素材的手段。

D12.en: At 22, he rode a donkey but carried a big backpack himself, tiring himself without lightening the donkey.
D12.zh: 22岁时，他骑在毛驴上却自己背着大背包，自己受累，毛驴也没减轻重量。

D14.en: At 17-18, he copied a painting as a wall newspaper header, earning praise from teachers and classmates.
D14.zh: 17到18岁时，他为墙报临摹了一幅画作报头，引得老师和同学们称赞。

D15.en: Her smashed pelvis caused miscarriages and therapeutic abortions.
D15.zh: 她碎裂的骨盆导致流产和治疗性堕胎。

D5.en: At 15, she soaped the stairway from the amphitheater stage.
D5.zh: 15岁时，她在圆形剧场舞台的楼梯上涂了肥皂。

D6.en: At 18, she said, 'I want you to come, that is to say you and the boys.'
D6.zh: 18岁时，她说：“我希望你来，就是说你和那些男孩。”

D7.en: At 22, she ignored her mother's warnings and married Diego Rivera.
D7.zh: 22岁时，她不顾母亲警告，嫁给了迭戈·里维拉。

D3.en: At 24, she spent most of her time painting and expected her first exhibition in New York.
D3.zh: 24岁时，她大部分时间在画画，期待在纽约举办首次个展。

D4.en: At 24, she spent most of her time painting and expected her first exhibition in New York.
D4.zh: 24岁时，她大部分时间在画画，期待在纽约举办首次个展。

D14.en: Five days after her miscarriage at 25, she drew a bust-length self-portrait.
D14.zh: 25岁流产五天后，她画了一幅半身自画像。

D12.en: Her smashed pelvis led only to miscarriages and at least three therapeutic abortions.
D12.zh: 她的骨盆碎裂只导致了流产和至少三次治疗性流产。

D2.en: At 24, she spent most of her time painting.
D2.zh: 24岁时，她大部分时间都在画画。

M1.en: At 24, she expected to have her first exhibition in New York in September.
M1.zh: 24岁时，她期待在纽约举办她的第一次画展。

M4.en: As a teenager, she and her friends believed the poor were oppressed by the wealthy.
M4.zh: 十几岁时，她和朋友们认为穷人受富人压迫。

D19.en: She kept her small sable brushes immaculately clean and laid down delicate strokes.
D19.zh: 她把自己的小貂毛笔保持得非常干净，小心地画上细腻的笔触。

D13b.en: She chose 1910, the year of the Mexican Revolution, as her birth date instead of the true year.
D13b.zh: 她选择墨西哥革命爆发的1910年作为自己的出生年份，而不是真实年份。

D10.en: At 18, she felt a horrible pain in her waist and an anguish more terrible than you can imagine.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她感到腰部剧痛和难以想象的痛苦。

D20.en: At 25, she and another woman began drawing on the lithographer's stones.
D20.zh: 25岁时，她和另一个女人开始在石版画石上作画。

M7.en: She embodied the whole notion of culture for Chicano women.
M7.zh: 她体现了奇卡诺女性文化的全部概念。

M6.en: She painted because she needed to.
M6.zh: 她画画是因为她需要画。

M2.en: She claimed her birth year as 1910, the start of the Mexican Revolution.
M2.zh: 她把自己的出生年份说成1910年，墨西哥革命爆发那年。

M3.en: She and Diego lived in this house from 1929 to 1954.
M3.zh: 她和迭戈从1929年到1954年住在这所房子里。

D16.en: She said, 'It is I who am the big artist.'
D16.zh: 她说：‘我才是大艺术家。’

D17.en: She made the basket's contents less bountiful than usual.
D17.zh: 她让篮子里的东西比平时少。

D18.en: She won sweets from peddlers by coin toss, never losing.
D18.zh: 她和小贩掷硬币赢糖果，从来没输过。

D8.en: She stiffened and returned Lupe's stare without a word.
D8.zh: 她僵住，一言不发地回瞪卢佩。

D9.en: She made a study schedule and gave Lucienne Spanish lessons.
D9.zh: 她制定了学习计划，并给卢西安娜上西班牙语课。

D20X.en: She neither competed with nor deferred to Rivera.
D20X.zh: 她既不与里维拉竞争，也不屈从他。

D11.en: At 16, he stared at scratches on bare walls and composed allegorical pictures with his fingers.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他盯着光墙上的划痕，用手指构思寓言画。

D14.en: At 15, he always carried a cane topped by a golden double-headed eagle.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他总拄着一根镶金双头鹰手杖。

D7.en: At 17, he said, 'Spain is shit, both the Government and the people.'
D7.zh: 17岁时，他说：“西班牙是狗屎，政府和人民都是。”

D2.en: At 13, he received a Special Merit certificate for his performance at the Municipal Drawing School.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他因在市立绘画学校的表现获得荣誉证书。

D3.en: At 23, he said, 'I'm happily back in Figueres with new records and an infinity of things to read.'
D3.zh: 23岁时，他说：“我高兴地回到菲格拉斯，带着新唱片和无数读物。”

D6.en: At 18, he accompanied Buñuel on forays through nocturnal Madrid.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他陪布努埃尔夜游马德里。

D4.en: At 17, he let his hair grow long and grew sideburns.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他留起长发和鬓角。

D9.en: At 22, he sent a photograph to Lorca for publication in the magazine gallo.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他把照片寄给洛尔迦，用于先锋杂志《加洛》发表。

D20.en: At 24, he was about to launch a Surrealist magazine with contributions from Buñuel and 'our Catalan group'.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他即将创办一本超现实主义杂志，由布努埃尔和“我们加泰罗尼亚小组”供稿。

D20X.en: At 22, he was expelled for announcing he was more intelligent than the professors and refusing to be examined.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他因宣称比教授更聪明并拒绝考试而被开除。

M1.en: At 13, he received a Special Merit certificate for his performance at the Municipal Drawing School.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他因在市立绘画学校的表现获得特别荣誉证书。

D5.en: At 25, he referred to a painting on which the artist had written 'I have spat on my mother'.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他提到一幅画，画家在上面写了‘我朝我母亲吐了口水’。

D10.en: At 25, the arrival of a letter moved him to cut off his hair and bury it on the beach.
D10.zh: 25岁时，一封信的到来让他剪掉头发并埋在埃斯拉内海滩。

D12.en: At age 0, he was baptized Salvador Felipe Jacinto in the parish church of Saint Peter.
D12.zh: 0岁时，他在圣彼得教区教堂受洗，得名萨尔瓦多·费利佩·哈辛托。

D16.en: At 21, he gave a series of lectures to a working-class audience in Barcelona's Federalist-Republican Centre.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他在巴塞罗那联邦共和中心为工人听众做了一系列讲座。

M4.en: At 17, he said 'I'm now more Communist than ever.'
M4.zh: 17岁时，他说：‘我现在比以往任何时候都更共产主义。’

D18.en: At age 4-6, he tried in vain to buy her from her mother with his piggy-bank savings.
D18.zh: 4到6岁时，他试图用存钱罐里的积蓄从她母亲那里买她，但没成功。

D15.en: At 12, given his love of high places, he spent much time on top of a squat tower at the Pichots.
D15.zh: 12岁时，由于喜欢高处，他常在皮肖家矮塔顶上待着。

D13a.en: He had to throw his son out of the house.
D13a.zh: 他不得不把儿子赶出家门。

D11.en: At 17-18, he drew an amorous couple with their faces left blank.
D11.zh: 17到18岁时，他画了一对情侣，但把他们的脸留白了。

D3.en: He said he didn't believe in following one particular school.
D3.zh: 他说他不相信追随某一特定流派。

D13b.en: His parents sent him to Colegio de San Rafael after he recovered.
D13b.zh: 他病好后，父母送他去了更现代的圣拉斐尔学校。

D2.en: He agreed to go to school only if he could draw as much as he liked.
D2.zh: 他同意上学，条件是到了学校可以随便画画。

D1.en: Defeat made him more determined to go back to Paris and prevail.
D1.zh: 失败让他更决心回巴黎并取得成功。

D7.en: He told someone to tell Opisso to send Gaudi and the Sagrada Familia to hell.
D7.zh: 他让人告诉奥皮索，叫他把高迪和圣家堂送去见鬼。

D14.en: He made sketches of people.
D14.zh: 他画了人物速写。

D17.en: At 23, he was carrying a kitten he rescued from a storm to his studio.
D17.zh: 23岁时，他抱着从暴风雨中救出的小猫去工作室。

D4.en: He sat up in bed and sketched the floor of his room.
D4.zh: 他坐在床上画了房间的地板。

D9.en: He was listed as art editor of the first issue of Arte Joven.
D9.zh: 他被列为《青年艺术》创刊号的艺术编辑。

M1.en: He entered competitive works for exhibitions at Els 4 Gats and the Paris Universal Exhibition.
M1.zh: 他提交了竞争作品，参加四只猫咖啡馆和巴黎世界博览会的展览。

D13a.en: At 20-21, he was able to return to Barcelona thanks to funds from Malaga.
D13a.zh: 20到21岁时，他因马拉加寄来的钱得以返回巴塞罗那。

M4.en: He found the thought of an obligatory interruption to his artistic development intolerable.
M4.zh: 他觉得被迫中断艺术发展的想法完全无法忍受。

D18.en: He tried doing illustrations for magazines to finance his Paris trip.
D18.zh: 他尝试为杂志画插图来资助他的巴黎之行。

D20.en: He described himself on official forms as a pupil of Don Antonio Muñoz Degrain, not his father.
D20.zh: 他在官方表格上自称是唐·安东尼奥·穆尼奥斯·德格拉因的学生，而非他父亲的学生。

M2.en: He saw fulfilling those dreams as a point of family honor and a debt for financial sacrifices.
M2.zh: 他认为实现那些梦想是家族荣誉，也是对经济牺牲的回报。

D12.en: By age nine he was handling all his grandfather’s correspondence.
D12.zh: 九岁时他就处理祖父的所有信件。

D5.en: At 20, he had his work supported by sales of an electric belt.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他的作品靠销售电腰带获得支持。

D10.en: At 14, he displayed a lifelong phobic reaction to illness.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他表现出对疾病的恐惧反应，并持续一生。

M7.en: At 18-19, he saw fulfilling dreams as a family honor and debt repayment.
M7.zh: 18到19岁时，他认为实现梦想是家庭荣誉和偿还债务。

D15.en: He may have gone to Holland that summer, the only such trip in his life.
D15.zh: 他可能那年夏天去了荷兰，这是他一生中唯一一次这样的旅行。

D8.en: At 17-18, he accepted Sabartés' adulation as his due and caricatured him.
D8.zh: 17到18岁时，他接受萨巴特斯的崇拜视为理所当然，并把他画成颓废诗人。

D19.en: At 18-19, he elaborated with intense concentration the intellectual sap he extracted.
D19.zh: 18到19岁时，他极度专注地阐述他汲取的思想精华。

D20X.en: At 19-20, he left for Paris before the Sala Parés exhibition opened.
D20X.zh: 19到20岁时，他在萨拉·帕雷斯展览开幕前就动身去了巴黎。

M5.en: At 18-19, he proclaimed himself peer by doing portraits of celebrated figures.
M5.zh: 18到19岁时，他通过为名人画肖像来宣称自己是他们的同辈。

M6.en: At 23, he left for Paris on April 12, 1904, with Sebastià Junyer-Vidal.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他于1904年4月12日与塞巴斯蒂安·朱尼耶-维达尔一起前往巴黎。

M3.en: At 19-20, he beckoned Sabartés back to Barcelona, missing his hero worship.
M3.zh: 19到20岁时，他召唤萨巴特斯回巴塞罗那，因为想念他的崇拜。

M8.en: As a small child, he said he could have stayed there forever drawing without stopping.
M8.zh: 幼年时，他说他本可以永远待在那里不停地画。

D11.en: He painted The Wrecked Vessel, an atmospheric beachscape on a rainy summer day.
D11.zh: 他画了《失事船》，一幅夏日雨天的氛围海滩风景。

D15.en: He could study murals in the Sorbonne and the Panthéon without getting his feet wet.
D15.zh: 他能在索邦和先贤祠研究壁画而不弄湿脚。

D14.en: He showed his paintings in that autumn's studio exhibition and had more success.
D14.zh: 他在那年秋季的工作室展览中展出画作，获得了更多成功。

D16.en: He wrote her a long letter from Madrid.
D16.zh: 他从马德里给她写了一封长信。

D4.en: He went out into the country in search of landscapes, as he had done before.
D4.zh: 他像以前一样去乡下寻找风景。

D13a.en: At 20-21, he labored patiently under an academic guide for two years.
D13a.zh: 20到21岁时，他耐心地跟随一位学术导师工作了两年。

M4.en: He abandoned his early Catholicism and adopted the conventional atheism of his peers.
M4.zh: 他放弃了早年的天主教信仰，接受了同龄人常见的无神论。

M2.en: He never ceased to regard himself as an exile and an outsider.
M2.zh: 他始终认为自己是一个流亡者和局外人。

M1.en: He passed out of San Carlos and was awarded a bursary to study in Europe.
M1.zh: 他毕业于圣卡洛斯学院，并获得奖学金赴欧洲学习。

D9.en: He started a portrait of Angelina in the manner of Puvis de Chavannes.
D9.zh: 他开始以皮维·德·夏凡纳的风格画安吉丽娜的肖像。

D20.en: He was a member of the Revolutionary Union of Technical Workers, Painters and Sculptors.
D20.zh: 他是革命技术工人、画家和雕塑家联盟的成员。

M6.en: He never ceased to regard himself as an exile and an outsider.
M6.zh: 他始终认为自己是一个流亡者和局外人。

D19.en: He spent weeks copying Spanish masters and Brueghel, Cranach, Bosch, Patenir.
D19.zh: 他花了几周临摹西班牙大师以及布勒哲尔、克拉纳赫、博斯和帕蒂尼尔的作品。

D8.en: He abandoned thoughts of Rome or Spain and settled in Paris.
D8.zh: 他放弃了去罗马或回西班牙的念头，在巴黎定居下来。

D17.en: He was working too hard to entertain his mother.
D17.zh: 他工作太忙，无法招待母亲。

D20X.en: He tended to isolate himself from the Mexican community in Madrid.
D20X.zh: 他倾向于与马德里的墨西哥社区隔离。

M3.en: He kept a reproduction of El Greco's Burial on his studio wall in Paris until 1915.
M3.zh: 他在巴黎的工作室里一直挂着埃尔·格列柯《葬礼》的复制品，直到1915年。

M8.en: He set a firm boundary at the threshold.
M8.zh: 他在门槛处划清了界限。

D1.en: He completed long academic training but failed to find his own style.
D1.zh: 他完成了长期的学术训练，但未能找到自己的风格。

D18.en: His sole interest in railway timetables was selecting landscapes.
D18.zh: 他对铁路时刻表的唯一兴趣是选择风景。

D2.en: He was singled out for a religious education, which he grew to despise.
D2.zh: 他被选中接受宗教教育，他对此越来越厌恶。

D7.en: He grew to despise the religious education he was singled out for.
D7.zh: 他越来越厌恶自己被选中接受的宗教教育。

D6.en: He joined the circle of Milton Avery, finding his place in this informal community.
D6.zh: 他加入了米尔顿·埃弗里的圈子，在这个非正式团体中找到了自己的位置。

D9.en: He joined the Artists’ Union and participated in the first American Artists’ Congress.
D9.zh: 他加入了艺术家工会，并参加了第一届美国艺术家大会。

D11.en: He kept a notebook in which he composed poems, a story, and a play in Hebrew.
D11.zh: 他有一个笔记本，里面用希伯来语写了诗、一个故事和一个剧本。

M1.en: He went to Yale hoping to become a labor lawyer.
M1.zh: 他去耶鲁大学，希望成为一名劳工律师。

D3.en: He was an avid reader, especially engaged by the Greeks and by the theater.
D3.zh: 他是个如饥似渴的读者，尤其对希腊作品和戏剧感兴趣。

D16.en: He was absorbed in politics, classical music, and debating.
D16.zh: 他专注于政治、古典音乐和辩论。

D18.en: He started his own lubricating-oil business and built several gas stations.
D18.zh: 他创办了自己的润滑油生意，并建了几家加油站。

M4.en: He believed art was a noble ideal he could freely choose.
M4.zh: 他认为艺术是一个他可以自由选择的崇高理想。

M7.en: He took political stances and fought as a young rebel.
M7.zh: 他采取政治立场，像年轻叛逆者一样战斗。

D14.en: He was included in his first group exhibition at the Opportunity Gallery.
D14.zh: 他参加了在机会画廊的首次群展。

D4.en: He enrolled at the New School of Design, a commercial art school.
D4.zh: 他进入新设计学校，一所商业艺术学校。

D20.en: He started a debate club in high school.
D20.zh: 他在高中创办了辩论俱乐部。

M5.en: He refused to sign a release, declaring 'I will see you in hell.'
M5.zh: 他拒绝签署免责书，宣称‘我会在地狱见你。’

M6.en: At 18-22, he decided to become a painter.
M6.zh: 18到22岁间，他决定成为一名画家。

D5.en: He pointed an unloaded revolver at Browne.
D5.zh: 他用一把没装子弹的左轮手枪指着布朗。

D10.en: At 17, he felt emptiness and despair that would cause lifelong depressions.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他感到空虚和绝望，这导致了他一生的抑郁。

D15.en: As a child and teen, he hiked through Portland hills and climbed to the top.
D15.zh: 他小时候和十几岁时喜欢徒步穿越波特兰的山丘，爬到山顶眺望山谷。

D20X.en: At 25, he refused to sign a release and declared 'I will see you in hell.'
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他拒绝签署免责声明，并宣称‘我会在地狱见你’。

M3.en: He became a fanatic on the work of Milton.
M3.zh: 他成了弥尔顿作品的狂热爱好者。

D18.en: He thought he might make a little money from a book.
D18.zh: 他觉得自己可能从一本书上赚点钱。

D11.en: He saw Guernica and was awakened to abstract painting's power.
D11.zh: 他看到《格尔尼卡》，被抽象画的力量唤醒。

D4.en: He helped organize a group and set up a furnace to fire their work.
D4.zh: 他组织了一个小组，安排了一个窑炉来烧制他们的作品。

D2.en: He attacked his work and the obstacles seemed to yield.
D2.zh: 他全力投入工作，障碍似乎退让了。

D1.en: After a year in Hinton's cast drawing class, he was determined to break out.
D1.zh: 在欣顿的石膏素描班待了一整年后，他决心突破。

D6.en: He and others formed a group and set up a furnace for firing their work.
D6.zh: 他和别人组织了一个小组，并安排了一个窑炉来烧制他们的作品。

M6.en: He visited Ojai, read theosophical tracts, and shared his findings with his father.
M6.zh: 他去了奥海，读了神智学著作，并与父亲分享他的发现。

D13b.en: At 6, his father LeRoy auctioned off animals and sold the farm.
D13b.zh: 6岁时，他父亲勒罗伊拍卖了牲口并卖掉了农场。

D3.en: As a small child, he waited eagerly in a roadside ditch every day for his father's car.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他每天急切地等在路边的沟里，等着父亲的车来。

M3.en: At 18, he idolized his brother Charles and wanted to do what Charles did.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他崇拜哥哥查尔斯，想和他做一样的事。

D10.en: At 25, he was arrested for disorderly conduct after drunkenly chasing girls on his bicycle.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他因醉酒骑自行车追女孩、行为不检而被捕。

D15.en: At 10, he said, 'I killed one on the run a few days ago.'
D15.zh: 10岁时，他说：“我前几天在奔跑中杀了一个。”

D7.en: At 19, he was suspended from the Academy for painting figures without permission.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他因未经许可画人物被学院停学。

D8.en: At 18, he delighted in rousing his friend to anger and signed a yearbook 'For more and better arguments. Hugo Pollock.'
D8.zh: 18岁时，他喜欢激怒朋友，在年鉴上签名“为了更多更好的争论。雨果·波洛克。”

D13a.en: He told someone to find another place to live.
D13a.zh: 他告诉别人另找住处。

D20.en: At 20, he shared a studio with a group of friends on Fifth Avenue at Fifteenth Street.
D20.zh: 20岁时，他和一群朋友合租了第五大道第十五街的一个工作室。

M1.en: At 22, he worked conscientiously and never loafed when committed.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他做事认真负责，一旦承诺就不会偷懒。

D16.en: At 19, he angrily blurted out, "What do we need those Europeans for?"
D16.zh: 19岁时，他愤怒地脱口而出：“我们要那些欧洲人干什么？”

M2.en: At 20, he made a transparent effort at reconciliation after a confrontation.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他在一次冲突后明显试图和解。

D12.en: He felt duty-bound to support those who materially obliged him.
D12.zh: 他觉得有责任帮助那些在物质上帮助过他的人。

D4.en: At 24, he worked in Paris, feeling something essential still alive.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他在巴黎工作，感到某种本质的东西仍然活着。

D11.en: As a child, he repeatedly repainted a scene that became commonplace to him.
D11.zh: 他小时候反复重画一个对他来说变得平常的场景。

D2.en: At 13-14, he trained with forty-pound weights to build muscle like his father.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他用40磅的哑铃锻炼，想练出像父亲那样的肌肉。

D1.en: At 21, he was called for military service, disrupting his art training for three years.
D1.zh: 21岁时他被征召入伍，中断了艺术训练三年。

M2.en: He studied to win the Rosenfelds' approval through his achievements.
M2.zh: 他努力学习，想用成绩赢得罗森菲尔德家的认可。

M4.en: He sent works done in Paris, desiring to remember Russia.
M4.zh: 他寄出在巴黎创作的作品，渴望记住俄罗斯。

M7.en: As a teenager, he began writing poetry in Russian and considered art as an escape from his parents' constrained lives.
M7.zh: 十几岁时他开始用俄语写诗，并考虑把艺术作为逃离父母压抑生活的途径。

D14.en: Chagall sent a cubist painting to an exhibition in Moscow in March 1912.
D14.zh: 1912年3月，夏加尔送了一幅立体主义画作到莫斯科参展。

D16.en: At 13, he stammered 'Ta-ta-ta-' when asked about the Tartar occupation.
D16.zh: 13岁时，当被问及鞑靼人占领时，他结结巴巴地说“嗒-嗒-嗒-”。

D18.en: At 16-19, he worked for three years for an Old Believer jewellery merchant in Nizhni-Novgorod.
D18.zh: 16到19岁间，他在下诺夫哥罗德为一位旧礼仪派珠宝商人工作了三年。

D5.en: At 24, he said he had to do it because it was his duty to persons he was materially obliged to.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他说他必须这样做，因为他对一些在物质上帮助过他的人有责任。

D7.en: At 20-23, he deliberately refused to give any ready examples for imitation or helpful theories.
D7.zh: 20到23岁间，他故意不提供任何现成的模仿例子或有用的理论。

D10.en: At 24, he hated Paris and all contemporary art, though he was half French.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他讨厌巴黎和所有当代艺术，尽管他有一半法国血统。

M6.en: He hazily considered art as an escape from his parents' constrained lives.
M6.zh: 他模糊地考虑将艺术作为逃离父母受束缚的生活的途径。

M8.en: At 16, he converted to Lutheranism in Finland.
M8.zh: 16岁时，他在芬兰改信路德教。

D6.en: At 20, he finished Gymnasium in 1907.
D6.zh: 20岁时，他在1907年完成了中学学业。

D17.en: At 21, he was anxious during Ida's serious stomach operation.
D17.zh: 21岁时，他对伊达的严重胃手术感到非常焦虑。

D2.en: At 24, he painted outdoors at Madam Jammes' and didn't need a studio yet.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他在詹姆夫人那里画户外画，还不需要画室。

D9.en: At 20, a classmate wrote a verse about him at school.
D9.zh: 20岁时，同学在学校编了首关于他的诗。

D18.en: He began working part-time as an illustrator for a New York ad agency.
D18.zh: 他开始在纽约一家广告公司做兼职插画师。

D6.en: He went with two others to Sèvres by boat, and wanted to visit many places if the weather were fine.
D6.zh: 他和另外两人坐船去塞夫尔，如果天气好，他想去很多地方。

D11.en: He made a pen-and-ink sketch of Charles Dana Gibson.
D11.zh: 他画了一幅查尔斯·达纳·吉布森的钢笔素描。

D12.en: As a teenager, he helped out in the store after school.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他放学后帮忙看店。

D7.en: He wrote a letter to his father on May 7, 1907.
D7.zh: 他于1907年5月7日给父亲写了一封信。

D13a.en: At 24, he reluctantly answered his mother's persistent inquiries.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他勉强回复了母亲的一再追问。

D10.en: At 18, he was described as long, skinny, awkward, and grimacing.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他被描述为又高又瘦、笨拙、表情扭曲。

M3.en: At 19-21, he produced sketches after other artists.
M3.zh: 19到21岁时，他临摹其他艺术家的作品。

M6.en: Seven years of intense practice and exchange transformed his generic talent into a vocation for painting.
M6.zh: 七年的密集练习和交流将他普通的天赋转化为绘画事业。

D14.en: He grew a moustache for two weeks.
D14.zh: 他留了两周胡子。

D6.en: He went home to see family and go dancing with friends.
D6.zh: 他回家看望家人，和朋友去跳舞。

D11.en: He planned a theatrical production about Vesuvius with Léon.
D11.zh: 他和莱昂策划了一部关于维苏威火山爆发的戏剧。

D15.en: He slipped on grease and mud outside her house and got injured.
D15.zh: 他在她家外面的油污和泥巴上滑倒受伤。

D14.en: He married Amélie Parayre on 10 January 1898.
D14.zh: 他于1898年1月10日与阿梅莉·帕雷尔结婚。

D9.en: He proposed studying in Paris for preliminary law exams.
D9.zh: 他提议去巴黎学习，准备初级法律考试。

D7.en: Only a family of cousins welcomed him as a renegade.
D7.zh: 只有一家表亲欢迎他这个叛逆者。

D2.en: He decided to halve his outgoings to ensure a second year in Paris.
D2.zh: 他决定削减一半开支，以确保在巴黎的第二年。

D5.en: At 14, he challenged and defeated the legendary hypnotist Donato.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他挑战并击败了传奇催眠师多纳托。

M1.en: From age 22 to 32, he worked obsessively on color, reaching his peak as a painter.
M1.zh: 22到32岁间，他痴迷于色彩问题，达到了作为画家的高峰。

D10.en: At 15-17, he had regular bouts of illness, bedridden for up to eight weeks.
D10.zh: 15到17岁时，他经常生病，每次卧床长达八周。

D13a.en: At 15-17, he obediently returned to school for two more years.
D13a.zh: 15到17岁时，他顺从地回到学校，又读了两年。

D1.en: At 25-26, after his portrait was rejected in 1895, the state bought his picture in 1896.
D1.zh: 25到26岁时，他的肖像画1895年被拒，但1896年国家买下了他的画。

D13b.en: At 22, he studied evenings for a teaching diploma at the École des Arts Décoratifs.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他晚上在装饰艺术学校学习，考取教师资格。

D18.en: At about 13, he taught Amélie to shoot and think for herself.
D18.zh: 大约13岁时，他教阿梅莉射击和独立思考。

D17.en: As a small child, he was brought up by his sister.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他由姐姐抚养长大。

D3.en: He visited the museum and was impressed by Roman paintings and bronzes.
D3.zh: 他参观了博物馆，对罗马绘画和青铜器印象深刻。

D4.en: He took Max Ernst to Maloja where both carved and painted boulders.
D4.zh: 他带马克斯·恩斯特去马洛亚，两人一起雕刻和彩绘巨石。

D2.en: He received an elite education specializing in philosophy and graduated in 1941.
D2.zh: 他接受了精英教育，专攻哲学，并于1941年毕业。

D5.en: He ran after prostitutes, spending his father's money on them and in cafés.
D5.zh: 他追妓女，把父亲的钱花在她们身上和咖啡馆里。

D6.en: He was invited by Breton to join the Surrealist group after seeing his Suspended Ball.
D6.zh: 布勒东看到他的《悬浮球》后邀请他加入超现实主义团体。

D9.en: He bought paints and canvases and encouraged his brother to use them.
D9.zh: 他买了颜料和画布，鼓励弟弟使用它们。

D1.en: Accused of theft, he would become a thief and make theft a moral imperative.
D1.zh: 被指控偷窃后，他成了小偷，并把偷窃视为道德义务。

D16.en: He said he would give his entire lifework for one conversation.
D16.zh: 他说他愿意用毕生作品换一次对话。

M6.en: At 24, he glimpsed the power of the creative will toward its destiny.
M6.zh: 24岁时，他瞥见了创造意志实现其命运的力量。

D17.en: In his early Paris years, he was vigilantly protective of Diego, showing care like for no one else.
D17.zh: 在巴黎早期，他警惕地保护着迭戈，表现出对他人从未有过的关心。

D8.en: In the 1920s, he went to unusual lengths to demonstrate he was her master.
D8.zh: 1920年代，他不遗余力地证明自己是她的主人。

D6.en: He and Pearlstein shared a loft with dance therapist Franziska Boas.
D6.zh: 他和珀尔斯坦与舞蹈治疗师弗朗西斯卡·博厄斯合住一个阁楼。

D5.en: He abandoned the subject for decorative images like cherubs and butterflies.
D5.zh: 他放弃了主题，转而画小天使和蝴蝶等装饰性图像。

D4.en: He watched Mickey Mouse and drew a lot after getting ideas.
D4.zh: 他看米老鼠，有了想法后就画很多。

D12.en: He moved into bed with his mother in the dining room while his father slept upstairs.
D12.zh: 他搬到餐厅和母亲同睡，而父亲睡在楼上。

D2.en: Miss Vickermann got him admitted to Saturday drawing classes at the Carnegie Institute.
D2.zh: 维克尔曼小姐让他进入卡内基学院的周六绘画班。

D14.en: He wore a suit with mismatched shoes, a paint-dipped tie, and bright fingernails.
D14.zh: 他穿着西装，鞋子不配对，领带蘸了颜料，指甲涂得鲜艳。

D20X.en: At 22, he parted company with Philip when Philip was about to marry.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他在菲利普即将结婚时与其分道扬镳。

D13b.en: He was christened and confirmed within weeks of birth.
D13b.zh: 他在出生后几周内接受了洗礼和坚信礼。

D8.en: In high school, he pointed at Bill Shaffer and said 'Hee hee hee hee hee hee.'
D8.zh: 高中时，他指着比尔·谢弗说‘嘻嘻嘻嘻嘻嘻’。

M6.en: He developed his trademark illustration style while at Carnegie Tech.
M6.zh: 他在卡内基理工学院时形成了标志性的插画风格。

M7.en: He worked night and day drawing, tracing, and blotting.
M7.zh: 他夜以继日地画画、描摹、吸墨。

M8.en: He prayed at a seven-thirty mass most mornings.
M8.zh: 他大多数早晨在七点半的弥撒上祈祷。

M1.en: He won his first Art Directors' Club gold medal.
M1.zh: 他赢得了第一个艺术指导俱乐部金奖。

D13a.en: His father threatened to smash his Super-8 camera if he took the job.
D13a.zh: 他父亲威胁说，如果他接受那份工作就砸了他的超8摄影机。

D11.en: He presented a blank white sheet with a splat of blood-red paint.
D11.zh: 他交了一张白纸，中间有一大块血红色颜料。

D18.en: He worked a summer job for three dollars a day, three or four mornings a week.
D18.zh: 他暑假打工，每周三四个早上，每天三美元。

D19.en: At 19, he sorted things out, putting cans with cans and paintings with paintings.
D19.zh: 19岁时，他把罐头和画作分类整理。

M2.en: At age 13-17, he aimed to fulfill his father's prediction and go to college.
M2.zh: 13到17岁时，他立志实现父亲的预言去上大学。

D10.en: He persistently tried to come to terms with the memory of his dying mother.
D10.zh: 他不断试图与母亲临终的记忆和解。

D3.en: He started on his long journey of exploration, writing almost as much as he painted.
D3.zh: 他开始了漫长的探索之旅，写作几乎和绘画一样多。

D6.en: He sat in 'the Grand' with Hans Jaeger, who was to be jailed the next day.
D6.zh: 他和汉斯·耶格坐在‘格兰德’咖啡馆，耶格第二天就要进监狱。

D7.en: He charged art materials against his father's credit at the art supplier.
D7.zh: 他用自己的父亲在美术用品店的赊账购买了材料。

D2.en: Since the end of August he had been attending drawing school fairly regularly.
D2.zh: 从八月底开始，他相当规律地去上绘画学校。

D18.en: At 18, he sent three of his smaller works to an auction house where they sold off a whole lot of paintings.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他把自己三幅较小的作品送到拍卖行，那里卖掉了一大批画。

D4.en: At 18, he finished a picture for his Munch aunts.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他完成了一幅画送给他的蒙克阿姨们。

D16.en: At 23, he said, 'We cannot all paint nails and twigs.'
D16.zh: 23岁时，他说：‘我们不可能都画钉子和树枝。’

D1.en: At 19, after failing to place his drawings, he said he 'will draw a few smaller scenes of the Kristiania district.'
D1.zh: 19岁时，投稿失败后，他说他‘会画几幅克里斯蒂安尼亚区的小场景’。

D14.en: At 20, he had his first picture publicly exhibited: Study of a head.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他的第一幅画《头像习作》公开展出。

D8.en: At 23, Wentzel screamed 'Pretentious rubbish!' in my face, but Ludwig Meyer congratulated me on a great painting.
D8.zh: 23岁时，文策尔冲我脸上喊‘华而不实的垃圾！’，但路德维希·迈耶祝贺我画了一幅伟大的画。

D17.en: At 23, he believed few painters experienced the full grief of their subject as he did in The Sick Child.
D17.zh: 23岁时，他相信很少有画家像他在《病孩》中那样体验过主题的全部悲伤。

D20X.en: He never made copies of his work, only reused subjects for artistic reasons.
D20X.zh: 他从未复制自己的作品，只为艺术原因重复使用主题。

M2.en: He wrote countless letters to his aunt Karen expressing gratitude and filial devotion.
M2.zh: 他给姨妈凯伦写了无数封信，表达感激和孝顺之情。

D5.en: He became violent, involved in brawls, fist fights, and public scandals.
D5.zh: 他变得暴力，参与斗殴、拳击和公开丑闻。

D19.en: He painted friends and literary men, and was inspired to paint The Meadow of Flowers.
D19.zh: 他画朋友和文人，并受启发画了《花草地》。

D13b.en: He was brought up to respect superiors and lead a truly Christian life.
D13b.zh: 他从小被教育尊重上级，过真正的基督徒生活。

M1.en: He was awarded Schäffer's Legacy of 500 kroner in 1884.
M1.zh: 1884年他获得谢弗遗赠500克朗。

D7.en: At 11, he ran away from home for good after two previous attempts.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他两次离家出走后最终彻底离开家。

D2.en: At 22, he made a bust of Emperor Vitellius after the antique and won an honorable mention.
D2.zh: 22岁时，他仿古制作了维特利乌斯皇帝头像，获得了荣誉提名。

M1.en: At 19-22, he excelled in woodworking and sculpture, earning high grades.
M1.zh: 19到22岁间，他擅长木工和雕塑，各科成绩优异。

D3.en: At 22-25, he had an excellent voice and interests in music, folklore, math, biology, and physics.
D3.zh: 22到25岁间，他嗓音出色，对民间音乐、民俗、数学、生物和物理感兴趣。

D4.en: At 16, he made his own violin, understanding its principles without instruction.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他自制了一把小提琴，无师自通其原理。

D7.en: At 16-17, he presented himself to Frank Furness and asked for a job.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他去找弗兰克·弗内斯求职。

D2.en: At 14-16, he established a reputation as a brilliant scholar at Boston English High School.
D2.zh: 14到16岁间，他在波士顿英语高中建立了杰出学者的声誉。

D13b.en: At 14-24, he dropped out of college and repeatedly started and quit medical training.
D13b.zh: 14到24岁间，他辍学并多次开始又放弃学医。

D11.en: At 22-42, he would reorganize furniture or knock down walls for aesthetic effect.
D11.zh: 22到42岁间，他会重新布置家具或拆墙以追求美学效果。

D1.en: At 24, he graduated from Madison College (now Colgate University).
D1.zh: 24岁时，他从麦迪逊学院（现科尔盖特大学）毕业。

D5.en: At 16-17, he boldly visited Richard Morris Hunt without an introduction.
D5.zh: 16到17岁时，他大胆地未经介绍就去拜访了理查德·莫里斯·亨特。

D10.en: At age 7-9, he slid backward down the attic stair believing himself dying.
D10.zh: 7到9岁时，他向后滑下阁楼楼梯，以为自己快死了。

D15.en: Around age 20, he walked twenty miles a day as a young athlete.
D15.zh: 大约20岁时，他作为年轻运动员每天步行二十英里。

M1.en: At 18-20, he performed poorly even in courses he might have enjoyed.
M1.zh: 18到20岁时，他即使在自己可能喜欢的课程上也表现不佳。

M2.en: As a teenager, he grew up with a family tradition valuing education like godliness.
M2.zh: 十几岁时，他在视教育如敬神的家庭传统中长大。

D18.en: At 12, he started peddling Larkin Company soap on Brooklyn streets.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他开始在布鲁克林街头兜售拉金公司的肥皂。

D14.en: At 20, he attended his first ballet at the Chicago Opera House for one dollar.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他花一美元在芝加哥歌剧院第一次看了芭蕾舞表演。

D16.en: At 16-17, he sang a song of praise like a youthful bard to his lord.
D16.zh: 16到17岁时，他像年轻的吟游诗人一样向他的领主唱赞歌。

D19.en: He obsessively kept records of all kinds.
D19.zh: 他痴迷于保存各种记录。

D12.en: She traveled with Joséphine and took on the burden of nursing her.
D12.zh: 她和约瑟芬一起旅行，并承担了照顾她的责任。

D7.en: She countered her father's displays by throwing a tantrum.
D7.zh: 她通过发脾气来反抗父亲的表演。

D4.en: She made clay figures copied from casts of ancient statues and took a sketch class.
D4.zh: 她用黏土复制古代雕像的石膏模型，并上了素描课。

D3.en: I was very interested in the spiral of the shaving, its form and trembling quality.
D3.zh: 我对刨花的螺旋形状和颤抖质感很感兴趣。

D11.en: At 10, she molded a figure of her father from bread and spit, then cut off its limbs with a knife.
D11.zh: 10岁时，她用面包和口水捏了一个她父亲的泥人，然后用刀切掉了四肢。

D10.en: She felt humiliated by his teasing and ashamed not to be the boy he wanted.
D10.zh: 她因他的取笑感到羞辱，并为自己不是他想要的男孩而羞愧。

D2.en: As a translator, she was allowed to join the classes.
D2.zh: 作为翻译，她被允许参加课程。

M1.en: I was an overachiever.
M1.zh: 我是个成绩超常的人。

M2.en: I was supposed to make myself forgiven for being a girl.
M2.zh: 我应该为自己是个女孩而寻求原谅。

D13a.en: She studied diligently for her mother and accompanied her father to cabarets.
D13a.zh: 她为母亲勤奋学习，并陪父亲去夜总会。

D12.en: As a small child, Albert began practicing intensely.
D12.zh: 幼年时，阿尔伯特开始刻苦练习。

D2.en: At 20, he worked five hours a day, leaving time to study.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他每天工作五小时，留出时间学习。

D11.en: At 25, he dreamed of a Biberstein autumn with deep puddles of blood.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他梦见比伯施泰因的秋天，血泊深不见底。

D13b.en: At 14, he enrolled in the Ecole d'Art as a first step toward watch engraving.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他进入拉绍德封艺术学校，作为学习钟表雕刻的第一步。

D3.en: At 20, he visited a Chartreuse and found a unique solution to workers' housing, envying the monks.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他参观了一所修道院，发现了一个独特的工人住房解决方案，羡慕那些僧侣。

D4.en: At 19-20, he spent hours doing watercolours and sketches of art.
D4.zh: 19到20岁时，他花大量时间画水彩和速写艺术。

D1.en: At 25, he said: 'I spoil everything in ten minutes and yet I don’t give up.'
D1.zh: 25岁时，他说：‘十分钟内我搞砸一切，但我没有放弃。’

D5.en: At 19-20, he set off for Italy with Villa Fallet fees in his pocket.
D5.zh: 19到20岁时，他带着法莱别墅的报酬动身去意大利。

D6.en: At 24, he decided to embark on a long trip with a friend he met in Munich.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他决定与在慕尼黑认识的朋友开始长途旅行。

D7.en: He cut ties with the Perrets and postponed his return until his money ran out.
D7.zh: 他与佩雷家族断绝关系，推迟返回直到钱花光。

D13a.en: He wrote to L'Eplattenier blaming him for the impracticality of his early education.
D13a.zh: 他写信给莱普拉特尼埃，指责他早期教育不切实际。

D15.en: As a small child, he went on strenuous hikes with his family to beautiful views.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他和家人一起进行艰苦的远足，到达壮丽的景色。

M3.en: At 20, Orcagna became his lifelong hero.
M3.zh: 20岁时，奥尔卡尼亚成为他终生的英雄。

M1.en: At 16, he hammered a copper relief of Dante and won the school's top prize.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他锤制了但丁的铜浮雕，获得学校最高奖。

D10.en: At 22, he used senseless allegories and strained humor bordering on hallucinatory.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他使用无意义的寓言和近乎幻觉的勉强幽默。

M4.en: At 25, he decided that making beauty for others was all that mattered.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他决定为他人创造美才是最重要的。

M6.en: At 23, he said he was obeying his destiny by leaving everything to travel.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他说他服从命运，放弃一切去旅行。

M8.en: At 23, he felt gloriously connected to the larger world.
M8.zh: 23岁时，他感到自己与更广阔的世界辉煌地相连。

M2.en: At 25, he said, "I have a papa and a mama."
M2.zh: 25岁时，他说：“我有一个爸爸和一个妈妈。”

D18.en: At 25, he got his family into deeper water with a new house, an enormous blunder.
D18.zh: 25岁时，他因新房子让家人陷入困境，是个大错误。

D17.en: At 25, he got his family into deeper water with a new house, an enormous blunder.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他因新房子让家人陷入困境，是个大错误。

D20X.en: At 25, he acted as if obliged to return from Greece to assume responsibility for both parents because Albert was away.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他表现得好像不得不从希腊回来承担对父母的责任，因为阿尔伯特不在。

M7.en: Around 30, he said, "I see the happy days shining anew, and I wrest myself from the spleen of those days of terrible cold."
M7.zh: 大约30岁时，他说：“我看到快乐的日子重新闪耀，我把自己从那些严寒日子的忧郁中挣脱出来。”

D12.en: She spent the winter at home with her younger siblings.
D12.zh: 她冬天在家照顾弟弟妹妹。

D11.en: At 18, she dreamed of being a dancer and leading the cotillion with the best-looking man.
D11.zh: 18岁时，她梦想成为舞者，与最帅的男人领舞。

D6.en: She got to know all of her husband's friends.
D6.zh: 她认识了丈夫的所有朋友。

D8.en: At 16-18, she thought she could think for others and get them to do what she wanted.
D8.zh: 16到18岁时，她觉得自己可以替别人思考，让他们做她想做的事。

D2.en: For a year, she labored at dispiriting commercial work in a cold urban environment.
D2.zh: 一年里，她在寒冷的城市环境中辛苦做商业工作。

D14.en: At 18, she dreamed of being a dancer and leading the cotillion with the best-looking man.
D14.zh: 18岁时，她梦想成为舞者，与最帅的男人领舞。

D10.en: She contracted typhoid and was confined to bed for four months.
D10.zh: 她感染了伤寒，卧床四个月。

D9.en: At 18, she was art editor of the yearbook and illustrated it with ink-and-white pen drawings.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她担任年鉴的美术编辑，并用黑白钢笔插画进行绘制。

M1.en: At 17-18, she ranked first in her class in February after being fifth and seventh.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，她在二月班级排名第一，此前是第五和第七。

D16.en: At 24, when asked why she didn't curl her hair, she replied, "Probably not my style."
D16.zh: 24岁时，被问为什么不卷头发，她回答：“可能不是我的风格。”

D1.en: At 22, unable to continue with demanding closeup illustration work, she returned to Virginia.
D1.zh: 22岁时，无法继续高强度的特写插画工作，她回到了弗吉尼亚。

D7.en: At 20-21, he refused Bruno Paul's offer to work in his office, surprising Paul with his nerve.
D7.zh: 20到21岁时，他拒绝了布鲁诺·保罗让他去自己办公室工作的提议，保罗对他的胆量感到惊讶。

D6.en: At 22, the Riehls sent him on a six-week tour from Germany to Italy at their expense.
D6.zh: 22岁时，里尔夫妇出资送他进行了为期六周的德国至意大利之旅。

D1.en: At 25, he realized Behrens' greatness and returned to work for him after being separated.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他认识到贝伦斯的伟大，并决定回到他那里工作。

D12.en: At 12, he attended vocational school.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他上了职业学校。

D13a.en: At 12, he attended vocational school.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，他上了职业学校。

D19.en: He was known for his preciseness.
D19.zh: 他以精确著称。

D12.en: She worked part-time while studying at the university to support herself.
D12.zh: 她在政法大学半工半读，靠做社会工作贴补生活。

D7.en: She was unjustly imprisoned with six others in 1936.
D7.zh: 1936年，她和另外六人无辜被投入监狱。

D9.en: At 24, she joined the national salvation movement after Shen Henglao.
D9.zh: 24岁时，她追随沈衡老之后参加了爱国救亡运动和后来的民主运动。

D5.en: She shouted at soldiers to not be pawns of warlords and charged forward.
D5.zh: 她高声对士兵说不要给军阀当走狗，然后冲上去。

D3.en: She enrolled in Jianghan University to seek truth.
D3.zh: 她考入江汉大学寻找真理。

D3.en: At 23, he discovered Heart of Darkness and Journey to the End of the Night.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他发现了《黑暗之心》和《长夜行》。

D11.en: At 22, he destroyed nearly all his canvases.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他毁掉了几乎所有的画布。

D12.en: At 22, he wrote a testament warning of his imminent demise and setting out his last wishes.
D12.zh: 22岁时，他写了一份遗嘱，警告自己即将离世并列出遗愿。

D13b.en: He chose to study commerce to take over the general direction of the company as soon as possible.
D13b.zh: 他选择学习商科，以便尽快接手公司的总体方向。

D10.en: As a small child, he would roll on the ground or bang his head when annoyed.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他生气时会在地上打滚或撞墙。

D6.en: At 20-21, he became friends with Max Ernst and many others.
D6.zh: 20到21岁时，他和马克斯·恩斯特等人成了朋友。

D4.en: As a teenager, he used a Box Brownie to capture holiday souvenirs.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他用布朗尼相机拍假期纪念照。

D2.en: As a child, he applied himself with complete discipline and devotion to art.
D2.zh: 他小时候对艺术投入了完全的纪律和奉献。

D1.en: At 19, he took the baccalaureate three times and failed each time.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他考了三次学士学位，三次都失败了。

D16.en: He refused to listen to any argument or invocation of surrealism, consumed by destructive fury.
D16.zh: 他不听任何争论或超现实主义的召唤，被破坏性的愤怒吞噬。

D13a.en: He informed his father of his intentions, accompanied by Max Ernst.
D13a.zh: 他在马克斯·恩斯特的陪同下告诉了父亲他的打算。

D9.en: He received his first commission, an unforgettable moment for a man who always refused orders.
D9.zh: 他接到了第一个委托，对于一个从不听命的人，这是难忘的时刻。

D5.en: To shut Henri up, he seized the Leica and threatened to throw it into the Grand Canal.
D5.zh: 为了让亨利闭嘴，他抓起徕卡相机，威胁要扔进大运河。

D15.en: At 22-23, he hunted at night with an acetylene lamp, dried meat, and sold it in villages.
D15.zh: 22到23岁时，他头戴乙炔灯夜间狩猎，把肉晒干后到村里卖。

D18.en: At 22-23, he hunted at night with an acetylene lamp on his forehead, selling dried meat in villages.
D18.zh: 22到23岁间，他头戴乙炔灯夜间狩猎，卖干肉到村庄。

D8.en: He was imperious, as if obeying an urgent command, and brooked no delay.
D8.zh: 他态度专横，仿佛在服从紧急命令，不容任何耽搁。

D14.en: In 1933, Julien Levy offered to show his work at a September exhibition.
D14.zh: 1933年，朱利安·列维提出在九月展览上展出他的作品。

D15.en: He completed a physical training course at Aldershot with Douglas Houghton in summer 1918.
D15.zh: 1918年夏天，他和道格拉斯·霍顿在奥尔德肖特完成了体能训练课程。

D13b.en: His father inflicted violin lessons on him as a protesting youngest son.
D13b.zh: 他父亲强迫他这个小儿子上小提琴课，他当时很不情愿。

D6.en: He was in the Blue house at school.
D6.zh: 他在学校被分在蓝队。

D4.en: He chopped sticks, got water and coal, washed dishes, and shopped.
D4.zh: 他劈柴、打水、取煤、洗碗碟和购物。

D3.en: He spent his second afternoon in the British Museum with Egyptian and Assyrian sculptures.
D3.zh: 他在大英博物馆的第二个下午，看了埃及和亚述雕塑。

D13a.en: At 17, he was assigned as a student teacher to his old elementary school.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他被指派到自己的小学母校做实习教师。

D2.en: He attended evening classes three nights a week during the spring and summer terms.
D2.zh: 他在春夏季学期每周三个晚上上夜校。

D1.en: He was accepted at the second attempt by Castleford Secondary School at age twelve.
D1.zh: 他12岁时第二次尝试才被卡斯尔福德中学录取。

D9.en: At 24, he was among bright students invited to the principal's Sunday open house.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他作为优秀学生之一被邀请参加校长的周日开放活动。

D7.en: At 24, he was briefly detained at a barrier, suspected of being a Bolshevist infiltrator.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他因穿越关卡被短暂拘留，被怀疑是布尔什维克渗透者。

M8.en: At 15-16, he felt so saintly after confirmation that he floated four feet off the ground.
M8.zh: 15到16岁时，他坚信礼后感觉非常圣洁，飘离地面四英尺。

D11.en: At 18, he painted life-sized Shakespearean characters in pastel with friends.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他和朋友用粉笔画了真人大小的莎士比亚人物。

D5.en: At 19-20, he volunteered to return to his old battalion in France shortly before the Armistice.
D5.zh: 19到20岁时，他在停战前夕自愿返回法国的旧部队。

M1.en: At 21, he aimed at Michelangelo and Titian, trying to make up for lost army time.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他以米开朗基罗和提香为目标，试图弥补军队失去的时间。

D10.en: At 19, he showed regret at not getting a medal.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他因未获得奖章而流露出遗憾。

D14.en: At 22, he designed the programme cover and played the role of Bhataryan.
D14.zh: 22岁时，他设计了节目封面并扮演了巴塔里安。

D8.en: At 24, he was piqued that Coxon did not arouse suspicions.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他因考克森没有引起怀疑而不快。

D17.en: As a child, he massaged his mother's hip to ease her lumbago, sparking his interest in three-dimensional form.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他按摩母亲的臀部以缓解她的腰痛，这激发了他对立体形态的兴趣。

D2.en: At 19, he studied hard for final exams, reciting assignments and exercises.
D2.zh: 19岁时，他努力备考，背诵作业和习题。

D3.en: At 10, his exam scores far exceeded the second place, earning teachers' praise.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他的考试成绩远超第二名，得到老师夸奖。

D12.en: At 19, he said he wanted to work and study simultaneously to gain experience.
D12.zh: 19岁时，他说想边工作边读书，从工作中获取经验。

D9.en: At 20, he proposed organizing a Guangdong Independence Association to unite patriots.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他提议组织广东独立协会，联合爱国侨胞。

D6.en: At 20, he and seven others raised glasses to celebrate a new member.
D6.zh: 20岁时，他和另外七人举杯庆祝新成员加入。

D4.en: At 19, he quickly packed his bags and went to Shanghai.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他迅速收拾行囊去了上海。

M4.en: At 20, he said as a Chinese, especially a Cantonese, he must take action.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他说作为中国人尤其是广东人，必须有所行动。

M7.en: At 20, he said the Qing court's repeated cessions of territory were intolerable.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他说清廷屡次割让土地，不能再忍受。

D12.en: He was born in a grocery store in Singapore.
D12.zh: 他在新加坡一家杂货铺里出生。

D1.en: He ignored the cook to avoid suspicion from bandits for over 30 days.
D1.zh: 他不再理会那伙夫，以免引起匪徒怀疑，这样过了30多天。

D5.en: At 13, he ignored the cook to avoid arousing the bandits' suspicion, and 30-plus days passed.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他不理会那伙夫以免引起匪徒怀疑，这样过了30多天。

D13a.en: His father wanted him to study classics but gave up and found another path for him.
D13a.zh: 他父亲想让他苦读经书，但见他不上路，只好另指一条出路。

D3.en: He visited a British gospel church to listen to preachers.
D3.zh: 他常去英国人的福音教堂听牧师传道。

D2.en: He walked with a teacher on church grounds, reciting and making strange sounds.
D2.zh: 他和一位老师在教堂内外散步，用心背诵，发出谁也听不懂的怪音。

D6.en: Pastor Briand welcomed him and enrolled him at St. Paul's College.
D6.zh: 白利安牧师接待了他，介绍他到圣保罗书院就读。

D9.en: He and others decided to use the type to publish a Chinese newspaper.
D9.zh: 他和黄胜等人议决利用这副铅字出版中文报纸。

D4.en: He and others decided to use the type to publish a Chinese newspaper.
D4.zh: 他和黄胜等人议决利用这副铅字出版中文报纸。

D20.en: He and Huang Sheng decided to use the type to publish a Chinese newspaper.
D20.zh: 他和黄胜等人议决利用这副铅字出版中文报纸。

M7.en: He resolved to start a newspaper to enlighten the people and promote their power.
M7.zh: 他决意创办报纸以开民智、宣民力。

M4.en: He angrily wrote to the Hong Kong authorities, denouncing the new gambling ordinance.
M4.zh: 他极为气愤，当即上书港英当局，严正指出新条例公开奖励赌博。

D13b.en: His mother, wife, and father-in-law were Christians.
D13b.zh: 他的母亲、妻子和岳父都是基督徒。

D7.en: He angrily wrote to the Hong Kong authorities, denouncing the new gambling ordinance.
D7.zh: 他极为气愤，当即上书港英当局，严正指出新条例公开奖励赌博。

D18.en: In his spare time, he co-founded the Zhongwai Xinbao, China's first daily newspaper.
D18.zh: 他利用闲暇与友人创办《中外新报》，中国自此有日报。

D11.en: She found her unique method: ink paintings with tiny dots and pen drawings of cellular forms.
D11.zh: 她找到了自己的独特表达方式：用墨画小点，用笔画细胞状结构。

D4.en: At 19, she made her earliest known work in 1948.
D4.zh: 19岁时，她在1948年创作了最早的作品。

D10.en: As a small child, she hid in a cupboard, pale and trembling, to breathe.
D10.zh: 幼年时，她脸色苍白、颤抖着钻进壁橱才能呼吸。

D7.en: When her mother found out what she did, she beat her nearly unconscious.
D7.zh: 母亲发现她做的事后，把她打得几乎失去知觉。

D13a.en: Her mother once kicked her palette across the room; sometimes they grappled physically.
D13a.zh: 母亲曾把她的调色板踢到房间那头，有时她们甚至扭打在一起。

D18.en: She enlisted a cousin as business manager and charged admission.
D18.zh: 她让表亲当经理，收门票。

D5.en: At age 5-10, she danced naked before her aunts and cousins.
D5.zh: 5到10岁时，她赤身裸体在姑妈和表亲面前跳舞。

D9.en: At around 16-18, she fled to Tokyo with a girlfriend.
D9.zh: 16到18岁左右，她和女友一起逃到东京。

D14.en: At 21, her Nihonga painting Cat was selected for an exhibition.
D14.zh: 21岁时，她的日本画《猫》入选了展览。

M1.en: At 21, her Nihonga painting Cat was selected for an exhibition.
M1.zh: 21岁时，她的日本画《猫》入选了展览。

D19.en: At 20-25, she received money from someone but had to report every expense in detail.
D19.zh: 20多岁时，她收到别人的钱，但必须详细报告每一笔开销。

M4.en: She said she cannot help feeling Japan is like a village of wealthy country bumpkins.
M4.zh: 她说她忍不住觉得日本就像一个土财主的村子。

D7.en: He physically attacked photographers and verbally abused those who came too near.
D7.zh: 他动手攻击摄影师，并辱骂靠得太近的人。

D13a.en: He scorned conventional family life, preferring not to live with his children.
D13a.zh: 他鄙视传统家庭生活，宁愿不与子女同住。

D2.en: He painted quinces while making quince jam.
D2.zh: 他一边做榅桲果酱一边画榅桲。

D11.en: He drew a picture illustrating Churchill's remark about the Japanese menace.
D11.zh: 他画了一幅画，配上丘吉尔关于日本威胁的话。

D14.en: He showed off at the Café Royal before his amorous adventures.
D14.zh: 他在情场冒险前在皇家咖啡馆炫耀。

D16.en: He gave no press interviews from 1940 until the last decade of his life.
D16.zh: 他从1940年起直到生命最后十年都不接受媒体采访。

D19.en: He gave no press interviews from 1940 until the last decade of his life.
D19.zh: 他从1940年起直到生命最后十年都不接受媒体采访。

D20X.en: He scorned conventional family life, preferring not to live with his children.
D20X.zh: 他鄙视传统家庭生活，宁愿不与子女同住。

M4.en: He signed a protest in support of Republican Spain on Regent Street.
M4.zh: 他在摄政街签名支持西班牙共和国的抗议。

M5.en: He physically attacked photographers and verbally abused those who came too near.
M5.zh: 他动手攻击摄影师，辱骂靠得太近的人。

M6.en: He felt a sense of destiny and towering ambition to be an artist.
M6.zh: 他感到自己的命运，有成为艺术家的巨大野心。

M7.en: He scorned conventional family life and preferred not to live with his children.
M7.zh: 他鄙视传统家庭生活，宁愿不和孩子们住在一起。

M8.en: He gave no press interviews from 1940 until the last decade of his life.
