M8.zh: 他从1940年起不接受媒体采访，直到生命最后十年。

D12.en: At 16-17, he felt he had to pull away from his mother, who was devoted to him and proud of him.
D12.zh: 16到17岁时，他感到必须疏远母亲，母亲以他为傲并全心奉献。

D3.en: He downplayed anything German and immersed himself in English life.
D3.zh: 他淡化任何德国事物，迅速融入英国生活。

M1.en: He felt a sense of destiny and towering ambition to be an artist.
M1.zh: 他感到自己的命运，有成为艺术家的巨大野心。

M2.en: He never knew anyone who wanted to cut themselves off from their parents to such a degree.
M2.zh: 他从未见过有人想如此彻底地与父母断绝关系。

D15.en: He rode a great deal and found it one of the most exciting things.
D15.zh: 他骑了很多马，觉得这是最刺激的事情之一。

D6.en: He asked a sinister woman to write to the British Council to send him to Spain to radiate British culture.
D6.zh: 他让一个阴险的女人写信给英国文化协会，请求派他去西班牙传播英国文化。

D4.en: At 20, he painted a boy who wouldn't sit still, claiming a neurosis.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他画一个不肯坐着的男孩，男孩说自己得了神经症。

D9.en: As a young man, he was taken up by London's intellectual elite.
D9.zh: 年轻时，他被伦敦的知识精英接纳。

D8.en: At 20-21, he beat someone up and got blood on his hand.
D8.zh: 20到21岁时，他揍了别人，手上沾了血。

D10.en: At 20-21, he said that moment was the worst in his life.
D10.zh: 20到21岁时，他说那一刻是他人生中最糟的。

D20.en: At 17, he and Stephen had a business called Freud & Schuster.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他和斯蒂芬有个叫弗洛伊德与舒斯特的生意。

D13b.en: At 11, his mother took him and his brother to England.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他母亲带他和弟弟去了英国。

D18.en: At 22, he sold a picture to Matsie.
D18.zh: 22岁时，他卖了一幅画给马齐。

D1.en: He pursued painting with stubborn effort, like a siege assault, ignoring all advice.
D1.zh: 他固执地投入绘画，像攻城一样，不听任何建议。

D12.en: At 14-15, he worked part-time for a photo finisher in San Francisco.
D12.zh: 14到15岁时，他在旧金山一家照片冲印店做兼职。

D18.en: At 24-25, wealthy San Franciscans hired him for portraits and home photos.
D18.zh: 24到25岁时，旧金山富人雇他拍摄肖像和住宅。

D9.en: At 23-24, he made an unwritten pact to devote his life to creating beauty.
D9.zh: 23到24岁间，他立下不成文约定，献身于创造美。

D13a.en: At 12, after his final expulsion, he ended classroom education and studied at home.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，最后一次被开除后，他结束了课堂教育，在家自学。

D3.en: At 21-23, he took inspiration from the writings of Edward Carpenter.
D3.zh: 21到23岁时，他从爱德华·卡彭特的作品中获得灵感。

D19.en: As a small child, he assembled meticulous displays of bugs impaled on pins.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他精心排列用大头针钉住的虫子展示。

D2.en: At 25, he calculated the exposure and released the shutter, then had second thoughts.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他计算曝光并按下快门，随即又犹豫了。

D4.en: At 9, he had a great time clamming, crabbing, and playing with baby chicks.
D4.zh: 9岁时，他挖蛤蜊、捉螃蟹、和小鸡玩耍，玩得很开心。

D5.en: He slipped and slid down a steep slope, pulling a friend along.
D5.zh: 他滑倒并滑下陡坡，把朋友也带了下去。

D11.en: At 18-19, he wrote romantic poetry full of nature imagery and spiritual yearning.
D11.zh: 18到19岁时，他创作了充满自然意象和精神渴望的浪漫主义诗歌。

D14.en: At 24, he sat at the piano for an impromptu concert with a violinist.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他坐在钢琴前与小提琴手即兴合奏。

D1.en: He suggested trying again outdoors with natural light after the children recovered.
D1.zh: 孩子们恢复后，他建议再次尝试，这次在户外用自然光。

M8.en: At 17, nature became his religion, and Yosemite his temple.
M8.zh: 17岁时，自然成了他的宗教，约塞米蒂是他的殿堂。

M1.en: At 17, he entered a print in a photo competition and won an honorable mention.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他投稿一张照片参加摄影比赛，获得了荣誉奖。

M6.en: At 10, he was more interested in what he could discover for himself.
M6.zh: 10岁时，他更感兴趣的是自己能发现什么。

D7.en: At 22, he said he did not repent of his decision despite feeling the loss.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他说他不后悔自己的决定，尽管感到失去了大家。

D13b.en: At 12-13, he spoke some English and was told by a teacher that boys would be punished for speaking German with him.
D13b.zh: 12到13岁时，他说一些英语，老师警告男孩们跟他说德语会受罚。

D11.en: At 16, he began a detailed diary about the ship, crew, and passengers.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他开始写日记，详细记录船、船员和乘客。

D2.en: At 14, he studied French, Latin, Chemistry, German, Algebra, Composition, Trigonometry.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他学习法语、拉丁语、化学、德语、代数、作文、三角学。

D3.en: At 25-28, he would not answer directly but sent them to look it up.
D3.zh: 25到28岁时，他不直接回答，而是让他们去查字典或百科全书。

D5.en: At 22, he recognized risks as very great but said happiness is worth chasing.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他承认风险很大，但说幸福值得追求。

M1.en: At 21, he would receive a dispensation from the age rule and his degree cum laude at the ceremony.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他获得了年龄规定的豁免，并在毕业典礼上以优等成绩获得学位。

D6.en: At 21, the Union Boat Club elected him a member, giving him access to canoes.
D6.zh: 21岁时，联合船俱乐部选举他为会员，让他可以使用独木舟。

D15.en: He was encouraged by friends to join them in testing himself against the Alps.
D15.zh: 他被朋友鼓励加入他们，一起挑战阿尔卑斯山。

D9.en: He arranged to enter Harvard Law School in fall 1875 at age 19.
D9.zh: 他19岁时安排进入哈佛法学院，1875年秋季入学。

D20.en: He received a special exemption to graduate early at age 21.
D20.zh: 他21岁时获得特别豁免，提前毕业。

D13a.en: He opted to work for a St. Louis firm instead of his brother-in-law's at age 22.
D13a.zh: 他22岁时选择去圣路易斯一家律所工作，而非他姐夫的律所。

D18.en: He took a job in a Cincinnati grocery at age 23 and saw a possible path.
D18.zh: 他23岁时在辛辛那提一家杂货店工作，意识到这可能是一条成功之路。

D1.en: He recuperated in Louisville at age 22.
D1.zh: 他22岁时在路易斯维尔休养。

M3.en: He changed his middle name to Dembitz in admiration of his uncle.
M3.zh: 他因崇拜叔叔，将中间名改为Dembitz。

D16.en: He argued with the rector at age 17.
D16.zh: 他17岁时与校长争论。

D14.en: He kept declining offers to act in local amateur plays.
D14.zh: 他不断拒绝参演本地业余剧目的邀请。

D19.en: He requested monthly expense reports by the 8th.
D19.zh: 他要求每月8号前提交开支账目。

D12.en: As a teenager, he lived at school and managed boarders' meals.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他住在学校，管理寄宿生的伙食。

D18.en: He often visited bookstores and saved meal money to buy law books, bringing back at least four cases.
D18.zh: 他经常跑书店，省下饭钱买法学书，回国时带了至少四箱。

D13b.en: At 21, he attended a farewell banquet where Premier Zhou received the group.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他参加高教部欢送宴，周总理接见了他们。

D6.en: He helped a Czech friend develop nude photos taken in a forest.
D6.zh: 他帮一位捷克朋友冲洗在森林里拍的裸照。

D9.en: At 19, he gathered with 30 arts activists from Yenching and Tsinghua universities and rode a truck to Dongchang'an Street No. 8.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他和燕京大学、清华大学的30名文艺骨干一起，乘坐卡车前往东长安街8号。

D14.en: At 19, he rehearsed and performed at Huai Ren Tang for the new CPPCC closing ceremony.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他加班排演节目，到怀仁堂演出。

D2.en: In the USSR, he prepared thoroughly and volunteered to speak in class discussions.
D2.zh: 在苏联，他主动准备材料，在课堂讨论上举手发言。

D1.en: He made up five missed courses by taking exams in later semesters.
D1.zh: 他后来陆续补考了第一学期缺考的五门课程。

D3.en: He puzzled over a legal term until he learned it meant 'legal act'.
D3.zh: 他琢磨一个民法术语，后来才知道是“法律行为”。

D15.en: At 19, he danced yangge with big moves in winter and sweated.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他跳秧歌舞动作很大，寒冬腊月跳到出汗。

D20.en: At 17, he was elected student council president of Chongde Middle School.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他当选崇德中学学生自治会主席。

D13a.en: At 19, he signed up for the Southward Work Corps.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他报名参加南下工作团。

D11.en: As a child, he enjoyed stargazing with adults pointing out constellations.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他喜欢在院子里看星星，大人教他认星座。

D10.en: At 19, he felt extremely embarrassed but his comrades didn't blame him.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他感到无地自容，但团友们没有责备他。

D19.en: At 19, he enjoyed placing red flags on maps with comrades during the Yangtze crossing campaign.
D19.zh: 19岁时，他喜欢和团友每天看地图插红旗，寻找刚解放的县名。

D5.en: At 18, he didn't go because he was timid and afraid of the unknown.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他胆子小，没敢去。

M2.en: He felt his father saw sending children to top universities as life's highest ideal.
M2.zh: 他觉得父亲把供孩子上名牌大学视为人生最高理想。

M6.en: He felt his father saw sending children to top universities as life's highest ideal.
M6.zh: 他觉得父亲把供孩子上名牌大学视为人生最高理想。

M8.en: As a child, he witnessed Wu Peifu's grand funeral, the most lavish he had ever seen.
M8.zh: 他童年时目睹吴佩孚的盛大葬礼，那是他见过最隆重的。

D17.en: He participated in a charity school and got in touch with real life.
D17.zh: 他参加义校，接触生活。

D2.en: He lectured at Harvard six years after graduating from law school in 1866.
D2.zh: 他1866年从哈佛法学院毕业，六年后在哈佛讲课。

D15.en: At 20, he marched 15 miles with his unit to Poolesville.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他随部队行军15英里到达普尔斯维尔。

D5.en: He served as lieutenant in the Twentieth Massachusetts Infantry and was wounded at Ball's Bluff.
D5.zh: 他在马萨诸塞第二十步兵团任中尉，在鲍尔布拉夫受伤。

D6.en: He had breakfast with Leslie Stephen and lunch with Frederick Pollock.
D6.zh: 他和莱斯利·斯蒂芬共进早餐，又和弗雷德里克·波洛克共进午餐。

D19.en: For years through 1904, he listed all the books he read with title and author.
D19.zh: 直到1904年，他多年列出所有读过的书的书名和作者。

D11.en: He had to patch up a Class Poem as quickly and as well as he could.
D11.zh: 他不得不尽快尽力修补一首班级诗。

D4.en: He played soldiers among the ruins of Revolutionary forts.
D4.zh: 他在革命战争堡垒的废墟中玩打仗游戏。

D14.en: He wrote poems for the college Annual and long letters to his friend.
D14.zh: 他为大学年鉴写诗，并给朋友写长信。

D13b.en: He went to Harvard at sixteen, as was the rule.
D13b.zh: 他十六岁上哈佛，这是惯例。

D9.en: At 20, he tried for a commission in a Massachusetts regiment.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他试图在马萨诸塞州的一个团获得军官任命。

D10.en: At 21, he walked around camp in a daze, his eyes dead and lips quivering.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他失魂落魄地在营地走来走去，眼神呆滞，嘴唇松弛颤抖。

D13a.en: At 23, he said he was going to the Law School.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他说他要去法学院。

D7.en: At 21, he hit a man on the back of the neck with his saber, knocking him over.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他用军刀刀背击打一名男子的后颈，将其打翻在地。

M4.en: At 20, he joined the Fourth Battalion, New England Guard.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他加入了新英格兰警卫队第四营。

M7.en: At 20, he went into the army upon principle and because of sympathy with the cause.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他出于原则和对事业的同情而参军。

D12.en: At 20-24, he was taken home wounded during the Civil War.
D12.zh: 20到24岁间，他在内战中受伤被送回家。

D16.en: At 21, he said, 'War is an organized bore.'
D16.zh: 21岁时，他说：“战争是一种有组织的无聊。”

M1.en: At 20, he and Hallowell were to take their examinations in June with the rest of the class.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他和哈洛韦尔要和其他同学一起在六月参加考试。

D1.en: At 25, he felt an enormous impatience, unable to find what he wanted fast enough.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他感到极大的不耐烦，无法足够快地找到他想要的东西。

M6.en: At 18, he came home again to be an American.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他再次回家，要成为一个美国人。

D12.en: At age 6-10, he had to finish his calligraphy practice under his mother's supervision before being allowed to sleep.
D12.zh: 6到10岁时，他必须在母亲监督下写完描红和仿字才能睡觉。

D2.en: At age 17-19, he would get up before dawn every day to memorize Russian words and read assigned books.
D2.zh: 17到19岁时，他每天天没亮就起来背诵俄语单词和阅读指定书目。

D3.en: As a child and teenager, he loved music and performed in multiple school concerts.
D3.zh: 他自幼喜欢音乐，中小学时多次举行音乐演出。

D9.en: At age 22, he angrily wrote an appeal to the Central Control Commission of the CPSU against an unjust verdict.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他愤然向联共（布）中央监察委员会写申诉信反对不公正判决。

D20.en: At age 15, he was elected secretary of the first Socialist Youth League branch in Wuxi County.
D20.zh: 15岁时，他被选为无锡县第一个社会主义青年团支部书记。

M4.en: At age 20-25, his faith became his backbone, and he was ready to sacrifice everything precious for it.
M4.zh: 20到25岁时，信仰铸就了他的人生脊梁，他准备为它付出一切最宝贵的东西。

M7.en: At age 15, he volunteered to join the Chinese Socialist Youth League, vowing to fight for communism.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他志愿加入中国社会主义青年团，愿为共产主义事业奋斗终生。

D7.en: At age 22, he angrily wrote an appeal to the Central Control Commission of the CPSU against an unjust verdict.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他愤然向联共（布）中央监察委员会写申诉信反对不公正判决。

D14.en: At 16, he starred in the new play 'Chuishu Dream' led by Sun Yefang.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他主演了由孙冶方领导的新话剧《炊黍梦》。

D5.en: At 17, he volunteered to go to Shanghai during summer break to experience the workers' movement.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他主动申请暑假去上海体验工人运动。

D18.en: At 23, he earned nearly 100 rubles monthly as a translator while working at the school.
D18.zh: 23岁时，他留校工作，当翻译每月有近百卢布工资。

D10.en: At 22, he left a meeting, secretly glad he could stay in the party and continue working for it.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他离开会场，暗自庆幸还能留在党内为党工作。

D4.en: At 24, he secretly carried a lime bucket onto the street at night and painted slogans with a brush.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他夜里偷偷拎着石灰筒上街，用板刷写标语。

D1.en: From age 20 to 25, he ran around asking for help but got no results for years.
D1.zh: 20到25岁间，他奔走询问，但多年来毫无结果。

D15.en: At 24, he secretly carried a lime bucket onto the street at night and painted slogans with a brush.
D15.zh: 24岁时，他夜里偷偷拎着石灰筒上街，用板刷写标语。

D19.en: At 21, he drafted a survey plan and created statistical tables.
D19.zh: 21岁时，他草拟了调查计划，制订出统计表格。

D13a.en: At 25, he voluntarily resigned in the spring of 1933.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他于1933年春主动辞职。

D17.en: At 24, he did everything he could to take more direct revolutionary action.
D17.zh: 24岁时，他竭尽全力采取更直接的革命行动。

M2.en: At 20-25, he was ready to sacrifice everything precious for his faith.
M2.zh: 20到25岁间，他准备为信仰付出一切最宝贵的东西。

M6.en: At 20-25, he felt his faith had forged his backbone, and he would give up anything for it.
M6.zh: 20到25岁间，他觉得信仰铸就了他的人生脊梁，愿为它付出一切。

M8.en: At 20-25, he believed his faith shaped his life and was willing to sacrifice all for it.
M8.zh: 20到25岁间，他相信信仰塑造了自己的人生，并愿为之牺牲一切。

D3.en: At 25, he tried one substitute for religion after another, but none satisfied him.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他尝试了一个又一个宗教替代品，但都不满意。

D12.en: At 10, he lost his father.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他失去了父亲。

D6.en: At 25, he visited Léon Bloy's tomb with a French professor.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他和一位法国教授参观了莱昂·布洛伊的墓。

D4.en: At about 10, he tied a rope between a pillar and a millstone and walked on it with a pole for balance.
D4.zh: 大约10岁时，他在柱子和磨盘间绑了根绳子，拿着杆子走上去保持平衡。

D10.en: He burst into violent weeping as soon as he finished the last paragraph.
D10.zh: 他刚读完最后一段就放声大哭。

D2.en: At 21-22, he was so interested in his studies that he had no time to feel homesick.
D2.zh: 21到22岁时，他对学习太投入了，没时间想家。

D18.en: As a small child, he used to steal dimes from his mother to buy kites.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他常从母亲那儿偷一角钱去买风筝。

D17.en: At 15, he had typhoid fever; his mother nursed him for twenty days until she collapsed.
D17.zh: 15岁时，他得了伤寒，母亲照顾了他20天，直到她累倒了。

D14.en: At 24, his lecture was scheduled for half past four in the afternoon.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他的讲座安排在下午四点半。

D11.en: At 12, he felt he had beaten Confucius by three years.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他觉得自己比孔子早了三年。

D15.en: In secondary school, he was a champion at kicking shuttlecocks.
D15.zh: 中学时，他是踢毽子冠军。

D5.en: At 17-18, he tried to light a bottle with a match and it burst, but he was unhurt.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他用火柴点瓶子，瓶子炸了，但他没受伤。

D13a.en: At 21-22, he stopped praying and going to church.
D13a.zh: 21到22岁时，他停止祈祷和去教堂。

D7.en: At 21-22, he stopped praying and going to church.
D7.zh: 21到22岁时，他停止祈祷和去教堂。

D9.en: At 25, the editorial board of T'ien Hsia Monthly, which he organized, met for the first time.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他组织的《天下月刊》编委会首次开会。

M1.en: At 18-21, he was first honor man for six consecutive terms.
M1.zh: 18到21岁间，他连续六个学期获得第一名。

M3.en: At 21-26, he adored Him in the spirit of hero-worship from Thomas Carlyle.
M3.zh: 21到26岁时，他以从卡莱尔那里学来的英雄崇拜精神崇拜他。

D16.en: At 24, he wrote a letter in December 1936 to a lady missionary that scandalized her.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他于1936年12月给一位女传教士写信，让她大为震惊。

M7.en: At 22, he said as a Chinese he has a country to save, a people to enlighten, a race to uplift, a civilization to modernize.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他说作为中国人，他有国家要救，有人民要启蒙，有种族要提升，有文明要现代化。

M4.en: At 22, he supplicated on behalf of the younger generation for a bequest to save lives.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他代表年轻一代恳求留下一份拯救生命的遗产。

D20.en: He consulted with Yuan-ning on the editorial board personnel.
D20.zh: 他和沅宁商量了编委会的人选。

M8.en: He tried one substitute for religion after another, but none satisfied him.
M8.zh: 他试了一个又一个宗教的替代品，但都不满意。

M6.en: He presented his credentials to Pope Pius XII, the first Catholic to represent a non-Catholic nation to the Holy See.
M6.zh: 他向教皇庇护十二世递交国书，成为首位代表非天主教国家向教廷递交国书的天主教徒。

D12.en: He borrowed tuition money from his uncle.
D12.zh: 他向舅舅借了学费。

D3.en: He read most of the 500 books in Frewsburg's library.
D3.zh: 他读完了弗里茨堡图书馆五百本书中的大部分。

D4.en: He slept in the stalls with horses, rubbed them down, and helped train them.
D4.zh: 他睡在马厩里，给马擦洗，帮忙训练它们。

D15.en: He hiked alone with his dog or rode a horse on adventures.
D15.zh: 他独自带着狗徒步或骑马去探险。

D5.en: He ignored warnings that representing unions would hurt his career.
D5.zh: 他无视了代理工会会损害事业的警告。

D16.en: As class president and valedictorian, he gave a graduation speech on labor vs. capital.
D16.zh: 作为班长和毕业生代表，他发表了关于劳资关系的毕业演讲。

D14.en: He was invited to speak at social gatherings in Frewsburg and nearby towns.
D14.zh: 他受邀在弗里茨堡及附近城镇的社交聚会上演讲。

M1.en: He joined the debate team that competed with other schools.
M1.zh: 他加入了与其他学校比赛的辩论队。

D9.en: As class president and valedictorian, he gave a graduation speech on labor vs. capital.
D9.zh: 作为班长和毕业生代表，他发表了关于劳资关系的毕业演讲。

D20.en: He was voted president of the Lyceum.
D20.zh: 他被选为学园的主席。

D2.en: At 17, he decided to study an extra year at high school in Jamestown.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他决定在詹姆斯敦的高中多读一年。

D6.en: At 17, he was glad to find his new school had a debate club, the Lyceum.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他高兴地发现新学校有辩论社，叫莱西姆社。

D7.en: He ignored his father's objections and accepted Frank Mott's offer.
D7.zh: 他无视父亲的反对，接受了弗兰克·莫特的提议。

D1.en: At 21, he put in long hours investigating the pole cutting night, talking to everyone and studying riot laws.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他花长时间调查砍电线杆那晚的事，和所有人谈话并研究暴动和阴谋法律。

D2.en: After two years in Oklahoma, she earned readmission to Harvard.
D2.zh: 在俄克拉荷马两年后，她重新被哈佛录取。

D1.en: She failed her driving test five times before passing on the sixth.
D1.zh: 她考驾照五次没过，第六次才通过。

D12.en: At 17, Ruth's mother died of cancer after a four-year struggle.
D12.zh: 17岁时，露丝的母親因癌症去世，抗争了四年。

D14.en: She was chosen as one of four students to speak for 800 classmates.
D14.zh: 她被选为四名学生代表之一，为八百名同学发言。

D15.en: Riding and waterskiing became her favorite sports as an adult.
D15.zh: 骑马和滑水成为她成年后最爱的运动。

D6.en: Her personal friendships readily crossed religious and ethnic boundaries.
D6.zh: 她的个人友谊轻松跨越宗教和种族界限。

D20.en: She sat on the student-faculty committee handling plagiarism cases.
D20.zh: 她参加了处理抄袭案件的学生-教师委员会。

D11.en: She acquired the title of camp rabbi.
D11.zh: 她获得了营地拉比的称号。

D3.en: A full year at Cornell would allow her to explore more art history, music, and literature.
D3.zh: 在康奈尔一整年让她能探索更多艺术史、音乐和文学。

M1.en: She resolved to match the standard set by legal analyst and columnist Anthony Lewis.
M1.zh: 她决心达到法律分析师兼专栏作家安东尼·刘易斯设定的标准。

M4.en: At 17, she felt growing alienation from religion and a new steeliness in her resolve.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她对宗教越来越疏远，决心也变得更加坚定。

D18.en: At 19, she worked one summer at A&S, a Federated store.
D18.zh: 19岁时，她暑假在联邦百货的A&S商店工作。

D9.en: At 18-21, she led a group concerned with careers for women.
D9.zh: 18到21岁时，她领导了一个关注女性职业的团体。

D13b.en: At 21, she married in the Ginsburgs' living room days after graduating Cornell.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，她在康奈尔毕业几天后在金斯伯格家的客厅结婚。

D4.en: At 21-23, she shared cooking duties with Marty, cooking weekdays while he cooked weekends.
D4.zh: 21到23岁间，她和马蒂分担做饭，她做工作日，他做周末。

D10.en: At 17, she and her father were engulfed by waves of inconsolable grief.
D10.zh: 17岁时，她和父亲被无法安慰的悲伤淹没。

D13a.en: She appealed to the vice-dean, who denied her request, and Griswold refused to overrule him.
D13a.zh: 她向副院长提出请求，被拒绝，格里索尔德也拒绝推翻副院长的决定。

D5.en: At 13, she smoked her first cigarette when a friend dared her to inhale without coughing.
D5.zh: 13岁时，她抽了第一支烟，因为朋友激她吸进去不咳嗽。

D17.en: At 25, she could not resist teaching her to read.
D17.zh: 25岁时，她忍不住教她读书。

D18.en: He delivered the Daily Californian and sold ice successfully.
D18.zh: 他送《加州日报》并成功卖冰。

D2.en: He learned to play the clarinet and played it well.
D2.zh: 他学会了吹单簧管，而且吹得很好。

D5.en: His mother refused to go to games afraid he'd get hurt, but he played anyway.
D5.zh: 他妈妈怕他受伤不去看比赛，但他还是上场了。

D6.en: He went to dances and parties, met women, but dated infrequently.
D6.zh: 他去舞会和派对，认识了一些女性，但很少约会。

D17.en: He tended to his ailing sister, rubbing her legs and soothing her to sleep.
D17.zh: 他照顾生病的妹妹，揉她的腿，哄她入睡。

D9.en: He served as secretary at the organizational meeting and carried a union card for life.
D9.zh: 他在组织会议上担任秘书，并终生持有工会卡。

D4.en: He worked as a mechanic's helper in the Southern Pacific roundhouse during summers.
D4.zh: 他暑假在南太平洋铁路的机车库当机械工助手。

D20.en: At 25, he unexpectedly found himself elected vice president of the state's third largest bar association.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他意外地当选为该州第三大律师协会的副主席。

D13b.en: Teachers tied his left hand to force him to write with his right.
D13b.zh: 老师把他的左手绑起来，强迫他用右手写字。

D19.en: His schoolbooks had careful notations and questions in precise handwriting.
D19.zh: 他的课本上有工整的笔记和问题，字迹精确。

D15.en: He tried out for the university baseball team but did not make it.
D15.zh: 他参加了大学棒球队的选拔，但没被选上。

D10.en: He wept in a quiet hallway outside his daughter's hospital room.
D10.zh: 他在女儿病房外的安静走廊里哭泣。

M1.en: He made the all-city team in his senior year of high school.
M1.zh: 他高中最后一年入选了全市最佳阵容。

D13b.en: As punishment, his grammar school principal required him to learn a section of the Constitution.
D13b.zh: 作为惩罚，他的小学校长要求他背诵宪法的一部分。

D2.en: He was never late to school and absent just one day.
D2.zh: 他上学从不迟到，只缺勤一天。

D7.en: As a small child, his family was full of strong people not intimidated by violent segregation.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他的家庭充满不畏暴力隔离的强者。

D6.en: He won election to the student council and to his class's treasury committee.
D6.zh: 他当选了学生会和班级财务委员会的成员。

D18.en: He started working at age seven for ten cents a day plus all he could eat.
D18.zh: 他七岁开始打工，每天赚十美分加管饱。

D9.en: He led the majority of students to vote to keep the faculty all white.
D9.zh: 他带领多数学生投票保持全体白人教职员工。

D5.en: As a small child, his family was full of strong people not intimidated by violent segregation.
D5.zh: 幼年时，他家人不畏暴力隔离。

D1.en: He quit the team to focus on schoolwork, then rejoined and was reelected captain.
D1.zh: 他退队专心学业，后又归队并再次当选队长。

D13a.en: He took pre-medical classes to please his mother.
D13a.zh: 他为取悦母亲选修了医学预科课程。

D17.en: He pleaded for a saucer of milk and began regularly caring for the cat.
D17.zh: 他央求得到一碟牛奶，并开始定期照顾那只猫。

D3.en: He turned to Uncle Fee to learn about Baltimore politics and corporate power.
D3.zh: 他向费叔叔请教，了解巴尔的摩政治和掌控城市的强大白人企业。

M2.en: His mother's ambition for education and excellence pressured him to excel.
M2.zh: 母亲对教育和卓越的追求给他施加了压力，要求他出类拔萃。

D16.en: He and his father continued to one-up each other.
D16.zh: 他和父亲继续互相较劲。

D4.en: He spent countless hours in the library and in Virginia researching extradition law.
D4.zh: 他在图书馆和弗吉尼亚州花了无数时间研究引渡法。

M1.en: At 25-27, he saw his chance to take revenge for the hurt of being barred from his home state law school.
M1.zh: 25到27岁时，他看到了报复被家乡法学院拒之门外的伤痛的机会。

D6.en: At age 9, he earned a free place at Andover Grammar School after exams.
D6.zh: 9岁时通过考试获得安多弗文法学校免费名额。

D2.en: At age 16, he sat for the Oxford and Cambridge examination.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他参加了牛津和剑桥的考试。

D5.en: At age 25, he pulled the communication cord, walked half a mile along the track, and got to court in time.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他拉下通讯索，沿铁轨走了半英里，及时赶到法庭。

D13b.en: He joined the Royal Engineers under compulsory military service.
D13b.zh: 他在强制兵役下加入了皇家工兵部队。

D16.en: He came top in English and mathematics at age 16.
D16.zh: 他16岁时在英语和数学中名列前茅。

M7.en: He and his unit went two days and nights without sleep.
M7.zh: 他和他的部队两天两夜没睡觉。

D3.en: He bought a copy of Anson on contract and began his study of the law.
D3.zh: 他买了一本安森的合同法，开始学习法律。

D7.en: He was advised to list his father as 'Gentleman' when applying for an Army commission.
D7.zh: 他申请陆军军官时，被建议将父亲职业填为‘绅士’。

D18.en: His father earned a Ph.D. at Columbia and taught Romance languages at Brooklyn College.
D18.zh: 他父亲在哥伦比亚大学获博士学位，后在布鲁克林学院教罗曼语。

D14.en: At 12-17, he became president of the dramatic society and starred in school plays, including Macbeth.
D14.zh: 12到17岁间，他担任戏剧社社长并主演校园剧，包括《麦克白》。

D2.en: He won 19 First Honor awards between ages 12 and 17.
D2.zh: 他12到17岁间获得19次一等奖。

D15.en: At 13-17, he learned to fire a rifle and march in formation.
D15.zh: 13到17岁间，他学会了开枪和列队行进。

D3.en: At 21-24, pursuing the interconnection between religion and law became his lifelong mission.
D3.zh: 21到24岁间，追求宗教与法律的关联成为他毕生使命。

D16.en: At 21, he was chosen to deliver the Cohonguroton Address, the school's valedictory speech.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他被选为科洪古罗顿演讲者，即学校的告别演讲。

D11.en: As a senior, he discovered his oratory and acting skills and won the lead in Macbeth.
D11.zh: 高年级时，他发现了自己的演讲和表演才能，并赢得了《麦克白》的主角。

M4.en: He rejected liberalism and followed Dwight D. Eisenhower's 1952 presidential campaign.
M4.zh: 他拒绝自由主义，关注德怀特·D·艾森豪威尔的1952年总统竞选。

M1.en: After first-year grades, he ranked in the top 5 percent of his class.
M1.zh: 第一年成绩出来后，他排名班级前5%。

D9.en: He was put in charge of running a meeting when the senior attorney was unavailable.
D9.zh: 当资深律师无法出席时，他被安排负责主持会议。

D20.en: He was elected president of the Mask and Bauble club in his sophomore year.
D20.zh: 他在大二时被选为面具与饰品俱乐部主席。

D7.en: He deemed warrantless wiretaps a practical necessity and opposed removing executive control.
D7.zh: 他认为无证窃听是实际必要，反对取消行政控制。

D1.en: After a failure, he won a full scholarship to Xavier High School.
D1.zh: 在经历一次失败后，他获得了泽维尔高中的全额奖学金。

D13a.en: Guided by the Jesuits, he decided to attend Georgetown.
D13a.zh: 在耶稣会士的指导下，他决定就读乔治城大学。

M7.en: He said it is our task to carry reason and faith into all sections of society.
M7.zh: 他说，我们的任务是将理性与信仰传播到社会的各个角落。

D13b.en: As a child, he was raised to work hard and know his place in segregated Savannah, Georgia.
D13b.zh: 他小时候被教育要努力工作，并知道自己在种族隔离的萨凡纳的位置。

D12.en: At 14, he had just turned fourteen; it is unlikely he came to Vienna entirely alone.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他刚满十四岁；不太可能独自来到维也纳。

D13b.en: He likely received private instruction in Israelite religion as a child.
D13b.zh: 他幼年时可能接受了以色列宗教的私人教育。

D1.en: He earned a distinction in economics, with passing grades in other subjects.
D1.zh: 他获得了经济学优秀奖，其他科目成绩为及格。

D3.en: He did not enroll in any courses taught by Tezner during his studies.
D3.zh: 他学习期间没有选修特兹纳的任何课程。

D14.en: Three of his poems appeared in the Wiener Hausfrauen-Zeitung.
D14.zh: 他的三首诗发表在《维也纳家庭主妇报》上。

D2.en: He passed the reserve officer exam with distinction.
D2.zh: 他以优异的成绩通过了预备军官考试。

M1.en: He completed all required lectures within the minimum statutory time.
M1.zh: 他在法定最短时间内完成了所有必修课程。

D20.en: He was admitted to the Matura exam in summer 1900 with 31 other students.
D20.zh: 1900年夏天，他与另外31名学生一起获准参加高中毕业考试。

D7.en: He refused to make corrections to his dissertation despite his advisor's demand.
D7.zh: 他拒绝按导师要求修改论文。

M3.en: He decided to follow in his idol's footsteps academically.
M3.zh: 他决定追随偶像的学术道路。

D11.en: He published 'The Doctrine of the State of Dante Alighieri' as a 150-page booklet.
D11.zh: 他出版了150页的小册子《但丁·阿利吉耶里的国家学说》。

D18.en: He started working for a Vienna lawyer named Heinrich Singer on March 2.
D18.zh: 他3月2日开始为维也纳律师海因里希·辛格工作。

D9.en: He earned his JDr. in February 1906 and gave a talk in Menzel's seminar on suffrage.
D9.zh: 他1906年2月获得法学博士学位，并在门采尔的研讨会上做了关于选举权的报告。

D15.en: He completed mandatory military drills during semester breaks in 1902 and 1904.
D15.zh: 他在1902年和1904年的假期完成了义务军训。

D4.en: He deposited an early version of his dissertation at the Academy of Sciences to secure priority.
D4.zh: 他在科学院寄存了论文初稿以确保优先权。

D5.en: He published his magnum opus in June 1903 at his own risk.
D5.zh: 他1903年6月自费出版了代表作。

D12.en: He was born on January 11, 1939, in New York City, an only child.
D12.zh: 他于1939年1月11日出生在纽约市，是独生子。

D7.en: He overrode the decision of the book review editor.
D7.zh: 他否决了书评编辑的决定。

D13b.en: At 16, he followed his father's encouragement and applied to Yale and Harvard, and was accepted at Yale.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他听从父亲的鼓励申请了耶鲁和哈佛，并被耶鲁录取。

D3.en: At 23-24, he filled his free time with reading literature, especially novels.
D3.zh: 23到24岁时，他用空闲时间阅读文学作品，尤其是小说。

D2.en: He graduated with a 79.9 average and missed graduating summa cum laude by a tenth of a point.
D2.zh: 他以79.9的平均分毕业，因差0.1分未获得最优等荣誉。

D9.en: He was active in the drama group Saybrook Players and served as its president.
D9.zh: 他活跃于戏剧团体Saybrook Players，并担任其主席。

D6.en: He was on the board of the undergraduate literary magazine, Yale Lit.
D6.zh: 他是本科文学杂志《耶鲁文学》的编委。

D14.en: At 15, he was in the tenth-grade play.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他参加了十年级的戏剧演出。

D20.en: At 22, he was selected as president of the law review because he was such a standout guy.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他被选为法律评论的主席，因为他非常出色。

M1.en: He graduated first in his class from Harvard Law School in 1962.
M1.zh: 他于1962年以班级第一名的成绩从哈佛法学院毕业。

D18.en: At 13, he agreed to give away his train set because he had outgrown it.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他同意把火车套装送人，因为自己已经玩够了。

D13a.en: At 13, he said his parents could give away his train set.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他说父母可以把他的火车套装送人。

D11.en: As a teenager, he made up mysteries with friends as they went along.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他和朋友们即兴编造各种谜案。

D15.en: As a teenager, he shot rifles at a shooting range and played tennis with friends.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他在射击场打步枪，还和朋友打网球。

D10.en: At 22-23, he tried to keep a grave face but couldn't during a Captain Queeg-like moment.
D10.zh: 22到23岁时，他试图保持严肃表情，但在一个类似奎格船长的时刻没忍住。

D16.en: At 18-22, he debated as part of the Political Union.
D16.zh: 18到22岁时，他作为政治联盟成员参加辩论。

D1.en: At 18-22, he wrote a 1700-word rejoinder in response to a grade and comments.
D1.zh: 18到22岁时，他写了一篇1700字的反驳文章回应成绩和评语。

D5.en: At 20, he chose Harvard Law over Yale because it was more challenging and he didn't want to flinch.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他选了哈佛法学院而不是耶鲁，因为哈佛更难，他不想在挑战面前退缩。

D19.en: At 22-23, he rewrote Supreme Court developments notes on an old electric typewriter.
D19.zh: 22到23岁间，他用旧电动打字机重写最高法院进展笔记。

D2.en: He traveled in Germany with Renato Treves and Ludovico Geymonat, and studied at Marburg University.
D2.zh: 他与雷纳托·特雷维斯和卢多维科·盖莫纳特在德国旅行，并在马尔堡大学学习。

D11.en: He wrote his first poems in 1923 when he was in the fourth year of junior secondary school.
D11.zh: 1923年，他在初中四年级时写下了第一首诗。

D3.en: He started the third year of philosophy with the intention of obtaining a second degree.
D3.zh: 他开始了哲学第三年的学习，打算获得第二个学位。

D6.en: He frequently visited the well-known anti-fascist circle of Prof. Barbara Allason.
D6.zh: 他经常拜访芭芭拉·阿拉森教授的著名反法西斯圈子。

D9.en: He took part in a meeting with Vittorio Foà, Antonicelli, Muggia and a fourth unidentified individual.
D9.zh: 他参加了一次会议，与会者有维托里奥·福阿、安东尼切利、穆贾以及一名身份不明的人。

D20.en: He became a member of the editorial committee of Rivista di Filosofia.
D20.zh: 他成为了《哲学杂志》编辑委员会的成员。

M4.en: He belonged to the movement through frequent visits to Allason's circle and persistent association with its supporters.
M4.zh: 他通过频繁访问阿拉森的圈子并持续与运动支持者交往，属于该运动。

D7.en: He became a member of the Youth Vanguard in 1927 when the first group was set up at D'Azeglio Senior Secondary School.
D7.zh: 1927年，当达泽利奥高中的第一个先锋青年团成立时，他成为了成员。

D13b.en: He has been a member of the Fascist National Party and the Fascist University Group since 1928, when he went to university.
D13b.zh: 自1928年上大学起，他一直是法西斯国家党和法西斯大学集团的成员。

D13a.en: He grew up in a patriotic and fascist family; his father was a Fascist Party member since 1923.
D13a.zh: 他在一个爱国且法西斯主义的家庭长大，他父亲自1923年起就是法西斯党成员。

D12.en: He was rejected from military service due to ankylosis in his left shoulder from a childhood illness.
D12.zh: 他因童年疾病导致左肩强直，在兵役体检中被拒。

M1.en: At 22, he graduated in philosophy of law.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他毕业于法律哲学专业。

D3.en: Brennan had seen police and governmental abuse since age 10
D3.zh: 布伦南从10岁起就目睹了警察和政府滥用权力

D20.en: At 24-25, he was a member of the Legal Aid Society.
D20.zh: 24到25岁间，他是法律援助协会成员。

M2.en: At 24, he had to support his wife and seven siblings.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他需要养活妻子和七个兄弟姐妹。

D4.en: As a girl, she climbed Round Mountain with her father, avoiding rattlesnakes.
D4.zh: 小时候，她和父亲一起爬圆山，避开响尾蛇。

D15.en: At 15, she stood on a lug wrench and jumped to create more force.
D15.zh: 15岁时，她站在轮胎扳手上跳，想加大力气。

D2.en: At 22, she condensed three years of graduate study into two, graduating from Stanford Law.
D2.zh: 22岁时，她把三年研究生课程压缩成两年，从斯坦福法学院毕业。

D6.en: At 16, she came back with a cute guy, Andy, a returning vet with a red convertible.
D6.zh: 16岁时，她带回来一个帅小伙安迪，是个开红色敞篷车的退伍兵。

D3.en: In college, she was the protégée and research assistant of a legendary liberal, Robert Cushman.
D3.zh: 大学时，她是传奇自由派罗伯特·库什曼的门生和研究助理。

D14.en: At 22, at a dance party in San Francisco, the crowd stopped to watch her and her partner dance.
D14.zh: 22岁时，在旧金山的一个舞会上，人群停下来看她和她舞伴跳舞。

D13a.en: At 20, her engagement broke off, like many wartime courtships.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，她的婚约解除了，像许多战时匆忙的恋爱一样。

M1.en: She scored in the top 15% on the law school test and expected to get in.
M1.zh: 她的法学院考试成绩在前15%，她觉得自己肯定能被录取。

D10.en: She was so shaken that she considered abandoning her law ambitions.
D10.zh: 她深受震动，甚至考虑放弃法律方面的抱负。

D5.en: She took a bucket of paint and painted a reserved parking space for herself.
D5.zh: 她提了一桶油漆，在校园停车场给自己画了一个预留车位。

D17.en: She cared for her husband, their young daughter, attended law school classes, and worked on law review.
D17.zh: 她照顾丈夫和年幼的女儿，同时上法学院课程并参与法律评论工作。

D8.en: She insisted that the office reserve parking spaces for her.
D8.zh: 她坚持要求办公室为她预留车位。

D18.en: She wrote to her parents Harry and Ada Mae Day around 1955.
D18.zh: 她大约在1955年给父母哈里和艾达·梅·戴写信。

M8.en: She wrote to John O'Connor on June 21, 1952.
M8.zh: 她于1952年6月21日给约翰·奥康纳写信。

D12.en: At 10, his father died and left a will hoping he would study industrial technology.
D12.zh: 10岁时父亲去世，遗嘱希望他学工业技术。

D7.en: At 25, he told his mother he had joined the Communist Party and must attend weekly meetings.
D7.zh: 25岁时对母亲说明已加入共产党，每周支部会议非去不可。

D13b.en: At 20, due to family financial difficulties, he stopped studying and joined the Commercial Press through a friend's introduction.
D13b.zh: 20岁时因家庭经济困难，经友人介绍入商务印书馆工作。

D3.en: At 11, he was deeply impressed by tragic songs, admired patriotic heroes, and developed progressive thoughts.
D3.zh: 11岁时对悲壮歌曲印象深，崇拜忧国忧民的英雄，萌生救国思想。

D9.en: At 25, he was one of 12 signatories of the Literature Research Association's manifesto.
D9.zh: 25岁时作为十二人之一在文学研究会宣言上签名。

D2.en: At 24, he translated four communist texts in one go.
D2.zh: 24岁时一口气翻译了四篇共产主义译文。

M1.en: At 8-11, he won prizes in monthly exams and became famous at Lixiao Primary School for his history essays.
M1.zh: 8到11岁间每月考试得奖，史论文章使他在立志小学出名。

D10.en: At 10, when his mother tried to beat him with a ruler, he felt wronged and fled.
D10.zh: 10岁时母亲拿戒尺要打他，他满腹委屈夺门而逃。

D11.en: At 21, he spent over half a year compiling 'A First Collection of Chinese Fables'.
D11.zh: 21岁时用半年多时间编成《中国寓言初编》。

D6.en: At 23, he co-founded the 'Tongxiang Youth Society' with his brother and friends.
D6.zh: 23岁时和弟弟等人发起成立桐乡青年社。

D4.en: He spent over three months translating three books by Carpenter on food, clothing, and shelter.
D4.zh: 他花了三个多月时间翻译卡本脱关于衣、食、住的三本书。

D15.en: He was physically frail and couldn't handle the intense school sports training.
D15.zh: 他身体单薄，无法适应学校高强度的体育训练。

D16.en: He wrote an essay ending with 'A man should take the world as his duty.'
D16.zh: 他以'大丈夫当以天下为己任'结尾写了一篇文章。

M2.en: He wished to become famous and repay his parents.
M2.zh: 他渴望成名成家以报答父母。

M4.en: He read Western philosophy and sociology, then chose Marxism.
M4.zh: 他阅读大量西方哲学和社会学著作后选择了马克思主义。

M7.en: He read Western philosophy and sociology, then chose Marxism.
M7.zh: 他阅读大量西方哲学和社会学著作后选择了马克思主义。

D1.en: His childhood hardships deeply stimulated and motivated him.
D1.zh: 少年时代的生活磨难深深刺激并激励了他。

D20.en: The Central Committee appointed him as a courier due to his position at the Commercial Press.
D20.zh: 中共中央因他在商务印书馆的有利条件，决定由他担任交通员。

D14.en: He hid in bed, pretending to be a meditating monk, making his cousin laugh.
D14.zh: 他躲进床里扮和尚打坐，逗得表弟笑弯了腰。

D3.en: He discovered Cooper Union's reading room and read newspapers for hours, sparking his interest in politics.
D3.zh: 他发现库珀联盟的阅览室，花数小时读报纸，激发了对政治的兴趣。

D14.en: He could entrance people with his voice and speech, described as fresh, romantic, and hopeful.
D14.zh: 他能用嗓音和演讲迷住人，被形容为清新、浪漫、充满希望。

D12.en: At 12, his two eldest brothers went to work to support the family, while he and his younger brother entered P.S. 25.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他的两个哥哥去工作养家，而他和弟弟进入了第25公立学校。

D18.en: His father sold linens from the family apartment and peddled door to door in summer.
D18.zh: 他父亲在公寓里卖亚麻布，夏天挨家挨户推销。

D16.en: At City College, he developed skill as a public speaker and debater.
D16.zh: 在纽约市立学院，他培养了公开演讲和辩论的技巧。

D9.en: He was vice president of the senior class and assistant editor of a college magazine.
D9.zh: 他担任高年级副班长和大学杂志助理编辑。

D1.en: After doing poorly on tests, he buckled down and ended up first in the class.
D1.zh: 考试考砸后，他埋头苦干，最终成为全班第一。

D2.en: He buckled down and remained first in the class for the three years he was there.
D2.zh: 他埋头苦干，在接下来的三年里一直保持全班第一。

D20.en: He was vice president of the senior class and assistant editor of a college magazine.
D20.zh: 他担任高年级副班长和大学杂志助理编辑。

M1.en: He graduated from City College at nineteen, third highest in his class.
M1.zh: 他19岁时以全班第三名的成绩从城市学院毕业。

D4.en: He brought his first case to trial, prosecuting a man who falsely posed as a secret service agent.
D4.zh: 他首次出庭公诉，起诉一名冒充特工的男子。

M4.en: Antitrust cases he saw raised his political consciousness and crystallized his progressivism.
M4.zh: 他看到的反垄断案件提升了他的政治意识，并巩固了他的进步主义立场。

D11.en: At 13-14, he wrote sorrowful poems after his younger brother died of asthma.
D11.zh: 13到14岁时，他因四弟患喘去世而写悲感俚词。

D6.en: At 13-14, he had a crush on a girl called Yang Sangu.
D6.zh: 13到14岁时，他初恋的对象是杨三姑。

D4.en: At 16, he wore a white linen gown while buying groceries at the market.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他穿着白色夏布长衫去菜场买菜。

D3.en: At 11-15, he read many novels including Jing Hua Yuan at school and in Hangzhou.
D3.zh: 11到15岁时，他在三味书屋读《镜花缘》，在杭州读了很多小说。

D12.en: At 13-16, he took on family duties like collecting rent and paying taxes after his father died.
D12.zh: 13到16岁时，他因父亲早死而担负起收租完粮的责任。

D10.en: At 14, he felt both upset and relieved imagining her tragic death.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他想象她悲惨的死相，既觉得不快，又似乎很安静，仿佛心里一块大石头放下了。

D2.en: At 14, he started keeping a diary on February 18, 1898.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他于1898年2月18日开始记日记。

D13b.en: At 13-15, he took the imperial exam with a pious attitude.
D13b.zh: 13到15岁时，他以虔诚心情参加最后一次八股取士考试。

D9.en: At 13-16, he took on family duties like collecting rent and paying taxes.
D9.zh: 13到16岁时，他担负起收租完粮的主要责任。

D7.en: At 16, he felt depressed and longed to travel abroad, wanting to abandon his pen for a sword.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他连日郁郁不快，想乘长风破万里浪去海外，投笔执戈。

D1.en: At 13-15, he failed the imperial exams after repeated attempts.
D1.zh: 13到15岁时，他反复考试后落榜了。

D16.en: At 15, he recorded in his diary that the Boxers destroyed foreign buildings and railways.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他在日记里记下义和拳拆毁洋房、电杆和铁路。

D15.en: At 15, he sat bare-chested and bareheaded, legs spread, under the cabin.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他光着上身光着头，叉开腿坐在船舱下面。

D17.en: At 13-16, he had to take on the main responsibility for his family.
D17.zh: 13到16岁时，他不得不担负起全家的主要责任。

D20.en: At 13-16, he had to take on the main responsibility for his family.
D20.zh: 13到16岁时，他不得不担负起全家的主要责任。

D20X.en: At 16, he felt depressed and wished to leave home to explore the world.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他连日郁郁不快，想乘风破浪去海外游历。

M1.en: At 13-15, he took the imperial exams with a devout heart.
M1.zh: 13到15岁时，他几乎以虔诚的心情去应试。

M2.en: At 13-16, he had to take on the main responsibility for his family.
M2.zh: 13到16岁时，他不得不担负起全家的主要责任。

M4.en: At 15, he believed it was time to expel the foreigners.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他认为驱逐洋人的时候到了。

M5.en: At 13-15, he took the imperial exam with a devout attitude.
M5.zh: 13到15岁时，他怀着虔诚的心情去参加科举考试。

M6.en: At 16, he longed to sail overseas and throw away his writing brush to fight.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他渴望乘长风破万里浪去海外游历，投笔从戎。

M7.en: At 15, he said it was time to drive out the foreigners.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他说驱逐洋人的时候到了。

M8.en: At 13, he bought a book about the Taiping Rebellion and was deeply impressed by its destruction.
M8.zh: 13岁时，他买了本讲太平天国的书，对破坏印象深刻。

D18.en: At 18, she was paid fifty dollars a month.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她每月挣50美元。

D2.en: At 12, she learned of Negro spirituals from her sister's attendance at St. Augustine's.
D2.zh: 12岁时，她从姐姐在圣奥古斯丁的经历中了解到黑人灵歌。

D14.en: At age 8-9, she was put out of the church choir.
D14.zh: 八九岁时，她被赶出教堂唱诗班。

D10.en: At 25, she melted in anger, glad she was not a candle.
D10.zh: 25岁时，她气得融化，庆幸自己不是蜡烛。

D6.en: At 25, she stayed in a rooming house operated by blacks, a large white two-story framed building called a hotel.
D6.zh: 25岁时，她住在一家黑人经营的寄宿公寓，一栋白色两层木结构建筑，被称为旅馆。

D13a.en: As a child and teenager, she could not address adults by first names, only as Uncle, Aunt, Cousin.
D13a.zh: 幼年和青少年时期，她不能直呼成年人名字，只能叫叔叔、阿姨、表亲。

D4.en: At 22, she first volunteered at LDF for about four weeks in spring and summer 1945.
D4.zh: 22岁时，她第一次在LDF（法律援助基金会）做志愿者，大约四周，在1945年春夏。

M1.en: At 17-18, she won a prize for an essay on tuberculosis.
M1.zh: 十七八岁时，她因一篇关于结核病的文章获奖。

D3.en: Between ages 11 and 21, Father Edwards introduced her to black history through lectures and books.
D3.zh: 11到21岁间，爱德华兹神父通过讲座和书籍向她介绍黑人历史。

D16.en: As a teenager, she debated adults on current events and socialized with older people.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，她与成年人辩论时事，并与年长的人交往。

D20.en: She started working at the NAACP Legal Defense and Educational Fund in October 1945.
D20.zh: 她于1945年10月开始在NAACP法律辩护和教育基金工作。

D11.en: In high school, she wrote a poem that her English department head published in a textbook.
D11.zh: 高中时，她写了一首诗，被英语系主任收录在教材中。

D7.en: She felt anger when Bob pulled her out the door.
D7.zh: 当鲍勃把她拉出门时，她气得发火。

M4.en: She went to Jackson ten days early to prepare witnesses and exhibits.
M4.zh: 她提前十天去杰克逊准备证人和证据。

M7.en: She believed their time had come to launch a broad legal assault on segregated education.
M7.zh: 她相信他们的时机已到，应当对隔离教育发起广泛的法律攻击。

D12.en: At 17, she quit school to run the store, care for siblings, and nurse her dying mother.
D12.zh: 17岁时，她辍学经营店铺、照顾弟妹并护理病危的母亲。

D4.en: As a child, she listened to her father play the trombone for her.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她听父亲为她吹长号。

D3.en: At 18, she went on a summer school trip to Algeria and Europe.
D3.zh: 18岁时，她参加了去阿尔及利亚和欧洲的暑期学校旅行。

D7.en: She suspected prejudice when her invitation to sing at an IODE concert was withdrawn.
D7.zh: 她怀疑自己的IODE音乐会演唱邀请被取消是出于偏见。

D13a.en: At 12, she would squeeze her eyes shut and pretend to go along, not admitting her doubts.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，她会紧闭双眼假装顺从，不承认内心的疑虑。

D15.en: At 10, she cracked her collarbone on a doorstop stick left out by a boy.
D15.zh: 10岁时，她撞上一个男孩留下的门挡棍，摔断了锁骨。

D17.en: At 10, she loved helping her mother bathe and wheel her baby sister in a big black buggy.
D17.zh: 10岁时，她喜欢帮妈妈给妹妹洗澡，推着她的大黑婴儿车。

D10.en: At 25, she felt the room swirl and fainted.
D10.zh: 25岁时，她感觉房间旋转，晕倒了。

D1.en: At 18, she refused to drop out in ignominy and sought a respectable exit.
D1.zh: 18岁时，她拒绝不光彩地退学，拼命寻找体面的出路。

D11.en: At 12, her books took her to strange and distant worlds for a few hours.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她的书带她进入陌生遥远的世界几个小时。

M1.en: At 21, she graduated from law school with the Horace Harvey Gold Medal for top grades.
M1.zh: 21岁时，她以最高分从法学院毕业，获得霍勒斯·哈维金奖章。

M8.en: At 12, she began to question the religious beliefs she once held sacred.
M8.zh: 12岁时，她开始质疑曾经视为神圣的宗教信仰。

D9.en: At 25, she graduated from law school in 1968.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她于1968年从法学院毕业。

D9.en: At 20-22, he published many articles with serious errors in several newspapers and magazines.
D9.zh: 20到22岁时，他在多家报刊上发表了不少观点有严重错误的文章。

D14.en: At 25-26, he wrote pamphlets introducing Russian revolutionary democrats and populist leaders.
D14.zh: 25到26岁时，他编写了介绍俄国革命民主主义者及民粹派领袖的小册子。

D6.en: As a child and young man, he often interacted with servants, sympathized with them, and earned their respect.
D6.zh: 他从小常与仆人往来，同情他们的命运，得到他们的敬爱。

D13b.en: At 15-16, his grandfather allowed him to take English classes at the YMCA and hoped to get him a job at a post office.
D13b.zh: 15到16岁时，祖父准许他去基督教青年会上英文课，并想为他在邮局谋职。

D7.en: At about 5-6, he hid in a stable bed during New Year's Eve worship, refusing to come out when called.
D7.zh: 约5到6岁时，除夕敬神时他躲在马房轿夫床上，别人叫他也不应。

D1.en: At 23, he wrote letters all day to protest the 'gold dollar country' and poured his feelings into a notebook.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他写了一天的信控诉那个‘金圆国家’，又把心情写在练习本上。

M7.en: He became an uncompromising critic of the old society.
M7.zh: 他成为一个不妥协的旧社会批判者。

D11.en: At 19, he published a short poem titled 'Night Boat' in a women's magazine.
D11.zh: 19岁时，他在《妇女杂志》上发表了一首题为《黑夜行舟》的小诗。

D2.en: At 24-26, he wrote and translated anarchist works based on Russian revolutionaries.
D2.zh: 24到26岁间，他根据俄国革命者事迹编写并翻译无政府主义著作。

D10.en: At 5-6, he hid in a sedan chair bearer's bed during New Year worship, ignoring calls.
D10.zh: 5到6岁时，除夕敬神时他躲在轿夫床上，不应别人的呼唤。

D5.en: As a child to young adult, he left home thinking not of himself but of joining the masses.
D5.zh: 幼年到青年时，他离家时想的不是个人，而是投身群众之中。

D4.en: At 20-25, he left Sichuan for Shanghai and Nanjing for progressive activities, then went abroad for revolutionary theory.
D4.zh: 20到25岁间，他出川到上海南京参加进步活动，又远赴西方寻求革命理论。

D20.en: At 22-23, he co-founded the monthly magazine Equality with other anarchists in France.
D20.zh: 22到23岁时，他与留法无政府主义者创办《平等》月刊。

D20X.en: At 15, he formed a study group that was soon banned by his sixth sister's mother.
D20X.zh: 15岁时，他组织了一个研究会，很快被六姐的母亲禁止了。

M6.en: At 20-21, he engaged in theoretical research on anarchism.
M6.zh: 20到21岁时，他从事无政府主义的理论研究。

D15.en: At 23-24, he walked alone across a bridge to the woods by the river every morning and after lunch.
D15.zh: 23到24岁时，他每天早晨和午饭后独自走过小桥到河边树林散步。

M1.en: At 24-25, the success of Destruction would change his life choices.
M1.zh: 24到25岁时，《灭亡》的成功将改变他的人生选择。

D16.en: He published articles mocking Qian Xingcun in Free Monthly.
D16.zh: 他在《自由月刊》上发表文章嘲讽钱杏邨。

D19.en: He used five hardcover notebooks to organize and copy the manuscript of Destruction.
D19.zh: 他用五个硬纸面练习本整理和抄写《灭亡》全稿。

M2.en: He hoped his brother would return from studies and bring honor to the family.
M2.zh: 他希望弟弟学成归来，耀祖光宗。

D3.en: At 24, she fell ill while studying in the US and wrote to her father about it.
D3.zh: 24岁时，她在美国留学时旧病复发，写信给父亲。

D11.en: At 19-20, she read Tagore's translation manuscript via a fellow townsman and was captivated.
D11.zh: 19到20岁时，她通过同乡郑振铎读到泰戈尔的翻译手本，被征服了。

D14.en: At 23, she rose early, went to Lake Weibing, and continued writing 'To Little Readers'.
D14.zh: 23岁时，她早早起床，到慰冰湖畔继续写作《寄小读者》。

D1.en: At 24, she was ill and isolated from the world and studies for half a year.
D1.zh: 24岁时，她病得与世隔离、与学业隔离长达半年。

D17.en: At 23-24, she went daily to the cowshed to feed a large dappled horse.
D17.zh: 23到24岁时，她每天到牛棚喂那匹斑白大马。

D6.en: At 24, she went on a boat trip with 15 companions and was teased by Dr. B to act as helmsman.
D6.zh: 24岁时，她与15名游伴乘船出海，B博士开玩笑让她当舵手。

D12.en: At 13, she began to understand her mother's difficulty in managing household chores.
D12.zh: 13岁时，她开始懂得母亲料理家务的难处。

D2.en: At 24, she made reading, contemplation, and enjoying nature her daily routine, feeling grateful.
D2.zh: 24岁时，她以读书、凝想、赏明月、看朝霞为日课，心中空灵，感谢造物者。

D15.en: At 23, she felt she had passed the first storm test and was proud to be her father's daughter.
D15.zh: 23岁时，她感到经受住了第一次风浪考验，骄傲地宣布配作父亲的女儿。

D4.en: At 23, she brought narcissus from Fujian to the US and tended it herself.
D4.zh: 23岁时，她把福建水仙花带到美国，自己添水修剪。

D7.en: At 7-11, she took back a torn book and kept reading.
D7.zh: 7到11岁时，她把撕成两段的书拿起来接着看。

D18.en: At 11, she donated her saved 10 yuan to a newspaper.
D18.zh: 11岁时，她捐出了积攒的十块压岁钱给《申报》。

D9.en: At 14, she asked her uncle to let her go to school.
D9.zh: 14岁时，她向舅舅提出要去学校上学。

D13a.en: At 19, she was a pre-med student at a women's college.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，她在协和女子大学理预科读书，一心想当医生。

M1.en: At 14, she resolved to excel in math and sciences.
M1.zh: 14岁时，她下决心要学好数学和数理化功课。

M4.en: At 15, she joined patriotic protests against Japan and Yuan.
M4.zh: 15岁时，她毅然参加了反日讨袁的示威游行和募捐。

D16.en: At 19, she organized groups to attend court hearings.
D16.zh: 19岁时，她组织人马开进法庭去听审。

M7.en: At 24, she declared her love for Beijing and its people.
M7.zh: 24岁时，她说北京有她的爱，便是一切。

D10.en: At 24, she felt homesick in a sanatorium, thinking of Yantai.
D10.zh: 24岁时，她在疗养院思念故乡烟台。

D12.en: At 13-15, he took on family burdens after his father fell ill, handling external affairs.
D12.zh: 13到15岁时，父亲病后他挑起家中重担，出面交涉外界事务。

D11.en: At 13-14, he and his brother Zhou Zuoren invented fairy tales about immortal mountains and giant ants.
D11.zh: 13到14岁时，他和周作人编童话，讲仙山上有巨蚁和仙人。

D7.en: At 11-17, he and classmates stormed a teacher's private school, overturning inkstones and breaking bamboo slips.
D7.zh: 11到17岁时，他和同学冲进私塾，打翻砚台折断竹签。

D17.en: At 8, he wept in a corner after his baby sister died, saying 'For my little sister.'
D17.zh: 8岁时，妹妹端姑病逝，他躲在屋角哭说“为妹妹啦”。

D13b.en: At 6, he began formal schooling, first with relatives and later at Sanwei Bookhouse.
D13b.zh: 6岁时，他开蒙读书，先随本家亲戚学，后到三味书屋读经。

D4.en: At 6-12, he traced all illustrations from a borrowed novel and later bought painting manuals to copy.
D4.zh: 6到12岁时，他描下借来的《荡寇志》绣像，又买画谱一页页描。

D6.en: At 22, he joined the anti-Qing group Zhejiang Study Society with Tao Chengzhang and others.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他与陶成章等人加入反清团体“浙学会”。

D1.en: At 13-15, he took on family burdens after his father fell ill, handling external affairs.
D1.zh: 13到15岁时，父亲病后他挑起家中重担，出面交涉外界事务。

M2.en: Zhou Jiefu aspired for his descendants, including Lu Xun, to all become Hanlin scholars.
M2.zh: 周介孚希望儿孙包括鲁迅都考取翰林

M1.en: At 18-20, he often ranked first in exams without much review.
M1.zh: 18到20岁时，他几乎不温习却常考全班第一名。

D10.en: At 17, he felt heartbroken and couldn't stop tears when surrounded by strangers in a foreign land.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他身处异乡，满眼陌生人，满耳异乡话，想到家乡万里，柔肠欲断，涕不可遏。

D9.en: At 25-26, he planned a magazine, gathered contributors, secured funding, and named it 'New Life'.
D9.zh: 25到26岁间，他计划办杂志，聚集了撰稿同志，有人提供经费，他设计封面、选插图，定名《新生》。

D3.en: At 22, he wrote to his brother recommending John Stuart Mill's 'A System of Logic'.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他写信给周作人，推荐约翰·穆勒的《名学部甲》。

D5.en: At 18-20, he angrily chased stone-throwers on horseback, fell off, and was bleeding from the head.
D5.zh: 18到20岁间，他大怒，扬鞭追赶掷石者，摔下马来，跌得头破血流。

D14.en: At 22, he published impassioned writing 'The Soul of Sparta'.
D14.zh: 22岁时，他发表了慷慨激昂的《斯巴达之魂》。

D15.en: At 21-23, he practiced judo and wrestling at a dojo with friends, and owned a sword.
D15.zh: 21到23岁间，他和朋友去讲道馆练柔道、摔跤，还备了一把刀。

M4.en: At 24-25, he accepted a dangerous mission but asked who would support his mother if he was beheaded.
M4.zh: 24到25岁间，他接受了任务，但临行前问：如果被砍头，谁赡养母亲？

D2.en: At 24, he sold his fur coat to buy winter clothes and food for his mother, then studied in a freezing empty room.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他卖掉皮袍给母亲添置寒衣和买米面，然后在冰冷的空屋里用功。

D11.en: As a small child, he spent hours making toys from cotton, thread, and cloth scraps.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他花半天时间用棉花、小线、布头捏小鸡小鸟。

D7.en: At 24, he fiercely criticized religious practices for children, opposing prayers, Bible memorization, and fairy tales.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他全力抨击宗教对儿童的做法，反对赎罪祷告、背诵圣经和神仙故事。

D3.en: At 25, he wrote a calligraphy scroll for a friend with the motto 'Faithful and fond of learning' and a note on virtue.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他写了一个条幅“笃信好学”送给好友，附注关于读书达理、做真君子的文字。

D5.en: At 21-22, he started drinking, visiting parks, listening to opera, and often gave his wallet to a rickshaw driver when drunk.
D5.zh: 21到22岁间，他开始喝酒，逛公园听戏，醉酒后常把手绢钱包交给洋车夫。

D4.en: From age 5-6, he helped with silkworm raising, from cleaning ants to the cocoon stage.
D4.zh: 从五六岁起，他就帮忙养蚕，从掸蚁到蚕宝宝上山都会做。

D6.en: He brought out red paper slips with riddles he wrote and had us hang them on a wire.
D6.zh: 他拿出自己写的谜语红纸条，让我们挂在铁丝上。

D20.en: At 21-23, he served as a school inspector in the outer city's northern suburbs.
D20.zh: 21到23岁时，他担任外城北郊劝学员。

M1.en: At 11-14, he excelled in his studies and was often praised by teachers.
M1.zh: 11到14岁时，他成绩拔尖，常得到老师夸奖。

M4.en: At 20, he said the May Fourth Movement gave him new eyes and stirred his heart.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他说五四给了他新眼睛，让他的心无法平静。

D13a.en: At 22, he refused the marriage his grandmother arranged for him.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他拒绝了祖母给他订的婚。

D16.en: At 18, he improvised a couplet at a classmate's wedding, praised by all.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他在同学婚礼上即席作对子，大家都说对得工。

D15.en: At 9-10, he tried to work a waterwheel and injured his left leg.
D15.zh: 9到10岁时，他试车水，被踏脚打伤了左腿。

D10.en: At 9, he was heartbroken and tearful for his mother's suffering.
D10.zh: 9岁时，他眼里装满泪水，心痛他可怜的妈妈。

D18.en: His mother helped the poor and hosted traveling nuns annually.
D18.zh: 他母亲周济孤苦，每年留宿远村尼姑。

D17.en: As a small child, he had no milk and his mother fed him dried bread porridge.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他没奶吃，母亲把馍馍晾干煮成糊喂他。

D20X.en: At 22, he decided to rearrange his life.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他决定重新安排自己的生活。

M6.en: As a teenager, he buried himself in books, his paradise, and gritted his teeth to study hard.
M6.zh: 十几岁时，他宁肯钻到书里去，书是他的乐园，他咬着牙发奋念书。

M7.en: At 20, he developed critical eyes that gave him the basic thoughts and feelings for his later writing.
M7.zh: 20岁时，这双批判的眼睛给了他后来写作的基本思想和情感。

M5.en: He avoided worldly wealth and status, feeling indignant at others' disdain.
M5.zh: 他对世俗的荣华富贵躲得远远的，愤愤不平于别人的白眼和蔑视。

D1.en: After two years as a school inspector, he resigned in August 1922 to teach at Nankai Middle School in Tianjin.
D1.zh: 当了两年北郊劝学员后，他于1922年8月辞职，去天津南开中学教书。

D14.en: At 24, he explained parallel prose and occasionally improvised a Kunqu aria or a Peking Opera segment, astonishing students.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他讲解骈文，偶尔即兴唱昆曲或京戏，使学生们大为惊讶。

D8.en: At 15, he took the gong hammer but was too sad to speak, so he let it go.
D8.zh: 15岁时，他接过锣锤，但悲从中来，不能成语，就算了。

D11.en: She published two unsigned playful poems in a school magazine, mocking two male teachers.
D11.zh: 她在《国光》上登了两首未署名的打油诗，调侃了两位男教师。

D14.en: She published novels and essays like 'The Golden Cangue' and 'Love in a Fallen City' in several magazines.
D14.zh: 她在《紫罗兰》《万象》等杂志上发表了《金锁记》《倾城之恋》等小说散文。

D6.en: She was accompanied by her aunt and Yan Ying when meeting guests or in public.
D6.zh: 她由姑姑和炎樱陪同见客或去公共场合。

D12.en: She interrupted her studies due to the war and returned to Shanghai.
D12.zh: 她因战事影响中断学业，回到上海。

D18.en: At 24, she discussed specific issues like monthly manuscript fees.
D18.zh: 24岁时，她讨论了每月稿费收入之类的具体问题。

D16.en: She participated in a recorded dialogue with Su Qing on women, family, and marriage.
D16.zh: 她参加了《苏青张爱玲对谈记》，讨论妇女、家庭、婚姻问题。

D3.en: She was admitted to the University of London but switched to the University of Hong Kong due to the war.
D3.zh: 她考取伦敦大学，因战事改入香港大学。

D2.en: She temporarily gave up writing fiction and reading chapter novels for three years.
D2.zh: 她暂时放弃了写小说和看章回小说的嗜好，持续三年。

D13a.en: She stopped writing for nearly two years from 1945 to 1947 due to a rift with Hu Lancheng and unfavorable circumstances.
D13a.zh: 她因与胡兰成的裂隙和不利情势，从1945年8月到1947年4月没有发表任何文字。

D9.en: At 25, she participated in a dialogue arranged by a magazine reporter.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她参加了一场由杂志记者组织的对谈。

D7.en: She was threatened with not graduating for publishing a satirical poem.
D7.zh: 她因发表打油诗被威胁不能毕业。

M1.en: She vowed to surpass her idol in fame.
M1.zh: 她发愿要超过她的偶像的名气。

D13b.en: She studied to prepare for the University of London exam.
D13b.zh: 她补书预备考伦敦大学。

D1.en: She vowed to surpass her idol in fame.
D1.zh: 她发愿要超过她的偶像的名气。

D4.en: She only kept up painting because it didn't take much time.
D4.zh: 她只保留了绘画，因为不占太多时间。

D5.en: She took her biggest risk by letting go.
D5.zh: 她最大胆的一次撒手。

D8.en: She insisted big newspapers were uninteresting due to gray language.
D8.zh: 她不肯让步，偏说大报使用灰色语汇，毫无兴趣。

D10.en: She felt everything was fuzzy and hopeless during the siege.
D10.zh: 她回忆围城时感到一切模糊、瑟缩、靠不住，空虚与绝望。

D15.en: She took a rickshaw to the hospital daily for three months but still didn't know the way.
D15.zh: 她天天乘黄包车上医院打针三个月，仍然不认识那条路。

D17.en: She massaged his acupressure points to ease his pain.
D17.zh: 她会些穴位按摩，在他疼时按摩几下缓解疼痛。

D19.en: At 25, she ran to the printing house several times and negotiated with the printer three times to get a photo right for her book.
D19.zh: 25岁时，她为《流言》的照片跑印刷所交涉了不下三次。

D20.en: At 25, she participated in a dialogue organized by a magazine reporter.
D20.zh: 25岁时，她参加了杂志记者搞的一次对谈。

M2.en: At around 38, she felt family heirlooms should go to those who value them.
M2.zh: 38岁左右，她觉得家里的东西应该留给珍惜它们的人。

M3.en: As a teenager aged 12-17, she admired Lin Yutang and saw him as a role model.
M3.zh: 12到17岁时，她特别心仪林语堂，把他当作参照人物。

M4.en: At 24, she declared that a thorough individualist does not bow to others and only listens to inner commands.
M4.zh: 24岁时，她说彻底的个人主义者不屈己，不干人，唯自我内心的命令是听。

M5.en: At 24, she dared to say she could speculate about Su Qing purely from a novelist's perspective.
M5.zh: 24岁时，她敢说她能纯粹以写小说的态度推测苏青。

M6.en: At 24, she indulged in what may have been her most romantic flight and boldest letting go.
M6.zh: 24岁时，她沉溺于可能是她一生中最浪漫的飞扬和最胆大的撒手。

M7.en: At 24, she firmly held a female standpoint and consciously viewed society from a woman's perspective.
M7.zh: 24岁时，她牢守女性本位，自觉地从女性立场看社会。

M8.en: At age 24, she was immersed in what might be her most romantic and daring moment.
M8.zh: 24岁时，她沉溺于可能是一生中最浪漫最大胆的时刻。

D3.en: At 20-21, he exchanged letters full of quotes from Yeats and Blake.
D3.zh: 20到21岁时，他信中常引用叶芝和布莱克的诗句。

D7.en: At 13-14, he demanded what legitimate basis Jenifer claimed for her authority over him.
D7.zh: 13到14岁时，他质问珍妮弗有什么正当理由对他行使权威。

D10.en: At 25, his diary served as the kind of dialogue he would now have with a friend, partner, or therapist.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他的日记充当了那种他如今会与朋友、伴侣或治疗师进行的对话。

D6.en: He inspired Herbert to think and talk better, so Herbert resolved to talk with him more.
D6.zh: 他让赫伯特思考和表达得更好，所以赫伯特决心多和他交谈。

D15.en: At 18, he wrote to his parents about his matches with the First Rugby XV.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他写信给父母讲述他参加第一橄榄球队的比赛。

D1.en: At 21, acute jaundice left him bedridden for almost six weeks before his final exams.
D1.zh: 21岁时，急性黄疸让他期末考试前卧床近六周。

D5.en: At 14, he contemplated running away from school.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他考虑过从学校逃跑。

D9.en: At 20, he apologized for interrupting and asked for a book list.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他道歉打扰并请求一份书单。

D19.en: At 18, he nearly missed trains, kept incomplete accounts, and had diary gaps.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他差点错过火车，账目不全，日记也有空缺。

D16.en: At 18, he firmly wrote 'No' in the margin.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他在页边坚定地写下‘不’。

M1.en: At 21, he graduated with one of the top first class degrees.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他以当年最优秀的一等学位之一毕业。

D20.en: At 18-21, he became President of the Jowett Society.
D20.zh: 18到21岁间，他成为乔伊特学会主席。

D4.en: At 20, he took a bike tour through France, Italy, and Austria, with punctures and collisions.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他骑自行车穿越法国、意大利和奥地利，途中爆胎和碰撞不断。

D13a.en: At 23, he said wild horses wouldn't drag him to that exam again.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他说九头牛也拉不回他再去考那场试。

D17.en: He used his parents' home as a holiday retreat, though he had become distant from them.
D17.zh: 他把父母家当度假去处，尽管和父母疏远了。

M4.en: At about 21-23, he made a serious political and intellectual decision to join the Communist Party.
M4.zh: 约21到23岁间，他做出严肃的政治和思想决定，加入共产党。

D6.en: He said he never made the acquaintance of a finer set of fellows.
D6.zh: 他说他从未结识过更好的一群伙伴。

D3.en: He became quite interested, picking up information and attending one dissection and one operation.
D3.zh: 他变得很感兴趣，到处收集信息，并参加了一次解剖和一次手术。

D9.en: He was unanimously elected senior delegate to represent the Brown Chapter at the Delta Upsilon Convention at Amherst.
D9.zh: 他被一致推选为高级代表，代表布朗分会参加在阿默斯特举行的Delta Upsilon大会。

D7.en: He intended to reform many of their opinions, aided by professors and subtle English.
D7.zh: 他打算借助教授们的讲座和巧妙的英语，改变他们的许多看法。

D19.en: At age 6, he listed subjects to study at home with hours for each.
D19.zh: 6岁时，他列出在家自学的科目，并分配了时间。

D5.en: At 16, he participated in the forbidden cane rush and fist-swinging.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他参与了被禁止的藤杖冲刺和挥拳打斗。

D11.en: At 11, he wrote a theme about Warwick Castle after finding it fascinating.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他觉得沃里克城堡很迷人，回校后写了一篇作文。

D4.en: At 15, he worked on geometrical inventions and conjured up a figure for an original demonstration.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他继续搞几何发明，并构思了一个图形来做原创演示。

D14.en: At 18, he was the center of a crowd of boys congratulating him on his report.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他成为一群男孩的中心，他们围着他祝贺他的报告。

D1.en: At 20, he was determined to have a career at the bar but dissatisfied with his progress.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他决心在法律界发展，但对在律师事务所读书的进展不满意。

D13a.en: At 19, he said he would pull in his horns and let the other stand victor.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他说他会收敛自己，让对方成为胜利者。

M1.en: At 18, he proposed resolutions he had drawn up supporting Garfield.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他提出自己起草的支持加菲尔德的决议。

D16.en: At 18, he warmed to his theme until something gave way in his head and he ran on profusely.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他逐渐投入主题，直到脑子里好像有什么东西松开，他滔滔不绝地讲下去。

D18.en: At 15, he wrote claiming to be a ministerial student to get a free copy of Channing's works.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他写信谎称自己是神学生，免费得到了一本钱宁的著作。

M3.en: At 15, he thought of a man as embodying his ideals.
M3.zh: 15岁时，他认为一个人体现了他自己的理想。

D10.en: At 18, he talked for 20 minutes in class meeting, bathed in sweat and almost wilted.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他在班会上讲了大约20分钟，浑身湿透，几乎虚脱。

M6.en: At 18, he prayed for God to bless and sanctify him to His service.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他祈求上帝祝福并使他为上帝服务。

D20.en: At 18, he was unanimously elected senior delegate to represent Brown at the Delta Upsilon convention at Amherst.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他被一致推选为布朗分会的高级代表，参加在阿默斯特举行的ΔΥ兄弟会大会。

D17.en: At 19, he was cared for by Hughes due to no college infirmary.
D17.zh: 19岁时，他因大学没有医务室而被休斯照顾。

D13a.en: He gave up his dream and glided into law school.
D13a.zh: 他放弃梦想，滑入法学院。

D10.en: He never forgot how physically sick the thought of losing her made him feel.
D10.zh: 他永远忘不了想到失去她时那种生理上的恶心。

M7.en: He believed liberty depended on free interchange of ideas.
M7.zh: 他认为自由依赖于思想的自由交流。

M2.en: To please her, he spent hours copying his father's writings and obituaries.
M2.zh: 为了取悦她，他花数小时抄写父亲的文章和讣告。

D2.en: He worked as hard in his first year at Harvard as at Albany Academy.
D2.zh: 他在哈佛第一年像在奥尔巴尼学院一样努力。

D4.en: As a child, he joyfully prepared the evergreen hut at the campsite.
D4.zh: 他小时候在营地准备常青小屋，乐此不疲。

D18.en: At 9, he eagerly told his cousin about his growing stamp collection.
D18.zh: 9岁时，他兴奋地告诉表兄他不断增加的邮票收藏。

M8.en: At 14, he clung to Jesus Christ as his counsellor, guide, comforter and father.
M8.zh: 14岁时，他紧紧依靠耶稣基督作为他的顾问、向导、安慰者和父亲。

D9.en: At 20, he was elected president of the Harvard Advocate, a student literary magazine.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他被选为《哈佛倡导者》学生文学杂志的社长。

D15.en: At 16, he walked more than 100 miles in Alpine scenery with the headmaster.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他和校长在阿尔卑斯山风景中走了100多英里。

D7.en: At 18, he rejected the view that friends should be governed by antecedents or parentage.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝接受朋友应由出身或家世决定的观点。

D14.en: At 18-22, he served as president of the Harvard Advocate.
D14.zh: 18到22岁间，他担任《哈佛倡导者》的主席。

D20.en: At 20, he was elected president of the Harvard Advocate.
D20.zh: 20岁时，他被选为《哈佛倡导者》的主席。

D20X.en: At 22, he resigned from the Review after working on only four issues.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他在只参与了四期后从《评论》辞职。

D11.en: At age 22-25, he wrote essays for The New Republic.
D11.zh: 22到25岁间，他为《新共和》撰稿。

D12.en: As a small child, she was raised by her siblings because their mother was helpless.
D12.zh: 幼年时，她由兄弟姐妹抚养，因为母亲无能为力。

D2.en: At 5-9, she had to make two dozen handkerchiefs a week, stitching and ironing them.
D2.zh: 5到9岁时，她每周要做两打手帕，缝边并熨烫。

D4.en: At 10-12, she raced to stick her head out for air until Titi shooed her back in.
D4.zh: 10到12岁时，她冲过去把头伸出去透气，直到蒂蒂把她赶回去。

D1.en: At 24, she gave herself a shot and ate a sandwich with a towel as a bib.
D1.zh: 24岁时，她给自己打了一针，用毛巾当围嘴吃三明治。

D7.en: At 5-9, she stood outside Tío Foro's store to listen to the jukebox where men drank.
D7.zh: 5到9岁时，她站在蒂奥·福罗的店外听点唱机，那里男人们在喝酒。

D10.en: At 24, she said she has long known how to control her anger, but still feels it.
D10.zh: 24岁时，她说她早就知道如何控制愤怒，但并不意味着她不感到愤怒。

M1.en: At 17-18, she wanted to apply to Harvard, Yale, Princeton, Columbia, Stanford.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，她想申请哈佛、耶鲁、普林斯顿、哥伦比亚、斯坦福。

D6.en: At 18, she found herself with a group of girls in her resident adviser's dorm room.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她和一群女孩在宿舍辅导员的房间里。

D3.en: At 5-9, the best part of school for her was the library and carrying home a book.
D3.zh: 5到9岁时，她认为学校最好的部分是图书馆和带一本书回家。

D11.en: As a child, she copied poems onto slips of paper to learn them.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她把诗抄在小纸片上来背诵。

D18.en: At 15, she decided to get a job.
D18.zh: 15岁时，她决定找份工作。

D13a.en: She compromised and agreed to look at what someone wrote and give advice.
D13a.zh: 她妥协了，同意看别人写的东西并给出建议。

D15.en: She went on a road trip to the Rocky Mountains with Kevin during her second summer at Yale.
D15.zh: 她在耶鲁的第二个暑假和凯文一起自驾去了落基山脉。

D17.en: At 19-20, she spent days at someone's side, studying while they slept.
D17.zh: 19到20岁间，她守在别人身边，趁对方睡觉时学习。

M4.en: She noted that after Pearl Harbor, Puerto Rico was in shock and boys enlisted as soon as they were old enough.
M4.zh: 她注意到珍珠港事件后，波多黎各陷入震惊，男孩们一到年龄就参军。

D9.en: At 25, she served as Assistant District Attorney for New York County.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她担任纽约县助理地区检察官。

D16.en: At 14, a desperate mob surrounded her and another student, asking them to plead the class's case.
D16.zh: 14岁时，一群绝望的人围住她和另一个学生，要他们为全班辩护。

D14.en: At 21-22, she took to the podium to give a speech.
D14.zh: 21到22岁时，她走上讲台发表演讲。

M2.en: At 24, she resigned herself to a production that met her mother's needs.
M2.zh: 24岁时，她顺从了一场满足母亲需要的安排。

M6.en: At 23-24, she still remembered her childhood dream of becoming a judge.
M6.zh: 23到24岁时，她仍记得儿时当法官的梦想。

D12.en: At 18, she entered a women's school founded by Chen Duxiu and Li Da in Shanghai.
D12.zh: 18岁时，她进入陈独秀、李达等创办的上海平民女校。

D6.en: At 15, she joined student movements and taught abacus at a night school.
D6.zh: 15岁时，她参加学生运动，还在平民夜校教珠算。

D3.en: At 15, she transferred to a progressive school and read works by Bing Xin, Hu Shi, and others.
D3.zh: 15岁时，她转入周南女中，读了冰心、胡适等人的作品。

M1.en: At 23-24, she published stories 'Meng Ke' and 'Miss Sophia's Diary', shocking the literary world.
M1.zh: 23到24岁间，她发表《梦珂》和《莎菲女士的日记》，震惊文坛。

D13a.en: At 24, she agreed to divorce Chen Ming after becoming a mother.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，刚做母亲的她同意和陈明离婚。

D10.en: At 25, while listening, she suddenly cried, and Hu Yepin comforted her, saying light was before them.
D10.zh: 25岁时，她听着听着突然哭起来，胡也频安慰她说光明就在面前。

D11.en: At 23, she wrote her first short story 'Meng Ke' and first used the pen name Ding Ling.
D11.zh: 23岁时，她首次写短篇小说《梦珂》，第一次用笔名丁玲。

D4.en: At 25, while writing, she felt her baby move and patted her belly, telling it not to rush.
D4.zh: 25岁时，她写作时感到胎动，轻轻拍拍肚子说别着急。

D1.en: From 1927 to 1933, she published nearly thirty short stories.
D1.zh: 1927年到1933年间，她发表了近三十篇短篇小说。

D17.en: Her best friend Wang Jianhong died of illness in Shanghai, which pained her.
D17.zh: 她最好的挚友王剑虹在上海病逝，刺痛了她。

D5.en: At 25, she followed her husband Hu Yepin's advice to stay and explain to classmates.
D5.zh: 25岁时，她依胡也频意见留下向同学说明。

D16.en: At 25, she saw a young man surrounded by the masses, steady and confident, and felt unspeakable joy and faith.
D16.zh: 25岁时，她见到被群众包围的年轻他，稳重自信，感到说不出的欣喜与信心。

D18.en: At 25, she and others named their publishing house Red Black Press and set up the ground floor as the publication office.
D18.zh: 25岁时，她和别人将出版社取名为红黑出版社，把底楼做出版处。

D20.en: She and others divided tasks: she and Shen Congwen did some work, while Hu Yepin handled printing and business.
D20.zh: 她和别人做了分工，她和沈从文做些工作，胡也频负责印刷和业务。

D7.en: In 1921, she and some progressive students withdrew from school to protest the unjust dismissal of a teacher.
D7.zh: 1921年，她和一些进步同学一起退学，抗议校方无理解聘老师。

D12.en: He changed his name to Ziqing and styled himself Peixian, then entered the philosophy department a year early.
D12.zh: 他改名自清，字佩弦，提前一年考入哲学系。

D13b.en: He accepted an arranged marriage without objection.
D13b.zh: 他遵从父母之命接受了包办婚姻。

D2.en: He spent nearly three months explaining a 30-40 page booklet to someone.
D2.zh: 他花了近三个月给一个人讲解一本三四十页的小册子。

M1.en: He hoped to do his best in every aspect without regret.
M1.zh: 他希望在每个方面都尽最大努力，做到无憾。

D6.en: He corresponded with Wang Jingzhi, then took a week off to meet friends in West Lake.
D6.zh: 他和汪静之通信，请假一周去西湖会友，结识了冯雪峰和潘漠华。

D11.en: He helped compile a poetry collection titled 'Lakeside' with others.
D11.zh: 他参与编选诗集《湖畔》。

D14.en: He gave two lectures on 'What is Popular Education' and 'Rely on Yourself'.
D14.zh: 他上下午各讲演一次，题目是“平民教育是什么”和“靠自己”。

D4.en: He forgot the danger and leaned out from a pavilion to look at the pool.
D4.zh: 他忘记危险，走出亭子探身向潭水望去。

D19.en: He wrote a school song for the school soon after arriving.
D19.zh: 他一到学校就为学校写了一首校歌。

M4.en: At 23, he committed to art for life's sake, writing poems about reality and workers' suffering.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他自觉遵循为人生而艺术的宗旨，写下反映现实黑暗和劳动者痛苦的诗作。

M2.en: At 18, he never forgot his father's kindness in borrowing money for his education.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他永远难忘父亲借钱供他读书的仁慈。

M5.en: At 23, unable to bear the whispers of 'little white flowers without names', he decided to resign.
M5.zh: 23岁时，他经受不住“没有名字的小白花们”的私语，决定辞职。

M6.en: At 23, he felt he could not find a single smile in that corner of the world.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他感到在那人间的那角上找不着一个笑。

D10.en: He was deeply moved and could not forget it for a long time.
D10.zh: 他看后激动不已，久久不能忘记。

D7.en: At 23, feeling greatly insulted, he immediately resigned.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他感觉受了极大的侮辱，立刻辞了职。

D1.en: At 19, he decided to skip a grade and apply for Peking University's undergraduate program.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他决定跳级投考北京大学本科。

D18.en: At 22, he hardened his heart and pawned his purple fur-collared coat for fourteen yuan.
D18.zh: 22岁时，他硬了心肠将结婚时父亲给的紫毛水獭领大氅当掉，当了十四元钱。

D16.en: At 22-23, he published works in Fiction Monthly, including short story 'Farewell' and poems.
D16.zh: 22到23岁间，他在《小说月报》连续发表作品，包括短篇小说《别》及新诗。

M7.en: At 23, he thought it was a new poet's duty to help new poetry grow.
M7.zh: 23岁时，他认为为新诗的成长壮大做点努力是新诗人的分内之事。

D12.en: At 20, he wept recalling his cousin's murder at a desolate pavilion.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他想起堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D2.en: At 13, he enjoyed school reforms: less rote memorization, no severe beatings, weekly holidays.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他喜欢学校改革：不用整天背书，体罚废除，每周有假。

D4.en: At 19, he volunteered to climb over barbed wire to retrieve soccer balls kicked outside.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他自告奋勇爬过铁丝网捡球。

D3.en: At 13, he found mill and oil press fascinating, especially the waterwheel mechanism.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他觉得碾坊油坊最耐看，特别是水车。

D7.en: At 7, punishment for truancy made him more defiant; he played pranks with classmates.
D7.zh: 7岁时，挨打罚跪反而让他更叛逆，和学伴恶作剧。

D5.en: At 13, he and friends played pranks by sending boats adrift, then quickly escaping downstream.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他和伙伴们搞恶作剧，把船放到河中漂流，然后迅速向下游逃去。

D8.en: At 6, he played leader, acting as 'commander' seven times and pooling money for toy weapons.
D8.zh: 6岁时，他当七回‘都督’，凑钱买军器。

D6.en: At 22, Hu Yepin brought Ding Ling to visit him; she stood at the door smiling at him.
D6.zh: 22岁时，胡也频带丁玲来访，她站在门边对他笑。

D10.en: At 6, his father threatened to cut off his finger if he kept lying about truancy.
D10.zh: 6岁时，父亲威胁再逃学说谎就砍他手指。

D11.en: At 20, he covered his room with calligraphy notes vowing to surpass ancient and living masters.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他贴满字条，立志胜过锺王、压倒曾李。

D15.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D18.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D9.en: At 22, he discussed with friends starting a weekly magazine if they each earned 30 yuan.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他和朋友商量，如果每人每月赚30块就自己办个周刊。

D13a.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D14.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D20.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D20.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

M1.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D19.en: At 20, he wept recalling passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他想起路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住落泪。

D1.en: As a child, his father threatened to cut off his finger if he kept playing truant and lying.
D1.zh: 幼年时，父亲威胁他再逃学说谎就砍掉他一根手指。

M4.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

M7.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

M3.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M3.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

D16.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

D17.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D17.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

D20X.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
D20X.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

M2.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

M5.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M5.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

M6.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

M8.en: At 20, he wept while passing the pavilion where his cousin was killed.
M8.zh: 20岁时，他路过堂兄被害的长亭，忍不住流泪。

D2.en: He alternated weekly between Chinese classics and Western masterpieces, reading and taking notes for four years.
D2.zh: 他每周轮换读中文经典和欧美名著，四年如一日，每读完一本就做笔记。

D3.en: At 23, he sought a passport to the universe's secrets from holy books and rabbinic magic.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他试图从圣书和拉比的魔法中找到通往宇宙奥秘的护照。

D9.en: After graduating from Tsinghua, he chose to teach at Guanghua University.
D9.zh: 清华毕业后，他选择去光华大学教书。

M1.en: He earned his B.Litt. in two years, a big achievement.
M1.zh: 他花两年时间获得B.Litt.，这是一个大成就。

D11.en: He published his classical poetry collection 'Zhongshujun Shi' in 1934, printing 100 copies as gifts.
D11.zh: 1934年他出版旧体诗集《中书君诗》，印100册作为非卖品赠送友人。

D12.en: He was born on November 21, 1910, and was less than a year old when the Qing dynasty fell.
D12.zh: 他生于1910年11月21日，不到一岁时清朝被推翻。

D4.en: He liked to underline and annotate in library books with thick black lines.
D4.zh: 他喜欢在图书馆的书上用又黑又粗的笔画线并加评语。

D7.en: He failed math in the entrance exam but was admitted due to the president's intervention.
D7.zh: 他入学考试数学不及格，但因校长力争被破格录取。

D8.en: He dared to criticize department heads Zhu Ziqing and Feng Youlan in casual conversation.
D8.zh: 他敢在闲聊中挑剔系主任朱自清和冯友兰的学问。

D14.en: He wrote for the school magazine, then for Dagong Bao and Crescent Moon, gaining social influence.
D14.zh: 他先在校刊上用中英文写稿，后来为《大公报》和《新月月刊》写稿，有了社会影响。

D16.en: At 23, he received his father's books and directly commented on them in a letter.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他收到父亲写的书，并在信中直截了当地评论了一番。

D5.en: At 11, he played with a slingshot in class and shot mud pellets at others.
D5.zh: 11岁时，他在课堂上玩弹弓，用泥丸弹人。

D6.en: As a small child, he followed his uncle to teahouses and storytelling sessions.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他跟着伯父上茶馆、听说书。

D13b.en: At 24, he held his wedding ceremony with Yang Jiang at her family home in Suzhou.
D13b.zh: 24岁时，他与杨绛在苏州杨绛娘家大厅内举行结婚典礼。

D20.en: At 25-27, he wrote an English article about Wu Mi for T'ien Hsia at Wen Yuan-ning's request.
D20.zh: 25到27岁间，他应温源宁之嘱为《天下》写了一篇关于吴宓的英文稿。

D4.en: As a child, I tied a rope to my toy cart and pulled it to my grandmother's house.
D4.zh: 小时候，我在车头上拴根绳子拖着走，去外婆家。

D6.en: At 18, she gathered with friends in Shanghai to prepare for performing arts troupe auditions.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她和一伙朋友在上海聚集，准备报考文工团。

D7.en: At 12, I stepped forward and told a woman her pants were unbuttoned, trembling.
D7.zh: 12岁时，我一步窜上前，对那女人说：“同志，等一等！你的裤腿。”

D10.en: At 12, she trembled and said, 'Your pants leg,' feeling disaster looming.
D10.zh: 12岁时，她浑身发抖地说出‘你的裤腿’，感觉大祸临头。

D14.en: As a child, she was beautiful, intelligent, excelled in all subjects, sang well, and was eloquent.
D14.zh: 小时候，她形象美丽，极其聪慧，功课门门优秀，唱歌也好，口齿伶俐。

D13a.en: At 10-12, she was forced to blindly point at something in a cornered, humiliating situation.
D13a.zh: 10到12岁时，她被逼无奈，很卑屈地瞎指了一个。

D1.en: At 25-27, after failing the exam, she studied part-time while working and passed on her second try.
D1.zh: 25到27岁间，她落榜后边工作边补习，再次进考场终于考上。

M1.en: At 21-22, she nearly fainted when a college spot was given to a less qualified boy because of his background.
M1.zh: 21到22岁时，她因一个大学名额给了背景好的男生而几乎昏厥。

D5.en: At 23-24, she used a valid medical reason and strong determination to retire due to illness and return to Shanghai.
D5.zh: 23到24岁时，她以过硬的病由和极大的决心办了病退，回到上海。

D13b.en: At 16, he got engaged to Zhang Youyi while at Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D13a.en: At 18, he was dissatisfied with the marriage but bowed to parental pressure and the Zhang family's influence.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他对这桩婚姻不满意，但碍于父母之命和张家的声势，还是订婚并结婚了。

D3.en: At 13-15, he was an idle student who loved reading novels printed on glossy paper.
D3.zh: 13到15岁时，他平时不用功，总爱看印在光纸上的石印小说。

D2.en: At 16, his calligraphy showed talent; his elder brother noted the strength and spirit in his strokes.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他的书法透出才气，四哥看出他字里的骨和气。

D1.en: At 23, he fasted and went to Sun Yat-sen's mausoleum, threatening to die to urge the government to resist Japan.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他绝食后奔赴中山陵，要以身殉国催促政府抗日。

D6.en: At 13, he met Jiang Fucong during a school outing to Lin Dichen's grave in Hangzhou.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他在杭州孤山凭吊林迪臣时与蒋复璁相识。

D7.en: At 25, he declared that a marriage without love is unbearable and freedom must be repaid with freedom.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他宣称无爱的婚姻无法忍受，自由要用自由来偿还。

D8.en: At 18, he was dissatisfied with the marriage but bowed to parental pressure and the Zhang family's influence.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他对这桩婚姻不满意，但碍于父母之命和张家的声势，还是订婚并结婚了。

D9.en: At 23-24, he co-edited issues of the Crescent Moon magazine with Liang Shiqiu and others.
D9.zh: 23到24岁时，他与梁实秋等人合编了《新月》杂志第二卷第二至五期。

D10.en: At 23, he expressed intense feelings to Lin Huiyin, who was alarmed and unsure how to respond.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他用情之烈令林徽因感悚，她惶恐不知如何回答。

D11.en: At 22, he wrote 'Goodbye Again, Cambridge!' and vowed to return west in spring.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他写下《康桥再会吧》，发誓来春西航。

D12.en: At 16, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi while attending Hangzhou Middle School.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D14.en: As a teenager, he would unexpectedly do something light and strange to attract attention.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他会出其不意地做一件轻快奇特的事来吸引注意。

D15.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefecture Middle School.
D15.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D16.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefecture Middle School.
D16.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D17.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefecture Middle School.
D17.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D18.en: At 25, he helped Hong Kong police bust a drug trafficking ring smuggling into China.
D18.zh: 25岁时，他帮助香港警方缉拿了一个往中国贩毒的犯罪团伙。

D19.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefecture Middle School.
D19.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D20.en: At 16, he was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefecture Middle School.
D20.zh: 16岁时在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D20X.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefectural Middle School.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M1.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefectural Middle School.
M1.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M2.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefecture Middle School.
M2.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M3.en: At age 16, Xu Zhimo was engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Prefectural Middle School.
M3.zh: 16岁时，徐志摩在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M4.en: At 16, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M5.en: At 16, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M6.en: At 16, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
M6.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

M8.en: At 16, he became engaged to Zhang Youyi while studying at Hangzhou Fu Middle School.
M8.zh: 16岁时，他在杭州府中学上学时与张幼仪订婚。

D5.en: At 25, he helped Hong Kong police arrest a drug trafficking gang to China.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他帮助香港警方缉拿了一个往中国贩毒的犯罪团伙。

D12.en: At 23, she wrote a legal complaint for her father, who only changed a few words.
D12.zh: 23岁时，她帮父亲写诉状，父亲只改了几个字。

D2.en: At 22, she studied extra hard to catch up with classmates in foreign literature.
D2.zh: 22岁时，她格外用功学习外国文学。

D7.en: At 16, she refused to do propaganda work, and the principal later stopped it.
D7.zh: 16岁时，她拒绝去宣传，校长后来也不许再宣传。

D3.en: At 18-19, she read most of the reference books, though she didn't fully understand them.
D3.zh: 18到19岁间，她读了大半参考书，但半懂不懂。

D11.en: At 22-23, she wrote a short story that Zhu Ziqing praised and recommended for publication.
D11.zh: 22到23岁间，她写短篇小说，得到朱自清赞许并推荐发表。

D6.en: At 21, she met Qian Zhongshu at Tsinghua and they felt an instant connection.
D6.zh: 21岁时，她在清华遇见钱钟书，两人一见钟情。

D9.en: At age 9-12, she led the team because she understood the White Russian aristocrat teacher's English commands.
D9.zh: 9到12岁时，她因能听懂白俄贵族体操老师的英语口令而被挑为领队。

D5.en: At 21, she cleverly escaped the school with a friend during a student protest.
D5.zh: 21岁时，她巧妙带好友逃出学潮中的学校。

D14.en: She signed up to learn Kunqu opera after hearing masters perform at Tsinghua.
D14.zh: 她听说老师请了红豆馆主，赶紧报名学唱昆曲。

D18.en: At 22, she spent her first earnings on wool yarn and coffee candy.
D18.zh: 22岁时，她用第一笔稿费买了毛线和咖啡糖。
