D17.en: At 25, she nursed Qian Zhongshu with hot compresses every few hours.
D17.zh: 25岁时，她每隔几小时给钱钟书做热敷。

D4.en: At 25, she gathered friends to figure out how to cook braised pork.
D4.zh: 25岁时，她叫来朋友一起研究红烧肉的做法。

D1.en: As a child, she skipped two grades in English class.
D1.zh: 幼年时，她学英语连跳两级。

D20.en: At 25, she was asked to be a Communist delegate to the World Youth Congress.
D20.zh: 25岁时，她被邀请担任世界青年大会的共产党代表。

D10.en: At 24, she felt heartbroken at leaving her parents to marry.
D10.zh: 24岁时，她因要离开父母嫁人而心情悲苦。

D15.en: She demonstrated a jump with clap and foot slap in calisthenics.
D15.zh: 她在徒手操中示范了拍手同时拍脚的跳跃动作。

D19.en: At 25, she devoted herself to cooking lunch, calling Qian only when stuck.
D19.zh: 25岁时，她把全部爱倾注在午饭上，搞不定才喊钱钟书帮忙。

D20X.en: At 24, she was content as an auditor, enjoying time to read.
D20X.zh: 24岁时，她作为旁听生不觉得难过，反而享受读书时间。

M1.en: She finished a six-year curriculum in five years, graduating first in her class in 1928.
M1.zh: 她用五年修完六年课程，1928年以第一名的成绩毕业。

M2.en: Her husband credited her for his weight gain in a letter to his parents.
M2.zh: 钱钟书在给父母的信中说，自己变胖是她的功劳。

M4.en: She agreed to be a Communist delegate to the World Youth Congress to accompany her husband to Geneva.
M4.zh: 她同意当世界青年大会的共产党代表，以便名正言顺地陪钱钟书去日内瓦。

M5.en: At 24-26, she regarded fame and fortune lightly, not pursuing utility.
M5.zh: 24到26岁间，她把名利看得很淡，不追求功利。

M6.en: She chose to be an auditor at Oxford because she enjoyed the time with her husband and didn't pursue a degree.
M6.zh: 她觉得在牛津当旁听生的时光很美好，因为有一个互相懂得的男人在身边，所以没有争取学位。

D8.en: She drew a caricature of Fei Xiaotong in the sand with a stick.
D8.zh: 她用树枝在沙地上给费孝通画过一个丑像。

D2.en: At 18, he sat in the front row and didn't move as the teacher's spit wet his book and face.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他坐在第一排，老师的唾沫溅湿了课本和脸，他一动不动。

M1.en: At 15, he gritted his teeth and counted steps to reach the resting place, a habit that became his key to success.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他咬牙默数着数字赶到歇脚地，这种劲头成为他成功的保证。

D6.en: At 16-20, he and his friends decided to steal pumpkins and corn to put in Aunt Liu's kang to help her conceive.
D6.zh: 16到20岁间，他和伙伴们决定偷南瓜、包谷棒子给六婶塞炕，帮她生育。

D18.en: At 14-17, he and his sister dug along a tree root all day and got three baskets of firewood.
D18.zh: 14到17岁间，他和妹妹顺着树根刨了一天，弄到三背篓劈柴。

D7.en: At 14-17, he muttered 'That Pockmarked Tian...' and was scolded by others.
D7.zh: 14到17岁时，他嘀咕了一句'那田麻子……'，被旁边的人训斥。

D11.en: At 20, he felt everything was alive, imagining ghosts in the hospital and spirits or fox fairies on the street.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他觉得万物有灵，想象医院里有鬼，街上有神祇或狐狸精。

D13b.en: At 14, he graduated from junior high school in 1967.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他1967年初中毕业。

D9.en: At 14-17, he divided the bread with his fingernails, took dough, and left.
D9.zh: 14到17岁间，他用指甲分锅盔，拿面团走了。

D4.en: At 15, he and two classmates built a three-step platform as a good deed.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他和两个同学修了一个三层台阶，作为学雷锋做好事。

D5.en: At 14-17, he realized a stranger was fleeing and his parents warned him to keep quiet.
D5.zh: 14到17岁时，他意识到那个陌生人是偷渡出境，父母叮嘱他别说出去。

D15.en: At 16-18, he was stung by wasps while cutting grass, applied snot, and crossed a river.
D15.zh: 16到18岁时，他割草被蜂蜇了，用鼻涕抹了，背着草篓趟过齐腰深的河水。

M2.en: At 17, he helped his father write an appeal because his father's hands shook.
M2.zh: 17岁时，他帮父亲写翻案申诉书，因为父亲手颤握不住笔。

D10.en: At 16, he sat on a grave and feared a female demon might appear from a mouse hole.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他蹲在坟丘上，害怕鼠洞里冒出烟来现出女妖怪。

M7.en: As a child to young adult, he tipped off Sanwa's family to avoid study sessions.
M7.zh: 幼年到青年时，他给三娃家通风报信，逃避学习班开会。

D19.en: At 15, he chopped firewood into uniform pieces and stacked them neatly on the steps.
D19.zh: 15岁时，他把柴劈碎，长短整齐，在台阶上垒好，然后坐在捶布石上观赏。

M4.en: As a child to young adult, he tipped off Sanwa's family to avoid study sessions.
M4.zh: 幼年到青年时，他给三娃家通风报信，逃避学习班开会。

D13a.en: He skipped meetings and tipped off a family about raids.
D13a.zh: 他逃避学习班开会，还给三娃家通风报信。

D20X.en: He went home in anger, refused to attend the study class, and on pretext stayed at his aunt's in the county for over ten days.
D20X.zh: 他很气愤地回了家，不愿再去学习班，借故到县城他大姨家待了十多天没回来。

M5.en: At 15, he was robbed and went hungry, too ashamed to tell anyone.
M5.zh: 15岁时，他遭抢劫饿了大半天，却不敢对人说。

D14.en: At 18, his diary was stolen and read aloud in other companies.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他的日记本被人偷去当众念，还传到别的连队。

D12.en: At 4, he started walking home alone after his brother forgot him.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他开始自己走回家，因为哥哥把他忘了。

D13a.en: As a small child, he sat still all day in kindergarten until his mother picked him up.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，他上幼儿园一整天都坐在同一个位置等妈妈来接。

D7.en: As a child, he chased his brother with a badminton racket and hit him in the head.
D7.zh: 小时候，他拿着球拍追哥哥，扔出去打中了哥哥的头。

D4.en: At 19, as a dentist, he didn't know which pliers to use for a patient's tooth.
D4.zh: 19岁时当牙医，他不知道该用哪把钳子拔病人的牙。

D3.en: At 12, his father got him a library card, which thrilled him.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他父亲给他弄了张借书证，他欣喜若狂。

D5.en: At 25, he went on a risky trip to the mountains in western Sichuan.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他冒险去了四川西部的山区。

D10.en: As a teenager, he and his classmates were anxious about masturbation.
D10.zh: 十几岁时，他和同学们因为手淫而惶惶不可终日。

D11.en: At 12-16, he used a whole notebook to turn Lu Xun's 'Diary of a Madman' into a musical score.
D11.zh: 12到16岁时，他用一册作业本把鲁迅的《狂人日记》谱成了乐曲。

D18.en: At 14-16, he traded a meat bun for his brother's belt for a day.
D18.zh: 14到16岁时，他用一个肉包子换哥哥的腰带用一天。

D2.en: At 18-23, he bought every Kawabata book he could find, one to keep and one to study.
D2.zh: 18到23岁时，他买下所有能找到的川端康成作品，一本珍藏一本研读。

D1.en: He thought of his future pulling teeth on the same street, so he wrote with renewed confidence.
D1.zh: 他想到以后还是拔牙，守着海盐一条街，就又信心百倍地写作了。

M1.en: He thought of his future pulling teeth on the same street, so he wrote with renewed confidence.
M1.zh: 他想到以后还是拔牙，守着海盐一条街，就又信心百倍地写作了。

M3.en: Kawabata Yasunari became his most admired writer in his early writing years.
M3.zh: 川端康成成了他写作初期最崇拜的作家。

D9.en: In September 1985, he went on his first field trip with young writer Zhao Ruiyong.
D9.zh: 1985年9月，他和青年作家赵锐勇一起开始了第一次采风考察。

D3.en: At 11.5, she hid Dream of the Red Chamber under her skirt and read it in class.
D3.zh: 11岁半时，她把《红楼梦》藏在裙子下面在课堂上偷偷读。

D7.en: At 12, after being humiliated with ink, she started skipping school and then dropped out.
D7.zh: 12岁时，被墨汁涂脸后开始逃学，休学在家。

D4.en: At 18, she began learning sketching from Gu Fusheng after a friend showed her oil paintings.
D4.zh: 18岁时，朋友展示油画后，她开始跟随顾福生学素描。

D9.en: At 19, she chose philosophy as her major and told Mr. Zhang she wouldn't regret it.
D9.zh: 19岁时，她填了哲学系，告诉张先生绝对不会后悔。

D5.en: At 20-25, the day before leaving for the desert, she borrowed a motorcycle and sped through empty streets at night.
D5.zh: 20到25岁间，去沙漠前一天，她借了机车在深夜空旷大街上飞驰。

D11.en: At 19, she published her first work 'Confusion' in Modern Literature under her real name Chen Ping.
D11.zh: 19岁时，她以原名陈平在《现代文学》发表处女作《惑》。

D2.en: She gradually improved, but felt ashamed she couldn't sit on mats like the Sahrawi, and longed for a table.
D2.zh: 她逐渐有些进步，但羞愧自己无法像沙哈拉威人那样坐在席子上，渴望有张桌子。

D10.en: At 22, she held back tears watching José leave, later recalling he was a rare true love.
D10.zh: 22岁时，她强忍眼泪看着荷西远去，后来回忆他是难得真心相爱的人。

D13a.en: At 13-14, she refused to go to school after second year of junior high, and her father let her drop out.
D13a.zh: 13到14岁时，她初二后不肯再去学校，父亲让她休学。

D14.en: At 19, she secretly entered an art exhibition and won a bronze medal for a still life.
D14.zh: 19岁时，她瞒着父母报名艺林美展，一张《静物》拿到铜牌奖。

D6.en: At 22, Spanish boys serenaded her outside her dorm, with the last song dedicated to her.
D6.zh: 22岁时，西班牙男生在她宿舍窗外唱歌，最后一首是给她的。

D12.en: She carried heavy water tanks, stopping to catch her breath every few steps, her spine aching and legs trembling.
D12.zh: 她双手提着水箱的柄，走几步就停下来喘气，脊椎痛得发抖，步子发软。

D1.en: At 20-25, after carrying water tanks, she had to lie flat on a mat to ease her spine.
D1.zh: 20到25岁间，她提水箱回家后必须平躺席子上才能让脊椎舒服。

D15.en: At 20-25, she carried water tanks, walking a few steps then resting repeatedly.
D15.zh: 20到25岁间，她双手提水箱，走四五步停一停，再走十几步。

D16.en: At 20-25, she wrote to the editor to cancel her writing contract.
D16.zh: 20到25岁间，她给编辑写信取消稿约。

D18.en: At 20-25, she asked for five large wooden crates and hired two donkey carts to haul them.
D18.zh: 20到25岁间，她跟老板要了五个大木箱，叫了两辆驴车拉回去。

D20.en: At 20-25, she asked for five large wooden crates and hired two donkey carts to haul them.
D20.zh: 20到25岁间，她跟老板要了五个大木箱，叫了两辆驴车拉回去。

M4.en: At 20-25, she was drawn to the Sahara Desert, the lover in her heart.
M4.zh: 20到25岁间，她被内心深处的爱人撒哈拉沙漠吸引。

M5.en: At 20-25, angered that José thought her vain, she wanted to prove her capability.
M5.zh: 20到25岁间，她因荷西认为她虚荣而愤怒，想证明自己的生活能力。

M6.en: At 20-25, she felt an inexplicable nostalgia for the Sahara after seeing it in National Geographic.
M6.zh: 20到25岁间，她翻到一本《国家地理》杂志，看到撒哈拉沙漠，产生了一种说不清的乡愁。

M8.en: She flipped through a National Geographic issue on the Sahara and felt an inexplicable, past-life-like nostalgia for the desert.
M8.zh: 她无意间翻阅一本《国家地理》杂志，看到撒哈拉沙漠的介绍，产生了一种无法解释的、像前世回忆般的乡愁。

D19.en: At 24-25, she decorated her small room carefully and planted flowers in the yard.
D19.zh: 24到25岁间，她用心装潢小房间，在院子里种花种草。

M2.en: At 24-25, she worried about her parents' approval, as a Chinese girl might.
M2.zh: 24到25岁间，她像中国女孩一样担心父母是否同意。

D3.en: He learned practical aspects of play production unattainable from books.
D3.zh: 他学到了书本上学不到的戏剧制作的实际方面。

D12.en: As a child, he performed with his younger brother for his mother.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他和弟弟为母亲表演。

D4.en: At school, he learned oil painting and sculpture from artist Yun Zongying.
D4.zh: 他在学校师从艺术家云宗英学习油画和雕塑。

D9.en: At 18-22, he and his society members watched performances by Beijing People's Art Theatre and National Youth Theatre.
D9.zh: 18到22岁间，他和社团成员观看了北京人民艺术剧院和国家青年剧院的演出。

D11.en: By graduation, he had written two stage plays and a screenplay.
D11.zh: 到毕业时，他已经写了两部舞台剧和一个剧本。

D13a.en: Upon graduation, he was assigned as a translator in the Foreign Language Bureau.
D13a.zh: 毕业后，他被分配到外文局做翻译。

D2.en: He spent most spare time writing plays and stories, often working through the night.
D2.zh: 他大部分业余时间都用来写剧本和故事，经常通宵工作。

D12.en: At 11-14, he herded cattle and sheep alone, carrying his own food.
D12.zh: 11到14岁时，他一个人赶着牛羊去远处放牧，自己带干粮。

D3.en: From age 11 to 18, he roamed his hometown, studied medicine with his granduncle, and memorized hundreds of herbs.
D3.zh: 11岁到18岁间，他游荡在家乡，跟伯祖学医，背熟了几百味中药。

D4.en: As a child, he invented a trick: dyeing his hands green with grass juice before catching grasshoppers.
D4.zh: 小时候，他发明了一个诀窍：捉蚂蚱前先用青草汁把手染绿。

D2.en: At 24-25, he memorized textbooks by rote for a year, then revived his literary dream.
D2.zh: 24到25岁时，他天天死背教科书，一年后背熟，文学梦死灰复燃。

D6.en: At 18-21, he befriended two workers whose fathers were powerful officials, to gain their favor.
D6.zh: 18到21岁时，他结识了两名有背景的工友，特别注意团结他们。

D9.en: At 21, he wrote a letter to the commune's armed forces minister and had his friend deliver it.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他给公社武装部长写了一封信，让部长的儿子送去。

D7.en: At 23, he wrote a story about a landlord's daughter who joins the Eighth Route Army and returns to kill her traitor father.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他写了一个地主女儿加入八路军并回来打死汉奸爹的故事。

D5.en: At 18, he imitated Zhang Tiesheng and wrote a letter to the Minister of Education.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他模仿张铁生，给教育部长周荣鑫写了一封信。

D1.en: From 18 to 20, he applied to join the army every year but was rejected each time.
D1.zh: 18到20岁，他每年报名参军，每年都被刷下来。

D10.en: As a child, he often lost his resolve when seeing food, despite his mother's criticism.
D10.zh: 小时候，他常被母亲批评没志气，但一见食物就忘了。

D15.en: At 18, he dug the Jiaolai River with villagers in Changyi County.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他跟村里人去昌邑县挖胶莱河。

D13b.en: From age 11 to 18, he grew up quietly in the fields of his hometown.
D13b.zh: 11到18岁间，他在家乡的田野里悄无声息地成长。

M1.en: At 18-21, he desperately wanted to escape the land, with unyielding determination.
M1.zh: 18到21岁间，他逃离土地的愿望百折不挠。

D13a.en: At 25, he rushed home and changed his birth date to March 25, 1956, to get promoted.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他跑回老家把生日改成1956年3月25日，提干成功。

D18.en: At 18, he got a seasonal job at a cotton processing plant through his uncle's connections.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他通过五叔的关系走后门进棉花加工厂做季节工。

D11.en: At 18, he bought ink and a notebook with 5 mao from his mother and started writing 'By the Jiaolai River'.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他跟母亲要了五毛钱买墨水笔记本，趴在炕上开始写《胶莱河畔》。

M4.en: At 18-21, he desperately wanted to escape the land, with unyielding determination.
M4.zh: 18到21岁间，他逃离土地的愿望百折不挠。

D17.en: At 10, he secretly brought beans and carrots from home to feed the mother rabbit during her postpartum recovery.
D17.zh: 10岁时，他偷偷从家里拿豆子和胡萝卜给母兔子坐月子补充营养。

D19.en: At 18-21, he befriended two workers at the cotton plant whose fathers were powerful officials.
D19.zh: 18到21岁间，他在棉花加工厂结识了两名工友，特别注意团结他们。

D16.en: At 11, he shouted revolutionary slogans to smash the bourgeois education line.
D16.zh: 11岁时，他高喊革命口号要砸烂资产阶级教育路线。

D20.en: At 11, he formed a rebel group called the Caltrop Combat Team and wrote its manifesto.
D20.zh: 11岁时，他联合几个死党成立了“蒺藜战斗队”并写了战斗宣言。

D14.en: At 21, he was chosen to give a speech as a new soldier representative.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他被选为新兵代表讲话。

D20X.en: At 21, he joined the army without the militia company commander's knowledge.
D20X.zh: 21岁时，他参军没有经过民兵连长，对方几乎一无所知。

M6.en: At 18-21, his desire to escape the land was so strong it became indomitable.
M6.zh: 18到21岁间，他逃离土地的愿望强烈到百折不挠。

M2.en: At 21, he wanted to bring honor to his parents and divorce himself from sweet potatoes.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他想为父母争气，与地瓜离婚。

D6.en: He began university studies at the German University in Prague and spent time in Munich.
D6.zh: 他在布拉格的德国大学开始大学学习，并在慕尼黑待了一段时间。

D18.en: He began work at the Workers' Accident Insurance Institute.
D18.zh: 他开始在工人意外保险研究所工作。

D15.en: He rushed off to the Civilian Swimming School to spend the rest of the day there.
D15.zh: 他冲到平民游泳学校，在那里待了一整天。

D1.en: At 22-30, he was determined to start his own business and it took him eight years.
D1.zh: 22到30岁间，他决心创业，花了八年时间。

D2.en: He said he was delighted to be studying at last and would not come to the café that week.
D2.zh: 他说他终于在学习，很高兴，这周不会去咖啡馆了。

D3.en: He declared in a letter that the essential task of literature was to open privileged access to the reader.
D3.zh: 他在一封信中宣称，文学的基本任务是向读者打开特权通道。

D13b.en: He was born at the edge of the ghetto in Prague shortly before its demise.
D13b.zh: 他出生在布拉格犹太区边缘，不久后该区就消失了。

D11.en: At 12-13, he wrote novel-length prose, a less rule-bound pursuit.
D11.zh: 12到13岁时，他写起了长篇散文，一种不那么循规蹈矩的追求。

D16.en: At 10-14, he could recite demanding texts like Ovid and Homer translations.
D16.zh: 10到14岁时，他能背诵奥维德和荷马译作等难度很大的文本。

M1.en: At 12, he was the top pupil in his class.
M1.zh: 12岁时，他是班上的尖子生。

D5.en: At 20, feeling bold and curious, he sat next to them and ordered beer.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他感到大胆和好奇，坐到他们旁边并点了啤酒。

D9.en: At 24, he applied for a job and had a medical exam, but was rejected.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他申请了一份工作并接受了体检，但被拒绝了。

D14.en: At 13, he had to learn a Torah portion by heart and chant it before the congregation.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他必须背诵一段托拉并在会众面前吟唱。

D4.en: At 23, he worked as an unpaid probationer in a Prague lawyer's office.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他在布拉格律师理查德·勒维的办公室做无薪见习生。

D20.en: At 18, in his first semester, he became chairman of the Bar Kokhba association.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他在大学第一学期成为巴尔·科赫巴协会的主席。

M2.en: At ages 6-10, his parents saw child-rearing as shaping and taming, and he was driven more by fear than curiosity.
M2.zh: 6到10岁时，父母视育儿为塑造驯服，他更多受恐惧而非好奇驱使。

M5.en: At age 10, his family would not understand a model student willingly repeating a year with weaker classmates and extra costs.
M5.zh: 10岁时，家人无法理解模范生自愿留级一年与差生为伍并增加开支。

D8.en: As a boy, he directed and commanded his peers like a flock, soon crossing into tyranny.
D8.zh: 他小时候指挥同伴像一群羊，很快就变得专横。

M3.en: He worshipped Brentano as a guru, according to Bergmann's letters.
M3.zh: 根据伯格曼的信件，他崇拜布伦塔诺如同导师。

D6.en: He wrote to his grandmother, embarrassed about how much he was eating.
D6.zh: 他写信给祖母，对自己吃太多感到不好意思。

D13a.en: He told his family he would choose a practical career and study for the exam they preferred.
D13a.zh: 他告诉家人他会选择一份实际的职业，并为他们喜欢的考试而努力学习。

D11.en: He wrote about the Roman sacking of Corinth for a private tutor.
D11.zh: 他为私人教师写了一篇关于罗马洗劫科林斯的文章。

D10.en: He wrote in a rage when his mother forbade him to see Jacques.
D10.zh: 母亲禁止他见雅克时，他愤怒地写信。

D3.en: He recalled happy vacation days at Le Tréport, enjoying the sea air and beach.
D3.zh: 他回忆起在勒特雷波尔度过的快乐假期，享受海风和沙滩。

D4.en: He could stare transfixed at a rose or anything on his wavelength.
D4.zh: 他能凝视一朵玫瑰或任何与他合拍的事物，目不转睛。

D2.en: He went to bed extremely late and slept until nearly noon.
D2.zh: 他睡得很晚，几乎睡到中午。

D15.en: At age eight, he tripped and broke his nose while playing on the Champs-Élysées.
D15.zh: 八岁时，他在香榭丽舍大街玩耍时绊倒摔断了鼻子。

D7.en: He failed to stop publication of two articles by Blum in the July issue.
D7.zh: 他未能阻止布卢姆的两篇文章在七月号上发表。

D16.en: He handed Jacques a letter at school, inviting him to have sex.
D16.zh: 他在学校递给雅克一封信，邀请他发生性关系。

D14.en: He enjoyed staging amateur theatricals, musical reviews, and shadow plays with his group.
D14.zh: 他和他的团队喜欢上演业余戏剧、音乐评论和皮影戏。

D8.en: He frightened his playmates by seizing their hands and announcing his need to possess them like a tyrant.
D8.zh: 他抓住玩伴的手，宣布要像暴君一样占有他们，吓坏了他们。

D5.en: He handed a letter to Jacques at school, inviting him to have sex.
D5.zh: 他在学校递给雅克一封信，邀请他发生性关系。

M1.en: He received the fifth certificate of merit in French, fourth in Latin composition, and second prize in natural sciences.
M1.zh: 他获得了法语第五名优秀证书、拉丁文作文第四名和自然科学二等奖。

D9.en: He collaborated on a novel in letter form with three fellow contributors.
D9.zh: 他与三位合作者共同创作了一部书信体小说。

D20.en: He was selected as one of three on an editorial committee.
D20.zh: 他被选为三人编委会之一。

M7.en: He offered to let his father choose between two career alternatives.
M7.zh: 他提出让父亲在两个职业选择中做决定。

D1.en: At 23, he admitted to a near addiction to gambling and swore to reform.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他承认自己几乎沉迷赌博，发誓要改过。

M6.en: At 23, he felt he had nothing of value to give until he found his words.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他觉得自己没有有价值的东西可以给予别人，直到找到自己的语言。

M5.en: At 17, he felt as if he had left a part of his moral being there.
M5.zh: 17岁时，他感觉自己把一部分道德留在了那里。

M3.en: At 23, he was convinced he had found a disguised god named Reynaldo.
M3.zh: 23岁时，他确信自己找到了一个伪装的神，名叫雷纳尔多。

D3.en: At 25, he decided to pursue a master's degree through writing, combining interest and degree.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他决定通过写作拿硕士学位，把兴趣和学位结合起来。

D2.en: At 23, he first learned the term 'aplastic anemia' when picking up test results for a friend.
D2.zh: 23歲時，他第一次知道「再生不良性貧血」，那天去取朋友王國祥的化驗結果。

D14.en: At 13, he won first prize in a Hong Kong children's art competition with his painting 'Children's Day'.
D14.zh: 13歲時，他代表學校參加全港小學生美術比賽，畫作《兒童節》得了冠軍。

D6.en: At 22, he expressed hope that his group could start a newspaper or magazine together.
D6.zh: 22歲時，他滿懷憧憬地說希望他們能一起辦報紙或雜誌。

M1.en: He decided to pursue a master's degree through writing, combining his interest with his degree.
M1.zh: 他決定通過寫作拿碩士學位，把興趣和學位結合起來。

D5.en: At 24, he lost his principal and interest when the steel factory he lent money to at high interest went bankrupt.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他放高利贷的那家伸铁厂宣布改组（破产），本金和利息都收不回来。

D7.en: At 19-20, he decided to retake college entrance exams to study literature, without telling his parents.
D7.zh: 19到20歲時，他決定重考大學轉攻文學，沒敢告訴父母。

D9.en: At 22, he discussed starting a magazine with friends and asked his parents to raise 100,000 NT dollars.
D9.zh: 22歲時，他和朋友探討辦刊，並請父母籌措了十萬新臺幣做基金。

D20.en: At 22, he was elected president of the 'North-South Society' at National Taiwan University.
D20.zh: 22歲時，他在臺灣大學「南北社」選舉中高票當選社長。

D4.en: While drawing, he often ruined the whole sheet with a drop of ink and had to start over.
D4.zh: 绘图时常不经意滴落一大滴墨汁，只好从头再来。

M6.en: He felt the growing dream of becoming a writer was his true calling.
M6.zh: 他心中越来越强烈的作家梦才是真正属于自己的志愿。

M4.en: At 23, he felt deeply moved by the rebellious and sorrowful tones of Western modernist works, as a post-war youth seeking change.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他被西方现代主义作品中叛逆和哀伤的调子深深打动，作为战后求新求变的青年。

M7.en: He helped promote Western modernist literature in Taiwan.
M7.zh: 他把西方现代主义文学在台湾推广开来。

D12.en: As a child, his mother led their extended family of over 80 people through hardships to safely reach Chongqing.
D12.zh: 幼年时，母亲率领白马两家八十余口千山万水安抵重庆。

D10.en: His mother's death shocked him deeply, feeling a life full of light and heat vanish in an instant.
D10.zh: 母亲的死亡使他心灵受到巨大震撼，那样充满光热的生命转瞬烟消云散。

D16.en: During map class, he couldn't understand and wrote his novel 'Lonely Seventeen' instead.
D16.zh: 上地图课时他听不懂，就趴在桌上写小说《寂寞的十七岁》。

D17.en: He took on the responsibility of caring for a friend, driving him to blood transfusions and accompanying him.
D17.zh: 他承担起照顾责任，常从异地赶往洛杉矶接送王国祥输血并陪伴。

D15.en: Once, returning from school, the rickshaw overturned and he and his sister were thrown out.
D15.zh: 有一次放学回家，人力车翻了盖，他和明姊都飞了出去。

D12.en: At 9, his father fled Nanjing without saying goodbye, leaving him and his mother behind.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他父亲不辞而别逃离南京，留下他和母亲。

D4.en: He drew maps for classmates, surpassing the geography teacher in knowledge.
D4.zh: 他给同学画地图，地理知识超过了老师。

D3.en: He and a classmate tested their teacher's knowledge of a classical text.
D3.zh: 他和同学拿古文考老师，想试试老师的深浅。

D2.en: At 21-22, he learned to write new poetry by positive study of predecessors.
D2.zh: 21到22岁时，他通过正学前辈作品来学写新诗。

D11.en: As a teenager, he read old Chinese novels, learning about folk customs and bridging classical and vernacular Chinese.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他读中国旧小说，认识民情风土，在文言与白话间搭桥。

D6.en: As a child, he drew horses and door gods, and his cousins loved to play with him.
D6.zh: 他小时候能画马和门神，表兄妹们都喜欢围着他玩。

D16.en: He wrote a rebuttal to an article attacking Shakespeare, defending the playwright.
D16.zh: 他写文章驳斥攻击莎士比亚的文章，为莎翁辩护。

D9.en: He drew maps for clumsy female classmates, always leaving a deliberate mistake.
D9.zh: 他帮笨手笨脚的女同学画地图，每张都故意留个错。

D1.en: As a child, he fled the occupied zone with his mother, sailing from Shanghai to Hong Kong after half a year of effort.
D1.zh: 他幼年时和母亲逃离沦陷区，经过半年努力，从上海乘船绕道到了香港。

M2.en: His father bought a house and gave him a north-facing room on the second floor as a study.
M2.zh: 他父亲买了房子，把二楼朝北的房间给他做书房。

M1.en: He studied and drew maps so much that he knew more than his geography teacher.
M1.zh: 他研究并画了那么多地图，以至于他知道的比地理老师还多。

D13b.en: At 19, he gave up Peking University to attend Jinling University out of filial piety.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，他为了孝顺放弃了北大，选择了金陵大学。

D14.en: He woke early on publication days, waited for the newspaper, and found his poem in it.
D14.zh: 他早早起来等邮递员，捡起报纸，看到自己的诗句。

D10.en: He received a letter from Mr. Liang and couldn't sleep that night.
D10.zh: 他收到梁先生的信，那晚失眠了。

D15.en: He couldn't be a team member but happily went as a cheerleader.
D15.zh: 他不能成为队员，但兴高采烈地去当啦啦队员。

M4.en: In his 20s, he clung to modern poetry as a spiritual pillar amid collapsed faith and uncertain future.
M4.zh: 20多岁时，他在信仰崩塌、前途渺茫中抓住现代诗歌作为灵魂支柱。

D20.en: He published seven new poems, seven literary critiques, and two translations in two newspapers.
D20.zh: 他在两家报纸上发表了七首新诗、七篇文艺论评和两篇译文。

D5.en: He fled with his mother from Changzhou to the Jiangsu-Anhui border and hid in a temple where Japanese soldiers also stayed.
D5.zh: 他随母亲从常州逃往苏皖边境，在高淳县躲入佛寺，日军也在那里驻扎。

D17.en: He stayed alone in Taipei after his girlfriend left, missing her and recalling memories.
D17.zh: 他独守台北，思念远方的恋人，回忆过去。

D18.en: As a teenager, he used his meager allowance to buy an old Turkish map.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他用微薄的零用钱买了一张旧的土耳其地图。

D13a.en: At 24, he ranked first in the entrance exam for the Taiwan military translation training program.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他以第一名考取了台湾联勤陆海空编译人员训练班。

D8.en: At 24, he ranked first in the entrance exam for the Taiwan military translation training program.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他以第一名考取了台湾联勤陆海空编译人员训练班。

D7.en: At 24-26, he said, 'I will not hesitate to confront Thunder!'
D7.zh: 24到26岁时，他说：‘我不惜与雷霆对抗！’

D20X.en: At 24, he did not continue to entrust bookstores with sales.
D20X.zh: 24岁时，他没有继续委托书店销售。

M3.en: At 24-26, he respectfully called Liang Shiqiu his 'mentor'.
M3.zh: 24到26岁时，他尊称梁实秋为自己的‘恩师’。

M5.en: At 24, he ranked first in the entrance exam for the Taiwan military translation training program.
M5.zh: 24岁时，他以第一名考取了台湾联勤陆海空编译人员训练班。

M8.en: He was saved from deep suffering by Van Gogh's artistic devotion and creativity.
M8.zh: 凡高对艺术无上执着的殉道精神和旺盛的创造力把他从身心痛苦的深渊里拯救了出来。

M6.en: He saw ancient stars as literary geniuses like Li Bai and Shelley, guiding him through turmoil.
M6.zh: 那些亘古以来就俯视着大地、海洋的恒星，在他看来，就是中外的文学天才，将永远引领着他战胜动荡和不安。

D19.en: As a teenager, he drew many maps and knew more than his geography teacher.
D19.zh: 他画了许许多多地图，他所知道和熟记的竟然超过地理老师。

D12.en: At age 4-5, he began helping with chores like caring for siblings, gathering pigweed in summer, and chopping firewood in winter.
D12.zh: 四五岁时，他就开始跟在大人后面干活，照看弟弟妹妹，夏天寻猪草，冬天砍柴。

D15.en: At 14-15, he played basketball well, was quick and fierce, and became a main player on the school team.
D15.zh: 十四五岁时，他篮球打得好，个子不高但灵活凶猛，被选入校队成为主力。

D1.en: At 14, he cried to Liu Junkuan that the school had expelled him, and Liu persuaded the principal to readmit him.
D1.zh: 14岁时，他哭着找刘俊宽说学校不收他了，刘俊宽去说情，校长破例收他入学。

D7.en: He gave up his spot to his lover and made her a quilt with his monthly allowance.
D7.zh: 他把名额让给恋人，用每月十八元补贴扯布缝了新被褥送给她。

D2.en: He worked as an editor, pulled curtains for shows, and wrote literature.
D2.zh: 他在宣传队当编辑，有演出拉大幕，没演出就编《山花》搞创作。

D13b.en: At 21, on November 9, 1969, he joined the Chinese Communist Party in his village, introduced by the village head and a teacher.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，1969年11月9日，他在本村由村主任和教师介绍加入中国共产党。

D6.en: At 22, he was mobilized while temporarily helping at the communications team as a 'migrant worker creator'.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他以‘民工创作员’身份在通讯组帮忙时被动员起来。

M1.en: At 14, he ranked second in the county in the exam, achieving the title of 'bangyan'.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他以全县第二名的成绩考中，名列榜眼。

D18.en: He walked from the county seat to Dongjiaying Village to hand-deliver important materials to Zhang Hong.
D18.zh: 他步行从县城到董家营村，亲手把重要材料交给张虹。

D4.en: At 23, he told a friend he sensed his future girlfriend would be among the Beijing educated youth.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他告诉朋友他预感未来的女朋友会在北京知青中。

D3.en: At 22, he asked about lyrics, then wrote several songs that met requirements.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他询问歌词特点，随后写了几首符合要求的歌词。

D14.en: As a child, he used whistling to learn tunes, impressing teachers with his loud voice and boldness.
D14.zh: 幼年时，他用吹口哨学曲子，嗓门大胆子大，赢得老师另眼相看。

D9.en: At 11-12, he became class monitor due to his academic excellence and organizational skills.
D9.zh: 11到12岁时，他因成绩优异和组织才能担任班长。

D11.en: At 25-27, he independently wrote essays and a short story published in journals.
D11.zh: 25到27岁间，他独立创作散文和短篇小说并发表。

D20.en: At 20, he was appointed deputy director of the county revolutionary committee.
D20.zh: 20岁时，他被协商为县革委会副主任。

D5.en: At 21, he gave up a quota to his lover, using his allowance to make bedding for her.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他把名额让给恋人，用补贴扯布缝被褥送她。

D10.en: At 21, he wore white clothes in winter, saying he was mourning for himself.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他冬天穿白衣，说在为自己戴孝。

M2.en: At 24, he brought a Beijing female educated youth home for New Year, pleasing his parents.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他带北京女知青回家过年，让父母满心欢喜。

M4.en: At 22-23, he devoted himself to editing a poetry collection for Yanchuan workers and peasants.
M4.zh: 22到23岁间，他全身心投入编辑延川工农兵诗歌集。

M7.en: At 23, he described the melody of 'Mountain Flower' as 'Xintianyou', used by literary youth to express lofty revolutionary ideals.
M7.zh: 23岁时，他说《山花》的旋律是“信天游”，文艺青年用它抒志咏怀，表现崇高的革命理想。

D8.en: He judged manuscripts more sharply than some official editors.
D8.zh: 他鉴赏稿件比有些正式编辑还强，能一针见血指出优缺点。

D16.en: He judged manuscripts more sharply than some official editors.
D16.zh: 他鉴赏稿件比有些正式编辑还强，能一针见血指出优缺点。

D17.en: At 24-25, as an intern at Shaanxi Literature and Art, he used his innate compassion to help others wholeheartedly.
D17.zh: 24到25岁间，他在《陕西文艺》实习，用天生的同情心竭尽全力帮助他人。

D20X.en: He was a grade above Haibo at Yichuan County Cheng Primary School.
D20X.zh: 他在延川县城关小学比海波高一个年级。

M8.en: At 25-26, he interned at Shaanxi Literature and Art for about a year.
M8.zh: 25到26岁间，他在《陕西文艺》实习了大约一年。

D11.en: She produced the Hyde Park Gate News from 1891 to 1892 and again in early 1895.
D11.zh: 她在1891到1892年以及1895年初编写了《海德公园门新闻》。

D2.en: As a child she was taught to discuss nerves, and did so with angry impatience in the 1890s.
D2.zh: 她小时候被教导谈论神经问题，并在1890年代愤怒而不耐烦地谈论它们。

D5.en: She attempted to climb a lesser monument in Munich and crawled up stairs on all fours.
D5.zh: 她试图爬上慕尼黑一座较小的纪念碑，并四肢着地爬上楼梯。

D3.en: She began her lifelong hobby of 'street haunting', writing 'I like looking at things'.
D3.zh: 她开始了终身爱好“街头游荡”，写道“我喜欢看东西”。

D6.en: At 15, she wrote that Nessa preaches our destinies lie in ourselves.
D6.zh: 15岁时，她写道内莎宣扬命运掌握在自己手中。

D10.en: At 15, she made 'very furious and tantrumical' preparations for a wet, gruesome family holiday in Brighton.
D10.zh: 15岁时，她为布莱顿一个潮湿又可怕的家庭假期做了‘非常愤怒和闹脾气’的准备。

D4.en: She made weak but not insensitive copies of Blake and Rossetti drawings.
D4.zh: 她画了布莱克和罗塞蒂的临摹，虽弱但并非不敏感。

D9.en: She joined a play-reading society started by Clive in December 1907.
D9.zh: 她加入了克莱夫在1907年12月创办的剧本朗读会。

D14.en: She babbled nonsense in a high-pitched voice to a child.
D14.zh: 她用尖声对孩子胡言乱语。

D13b.en: Her birth was registered as Adeline Virginia in Kensington.
D13b.zh: 她的出生登记在肯辛顿，名为阿德琳·弗吉尼亚。

D7.en: She refused to kneel during a service.
D7.zh: 她在仪式中拒绝下跪。

D13a.en: She weakly acquiesced to the past coming to live with them.
D13a.zh: 她软弱地默许了过去来与他们同住。

D16.en: She became known as a roaring, crushing debater in the Union.
D16.zh: 她在辩论社中以咆哮、压倒性的辩手闻名。

D20.en: She and Adrian began to entertain and revive Thursday Evenings.
D20.zh: 她和阿德里安开始招待并复兴周四晚聚会。

M1.en: She tallied the total words she wrote for her reviews.
M1.zh: 她统计了为评论所写的总字数。

M8.en: She revered Greek scholars as rulers of a territory she tried to journey into.
M8.zh: 她崇敬希腊学者，视其为曾试图踏入的领土的统治者。

D19.en: At 23, she tallied the total words she wrote for her reviews.
D19.zh: 23岁时，她统计了自己为评论写的总字数。

M7.en: At 22-23, she taught at Morley College.
M7.zh: 22到23岁间，她在莫利学院教书。

D5.en: He was popular but dared not give up his job despite Berlitz forbidding private teaching.
D5.zh: 他很受欢迎，但不敢放弃工作，尽管贝立兹禁止私下授课。

D11.en: At 22, he begins his novel, writing with some anger to show it's more important than empty debates.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他带着一些愤怒开始写小说，以此表明这比空谈更重要。

D7.en: He came to accept his current situation as a voluntary exile.
D7.zh: 他接受了自己目前自愿流亡的状态。

D18.en: At 21, he gave a pawn ticket for expensive technical books like Gray's Anatomy.
D18.zh: 21岁时，他给出了像《格雷氏解剖学》这样昂贵的技术书籍的当票。

D13b.en: He signed up to begin classes at St Cecilia's, the Catholic medical school.
D13b.zh: 他报名开始在圣塞西莉亚天主教医学院上课。

D4.en: He admired Norman furniture but couldn't buy it yet for his current room.
D4.zh: 他欣赏诺曼家具，但暂时买不起放在现在的房间。

D14.en: He won a bronze medal in a tenor competition at the Feis Ceoil on 14 May 1904.
D14.zh: 他在1904年5月14日的爱尔兰音乐节男高音比赛中获得铜牌。

D9.en: He boldly mentioned the addressee's little-known name at college and defended their place in drama history.
D9.zh: 他在大学里大胆提起了收信人鲜为人知的名字，并为其在戏剧史上的地位辩护。

D13a.en: He was drawn to the Royal University Medical School but quit due to chemistry class.
D13a.zh: 他被皇家大学医学院吸引，但因化学课又放弃了。

D2.en: He bought an alarm clock for 4 francs to wake up on time for school.
D2.zh: 他花4法郎买了个闹钟，为了准时起床上学。

D3.en: He obtained a reader's card for the Bibliothèque Nationale.
D3.zh: 他拿到了国家图书馆的阅览证。

D15.en: As a teenager, he rowed, ran cross-country, threw shot put, and set a triple jump record.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他划船、越野跑、推铅球，并创下三级跳远纪录。

M4.en: He argued that socialist tendencies mean revulsion against despotism, not delaying proletarian freedom.
M4.zh: 他辩称社会主义倾向意味着对专制的厌恶，而非拖延无产阶级自由。

D16.en: At 19, he boldly mentioned the recipient's little-known name in college, defending their place in drama history.
D16.zh: 19岁时，他大胆地在大学里提起收信人鲜为人知的名字，为其在戏剧史上的地位辩护。

M6.en: He had a sacred mission, which he explained in his early stories and the opening of Stephen Hero.
M6.zh: 他有一个神圣的使命，他在早期故事和《斯蒂芬英雄》的开头解释过。

M7.en: He was perhaps determining his own place in world literature.
M7.zh: 他也许在确定自己在世界文学中的位置。

D19.en: He carefully measured everything he read in terms of naturalist realism, nobility of purpose, and style.
D19.zh: 他仔细衡量他读过的每本书，从自然主义真实性、目的的高尚性和风格方面。

D20X.en: At 23, he abandoned his ardently begun novel Stephen Hero unfinished.
D20X.zh: 23岁时，他放弃了热情开始的小说《斯蒂芬英雄》，未完成。

M2.en: He gave his son the name of his dead brother, also the name of the distant founder of their clan.
M2.zh: 他给儿子取了他死去的兄弟的名字，也是他们家族远祖的名字。

M5.en: He was anxious that his respectable friends should not know about Nora.
M5.zh: 他担心他那些体面的朋友不知道诺拉的事。

M8.en: He said, 'I spit upon the image of the Tenth Pius.'
M8.zh: 他说：“我唾弃庇护十世的形象。”

D8.en: At age 6-9, he stood confidently in front of the tallest boy in third class, strutting.
D8.zh: 6到9岁时，他自信地站在三年级最高的男孩面前，趾高气扬地走着。

D3.en: He received books as gifts from a teacher who recognized his special gifts.
D3.zh: 他收到老师送的书籍，老师很早就发现他的特殊天赋。

D4.en: He assembled a car by mounting a racer body onto a Ford chassis.
D4.zh: 他把一个赛车车身装到福特底盘上，拼装了一辆车。

D10.en: He was too shy to speak to Faulkner at a chance meeting.
D10.zh: 他在一次偶遇中太害羞，没敢跟福克纳说话。

D11.en: He had his first prose publication, a story titled 'Landing in Luck,' in the Mississippian.
D11.zh: 他的第一篇散文《幸运着陆》发表在《密西西比人》上。

D2.en: He began a four-year personalized tutorial with Phil Stone in the summer of 1914.
D2.zh: 1914年夏天，他开始接受菲尔·斯通的四年私人辅导。

D14.en: He was seen wandering streets wearing an old Confederate cap.
D14.zh: 有人看到他戴着旧邦联帽在街上闲逛。

D13b.en: He wore a canvas back brace at his mother's insistence to improve his posture.
D13b.zh: 他听从母亲的要求，穿上帆布背带矫正姿势。

D16.en: He challenged an author's English in a newspaper letter.
D16.zh: 他在报纸上质疑一位作者的英语水平。

D15.en: He slipped out before daybreak to see fruit trains carrying strawberries north.
D15.zh: 他天没亮就溜出去看运草莓的火车往北开。

D18.en: He worked as a fireman, brakeman, engineer, and conductor on the railroad.
D18.zh: 他在铁路上当过司炉工、制动员、火车司机和列车长。

D5.en: He joined the Royal Flying Corps and left for training.
D5.zh: 他加入英国皇家飞行队，前往多伦多训练。

D9.en: He quarterbacked his high school football team.
D9.zh: 他担任高中橄榄球队的四分卫。

D7.en: At 21, he dropped out of school in spring 1928.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他在1928年春天辍学了。

M4.en: He gave blacks the benefit of the doubt when others were racist.
M4.zh: 他在周围人种族歧视时仍对黑人持善意。

D1.en: He tried school again, switching from Engineering to Liberal Arts.
D1.zh: 他重新上学，从工程转到文科。

M1.en: He wanted a sense of achievement in battle.
M1.zh: 他渴望在战斗中获得成就感。

M5.en: At 21, he craved attention, acclaim, and love.
M5.zh: 21岁时，他渴望关注、赞誉和爱。

M6.en: At 21, he saw the uniform as confirming a dream he would make real.
M6.zh: 21岁时，他认为这身制服证实了他将实现的梦想。

M2.en: At 21, he may have wanted to bask in the aura of Jack's hero's return.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他可能想享受杰克作为英雄归来时的光环。

M7.en: At 21, he shared in the prestige of that elite group.
M7.zh: 21岁时，他分享了那个精英群体的声望。

M8.en: At 21, he felt the dark angel had not entirely overlooked Toronto.
M8.zh: 21岁时，他觉得黑暗天使并未完全忽略多伦多。

D13b.en: At 23, familial and institutional demands drove him back to Harvard for three years.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，家庭和机构的要求迫使他回到哈佛，待了三年。

D15.en: At 19, his name was listed as an aspiring freshman crew member.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他的名字出现在新生赛艇队员名单上。

D16.en: At 25, he surprised Russell by comparing Heraclitus to Villon.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他比较赫拉克利特和维庸，令罗素惊讶。

D13a.en: He persuaded his family to allow him to venture beyond their backyard; his father subsidized the trip at age 21.
D13a.zh: 他21岁时说服家人让他外出闯荡，父亲资助了这次旅行。

D7.en: At 14, he headed north to Quebec instead of staying at Eastern Point.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他前往魁北克，而不是留在东点。

D5.en: He rounded Mt. Desert Rock in a fog, rough sea, and two-reef breeze.
D5.zh: 他在大雾、汹涌海浪和二级缩帆风中绕过了沙漠山岩。

D10.en: He experienced a fugitive sensation of peace that he would try all his life to recapture at age 21.
D10.zh: 他21岁时体验到一种转瞬即逝的平静感，他一生都试图重新捕捉这种感觉。

M2.en: His mother decided he should adopt a profession within the American university system at age 23.
M2.zh: 他23岁时，母亲决定他应该在美国大学系统内从事一份职业。

M4.en: He had the idea of giving up English and settling in Paris to write French at age 22-23.
M4.zh: 他22到23岁时曾想放弃英语，在巴黎定居并逐渐用法语写作。

M6.en: He decided to return to Harvard and become a philosopher at age 23.
M6.zh: 他23岁时决定返回哈佛大学成为一名哲学家。

D20.en: He became President of the University Philosophical Society in his last year at Harvard at age 24-25.
D20.zh: 他24到25岁时在哈佛的最后一年成为大学哲学学会主席。

D1.en: At 18-20, he published poems and endured literary rejection and mockery.
D1.zh: 18到20岁间，他发表了几首诗，并忍受了文学界的拒绝和嘲笑。

D1.en: At 24, he traveled nearly 10,000 miles.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他旅行了近一万英里。

D10.en: At 23, he vented his sorrow and anger in a letter to Howell Jenkins.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他在给豪厄尔·詹金斯的信中发泄了悲伤和愤怒。

M1.en: At 19, he studied men's voices and attitudes and could imitate them.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他研究别人的声音和态度，并能模仿他们。

M4.en: He was determined to show that in Hemingway country, there would be no need for a message.
M4.zh: 他决心证明，在海明威的家乡不需要任何寓意。

D4.en: At 16-17, he kept fishing through the open window, adding trout to the basket.
D4.zh: 16到17岁时，他继续通过开着的窗户钓鱼，把鳟鱼放进篮子里。

D16.en: At 17-18, he gave a speech at First Congregational Church on June 17, 1917.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他于1917年6月17日在第一公理会教堂发表了演讲。

M6.en: At 19, he said he had been very frightened after his wounding and therefore very devout.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他说自己受伤后非常害怕，因此非常虔诚。

D13a.en: At 18, he rejected college, though his father wanted him to go to Oberlin with Marcelline.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝了大学，尽管他父亲希望他和马塞琳一起去欧柏林。

D19.en: At 20, he clipped his publications and sent them back to Oak Park.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他剪下自己的出版物寄回橡树园。

M7.en: At 22, he wanted Bill to be his best man at the wedding.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他想让比尔在婚礼上做伴郎。

D17.en: At 18, he helped seriously wounded Italian soldiers after an explosion.
D17.zh: 18岁时，他帮助了爆炸中重伤的意大利士兵。

M8.en: At 22, smells of pine smoke or fresh coffee could bring back memories for him.
M8.zh: 22岁时，松烟或新鲜咖啡的气味能唤起他的回忆。

D2.en: At 15, he scribbled on blank textbook pages during class.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他在课堂上往课本空白页上乱写。

D12.en: At 12, he prayed they wouldn't have to go to the poorhouse.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他祈祷他们不必去救济院。

D13a.en: He agreed to Zelda's condition: if he succeeded in New York, she would follow him.
D13a.zh: 他接受了泽尔达的条件：如果他在纽约成功，她就跟他走。

D7.en: At 16-17, he disturbed the Christmas service at St. John's Church.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他扰乱了圣约翰教堂的圣诞礼拜。

D11.en: At 21, he had neither leisure to browse nor education to cram.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他既没有闲暇从容阅读，也没有足够的教育来死记硬背。

D13b.en: As a child up to 23, he was raised Catholic with habitual convictions.
D13b.zh: 从幼年到23岁，他从小受天主教教育，养成了习惯性的信念。

D14.en: At 23, Scribners published his novel This Side of Paradise on 26 March 1920.
D14.zh: 23岁时，斯克里布纳出版社于1920年3月26日出版了他的小说《人间天堂》。

D5.en: He seized her in his arms and tried to kiss her into marrying him at once.
D5.zh: 他一把抱住她，想用亲吻让她立刻嫁给他。

D15.en: He turned the attic into a gymnasium and persuaded his family to give him a football outfit.
D15.zh: 他把阁楼改造成健身房，说服家人给他买了套足球装备。

M3.en: He said his view of life was that of Theodore Dreisers and Joseph Conrads.
M3.zh: 他说他的人生观是西奥多·德莱塞和约瑟夫·康拉德那样的。

D16.en: He had become St. Paul Academy's star debater; no one could shut him up.
D16.zh: 他成了圣保罗学院的明星辩手，没人能让他闭嘴。

D6.en: He wrote in his ledger: 'Dancing school. Marie. Love.'
D6.zh: 他在日记里写道：'舞蹈学校。玛丽。爱。'

D9.en: He and Bishop planned to set themselves up in a similar apartment in New York.
D9.zh: 他和毕晓普计划在纽约租一间类似的公寓。

D20.en: He was elected secretary of the Triangle Club for 1915–16.
D20.zh: 他被选为三角俱乐部1915–16年度的秘书。

D8.en: At 25, he agreed to be Baby Warren's husband and private doctor for life.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他同意做Baby Warren的丈夫和终身私人医生。

D1.en: At 18, he wrote: 'If I couldn't be perfect I wouldn't be anything.'
D1.zh: 18岁时，他写道：'如果我不能完美，我就什么都不是。'

M1.en: At 17, he sent a telegram asking for football pads and shoes.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他发电报要求寄足球护垫和鞋子。

D18.en: At 23, he revised his rejected stories and salvaged stories from the Nassau Lit.
D18.zh: 23岁时，他修改了被拒的故事，并从《拿骚文学》中抢救了一些故事。

D4.en: At 8, he turned the attic into a gymnasium and got a football outfit with shinguards.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他把阁楼改造成健身房，并得到了带护胫的足球装备。

D19.en: At 23, he rewrote The Romantic Egotist on a schedule pinned to his desk curtain.
D19.zh: 23岁时，他按照钉在书桌帘子上的时间表重写《浪漫的自大狂》。

M4.en: At 22, he was a poor soldier because he saw the army as an impediment to his writing.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他是个差劲的士兵，因为他认为军队妨碍了他的写作。

M5.en: At 23, he hoped to produce a best seller, win Zelda back, and become famous.
M5.zh: 23岁时，他希望写出畅销书，赢回泽尔达，并成名。

M7.en: At 23, he said many things depended on its success, including a girl.
M7.zh: 23岁时，他说很多事情取决于它的成功，包括一个女孩。

D10.en: He had a passionate desire to write in Russian but felt he must not.
D10.zh: 他强烈渴望用俄语写作，但觉得自己不能写。

D3.en: He watched the Wright brothers perform a short flight in their unpredictable machine.
D3.zh: 他观看了莱特兄弟驾驶他们那极不可靠的机器进行短途飞行。

M1.en: He reached the semifinals in the boxing trials held at the Corn Exchange.
M1.zh: 他在玉米交易所举行的拳击选拔赛中进入了半决赛。

D13b.en: He took his school-leaving exams a month before the formal time.
D13b.zh: 他在正式时间前一个月参加了毕业考试。

D7.en: At 24, he permitted himself the luxury of writing, liberated by geography.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他因地理上的解放而允许自己享受写作的奢侈。

D4.en: He captured a specimen of Cupido minimus on the summit of Ai Petri.
D4.zh: 他在艾佩特里山顶捕获了一只极小灰蝶标本。

D9.en: At 23, he publicly withdrew from Vereteno to protest its spirit.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他公开退出Vereteno，以抗议该组织的精神。

D12.en: At 23, his father was killed by a rightist assassin's bullet.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他的父亲被右翼刺客的子弹杀死。

M4.en: At 18, he was arrested in 1908 for signing a protest against the dissolution of the Duma.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他因签署反对解散杜马的抗议于1908年被捕。

D8.en: In the 1920s, he drew straws with a friend to see who would hit someone first, and he won.
D8.zh: 1920年代，他和朋友抽签决定谁先打那个人，他赢了。

D20.en: At 25, he was made a junior gentleman of the chamber.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他被任命为宫廷低级侍从。

D19.en: At age 20, he had a chess problem for virtually every poem in his London notebook.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他的伦敦笔记本里几乎每首诗都配有一个棋题。

D13a.en: At 23, Vladimir worked in a German bank for three hours.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，弗拉基米尔在德国银行工作了3小时。

D1.en: At age 19 in 1918, a severe toothache became the pretext for a poem.
D1.zh: 19岁时，1918年的一场严重牙痛成为一首诗的由头。

D3.en: At 13-14, he sat reading in the shade during break while others played football.
D3.zh: 13到14岁时，他休息时坐在阴凉处读书，而其他人在踢足球。

D11.en: At 23, he wrote a two-page letter to his friend Carlos Alemán in Bogotá.
D11.zh: 23岁时，他给在波哥大的朋友卡洛斯·阿莱曼写了一封两页的信。

M6.en: He knew he had to be a novelist and wrote La casa, completing and abandoning it in 1953.
M6.zh: 他知道自己必须成为小说家，写了《房子》，在1953年完成并放弃了它。

D13a.en: At 17-18, he accepted finishing the baccalaureate and studying law at the National University.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁时，他接受了完成中学学业并去国立大学学习法律。

D9.en: At 22, he made up his mind to leave Cartagena.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他下定决心离开卡塔赫纳。

D2.en: At 16-19, he read obsessively and wrote willful, mimetic prose and verse.
D2.zh: 16到19岁时，他痴迷地阅读，并写下了刻意模仿的散文和诗歌。

D12.en: At 22-23, he couldn't afford rent and lived in a brothel for almost a year.
D12.zh: 22到23岁时，他付不起房租，在妓院住了将近一年。

D7.en: At 22-23, he wrote an article about the barber who shaved the President with an open razor.
D7.zh: 22到23岁时，他写了一篇文章，提到每天用直剃刀给总统刮胡子的理发师。

D5.en: At 17-18, he disappeared to Majagual for ten days and lived it up in a whorehouse.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他消失到马哈瓜尔十天，在妓院里狂欢。

D16.en: At 24, he noted that Sophocles broke rules of detective fiction before the genre existed.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他指出索福克勒斯在侦探小说出现前就打破了其规则。

D15.en: He was born with a herniated testicle.
D15.zh: 他出生时就患有疝气睾丸。

D18.en: He got a job with a local printer and hawked samples on the streets.
D18.zh: 他在当地一家印刷厂找到工作，并在街上兜售样品。

D13b.en: At 21, he married a distinguished girl from Riohacha who was his first cousin.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他娶了一位来自里奥阿查的杰出女孩，她是他的表妹。

D1.en: He crawled back to El Universal with his tail between his legs and was received with open arms.
D1.zh: 他夹着尾巴回到《环球报》，却意外受到热烈欢迎。

D10.en: He remained eternally cheerful and never felt sorry for himself or appealed for help.
D10.zh: 他永远保持乐观，从不自怜或求助。

D4.en: He would give the doorman, Dámaso Rodríguez, a copy of his latest manuscript as a deposit.
D4.zh: 他会把最新手稿的副本交给门卫达马索·罗德里格斯作为押金。

M4.en: He vowed to give his life to the liberation of the colonies held by Spain.
M4.zh: 他发誓要献身于解放西班牙殖民地的斗争。

D19.en: He was very proud to be the first to obey an order.
D19.zh: 他为自己是第一个服从命令的人而感到非常自豪。

M1.en: At 20-21, he was already considered the brightest promise of Colombian narrative.
M1.zh: 20到21岁时，他已被视为哥伦比亚叙事文学最耀眼的新星。

D20.en: He was the author of all the vignettes for the first six issues of a literary magazine.
D20.zh: 他是前六期杂志所有装饰画的作者。

M3.en: At 17, he became magnetized by the figure and work of Rubén Darío.
M3.zh: 17岁时，他被鲁文·达里奥的人物和作品深深吸引。

D17.en: At 22-23, he made friends with prostitutes and wrote their letters for them.
D17.zh: 22到23岁时，他与妓女交朋友，并帮她们写信。

D6.en: He joined the group publishing a weekly newspaper called Ikdam.
D6.zh: 他加入了出版周报《伊克丹》的小组。

D15.en: He handled himself well in a fight, though beaten due to floundering.
D15.zh: 他在打斗中表现不错，但因笨拙被打败。

D13b.en: At 25, he was denied by educational services due to tuberculosis.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他因肺结核被教育机构拒绝。

D9.en: He proposed creating a revolutionary political theater.
D9.zh: 他提议创建革命政治剧院。

D2.en: He reflected that two years are not too many to reflect on a single point.
D2.zh: 他认为用两年思考一个点并不算多。

D20.en: At 22, he was involved with the Théâtre du Travail, which opened on Jan 25, 1936.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他参与劳动剧院，该剧院于1936年1月25日开幕。

D3.en: He said he must learn to tame his overflowing sensibility.
D3.zh: 他说他必须学会控制自己过于外溢的情感。

D16.en: He would utter a few paradoxes, like a maxim he invented about women.
D16.zh: 他会说几句悖论，比如他编的关于女人的格言。

M4.en: He said each of us must do everything we can in our little circle to start the movement.
M4.zh: 他说我们每个人都必须在自己小圈子里尽力发起运动。

D10.en: He asked more from people than they could give, calling it a mistake and despair.
D10.zh: 他向人们索要超出他们所能给予的，称这是错误和绝望。

D14.en: He played the role of Ivan and felt profound joy.
D14.zh: 他扮演了伊万的角色，感到深深的喜悦。

D4.en: He continued to work whenever he could at a plain wood kitchen table at the Maison Fichu.
D4.zh: 他继续在梅松菲舒的一张普通木厨桌上工作，只要有机会。

D1.en: He thought it was his last chance, but he was wrong and things got better.
D1.zh: 他以为那是他最后的机会，但他错了，后来情况变好了。

M1.en: He was number one in December and again in January.
M1.zh: 他在十二月是第一名，一月又是第一名。

D18.en: He got a job in an ironmonger's shop for eight hours a day at 150 francs per month.
D18.zh: 他在一家五金店找到工作，每天八小时，每月150法郎。

D11.en: He must learn to tame his sensibility and sing a different tune.
D11.zh: 他必须学会驯服自己的感性，唱不同的调子。

M3.en: He idolized the thirty-two-year-old French novelist André Malraux.
M3.zh: 他崇拜三十二岁的法国小说家安德烈·马尔罗。

D17.en: He stayed in Algiers to watch over Simone, who was still taking drugs.
D17.zh: 他留在阿尔及尔照顾仍在吸毒的西蒙娜。

D5.en: He and another actor tossed the valises onto the station platform.
D5.zh: 他和另一个演员把行李箱扔到了站台上。

D8.en: He prevented his widowed sister from seeing the man she loved.
D8.zh: 他阻止守寡的姐姐见她爱的男人。

M5.en: They spent the wedding night apart as a humorous gesture.
M5.zh: 他们以分开度过新婚之夜作为幽默的表示。

M8.en: He wanted to break the narrow bounds of thought and human things, above Time and Space.
M8.zh: 他想打破思想和人事的狭隘界限，超越时间和空间。

D3.en: He industriously wrote essays and manifestos alongside his poems.
D3.zh: 他勤奋地写诗，同时也写各种文章和宣言。

D6.en: He was introduced to the ultraísta comrades in the capital by Pedro Garfias.
D6.zh: 他经佩德罗·加菲亚斯介绍，认识了首都的超现实主义同志们。

D9.en: He did most of the editing for Prisma, maintaining its intellectual and visual spirit.
D9.zh: 他负责《棱镜》杂志的大部分编辑工作，保持了其思想和视觉精神。

D11.en: He wrote to Sureda about a fantasy novel plot he thought up, titled 'El hombre que será presidente'.
D11.zh: 他写信给苏雷达，讲述他构思的一部奇幻小说情节，标题是《将成为总统的人》。

D2.en: He decided to teach himself German to read Schopenhauer in the original.
D2.zh: 他决定自学德语，以便阅读叔本华的原著。

D4.en: He often lay on the floor drawing tigers.
D4.zh: 他常常趴在地板上画老虎。

D15.en: He learned to swim, becoming strong, fearless, and coordinated.
D15.zh: 他学会了游泳，变得强壮、无畏且协调。

D16.en: He rebutted a charge in a letter to the editor of Nosotros.
D16.zh: 他写信给《我们》杂志的编辑，反驳了这项指控。

D14.en: He published his first work, a Spanish translation of Oscar Wilde's 'The Happy Prince'.
D14.zh: 他出版了自己的第一部作品，即奥斯卡·王尔德《快乐王子》的西班牙语译本。

M1.en: At 10, he published his first work, a Spanish translation of Oscar Wilde's 'The Happy Prince'.
M1.zh: 10岁时，他发表了自己的第一部作品，翻译了奥斯卡·王尔德的《快乐王子》。

D1.en: At 22, he enlisted Norah Lange as a go-between and secretly resumed his relationship with Concepción.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他请诺拉·兰格做中间人，秘密恢复了与康塞普西翁的关系。

M6.en: At 25, he felt his destiny as a writer lay in Argentina, in interpreting Argentina.
M6.zh: 25岁时，他感到自己作为作家的命运在阿根廷，在于诠释阿根廷。

D20.en: At 25, his bid to regain leadership of the New Generation began to falter.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他重新领导新一代的尝试开始失败。

D12.en: At 14, he departed Buenos Aires on February 3, 1914, aboard the Sierra Nevada.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他于1914年2月3日乘坐内华达号离开布宜诺斯艾利斯。

D10.en: At 22, he was lonely and depressed during his first few months in Buenos Aires.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他在布宜诺斯艾利斯的头几个月感到孤独和沮丧。

D13a.en: He decided to follow his own conscience rather than seek the approval of the compadritos.
D13a.zh: 他决定遵循自己的良心，而不是寻求那些混混的认可。

D5.en: At 23, he and Lanuza tried to outdo each other in impertinence toward an older poet.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他和拉努萨争相对一位年长诗人无礼。

M4.en: At 18, he opposed the capitalist system and was drawn to revolutionary ideas.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他反对资本主义制度，被革命思想吸引。

M3.en: At 17, he began hero-worshipping the first in a series of writers.
M3.zh: 17岁时，他开始崇拜一系列作家中的第一位。

D8.en: At 25, he spiked his rival's guns by neutralizing his claim to be a revolutionary.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他挫败了对手的锐气，否定了对方自称革命者的说法。

D18.en: At 18-19, he bought and sold cattle, planted trees, restored ranch buildings, and speculated in land sales.
D18.zh: 18到19岁间，他买卖牛、种树、修复牧场建筑，并通过投机土地买卖来融资。

D11.en: At 15-19, he wrote 'Lament of the Medic Who Dissected His Girlfriend While Smoking Two Packs of Marylands'.
D11.zh: 15到19岁时，他写了《解剖女友时抽两包玛丽兰香烟的医学生的哀歌》。

D6.en: At 24, he went swimming with his childhood friends Magondeaux and Durieux.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他和儿时朋友马贡多、杜里厄一起去游泳。

D3.en: At about 24, he felt he thinks with his eyes.
D3.zh: 大约24岁时，他感到自己用眼睛思考。

D2.en: At 12-25, he became a vein to be exploited until exhausted.
D2.zh: 12到25岁间，他成了一根被开采到枯竭的矿脉。

D7.en: At 22, he faced a court of inquiry and disciplinary counsel over a scandal.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他因丑闻面临调查法庭和纪律委员会。

D13a.en: At 11, his mother requested a guardianship meeting about his custody.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他母亲要求召开监护会议解决他的抚养权问题。

D13b.en: At 22, he chose French citizenship and married Louise Guillemin.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他选择了法国国籍并娶了路易丝·吉耶曼。

D9.en: At 22, he spread news that Lindbergh was an honorary ENS student.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他散布消息说林德伯格是巴黎高师的荣誉学生。

D1.en: At 25-26, he shelved rejected manuscripts and returned to research with determination.
D1.zh: 25到26岁间，他搁置被拒的手稿，坚定地回到研究工作中。

M1.en: He told his sister he wanted to get into the Ecole Polytechnique.
M1.zh: 他告诉姐姐他想考入巴黎综合理工学院。

D15.en: He was a sickly child, skinny, frail, often ill with flu, bronchitis, ear inflammations.
D15.zh: 他幼年时体弱多病，瘦小虚弱，常患流感、支气管炎和耳炎。

M2.en: At 24, Jean-Baptiste planned to go to Paris and then to the Ecole Polytechnique.
M2.zh: 24岁时，让-巴蒂斯特计划去巴黎，然后上巴黎综合理工学院。

D4.en: He learned to calculate sight-lines, sail by dead reckoning, operate sextants, and keep a logbook.
D4.zh: 他学会了计算视线、航位推算、操作六分仪和记航海日志。

D5.en: He faced a court of inquiry and disciplinary counsel due to the scandal caused by the Revue of 1927.
D5.zh: 他因1927年《杂志》引发的丑闻面临调查法庭和纪律委员会。

D16.en: At 17, he excelled at quipping, teasing, and wisecracking with irony and Gallic scorn.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他擅长用讽刺和高卢式的轻蔑说俏皮话、挖苦人。

D8.en: He got angry when he felt cornered.
D8.zh: 他感到被逼入绝境时会生气。

D3.en: At 21, she gave a talk on Leibniz in class, impressing Sartre with her beauty and brilliance.
D3.zh: 21岁时，她在课堂上做了关于莱布尼茨的演讲，萨特被她的美貌和才华打动。

D11.en: At 20, she collaborated with three others on a song called 'L’Air connu'.
D11.zh: 20岁时，她与另外三人合作创作了一首名为《熟悉的旋律》的歌。

D7.en: At 21, she angrily told her father not to speak that way to her friend.
D7.zh: 21岁时，她生气地告诉父亲不要那样对她的朋友说话。

D4.en: At 23, she hiked in the same clothes she taught in, usually espadrilles.
D4.zh: 23岁时，她穿着上课的衣服去徒步，通常穿帆布鞋。

D10.en: At 21, she asked if she was expected to soften before his interesting pose.
D10.zh: 21岁时，她问他是否指望她在他那有趣的姿态面前软化。

D2.en: At 20-21, her final year of study was arduous, but she worked with intensity.
D2.zh: 20到21岁间，她最后一年的学业非常艰苦，但她全力以赴。

D9.en: At 20, she collaborated with three others on a song called 'L’Air connu'.
D9.zh: 20岁时，她与另外三人合作创作了一首名为《熟悉的旋律》的歌。

D15.en: At 23, she decided to spend her free half days and Sundays walking in the countryside.
D15.zh: 23岁时，她决定在空闲的半天和周日去乡下散步。

D8.en: At 24, she was overruled by him on who supplied something.
D8.zh: 24岁时，她被他否决，关于谁提供了什么。

M1.en: At 19, she and a partner succeeded in entering the ENS.
M1.zh: 19岁时，她和同伴成功考入巴黎高师。

D13a.en: At 23, she chose a less conventional future and revised their pact to last for life.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，她选择了不那么传统的未来，并将他们的约定改为终身有效。

M2.en: At 21, she accepted homage as if the triumph were her own.
M2.zh: 21岁时，她接受敬意，仿佛胜利是她自己的。

D14.en: As a small child, she performed with such aplomb she was praised as well-mannered.
D14.zh: 幼年时，她表演得如此从容，总被夸赞很有礼貌。

D5.en: At 24, she walked, hitchhiked, or took local buses into the mountains.
D5.zh: 24岁时，她步行、搭便车或坐当地巴士进山。

D20.en: At 23, she had a relationship based on total equality and reciprocity.
D20.zh: 23岁时，她建立了一段完全平等和互惠的关系。

D17.en: At 21, she proposed moving to her grandmother's apartment to rent rooms for income.
D17.zh: 21岁时，她提议搬到祖母的公寓，出租房间以增加收入。

M5.en: At 21, she suddenly came to the attention of Sartre and his student band.
M5.zh: 21岁时，她突然引起了萨特和他的学生群体的注意。

D18.en: At 23-24, she managed to save a little money during her year in Marseille.
D18.zh: 23到24岁间，她在马赛的一年里攒了一点钱。

D2.en: He saved money for an inexpensive instrument and sacrificed sleep to practice.
D2.zh: 他攒钱买了便宜的乐器，并牺牲睡眠来练习。

D3.en: At 16, he had his first magazine publication: Kuraki Sensei no Sōshiki in Danran.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他在杂志《团栾》上首次发表作品《仓木先生之葬》。

D4.en: He saved money for an inexpensive instrument and sacrificed sleep to practice.
D4.zh: 他攒钱买了便宜的乐器，并牺牲睡眠来练习。

D6.en: At 24, he traveled with a friend from the university dormitory to the devastated area.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他和大学宿舍的朋友一起去了灾区。

D12.en: As a teenager, he was apprenticed to a pharmaceutical business in Osaka and escorted the owner's blind daughter to music lessons.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他在大阪的药房当学徒，并护送老板失明的女儿去上音乐课。

D9.en: At 19, he entered Tokyo Imperial University in October.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他于十月进入东京帝国大学。

D10.en: As a teenager, he had a feverish encounter with a distant relation, clashing teeth.
D10.zh: 十几岁时，他和一个远亲在发烧般的相遇中牙齿相撞。

D7.en: He considers various motives that will lead him to arson.
D7.zh: 他考虑各种最终会让他纵火的动机。

D13b.en: At 23, he works at the Finance Ministry for eight months.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，他在财务省工作了八个月。

D3.en: He agreed to teach his landlord's daughter Bengali in exchange for help with his English.
D3.zh: 他同意教房东女儿孟加拉语，以换取她帮他纠正英语。

D2.en: He began lessons before dawn, memorizing Sanskrit declensions.
D2.zh: 他在黎明前开始上课，背诵梵语词形变化。

D9.en: He formed a secret society that met in a dilapidated building in Calcutta.
D9.zh: 他成立了一个秘密社团，在加尔各答一条偏僻小巷的破房子里聚会。

M6.en: He became conscious of a stirring within, and his world of experience seemed lighted.
M6.zh: 他意识到内心有股骚动，他的经验世界似乎被照亮了。

D12.en: He took over his late father's estates and worked to understand zamindari law.
D12.zh: 他接管了已故父亲的庄园，努力理解柴明达尔法律。

D13a.en: He was instructed by his father to return from London instead of staying for a qualification.
D13a.zh: 他父亲指示他从伦敦回来，而不是留在那里取得资格。

D13b.en: In 1811 he married a nine-year-old Pirali Brahmin girl from Jessore.
D13b.zh: 1811年，他娶了一个来自杰索尔的九岁皮拉利婆罗门女孩。

D20.en: He took over as secretary of the original Brahmo Samaj in 1884.
D20.zh: 1884年，他接任原梵社的秘书。

D7.en: At 18, he tore off his offending facial hair and stuffed it in his pocket.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他扯掉惹事的胡子塞进口袋。

D6.en: At 18, he swiftly picked up and came to like a string of English songs.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他很快学会并喜欢上一连串英文歌。

D5.en: At 19, he and a nephew got as far as Madras on a boat for England, then turned back.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他和一个侄子乘船去英国，到了马德拉斯又折返。

D14.en: At 19, he performed as Valmiki in the first performance held at Jorasanko.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他在乔拉桑科的首演中扮演瓦尔米基。

D15.en: At 12, he walked alone with only a staff in hand.
D15.zh: 12岁时，他独自一人，手里只有一根手杖。

D16.en: At 18, he deeply resented being mocked but had to give in out of politeness.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他深感被嘲弄，但出于礼貌只好让步。

D4.en: At 17, he wandered curiously from room to room, imagining stones held secrets.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他好奇地从一个房间逛到另一个，想象石头藏着秘密。

D10.en: At 17, he said: 'I could never insult her memory by putting a common label on her love for me.'
D10.zh: 17岁时，他说：'我绝不能给她对我的爱贴上普通标签，侮辱她的记忆。'

D7.en: At 10-11, she touched a lighted match to a little empty hut where dogs sheltered.
D7.zh: 10到11岁时，她用点燃的火柴碰了狗避雨的小空棚。

D3.en: At 18-19, she ordered books from England and received them like luxurious gifts.
D3.zh: 18到19岁时，她从英国订购书籍，收到时像收到最奢侈的礼物。

D13a.en: At 24, she decided to run away from her husband and two children.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，她决定逃离丈夫和两个孩子。

D12.en: At 24, she visited the Taylers' house several times a week to give Maude respite.
D12.zh: 24岁时，她每周去泰勒家几次，让莫德休息一下。

D18.en: From 14 to 19, she worked intermittently in Salisbury as a nurse-maid and telephone operator.
D18.zh: 14到19岁间，她在索尔兹伯里间断地做保姆和电话接线员。

D13b.en: At 19, she married a civil servant on the eve of the war.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，她在战争前夕嫁给了一个公务员。

D11.en: At 17, she left Salisbury to return to the farm to write a novel.
D11.zh: 17岁时，她离开索尔兹伯里回到农场写小说。

D9.en: In 1943 at age 24, her comrades supported her desire to leave her family for political activism.
D9.zh: 1943年24岁时，她的同志支持她离开家庭投身政治活动的愿望。

D10.en: At 12-13, a comment from her mother made her explode with fury.
D10.zh: 12到13岁时，母亲的一句话让她暴怒。

D4.en: She wandered among extraordinary animals, jagged rocks, rushing streams, and aromatic bushes.
D4.zh: 她在奇异的动物、嶙峋的岩石、湍急的溪流和芳香的灌木丛中漫步。

D15.en: She thought nothing of walking ten or fifteen miles to attend dance parties.
D15.zh: 她不在乎走十到十五英里路去参加舞会。

D5.en: She touched a lighted match to the little empty hut where the dogs sometimes took shelter.
D5.zh: 她把点燃的火柴伸向小狗有时躲藏的小空棚屋。

M7.en: At 24, she felt a sacred obligation to represent Communism to the outside world.
M7.zh: 24岁时，她感到有神圣义务向外界代表共产主义。

D16.en: In the 1950s, she insisted no one was safe from the witch hunt and all US academic freedom was shut down.
D16.zh: 1950年代，她坚持认为没有人能逃过政治迫害，美国所有学术自由都被关闭。

D8.en: In the 1960s, she could not contain her political hostility and contempt for the affair.
D8.zh: 1960年代，她无法抑制对这件事的政治敌意和轻蔑。

D17.en: At 11, her Persian cat jumped into hot water, screamed, and later sneezed and coughed after being placed in her bed.
D17.zh: 11岁时，她的波斯猫跳进热水，尖叫，被放到她床上后开始打喷嚏咳嗽。

D3.en: At 8, he first heard bullets fired on a demonstration.
D3.zh: 8岁时，他第一次听到示威中枪声。

D7.en: As a teenager and young adult, he proudly announced his atheism and stopped praying and fasting.
D7.zh: 十几岁到二十多岁时，他自豪地宣称自己不再祈祷和斋戒。

D6.en: During adolescence, he made many friends.
D6.zh: 青春期时，他交了很多朋友。

D12.en: He was born on 11 December 1911 in al-Jamaliyya, Cairo.
D12.zh: 他于1911年12月11日出生在开罗老城的贾马利亚区。

D10.en: At 20-25, he experienced a love that shook him, perhaps the ultimate love of his life.
D10.zh: 20到25岁时，他经历了一段震撼他的爱情，也许是他一生中最深刻的爱情。

M4.en: At 7, he watched 1919 revolution demonstrations from his roof, seeing women on donkey carts and English soldiers firing.
M4.zh: 7岁时，他从屋顶观看1919年革命示威，看到妇女乘驴车，英国士兵开枪。

D2.en: At about 5-7, he began his education at a kuttab (Qur'an school), learning religion and literacy before primary school.
D2.zh: 大约5到7岁时，他在古兰经学校开始学习宗教和识字，然后上小学。

D11.en: At 8, he first heard bullets fired on a demonstration.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他第一次听到示威中枪声。

D9.en: At 19, he hailed socialism as a compromise between communism and individualism.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他称赞社会主义是共产主义和个人主义之间的妥协。

D16.en: He resolved to reject myth and superstition, even if it meant disowning Adam as his father.
D16.zh: 他决心拒绝神话和迷信，哪怕这意味着不认亚当为父。

D2.en: At age 12-16, he exchanged voluminous letters nearly every day.
D2.zh: 12到16岁间，他几乎每天交换大量信件。

M1.en: At 19, he graduated top of his class at Gakushūin.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他以班级第一的成绩从学习院毕业。

D9.en: At 15, he became the youngest editorial board member in the literary club's history.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他成为文学社百年历史上最年轻的编辑委员。

D13a.en: At 23, he resigned from the ministry after nine months with his father's permission.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他得到父亲允许，在任职九个月后从省厅辞职。

D11.en: At 16, his story was serialized in four issues of Bungei-Bunka from September to December 1941.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他的小说在1941年9月至12月间连载于《文艺文化》四期。

D16.en: At 21, he made a paradoxical statement that startled and troubled Kawabata.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他做了一个让川端康成震惊和困扰的悖论式发言。

D12.en: At 20, he was mobilized as a student and served as a second lieutenant when Japan surrendered.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他被作为学生动员，在日本投降时是一名少尉。

D1.en: At 20, he waited four weeks for a decision on his stories, which were finally rejected.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他等了四周才得到故事的决定，最终被退回。

D6.en: At 17, he associated with a group of writers who believed the war was holy.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他与一群认为战争是神圣的作家交往。

D3.en: At 23, he read Havelock Ellis's works on sexual psychology.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他读了哈夫洛克·埃利斯关于性心理的著作。

D14.en: At 21, he took lessons from novelist Kunieda Shiro's widow.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他向小说家国枝四郎的遗孀学习。

D4.en: At 13, his grandmother took him to see Kanadehon Chushingura for the first time.
D4.zh: 13岁时，祖母第一次带他去看《假名手本忠臣藏》。

M6.en: At 23, he set to work with resolve on a novel titled Confessions of a Mask.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他下定决心开始写一部名为《假面的告白》的小说。

M4.en: At 19, upon receiving induction order, he wrote 'Long Live the Emperor!' as his will.
M4.zh: 19岁时，接到入伍通知后，他写下'天皇陛下万岁！'作为遗书。

D15.en: At 19, he was sent to a naval engineering school in Maizuru for a fortnight of training.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他被派往舞鹤的海军工程学校接受为期两周的训练。

D10.en: Just before his fifth birthday, he vomited something 'the color of coffee' and went into a coma.
D10.zh: 快5岁时，他吐出了'咖啡色'的东西，然后陷入昏迷。

D5.en: At 16, he wrote to a friend hoping for a tumultuous war.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他写信给朋友东，希望有一场轰轰烈烈的战争。

D18.en: He was a fan of manga at age 6-8.
D18.zh: 他6到8岁时是漫画迷。

D20X.en: At 16, he took steps to end his friendship with an older boy.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他采取措施终止与一个年长男孩的友谊。

M3.en: At 16-18, he hero-worshipped Azuma, perhaps the first indelibly important figure in his literary life.
M3.zh: 16到18岁时，他崇拜阿兹玛，这可能是他文学生涯中第一个不可磨灭的重要人物。

M2.en: At 22, his mother demanded he prepare for the top examination.
M2.zh: 22岁时，他母亲要求他准备顶尖考试。

D20.en: At 25, he helped choose the play Hanago as a comic follow-up to Hanjo.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他参与选择了喜剧《哈纳戈》作为《汉乔》的续作。

M5.en: From age 0 to 25, he showed arrogance.
M5.zh: 0到25岁间，他表现出傲慢。

M7.en: At 16, he was mobilized for student labor.
M7.zh: 16岁时，他被动员参加学生劳动。

D6.en: He toured England with Trinity College, Dublin, playing against Northants.
D6.zh: 他随都柏林三一学院巡游英格兰，与北安普敦郡队比赛。

D7.en: He was as naughty as the rest in tormenting Mr Breul to a rage.
D7.zh: 他和别人一样淘气，把布勒尔先生气得发狂。

D12.en: He returned home to take up his appointment as Lecturer in French at Trinity College.
D12.zh: 他回到家乡，担任三一学院法语讲师。

D15.en: He won a juvenile tennis tournament with S Roe in August 1920.
D15.zh: 1920年8月，他和S·罗一起赢得了青少年网球赛。

D9.en: He came up with the idea of a parody on Corneille's Le Cid, called Le Kid.
D9.zh: 他想出了模仿高乃依《熙德》的戏仿作品《小子》。

D5.en: He did not want to be alone with her, so he persuaded Georges Pelorson to go along.
D5.zh: 他不想和她单独相处，于是说服乔治·佩洛尔松一起去。

D3.en: He continued to go to the cinema, enjoying early silent feature films of Buster Keaton.
D3.zh: 他继续去电影院，欣赏巴斯特·基顿的早期无声长片。

D20.en: At 23, he asked Alfred Péron to assist him and brought him to meet Joyce.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他请阿尔弗雷德·佩龙帮忙，带他去见乔伊斯。

D18.en: At 24, he won ten pounds for his poem 'Whoroscope' to fund a trip to Kassel.
D18.zh: 24岁时，他凭诗作《霍罗斯科佩》赢得10英镑，用于前往卡塞尔的路费。

M3.en: He was under the spell of her father's genius and character.
M3.zh: 他被她父亲的才华和性格深深吸引。

D16.en: At 24, he sent a poem to George Russell, a senior figure of the Irish literary revival.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他给爱尔兰文学复兴前辈乔治·拉塞尔寄了一首诗。

M2.en: He was under the spell of her father's genius and character.
M2.zh: 他被她父亲的才华和性格深深吸引。

D17.en: At 25, he confided in her about his own state of mind and miserable situation.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他向她倾诉自己的心境和悲惨处境。

M6.en: At 22, went to Paris as exchange student to École Normale and settled there.
M6.zh: 22岁时作为交换生去巴黎高师，并定居下来。

M7.en: At 24, he was delighted that the publication would make his return to Trinity a success.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他因这出版物能让他成功回到三一学院而高兴。

M8.en: He was deeply influenced by the genius and character of her father.
M8.zh: 他被她父亲的才华和性格深深影响。

D4.en: He poured sulfuric acid into the sink, thinking it was water.
D4.zh: 他把硫酸倒进水槽，以为那是水。

D3.en: At 15, he avidly read Tolstoy, Dostoyevsky, and Chekhov given by her.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他如饥似渴地阅读她给的托尔斯泰、陀思妥耶夫斯基和契诃夫的作品。

D15.en: He had fevers from malaria but they soon went away.
D15.zh: 他得了疟疾发烧，但很快就好了。

D16.en: He persuaded a cinema owner to buy two hundred postcards.
D16.zh: 他说服一家电影院老板买了两百张明信片。

D10.en: He said he's tired of solitude and may marry another woman if she doesn't come.
D10.zh: 他说他厌倦了孤独，如果她不来，他可能会娶别的女人。

D5.en: He smoked many opium pipes there, finding it different.
D5.zh: 他在那里抽了很多鸦片烟，觉得不一样。

D14.en: At 22, he attended a farewell celebration thronged by 150 prominent citizens at his hotel.
D14.zh: 22岁时，他参加了150位名流在酒店为他举办的告别庆祝活动。

D1.en: At 13, he called for people to follow their desires with enthusiasm and perseverance, citing Columbus and Marconi.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他呼吁人们以热情和毅力追随自己的欲望，并举哥伦布和马可尼为例。

D9.en: He called for people to follow their desires with enthusiasm and perseverance.
D9.zh: 他呼吁人们以热情和毅力追随自己的欲望。

D7.en: At 22, he said, 'I'm so bored of fighting with my father.'
D7.zh: 22岁时，他说：‘我和父亲吵架吵烦了。’

D11.en: At 14-15, he had 16 poems published in a magazine and others in student journals.
D11.zh: 14到15岁时，他在杂志上发表了16首诗，还在学生刊物上发表了其他作品。

M1.en: At 19, he made many personal sacrifices to ensure his book Crepusculario was published.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他做出许多个人牺牲，以确保《黄昏》得以出版。

M4.en: At 17, he was arrested by Peruvian police on suspicion of being a leftist.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他因被怀疑是左派而被秘鲁警方逮捕。

D2.en: At 22, he continued to read voraciously and write feverishly despite hunger.
D2.zh: 22岁时，他尽管饥饿，仍如饥似渴地阅读并狂热地写作。

D13a.en: At 23, in a letter he asked Laurita to see if his father would bail him out.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他在信中请劳丽塔看看他父亲是否愿意保释他。

D18.en: At 22-23, he produced and printed comic postcards with Alvaro Hinojosa.
D18.zh: 22到23岁时，他和阿尔瓦罗·伊诺霍萨一起制作并印刷了漫画明信片。

D11.en: She worked on a long romantic poem, At the Edge of the Sea.
D11.zh: 她正在创作一首长浪漫诗《海的边缘》。

D13a.en: At 18, she married Nikolay Gumilyov, believing it was her fate to be his wife.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，她嫁给了尼古拉·古米廖夫，相信这是她的命运。

D3.en: By thirteen, she was infatuated with poetry, especially Alexander Blok's.
D3.zh: 十三岁时，她迷上了诗歌，尤其是亚历山大·勃洛克的诗。

D15.en: As a child, she was a tomboy who could swim like a fish and climb like a cat.
D15.zh: 幼年时，她是个假小子，游泳像鱼，爬树像猫。

D5.en: At 23, she refused to go to Africa with him.
D5.zh: 23岁时，她拒绝和他去非洲。

D12.en: At age 14, she sent him 45 roubles by telegraph on 15 January 1926.
D12.zh: 14岁时，她于1926年1月15日给他电汇了45卢布。

D18.en: At 25, she wrote to Gumilyov worrying about their finances, mentioning pawned items.
D18.zh: 25岁时她写信给古米廖夫，担心经济状况，提到典当物品。

D3.en: At 10-12, she read Moby Dick, skipping the people parts and identifying with the whale.
D3.zh: 10到12岁时，她读《白鲸》，跳过人物部分，认同鲸鱼。

D14.en: At 20, she began reading her poetry at the Bohemian Embassy during her second year of university.
D14.zh: 20岁时，她大学二年级开始在波希米亚大使馆朗读自己的诗歌。

D11.en: As a small child, she drew things like flying rabbits in her pictures.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她画的东西里有会飞的兔子。

D1.en: At 18, she resolved to write the 'Great Canadian Novel' and kept that determination.
D1.zh: 18岁时，她决心写“伟大的加拿大小说”，并保持了决心。

D6.en: At 22-23, she cooked and ate meals with Mary Irving and shared a bathroom.
D6.zh: 22到23岁间，她和玛丽·欧文一起做饭吃饭，共用浴室。

D16.en: At 20, she droned on with no inflection in her voice, and you could hear a pin drop.
D16.zh: 20岁时，她毫无抑扬顿挫地长篇大论，全场鸦雀无声。

D15.en: In high school, sports weren't really her strong point.
D15.zh: 高中时，体育不是她的强项。

D4.en: At 21, she set type and printed 250 copies of a chapbook on a flatbed press.
D4.zh: 21岁时，她在科伦坡的地下室用平板印刷机排字并印了250本小册子。

D9.en: At 19, she roped a friend into a show and wrote lyrics for songs.
D9.zh: 19岁时，她拉朋友参与演出，并为歌曲写词。

D18.en: At 21, she distributed her books to local bookstores, selling them for fifty cents each.
D18.zh: 21岁时，她把自己的书分发到当地书店，每本卖50分。

M1.en: At 21, she completed an honours English degree with minors in philosophy and French.
M1.zh: 21岁时，她完成了英语荣誉学位，辅修哲学和法语。

D12.en: At 19, she was den mother to a group of about ten little campers.
D12.zh: 19岁时，她担任大约十名小露营者的女领队。

D13a.en: At 11, she abruptly stopped babysitting for a family, leaving them wondering why.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，她突然停止为一家照看孩子，让他们不解。

D20.en: At 24, she and two other well-educated women developed a lasting friendship.
D20.zh: 24岁时，她和另外两位受过良好教育的女性建立了持久的友谊。

M4.en: At 23, she was firmly convinced she would be a writer, specifically a Canadian writer.
M4.zh: 23岁时，她坚信自己会成为作家，而且是加拿大作家。

M7.en: At 23-24, she developed a sense of her place of origin, prompted by others' lack of awareness of Canada.
M7.zh: 23到24岁时，她因他人对加拿大缺乏了解而萌生了对自己出生地的意识。

D13b.en: At 24-25, she felt she could not handle both writing and political activity at once.
D13b.zh: 24到25岁间，她觉得她无法同时处理写作和政治活动。

D12.en: At 12, he helped his brother with cooking, a skill he learned at home.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他帮哥哥做饭，这是他在家学会的技能。

D2.en: At 18, he matriculated with a scholarship to study medicine at University College, Ibadan.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他获得奖学金进入伊巴丹大学学院学医。

D11.en: As a teenager, he edited ephemeral publications and contributed poems, essays, and short stories.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他编辑临时刊物并投稿诗歌、散文和短篇小说。

D6.en: At 22-23, he associated with Ezeilo in the Dancing Club and their friendship progressed.
D6.zh: 22到23岁间，他与埃泽伊洛在舞蹈俱乐部交往，友谊加深。

D9.en: At 21-22, he and other officials decided to leave the Dancing Club and form the Social Circle.
D9.zh: 21到22岁时，他和其他负责人决定离开舞蹈俱乐部，成立社交圈。

D1.en: At 18, he brought honour to his village by throwing Amalinze the Cat.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他摔倒了阿马林泽猫，为村子带来荣誉。

M1.en: At 18, he brought honor to his village by throwing Amalinze the Cat in a wrestling match.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他摔跤击败了阿马林泽猫，为村子带来了荣誉。

D16.en: He published two articles, 'Mr Okafor versus Arts Students' and 'Hiawatha', on the same page in 1952.
D16.zh: 1952年，他发表了《奥卡福先生对文科学生》和《海华沙》两篇文章，在同一页上。

D5.en: He asked the Controller to come to Onitsha, and he did.
D5.zh: 他请主管来奥尼查，主管来了。

D14.en: He was elected editor of the English Department journal in his third year.
D14.zh: 他在英语系三年级时被选为期刊编辑。

D15.en: He accompanied his mates to fetch water at a spring along a rough path.
D15.zh: 他陪同伴去泉边打水，路很崎岖。

D20.en: He and other officials decided to leave the Dancing Club and form the Social Circle.
D20.zh: 他和别的干事决定离开跳舞俱乐部，成立社交圈。

D13b.en: At 12-18, he was punished for speaking Igbo at college.
D13b.zh: 12到18岁时，他因在学院说伊博语而受罚。

M4.en: At 20, he published 'Polar Undergraduate' in a journal.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他在期刊上发表了《极地本科生》。

M6.en: At 23, he accepted the outcome as perhaps his destiny.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他接受结果，认为可能是他的命运。

D12.en: At 24, he became a history and Chinese teacher at Jinhua Middle School and a special correspondent for Shanghai newspapers.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他受聘为金华中学堂历史、国文教员，并被《申报》聘为特约通讯员。

D2.en: At 5, his father woke him before dawn to study, saying 'rise at cockcrow and practice the sword'.
D2.zh: 5岁时，天未亮父亲就催他早起读书，说是“闻鸡起舞”。

D17.en: At 6, he gave his own trousers to a shivering classmate wearing only a torn pair.
D17.zh: 6岁时，他见同学穿破单裤冷得发抖，便脱下自己的裤子给他穿上。

D3.en: At 23-24 in Japan, he frequented bookstores and libraries, collecting Marxist works and studying late into the night.
D3.zh: 23到24岁在日本，他常跑书店图书馆，搜集马克思主义著作日译本，白天工作，晚上攻读至深夜。

D16.en: At 23, he spoke calmly and argued on principle, showing a journalist's fearless demeanor.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他侃侃而谈，从容应对，据理力争，展现了报人的绝佳风度。

M1.en: At 13, he passed the imperial exam with top marks but was deliberately ranked tenth to curb his pride.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他考秀才成绩可列榜首，但主考官怕他骄傲，降格录取为第十名。

M2.en: At 14, he became a xiucai, bringing honor to his family.
M2.zh: 14岁时，他中秀才，为邵氏光宗耀祖。

D14.en: At 14, he became a xiucai, bringing honor to his family.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他中秀才，为邵氏光宗耀祖。

D18.en: At 14, after winning prize silver for his xiucai degree, he gave it all to jealous youths who confronted him.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他中秀才领了银洋，被几个嫉妒的青年拦住，他微微一笑，把银元全给了他们。

D7.en: At 23, he grabbed a foot-binding cloth and chopped it into pieces with a cleaver.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他夺过裹脚布，拿起柴刀剁成了好几段。

D9.en: He was elected as the director of the provincial press association.
D9.zh: 他被推选为省报界公会干事长。

D6.en: He joined the 'Southern Society' through an introduction.
D6.zh: 他经人介绍参加了“南社”。

D13a.en: He wrote to Mr. Tang the next day, apologetically declining the matter.
D13a.zh: 他第二天写信给汤先生，抱歉地辞谢了这件事。

M4.en: He resolved to take Russia as a model to transform China.
M4.zh: 他决心以俄为师，改造中国。

M7.en: At 22-23, he used his publication to expose evils and advocate patriotism.
M7.zh: 22到23岁时，他利用自己的报刊揭露丑恶，倡导爱国主义。

D11.en: At 8, he was often carried to the prefect's office to compose poems, gaining fame as a child prodigy.
D11.zh: 他8岁时常被知府派人背抱入衙答对作诗，被称为“神童”。

D4.en: At 23, he co-founded the One Daily with Chen Bulei and Zhang Rentian, his first newspaper venture.
D4.zh: 他23岁时与陈布雷、张任天办《一日报》，初次尝试办报。

D13b.en: At 24, he married Shen Xiaoreng as arranged by his parents.
D13b.zh: 他24岁时由父母作主，与沈小仍成亲。

D20.en: He served as class secretary for two terms.
D20.zh: 他曾担任两届本年级的书记。

D8.en: At 23, he confronted his accuser in court with irrefutable facts and eloquence, forcing the case to be dismissed.
D8.zh: 23岁时，他毅然出庭对质，用无可辩驳的事实和口才迫使法院宣布原告不能成立。

D9.en: At 20-23, he actively participated in the New Culture movement at Peking University, joined student groups, and founded two clubs.
D9.zh: 20到23岁时，他积极参加北大新文化运动，加入多个学生社团，并创办了两个社团。

D12.en: At 10, his father was falsely accused; he accompanied his father seeking help from a journalist.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他父亲被诬陷，他跟着父亲四处求助，得到记者帮助。

D3.en: At 23, he published a translation of Lenin's article in New Youth and a book on Chinese fiction history.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他在《新青年》发表列宁译文，并出版《中国小说史大纲》。

D16.en: At 15, influenced by Sun Yat-sen's party, he became a reporter for Anhui's Minyan Bao.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他受国民党影响，担任安徽《民岩报》外勤记者。

D1.en: At 17-20, he struggled from Anhui to Shenyang, was arrested, exiled to Shanghai, and sold writings to survive.
D1.zh: 17到20岁时，他从安庆到沈阳，被捕后流亡上海，卖文为生。

D13a.en: At 13, he secretly tried to join the youth army but was stopped by his father.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他偷偷参加青年军，被父亲拦下。

D5.en: At 18, he returned to Anqing to start an anti-Yuan newspaper, was arrested, and released after 3 days.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他回安庆办反袁报纸，被捕，3天后获释。

D6.en: At 17, he formed a 'writing company' with friends to sell articles to magazines.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他与朋友组成“卖文公司”，向杂志投稿卖文。

D20.en: He founded two student organizations where he was the leader.
D20.zh: 他创办了两个由他负责的学生社团。

M4.en: He resolved to become an upright journalist, using his pen to write about justice and injustice in society.
M4.zh: 他立志要做一名正派的新闻记者，用笔写出人间社会的公理与不平。

M7.en: He aspired to serve the country through journalism.
M7.zh: 他以新闻为报国的志向。

D18.en: He became a Chinese teacher at Beijing Municipal Normal School and later returned to Beijing Yishi Bao as an editor.
D18.zh: 他经人介绍入北京市立师范学校任国文教员，不久又回北京《益世报》担任编辑工作。

M3.en: He was amazed by the power of journalists and decided to enter the newspaper industry.
M3.zh: 他震惊于新闻记者的威力，决心投身报业。

D12.en: At 13, he became an apprentice at a tea shop.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他到茶叶店当学徒。

D18.en: At 13, he became an apprentice at a tea shop.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他到茶叶店当学徒。

D2.en: At 17, he decided to learn English and calculated the cost of a correspondence course.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他决定学英文，算了一笔函授学校的费用。

D3.en: At 13-15, he cut out favorite articles from a newspaper supplement with a pedicure knife.
D3.zh: 13到15岁时，他用修脚刀挖下副刊上喜爱的文章。

D13a.en: At about 19, he reluctantly married a woman chosen by his mother and didn't consummate for three days.
D13a.zh: 约19岁时，他勉强接受母亲选的妻子，三天未圆房。

M4.en: At 25, he joined the Chinese Communist Party with Qin Bangxian and Peng Shuzhi as sponsors.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他由秦邦宪和彭述之介绍加入中国共产党。

D4.en: At 13-15, he cut out favorite articles from a newspaper supplement with a pedicure knife.
D4.zh: 13到15岁时，他用修脚刀挖下副刊上喜爱的文章。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote his first article to a newspaper supplement under the pen name 'Juan Fei'.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他写了第一篇稿子寄给《益世报》副刊，署名“倦飞”。

D13b.en: At 18, he reluctantly married, didn't consummate for three days, but later grew attached to his wife.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他勉强结婚，三天未圆房，后来离不开妻子了。

D9.en: At 24, he was elected propaganda minister and edited a union weekly.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他被推选为宣传部长，主编工会周刊。

D5.en: At 25, he abandoned his jointly-run small newspaper after a friend warned him.
D5.zh: 25岁时，朋友报信后他丢弃了合资经营的小报馆。

D7.en: At 25, he was wanted by warlord Chu Yupu and left Tianjin for Shanghai.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他遭军阀褚玉璞通缉，离津赴沪。

D3.en: He told his mother he would spend the money on studies at the University of Marburg.
D3.zh: 他告诉母亲，他会把钱花在马尔堡大学的学习上。

D2.en: He began serious study of musical composition under Iurii Engel and Reinhold Glière in Moscow.
D2.zh: 他在莫斯科开始跟随尤里·恩格尔和莱因霍尔德·格利埃尔认真学习作曲。

D10.en: He wrote an immense, original love-letter to Olya.
D10.zh: 他给奥莉娅写了一封巨大而独创的情书。

M8.en: In his early childhood, he was baptized by the nurse who looked after him.
M8.zh: 他幼年时被照顾他的保姆施洗。

D14.en: His poem and other contributions to 'Funereal Hurrah' were so out of line.
D14.zh: 他的诗和对《葬礼欢呼》的其他投稿非常出格。

D6.en: Aleksandr Gavronsky visited and regaled him with accounts of his own recent studies abroad.
D6.zh: 亚历山大·加夫龙斯基拜访了他，并讲述了自己最近在国外学习的经历。

D11.en: His student jottings contained a snide verse lampoon aimed at Theodor Lipps.
D11.zh: 他的学生笔记中有一首针对特奥多尔·利普斯的讽刺诗。

D5.en: He was whiplashed by one of the mounted Cossacks during the demonstrations.
D5.zh: 他在示威中被一名骑马的哥萨克鞭打。

D7.en: At 24, he insisted on accepting a challenge to a personal encounter.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他坚持接受个人对决的挑战。

D1.en: His third approach to Cohen was successful despite initial unfriendliness.
D1.zh: 尽管科恩一开始很不友好，他第三次尝试成功了。

D13b.en: He registered at the Law Faculty of the University of Novorossiisk in Odessa.
D13b.zh: 他在敖德萨的新俄罗斯大学法律系注册了。

M6.en: He was converted for life by the emotions he experienced on the streets of Marburg.
M6.zh: 他在马尔堡街头经历的情感让他终生改变。

D9.en: He took part in activities of Centrifuge, a group of literary innovators.
D9.zh: 他参加了文学创新团体“离心机”的活动。

M4.en: He desired to cut loose from his family.
M4.zh: 他渴望摆脱家庭束缚。

D15.en: He enjoyed walking the busy streets and breathing the un-Muscovite atmosphere.
D15.zh: 他喜欢走在繁忙的街道上，呼吸非莫斯科的氛围。

M1.en: His poem reflected a move toward Mayakovskian poetics.
M1.zh: 他的诗反映了向马雅可夫斯基诗学的转变。

D13a.en: At 20, he transferred to the Law Faculty due to aversion to the dissecting room.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他因厌恶解剖室而转到法律系。

M7.en: As a child of 5-10, he maintained an early serious concern to rescue all folk by some unprecedented shining deed.
M7.zh: 5到10岁时，他坚持认为要通过某种前所未有的光辉事迹拯救所有人，这成了他早期的严肃关切。

D16.en: At 24, his poem seemed to reflect a move toward Mayakovskian poetics.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他的诗似乎反映了向马雅可夫斯基诗学的转变。

D15.en: As a child, he jumped over fireworks as they shot up from the ground.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他从地上喷出的焰火上跳过去。

D3.en: In Japan, he studied Japanese newspapers and wrote for many papers; after returning, he submitted articles whenever free.
D3.zh: 到了日本后，他留心日本报业，为多家报纸撰稿；回国后一有空就向报馆投稿。

D9.en: At 17, he was elected by classmates to draft a telegram supporting Shanghai students.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他被同学公推执笔，发了一封声援上海学生的电报。

D5.en: At 25, he traveled from Shanghai through Anhui, Henan, Shaanxi to Sichuan, then to Chongqing.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他从上海经安徽、河南、陕西进入四川，再到重庆。

D13b.en: At 17, after failing the imperial exam, he became more determined to reject the exam system.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他考秀才未中，这使他更坚决地无意科举。

D6.en: At 18, he entered a garden with his group, chose a tea shop, and sat down to drink tea.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他和一行人进到园内，选了家茶店坐下喝茶。

D13a.en: At 20, he was appointed as a county magistrate in Henan but firmly declined.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他以知县即用外放河南，但他坚辞不受。

D2.en: From age 20 to 25, he studied law at Chuo University in Japan, working hard day and night, also learning English.
D2.zh: 20到25岁间，他赴日本入中央大学习法律科，日夜勤勉研习，并抽空学习英语。

M1.en: As a student, he finished a physics exam first, believing academic work must be honest.
M1.zh: 他第一个做完物理考试，觉得学术不能作假。

D14.en: He soon began writing articles in Japanese newspapers.
D14.zh: 他不久就开始在日文报刊上撰写文章。

M4.en: After the Sino-French war, he resolved to overthrow the Qing and establish a republic.
M4.zh: 中法战争失败后，他决心推翻清朝，建立民国。

D7.en: At 19, he passed the imperial exam but refused to serve as a county magistrate.
D7.zh: 19岁时他考中进士，但坚决不去当知县。
