D18.en: As a child, he studied in Ningbo and befriended many local literati.
D18.zh: 他小时候在宁波读书，结交了很多当地文人。

D16.en: At gatherings, people urged him to speak, and his speeches won applause.
D16.zh: 宴会上，认识他的人总催他讲话，话音一落，赞叹声满屋。

D19.en: His writing was detailed and elegant, impressing readers.
D19.zh: 他叙述详尽，文辞雅致，读者赞叹。

D17.en: His education relied entirely on his mother's teaching.
D17.zh: 他的学问全靠母亲教导。

D11.en: He enjoyed singing Peking opera, especially Mei Lanfang's pieces, and learned from records.
D11.zh: 他喜欢唱京戏，尤其喜欢梅兰芳，常跟着唱片学唱。

D3.en: He loved reading extracurricular books, from children's magazines to novels.
D3.zh: 他爱读课外书，从儿童画报到章回小说。

D4.en: He played with printing blocks and memorized poems from them.
D4.zh: 他把雕版当玩具，钻进去捉迷藏，还记住了上面的诗词。

D12.en: He relied on government stipends, wore straw sandals, and moved around.
D12.zh: 他靠公费度日，穿草鞋，受军训，四处颠沛。

D9.en: He co-edited a fifth-grade class magazine with his teacher.
D9.zh: 他和老师一起编五年级级刊《喔喔啼》。

D18.en: He spent most of his allowance on martial arts novels.
D18.zh: 他把大部分零用钱都花在买武侠小说上。

D7.en: He stood up to bullying students and was expelled.
D7.zh: 他看不惯国民党学生横行霸道，打抱不平，被勒令退学。

M4.en: He stood up to bullying students and was expelled.
M4.zh: 他看不惯国民党学生横行霸道，打抱不平，被勒令退学。

M6.en: He left his wife and job to pursue his dream of becoming a diplomat.
M6.zh: 他抛妻离职，北上追求外交官之梦。

D14.en: He wrote, directed, and starred in an English play.
D14.zh: 他自编自导自演英语话剧《月亮升起》。

D11.en: He wrote a story for the wall newspaper about a girl meeting a snake.
D11.zh: 他在壁报上写了一篇阿丽丝遇眼镜蛇的故事。

D1.en: At 13, he fled from Xincheng to Yuqian by boat and on foot after hearing Hangzhou had fallen.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他听说杭州沦陷，从新塍坐船又步行逃到於潜。

D10.en: He may not have known when his mother and brother died.
D10.zh: 他可能不知道母亲和弟弟何时病故。

D2.en: He studied hard in the fields at dawn despite ragged clothes and hunger.
D2.zh: 他衣衫破旧面带饥色，但天蒙蒙亮就在田野上朗读英语和语文。

D15.en: He always finished first in the armed负重 race at school sports meets.
D15.zh: 他在校运动会的武装负重赛跑中总是第一个冲刺。

M1.en: He stood out among 109 applicants and was the first hired after exams.
M1.zh: 他在109名应聘者中脱颖而出，第一个被录用。

D5.en: He boarded a plane to Hong Kong, expecting to return in half a year.
D5.zh: 他带着半年就回来的期待登上飞往香港的飞机。

D13a.en: He wrote to his father and girlfriend for advice; his father encouraged him.
D13a.zh: 他写信给父亲和女友征询意见，父亲回信鼓励他。

D12.en: At 9, orphaned, he sold sugarcane and olives, memorized the Thousand Poets by peeping through a school window.
D12.zh: 9岁时父母双亡，他卖甘蔗橄榄，偷看私塾窗口背下整本《千家诗》。

D18.en: As a child, he carved lychee pits into spinning toys to amuse other kids.
D18.zh: 小时候，他把荔枝核切成陀螺逗小孩玩。

D3.en: In Calcutta, he kept asking locals questions, then pestered a student on a crowded train.
D3.zh: 在加尔各答，他不停问当地人，又在火车上追问一个中学生。

D6.en: In a Chiba inn, he and a friend sawed a dried radish with a beer cap for a midnight snack.
D6.zh: 在千叶旅馆，他和朋友用啤酒瓶盖锯咸萝卜干下酒。

D2.en: At 9, he memorized the entire Thousand Poets by rote.
D2.zh: 9岁时，他强记了整本《千家诗》。

D5.en: In junior high, he guzzled several kilos of coconut wine and collapsed drunk.
D5.zh: 初中时，他狂饮几公斤椰酒，醉倒在地。

D7.en: As a teen, he often fought, got drunk, and stayed out all night with bad friends.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他常打架、喝醉，和坏朋友彻夜不归。

D13a.en: At 5, hearing footsteps, he ran out and yelled to his nanny, 'I didn't steal a drink!'
D13a.zh: 5岁时，听到脚步声他冲出去对奶妈喊：“我没有偷喝！”

D4.en: As a child, he carved a wooden axle with gear wheels and rubber bands to make a toy car.
D4.zh: 小时候，他用小刀把木轮刻成齿轮，绑上胶圈做玩具车。

D13b.en: His father, a scholar, could do nothing about his son's refusal to study.
D13b.zh: 他父亲是读书人，拿不肯做功课的他没办法。

D1.en: At 9, orphaned, he sold sugarcane and olives, and memorized the entire 'Thousand Poems' by eavesdropping on a school.
D1.zh: 9岁时父母双亡，他卖甘蔗橄榄，偷听私塾背下整本《千家诗》。

M1.en: At 12-15, he first tasted coconut wine, gulped several kilograms from a large iron can, felt surges in his stomach, and collapsed.
M1.zh: 12到15岁时，他第一次尝椰酒，从大铁罐里狂饮几公斤，觉得肚子一阵翻涌，四肢无力，晃荡倒地。

M2.en: At 9, orphaned, he was forced to sell sugarcane and olives.
M2.zh: 9岁时父母双亡，他被迫卖甘蔗橄榄。

M5.en: As a teenager, he was competitive and often picked fights.
M5.zh: 十几岁时，他好胜心强，到处找人打架。

D15.en: As a child, he played stone marbles with both hands, aiming to hit opponents' marbles or roll into a hole.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他双手玩石弹子，瞄准对方弹子击开，或滚入小洞。

M3.en: As a child, he had neighbors who planned to marry their daughter to him, so he got first dibs on treats.
M3.zh: 幼年时，邻居有意将女儿嫁他，他因此先享好吃的。

D12.en: At 18, he struggled financially and studied hard to earn top grades for a tuition waiver.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他经济困难，努力考取头三名以获得学费豁免。

D6.en: At 16, he saw Sun Yat-sen riding a horse through a crowded street.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他亲眼见到孙中山先生骑马穿过拥挤的街道。

D2.en: At 23, he taught multiple subjects and graded essays daily, including night classes.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他教多门课，天天出题改作文，还有夜课。

D1.en: At 13, he cried with his uncle and vowed to memorize letters by repeating them dozens of times.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他和叔父抱头痛哭，发誓要记住字母，反复默写数十次。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote an essay denouncing the filial piety story of Guo Ju as cruel and inhuman.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他写文章痛斥郭巨埋儿奉母的行为残忍无人心。

D18.en: As a teenager, he kept submitting manuscripts despite repeated failures.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他屡次投稿失败，但毫不灰心，继续尝试。

D3.en: From 17 to 24, he was fascinated by Liang Qichao's and Huang Yuansheng's writings.
D3.zh: 17到24岁间，他对梁启超的《新民丛报》和黄远庸的通讯着了迷。

D16.en: At 14, he wrote a sharp essay criticizing the filial piety story of Guo Ju.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他写文章犀利痛斥郭巨的荒谬行为。

D7.en: At 24-25, he refused to let a student stand up for an elder during his lesson.
D7.zh: 24到25岁时，他在教课时不许学生因长辈到来而中断回答。

D14.en: As a teenager, he felt encouraged when his article was finally published.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他见到自己的文章刊登出来，受到很大鼓舞。

D9.en: At 24-25, he volunteered for a library assistant job, writing to the principal and getting approved.
D9.zh: 24到25岁时，他毛遂自荐给校长写信，申请图书馆助理员工作，被核准了。

D13b.en: At 17, he was sent to Nanyang Public School's elementary school to become an engineer.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他被送进南洋公学附属小学读书，为了将来当工程师。

D13a.en: At 23, he felt depressed about being unable to continue school and reluctantly became a tutor for a few months.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他因不能继续上学而烦闷，无可奈何地去做几个月的老学究。

D12.en: He was born in the middle of the night on June 7, 1952, in a small private hospital in Moda.
D12.zh: 他于1952年6月7日半夜出生在莫达的一家小私立医院。

D11.en: He wrote parodies, pastiches, and simple rhyming couplets about what was going on in class.
D11.zh: 他写关于课堂情况的模仿作品、拼凑诗和简单的押韵对句。

D14.en: He mentioned in passing that Adnan Menderes, the prime minister, was his uncle.
D14.zh: 他随口提到总理阿德南·曼德列斯是他叔叔。

D19.en: He arranged miniature cars into an obsessively neat line and admitted them one by one into his garage.
D19.zh: 他把微型车排成一条强迫症般整齐的线，然后一辆一辆地放进他的车库。

D10.en: He would sometimes panic and rise abruptly from the table, trying to get his mother to face facts.
D10.zh: 他有时会惊慌失措，突然从桌边站起来，试图让他母亲面对现实。

D15.en: His father took him to soccer matches from an early age.
D15.zh: 他从小被父亲带去观看足球比赛。

D6.en: He spent long evenings with friends in meyhanes, drinking and conversing.
D6.zh: 他和朋友们在小酒馆里度过漫长的夜晚，喝酒聊天。

D2.en: He took a thick book (The Possessed, War and Peace, Buddenbrooks) and read it during class.
D2.zh: 他带一本厚书（《群魔》《战争与和平》《布登勃洛克一家》）在课堂上读。

D5.en: He bolted out of the building as if for his life, heedless of stepping on cracks between pavement slabs.
D5.zh: 他像逃命一样冲出大楼，不顾是否踩到人行道石板之间的缝隙。

M6.en: At 20, he imagined himself the only one who had unlocked the city's secret.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他想象自己是唯一解开城市秘密的人。

D20.en: At 5, he copied lines from a Brockhaus book.
D20.zh: 5岁时，他抄写布罗克豪斯书中的句子。

D4.en: At 20-25, he combed through Ottoman archives for handwritten chronicles.
D4.zh: 20到25岁间，他翻查奥斯曼档案中的手写编年史。

D1.en: At 6-7, his hand shot up to answer even when he didn't know.
D1.zh: 6到7岁时，即使不知道答案，他的手也会不由自主地举起。

D9.en: At 20-25, he became an assistant to his beloved teacher Ahmet Refik.
D9.zh: 20到25岁间，他成为敬爱的老师艾哈迈德·雷菲克的助手。

M3.en: At 20-25, he became an assistant to his beloved teacher Ahmet Refik.
M3.zh: 20到25岁间，他成为敬爱的老师艾哈迈德·雷菲克的助手。

D7.en: At 20, he rashly declared he would never set foot in the architecture faculty again.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他鲁莽地说再也不踏进建筑系。

D13a.en: He ended their Wednesday meetings at once.
D13a.zh: 他立刻结束了他们的周三聚会。

M1.en: At 16-17, he was someone who tried to do a painting like Utrillo.
M1.zh: 16到17岁时，他是尝试像郁特里罗那样画画的人。

D13b.en: At 13-16, he spent four years at Robert Academy.
D13b.zh: 13到16岁间，他在罗伯特学院待了四年。

D16.en: As a teenager, he constantly joked, told stories, and imitated teachers to make classmates laugh.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，他不停开玩笑、讲故事、模仿老师逗同学笑。

M8.en: At 18-20, he wanted to paint another Reclining Woman of you.
M8.zh: 18到20岁间，他想再画一幅你的《斜躺的女人》。

D12.en: At 21, he chose a school with free tuition and a monthly allowance to support his aging father.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他为了减轻老父负担，选择了免学费、伙食费且每月有3元零用钱的杭州蚕学馆。

D2.en: At 21, he chose a free school with a monthly stipend to support his aging father.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他为了减轻老父负担，选择了免学费、伙食费且每月有3元零用钱的杭州蚕学馆。

D3.en: At 20-21, he and classmates spent a year studying Western social and natural sciences, seeing their value.
D3.zh: 20到21岁时，他和学友用一年时间研讨西方社会科学和自然科学，深感西学广博有用。

D9.en: At 22, he decided to start a practical school in Sijing to benefit the people.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他决定为泗泾试办一所学以致用的小学堂，做有益于国计民生的事。

D13a.en: At 19, he abandoned the imperial exams after suffering humiliation from a false registration incident.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他因冒籍受辱，人格受打击，毅然放弃举子业。

M1.en: From age 7 to 19, he showed great intelligence and excelled in his studies.
M1.zh: 7到19岁间，他天资聪颖，学业大进。

D4.en: He served as the first principal and founder of Sijing Miye Private Yangzheng Primary School.
D4.zh: 他担任泗泾米业私立养正小学的第一任校长和创始人。

D16.en: At 23, he delivered a speech on education for national salvation at the school's opening ceremony.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他在开学典礼上发表了教育救国的演讲。

D5.en: At 23, he resolutely gave up his plans to develop in Sijing town.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他决然放弃在泗泾小镇图谋发展的打算。

D1.en: At 23, he lobbied and gained support from the rice guild to quickly open a school.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他四处奔走游说，得到米业公会支持，因陋就简地把学堂开了出来。

M4.en: At 23, he gave a long-held speech on saving the nation through education.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他发表了久蓄心头的教育救国演讲。

D7.en: At 13, he complained about the same old dishes, refusing to eat winter melon soup again.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他抱怨又是老三样，不肯再吃冬瓜汤。

D10.en: At 13, he shouted about his family's egg drop soup being mocked as their signature dish.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他抬高嗓子说家里的蛋花汤被人笑话是名菜。

D12.en: At 25, Lei Zhen taught in person and invited Liu Chuqing and Zhu De'an to assist.
D12.zh: 25岁时，雷震亲自授课并请刘楚青、朱得安协助教学。

D3.en: At 23-25, Lei Zhen read a book by a 'political monk' named Otani Kōzui titled 'On China'.
D3.zh: 23到25岁间，雷震读到政治和尚大谷光瑞写的《对支那议》

D1.en: At 23-24, Lei Zhen scored 50 in math, then 70 by memorizing formulas.
D1.zh: 23到24岁间，雷震数学考了50分，后靠背公式得70分。

D13a.en: At age 12, his mother beat him for playing truant and sent him back to school that night.
D13a.zh: 12岁时逃学被母亲痛打一顿，连夜送回私塾。

D9.en: At age 25, Lei Zhen taught classes and invited Liu Chuqing and Zhu De'an to assist due to shortage of staff.
D9.zh: 25岁时，雷震亲自授课，并请刘楚青、朱得安协助教学。

D5.en: At 18, he was almost arrested by military police while under surveillance.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他因被军警密探监视而几乎被捕坐牢。

D12.en: As a child, he wore adult clothes altered to fit because his family was poor.
D12.zh: 他小时候因为家里穷，穿的都是大人改小的衣服。

D2.en: At 22-26, he buried himself in books, studying society, history, philosophy, and more.
D2.zh: 22到26岁间，他把自己埋进书本，潜心阅读社会、历史、哲学等大量书籍。

D5.en: At 18, he was stranded in Shantou, begging in tattered uniform, and fell ill with typhoid.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他在汕头沦为散兵，穿着破军衣乞讨，患上严重的伤寒病。

D1.en: At 13-14, he secretly studied by oil lamp under his blanket after lights-out.
D1.zh: 13到14岁时，他熄灯后在被窝里点油灯偷偷学习。

M1.en: At 18, he sought to understand right and wrong and find a way out for China through reading.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他想通过读书弄清谁是谁非，寻求中国革命和个人出路。

D13b.en: As a child, his mother worked extra to pay for his schooling since the family didn't fund it.
D13b.zh: 他小时候，大家庭不供读书，母亲靠做针线活和卖挂面挣钱供他上学。

D9.en: At 24, he invited classmates in Nanjing to co-found the China Youth Western Expedition Group.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他邀约南京的同乡同学，共同发起组织中国青年西部考察团。

D7.en: At 24, he stood up in class to question a professor about good and evil in resisting Japan.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他站起来向教授提问：主张抗战是善是恶，是对是错。

D6.en: At 19, he ran into an old classmate from his middle school on a Nanjing street.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他在南京街头惊喜地遇到内江中学的旧日同学。

D20.en: He co-founded the 'China Youth Western Expedition Team'.
D20.zh: 他共同发起组织了'中国青年西部考察团'。

D3.en: At 25-26, he wrote four news dispatches introducing Peking University's academic activities for Beiping Morning News.
D3.zh: 25到26岁时，他为《北平晨报》写了四篇介绍北大学术的新闻通讯。

D14.en: He volunteered on stage to share his war experiences and promote anti-Japanese resistance.
D14.zh: 他常常自告奋勇上台，用自己的见闻进行抗日鼓动宣传。

D4.en: He traveled alone over ten thousand li through northwestern China as a special correspondent.
D4.zh: 他以《大公报》特约通讯员名义只身赴西北考察，行程万余里。

M7.en: He voluntarily and diligently studied and pondered the issue of anti-Japanese resistance.
M7.zh: 他自觉自愿、勤奋刻苦地研究思索抗日的问题。

D14.en: At 18, she had five poems published anonymously in the Atlantic Monthly.
D14.zh: 18岁时，她匿名在《大西洋月刊》上发表了五首诗。

D16.en: At 17, she stood paralyzed with fear.
D16.zh: 17岁时，她吓得呆立不动。

D2.en: From age 17 to 37, she worked alone on a long literary apprenticeship without mentors.
D2.zh: 17到37岁间，她独自进行漫长的文学学徒训练，没有导师。

D3.en: At 6-8, her parents discovered she was reading a play about a prostitute.
D3.zh: 6到8岁时，父母发现她在读一部关于妓女的剧本。

D6.en: At 24-35, she made new and important close friendships during gregarious years.
D6.zh: 24到35岁间，她在社交活跃的岁月里结交了重要亲密朋友。

D11.en: At 12, she wrote her earliest known letter, a breathless account of life at Pencraig.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她写了最早的一封信，兴奋地描述在彭克雷格的生活。

D7.en: At 20, she told a friend she had been forced to break her engagement.
D7.zh: 20岁时，她告诉朋友自己被迫解除了婚约。

D5.en: At 6-8, she amused herself by stealing a German fiancé of a Livingston daughter.
D5.zh: 6到8岁时，她偷走了利文斯顿家女儿的德国未婚夫来取乐。

D10.en: At 10, she delighted in the family home with its honeysuckled veranda.
D10.zh: 10岁时，她喜爱带有金银花阳台的宽敞家宅。

D12.en: She was an orphaned single woman with no inheritance.
D12.zh: 她是个孤儿，单身，没有遗产。

D13a.en: She was forced to break her engagement in 1883.
D13a.zh: 1883年，她被迫解除婚约。

D4.en: She devoted 20 years to studying Italian art after seeing a simple armchair.
D4.zh: 看到一把简朴的扶手椅后，她花了20年研究意大利艺术。

M1.en: She published Verses as a teenager.
M1.zh: 她十几岁时出版了《诗集》。

D15.en: She enjoyed outdoor pastimes like walking, riding, and archery.
D15.zh: 她喜欢户外活动，如散步、骑马和射箭。

D2.en: At age 4-8, he practiced calligraphy by writing on bricks with a homemade brush.
D2.zh: 4到8岁时，他用麻草扎成的扫帚笔蘸清水在方砖上练字。

D3.en: At age 23-24, he published two articles on China's feudal stagnation under a pseudonym.
D3.zh: 23到24岁时，他以笔名邓云特连续发表两篇关于中国封建社会停滞问题的论文。

D11.en: At age 18, he wrote a poem titled 'Book City' reflecting his life in Shanghai.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他写了题为《书城》的诗，记录他在上海的生活。

D15.en: At age 13-16, he learned Shaolin boxing from a middle school PE teacher.
D15.zh: 13到16岁时，他向一位中学体育教师学习少林拳术。

D16.en: At age 15-16, he won second place in a school speech contest on current events.
D16.zh: 15到16岁时，他在全校时事讲演比赛中获得第二名。

D4.en: At age 18, he helped run a night school for rickshaw pullers in Shanghai.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他和同学在打浦桥一带办工人夜校，帮助黄包车工人识字读书。

D5.en: At age 20, he was arrested at a protest and sentenced to 15 years for sedition.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他在参加反帝大会时被捕，以危害民国罪被判十五年徒刑。

M4.en: At age 23-24, he published two articles on China's feudal stagnation under a pseudonym.
M4.zh: 23到24岁时，他以笔名邓云特连续发表两篇关于中国封建社会停滞问题的论文。

M7.en: At age 20, he organized anti-Japanese volunteer and rescue teams with comrades.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他和同志们一起发动群众组织抗日义勇队和救护队。

D6.en: He was elected captain of the Kaifeng 'Minxian' team at a joint meeting.
D6.zh: 他在联席会上被推举为开封民先总队长。

D18.en: He sold roasted sweet potatoes and fried dough cakes as a street vendor.
D18.zh: 他摆摊卖烤白薯和炸油饼。

D14.en: At 16, he won second place in a school current affairs speech contest.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在全校时事演讲比赛中获得第二名。

D9.en: He ran a full-scale ad campaign to make the journal self-sufficient.
D9.zh: 他发起全面广告营销活动，使期刊自给自足。

D1.en: He was elected to D.K.E. in spring of his freshman year.
D1.zh: 他大一春天被选入D.K.E.兄弟会。

D3.en: As a child, he learned Chinese from the houseboy and could count to 100.
D3.zh: 他幼年时跟佣人学中文，能数到100。

D4.en: At 21, he engaged in a full-scale advertising and marketing campaign to make the journal self-sufficient.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他开展了一场全面的广告和营销活动，以使期刊自给自足。

D18.en: He said he could run a newspaper successfully if given the chance.
D18.zh: 他说如果有机会，他能成功经营报纸。

D13b.en: He was placed on academic probation in November of his sophomore year.
D13b.zh: 他大二那年11月被留校察看。

D2.en: He studied three hours daily and visited galleries and palaces.
D2.zh: 他每天学习三小时，还去画廊和宫殿参观。

D14.en: He put sporting news on Page One, a disturbing practice.
D14.zh: 他把体育新闻放在头版，引起争议。

D7.en: He created panic at his mother's reception by freeing a mechanical mouse.
D7.zh: 他在母亲的招待会上放出一只机械老鼠，引起恐慌。

D5.en: At age 23, he believed much profit would come from depriving Mexicans of their lands.
D5.zh: 他23岁时认为很多利润来自剥夺墨西哥人的土地。

D17.en: He wanted to give away all his money and clothes to help the poor.
D17.zh: 他想把自己所有的钱和衣服都捐出去帮助穷人。

D19.en: He began collecting German picture books, stamps, coins, beer steins, and porcelain.
D19.zh: 他开始收集德国图画书、邮票、硬币、啤酒杯和瓷器。

D11.en: He wrote carefully crafted letters filled with irony and descriptions of landscape and weather.
D11.zh: 他精心撰写信件，充满讽刺，并详细描述新英格兰的风景和天气。

D13a.en: He pleaded with his mother to let him walk home like the other boys.
D13a.zh: 他恳求母亲允许他像其他男孩一样走路回家。

D20.en: He was elected business manager of the Harvard Lampoon.
D20.zh: 他被选为哈佛讽刺杂志的业务经理。

D10.en: He felt anxious about how badly someone was looking and staying out all night.
D10.zh: 他担心某人脸色很差，而且整夜不归。

M1.en: He was convinced he could run a newspaper successfully.
M1.zh: 他确信自己能成功经营一家报纸。

M4.en: He joined a long campaign against the Southern Pacific Railroad.
M4.zh: 他参与了一场反对南太平洋铁路公司的长期运动。

D8.en: At 24, he was polite but determined to have his way.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他彬彬有礼但坚持己见。

D16.en: At 24, he wrote courteous suggestions that were actually orders.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他用最礼貌的措辞写下建议，但实际上是命令。

D15.en: As a small child, he tried to turn summersaults and climb poles.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他试着翻跟头和爬杆子。

D12.en: At 13, he was assigned the worst job of buying bread by the vengeful boss.
D12.zh: 13岁时，报复心强的老板娘把最不愿做的进面包活派给他干。

D4.en: At 17, he finished translating a 400,000-word logic book and said, 'Finally done!'
D4.zh: 17岁时，他译完40万字的《逻辑基本》，自语道：“总算完成了！”

D7.en: At 23, he debated leftist students on the street, accusing them of spreading rumors.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他在街上与左派学生辩论，指责他们“听信谣言”。

D1.en: At 14, he took his monthly salary and fled the food shop at night.
D1.zh: 14岁时，他领了当月薪水，天黑后提着包袱逃离了食品店。

D13a.en: At 19-20, he dropped out of NTU law and soon re-entered history department.
D13a.zh: 19到20岁间，他自动休学台大法律系，不久再考入台大历史系。

D3.en: As a teenager, he devoured articles on Russell and Western philosophy in a newspaper.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他废寝忘食地读《大公报》上介绍罗素和西方哲学的文章。

D2.en: At 15, he pleaded with his father to let him continue studying, citing his hard work.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他反复对父亲说：“我如果再不读书，有什么前途？”

M1.en: At 19, he entered Southwest Associated University philosophy department with excellent grades.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他以优异成绩考入西南联大哲学系。

D6.en: As a teenager, he often chatted with a friend who visited him after school.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，杜东生放学路过他家，必进去和他大谈。

D18.en: At 20, he wrote for Kunming newspapers and tutored a bank clerk in English.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他给昆明报刊写文章，并给银行职员补习英文。

D9.en: At 25-26, as a graduate student, he became the representative of right-wing soldiers at Lido barracks.
D9.zh: 25到26岁时，他作为研究生，成了丽多军营右派士兵的代表人物。

D16.en: At 22-23, he often debated a pro-Communist editor at the Independent Press.
D16.zh: 22到23岁时，他常与独立出版社一位倾向共产党的编辑论战。

D5.en: At 25, he enlisted and was sent to the Nationalist Youth Army artillery unit in Kunming on December 8, 1944.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他报名参军，1944年12月8日被送到昆明国民党青年军炮兵营。

M4.en: At 24-25, anxious about Nationalist defeats, he resolved to help through propaganda.
M4.zh: 24到25岁时，他见国民党军队败退，心急如焚，决定在宣传上出力。

D13b.en: At 23, with Zhang Daofan and Yuan Shouqian as sponsors, he became a Kuomintang member.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，由张道藩和袁守谦介绍，他加入了国民党。

D11.en: As a teenager, he published articles while still in middle school.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他读中学时就发表文章。

D7.en: At 20-22, Ni Kuang defended him for driving away wolves and saving the level, which was damaged but repairable.
D7.zh: 20到22岁间，倪匡为他辩护，说他赶走狼救回水准仪，虽坏可修。

D5.en: At 20-22, Ni Kuang defended him for driving away wolves and saving a level, which was later repairable.
D5.zh: 20到22岁间，倪匡为他辩护，说他赶狼救水准仪没错。

D2.en: He wrote up to 4,500 characters per hour and 20,000 per day for 12 newspapers, never missing a deadline.
D2.zh: 他写作最高纪录一小时4500字，一天写2万字，同时为12家报纸写长篇连载，从不脱稿。

D9.en: At 24-34, he wrote up to 4500 characters per hour, 20000 per day for 12 newspapers, never missing a deadline.
D9.zh: 24到34岁间，他每小时写4500字，日写2万字，同时为12家报纸写连载，从不脱稿。

D6.en: He met with a group of Shanghai friends, including Zhang Che, for dinner and chat.
D6.zh: 他和一班上海人，包括张彻，约在一起吃饭聊天。

D14.en: He played a drunken brothel customer as his first film role.
D14.zh: 他演的第一个角色是喝醉酒的嫖客。

D18.en: At 16, he saw a recruitment ad for East China People's Revolutionary University and decided to leave home.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他看到华东人民革命大学的招生广告，决定去外埠闯荡。

D20.en: He commissioned Gu Long to write for a magazine and recommended adapting his novels into films.
D20.zh: 他代杂志约古龙写稿，并推荐将古龙小说改编成电影。

M1.en: He wrote up to 4,500 characters per hour and 20,000 per day for 12 newspapers, never missing a deadline.
M1.zh: 他写作最高纪录一小时4500字，一天写2万字，同时为12家报纸写长篇连载，从不脱稿。

M2.en: At 22, he stayed at his great-uncle's in Shanghai, then sought to stow away to Hong Kong to join his parents.
M2.zh: 22岁时，他暂住上海舅公家，然后寻找机会偷渡香港投奔父母。

M7.en: He always believed he was right and that the Party would understand him one day.
M7.zh: 他始终认为自己没错，总有一天党会理解自己。

D1.en: He wrote up to 4500 characters per hour, 20000 per day for 12 newspapers, never missing a deadline despite hangovers.
D1.zh: 他写作最高纪录一小时4500字，一天写2万字，同时为12家报纸写长篇连载，从不脱稿，一夜花天酒地后头痛也支撑着写。

D12.en: At 20, he was transferred to Inner Mongolia as a public security cadre managing a labor camp.
D12.zh: 他20岁来内蒙古，从江苏省公安厅调来保安沼当公安干部。

D4.en: He dismantled a wooden bridge with tools and burned it for three to four days.
D4.zh: 他带了工具，把木桥拆了烧了三四天。

D15.en: At 20, he rode a horse for the first time and was reluctant to dismount.
D15.zh: 他20岁时第一次骑马，得意非凡，不愿下马。

D16.en: He stood up and argued that the criticism of the man was unreasonable, as he had saved the level from wolves.
D16.zh: 他站起来，一板一眼地说，你们批评他根本没道理，他赶走狼救了水准仪。

D11.en: At 11-12, reading Water Margin, he visualized a fat naked monk jumping out of bed.
D11.zh: 他十一二岁时读《水浒传》，脑中浮现一个又大又胖的和尚。

D3.en: As a child, he turned text into vivid mental movies while reading.
D3.zh: 他从小喜欢想像，看小说时脑中自动制作电影画面。

D10.en: He was stunned and asked, 'What do we do?'
D10.zh: 他惊呆说：'那怎么办？'

D17.en: He regularly untangled knots and groomed the animal's fur.
D17.zh: 他不时将牠身上的结解开，梳整理顺。

D19.en: He caught five especially fat bedbugs that had just fed and put them in a small bottle.
D19.zh: 他捉了五隻特別肥大、剛吸飽血的臭蟲，放進小瓶子裡。

M4.en: He often argued with his leaders, who then framed him.
M4.zh: 他經常與領導爭辯，領導就羅織他的罪名。

M5.en: He always believed he was right and that the Party would understand him someday.
M5.zh: 他始終認為自己沒錯，總有一天黨會理解他。

M6.en: He always believed he was right and that the Party would understand him someday.
M6.zh: 他始終認為自己沒錯，總有一天黨會理解他。

D12.en: At 8, he became a porter at the Guangzhou train station.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他在广州火车站当搬运工。

D18.en: He hustled from early childhood in China.
D18.zh: 他从小就在中国奔波谋生。

D5.en: At 12.5, he got out of line, hid a coin, and entered Macau without incident.
D5.zh: 12岁半时，他走出队伍藏起硬币，顺利进入澳门。

D10.en: At 7, he felt forgotten by the whole world, like people buried in a cemetery.
D10.zh: 7岁时，他感觉被全世界遗忘，像埋在墓地里的人。

D11.en: At 11-12, he wrote a poem pouring out his yearning to see his father.
D11.zh: 11到12岁时，他写了一首诗，倾诉对父亲的思念。

D13a.en: At 12, it took him a year to convince her.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，他花了一年时间说服她。

M4.en: He was drawn to older men as mentors, pivotal to his development.
M4.zh: 他被年长男性吸引为导师，这对他的发展至关重要。

D1.en: Before age 21, he rose to manage production at a sweater factory employing 300 people.
D1.zh: 21岁前，他升任一家300人毛衣厂的生产经理。

D9.en: Before age 21, he managed production at a sweater factory with 300 employees.
D9.zh: 21岁前，他管理一家300人毛衣厂的生产。

D20.en: Before age 21, he managed production at a sweater factory employing 300 people.
D20.zh: 21岁前，他管理一家300人毛衣厂的生产。

D12.en: As the eldest, he helped maintain discipline among his siblings.
D12.zh: 作为长子，他帮助维持兄弟姐妹间的纪律。

D4.en: At 8, he was given a bag of used type, perhaps sparking his career.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他得到一袋旧铅字，可能开启了他的职业生涯。

D9.en: At 23, he used the sum to publish the first edition of Answers to Correspondents.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他用这笔钱出版了《答读者问》第一期。

D15.en: As a teenager, he used cycling to build strength but often overexerted and was bedridden.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他骑车锻炼体力，但常因过度劳累而卧床。

D6.en: At 23, he gave Mary Milner an engagement ring bought with borrowed money.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他用借来的钱买了订婚戒指送给玛丽·米尔纳。

D5.en: As a teenager, he overexerted himself in cold rain and was bedridden.
D5.zh: 十几岁时，他在冷雨中过度劳累，卧床不起。

D11.en: At 20, he composed 'The Ellen Terry Waltz', which was somewhat successful.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他创作了《艾伦·特里华尔兹》，获得了一些成功。

D3.en: At 20, his curious nature made him recognize opportunity in Tit-Bits' format.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他好奇的天性让他看到了《趣闻》格式中的机会。

D7.en: At 16, he had made up his mind despite attempts to dissuade him.
D7.zh: 16岁时，尽管有人劝阻，他还是下定了决心。

D2.en: At 18-19, through perseverance he got work accepted by Weekly Budget and others.
D2.zh: 18到19岁间，他靠毅力让作品被《每周预算》等刊物接受。

D8.en: He was nicknamed 'the Yellow Headed Worm' by contributors whose work he often edited heavily.
D8.zh: 他因经常大幅修改投稿者的作品而被戏称为‘黄头虫’。

M1.en: At 22, he aimed to match Newnes's commercial success.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他试图效仿纽恩斯的商业成功。

D9.en: He and Song Jian set principles for collaboration: he would draft interview outlines while Song contacted sources.
D9.zh: 他和宋健确定了联手原则，他琢磨采访提纲，宋健联系采访单位。

D3.en: He felt dizzy but memorized the PR manager's stream of numbers and new terms about the hotel.
D3.zh: 他有些晕场，但抓紧记住公关部经理冒出的一串串数字和新鲜词汇。

D11.en: He spent half the night coming up with an exciting title for his article: 'Second Great Wall'.
D11.zh: 他用了半宿时间，给文章定下令人振奋的题目：第二长城。

D2.en: He submitted his work to 'Sprouts' and then 'Ugly Duckling', both rejected.
D2.zh: 他把作品投给《萌芽》和《丑小鸭》，都被退稿了。

D10.en: His first girlfriend left him, saying she couldn't stand the cold remarks.
D10.zh: 第一个女朋友离他而去，说听不了那些冷言冷语。

D13a.en: At 18, he grew his hair long in college, feeling free inside, and his hair grew fast.
D13a.zh: 18岁时他一上大学就留长发，内心觉得自由时头发长得飞快。

D6.en: At 25, he and friends paid for weekend hiking and bowling competitions.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他和朋友们出钱参加周末爬山和保龄球比赛。

D14.en: From elementary to high school, he was always the best student in class, first a 'Five Good' then 'Three Good' student.
D14.zh: 他从小学到高中都是班里最好的学生，先是五好学生，后改成三好学生。

D16.en: At 25, he said he enjoys exchanging ideas and wants to answer questions for academic exchange.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他说他喜欢和大家交流，想回答大家的问题做学术交流。

D2.en: At 14-15, he worked on literature and music, seeking perfection.
D2.zh: 14到15岁间，他继续在文学和音乐上努力，追求完美。

D7.en: At 6-7, he rebelled against having a governess.
D7.zh: 6到7岁时，他反抗请家庭女教师。

D9.en: He worked diligently with Hadden in 1922 to attract investors.
D9.zh: 1922年，他与哈登一起努力吸引投资者。

D13b.en: As a small child, he knelt in prayer before bed, a lifelong habit.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他睡前跪着祈祷，成了终身习惯。

M6.en: At 15-18, his greatest ambition was to be useful in the foreign field.
M6.zh: 15到18岁间，他最大的抱负是在海外发挥作用。

D14.en: At 3-4, he declaimed before an imagined crowd of parishioners.
D14.zh: 3到4岁时，他对着想象中的会众演讲。

D16.en: At 17-18, he was named class poet and read an ode at the ceremony.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他被选为班级诗人，在典礼上朗诵短诗。

M4.en: At 20, he embraced the military ethic with uncritical enthusiasm and strove to adapt himself to its demands.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他毫无批判地拥抱军事伦理，并努力适应其要求。

D5.en: At 14, he boarded a ship in Shanghai to begin a long journey back to America, alone.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他在上海登船，独自开始返回美国的漫长旅程。

D20.en: At 16, he spent much of his time scrambling for advancement in one organization after another.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他花大量时间在一个又一个组织中争抢晋升。

D3.en: At 17, he said: 'I learned a good deal of the mechanical part of publishing, and had a most interesting afternoon.'
D3.zh: 17岁时，他说：'我学到了很多出版机械方面的知识，度过了一个非常有趣的下午。'

D15.en: At 14, he went out briefly for the football team and later considered trying out for baseball.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他短暂加入足球队，后来考虑尝试棒球。

D4.en: At 14-15, he bought a bicycle, taught himself to ride it, and cycled through the countryside around St. Albans.
D4.zh: 14到15岁时，他买了一辆自行车，自学骑行，在圣奥尔本斯周围的乡村骑行。

D13a.en: At 6-7, he rebelled against having a governess, so his mother took over much of his instruction again.
D13a.zh: 6到7岁时，他反抗家庭女教师，于是母亲重新接管了他的大部分教育。

D10.en: At 12, he felt life was a hanging torture and sympathized with suicidal prisoners.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他觉得生活像悬吊的折磨，同情想自杀的囚犯。

D19.en: As a teenager, he kept meticulous notes on what was accepted: mostly poems, some essays, few stories.
D19.zh: 十几岁时，他详细记录被接受的作品：主要是诗歌，一些散文，少量故事。

M2.en: At 15-18, he believed missionary life was the most honorable calling in the world.
M2.zh: 15到18岁时，他认为传教士生活是世上最光荣的使命。

M7.en: At 14, he learned from his father to think of life as a great mission judged by contribution to the world.
M7.zh: 14岁时，他从父亲那里学到把人生看作一项伟大使命，以对世界的贡献来评判。

D20X.en: At 17-18, he lost the coveted class orator position to Brit.
D20X.zh: 17到18岁时，他渴望的班级演说家职位被布里特夺走。

M8.en: At 15-18, he believed missionary life was the most honorable calling in the world.
M8.zh: 15到18岁时，他认为传教士生活是世上最光荣的使命。

D6.en: He wrote a nine-page description in magenta ink to the Rivetts.
D6.zh: 他用品红墨水给里维特一家写了九页的描述。

D18.en: He pounced on a 1,000-franc chip an old lady dropped.
D18.zh: 他扑向一位老太太掉落的1000法郎筹码。

D5.en: He dropped out in 1994 to start Rawkus Records with friends.
D5.zh: 他1994年辍学，和朋友创办了Rawkus唱片公司。

D10.en: He often fled home in tears as a child.
D10.zh: 他小时候常哭着跑出家门。

D9.en: He dropped out in 1994 to start Rawkus Records with friends.
D9.zh: 他1994年辍学，和朋友创办了Rawkus唱片公司。

D20.en: He decided to stand as secretary of the University Labour Club.
D20.zh: 他决定竞选大学劳工俱乐部的秘书。

D4.en: He found the smell of ink, press noise, and atmosphere irresistible.
D4.zh: 他觉得油墨味、印刷机噪音和紧张气氛难以抗拒。

D14.en: He had a bust of Lenin on his mantelpiece.
D14.zh: 他的壁炉台上放着一尊列宁半身像。

D13b.en: He goes to study unhappily at the London School of Economics.
D13b.zh: 他不情愿地去伦敦经济学院学习。

D11.en: At Harvard he began a politically incorrect cartoon strip called Albrecht the Hun.
D11.zh: 在哈佛，他开始创作一个政治不正确的连环漫画《匈奴人阿尔布雷希特》。

D1.en: He fought the Herald and Weekly Times to a draw, merging their paper with his.
D1.zh: 他与《先驱与每周时报》打成平手，将他们的报纸与他的合并。

M2.en: He found the only way to get Sir Keith's attention was to annoy him.
M2.zh: 他发现引起基思爵士注意的唯一办法是惹恼他。

D16.en: He personally entirely deprecated this thoroughly new theory that the monarchy is above criticism.
D16.zh: 他个人完全反对君主制不受批评这一全新理论。

D3.en: He was picking up information, asking questions and absorbing new impressions with enthusiasm.
D3.zh: 他积极获取信息、提问并热情吸收新印象。

M1.en: He was terrified because he had done hardly any work.
M1.zh: 他吓坏了，因为几乎没做任何功课。

M7.en: At 22, he publicly deprecated the theory that the monarchy is above criticism.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他公开反对君主制不受批评的理论。

D20X.en: At 21, he was banned from standing for office again.
D20X.zh: 21岁时，他被禁止再次参选公职。

D12.en: At 22, his father's powers began to fail as his fortunes rose.
D12.zh: 22岁时，他父亲的精力开始衰退，而他的运势上升。

D13b.en: At 16, he left school and started work in his cousin's merchant bank.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他离开学校，开始在他表哥的商业银行工作。

D3.en: He built up a large library and read in his study until two or three in the morning.
D3.zh: 他建了一个大图书馆，在书房读书到凌晨两三点。

D2.en: At 16, he moved on to the University of Edinburgh.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他进入爱丁堡大学。

D18.en: At 21-22, he applied to a Montreal stockbroker for bonds to sell on commission.
D18.zh: 21到22岁间，他向蒙特利尔一家股票经纪公司申请债券代销。

D11.en: At 16, he turned in an essay on Warren Hastings that astonished his teachers.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他交了一篇关于沃伦·黑斯廷斯的文章，让老师大吃一惊。

D16.en: At 14, he turned in a brilliant essay discussing Macaulay's indictment of Warren Hastings.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他交了一篇精彩的文章，讨论麦考利对沃伦·黑斯廷斯的指控。

M1.en: At 14, he showed marked ability for mathematics and turned in a brilliant essay on Macaulay's indictment of Warren Hastings.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他表现出数学天赋，并交了一篇精彩文章讨论麦考利对沃伦·黑斯廷斯的指控。

D5.en: At 19-20, he lost money investing in frozen meat caught in a thaw.
D5.zh: 19到20岁时，他投资的一批冷冻肉因解冻而亏损。

D1.en: At 17-18, he felt misery and despair but persisted in becoming a lawyer.
D1.zh: 17到18岁时，他感到痛苦和绝望，但坚持要当律师。

D8.en: At 25, he negotiated better terms.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他谈判得到了更好的条件。

D4.en: At 10, he had his first venture into journalism.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他初次涉足新闻业。

D15.en: At 11, he attended Chatham.
D15.zh: 11岁时，他去了查塔姆。

D10.en: At 17, he snatched a large Chinese vase and smashed it on the floor.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他抓起一个大花瓶摔在地上。

D6.en: At 24, he took parties to the theatre, went to the regatta, opera, and occasionally church.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他带人看戏，去赛船会、歌剧院，偶尔去教堂。

D19.en: At 24, he acquired a secretary and began to keep recognized accounts.
D19.zh: 24岁时，他雇了秘书，开始记正规账目。

D14.en: As a child, he pumped the organ because he wanted the twenty-five cents.
D14.zh: 小时候，他踩风琴因为想要25美分。

D12.en: He went West on his own before age 16 and worked as a ranch hand.
D12.zh: 他16岁前独自去西部，当了牧场工人。

D7.en: At age 4-5, he froze and cried, then was whipped by Marie for stubbornness.
D7.zh: 4到5岁时，他僵住并哭了起来，玛丽因此用鞭子打他，认为他固执。

D10.en: At age 4-5, he froze and cried, then Marie whipped him for stubbornness.
D10.zh: 四五岁时，他吓呆了哭起来，玛丽因此用鞭子抽他。

D3.en: He returned to Europe and studied art.
D3.zh: 他回到欧洲学习艺术。

D4.en: He covered his bedroom walls with sketches of race cars at age 13.
D4.zh: 他13岁时在卧室墙上画满了赛车的草图。

D11.en: He covered his bedroom walls with sketches of race cars at age 13.
D11.zh: 他13岁时在卧室墙上画满了赛车的草图。

D15.en: He got caught in a trap and his plane had 268 bullet holes; armor plate saved him.
D15.zh: 他中埋伏，飞机上有268个弹孔，装甲板救了他一命。

D13b.en: He enlisted in the Naval Reserve in October 1918 at age 18.
D13b.zh: 他1918年10月18岁时加入海军预备役。

D9.en: He was appointed editor-in-chief of the summer Student in 1921 at age 21.
D9.zh: 他1921年21岁时被任命为夏季《学生》主编。

D5.en: He took off for a Caribbean fling, working as a seaman to Puerto Rico and Panama at age 23.
D5.zh: 他23岁时去加勒比海冒险，当海员到了波多黎各和巴拿马。

D6.en: At 21, he dated regularly in college but fell hard only for Harriet.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他在大学里经常约会，但只对哈丽特动了真情。

D13a.en: At age 17-18, his parents insisted he finish high school, disappointing him.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁时，父母坚持要他读完高中，这让他很失望。

D19.en: He had a very orderly mind.
D19.zh: 他思维非常有条理。

D18.en: At age 20-22, he worked for the Hughes Tool Company.
D18.zh: 20到22岁时，他在休斯工具公司工作。

D2.en: In his early Army days at age 18-21, he damaged his eyes by drawing too long at night.
D2.zh: 18到21岁刚参军时，他因晚上长时间画画用眼过度而损伤了眼睛。

M1.en: At 22 in 1943, he was the leading American ace in North Africa.
M1.zh: 1943年22岁时，他是北非战绩最好的美国王牌飞行员。

D1.en: At age 18-19, he did 64 days of KP duty in his first 4 months in the Army.
D1.zh: 18到19岁时，他在参军头4个月里做了64天厨房勤务。

D14.en: At 15-16, he performed as a clown in a small traveling circus.
D14.zh: 15到16岁时，他在一个小型巡回马戏团里当小丑。

D18.en: At 19, he sold his first story to Short Stories magazine for $15.
D18.zh: 19岁时，他把第一篇故事卖给《短篇小说》杂志，赚了15美元。

D2.en: At 20-22, he wrote columns between other assignments without extra pay.
D2.zh: 20到22岁间，他在其他任务间隙写专栏，没有额外报酬。

D3.en: At 12-13, he combined his interest in chemistry with photography.
D3.zh: 12到13岁时，他把对化学的兴趣和摄影结合起来。

D14.en: At 25, he was interviewed by a New York newspaper.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他接受了纽约一家报纸的采访。

D4.en: At 7-8, he received a printing press for Christmas.
D4.zh: 七八岁时，他圣诞节收到了一台印刷机。

D11.en: At 20-23, Short Stories accepted two more stories in 1901 and four in 1902-1903.
D11.zh: 20到23岁间，《短篇小说》杂志在1901年又接受了他两篇故事，1902到1903年接受了四篇。

M1.en: At 18-19, he was an excellent editor, attentive to detail and crisp in style.
M1.zh: 18到19岁时，他是个出色的编辑，注重细节，风格简洁。

D13a.en: Around age 20-21, he did not subsidize the publication because he lacked $300.
D13a.zh: 大约20到21岁时，他没有资助那本刊物，因为他拿不出300美元。

D7.en: At 25, he felt secure enough to criticize a famous novelist.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他自信到足以批评一位著名小说家。

D1.en: At 19, he sold his first story to Short Stories magazine.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他把第一篇故事卖给了《短篇小说》杂志。

D9.en: At 21, he became dramatic editor of the Herald, writing the daily theater column.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他成为《先驱报》戏剧编辑，撰写每日戏剧专栏。

D15.en: He kept a rug on his Hollins Street office floor for life.
D15.zh: 他终生在霍林斯街办公室地板上放着一块地毯。

D5.en: At 19-20, he drank, joked, frequented seamy areas, and boasted of whoring.
D5.zh: 19到20岁时，他喝酒、说笑、常去最肮脏的地方，并吹嘘嫖妓。

D10.en: At 20, he had a bad cough, lost weight, and felt exhausted.
D10.zh: 20岁时，他咳嗽厉害，体重下降，经常感到疲惫。

M4.en: At 18-19, he came to oppose capital punishment.
M4.zh: 18到19岁时，他开始反对死刑。

D16.en: At 20, he launched his first signed editorial column.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他推出了第一篇署名社论专栏。

D20.en: At 20-21, he became the Herald's man at City Hall, the paper's plum assignment.
D20.zh: 20到21岁时，他成为《先驱报》驻市政厅记者，那是报社的美差。

M3.en: At 20-22, he wrote respectful imitations of George Ade's Fables in Slang.
M3.zh: 20到22岁时，他模仿乔治·艾德的《俚语寓言》并表达敬意。

M5.en: He was pleased to have caught the eye of so noted an editor.
M5.zh: 他很高兴能引起一位著名编辑的注意。

D19.en: He wrote his columns in between other assignments, receiving no extra pay.
D19.zh: 他在其他任务之间写专栏，没有额外报酬。

M6.en: As managing editor, he found his calling.
M6.zh: 作为主编，他找到了自己的使命。

M7.en: As a young columnist, he predicted that critics would recognize 'Huckleberry Finn' as the greatest American novel.
M7.zh: 他作为年轻专栏作家预言，评论家终将认识到《哈克贝利·费恩》是美国最伟大的小说。

M8.en: He ranked Christian Scientists with Holy Rollers and chiropractors in his hierarchy of idiocy.
M8.zh: 他把基督教科学派与圣滚者和脊椎按摩师归为愚蠢等级。

D8.en: At twelve, he could not abide people he judged stupid or lazy.
D8.zh: 12岁时，他无法容忍他认为愚蠢或懒惰的人。

D17.en: At 18-25, he helped save a fallen farm girl from prostitution.
D17.zh: 18到25岁间，他帮助拯救了一个堕落的农家女，使其免于卖淫。

D4.en: She climbed tiny platforms and lay on the ground to photograph industrial machinery.
D4.zh: 她爬上小平台，躺在地上拍摄工业机械。

D3.en: She read Broom and The Dial, as noted in her diary.
D3.zh: 她在日记里写自己读了《扫帚》和《尺度》。

D11.en: She wrote a poem in 1918 promising to bake pies and cakes.
D11.zh: 她在1918年写了一首诗，承诺会烤馅饼和蛋糕。

D14.en: She was offered five dollars per click to use her legs in hosiery ads.
D14.zh: 有人出价每张五美元，用她的腿拍丝袜广告。

D9.en: She found a man who could fulfill her perfectionist expectations.
D9.zh: 她找到了一个能满足她完美主义要求的男人。

D20.en: She brought in pictures for the bank to choose from, with no direction.
D20.zh: 她带照片给银行挑选，银行没有给任何指示。

M1.en: She said, 'I want to become famous and I want to become wealthy.'
M1.zh: 她说：“我想出名，我想发财。”

D16.en: At 23, she noted her inclination to word herself in ways not quite ordinary.
D16.zh: 23岁时，她注意到自己倾向于用不太寻常的方式表达自己。

D6.en: She said she had two groups of friends: high hat and low hat.
D6.zh: 她说自己有两群朋友：高帽朋友和低帽朋友。

D18.en: At 23, she was offered $5 per photo for using her legs in hosiery ads.
D18.zh: 23岁时，她被人以每张5美元的价格雇佣，用她的腿拍丝袜广告。

D5.en: She climbed on tiny platforms and lay on the ground to look up at a ladle or a derrick.
D5.zh: 她爬上小平台，躺在地上仰视钢包或起重机。

D7.en: She decided with her partner to marry at the end of the school year.
D7.zh: 她和伴侣决定在学年末结婚。

D15.en: At 23, she spent hours clearing garbage from her viewfinder's path.
D15.zh: 23岁时，她花几个小时清理取景器路径上的垃圾。

D10.en: At 20, she said, 'We have agreed to forget this day.'
D10.zh: 20岁时，她说：‘我们同意忘记这一天。’

D13a.en: At 21, she decided to marry that June, before graduation.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她决定在当年六月毕业前结婚。

M3.en: At 22, she wanted to be in the region occupied by Stieglitz.
M3.zh: 22岁时，她想待在斯蒂格利茨所在的领域。

M8.en: As a child, she was raised with a strict moral code by Ethical Culture followers.
M8.zh: 幼年时，她被伦理文化运动的追随者以严格的道德准则抚养。

D2.en: At 13, she hoped someone would think she was good that summer.
D2.zh: 13岁时，她希望有人会觉得她那个夏天表现好。

D17.en: As a child, she fed and tended two hundred caterpillars under upturned glasses on the windowsill.
D17.zh: 小时候，她在窗台上用倒扣的玻璃杯喂养和照料了两百只毛毛虫。

M2.en: She repeated her father's creed of the beauty of utility as her own during childhood walks.
M2.zh: 童年散步时，她将父亲关于实用之美的信条当作自己的话重复。

M6.en: At 18-19, she believed she wanted to produce a book to 'cast my shadow over the world.'
M6.zh: 18到19岁时，她相信自己想写一本书来‘把我的影子投到世界上’。

D1.en: At 23, she said 'I'll wear blue jeans' and went back into the mill.
D1.zh: 23岁时，她说‘我穿蓝色牛仔裤’，然后回到了工厂。

M7.en: At 24-26, she felt her personal benefit contributed to the benefit of all.
M7.zh: 24到26岁间，她觉得自己的个人利益有助于所有人的利益。

M4.en: Around age 25, she brought the image of industry to the American public with glamour and force.
M4.zh: 大约25岁时，她将工业形象以魅力和力量呈现给美国公众。

D8.en: At 23, she said 'I can do anything I want to with these men, and through it all I like them.'
D8.zh: 23岁时，她说‘我可以对这些男人为所欲为，而且我始终喜欢他们。’

M5.en: Around age 24-25, she found fame delicious and good for business.
M5.zh: 大约24到25岁时，她觉得名声不仅美妙，而且对生意有好处。

D1.en: At 22, she noted Eugene would have refused the offer anyway, but a stipulation made it impossible to consider.
D1.zh: 22岁时，她指出尤金本会拒绝提议，但附加条件使其无法考虑。

D9.en: She traveled to Ohio State University for the founding convention of the American Student Union.
D9.zh: 她前往俄亥俄州立大学参加美国学生联盟的成立大会。

D13a.en: At 16, she simply refused.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她直接拒绝了。

D12.en: At 19, she was persuaded by her children to come down from a mountain after several days.
D12.zh: 19岁时，她的孩子们劝她几天后从山上下来。

D18.en: From age 17 to 20, she supported herself by tutoring and other part-time jobs.
D18.zh: 17到20岁间，她靠做家教和其他兼职养活自己。

D6.en: She was among the eighty Vassar students at that Albany demonstration.
D6.zh: 她是参加奥尔巴尼示威的八十名瓦萨学生之一。

M1.en: She supported herself by tutoring and working other part-time jobs.
M1.zh: 她靠做家教和其他兼职工作养活自己。

D10.en: Friends threw her a party after seeing her burst into tears.
D10.zh: 朋友们看到她突然大哭，就为她办了个派对。

D20.en: At 19, she was one of five elected to the Australian Student Union executive committee.
D20.zh: 19岁时，她成为澳大利亚学生联合会执行委员会仅有的五名成员之一。

M4.en: She was among the eighty Vassar students at that Albany demonstration.
M4.zh: 她是参加奥尔巴尼示威的八十名瓦萨学生之一。

D16.en: She went out for the debating team.
D16.zh: 她参加了辩论队选拔。

M7.en: She and Taylor Churchill leafletted against sweatshops with union members.
M7.zh: 她和泰勒·丘吉尔与工会成员一起发传单反对血汗工厂。

D14.en: At 16, she was voted the wittiest.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她被选为最风趣的人。

D12.en: He was born on October 22, 1913, in Budapest.
D12.zh: 他于1913年10月22日出生在布达佩斯。

D4.en: He made some of the most dramatic front-line battle photographs of his career.
D4.zh: 他拍摄了职业生涯中最震撼的前线战斗照片。

D2.en: He wandered around Berlin, photographing whatever caught his eye.
D2.zh: 他在柏林闲逛，拍摄任何吸引他目光的东西。

D6.en: He befriended David 'Chim' Seymour at the Dôme in early 1934.
D6.zh: 1934年初，他在多姆咖啡馆结识了大卫·奇姆·西摩。

D5.en: His plane crash-landed in a field outside Barcelona and broke apart.
D5.zh: 他的飞机在巴塞罗那郊外迫降并解体。

D13b.en: He entered the distinguished Evangelisches School at nearly six.
D13b.zh: 他快六岁时进入了著名的福音学校。

D18.en: He found work with the Veres travel agency, taking pictures of landmarks.
D18.zh: 他在维雷斯旅行社找到工作，拍摄地标照片。

D1.en: He started to consider photography seriously after briefly considering journalism.
D1.zh: 在短暂考虑新闻业后，他开始认真考虑摄影。

M7.en: At 17-18, he returned to Budapest to listen to a new music.
M7.zh: 17到18岁时，他回到布达佩斯听一种新音乐。

D10.en: After returning, he was so sick that he had an absolute breakdown.
D10.zh: 回来后，他病得很重，彻底崩溃了。

D13a.en: At 22, he hoped to quit photography and get into filmmaking.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他希望彻底放弃摄影，进入电影行业。

D3.en: At 18, he came to Berlin to study at the Deutsche Hochschule für Politik.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他来柏林就读于德意志政治学院。

D9.en: At 21, he taught her in days how to use a Leica and developed her first pictures.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他几天内教会她使用徕卡相机，还冲洗了她最初的照片。

D14.en: At 22, he threw himself into it enthusiastically, for more than money was at stake.
D14.zh: 22岁时，他全身心投入其中，因为事关重大，远不止金钱。

M1.en: At 19, his pictures were the most dramatic; he got within feet of his subject.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他的照片最具戏剧性；他离拍摄对象只有几英尺。

D11.en: As a small child, he daydreamed about saving girls from robbers to win a bride.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他幻想从强盗手中救出女孩，以此赢得新娘。

M4.en: At 22, he needed to feel the rush of fighting fascism with his camera.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他渴望用相机体验反法西斯战斗的激情。

M3.en: At 23, he saw a man to imitate: presenting life as a canvas of romance and heroism.
M3.zh: 23岁时，他找到了模仿对象：把人生描绘成浪漫与英雄的画卷。

D8.en: At 20-21, he was openly arrogant, scornful, and sarcastic toward his boss Block.
D8.zh: 20到21岁时，他对老板布洛克公开表现出傲慢、轻蔑和讽刺。

D20X.en: At 20-21, he left and went his own way.
D20X.zh: 20到21岁时，他离开并独自行动。

D6.en: He took the trolley across the Charles River to a pink Italianate villa on the Fenway.
D6.zh: 他乘坐有轨电车穿过查尔斯河，前往芬威一座粉色的意大利风格别墅。

D14.en: His book came out in spring 1913 to reviews that turned an author's head.
D14.zh: 他的书在1913年春天出版，获得了让作者飘飘然的评论。

D11.en: A few years after leaving Harvard, he wrote an article spoofing a fictitious clubman he called Albert the Male.
D11.zh: 离开哈佛几年后，他写了一篇文章，戏仿一个他称为“雄性阿尔伯特”的虚构俱乐部成员。

D13b.en: Since 1909, he had been a registered member of the Fabian Society, carrying his card and paying annual dues.
D13b.zh: 自1909年起，他一直是费边社的注册成员，随身携带会员卡并每年缴纳一英镑会费。

D3.en: At 15-16, he was captivated by Ruskin's vision in The Stones of Venice and Seven Lamps of Architecture.
D3.zh: 15到16岁时，他被拉斯金在《威尼斯之石》和《建筑的七盏明灯》中的视野所吸引。

D13a.en: He dealt with the Jewish issue by insisting it did not touch him.
D13a.zh: 他处理犹太问题的方式是坚称这与他无关。

D7.en: He dropped out of the athletic circle and concentrated on the intellectual side of Harvard.
D7.zh: 他退出了体育圈子，专注于哈佛的学术方面。

M1.en: Its publication, coinciding with the magazine's launch, added impressively to his reputation.
M1.zh: 它的出版恰逢杂志创刊，极大地提升了他的声誉。

D9.en: At 18-19, he helped form the Socialist Club.
D9.zh: 18到19岁时，他参与组建了社会主义俱乐部。

M4.en: At 25, he said the battle is against the chaos of a new freedom.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他说斗争不是针对顽固偏见，而是针对新自由的混乱。

D4.en: At 21, he burrowed through financial reports and interviewed many people.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他钻研财务报告并采访了从股票经纪人到信差等许多人。

D5.en: At 22, he resigned and attacked the administration, incurring leaders' wrath.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他辞职并抨击政府，招致领导人的愤怒。

D20X.en: At 22, he resigned and attacked the administration.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他辞职并抨击政府。

M3.en: At 22, he went a little mad when he heard TR speak at Madison Square Garden.
M3.zh: 22岁时，他在麦迪逊广场花园听罗斯福演讲时有点疯狂。

D8.en: At 24, he responded with a biting but perceptive portrait of Reed.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他用一篇尖锐但敏锐的肖像回应了里德。

M7.en: At 24, he showed increasing interest in administrative problems and constructive solutions.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他对行政问题和建设性解决方案表现出越来越大的兴趣。

D15.en: At 7, he crossed the Atlantic on the R.M.S. Etruria.
D15.zh: 7岁时，他乘坐皇家邮轮埃特鲁里亚号横渡大西洋。

D12.en: He was born in New York City.
D12.zh: 他出生在纽约市。

D3.en: At 22, he immediately tried his hand at writing two articles about his Panama experiences for Charles Hanson Towne.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他立即尝试为查尔斯·汉森·汤写了两篇关于巴拿马经历的文章。

D13a.en: He did not tell his parents until it was too late for them to protest.
D13a.zh: 他直到父母来不及反对才告诉他们。

D12.en: He sold his saxophone to pay for damage his father made him cover.
D12.zh: 他卖掉心爱的萨克斯来赔偿父亲让他付的损失。

D6.en: At 15, he borrowed his boyfriend's Stevens for a drive.
D6.zh: 15岁时，他借了男朋友的史蒂文斯车兜风。

D7.en: He refused the job, saying he was obligated to Mr. Powell.
D7.zh: 他拒绝了那份工作，理由是他对鲍威尔先生有义务。

D1.en: He won the World Wide Letter Writing Contest and got a Remington typewriter.
D1.zh: 他赢得了世界书信写作比赛，奖品是一台雷明顿打字机。

M1.en: He said he was worth far more money than Scovil would ever pay.
M1.zh: 他说他值比斯科维尔愿意支付的更多的钱。

D18.en: He worked as a deckboy on board the SS Radnor.
D18.zh: 他在拉德诺号上做甲板服务员。

D15.en: At 22, he felt his muscles hardening and never looked or felt better, despite hard ship work.
D15.zh: 22岁时，他感觉肌肉变硬，状态从未这么好，尽管船上工作很辛苦。

M6.en: At 22, he wanted to fill his youth with more than the pious platitudes around him.
M6.zh: 22岁时，他不想让青春被周围虔诚的陈词滥调填满。

D10.en: At 24, he felt lonesome, homesick, discouraged, and worried about lost time.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他感到孤独、想家、沮丧，担心浪费了太多时间。

M7.en: At 24, he insisted on visiting the area despite circumstances.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他坚持要访问那个地区。

D11.en: He circulated a secret newspaper titled 'Prison Flowers' with poems and short pieces by student prisoners.
D11.zh: 他传阅了一份秘密报纸《监狱之花》，上面有学生囚犯写的诗歌和短文。

D3.en: At 22-24, she debated ideas endlessly, once dissecting the word 'money' all night.
D3.zh: 22到24岁间，她没完没了地辩论各种想法，有一次整晚剖析‘金钱’这个词。

D11.en: At 12, she told someone she intended to become a newspaperwoman.
D11.zh: 12岁时，她告诉别人她打算成为一名女记者。

D2.en: At 22, she was determined and proud that she never called or wrote home asking for money.
D2.zh: 22岁时，她下定决心并自豪从未打电话或写信回家要钱。

D6.en: At 18, she and her colleagues in the campus Republican club worked for various candidates.
D6.zh: 18岁时，她和校园共和党俱乐部的同事为各种候选人工作。

D12.en: At 22, she was determined and proud that she never called or wrote home asking for money.
D12.zh: 22岁时，她下定决心并自豪从未打电话或写信回家要钱。

D14.en: At 20, she wrote a story for the Collegian in 1940 that stood the test of time.
D14.zh: 20岁时，她在1940年为《学院报》写了一篇经得起时间考验的故事。

D5.en: At 22, she decided Washington was the only place she wanted to be.
D5.zh: 22岁时，她决定华盛顿是她唯一想去的地方。

M1.en: At 22, she won a Rhodes scholarship to Oxford.
M1.zh: 22岁时，她获得了牛津大学的罗德奖学金。

D9.en: At 22, she went to work for Arch Eddy, who created and ran UP's City News Service, called WCNS.
D9.zh: 22岁时，她为创建并运营合众社城市新闻服务（WCNS）的阿奇·埃迪工作。

D1.en: At 22, she knocked on many doors and was turned down, but she was determined to stay.
D1.zh: 22岁时，她敲了很多门都被拒绝，但她决心留下来。

D7.en: At 22-24, she heard how someone refused to sign a loyalty oath and learned some things don't have to be done.
D7.zh: 22到24岁间，她听人讲述如何拒绝签署忠诚誓言，学到有些事不必做。

D10.en: At 24, she took to the streets, joining the joyous, hysterically happy throngs celebrating.
D10.zh: 24岁时，她走上街头，加入欢乐、狂喜的人群庆祝。

D13b.en: At 19, she married a Navy pilot on January 6, 1945.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，她于1945年1月6日嫁给了一名海军飞行员。

D11.en: He was an undistinguished student but a voracious reader.
D11.zh: 他学业平平，但酷爱读书。

D2.en: At 16-17, he sought out and read journalism books Birney suggested.
D2.zh: 16到17岁时，他找来并阅读了伯尼推荐的新闻学书籍。

D3.en: At 25, he had a fixed vision to excel as a UP war correspondent.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他决心成为合众社战地记者并脱颖而出。

D12.en: At 22, he was a bona fide executive with real responsibilities.
D12.zh: 22岁时，他成了真正的管理人员，承担实际责任。

D18.en: At 13-14, he delivered newspapers on a bicycle route.
D18.zh: 13到14岁时，他骑自行车送报纸。

D13a.en: At 10, his pleas to stay in Missouri were ignored.
D13a.zh: 10岁时，他请求留在密苏里，但未被采纳。

D14.en: At 10, he finagled getting both a saxophone and a clarinet for Christmas to learn Buddy Rogers songs.
D14.zh: 10岁时，他设法在圣诞节同时得到萨克斯管和单簧管，以便学巴迪·罗杰斯的歌。

D17.en: At 10-13, he shot a bird with a BB gun and gave up killing animals for life.
D17.zh: 10到13岁时，他用BB枪射中一只鸟，从此终生不再杀生。

D9.en: At 17-18, he was named sports editor of the student paper, the Campus Cub.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他被任命为学生报纸《校园幼兽》的体育编辑。

D4.en: At 13-14, he delivered copies of the Post on a regular bicycle route to customers.
D4.zh: 13到14岁时，他骑固定路线用自行车给客户送《邮报》。

M1.en: At 25, he had a fixed vision about excelling as a UP war correspondent in the European Theater.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他立志要在欧洲战场成为出色的合众社战地记者。

D19.en: At 19, he was asked to organize the morgue (newspaper archives).
D19.zh: 19岁时，他被要求整理报社的资料库。

M4.en: At 20, chasing a married woman left him feeling duplicitous.
M4.zh: 20岁时，追求有夫之妇让他觉得自己虚伪。

D7.en: At 20-21, he resolutely refused to go on air.
D7.zh: 20到21岁时，他坚决拒绝上节目。

M3.en: As a child, he clipped out Cronkite's Apollo news stories.
M3.zh: 他小时候剪下克朗凯特的阿波罗新闻故事。

D12.en: At age 5, he was aware of his parents going hungry while he ate.
D12.zh: 5岁时，他意识到父母挨饿而自己吃饭。

D13b.en: He was pulled out of school at 14 and didn't return until 20.
D13b.zh: 他14岁被拽出学校，直到20岁才回去上高中。

D1.en: He fell ill with a serious cold, missed his Paris schedule, and recovered after several days.
D1.zh: 他得了重感冒，错过了巴黎的行程，几天后才恢复。

D4.en: He started as an axman near the bottom of the lumber-jacking hierarchy.
D4.zh: 他从伐木工等级底部的斧头工做起。

D18.en: He decided to work a year in the logging camp to save money for going east.
D18.zh: 他决定在伐木营工作一年，攒够钱去东部。

D13a.en: He put aside cigarettes, drinking, and profanity and went home.
D13a.zh: 他暂时搁置了香烟、喝酒和脏话，回了家。

D6.en: At 21, he plotted late into the night to win Ed Murrow the NSFA presidency.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他深夜策划让埃德·默罗赢得NSFA主席职位。

D5.en: At 22, he applied for a passport to attend a student congress in Brussels.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他申请护照去布鲁塞尔参加国际学生联合会大会。

D17.en: As a teenager, he ordered flares, called for help, wrapped injured men in blankets, and comforted crying children.
D17.zh: 十几岁时，他让人设置信号灯，打电话叫医生和救护车，用毯子包裹伤者，并安慰哭泣的小孩。

D9.en: At age 21, he plotted strategy to win Ed Murrow the NSFA presidency.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他策划策略助爱德华·默罗赢得NSFA主席职位。

D7.en: At 21, he turned on his roommate for presuming to pick his girls.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他对室友发火，因为室友竟敢替他挑女孩。

M1.en: Between ages 18 and 22, he was class president, top ROTC cadet, and an able campus politician.
M1.zh: 18到22岁间，他当了班长、ROTC尖子生，还是个能干的学生会人物。

D10.en: As a small child, he had periodic outbursts the family called moody streaks, marking the onset of depression.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他周期性发脾气，家人称之为情绪化，标志着抑郁的开始。

D20.en: At 21, Ed began using tactics learned from TNE to pursue a new goal.
D20.zh: 21岁时，艾德开始运用从TNE学到的策略追求新目标。

D8.en: At 22, he told the committee he regarded it his duty to uphold the constitution.
D8.zh: 22岁时，他告诉委员会，他认为维护宪法是他的责任。

D19.en: At 4-6, he was drilled in manners like table behavior and greeting adults.
D19.zh: 4到6岁时，他接受了餐桌礼仪和问候长辈等规矩训练。

D6.en: At 11-12, she met cousins who arrived with their parents from Hollywood.
D6.zh: 11到12岁时，她见到了从好莱坞来的表亲们。

D11.en: At 19, she invented a false byline, A. G. Case, for critical stories.
D11.zh: 19岁时，她编造了假署名A. G. Case，用于批评其他校园刊物。

D7.en: At 17-18, she almost torched the place during a classroom experiment.
D7.zh: 17到18岁时，她差点在课堂实验中把地方烧了。

D15.en: At 10, she was amazingly fleet of foot.
D15.zh: 10岁时，她跑得飞快。

D2.en: At 15, she redid her room in elegant green striped wallpaper.
D2.zh: 15岁时，她重新装修了房间，用了优雅的绿色条纹壁纸。

D3.en: As a teenager, she kept up with restaurant and club ratings in trendy magazines.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她关注时尚杂志中的餐厅和俱乐部评级。

D14.en: At 16, she partied all night with Dempster and hundreds of celebrities.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她和登普斯特及数百名享乐者、名人彻夜狂欢。

D8.en: At 21-23, she had a lot to say on how the photos should be achieved.
D8.zh: 21到23岁时，她对如何完成照片有很多意见。

D19.en: At 15, she measured her knees with a ruler and compared to mine.
D19.zh: 15岁时，她用尺子量了自己的膝盖，又量了我的。

D5.en: At 17-18, she almost torched the place during a classroom experiment.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，她差点在课堂实验中把地方烧了。

D13a.en: At 25, she quit her job shortly after the Paris shows.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，她在巴黎时装秀后不久就辞职了。

M4.en: At 18-19, she prepared to go to an anti-Vietnam War rally with Blackburn.
M4.zh: 18到19岁时，她准备和布莱克本一起去参加反越战集会。

D9.en: At 21, she was active in rousing support for him.
D9.zh: 21岁时，她积极为他争取支持。

D16.en: At about 22, she said terrible, nasty things about people with no change in expression.
D16.zh: 大约22岁时，她说别人的坏话，表情毫无变化。

M1.en: At 22-24, she hoped to get herself on the cover against stiff competition like Charlotte Rampling.
M1.zh: 22到24岁间，她希望自己登上封面，面对夏洛特·兰普林等强劲对手。

D10.en: In her late 20s, she appeared rumpled, disheveled, with circles under her eyes, looking like a lost soul.
D10.zh: 快30岁时，她看起来皱巴巴的、邋遢，有黑眼圈，像个迷失的灵魂。

D4.en: At 23-25, she took clothes home, wore them, and returned them to the office.
D4.zh: 23到25岁时，她把衣服带回家穿，再带回办公室。

D12.en: At age 10, his father and Richard each handled a team of horses pulling the wagon.
D12.zh: 10岁时，他父亲和理查德各赶一队马拉车。

D13b.en: At 10, he dropped out of school to work full-time.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他辍学全职工作。

D10.en: At 20-21, he was stunned and immediately took a letter to a mutual friend.
D10.zh: 20到21岁时，他惊呆了，立刻把信拿给一个共同朋友。

D18.en: At 12, he worked for a movie-house owner, rounding up patrons for silent films.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他为电影院老板工作，招揽顾客看默片。

D7.en: At 20-21, he told his parents he would drop out of college to make his way.
D7.zh: 20到21岁间，他告诉父母他要辍学闯荡世界。

D9.en: At 15, he was elected homeroom president on his first day.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他第一天就被选为班级主席。

D4.en: At 3-5, he got a wooden wagon, carved cars, and a toy pistol from the metalworking shop.
D4.zh: 3到5岁时，他得到了木车、雕刻汽车和金属车间做的玩具手枪。

D15.en: At 12, he knew every Gopher player from reading the sports section weekly.
D15.zh: 12岁时，他每周读体育版，认识每个地鼠队球员。

D1.en: At 20-21, he begged Dr. Farber to be readmitted.
D1.zh: 20到21岁间，他求法伯医生让他重新入学。

M1.en: At 20-21, he got respectable if not prizewinning grades.
M1.zh: 20到21岁间，他取得了虽非顶尖但还不错的成绩。

D3.en: At 10-14, he joined a club for kids interested in collecting fossils and minerals.
D3.zh: 10到14岁时，他加入了一个为对收集化石和矿物感兴趣的孩子们设立的俱乐部。

D14.en: He introduced himself as a basketball player on the junior high team.
D14.zh: 他自我介绍是初中篮球队的球员。

D5.en: At 15, he hitched a ride from Pickstown and got off in Yankton, his new home.
D5.zh: 15岁时，他从皮克斯敦搭便车，在扬克顿下车，那是他的新家。

D6.en: He knew every Gopher player from reading the Tribune sports section weekly.
D6.zh: 他通过每周阅读《论坛报》体育版认识了所有地鼠队球员。

D2.en: At 15, he taught swimming, canoeing, and rowing before lifeguarding.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他在担任救生员之前教游泳、划独木舟和划船。

D11.en: At ages 3-5, he shot imaginary Japanese and German soldiers from a ditch.
D11.zh: 3到5岁时，他从一条浅沟里射击假想的日本和德国士兵。

D16.en: At 17-18, he won medals and ribbons as a member of the high school forensics club.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他作为高中辩论俱乐部的成员赢得了奖牌和绶带。

D20.en: He wrote a column for the student newspaper.
D20.zh: 他为学生报纸写专栏。

D13a.en: He begged Dr. Farber to readmit him and accepted a schedule on Farber's terms.
D13a.zh: 他恳求法伯博士让他重新入学，并接受了法伯安排的课程表。

M2.en: He felt he needed to make a stronger impression.
M2.zh: 他觉得自己需要留下更深刻的印象。

M3.en: At 21, he dreamed of commanding PT boats like JFK.
M3.zh: 21岁时，他梦想像肯尼迪一样指挥鱼雷艇。

D12.en: At 17, he was expected to work as a shop apprentice to support his family.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他被期望去商店当学徒挣钱养家。

D13a.en: At 17, he agreed to apply to medical school as a temporary measure, planning to later apply to Hunan Higher Industrial School.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他答应报考医科作为权宜之计，打算以后去考湖南高等工业学校。

D7.en: At 22, he actively participated in a student strike protesting local government suppression of anti-Japanese boycotts.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他积极参加学生罢课，抗议地方政府镇压抵制日货运动。

D6.en: At 16, he joined the "Furen Study Society" organized by classmates.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他参加了同学组织的“辅仁学社”。

M1.en: At 24, he graduated top of his class with highest scores in overall grades and thesis.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他以毕业总成绩和论文成绩双第一成为湘雅首届毕业生状元。

M2.en: At 17, he was a filial child, but his grandfather's words did not dissuade him from his industrial dream.
M2.zh: 17岁时，他是个孝顺的孩子，但祖父的话没能让他放弃学工业的理想。

M4.en: At 18, his passion for medicine began to burn eternally in his heart.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他为医学事业献身的热情在心中永远燃烧。

D2.en: From age 17 to 24, he studied hard for seven years and graduated top of his class.
D2.zh: 17到24岁间，他寒窗苦读七年，以双第一成绩成为湘雅首届状元。

D9.en: At 22, he and classmates cut two dishes per meal to save money for the magazine New Hunan.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他和同学们每餐减掉两道菜，省下伙食费为《新湖南》筹款。

D20.en: He was among the 14 students from Xiangya who formed a society.
D20.zh: 他是组成社团的14名湘雅学生之一。

M7.en: At 22, he stated the journal's aim to reform social thought by introducing new trends and knowledge from abroad.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他称杂志旨在引进国外新思潮改造社会。

D1.en: He repeatedly weighed 'poverty' and 'disease' in his mind.
D1.zh: 他反复把“贫”和“病”放在心里比较。

D3.en: He wrote an article 'Procedures for Public Health Facilities' in 1919.
D3.zh: 1919年他写了文章《公共卫生设施之程序》。

D17.en: He resolved to learn from Professor Shen and dedicate himself to medicine.
D17.zh: 他决心向沈教授学习，为医学奉献一切。

D18.en: He saved food from his own mouth to raise funds and start a publication with other Xiangya students.
D18.zh: 他和其他湘雅学生一起从嘴里省粮攒钱创办刊物。

D15.en: He was an active participant and a regular member.
D15.zh: 他是积极的参加者和当然的会员。

D19.en: He achieved top conduct and average score above 90.
D19.zh: 他操行甲等，学期平均成绩90分以上。

D10.en: In grief, he resolved to learn from Professor Shen and dedicate everything to medicine.
D10.zh: 他悲痛之余决心向沈教授学习，为医学奉献一切。

D11.en: At 18, he envisioned a crowd of people plagued by illness.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他脑海里浮现出一群病魔缠身的人。

D16.en: At 22, he was an avid reader and active contributor.
D16.zh: 22岁时，他是热心的读者和积极的撰稿人。

D8.en: At 17, he ranked first in entrance exams and became the first class student.
D8.zh: 17岁时，他以总分第一的成绩成为该校第一班学员。

D20X.en: At 17, he planned to take the entrance exam for Hunan Higher Industrial School.
D20X.zh: 17岁时，他准备去投考湖南高等工业学校。

M6.en: At 18, the passion to devote himself to medicine burned forever in his heart.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他为医学献身的热情在心中永远燃烧。

M8.en: At 22, he resolved to learn from Professor Shen and dedicate everything to medicine.
M8.zh: 22岁时，他决心向沈教授学习，为医学奉献一切。

M3.en: At 19, influenced by Dr. Hume, he immersed himself in internal medicine.
M3.zh: 19岁时，受胡美博士影响，他沉湎于内科学研究。

M5.en: At 17, he ranked first in entrance exams and became the first class student.
M5.zh: 17岁时，他以总分第一的成绩成为该校第一班学员。

D12.en: She worked her way through Fordham Jesuit University, training as a teacher.
D12.zh: 她靠打工读完福特汉姆耶稣会大学，受训成为教师。

D15.en: At 10, she crashed into an oak tree, breaking her nose and splitting her forehead.
D15.zh: 10岁时，她猛撞上一棵橡树，摔断鼻子，磕破额头。

D3.en: At 16, she applied to go to South America, which made news in Oyster Bay.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她申请去南美，这成了牡蛎湾的新闻。

D4.en: At 14, she had the tiller most of the day, sailing fast in the Sound.
D4.zh: 14岁时，她大部分时间掌舵，在长岛海峡快速航行。

D7.en: At 14, she wore a mini skirt to church, which her parents disliked.
D7.zh: 14岁时，她穿迷你裙去教堂，父母不喜欢。

D2.en: At 14, she had the tiller most of the day, sailing fast in the Sound.
D2.zh: 14岁时，她大部分时间掌舵，在长岛海峡快速航行。

D1.en: At 14-15, she got a lifesaving certificate, worked toward a safety license, and saved babysitting money.
D1.zh: 14到15岁时，她获得救生证书，考安全执照，攒下保姆费。

D9.en: At 16, she decided to go abroad for a year on a cultural exchange with the American Field Service program.
D9.zh: 16岁时，她决定参加美国战地服务团的文化交流项目出国一年。

M4.en: At 15, she joined an antiwar march in Washington, DC.
M4.zh: 15岁时她参加了华盛顿的反战游行。

D14.en: At 16, she sashayed along the catwalk with other debutantes and danced the waltz with Oswaldo.
D14.zh: 16岁时，她和其他初入社交界的女孩一起走秀，并与奥斯瓦尔多跳了华尔兹。

D18.en: At 22, she sent out résumés and looked for short-term assignments to keep her going.
D18.zh: 22岁时，她投递简历并寻找短期工作来维持生计。

M7.en: At 17, she said: 'I can’t believe they only get twenty-five cents an hour. I just can’t believe it.'
M7.zh: 17岁时，她说：‘我不敢相信他们每小时只挣25美分。我简直不敢相信。’

M1.en: At 20, she realized she didn't just want to become a journalist; she had to.
M1.zh: 20岁时，她意识到自己不只是想成为记者，而是必须成为记者。

D10.en: At 25, she said: 'I have not cried, sobbed like this, since my father died. It’s the same feeling as with Dad.'
D10.zh: 25岁时，她说：‘自从父亲去世后，我从没这样哭过。和父亲去世时感觉一样。’

M2.en: At 20, she said: 'I did everything to make him proud. If I made him proud that was the main thing that mattered.'
M2.zh: 20岁时，她说：‘我做的一切都是为了让他骄傲。让他骄傲是最重要的事。’

M6.en: At 22, she realized that to fail at writing would be real failure, and to succeed the only success she would value.
M6.zh: 22岁时，她意识到写作失败才是真正的失败，而写作成功是她唯一看重的成功。

D13a.en: At 16, she escaped.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她逃走了。

D3.en: She often read late into the night, befriending characters like Romeo and Jane Eyre.
D3.zh: 她常常在灯下读到深夜，和罗密欧、简·爱等人物交朋友。

D6.en: She was an active participant in performances, almost always involved.
D6.zh: 她是这类活动的积极参加者，每次演出几乎都少不了她。

D14.en: She stroked her father's head and sang a hymn with her family.
D14.zh: 她抚着父亲的头，和家人一起唱起了《睡主怀中歌》。

D13b.en: At age seven, she was baptized.
D13b.zh: 七岁那年，她参加了受洗礼。

D2.en: She often got up after midnight, covered the lamp with paper, and studied quietly.
D2.zh: 她常常十二点以后起来，把电灯罩上纸，悄悄地继续看书学习。

D4.en: She crocheted a black hairnet with fine thread, her hands moving skillfully.
D4.zh: 她用勾针勾织着一个黑色的发网，两手灵活地飞针挑线。

D9.en: She and a classmate carried a fainted student to shade and called her sister.
D9.zh: 她和另一个女同学把晕倒的同学抬到阴凉处，并给她姐姐打了电话。

D7.en: She declared that her lifelong matter is to study and pursue a career.
D7.zh: 她说她的终生大事就是上学，求学问，干事业。

D10.en: While singing, she thought of her father's kindness and burst into tears.
D10.zh: 她一边唱，一边想着父亲的抚育之恩，不由得泪如雨下。

M2.en: At 25, she wept while singing, thinking of her father's kindness.
M2.zh: 25岁时，她边唱边想父亲的养育之恩，泪如雨下。

M1.en: At 20-21, she studied after midnight, wrapping a lamp in paper, eating cold sweet potatoes.
M1.zh: 20到21岁时，她常在十二点后起来，把电灯罩上纸继续看书，饿了啃烤白薯。

M4.en: At 24, she printed and mailed leaflets exposing imperialist crimes to universities abroad.
M4.zh: 24岁时，她印刷并邮寄传单，向国外大学揭露帝国主义罪行。

D20.en: At 19, she wore the school pin upon graduation and kept it.
D20.zh: 19岁毕业时，她佩戴了校别针并一直保存着。

D5.en: At 19, she said, 'If I fail the exam, I won't go back.'
D5.zh: 19岁时，她说：考不上，我就不回去了。

D16.en: At 18, she answered fluently when questioned in English about life, study, and society.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她用英语流利地回答了关于生活、学习和社会的提问。

D15.en: At 20-28, she played basketball well, but few female classmates could play.
D15.zh: 20到28岁时，她篮球打得不错，但协和女同学中没几个能上场。

D11.en: At 9-10, she wished she had been born earlier and become a doctor to save her mother.
D11.zh: 9到10岁时，她天真地想：为什么不早生几年，当个神医把妈妈留在世上。

D1.en: At 20, after scoring zero on a test and hearing 'girls can't do it,' she resolved to break the prejudice through hard work.
D1.zh: 20岁时，考试得零分后听到‘女生就是不行’，她决心用行动打破偏见。

D17.en: At 25, she touched her father's head and sang a hymn with her family.
D17.zh: 25岁时，她抚着父亲的头，和家人一起唱赞美诗。

D19.en: At 21, she used a diagram to help explain a problem, and it worked well.
D19.zh: 21岁时，她用一张图帮助说明问题，效果很好。

D8.en: At 20, she said, 'Don't think only you boys are good; we girls are just as good!'
D8.zh: 20岁时，她说：“哼，别觉得就你们男生行，我们女生也行！”

M5.en: At 20, she resolved to break the prejudice against women through her actions.
M5.zh: 20岁时，她决心以实际行动打破对女人的偏见。

D13a.en: In her 20s, she decisively nipped a budding romance in the bud.
D13a.zh: 二十多岁时，她决然掐掉了爱情的萌芽。

D2.en: At 23, he joined Peking Union Medical College as a surgery trainee.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他进入北京协和医学院做外科实习生。

D3.en: At 25-26, he performed surgical experiments on 70 rabbits' femurs and tibias over six months.
D3.zh: 25到26岁间，他在六个月里对约70只兔子的股骨和胫骨做了各种外科实验。

D9.en: At about 32, he was assigned to Robert Koch Hospital to take over his work.
D9.zh: 大约32岁时，他被派到罗伯特·科赫医院接管他的工作。

D12.en: From 17 to 23, he studied at Xiao He Yan Medical University, graduating June 30, 1933.
D12.zh: 17到23岁间，他在小河沿医科大学学习，1933年6月30日毕业。

M1.en: At 17, he was pleased to be accepted and started his long journey in medicine.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他很高兴被录取，开始了漫长的医学之路。

D18.en: His father was a teacher and the family was not well off.
D18.zh: 他父亲是教师，家境不富裕。

M7.en: From 18 to 24, he told himself he should do something good for his country like Dugald Christie.
M7.zh: 18到24岁间，他常对自己说，如果英国医生司督阁能在中国建医院和医学院，他也应该为国家做点好事。

M4.en: At 23, his hatred for the loss of his country increased.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他对国家沦陷的仇恨加深了。

D6.en: At about 32-33, he built deep friendships with all medical personnel and patients.
D6.zh: 大约32到33岁时，他与所有医务人员和病人建立了深厚的友谊。

D5.en: He made a risky decision that was not easily reached.
D5.zh: 他做出了一个有风险的决定，这个决定并不容易做出。

D7.en: At 23, he was hit hard on the chest by a Japanese policeman, which deepened his hatred for his lost country.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他被日本警察重重打在胸口，这加深了他对亡国的仇恨。

D4.en: At 25-26, he performed surgical experiments on the femur and tibia of about 70 rabbits over six months.
D4.zh: 25到26岁时，他在六个月内对约70只兔子进行了股骨和胫骨手术实验。

D1.en: At 32-33, he noted that before his arrival, the wound complication rate had been as high as 20%.
D1.zh: 32到33岁时，他注意到在他到来之前，伤口并发症率高达20%。

D12.en: At 10-12, he earned up to $15 a week, helping his mother.
D12.zh: 10到12岁时，他每周赚多达15美元，补贴母亲家用。

D13a.en: He was never a Zionist, fearing an independent Jewish state would create disorder.
D13a.zh: 他从未是犹太复国主义者，担心独立的犹太国家会制造混乱。

D15.en: He played center forward on the freshman and varsity teams for four years.
D15.zh: 他四年里在校新生队和校队踢中锋。

D18.en: At 10, he earned a dollar for carrying golf clubs, including a 25-cent tip.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他扛球杆赚了一美元，其中25美分小费。

D6.en: At 18-21, his fraternity house of forty members was a refuge.
D6.zh: 18到21岁间，他所在的四十人兄弟会宿舍是个避风港。

D2.en: At 20-22, he produced seven sports sections weekly and wrote a daily column.
D2.zh: 20到22岁间，他每周出七版体育版，每天写专栏。

D5.en: At 12, he accidentally killed a playmate while tinkering with a gun.
D5.zh: 12岁时，他摆弄枪意外打死了一个玩伴。

M1.en: At 23, he called this his first big chance and made careful plans.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他说这是他的第一个大机会，并做了仔细计划。

D9.en: At 25, the AP gave him his first nationally syndicated column.
D9.zh: 25岁时，美联社给了他第一个全国专栏。

D10.en: He never got over the old fear of being laughed at.
D10.zh: 他从未克服对被嘲笑的恐惧。

D3.en: He took a course in seventeenth-century literature from Jacob Zeitlin.
D3.zh: 他选了雅各布·蔡特林的十七世纪文学课。

D11.en: He read unlikely things that nourished his dreams of being a reporter.
D11.zh: 他读了很多冷门东西，滋养了他当记者的梦想。

D7.en: He hitchhiked to Dayton without even going back to his room.
D7.zh: 他连房间都没回，就搭便车去了代顿。

D1.en: He said he loved the University of Illinois and had strong personal reasons for completing.
D1.zh: 他说他热爱伊利诺伊大学，有强烈的个人理由完成学业。

D8.en: He denounced a promotion as silly and greedy, and was threatened with firing but MacPhail backed him.
D8.zh: 他谴责一项促销愚蠢又贪婪，被威胁开除，但麦克费尔支持他。

D19.en: He wrote in longhand, read into a tape recorder, had it typed, and finished correcting by noon for a 12:30 pickup.
D19.zh: 他手写专栏，对着录音机读一遍，让人打字，中午前改好，十二点半准时有人来取。

D13b.en: By age 25, he transformed himself into an influential New York City newsman.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他把自己变成了一个有影响力的纽约市新闻记者。

D7.en: At 22, he said he wished all the people sitting out there weren't there.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他说他希望所有坐在那里的人都不在那里。

D3.en: At 14, he came upon a stack of back issues of the Nation and the New Republic.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他偶然发现了一堆过期的《国家》和《新共和》杂志。

D2.en: In his first two weeks at the Record, he wrote three stories about local banks closing.
D2.zh: 在《记录报》的头两周，他写了三篇关于当地银行倒闭的报道。

D6.en: At 18, he invited someone to use his dorm room and they shook hands.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他邀请别人用他的宿舍房间，他们握了手。

D15.en: At 19, he spent weeks trying to get a job on a New England farm.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他花了几个星期试图在新英格兰的一个农场找工作。

D13a.en: He told his managing editor he would quit unless he got a $5 raise, and got it.
D13a.zh: 他告诉主编，除非加薪5美元，否则就辞职，结果他得到了加薪。

M1.en: He had to abandon his ambition to attend Harvard.
M1.zh: 他不得不放弃上哈佛的志向。

M6.en: He decided to major in philosophy.
M6.zh: 他决定主修哲学。

D9.en: Calverton took a liking to him and recruited him for the magazine.
D9.zh: 卡尔弗顿喜欢他，并招募他为杂志工作。

D14.en: He said he wished the audience weren't there.
D14.zh: 他说他希望观众都不在场。

D4.en: He had a regular slot in the paper's critical rotation by summer 1928.
D4.zh: 到1928年夏天，他在报纸的评论专栏有了固定位置。

D1.en: At 19, he spent weeks after Sacco and Vanzetti's execution trying to get a farm job in New England.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他在萨科和万泽蒂被处决后花了几周时间，试图在新英格兰找一份农场工作。

D10.en: At 20, he felt betrayed.
D10.zh: 20岁时，他感到被背叛了。

D17.en: At 20, he wrote love poems for Esther.
D17.zh: 20岁时，他为埃斯特写了情诗。

D19.en: At 19, he worked from 1:30 to 5 doing rewrites in Camden, then edited until 11 pm in Philadelphia.
D19.zh: 19岁时，他从下午1点半到5点在卡姆登改写稿件，然后回费城编辑到晚上11点。

D20X.en: At 19, he left school for good on November 22.
D20X.zh: 19岁时，他在11月22日永远离开了学校。

M2.en: At age 19, his father was buying property and lacked interest or authority to keep him in school.
M2.zh: 19岁时，他父亲忙于购置房产，无兴趣也无权让他继续上学。

M5.en: At 20, he felt betrayed.
M5.zh: 20岁时，他感到被背叛。

M8.en: At 19, he thought of teaching philosophy but was repelled by faculty pettiness; preferred a newsroom.
M8.zh: 19岁时，他考虑过教哲学，但被教职人员的狭隘所排斥；更喜欢新闻编辑室的气味。

D7.en: At 13, he angrily refused the Kuomintang order to flee to Taiwan with hospital funds, staying in newly founded China.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他愤怒地拒绝了国民党让他携款撤去台湾的命令，留在了新中国。

M2.en: At 13, he angrily refused the Kuomintang order to flee to Taiwan with hospital funds and stayed in newly founded China.
M2.zh: 13岁时，他愤怒地拒绝了国民党让他携款撤去台湾的命令，留在了新中国。

D2.en: In 1960, he graduated from Beijing Medical College and stayed to work there.
D2.zh: 1960年，他毕业于北京医学院并留校工作。

D15.en: At 20, he won the 400-meter race in a sports meet, bringing honor to his school.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他在运动会中摘取400米跑桂冠，为北医大争了光。

M1.en: At 22, he broke the national record in the 400-meter hurdles with a time of 54.4 seconds.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他以54.4秒的成绩打破400米栏全国纪录。

D12.en: At age 10-13, he became the family's white mouse keeper.
D12.zh: 10到13岁时，他成了家里的白鼠饲养员。

D4.en: At 66, he tried high-dose corticosteroid intravenous drip treatment.
D4.zh: 66岁时，他试用大剂量皮质激素进行静脉点滴治疗。

D3.en: At 67, he said the most important thing is to trust your own eyes and to summarize and find patterns in time.
D3.zh: 67岁时，他说最重要的是相信自己的眼睛，及时总结发现规律。

D6.en: At 19-24, he was not just a bookworm but an active participant in activities, enjoying being with classmates.
D6.zh: 19到24岁间，他不只是书虫，还是活跃分子，积极参加文体活动，喜欢和同学打成一片。

M7.en: He chose to stay in Guangzhou out of patriotism.
M7.zh: 他出于爱国之心选择留在广州。

D5.en: He could only observe as a visitor because UK law didn't recognize his Chinese medical license.
D5.zh: 按照英国法律，他作为中国医生不能单独行医，只能以观察者身份参加查房和实验。

D9.en: He led a strong team to save critically ill patients with science and skill.
D9.zh: 他带领一个坚强的团队，以科学的精神和高超的医术抢救危重病人。

D1.en: At 44, his supervisor limited his study abroad to 8 months, so he had to find his own way out.
D1.zh: 44岁时，导师把他的留学时间限制为8个月，他只能自己找出路。

D10.en: He admitted he gets impatient and loses his temper, like just now when you came in.
D10.zh: 他承认自己也会急躁发脾气，就像刚才你们进来时他就发了脾气。

M4.en: His passion for medicine grew from understanding patients' will to live.
M4.zh: 他对医研专业的热爱和渴望提高的动力来自对病人求生愿望的理解。

M6.en: He believes people live not only in reality but also in ideals.
M6.zh: 他认为人不只生活在现实中，还生活在理想中。

D1.en: At 21, he aced his anatomy exam after two years of repetition, earning a perfect 100 from Professor Hayek.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他重复学习两年解剖学后考得非常好，Hayek教授给了100分。

D5.en: At 22, he and a friend got past a waiter with only $10, and the waiter didn't even say thanks.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他和朋友只给了侍者10美元就挺过去了，侍者连谢谢都没说。

M1.en: At 24, he and a friend gave up special treatment to take the German national exam and doctoral exam like German students.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他和过晋源放弃优待，决定像德国学生一样先参加国家考试再考博士。

D12.en: As a teenager, he ate lard and salt rice at school, and was bullied because he was a free student.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他在学校用猪油拌饭吃，因为是保送生被有钱同学欺负。

D7.en: He and Xie Yujin defied opposition from Waldeyer and others to perform a dissection that caused a sensation in Shanghai.
D7.zh: 他和谢毓晋不顾Waldeyer教授等人的反对，坚持做成了解剖，轰动了上海滩。

D18.en: As a small child, he once took a cigarette pack from a guest's pocket to collect cards, and his mother cried.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他曾从客人口袋里掏香烟盒收集烟标，母亲为此哭着教育他。

D4.en: At 23, he personally made many slides from specimens using different staining methods in the pathology lab.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他在病理研究所自己动手，用不同染色方法制作了许多标本切片。

D15.en: As a teenager, he almost daily climbed to the 13th floor of Liuhe Pagoda to read.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他几乎每天都上到六和塔第十三层去读书。

D14.en: At 21, he and his classmates wore white coats and served as volunteer docents.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他和同学们穿上白大褂，当义务讲解员。

D13a.en: At 21-22, he followed Xie Yujin's suggestion to study in Germany, and his family supported him.
D13a.zh: 21到22岁间，他听从谢毓晋的建议去德国留学，家人支持他。

D10.en: At 22, after a week in hospital, he walked alone on autumn leaves, thought of home, and cried.
D10.zh: 22岁时，住院一周后，他独自走在落叶上，想起祖国和家乡，哭了。

D3.en: At 23, he found similar reports in literature but only as unconfirmed speculation.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他查阅文献发现类似报道，但只是未经证实的推测。

D16.en: At 23-24, he bravely stepped up and declared they were Chinese, not Japanese, studying at Munich University.
D16.zh: 23到24岁间，他鼓起勇气走上台阶说：我们不是日本人，是中国人，在慕尼黑大学留学。

D6.en: At 24, he invited 6 fellow Chinese students from Munich University for beer and pork chops.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他邀请了6位慕尼黑大学的工科同学喝啤酒、吃小猪排。

D12.en: He was born in Berlin in 1906 to a Russian emigre father and a German mother.
D12.zh: 他1906年生于柏林，父亲是俄国移民，母亲是德国人。

D2.en: He was studious, had private math lessons, and took a mild interest in philosophy.
D2.zh: 他勤奋好学，上数学私教课，对哲学有点兴趣。

D14.en: He performed as a pianist on Berlin stages and wrote music criticism for a Berlin evening paper.
D14.zh: 他在柏林多个舞台上演奏钢琴，并为柏林晚报撰写乐评。

D3.en: He impressed colleagues with his encyclopedic knowledge of the literature and near-photographic memory.
D3.zh: 他以对文献的百科全书式知识和近乎过目不忘的记忆力给同事留下深刻印象。

D6.en: He often played four-handed piano duets with his Polish friend Mieczyslaw Kolinski.
D6.zh: 他常和波兰朋友米奇斯瓦夫·科林斯基弹四手联弹。

D10.en: He experienced outbursts with shouting and throwing things, and once hurt himself with glass.
D10.zh: 他有过几次大喊大叫、扔东西的爆发，一次还被碎玻璃弄伤了自己。

D16.en: At 24-27, he often said 'I hate that' and declared 'That's me, Ernst Boris Michaelovich Chain - Chaithin!'
D16.zh: 24到27岁间，他常说‘我讨厌那个’，并宣称‘那就是我，恩斯特·鲍里斯·米哈伊洛维奇·钱恩 - 柴廷！’

D13a.en: He began to swing towards science when he realized he would never be a great pianist.
D13a.zh: 他意识到自己成不了伟大钢琴家后，开始转向科学。

D9.en: He organized a private arrangement to borrow four volumes at a time for up to 24 hours.
D9.zh: 他私下安排每次借四卷书，最多借24小时。

D5.en: He thought if things went wrong he could return to Oxford with only a year lost.
D5.zh: 他认为如果事情不顺利，他可以回牛津，只损失一年时间。

D7.en: All his colleagues in England advised him against taking a job in Italy.
D7.zh: 他在英国的所有同事都建议他不要接受意大利的工作。

D8.en: He held the view that creative work does not require living in a place with a centuries-old creative tradition.
D8.zh: 他认为做创造性工作不一定非要住在有数百年创造传统的地方。

D20.en: He set about his work with financial backing from the Italian government.
D20.zh: 他在意大利政府的资助下开始工作。

M4.en: He had an interest in Zionism.
M4.zh: 他对犹太复国主义感兴趣。

D6.en: He played golf with an affable old man and had an extremely good time.
D6.zh: 他和一个和蔼的老人打高尔夫，玩得非常开心。

D12.en: He ran through all his money and asked for £5 more.
D12.zh: 他花光了所有钱，又找人要了5英镑。

D9.en: He found the accommodation inadequate and inconvenient, but hoped to remedy it.
D9.zh: 他发现住处不够用也不方便，但希望以后能改善。

D15.en: He played hockey for the college, ran in relay races, and played golf.
D15.zh: 他代表学院打曲棍球，参加接力赛，还打高尔夫。

D8.en: He would coldly reduce an opponent long after winning was clear.
D8.zh: 他在明显能赢后仍冷静地压制对手。

D3.en: He found only rats and guinea pigs have contractile lacteals, and wondered why others don't.
D3.zh: 他发现只有老鼠和豚鼠有收缩性乳糜管，好奇为什么其他动物没有。

M1.en: He won prize after prize: five in chemistry, seven in physics, math, and history.
M1.zh: 他拿了一个又一个奖：化学五个，物理、数学和历史七个。

D20.en: He arranged to do research in pharmacology with Professor Gunn next year.
D20.zh: 他安排好了明年跟冈恩教授做药理学研究。

D7.en: He didn't want to go but felt he ought to.
D7.zh: 他不想去，但觉得应该去。

D5.en: At 16, he said he didn't want to go but felt he ought to.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他说他不想去，但觉得自己应该去。

D13a.en: At 20, he gave up his job and entered medical school after receiving a small legacy.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他得到一小笔遗产后辞去工作，进入医学院。

M6.en: At 16-17, he decided on a research career in science.
M6.zh: 16到17岁时，他下定决心从事科学研究。

D4.en: At 25, he planned to observe cat brain circulation with a microscope.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他打算用显微镜观察猫脑的血液循环。

D17.en: At 25, he stayed to keep burglars away while Sherry was at the Royal Society.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他留下来防贼，而谢里在皇家学会忙活。

D1.en: At 15, despite a second pneumonia attack, he continued to excel at school.
D1.zh: 15岁时，尽管第二次患上肺炎，他仍在学校表现出色。

M4.en: At 16, he said he didn't want to go but felt he ought to.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他说他不想去，但觉得自己应该去。

D14.en: At 24, he showed a bat's wing specimen with contractile veins at a Science Club meeting.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他在科学俱乐部会议上展示了一个有收缩静脉的蝙蝠翅膀标本。

D16.en: At 25-26, he gave a well-received paper to the Physiological Society.
D16.zh: 25到26岁时，他向生理学会提交了一篇广受好评的论文。

D11.en: At 22, he wrote letters under a cherry tree like a novel's heroine.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他像小说女主角一样坐在樱桃树下写信。

D19.en: At 24, he tried to finish work for his Caius Fellowship thesis.
D19.zh: 24岁时，他努力完成凯斯学院奖学金论文。

M7.en: At 21, he said not to be self-righteous about the war and German atrocities.
M7.zh: 21岁时，他说别对战争和德国暴行自以为是。

M2.en: At 24, he was confident that if he got that, they wouldn't starve.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他自信如果得到那个，他们就不会挨饿。

M5.en: At 16, he thought a chap would feel foolish staying home while others returned from war.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他觉得待在家的人等其他人打仗回来会像个傻瓜。

M8.en: At 21, he often went to symphony concerts and his real appreciation of music began.
M8.zh: 21岁时，他常去听交响乐，真正开始欣赏音乐。

M3.en: At 25, he sat listening for hours, thrilled to hear a mentor talk of mythical figures.
M3.zh: 25岁时，他坐着听导师谈论神话人物，一连几小时都听得入迷。

D18.en: At 14, he became a junior clerk in a shipping office in Leadenhall Street.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他在利德贺街一家航运公司当小职员。
