D4.en: He was trained to make most of his gear with rubber teats and capillary tubes.
D4.zh: 他受训用橡胶乳头和毛细管制作大部分器材。

D3.en: From childhood he showed a lively interest in science, especially chemistry.
D3.zh: 他从小就对科学，特别是化学，表现出浓厚的兴趣。

D18.en: At 14-15, he found employment in a shipping company, the American Line.
D18.zh: 14到15岁时，他在一家航运公司美国航线找到了工作。

D15.en: At 19-23, he played tennis and football for Adelaide University.
D15.zh: 19到23岁时，他代表阿德莱德大学打网球和踢足球。

D5.en: At 19, he enlisted in the London Scottish.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他加入了伦敦苏格兰团。

D2.en: At 19-20, he found a teacher to coach him evenings and sat for the exam in July 1901.
D2.zh: 19到20岁时，他找了老师晚上辅导，并在1901年7月参加了考试。

D6.en: At 25, he was a good shot and wanted new members for St Mary's rifle club.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他枪法很好，并希望为圣玛丽步枪俱乐部招募新成员。

M1.en: At 19-20, he was first in English subjects and equal first in General Proficiency.
M1.zh: 19到20岁时，他在英语科目中排名第一，并在综合能力中并列第一。

D7.en: At 16, he showed independence by wearing a bow-tie, and never wore any other sort.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他表现出独立性，戴了领结，此后一生只戴这种领结。

D16.en: He feigned innocence while asking 'Why?'
D16.zh: 他假装无辜地问‘为什么？’

D9.en: He offered Wright a post in his new department; Wright accepted and moved to Cambridge in 1886.
D9.zh: 他给赖特提供了一个新部门的职位，赖特接受了并于1886年搬到剑桥。

D20.en: At St Peter's he was head boy.
D20.zh: 在圣彼得学校，他是男学生长。

D14.en: He was active in the Dramatic Society, often cast in female roles due to his small size.
D14.zh: 他活跃于戏剧社，因为个子小常被分配演女性角色。

D1.en: He faced a conflict between family ties and ambition for medical research; ambition won.
D1.zh: 他面临家庭情感与医学研究抱负的冲突，最终抱负胜出。

D13b.en: He accepted the position because it paid a small salary while allowing time to work for his London MB, BS.
D13b.zh: 他接受了这个职位，因为薪水虽少但有时间准备伦敦的MB、BS考试。

M4.en: He enlisted immediately as a private in the London Scottish in 1900.
M4.zh: 1900年，他立即以列兵身份加入伦敦苏格兰团。

D2.en: He received his MD from NYU and began a residency at Bellevue Hospital.
D2.zh: 他在纽约大学获得医学博士学位，并在贝尔维尤医院开始住院医师培训。

D16.en: He determined to explore a contradiction his professor couldn't explain.
D16.zh: 他决定探究教授无法解释的矛盾。

M1.en: He was elected to Alpha Omega Alpha in his third year of medical school.
M1.zh: 他在医学院三年级时被选入阿尔法欧米伽阿尔法荣誉学会。

D3.en: Enthralled by design in high school, he studied architecture at the University of Pennsylvania.
D3.zh: 高中时痴迷于设计，他在宾夕法尼亚大学学习建筑。

D4.en: He conducted immunization experiments with monkeys using Formalin-killed polioviruses.
D4.zh: 他用福尔马林灭活的脊髓灰质炎病毒对猴子进行免疫实验。

D9.en: He shared laboratory experiments and a reverence for research with his classmates.
D9.zh: 他和同学一起做实验，同样崇敬研究。

D6.en: At Townsend Harris, he found classmates as smart or smarter than him.
D6.zh: 在汤森哈里斯学校，他发现同学和他一样聪明甚至更聪明。

D1.en: When his demonstration failed, he meticulously repeated each step instead of abandoning his idea.
D1.zh: 当演示失败时，他仔细重复每一步，而不是放弃想法。

D20.en: He shared lab experiments with classmates in the same section.
D20.zh: 他和同班同学一起做实验室实验。

M4.en: At 20, he resolved to make scientific investigation his life's work.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他决心将科学研究作为毕生事业。

D15.en: At 23-25, he completed clinical rotations in medicine, pediatrics, surgery, and obstetrics with proficiency.
D15.zh: 23到25岁间，他熟练完成了内科、儿科、外科和产科的临床轮转。

D18.en: At 6, he peddled newspapers to help support the family.
D18.zh: 6岁时，他卖报纸贴补家用。

D19.en: At 20-21, he meticulously repeated each step and found that bacterial aggregation was not induced by freezing or alcohol.
D19.zh: 20到21岁间，他一丝不苟地重复每一步，发现细菌聚集并非由冷冻或酒精引起。

M5.en: At 20, he said, 'I was going to be different from others. I just had to accept that.'
M5.zh: 20岁时，他说：“我要和别人不一样。我不得不接受这一点。”

M6.en: At 20, he resolved to make scientific investigation his life's work.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他决心将科学调查作为终身事业。

M8.en: At 20, he resolved to make scientific investigation his life's work.
M8.zh: 20岁时，他决心将科学调查作为终身事业。

D3.en: He broke his journey to sample the quality of a new organ in Stuttgart.
D3.zh: 他中途停留，去试听斯图加特一个新管风琴的音质。

M4.en: At 25, he faced the upheaval of established views on Christianity's founder.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他面对对基督教创始人既定观点的颠覆。

D2.en: He felt anxious about his passion for play and gradually gave up all games.
D2.zh: 他开始对自己的游戏热情感到焦虑，逐渐放弃了所有游戏。

D9.en: He prepared the first lecture for the Foreign Language Society of Paris.
D9.zh: 他为巴黎外语协会准备了系列讲座的第一讲。

D6.en: He shooed birds away to save them from his companion's catapult, then fled home.
D6.zh: 他把鸟赶走，让它们躲开同伴的弹弓，然后跑回了家。

D10.en: As a small child, he hit a girl.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他打了一个女孩。

D14.en: He turned like a mannequin to let fellow candidates appreciate the full effect.
D14.zh: 他像模特一样转身，让其他候选人欣赏整体效果。

D7.en: He shooed birds away to save them from his companion's catapult, then fled home.
D7.zh: 他把鸟赶走，让它们躲开同伴的弹弓，然后跑回了家。

M3.en: At 10-12, he felt haunted by the idea that he ought to provide some sort of continuation of the man he was named after.
M3.zh: 10到12岁时，他因觉得自己应该为与他同名的人提供某种延续而感到困扰。

D4.en: At 21-24, he broke his journey to sample a new organ in Stuttgart.
D4.zh: 21到24岁间，他中途停留去试斯图加特的一架新管风琴。

D5.en: At about 10, he boarded a boat under his companion's imperious gaze.
D5.zh: 大约10岁时，他在同伴威严的目光下上了船。

D11.en: At 24, he planned a work called We Epigoni and discussed its theme with friends.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他计划创作《我们这些后辈》，并与朋友讨论主题。

D13a.en: At 25, he became a curate in the Lutheran Church.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他成为路德教会的助理牧师。

D1.en: At 14, he moved from the bottom to near the top of his class between Christmas and Easter.
D1.zh: 14岁时，他在圣诞节到复活节期间从班级末尾升到前列。

D16.en: At 16-18, he became argumentative and challenged unexamined statements.
D16.zh: 16到18岁间，他变得好辩，质疑未经审视的说法。

D18.en: At 16-18, he earned pocket money by tutoring mathematics, which was in demand.
D18.zh: 16到18岁间，他通过教数学赚零花钱，因为数学需求大。

D17.en: He sent his mother and sister on holiday to Switzerland.
D17.zh: 他送母亲和妹妹去瑞士度假。

D8.en: At 18-21, he imperiously demanded his sisters pump the organ bellows while he practiced.
D8.zh: 18到21岁间，他专横地要求妹妹们在他练习时拉风箱。

M1.en: Within a week of his curate's examination, he won his second degree.
M1.zh: 在牧师考试后一周内，他获得了第二个学位。

M6.en: He woke one morning with the conviction that the debt must be repaid.
M6.zh: 他一天早上醒来，坚信债务必须偿还。

D19.en: He summed up in favor of the French system, noting organists often have a free foot but seldom a free hand.
D19.zh: 他总结支持法国系统，指出管风琴手常有空闲的脚，但很少有闲手。

D15.en: At 20-25, he had piercing eyes, dark curly hair, and a strong jaw.
D15.zh: 20到25岁时，他眼睛锐利，深色卷发，下巴方正有力。

D2.en: At 9, she studied flowers in the garden with unusual intensity, noticing overlooked details.
D2.zh: 9岁时，她在花园里异常专注地研究花朵，注意到别人忽略的细节。

D3.en: At 9, she studied flowers until she understood the story of fertilization.
D3.zh: 9岁时，她研究花朵直到理解受精的过程。

D10.en: At 7, fearing tears would show, she ignored the teacher and ran home for solace.
D10.zh: 7岁时，她怕眼泪流下来，没听老师的话，跑回家寻求安慰。

D12.en: At 11, her mother died, and she grew closer to her father.
D12.zh: 11岁时，她母亲去世，她和父亲更亲近了。

D4.en: From ages 4 to 11, she enjoyed gardening: tilling, planting, watering, weeding.
D4.zh: 4到11岁间，她喜欢园艺：翻土、播种、浇水、除草。

D15.en: At 20-21, she played guard on both the freshman and varsity basketball teams.
D15.zh: 20到21岁时，她在新生队和校队都打后卫。

D1.en: At 24-25, she was hardworking and undaunted where others had failed.
D1.zh: 24到25岁时，她在别人失败的地方勤奋不懈。

D14.en: At 20-21, she tried acting, playing a footman in a play by Anthony Hope.
D14.zh: 20到21岁时，她尝试演戏，在安东尼·霍普的剧里演男仆。

D5.en: At 20, she broached the idea of transferring to Berkeley, and made a deal with her father.
D5.zh: 20岁时，她提出转学到伯克利，和父亲达成了协议。

M1.en: At 21, she raised a C in zoology to an A in the second semester.
M1.zh: 21岁时，她把动物学课的成绩从C提高到了A。

D6.en: At 20-23, she took all-day weekend walks with friends at Berkeley.
D6.zh: 20到23岁间，她周末和朋友们在伯克利全天徒步。

D7.en: At age 25, she studied at the University of Zurich and earned a Ph.D. summa cum laude.
D7.zh: 25岁时，她在苏黎世大学学习并获博士学位，成绩优异。

D13a.en: At 23, she said, 'I'll not be the first to disappoint you.'
D13a.zh: 23岁时，她说：“我不会是第一个让你失望的人。”

M4.en: At 23, a discussion helped steer her toward a medical career.
M4.zh: 23岁时，一次讨论帮助她走向了医学事业。

D8.en: At age 20, she insisted she was right and would not change her mind.
D8.zh: 20岁时，她坚持自己是对的，不肯让步。

M7.en: At 18, she believed women and men should have equal educational opportunities.
M7.zh: 18岁时，她认为男女应享有平等的教育机会。

D15.en: At 24, he rushed to join long lines of volunteers that evening.
D15.zh: 24岁时，他当晚冲去排长队加入志愿者队伍。

D6.en: At 21, he led group singing and conducted Sunday religious services.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他带领大家唱歌，并在周日主持宗教仪式。

D3.en: As a small child, he cut flesh from a boiled cow's leg.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他从煮过的牛腿上切下肉。

D14.en: At 8, he announced he was no longer to be called Henry, but Norman.
D14.zh: 8岁时，他宣布不再叫亨利，而要叫诺尔曼。

D5.en: At 24, he lined up with hundreds of men that evening.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他当晚与数百人一起排队。

D1.en: At 25, he turned away no call, day or night, especially for the poor.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他无论白天黑夜从不拒绝出诊，尤其是对穷人。

D7.en: At 19, he laid down rules and expected obedience.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他制定了一套规则，并要求大家遵守。

D18.en: At 17, he worked as a busboy on passenger ships.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他在客轮上做服务生。

M4.en: At 21, he was enthusiastic about the Reading Camp Association's mission.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他对阅读营协会的使命充满热情。

D9.en: At 15, he was elected class representative on the student executive.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他被选为学生执行委员会的班级代表。

D12.en: He grew up as a farm boy in a traditional rural culture.
D12.zh: 他作为农家男孩在传统乡村文化中长大。

D2.en: He roomed with his cousin, sang in the glee club, and worked hard.
D2.zh: 他和表弟同住，参加合唱团，并努力学习。

D7.en: He could not find his mother or be exactly like his father.
D7.zh: 他找不到母亲，也无法完全像父亲。

D3.en: He always kept an eye out for arrowheads and spearheads in the fields.
D3.zh: 他总是在田里留意箭头和矛头。

D4.en: He performed his first operation, opening and draining a soldier's abscess.
D4.zh: 他做了第一次手术，切开并引流一名士兵的脓肿。

D6.en: He became engaged to Edith Roach, sealing it with a diamond ring.
D6.zh: 他与伊迪丝·罗奇订婚，用钻戒作为信物。

D10.en: He tried not to cry on his birthday, went to the fairgrounds alone, and started crying despite himself.
D10.zh: 他生日那天努力不哭，独自去游乐场，还是忍不住哭了。

D5.en: He enlisted as a private and was soon promoted to sergeant in the Canadian Army Medical Service.
D5.zh: 他以列兵身份入伍，很快晋升为加拿大陆军医疗队中士。

D13a.en: He decided to defy his parents and change his career path, telling Rev. Addison he would speak to William Banting himself.
D13a.zh: 他决定违抗父母，改变职业道路，告诉艾迪生牧师他会亲自跟威廉·班廷说。

M1.en: As a teenager, he set unusually high aspirations partly to escape farm chores.
M1.zh: 十几岁时，他树立了异常高的志向，部分是为了逃避农场杂务。

D9.en: At 24, he was promoted to sergeant in the Canadian Army Medical Service.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他在加拿大陆军医疗队被提升为中士。

D1.en: At 17, he considered giving up but decided to stick with it after talking to his principal.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他考虑过放弃，但和校长谈过后决定坚持下去。

M4.en: At 23, intensely patriotic, he wanted to go to war.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他极其爱国，想要参战。

D11.en: He often talked about writing an autobiography and once got about eighty pages into it.
D11.zh: 他常说要写自传，有一次写了大约八十页。

D3.en: He and Watson started teaching each other what they knew.
D3.zh: 他和沃森开始互相教对方自己知道的东西。

D2.en: He won a prize for his work in his second year.
D2.zh: 他在第二年因自己的作品获奖。

D4.en: He had fun constructing the oscillating copper apparatus.
D4.zh: 他制作振荡铜装置时觉得很有趣。

D7.en: At about age 12, he suddenly refused to go to church.
D7.zh: 12岁左右，他突然拒绝去教堂。

D15.en: He spent the first weeks of the war playing squash with his brother.
D15.zh: 战争头几周，他和弟弟在米尔希尔学校的空球场上打壁球。

M1.en: At age 14, he won a scholarship to attend boarding school at Mill Hill.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他获得奖学金去米尔希尔寄宿学校读书。

D14.en: He once gave a talk on how Bohr theory and quantum mechanics explained the periodic table.
D14.zh: 他做过一次演讲，讲玻尔理论和量子力学如何解释元素周期表。

D9.en: At age 24, he was made leader of a team code-named MX, with about a dozen men under him.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他被任命为代号MX的团队负责人，手下有十几个人。

D6.en: Soon they were having lunch together almost every day in the Eagle.
D6.zh: 很快他们几乎每天都在鹰酒吧一起吃午饭。

D6.en: At 12-13, he crossed a river with a friend to fetch soup for school.
D6.zh: 12到13岁时，他和朋友过河去给学校取汤。

D18.en: At 25, he shoveled snow off walks near his rooming house for money.
D18.zh: 25岁时，他在寄宿公寓附近铲雪赚钱。

D5.en: At 25, he told a professor he had to finish in two years due to family and money.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他对教授说因为家庭和钱，他必须在两年内完成。

D7.en: At 18-19, he went to the university registrar and said he needed help or couldn't stay.
D7.zh: 18到19岁时，他去找大学注册官，说需要帮助否则无法留下。

D4.en: At 25, he built a mechanical pulse duplicator to watch them in action.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他造了一台机械脉搏模拟器来观察它们运作。

D12.en: At 25, he seldom got to bed until late, and the phone usually rang even then.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他很少早睡，而且即使睡了电话也常响。

D10.en: At 17, he turned away so someone wouldn't see his tears, but they came anyway.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他转过身不想让人看到眼泪，但眼泪还是流了下来。

D3.en: At 23-25, he helped a professor who had no assistant.
D3.zh: 23到25岁间，他帮助一位没有助手的教授。

D15.en: As a teenager, he ran the mile for his school and was unbeaten in meets.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他代表学校跑一英里，在校际比赛中从未输过。

D2.en: At 25, miserable but determined, he altered the technique slightly on the fourth try.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他虽痛苦但坚定，在第四次尝试时稍微改变了技术。

D13a.en: He told him there would be an eventual hospital fee for her bed and board, but none for the doctor.
D13a.zh: 他告诉他，她的床位和伙食费最终会有，但医生的费用没有。

D1.en: Miserable but determined, he proceeded to the fourth, altering the technique slightly.
D1.zh: 他痛苦但坚定地继续第四次，稍微改变了技术。

D9.en: At 12-13, friends carried him off on their shoulders in triumph.
D9.zh: 12到13岁时，朋友们把他扛在肩上欢呼。

D16.en: He had long talks that went beyond medicine and music to include politics and government.
D16.zh: 他进行了长谈，内容超越医学和音乐，涉及政治和政府。

M1.en: He ran the mile for his school and was unbeaten in track meets with other high schools.
M1.zh: 他代表学校跑一英里，在与其他高中的田径赛中保持不败。

M3.en: He had to learn how to operate this machine and use it at the operating table, like Dr. Lillehei.
M3.zh: 他必须学会操作这台机器并在手术台上使用它，像李拉海医生那样。

M6.en: He knew where he was going at last; his goal lay ahead, two winters away.
M6.zh: 他终于知道自己要去哪里了；他的目标就在前方，还有两个冬天。

M2.en: He returned to Ceres, telling himself this was an experience to be seen through to the end.
M2.zh: 他回到塞雷斯，告诉自己这是一段必须坚持到底的经历。

D20.en: At 25, he and Pikkie alternated operating and administering anesthetic as a team.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他和皮基轮流做手术和麻醉，配合默契。

D20X.en: At 25, he said goodbye to Dr. Louw, feeling he was a real friend.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他和卢医生道别，觉得他是个真正的朋友。

M4.en: At 23-25, he saw a machine as the gateway to advanced surgery.
M4.zh: 23到25岁时，他认为这台机器是通往先进外科手术的大门。

M7.en: He did private work and surgery in the morning, and gave consultation on provincial welfare cases in the afternoon.
M7.zh: 他上午做私人工作和手术，下午为省级福利案件提供咨询。

M8.en: He said goodbye to Dr. Louw, feeling he was a real friend secure enough to give others a chance.
M8.zh: 他向卢医生告别，觉得他是一个真正的朋友，足够自信，愿意给别人机会。

D12.en: He was born in North Adams in 1959, the second of six children.
D12.zh: 他1959年出生于北亚当斯，是六个孩子中的老二。

D3.en: He read widely in anthropology, history, sociology, and political science.
D3.zh: 他广泛阅读人类学、历史、社会学和政治学。

D11.en: He composed a poem called 'The Mango Lady' and read it aloud.
D11.zh: 他创作了一首名为《芒果女士》的诗，并大声朗读。

D6.en: He canceled whatever he could from his schedule to spend more time in Haiti.
D6.zh: 他尽可能取消日程安排，以便在海地多待些时间。

D2.en: He showed up in Cambridge just in time for lab practicums or exams, then went back to Haiti.
D2.zh: 他刚好在实验课或考试时出现在剑桥，然后返回海地。

D18.en: He won a $1,000 prize at Duke for an essay about Haitian artists.
D18.zh: 他在杜克大学因一篇关于海地艺术家的文章赢得1000美元奖金。

M1.en: He left malnourished babies in Haiti and overheard people in Miami talk about losing weight.
M1.zh: 他离开海地营养不良的婴儿，在迈阿密机场听到人们谈论减肥。

D7.en: His classmates nicknamed him Paul Foreigner.
D7.zh: 他的同学给他起了个外号叫保罗·外国人。

D5.en: He stole a real microscope from Harvard Medical School for Cange.
D5.zh: 他从哈佛医学院偷了一台真正的显微镜给坎热。

M3.en: At 20-25, he left peasant huts with malnourished babies and overheard weight-loss talk in Miami.
M3.zh: 20到25岁间，他离开满是营养不良婴儿的农舍，在迈阿密听到减肥话题。

D4.en: He stole a real microscope from Harvard Medical School.
D4.zh: 他从哈佛医学院偷了一台真正的显微镜。

D14.en: He attended a protest vigil at Duke Chapel in 1980.
D14.zh: 1980年，他参加了杜克教堂的抗议守夜活动。

D9.en: He asked the organization for a few thousand dollars to build a bread oven in Cange.
D9.zh: 他向组织要了几千美元在康吉建一个面包烤炉。

D15.en: He was quarterback and starting guard in high school.
D15.zh: 他高中时是四分卫和首发后卫。

D10.en: He said, 'I feel like weeping. The Haitians so deserve this.'
D10.zh: 他说：‘我想哭。海地人太值得这个了。’

D17.en: He left huts with malnourished babies and heard people talk about losing weight.
D17.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的棚屋，听到人们谈论减肥。

M6.en: He left huts with malnourished babies and heard people talk about losing weight.
M6.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的棚屋，听到人们谈论减肥。

D16.en: He said, 'In your particular case, that would be a sin.'
D16.zh: 他说：‘就你而言，那将是一种罪过。’

D1.en: He held onto a dream that seemed impossible.
D1.zh: 他坚持着一个看似不可能的梦想。

D19.en: He left huts with malnourished babies and heard people in Miami talk about losing weight.
D19.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的茅屋，在迈阿密机场听到人们谈论减肥。

D20X.en: He left huts with malnourished babies and heard people in Miami talk about losing weight.
D20X.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的茅屋，在迈阿密机场听到人们谈论减肥。

M2.en: He left huts with malnourished babies and heard people in Miami talk about losing weight.
M2.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的茅屋，在迈阿密机场听到人们谈论减肥。

M5.en: He left huts with malnourished babies and heard people in Miami talk about losing weight.
M5.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的茅屋，在迈阿密机场听到人们谈论减肥。

M8.en: He left peasant huts with malnourished babies and overheard people in Miami discussing weight loss.
M8.zh: 他离开满是营养不良婴儿的农舍，在迈阿密机场听到人们讨论减肥。

D12.en: He delivered prescriptions on his Schwinn bicycle on weekends and during school breaks and summers.
D12.zh: 他周末和假期骑施文自行车送处方药。

D15.en: His love of basketball and baseball began at age nine and intensified as a teenager.
D15.zh: 他九岁开始热爱篮球和棒球，十几岁时更加痴迷。

D6.en: Moving to Dyker Heights introduced him to new friends and organized sports.
D6.zh: 搬到戴克高地让他认识了新朋友和团体运动。

D3.en: As a child, he had books bought or borrowed for him by his mother.
D3.zh: 小时候，他妈妈常给他买书或从图书馆借书给他看。

D2.en: He realized on his own how much he enjoyed school and learning.
D2.zh: 他自己意识到多么喜欢上学和学习。

D18.en: He delivered prescriptions on his Schwinn bicycle on weekends and during school breaks and summers.
D18.zh: 他周末和假期骑施文自行车送处方药。

D7.en: He got into fistfights with guys from other neighborhoods when provoked.
D7.zh: 他被挑衅时会和其他街区的家伙打架。

D9.en: He applied and received a full academic scholarship to Holy Cross in fall 1958.
D9.zh: 他申请并获得了1958年秋季圣十字学院的全额学术奖学金。

D1.en: He spent the summer between junior and senior year at a basketball camp at Riverhead, Long Island.
D1.zh: 他在高二和高三之间的暑假参加了长岛河头的篮球训练营。

M1.en: He graduated first in his class at Cornell Medical School.
M1.zh: 他在康奈尔医学院以班级第一的成绩毕业。

D4.en: He spent a summer pushing a wheelbarrow and hauling bricks, watching medical staff.
D4.zh: 他花了一个夏天推独轮车、搬砖头，看着医护人员进出。

D5.en: He was stopped by a security guard while wearing dirty boots and a hard hat, and said he would be a student there someday.
D5.zh: 他穿着脏靴子和安全帽被保安拦住，他说总有一天他会成为这里的学生。

D17.en: He remembered Dr. Hochstein's teaching to provide care, compassion, and comfort to patients.
D17.zh: 他记得霍克斯坦医生的教导：要给予病人关怀、同情和安慰。

D10.en: He lost his mother to cancer when he was 24.
D10.zh: 他24岁时母亲因癌症去世。

D3.en: He was more interested in Luria's bacteriophages as a tool of genetics than in paramecia.
D3.zh: 他对卢里亚的噬菌体作为遗传学工具比对草履虫更感兴趣。

D2.en: He was more interested in Luria's bacteriophages as a tool of genetics than in paramecia.
D2.zh: 他对卢里亚的噬菌体作为遗传学工具比对草履虫更感兴趣。

D4.en: He spent the afternoon cutting representations of the four bases in stiff cardboard.
D4.zh: 他花了一下午用硬纸板剪出四种碱基的模型。

D6.en: He went with Luria and Dulbecco to Cold Spring Harbor for the summer of 1948.
D6.zh: 1948年夏天，他和卢里亚、杜尔贝科一起去了冷泉港。

D12.en: He and his sister were welcomed home by Nana and given supper when their mother was not home.
D12.zh: 他和妹妹放学回家，妈妈还没下班时，娜娜迎接他们并做晚饭。

D7.en: He stopped going to Mass and instead went bird-watching with his father at age 12.
D7.zh: 他12岁时不再去弥撒，而是和父亲去观鸟。

M1.en: He lasted three broadcasts as a Quiz Kid but was tripped up by questions about Shakespeare and the Old Testament.
M1.zh: 他作为问答小能手坚持了三期节目，但被莎士比亚和《旧约》的问题难住了。

D9.en: His mentors arranged for him to speak at meetings in Bloomington and Oak Ridge at age 20-21.
D9.zh: 他20到21岁时，导师安排他在布卢明顿和橡树岭的会议上发言。

D1.en: His scheme at once came crashing down and left him teetering on a high wire in late February 1953.
D1.zh: 1953年2月下旬，他的方案一下子崩溃，让他像在高空钢丝上摇摇欲坠。

D8.en: He irritated graduate students by steering conversations his way.
D8.zh: 他通过引导对话方向惹恼了一些研究生。

D16.en: He boldly approached Muller, Sonneborn, or Luria after seminars for discussions.
D16.zh: 他大胆地在研讨会后去找穆勒、索内伯恩或卢里亚讨论。

D14.en: He climbed on a table and squatted beside the king, wearing shorts.
D14.zh: 他穿着短裤爬上桌子，蹲在国王旁边。

M4.en: He read out sections of Hershey's letter to an audience of 400.
M4.zh: 他向400名听众朗读了赫尔希信中的部分内容。

D13a.en: He agreed to shorten his hair after his mother insisted.
D13a.zh: 他同意剪短头发，因为母亲坚持。

D10.en: He felt nervous from the attention and needed more confirmation.
D10.zh: 他因受到关注而紧张，需要更多确认。

D12.en: He was born in Catanzaro.
D12.zh: 他出生在卡坦扎罗。

D3.en: He felt they deserved a rest after that unforgettable lesson.
D3.zh: 他觉得在那堂难忘的课后他们理应休息一下。

D4.en: He built the apparatus and worked a bit with the young man.
D4.zh: 他造了那台设备，并和那个年轻人一起工作了一会儿。

D2.en: He learned the basics by studying hard over a summer.
D2.zh: 他通过一个夏天的刻苦学习掌握了基础知识。

D11.en: He said he wanted to be a sailor to travel the world, and join the navy on a destroyer that had visited the previous month.
D11.zh: 他说长大后想当水手环游世界，要上个月来过的那艘驱逐舰上当海军。

D10.en: He had to explain, 'My son is very sick,' and a colleague silently embraced him.
D10.zh: 他不得不解释说：“我儿子病得很重。”一位同事默默地拥抱了他。

D6.en: His mother enrolled him in Catholic Boy Scouts, so he wore the olive-gray uniform.
D6.zh: 他妈妈帮他报名参加了天主教童子军，于是他穿上了灰橄榄色制服。

D5.en: He conspired with two other interns and they decided to perform it secretly at night.
D5.zh: 他和另外两名实习医生密谋，决定在夜间秘密进行。

M1.en: He ended up winning the university prizes for both best graduate and best thesis.
M1.zh: 他最终获得了大学的最佳毕业生奖和最佳论文奖。

D9.en: He conspired with two other interns and they decided to perform it secretly at night.
D9.zh: 他和另外两名实习医生密谋，决定在夜间秘密进行。

D7.en: He conspired with two other interns and decided to perform it secretly at night.
D7.zh: 他和另外两名实习生密谋，决定在夜间秘密进行。

D13a.en: He went to Turin to study medicine.
D13a.zh: 他去都灵学医。

M4.en: He was told he was right to want to do a secret autopsy, more important than hospital rules.
M4.zh: 他被告知，他想做秘密尸检是对的，比医院规定更重要。

D1.en: He conspired with two other interns and decided to perform it secretly at night.
D1.zh: 他和另外两名实习生密谋，决定在夜间秘密进行。

D8.en: He went to someone and told him his thoughts in friendly but firm terms, perhaps with a sharp tone.
D8.zh: 他去找某人，用友好但坚定的语气告诉对方自己的想法，可能语气有点尖锐。

D20.en: He was surprised the cardiologist put his name first on the manuscript, since the idea and machine were his.
D20.zh: 他很惊讶心脏病专家把名字写在手稿第一位，因为想法和机器都是他的。

M2.en: He had a card printed announcing his graduation and prizes, sent to important people in Porto Maurizio.
M2.zh: 他印了卡片宣布毕业和获奖，寄给波多毛里齐奥的重要人物。

D4.en: He examined patients carefully when alive and dissected their brains when dead.
D4.zh: 他活着时仔细检查病人，死后解剖他们的大脑。

D2.en: He supplemented his medical studies at Edinburgh University for two summer sessions.
D2.zh: 他在爱丁堡大学参加了两个夏季学期的医学补充学习。

D3.en: At 17, he studied in the British Museum reading-room.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他在大英博物馆阅览室学习。

D5.en: At 22, he yielded to the urge to travel and obtained the post of Medical Officer for Formosa.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他屈服于旅行的冲动，获得了福尔摩沙的医官职位。

D6.en: He learned their language and made himself popular, long remembered by them.
D6.zh: 他学会了他们的语言，很受欢迎，被他们长久记住。

D7.en: At 20, he couldn't lie about reading the notice, so he stayed.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他无法撒谎说没看过通知，于是留了下来。

D12.en: At 11, he learned the family could get by without his father and saw his mother worry.
D12.zh: 11岁时，他发现家里没有父亲也能过，并看到母亲担忧。

D13a.en: At 12-13, he and a friend silently quit practice and never returned.
D13a.zh: 12到13岁时，他和朋友没告诉任何人就退出了训练，再也没回去。

D2.en: At 21 in 1972, he went to work each day and concentrated on doing his best.
D2.zh: 1972年21岁时，他每天去上班并专注于做到最好。

D3.en: At 10-11, he chose library books about animals, nature, and science.
D3.zh: 10到11岁时，他选了关于动物、自然和科学的图书馆书籍。

D4.en: At 8, he mixed chemicals into concoctions and watched them fizz and foam.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他把化学品混成奇怪的东西，看着它们嘶嘶冒泡变色。

D6.en: At 14-15, he hung out with popular guys and went to their parties and jam sessions.
D6.zh: 14到15岁时，他和受欢迎的朋友一起玩，参加他们的派对和即兴演奏。

D11.en: At 8, while listening, the picture became so vivid he felt he was being chased.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他听着听着，画面变得如此逼真，仿佛自己正在被追赶。

M6.en: At 22 in 1973, he believed God wanted him to be a doctor and had no doubts about being accepted.
M6.zh: 1973年22岁时，他坚信上帝要他当医生，对能被录取毫不怀疑。

D13b.en: At 8, he followed church custom and went to the front when asked, then was baptized weeks later.
D13b.zh: 8岁时，他遵循教会习俗，在牧师邀请时走到台前，几周后受了洗。

D1.en: He beat the streets for two weeks, applying at every business he came across.
D1.zh: 他连续两周走街串巷，去每一家看到的公司应聘。

M2.en: His mother wouldn't let him settle for such a lowly goal in fifth grade.
M2.zh: 他妈妈不愿让他满足于那么低的目标。

D5.en: He cast aside his pride, gave himself a pep talk, and got on the elevator to the executive suites.
D5.zh: 他抛开自尊，给自己打气，然后上了去行政套房的电梯。

D18.en: He stared at a ten-dollar bill, knowing he had to make a choice.
D18.zh: 他盯着桌上的十美元钞票，知道自己必须做出选择。

D10.en: Despair washed over him as memories of fifth grade flashed through his mind.
D10.zh: 绝望淹没了他，五年级的记忆在脑海中闪过。

D9.en: He worked with his crew in a way that broke the rules, but no supervisor commented.
D9.zh: 他带领团队不按规定时间工作，但没有主管过问。

M3.en: He watched Sharper drill the ROTC unit and thought he was incredible.
M3.zh: 他看着夏普训练整个ROTC队伍，觉得他太厉害了。

D8.en: He used his skill at capping on people to make them shape up.
D8.zh: 他用自己怼人的本事让那些人守规矩。

D14.en: He marched at the head of the Memorial Day parade at the end of twelfth grade.
D14.zh: 十二年级结束时，他走在阵亡将士纪念日游行队伍的最前面。

D15.en: At 22, he learned to operate a crane, picking up stacks of steel weighing several tons.
D15.zh: 22岁时，他学会了操作起重机，吊起几吨重的钢堆。

D2.en: At 22, she wrote asking for anatomy and physiology books to start reading.
D2.zh: 22岁时，她写信要解剖学和生理学书籍开始阅读。

D12.en: She was in charge of all other probationers in her set.
D12.zh: 她负责管理同组所有其他实习护士。

M4.en: She believed God was calling for a new Home.
M4.zh: 她相信上帝在召唤建立一个新的家。

M8.en: She was a newly committed Christian, waiting to know what to do.
M8.zh: 她是一个新归信的基督徒，等待知道该做什么。

D9.en: At 14-18, she became head of her house at boarding school.
D9.zh: 14到18岁时，她成为寄宿学校宿舍的舍长。

D1.en: At 21-25, her back couldn't handle heavy lifting, so she quit nursing on doctor's advice.
D1.zh: 21到25岁时，她的背承受不了重体力活，听从骨科建议放弃了护理。

D13b.en: At 22, she accepted his third marriage proposal.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，她接受了他的第三次求婚。

D7.en: At 21, she decided to disregard her family, leave Oxford, and become a nurse.
D7.zh: 21岁时，她决定不顾家人反对，离开牛津去当护士。

D6.en: At 25-26, she joined the Socratic Society, a group run by C. S. Lewis.
D6.zh: 25到26岁时，她加入了由C.S.刘易斯组织的苏格拉底学会。

D20.en: As a child, she and Lilian organized 'the aunts' party'.
D20.zh: 小时候，她和莉莲组织“阿姨派对”。

D17.en: At 20-25, she spent nights with Packie to ease his pain so he could sleep.
D17.zh: 20到25岁时，她晚上去陪帕基，帮他缓解疼痛好让他睡个好觉。

M1.en: At 22, she was ambitious and determined to conquer her own shortcomings and conceal her shyness.
M1.zh: 22岁时，她雄心勃勃，决心克服自己的缺点并隐藏害羞。

D13a.en: Between ages 18 and 25, she defied her father's advice to study medicine, first feeling no urge, later feeling physically unable.
D13a.zh: 18到25岁间，她违背了父亲让她学医的建议，起初没兴趣，后来觉得身体吃不消。

D19.en: As a child and young adult, she was efficient.
D19.zh: 幼年到青年时期，她做事高效。

D2.en: At 17, he decided to beat his soft body into shape until he could lick anyone in class, and he did.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他决定把软弱的身体练强壮，直到能打败班上任何人，他做到了。

D12.en: As a child, he was expected to take over as the only son.
D12.zh: 幼年时，作为独子，他被期望将来接班。

D13a.en: As a child, it was taken for granted he would carry on the family tradition into its fourth generation.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，他被认为理所当然会延续家族传统到第四代。

D15.en: At 17, he excelled in gymnastics after deciding to beat his soft body into shape.
D15.zh: 17岁时，他决定把软弱的身体练强壮，结果体操很出色。

M1.en: At 18, he suddenly moved to the head of his class at the University of Prague, staying there until graduation in medicine.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他突然在布拉格大学成为全班第一，并保持到医学毕业。

M2.en: As a child, it was taken for granted he would carry on the family tradition into its fourth generation.
M2.zh: 幼年时，他被认为理所当然会延续家族传统到第四代。

D3.en: At 9-10, she attended a lecture on how a river begins.
D3.zh: 9到10岁时，她听了一场关于河流如何起源的讲座。

D4.en: At 21, she finished a dress she had started making a year and a half before.
D4.zh: 21岁时，她完成了一件一年半前开始做的裙子。

D7.en: At 22, she stood up to a man and they had a first-class row.
D7.zh: 22岁时，她顶撞了一个男人，他们大吵了一架。

D1.en: At 10-11, she was gaining top place almost every week by the end of the summer term.
D1.zh: 10到11岁时，到夏季学期末她几乎每周都拿第一名。

D13a.en: At 22, she regarded her year with Norrish as a personal defeat.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，她认为在诺里什手下的一年是个人失败。

M1.en: At 17, she went to Cambridge to sit entrance exams in physics and chemistry.
M1.zh: 17岁时，她去剑桥参加物理和化学的入学考试。

D2.en: Rosalind Franklin set out to prove herself despite lack of encouragement.
D2.zh: 罗莎琳德·富兰克林在缺乏鼓励的情况下决心证明自己。

D9.en: At 18, she called everyone she knew to get opinions and chose Newnham.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她打电话给所有认识的人征求意见，选择了纽纳姆学院。

D10.en: At 19, irritated by meaningless rituals and false alarms, she sought a confrontation.
D10.zh: 19岁时，她对无意义的仪式和虚假警报感到恼火，寻求了一次对抗。

M4.en: At 17, she witnessed her parents' total unanimity in refugee relief work.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她目睹了父母在难民救济工作中完全一致。

M5.en: At 15, she decided what to do with her life and carried it out without deflections.
M5.zh: 15岁时，她决定了人生目标，并毫无偏离地实现了它。

M6.en: As a child and young adult, she had a deep sense of vocation.
M6.zh: 幼年到青年时期，她有强烈的使命感。

M7.en: At 22, she had something that met her own, her father's, and her country's requirements.
M7.zh: 22岁时，她拥有了满足自己、父亲和国家要求的东西。

M8.en: At 15, she made up her mind about her life and carried it out without deflections.
M8.zh: 15岁时，她决定了人生目标，并毫无偏离地实现了它。

D13b.en: At 18, she entered Cambridge in 1938.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，她于1938年进入剑桥大学。

D5.en: At 22, she risked giving up her scholarship.
D5.zh: 22岁时，她冒险放弃了奖学金。

D14.en: At 22-26, she sewed bright patch pockets on her lab coats.
D14.zh: 22到26岁间，她在实验服上缝了鲜艳的贴袋。

D16.en: At 20, she said, 'I am not one of the people.'
D16.zh: 20岁时，她说：“我不是那种人。”

D18.en: At 19, she said she made a frightful mess of exams.
D18.zh: 19岁时，她说自己考试考砸了。

D17.en: At 22, she put herself out to give her sister a good time.
D17.zh: 22岁时，她尽力让妹妹玩得开心。

D19.en: At 20, she said she knew exactly where everything was.
D19.zh: 20岁时，她说她知道每样东西的确切位置。

D20X.en: At 25, she talked herself out of returning to Cambridge.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，她说服自己不回剑桥。

D12.en: At 25, he spent all his savings on a honeymoon in Venice and Dubrovnik.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他把所有积蓄都花在了威尼斯和杜布罗夫尼克的蜜月上。

D3.en: At 24, he proved that scientific dogma about hormones and the mature ovary was wrong.
D3.zh: 24岁时，他证明了关于激素和成熟卵巢的科学教条是错误的。

D9.en: At 24, he suggested working together on reproductive hormones of giant and dwarf mice.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他提议合作研究巨鼠和侏儒鼠的生殖激素。

D13a.en: At 11, his mother insisted he accept a place at Manchester Central High School.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他母亲坚持让他接受曼彻斯特中央中学的录取。

M3.en: As a teenager, he admired four scientists: Rutherford, Mendel, Mendeleev, and Luke Howard.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，他崇拜四位科学家：卢瑟福、孟德尔、门捷列夫和卢克·霍华德。

M1.en: As a child, he and his brothers were competitive, determined to win or go down fighting.
M1.zh: 小时候，他和兄弟们有竞争精神，决心赢或战败。

D4.en: At 24, he injected giant mature mice as an afterthought.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他顺便给巨型成年小鼠注射了药物。

D7.en: In the 1960s, he was in open conflict with a colleague, refusing to be browbeaten.
D7.zh: 20世纪60年代，他与一位同事公开冲突，拒绝被威吓。

D14.en: At 16, he played the organ at St Mary's Church.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在圣玛丽教堂弹奏管风琴。

D2.en: At 16, he spent hours each week practicing the organ.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他每周花好几个小时练习管风琴。

D12.en: He took his notebook to the city to study, trembling with fear.
D12.zh: 他带着笔记本进城学习，心里害怕得发抖。

D6.en: He was elected into the Union Boat Club.
D6.zh: 他被选入联合划船俱乐部。

D2.en: He took his finished notebook to the city to study.
D2.zh: 他带着刚写完的笔记本进城学习。

D3.en: He found Tubercle Bacilli in a patient's urine at the hospital and was pleased.
D3.zh: 他在医院病人的尿液里发现了结核杆菌，非常高兴。

D4.en: He secretly plucked a dried seed sample at the Arnold Arboretum.
D4.zh: 他在阿诺德植物园偷偷摘了一个干种子样本。

D19.en: He neglected his scrapbook due to lack of time to search for clippings.
D19.zh: 他因为没时间找剪报而忽略了剪贴簿。

D10.en: He took his notebook to the city to study, but with fear and trembling.
D10.zh: 他战战兢兢地拿着笔记本去城里学习。

D15.en: He rowed in 'Kelpie' up to the 2nd bridge, beginning to feel at home in a shell.
D15.zh: 他划着‘Kelpie’号到第二座桥，开始对赛艇感到自如。

D14.en: As a teenager, he performed a tumbling routine in a charity show.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他在一场慈善演出中表演了一套翻滚动作。

D11.en: At 21, he neglected his scrapbook due to lack of time for clippings.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他因没时间剪报而忽略了剪贴簿。

D9.en: At 16, he and four other boys each contributed 50 cents for a Christmas gift.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他和四个男孩每人凑了50美分买圣诞礼物。

M1.en: At 22, he published a paper on intracranial tension.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他发表了一篇关于颅内压的论文。

D5.en: He took his notebook to the city to study, fearing its loss as it held a term's work and he had nearly lost it before.
D5.zh: 他把笔记本带到城里学习，担心丢失，因为那是一个学期的成果，而且他之前差点丢过一次。

D1.en: At 23, he etherized a patient for a breast operation.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他为一位乳腺手术患者实施乙醚麻醉。

D17.en: At 21, he took Grove to Century Magazine offices to comfort him.
D17.zh: 21岁时，他带格罗夫去《世纪杂志》办公室让他安心。

D8.en: At 21, he took Grove to Century Magazine offices to comfort him.
D8.zh: 21岁时，他带格罗夫去《世纪杂志》办公室让他安心。

D13a.en: At 23, he went off anyway.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他还是走了。

D18.en: He neglected his scrap book due to lack of time to search for clippings.
D18.zh: 他因没时间找剪报而忽略了剪贴簿。

D20.en: He was president of his class during senior year.
D20.zh: 他在高年级时担任班长。

M2.en: He hoped to prove himself worthy of his father.
M2.zh: 他希望证明自己配得上父亲。

M7.en: He congratulated himself for the opportunity to meet men on the same level in a Senior society.
M7.zh: 他庆幸有机会在高年级社团中与同水平的人交往。

D20X.en: He made vague plans with Grosvenor Atterbury to open an office in New York, but they fell through.
D20X.zh: 他和格罗夫纳·阿特伯里模糊计划在纽约开办公室，但未实现。

D16.en: He was assigned to Dr. Gannett's Auscultation division based on alphabetical order.
D16.zh: 他按字母顺序被分到甘尼特医生的听诊组。

M5.en: He finished his medical paper after complications with a friend's rheumatic fever required more writing.
M5.zh: 他完成了医学论文，但因朋友风湿热并发症而多写了内容。

M6.en: He spent Easter vacation trying to write a clinical paper on children's diseases without much success.
M6.zh: 他复活节假期试图写一篇儿童疾病临床论文，但不太成功。

M8.en: He saw the foundling hospital run by Sisters of Charity.
M8.zh: 他参观了由慈善修女会管理的育婴堂。

D18.en: As a teenager, he studied in a private school and later worked as a clerk in his father's money shop.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他在私塾念书，后在父亲的钱铺当伙计。

D2.en: At age 23-26, he interned with the Japanese 31st Infantry Regiment and entered the Japanese Army Academy.
D2.zh: 23到26岁间，他在日本弘前步兵第三十一联队实习，后进入日本陆军士官学校。

D3.en: At age 22-26, he attended several speeches by Sun Yat-sen while studying in Japan.
D3.zh: 22到26岁间，他在日本留学时听过孙中山几次演讲。

D4.en: He fostered his fourth son Yan Zhimin's interest in radio.
D4.zh: 他着意培养四子阎志敏对无线电的兴趣。

M4.en: At age 24, he returned to Shanxi with a mission from the Tongmenghui and transported bombs for an assassination plot.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他带着同盟会使命回山西，运送炸弹给暗杀巡抚的密谋者。

D9.en: At age 23, he traveled with Zhao Daiwen to promote revolution among students, teachers, merchants, and monks.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他和赵戴文一起向学生、教师、商人和僧侣宣传革命。

D5.en: At age 17, he secretly pawned his stepmother's jewelry to raise funds for activities.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他偷偷典当继母的首饰换成钱作为活动经费。

D6.en: At age 22, he joined the Tongmenghui introduced by Gu Sishen.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他经谷思慎介绍加入中国同盟会。

D19.en: He managed a business for over a decade, making it the top in the town.
D19.zh: 他管理该号十数年，使其生意成为全镇之冠。

M1.en: He worked while observing society, waiting for a chance to rise.
M1.zh: 他一边工作一边观察社会，等待飞黄腾达的机会。

D20.en: He was appointed as a student squad leader.
D20.zh: 他被任命为学生班长。

D11.en: He compiled a revolutionary army drill manual and tactics.
D11.zh: 他编著了革命军操典与革命军战法。

M6.en: He believed only revolution could save China.
M6.zh: 他认为只有革命才能救中国。

D13a.en: Yan Huiqing handled divorce procedures with Qu Peihuan.
D13a.zh: 阎慧卿与曲佩环办理了离婚手续

D13b.en: Yan Xishan told her about Liang's situation, and she gladly agreed, finalizing the matter.
D13b.zh: 阎锡山告知梁的情况，她欣然答应，事情就此定下。

D17.en: At age 24, he invited a doctor to treat Qu Peihuan and stayed to care for him with a nurse.
D17.zh: 24岁时，他请医生给曲佩环诊治，并留下与护士一起照看。

D4.en: He induced his father to buy him a microscope when none was available from the university.
D4.zh: 他让父亲给他买了一台显微镜，因为大学里没有可用的。

D7.en: He turned down the opportunity to emigrate because of his fondness for a girl, Emmy Fraatz.
D7.zh: 他因为喜欢一个叫艾米·弗拉茨的女孩，拒绝了移民的机会。

D1.en: As a fresh student of 22, he had already produced two substantial papers.
D1.zh: 22岁时，作为新生，他已经写出了两篇重要的论文。

M1.en: He won a prize of 30 ducats for carrying out a difficult anatomical study.
M1.zh: 他因完成一项困难的解剖研究而获得了30达克特的奖金。

D5.en: He considered becoming a ship's doctor to see the world, but gave up the idea.
D5.zh: 他考虑过当船医去看世界，但最终放弃了这个想法。

D12.en: He moved to Langenhagen as physician for a mental hospital.
D12.zh: 他搬到朗根哈根，担任一家精神病院的医生。

D6.en: At 14-15, he became engaged to Emmy Fraatz.
D6.zh: 14到15岁间，他与埃米·弗拉茨订婚。

D15.en: He served in the military at age 19-20.
D15.zh: 他19到20岁时在军队服役。

D15.en: At 18, he marched carrying a rifle, 150 rounds of ammunition, and other gear on a long march.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他肩上荷步枪一枝，腰间绑150发子弹，背着羊毡、水壶等，负重长途行军。

D1.en: At 9, he and his brother plotted to outwit the bully; one afternoon, he pushed him down stone steps, causing injuries.
D1.zh: 9岁时，他与弟弟密谋斗智，一日下午放学，他乘毛长林下石阶时从后用力一推，使其滚下受伤。

D4.en: At 18, while on sentry duty in heavy snow, he quickly became covered in snow.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他担任步哨，大雪纷飞，顷刻间变成了一个雪人。

D9.en: At 18, he joined the Guangxi Student Army Dare-to-Die Corps of 120 men, following the Northern Expedition.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他参加广西学生军敢死队，共120人随军北伐。

D6.en: At 14, he was admitted to the Guilin Army Primary School.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他考入桂林陆军小学。

D13b.en: At 19-23, he was admitted to Baoding Military Academy's third class, receiving a complete military education.
D13b.zh: 19到23岁间，他考入保定军校三期，接受完整的军事教育。

M6.en: At 18, he participated in the Wuchang Uprising of the Xinhai Revolution and witnessed the birth of the Republic.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他参加辛亥革命武昌起义，见证了民国的诞生。

D12.en: At 10, his education was supported by his brother-in-law selling beef and his sister doing needlework after his father's death.
D12.zh: 10岁时，父亲早逝，家道贫寒，靠姐夫卖牛肉和姐姐做针线供他读书。

D14.en: At 19, he recited a poem while overlooking the Yangtze River, feeling inspired to pacify the world.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他登楼吟诗，俯仰大江南北，顿生澄清海宇之志。

D12.en: At 18, he took on family responsibilities after elders died, arranged for siblings, and returned to the Weihaiwei camp.
D12.zh: 18岁时，长辈接连去世，他承担起家庭重任，安排好弟妹后重返威海卫军营。

D1.en: At 18, he became tougher and more resolute after family losses, then returned to the Weihaiwei camp.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他变得更加坚韧冷峻，安排好弟妹后毅然重返威海卫军营。

D2.en: At 21, he excelled in the entrance exam and entered the Beiyang Military Academy, specializing in artillery.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他以优异成绩考入北洋武备学堂，专攻炮科。

D5.en: At 24, he studied German military theory and technology while keeping his queue.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他拖着辫子学习德国先进的军事理论和军事技术。

D3.en: At 25, he interned at a top arms factory, learning advanced artillery manufacturing and usage.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他在世界一流兵工厂实习半年多，熟悉了先进火炮的制造和使用技术。

D4.en: At 25, he entered the Krupp factory during its boom period.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他进入克虏伯炮厂，正值该企业蓬勃发展时期。

D7.en: At 24, he declared he would rather be seen as a madman than suffer humiliation, and cut his hair.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他说宁可被视为狂人也不愿受屈辱，剪了自己的头发。

D13a.en: At 24, he felt as if waking from a dream after listening to Yinchang's advice.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他听了荫昌的劝导，方如大梦初醒。

D18.en: At 17, he relied on his uncle to get a position as a clerk in the Weihaiwei camp.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他投靠堂叔，补上了一名哨书。

M1.en: At 20, he passed the academy entrance exam with top scores thanks to his sharpness and Confucian foundation.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他凭着机敏精悍和儒学功底，以名列前茅的成绩考取了该学堂。

M2.en: At 15, he wanted to emulate his grandfather and achieve military glory to honor the family.
M2.zh: 15岁时，他想像祖父一样在军中建功立业光大门楣。

D12.en: At 18, he decided to solve his financial problems by translating books and articles for fees.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他决心靠翻译书籍和文章赚稿费来解决费用问题。

D2.en: At age 9-10, he finished studying the Four Books and Five Classics.
D2.zh: 9到10岁时，他学完了《论语》《孟子》《大学》《中庸》和《诗经》《尚书》。

D3.en: At 14-15, he and another person joyfully explored a room full of bookcases.
D3.zh: 14到15岁时，他和另一个人在书院小房间里徜徉在书海中。

D18.en: At 10, he began collecting ancient books and artworks.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他开始购置和收藏古书与金石书画。

D19.en: Between ages 17-47, he selected rare books from his collection to publish as fine editions.
D19.zh: 17到47岁间，他选择藏书中的珍品刊行善本。

D6.en: At 24, he met a middle-aged man in a long gown while walking in a hotel courtyard.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他在旅馆庭院散步时遇到一个穿长衫的中年人。

D13a.en: His father named him Guangxu according to clan genealogy rules.
D13a.zh: 父亲按宗谱辈分给他取名光煦。

D7.en: At 19, he copied a school charter, deliberately writing '字' instead of '是' to mock the authorities.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他抄了一份求是章程，故意把「是」字错写成「字」来讽刺学校当局。

D14.en: At age 6-7, he would grandly 'tell stories' to tea drinkers at the town teahouse.
D14.zh: 六七岁时，他常到镇上茶馆大模大样地为喝茶的人说书。

D5.en: At 22, he fled his hometown to join Yuan Shikai's Beiyang Army in Tianjin to avoid danger.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他为了避祸背井离乡，到天津投了袁世凯的北洋军。

D9.en: At 23, he rented a room and taught a temporary preparatory class for military students by himself.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他租了一间房，独自办了一个临时的士官预备班并担任教师。

D1.en: At 18, he resolved to earn money by translating books and articles to fund his studies.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他决心靠翻译书籍和文章赚取稿费来解决费用问题。

M1.en: At 19, he ranked first in both entrance exam essays at a military academy.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他在军校入学考试的两篇论文中都得了第一名。

D4.en: At 24, his excellent performance in Japan caught the attention of Zhejiang governor Zhang Zengyang.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他在日本的优异表现引起了浙江巡抚张曾敭的注意。

D11.en: At 21, he wrote a powerful inaugural essay that became a famous piece.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他写了一篇气势磅礴的发刊词，成为传诵一时的名篇。

M4.en: At 21, he actively participated in the anti-Russian rally with Cai E and others.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他和蔡锷等人积极参加了拒俄大会。

D20.en: At 20-21, he drafted the association's charter and managed magazine editing and publishing.
D20.zh: 20到21岁间，他草拟了同乡会会章，负责杂志的编辑和出版事宜。

M7.en: Between 18 and 23, he passionately expressed his love for his country with four billion people and 5,000 years of civilization.
M7.zh: 18到23岁间，他发自肺腑地呐喊，深爱着拥有四万万同胞和五千年文明史的祖国。

D16.en: At 23, he gave engaging lectures on military education and systems with his eloquence and expertise.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他凭借深厚的理论功底和好口才，讲解入学须知和日本军事教育，讲课妙趣横生。

D17.en: At 14, he cut his own flesh to save his mother's life.
D17.zh: 14岁时，他割肉疗亲，救了母亲一命。

M2.en: At 22, he prepared to pass the imperial exam to bring honor to his family.
M2.zh: 22岁时，他准备金榜题名，光宗耀祖。

D15.en: He said, 'I exercise every morning; see how much stronger I am.'
D15.zh: 他说：“我每晨锻炼体格，你看我不是比以前的身体结实多了吗？”

D12.en: He used his household management experience to run the company's mess, earning praise and promotion.
D12.zh: 他利用管家经验管理连队伙食，受到称赞并升为副排长。

D18.en: At 12, he managed household shopping better than his uncle, making lists and buying accurately.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他管理家务比舅父高明，列清单采购很少出错。

D2.en: As a teenager, he focused on sports and learned rifle shooting from a teacher he admired.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他重武轻文，跟体育老师学会了步枪射击要领。

D7.en: He faced family opposition when he wanted to join the army in Guangzhou.
D7.zh: 他想要去广州从军，遭到全家极力反对。

D5.en: At 19-20, he eagerly joined peers to go to Guangdong and apply for Whampoa Military Academy.
D5.zh: 19到20岁时，他响应同乡倡议，决定去广东考黄埔军校。

D9.en: At 19, he and 10 other Shaanxi youths took a British steamer south to Guangzhou.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他和10位陕籍青年搭乘英国轮船南下广州。

D4.en: He daily bought cheap vegetables and cooked with the mess crew, improving the company's meals.
D4.zh: 他每天买菜并下厨做饭，改善了全连伙食。

D10.en: He felt deeply moved and judged his past debauchery as unworthy of a revolutionary soldier.
D10.zh: 他深受感动，自认狎邪冶游违背革命军人品质。

D13a.en: He asked his father to study in Beijing; his father agreed on condition he not join the army.
D13a.zh: 他请求去北京学习，父亲答应但条件是不许当兵。

M2.en: He remained loyal to Chiang Kai-shek throughout, seeing him as teacher, commander, and benefactor.
M2.zh: 他对蒋介石一贯忠贞，视其为校长、长官和靠山。

M1.en: He always tried to complete tasks perfectly for Chiang Kai-shek, even at his own expense.
M1.zh: 他总是尽心竭力完成蒋介石的任务，不惜牺牲自己。

D6.en: At 19, he and 10 other Shaanxi youths took a British ship south to Guangzhou.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他和10位陕籍青年搭乘英国轮船南下广州。

D19.en: He was praised for his tidy dorm but once wrongly blamed for a messy mosquito net that was his neighbor's.
D19.zh: 他因内务整洁受表扬，但一次被错怪蚊帐乱，其实是和同学换错了。

D8.en: He punished soldiers harshly for minor mistakes, making them run until exhausted and carrying others' guns.
D8.zh: 他训练士兵时稍有不对就罚跑步，跑不动还得帮别人扛枪，不顺意就大骂。

D17.en: He made soldiers pay for any damage to villagers' property, sometimes covering the cost himself.
D17.zh: 他要求损坏老百姓东西必须赔偿，有时自己掏钱垫上。

D1.en: He faced a pay crisis where the whole company had to buy food with their own savings, and everyone wanted to beat him.
D1.zh: 全连军饷没了，官兵用自己积蓄买米买菜，大家都想揍他。

D3.en: He resolved to either learn English to study abroad or join the army to train elite troops.
D3.zh: 他立志要么学好英语出洋看世界，要么投笔从戎训练精兵。

D13b.en: He married Cao Xiuqing in a grand ceremony at his family home in August 1923.
D13b.zh: 1923年8月，他和曹秀清在杜家张灯结彩中成婚。

D15.en: At 21, he suffered from scabies all over his body.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他长了一身疥疮。

M3.en: At 20-21, he admired Chiang Kai-shek immensely at Whampoa, believing him to be the revolutionary leader.
M3.zh: 20到21岁时，他在黄埔军校时对蒋介石佩服得五体投地，认为他是革命的领袖。

D11.en: As a child, he loved playing with sticks and clubs.
D11.zh: 他从小就喜欢舞枪弄棒。

D14.en: At 20-21, he was a top student at Whampoa, studying hard and leading in grades.
D14.zh: 20到21岁时，他在黄埔军校刻苦钻研，成绩遥遥领先。

D12.en: As the eldest child, he took on heavy farm and housework from a young age.
D12.zh: 作为家中老大，他从小承担繁重的农活和家务。

D3.en: He learned about Sun Yat-sen's revolutionary ideas and renamed himself Xue Yue, vowing to save the nation like Yue Fei.
D3.zh: 他了解了孙中山的革命思想，改名薛岳，决心像岳飞那样救国。

D7.en: At 18, he and nine sworn brothers barged into the principal's office to demand joining the revolution.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他和九个盟兄弟闯入校长室，要求参加革命。

D5.en: At 14, he begged his mother to let him study; she made him grind all the grain first, which he did in freezing winter.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他恳求母亲让他求学；母亲让他先碾完谷物，他在寒冬中完成了。

D2.en: He worked day and night to grind all the autumn rice into white rice and then chopped firewood.
D2.zh: 他夜以继日地碾完秋收稻谷，又上山砍柴。

D6.en: At 16-18, nine classmates, impressed by his courage, made him their leader and they became sworn brothers.
D6.zh: 16到18岁时，九名同学因他胆识过人推他为领袖，结拜为兄弟。

D9.en: At 16-18, his classmates elected him their leader because of his courage.
D9.zh: 16到18岁时，同学们因他胆识过人推他为领袖。

D4.en: During a night drill, he led classmates to ambush the mock enemy, routing them.
D4.zh: 夜间演习时，他带领同学设伏，击溃了假想敌。

D15.en: He worked day and night grinding grain and chopping firewood.
D15.zh: 他夜以继日地碾米和砍柴。

D18.en: As the eldest child, he did farm work and household chores from a young age.
D18.zh: 作为家中老大，他从小干农活和家务。

M1.en: At 16, he ranked 32nd in the entrance exam and entered Whampoa Army Primary School.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他考试排第32名，进入黄埔陆军小学学习。

M4.en: At 18, he and his sworn brothers discussed joining the revolution together.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他与张发奎等盟兄弟商议，共约参加革命。

M6.en: As a teenager, he renamed himself Xue Yue, vowing to save the nation like Yue Fei.
M6.zh: 十几岁时，他改名薛岳，决心像岳飞那样救国救民。

D20.en: At 16-18, his classmates pushed him to be their leader and they became sworn brothers as the 'Stone' family ten brothers.
D20.zh: 16到18岁间，同学推他为小领袖，结拜为石家十兄弟。

D12.en: At 15, he cut off his queue, said goodbye to his mother, and left home for He County.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他剪掉辫子，辞别母亲，离家前往和县。

D2.en: At 15, he studied hard to prove himself in military school despite poor health.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他虽体弱但努力学习，要用军事成绩让同学刮目相看。

D18.en: At age 9, Wei Lihuang, as a poor child, might have been stuck in Weiyang Village without education.
D18.zh: 9岁时，卫立煌作为穷孩子，不读书可能一辈子困在卫杨村。

D7.en: At 15, he cut off his queue and joined the revolutionary tide.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他毅然剪掉辫子，投身革命洪流。

D5.en: At 21, he rallied his troops to launch a surprise attack on the enemy headquarters.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他号召弟兄们出其不意端掉敌军司令部。

D15.en: At 22, he grew a mustache to look older, earning the nickname 'Whiskers Wei'.
D15.zh: 22岁时，他蓄起胡须显得老成，得绰号“卫胡子”。

D9.en: At 21, he rallied his troops to launch a surprise attack on the enemy headquarters.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他号召弟兄们出其不意端掉敌军司令部。

D4.en: At 15, he practiced disassembling and reassembling his rifle during guard duty, surpassing veterans.
D4.zh: 15岁时，他站岗时拆装枪械，速度超过老兵。

D3.en: At 15, he practiced disassembling and reassembling his rifle during guard duty, surpassing veterans.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他站岗时拆装枪械，速度超过老兵。

D1.en: At 16, he ran off to Wuhu to join the Restoration Army.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他一个人跑到芜湖投奔光复军。

M4.en: At 20, he was thrilled to get the chance to work by Sun Yat-sen's side.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他日夜期盼的机会来了，到孙中山身边工作让他兴奋不已。

D13b.en: At 17, he was readily admitted to the student soldier camp due to his combat experience.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他凭战斗资历被学兵营痛快录取。

D16.en: At 20, he stated his ideal: to follow Sun Yat-sen and seek revolutionary truth.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他简单扼要地说出自己的理想：跟随孙中山寻求革命真理。

D19.en: At 15, he was strict in his duties and never left his post.
D19.zh: 15岁时，他苛于职守，从不离开岗位。

D17.en: At age 20, Wei Lihuang's mother bought 100 hatching eggs for him at high cost, walking through many villages.
D17.zh: 20岁时，卫立煌母亲高价为他买来100个孵鸡蛋。

D20X.en: At 18, he resisted high office and chose to go to Guangzhou after graduation.
D20X.zh: 18岁时，他抵拒高官厚禄，毕业后毅然选择去广州。

M1.en: At 15, he worked hard in military studies to prove himself despite poor health.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他非常努力，要用军事成绩让同学刮目相看。

M2.en: At 15, he studied hard to earn respect through military grades.
M2.zh: 15岁时，他努力学习，要用军事成绩让同学刮目相看。

M3.en: At 20, he eagerly awaited the chance to work beside Sun Yat-sen.
M3.zh: 20岁时，他日夜期盼到孙中山身边工作的机会。

M5.en: At 15, he studied hard to earn respect through military grades.
M5.zh: 15岁时，他努力学习，要用军事成绩让同学刮目相看。

M6.en: At 20, he eagerly awaited the chance to work beside Sun Yat-sen.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他日夜期盼到孙中山身边工作的机会。

M7.en: At 20, he eagerly awaited the chance to work beside Sun Yat-sen.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他日夜期盼到孙中山身边工作的机会。

M8.en: At 20, he eagerly awaited the chance to work beside Sun Yat-sen.
M8.zh: 20岁时，他日夜期盼到孙中山身边工作的机会。

D13a.en: At 18, he married his first wife as his mother ordered.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他遵照母命娶了首位夫人。

D2.en: He could hum Yu Shuyan's entire opera 'Da Gun Chu Xiang' without missing a word.
D2.zh: 他能一字不差地哼唱余叔岩的《打棍出箱》。

D12.en: He invited Miss Zhao to Shenyang, saying she could study at Northeastern University.
D12.zh: 他电邀赵四小姐北上，说可以就读东北大学。

D5.en: At 21, he attempted suicide by gun after a military defeat, but Gu Ruiyu stopped him.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他战败后想开枪自杀，被谷瑞玉抱住制止。

D13b.en: He married at age 16; his bride was two years older.
D13b.zh: 他16岁成婚，新娘比他大两岁。

D11.en: He loved calligraphy and painting, and spent heavily to collect famous works.
D11.zh: 他酷爱书画，年轻时不惜重金罗致名人法书和绘画。

D3.en: He learned English, joined YMCA, and became interested in Western culture.
D3.zh: 他学习英语，参加基督教青年会，对西方文化产生浓厚兴趣。

D13a.en: He had no choice but to lead troops to resist.
D13a.zh: 他唯有率兵抵抗之一途。

D20.en: At 23, he was appointed commander of the 1st and 3rd Joint Army.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他被任命为第一、三联军司令。

M1.en: He rose from major to deputy commander-in-chief of the national forces.
M1.zh: 他从少校升到全国陆海空军副总司令。

D9.en: He rose from major to deputy commander-in-chief of the national forces.
D9.zh: 他从少校升到了全国陆海空军副总司令。

D10.en: He felt only despair and sorrow from the victory.
D10.zh: 他从这次胜利中只感到绝望和悲哀。

D18.en: He originally wanted to study medicine but joined the military academy at 19.
D18.zh: 他原本想学医，但19岁进了讲武堂从军。

D15.en: He was told to train his body well as a soldier.
D15.zh: 他被告知当军人要把身体锻炼好。

D20X.en: He passed the exam for Baoding Military Academy but did not attend.
D20X.zh: 他考上了保定军官学校但没去。

D4.en: He watched many representative Peking opera performances.
D4.zh: 他观赏过很多场代表性的京剧节目。

D16.en: He quoted a poem by Wu Meicun to lament the state of society and criticize current affairs.
D16.zh: 他引用了吴梅村的诗来感慨世风，针砭时事。

D17.en: He opposed marrying his wife's niece while she was dying.
D17.zh: 他反对在妻子病重时娶她的侄女。

D19.en: At 18, he boasted of his strong memory, still able to recite key lessons from his youth.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他夸自己记忆力强，还能背下功课重点。

M2.en: At 24, he declared he would not betray his father for profit, as he could not betray friends.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他说不能背弃朋友之义，岂会见利忘义背叛父亲。

M6.en: In his 20s, as a Christian, he believed events were God's arrangement, not superstition.
M6.zh: 20多岁时，他作为基督徒，认为这些事是上帝的安排，不是迷信。

M7.en: In his 20s, he lamented that war kills the best, leaving the incompetent to claim rewards.
M7.zh: 20多岁时，他感叹战争打死佼佼者，无能后辈请功受赏。

M5.en: At 18, his father promised him a battalion commander post if he graduated from military academy.
M5.zh: 18岁时，他父亲答应他讲武堂毕业就给他当营长。

M3.en: At 18, he befriended Guo Songling, who also took a liking to him.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他和郭松龄结成朋友，郭也看中了他。

M8.en: In his 20s, as a Christian, he believed events were God's arrangement, not superstition.
M8.zh: 20多岁时，他作为基督徒，认为这些事是上帝的安排，不是迷信。

D8.en: At 24, he discerned the enemy's weaknesses and attacked them fiercely in battle.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他在作战中洞悉敌人弱点，狠狠攻击其侧翼。

D14.en: At 20, he was young and successful, full of pride and high spirits.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他少年得志，意气扬扬。

D12.en: At 6, he studied alongside his two older brothers in a family school.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他给大哥应桢、二哥应禄当伴读。

D2.en: At 24, during military internship, he strove to live and train exactly like a Japanese soldier, mastering all combat scenarios.
D2.zh: 24岁时，在入伍实习期间，他尽力让自己完全像日本士兵一样生活训练，熟悉所有战时动作。

D5.en: At 21, he did not desert but negotiated with Japanese authorities for leave to return to China.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他没有擅自离校，而是与同学向日本政府交涉，获准后才回国。

D4.en: At 15-16, out of curiosity, he took down his father's beloved copper gun from the wall and played with it.
D4.zh: 15到16岁时，他出于好奇，取下父亲心爱的铜炮枪摆弄。

D1.en: As a small child, he repeatedly tried to ride a black bull, getting thrown off and injured, but kept trying.
D1.zh: 幼年时，他一次次试图骑上黑牯牛，被甩下受伤，但仍继续尝试。

D6.en: At 16-17, he was easygoing and diligent in public chores, gradually gaining favor from teachers and classmates.
D6.zh: 16到17岁时，他为人随和，勤于公共杂务，逐渐得到师生好感。

D10.en: At 12, waking to find his mother still praying for him, he was deeply moved and became more filial.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他醒来见母亲仍在祈求保佑，深为感动，从此更加孝顺。

D15.en: At 15-16, he woke around 6 am daily to run and do calisthenics on the playground.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，他每天六点左右起床，到操场跑步、做柔软体操。

D18.en: At 4-5, he ran barefoot with brothers and friends, learning to gather firewood and pigweed.
D18.zh: 四五岁时，他光脚与哥哥们满山跑，学拾柴、扯猪草。

D19.en: At 15-16, he insisted on finishing that day's homework completely before the next day.
D19.zh: 15到16岁时，他坚持当天的功课必须当天做完做好，不留到明天。

M4.en: At 16-17, he saw applying to the Army Primary School as a way to save the nation.
M4.zh: 16到17岁时，他把投考陆军小学当作救国的一条出路。

M7.en: At 16-17, he saw applying to the Army Primary School as a way to save the nation.
M7.zh: 16到17岁时，他把投考陆军小学当作救国的一条出路。

D13b.en: At 17-18, he was described as absolutely obedient to orders and strict rules.
D13b.zh: 17到18岁时，他被描述为绝对服从命令、严守堂规而不敢越雷池一步的人。

M1.en: At 20, he ranked first in the exam for overseas study in Japan.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他应陆军部考选留日学生试以第一名应选。

D7.en: At 20, he joined the Tongmenghui.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他加入了同盟会。

D12.en: At 10-12, he and his brother became thin and grave beyond their years.
D12.zh: 10到12岁时，他和弟弟变得瘦弱、老成，远超实际年龄。

D2.en: At 20, he spent the summer drudging over classics for official exams.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他整个夏天苦读经典，准备科举考试。

D3.en: At 23-24, he read Montesquieu's Spirit of Laws, one of the first foreign books in Chinese.
D3.zh: 23到24岁时，他读了孟德斯鸠的《法意》，这是最早译成中文的外国书籍之一。

D1.en: At 20, with help from Xi Pin-san, he finally changed his father's mind to let him attend new school.
D1.zh: 20岁时，在席聘三的帮助下，他终于让父亲改变态度，同意他去顺庆府上新学堂。

D7.en: At 19, he urged his family to allow him to study at the new school in Shunching.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他恳求家人允许他到顺庆府的新学堂读书。

D15.en: At 23, he marched twenty hours to evade numerically superior forces.
D15.zh: 23岁时，他行军二十小时以躲避数量占优的敌军。

D18.en: At 12-13, he and his younger brother planted part of their land with opium poppy.
D18.zh: 12到13岁时，他和弟弟在部分新地上种了罂粟。

M1.en: At 21, he graduated after a glorious year.
M1.zh: 21岁时，辉煌的一年过去，他毕业了。

M2.en: At 21, he felt he had betrayed the ancient virtue of filial piety.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他觉得自己背叛了孝道这一古老美德。

M4.en: At 23, he took the blood oath of loyalty and was initiated.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他歃血为盟，宣誓效忠。

D4.en: As a small child, he played every musical instrument he could find.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他玩遍了能找到的各种乐器。

D9.en: At 25, he held long talks with soldiers about hated loans.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他与士兵长谈，讨论可恨的贷款。

D13b.en: At 25, he served as adjutant and personal servant to a company commander.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他担任连长副官兼勤务兵。

D11.en: As a small child, he shook branches to make petals shower down.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他摇晃树枝让花瓣如雨般落下。

D17.en: As a small child, he gathered dying birds and cried over them.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他捧起垂死的小鸟，为它们哭泣。

D6.en: Throughout his childhood and youth, he heard tales told and retold.
D6.zh: 童年和青年时期，他反复听人讲述那些故事。

D10.en: At 21, he lay awake all night re-evaluating his life.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他彻夜未眠，重新审视自己的人生。

D13a.en: At 18-20, he wrote a letter lying about passing exams to study in Chengtu.
D13a.zh: 18到20岁间，他写信谎称能通过考试去成都读书。

D14.en: At 19, he knelt in gratitude before his old teacher and foster parents.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他跪在恩师和养父母面前表达感激。

D16.en: At 21, he made his first public speech.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他做了第一次公开演讲。

D12.en: At 23, he abandoned his studies to join the army, driven by a desire to serve his country.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他投笔从戎，立志报国。

D2.en: At 21, he stayed in camp on New Year's Day to study Han Yu's essay and recited it perfectly by evening.
D2.zh: 21岁时，元旦他独留营中请人讲解《原道》，当晚背诵无误。

D3.en: At age 21, he stayed in camp on New Year's Day to study Han Yu's 'Yuan Dao' and recited it perfectly by evening.
D3.zh: 21岁时，元旦独留营中请人讲解韩愈《原道》，当晚背诵无误。

D10.en: His dying mother craved pears and meat.
D10.zh: 母亲弥留时，想吃梨和肉。

D15.en: At 15-18, he fought alone and knocked two men to the ground, beating them badly.
D15.zh: 15到18岁时，他独自与人格斗，打倒两人痛打一顿。

D5.en: At 15-18, he fought alone, knocked two to the ground, and beat them severely.
D5.zh: 15到18岁间，他独自打斗，将两人打倒在地痛揍一顿。

D4.en: At 19, he carried people across rivers on his back despite his superior's complaints.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他仗义背人过河，不顾长官申斥。

D13a.en: At 15, after his father scolded him, he quit drinking for life.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，被父亲严厉责骂后，他终身戒酒。

D9.en: At 15-18, he urged fellow soldiers to quit gambling, whoring, smoking, and drinking.
D9.zh: 15到18岁时，他苦劝同营弟兄戒嫖赌烟酒。

D7.en: At 22, he drew his sword against a superior who threatened him with a knife, causing the man to flee.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他拔刀相向，吓跑动刀砍人的哨官。

D16.en: At 15-18, he marched and shouted alone, earning the nickname 'Foreign Dim Sum' from fellow soldiers.
D16.zh: 15到18岁时，他自顾自地边走边喊，被同伍士兵讥笑，送他绰号‘外国点心’。

D18.en: He sold leftover gunpowder strips and bought meat at the east gate.
D18.zh: 他把六枚钱加上卖药条得的十余枚，跑上东关去买些肉。

M4.en: He fired several shots at a church's wooden plaque to vent anger.
M4.zh: 他举起枪向着教堂的木匾连放数响，以泄一口气。

M7.en: He said being killed by foreigners for the country is a great honor.
M7.zh: 他说被外国人打死，是为国而死，荣幸之极。

M1.en: He topped exams in both academics and drills, winning 47 yuan for reciting 47 books.
M1.zh: 每逢年中考试，他无论读书或兵操都名列第一，曾因背诵四十七册课本得奖四十七元。

D1.en: He swore never to gamble again, vowing to cut off his hand if he did.
D1.zh: 他深觉赌博之害，立誓终身不再赌，否则砍掉自己的手。

D17.en: He cared for two sick men day and night, tending to their every need.
D17.zh: 他把那两人安置于小室，昼夜服侍，煎药看护无微不至。

D13b.en: He served as a battalion commander under Wu Luzhen in the Sixth Division.
D13b.zh: 清末他在第六镇吴禄贞部下任管带。

D12.en: He was appointed to replace a company commander who resigned due to cowardice.
D12.zh: 他接替了因临阵畏缩而辞职的连长职务。

D4.en: He learned basic textile techniques, from sizing to weaving, in half a year.
D4.zh: 他半年内学到了从下水浆纱到上机织布的纺织初步技术。

D15.en: At 13-14, he competed with adults by carrying a heavy load of firewood.
D15.zh: 他十三四岁时好胜心强，挑了一担和成年人几乎一样多的柴。

D1.en: He refused offers to join militia units, considering himself a regular officer.
D1.zh: 他自认是正式军官，谢绝了加入民军的好意。

D2.en: He shared hardships with soldiers, maintaining discipline by example.
D2.zh: 他以身作则，与士兵同甘共苦，维持了纪律。

D3.en: With his mother's consent, he went to the provincial capital to attend a new primary school.
D3.zh: 他得到母亲同意，跟李先生上省城进了新创立的临桂县立两等小学。

D13a.en: He hesitated and dared not enter his grandmother's kitchen to ask for oil.
D13a.zh: 他因害怕外婆的冷酷面孔，在厨房外徘徊不敢进去。

D18.en: He learned basic textile techniques, from sizing to weaving, in half a year.
D18.zh: 他半年内学到了从下水浆纱到上机织布的纺织初步技术。

D6.en: At 25, he was close friends with Li Qizhao, a fellow townsman.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他与同乡李其昭过从甚密。

D10.en: He burst into loud crying.
D10.zh: 他嚎啕大哭起来。

M1.en: At 13-14, he loaded a huge load of firewood like the adults, driven by competitiveness.
M1.zh: 13到14岁时，他好胜心强，装了一大担柴，和成年人一样多。

M7.en: As a child, he hoped the things he saw and heard would never happen again in China.
M7.zh: 幼年时，他希望自己耳闻目睹的事永不再现于中国。

D7.en: At 25, he refused to join the militia, considering himself a regular officer.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他谢绝了民军的邀请，认为自己是正式军校出身。

D9.en: At 25, he ordered the flag bearer to raise the company flag and led the charge.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他命令掌旗兵举起连旗，率领全连冲锋。

M4.en: At 18, he joined the Tongmenghui, signing a pledge and swearing blood oath despite the risk of death.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他加入同盟会，填志愿书、歃血为誓，不怕杀身之祸。

D5.en: At 25, he was ordered to disarm a county with 200-300 troops, a dangerous task.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他奉命收缴海康县武器，对方有二三百人，危险至极。

D14.en: At 24, he often performed daring stunts on the drill ground for students.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他常在操场上给学生做惊险表演。

D20.en: At 25, he formally took command as company commander.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他正式接任本连连长。

D4.en: At 23, he jumped off Luo Liuguan's boat and traveled overnight.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他跳下罗六十老倌的船，连夜赶路。

D5.en: At 25, he sneaked to a friend's, changed into student clothes, and joined a protest.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他偷偷跑到朋友家换上学生装，走进游行队伍。

D20.en: At 23, he was one of nine members of the Poor Relief Society.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他是救贫会九名成员之一。

D1.en: At 24, he changed his name to Peng Dehuai and entered the military academy.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他改名彭德怀，考入讲武堂。

D13a.en: At 24, he married Liu Ximei and taught her to read and write.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他与刘细妹结婚，教她识字读书。

M4.en: At 25, he sneaked to a friend's, changed into student clothes, and joined a protest.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他偷偷跑到朋友家换上学生装，走进游行队伍。

D9.en: He and four others drafted four rules for a poverty relief society, including equal land rights and abolishing unequal treaties.
D9.zh: 他和郭得云等四人起草了救贫会议的四条章程，包括灭财主、平均地权等。

D13b.en: He rose from private to acting regiment commander through every rank in the old army.
D13b.zh: 他从二等兵一步步升到代团长，走过了旧军队里的每一级阶梯。

D3.en: At 12, he studied old books at night and borrowed novels like The Strange State of the World Witnessed Over Twenty Years.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他常在晚上温习旧书，借阅了《二十年目睹之怪现状》等小说。

D7.en: At 23, he sent three members to secretly execute a local bully, was arrested, and escaped en route to Changsha.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他派会员秘密处决恶霸，被捕后在押送途中逃脱。

D6.en: At 21, he befriended over twenty soldiers and students, and with Huang Gonglue and Li Can pledged to save the nation.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他结交二十多个士兵和学生，与黄公略、李灿相约救国救民。

D14.en: At 23, he sympathized with a singing girl at a banquet, raised 200 silver dollars to ransom her, and helped her return home.
D14.zh: 23岁时，他同情陪酒少女，筹款二百银元赎出并送其回乡。

D2.en: At 24, he changed his name to Peng Dehuai, entered Hunan Army Lecture Hall, and studied military subjects for ten months.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他改名彭德怀，考入湖南陆军讲武堂，学习十个月。

D18.en: When he couldn't find work, he carried coal from a pit over ten li away to sell at Zhao's market.
D18.zh: 揽不到活儿时，他就到十多里外的御河沟煤场挑煤炭，担到赵家场街上叫卖。

D2.en: At 20, he practiced military drills repeatedly, sometimes dozens or hundreds of times for one move.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他反复练习军事动作，有时一个动作练几十上百次。

D3.en: At 20, he read ancient military classics like Sunzi and Wuzi, keeping them on his desk.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他广泛阅读《孙子》《吴子》等古代兵书，案头必备。

D13b.en: At 18, he obeyed his mother and married Cheng Yizhi, though he was unhappy about it.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他奉母命与程宜芝结婚，虽不满但只得同意。

D6.en: As a child, he led other cowherds to graze cattle on rich folks' land, being tall and brave.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他个子高胆子大，带着放牛娃们去富人家的荒坡放牛。

D7.en: At 15, he told a debt collector, 'I'm poor but proud; I'll repay every cent.'
D7.zh: 15岁时，他对逼债的赵二娘说：“我人穷骨头硬，欠的钱日后一定还。”

D9.en: At 24, he led about 400 people in an uprising, forming the 4th Detachment of the Sichuan Eastern National Protection Army.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他集合约400人起义，编为川东护国军第四支队。

D11.en: As a child, he told stories from Romance of the Three Kingdoms and Water Margin to other cowherds.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他给放牛娃们摆龙门阵，讲《三国演义》《水浒传》故事。

D15.en: As a child, he practiced throwing pebbles at a tree trunk every morning until it had a hole.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他每天清晨用鹅卵石投树，树干被打出一个洞穴。

D19.en: As a child, he practiced calligraphy by tracing characters on his palm to save paper.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他练毛笔字时用食指在手心里写，以节省纸张。

M1.en: At 12-13, he ranked first in every subject due to diligence and intelligence.
M1.zh: 12到13岁时，他勤奋好学，各科成绩都是第一名。

M4.en: At 13-15, he read revolutionary works that broadened his view of the world.
M4.zh: 13到15岁时，他读了《革命军》《天演论》等，开阔了眼界。

M7.en: At 15-18, he felt his ideals conflicted with the corrupt Qing dynasty.
M7.zh: 15到18岁时，他感到自己的理想与腐朽的清王朝格格不入。

D4.en: At 19, he cut off his queue and joined the student army against the Qing.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他毅然剪掉辫子，参加了反对清朝的学生军。

D5.en: At 19, he said, 'A real man wields a sword to save the people, not seek wealth.'
D5.zh: 19岁时，他说：“大丈夫当仗剑拯民于水火，岂顾一身之富贵！”

D1.en: At 21, he led a charge and captured enemy positions, earning promotion to company commander.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他率先冲锋攻占敌阵，因功升为连长。

D10.en: At 24, despite severe pain, he insisted on holding a memorial for fallen soldiers.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他忍着剧痛力主召开阵亡将士追悼会。

D12.en: At 15, he had to drop out and farm after his father died.
D12.zh: 15岁时，因父亲病故，他被迫辍学务农。

D8.en: At 25, he represented his division in negotiations and met brigade commander Zhu De.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他代表第五师去谈判，结识了旅长朱德。

D2.en: At age 7, he helped clean the table and did homework by lamplight made from bamboo.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他帮妈妈收拾碗筷，用竹精子点灯做功课。

D3.en: At 7, he helped clean the table and studied by the light of a homemade bamboo torch.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他帮妈妈擦桌子，然后用竹精子点灯做功课。

D4.en: At age 24, he was appointed commander of the second escort battalion for river transport.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他被任命为护航第二营营长。

D6.en: As a child aged 7-11, he led friends in a game called 'Beat the Foreign Devils'.
D6.zh: 7到11岁时，他带领小朋友玩“打洋鬼子”游戏。

D7.en: At age 25, he drafted a telegram denouncing Chen Jiongming's rebellion.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他起草讨陈电文，历数陈逆罪行。

D9.en: At age 16-18, he was elected president of the student council and drafted its constitution.
D9.zh: 16到18岁时，他被选为学生自治会会长，制定《学生会章程》。

D14.en: At age 16-18, he played several instruments and was a skilled storyteller.
D14.zh: 16到18岁时，他会拉胡琴、打洋琴、弹钢琴、吹喇叭，还擅长讲古书。

D13a.en: At age 18, he refused to return to school, writing that heroes often come from the grassroots.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他拒绝返校，回信说自古英雄多出自草莽。

D18.en: He drafted a letter to Deng Zeru seeking guidance and assistance for revolutionary work.
D18.zh: 他起草致邓泽如函，恳求指示和帮助进行革命工作。

D1.en: At age 19, he followed advice to seek a living in Southeast Asia, traveling via Hong Kong.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他听从劝告到南洋谋生，经香港乘船前往。

D17.en: At 21, he jumped into a river fully clothed to rescue a drowning child.
D17.zh: 21岁时，他衣服没脱就跳进河里救起一名落水儿童。

D12.en: At 23, he borrowed money to bury his father, then returned to his army post.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他借钱办了父亲的丧事，然后返回粤军工兵营任职。

D13b.en: In his 20s, he passed a review that cleared his record.
D13b.zh: 20多岁时，他接受审查，被认为历史清楚，通过了。

M4.en: At 21, he resolved to follow Sun Yat-sen and become a revolutionary soldier.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他立志毕业后追随孙中山，做革命军人。

D12.en: He saved one dollar daily from his stipend for a year to fund a classmate's study abroad.
D12.zh: 他每天从公费里省一块钱，一年存了三百六十元寄给同学做出国旅费。

D2.en: He spent most of his time in the library or lab, often studying late into the night.
D2.zh: 他课余大多在图书馆念书或做实验，有时念到深夜不休息。

D5.en: He fell from a seesaw when his classmate stood up, rupturing his ureter.
D5.zh: 他从跷板上摔下来，输尿管被砸破了。

M4.en: He participated in the May Fourth Movement parade as a Boy Scout and camped out two nights.
M4.zh: 他以童子军身份参加五四天安门游行，露宿两夜。

M1.en: He feared lagging behind American classmates, so he studied in the library most of his free time.
M1.zh: 他怕成绩不如美国同学，课余大多在图书馆念书。

D13a.en: He opposed his arranged marriage but obeyed his parents and went through with the wedding.
D13a.zh: 他反对包办婚姻，但不敢违抗父母，还是拜堂成亲了。

D18.en: He saved money from his stipend for a friend while living on bread himself.
D18.zh: 他每天省一块钱寄给同学，自己啃面包过活。

D1.en: He left the hospital for exams but his wound inflamed, forcing him to repeat a year.
D1.zh: 他勉强出院考试，伤口发炎没考完，只好多读一年。

D6.en: His American friend Otis Oberholt recalled him as his closest and most intimate friend.
D6.zh: 他的美国好友欧塔士·奥伯莱特回忆说，孙将军是他最知己的朋友。

D8.en: He overheard a classmate insulting players, grabbed him, and threatened to punch him until he apologized.
D8.zh: 他听到同学骂球员像猪，抓住他举手要揍，直到对方讨饶。

D7.en: He defied his father and secretly wrote to the Chinese embassy to apply for military school in the US.
D7.zh: 他不顾父亲反对，偷偷写信向中国大使馆申请去美国军校。

D15.en: As a college basketball star, he joined the North China team for national tryouts.
D15.zh: 他以清华篮球校队主将身份，与北师大球员组队参加全国选拔赛。

M2.en: He felt that as an industrially backward country, they must cherish foreign-made weapons.
M2.zh: 他深感中国工业落后，依赖外国械弹，所以要特别珍惜。

D4.en: He fell from a seesaw when his classmate ran off at the bell.
D4.zh: 他骑在跷板一端，同学听到铃声跑去上课，把他摔了下来。

D17.en: He saved one dollar daily from his stipend for a year to help a friend's study abroad.
D17.zh: 他每天从公费中省一块钱，一年存三百六十元寄给同学作旅费。

D16.en: He overheard a classmate insulting players, grabbed him, and threatened to punch him until he apologized.
D16.zh: 他听到同学骂球员像猪，抓住他举手要揍，直到对方讨饶。

D14.en: He led his college basketball team to 45 consecutive wins and a regional championship.
D14.zh: 他率领清华篮球队连胜四十五场，获得华北各大学冠军。

D20X.en: He felt that as an industrially backward country, they must cherish foreign-made weapons.
D20X.zh: 他深感中国工业落后，依赖外国械弹，所以要特别珍惜。

M6.en: He felt that China, as an industrially backward country, must cherish foreign-supplied weapons.
M6.zh: 他深感中国是工业落后国家，要珍惜外国供应的武器。

M7.en: He felt that China, as an industrially backward country, must cherish foreign-supplied weapons.
M7.zh: 他深感中国是工业落后国家，要珍惜外国供应的武器。

M8.en: As a Christian, he felt responsible to make himself available for questions and help in his new environment.
M8.zh: 作为基督徒，他觉得有责任在新环境中让人随时向他提问和求助。

D19.en: He used bamboo rods, brushes, and cloths of various sizes to clean guns.
D19.zh: 他用竹签、毛刷和大小方布擦枪。

D11.en: He felt that China, as an industrially backward country, must cherish foreign-supplied weapons.
D11.zh: 他深感中国是工业落后国家，要珍惜外国供应的武器。

D3.en: As a small child, he was quiet and studious, memorizing texts silently after a few readings.
D3.zh: 幼年时他沉静好学，默读几遍就能记住塾师教的文章。

M3.en: He felt that China, as an industrially backward country, must cherish foreign-supplied weapons.
M3.zh: 他深感中国是工业落后国家，要珍惜外国供应的武器。

M5.en: He felt that China, as an industrially backward country, must cherish foreign-supplied weapons.
M5.zh: 他深感中国是工业落后国家，要珍惜外国供应的武器。

D12.en: At 14, his father became a copyist, and his mother rented land to support the family.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他父亲当了抄写员，母亲租种菜地贴补家用。

D2.en: At 15, he entered Chengdu A-class Industrial School to study dyeing and weaving.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他考入成都甲种工业学校读染织专业。

M1.en: At 18, he passed the exam for a government-funded work-study program in France.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他考取官费留法勤工俭学生。

D4.en: At 19, he was assigned to work at a Schneider factory outside Paris.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他被分配到巴黎克里西门外的一个施奈德工厂工作。

D3.en: At 21, studying these things made the October Revolution's application to China clearer to him.
D3.zh: 21岁时，学习了这些东西，十月革命道路之应用于中国在他心中更加明确起来。

D9.en: At 20, he co-founded a joint committee of work-study students in Paris to lead struggles.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他和蔡和森等发起在巴黎组织各地勤工俭学生联合委员会作为最高领导机关。

M4.en: At 25, he attended a memorial rally with Li Dazhao and gave a speech promoting communism.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他与李大钊出席纪念大会并发表演说宣传共产主义。

D7.en: At 22, he published an article attacking warlords, angering Yang Sen, and was expelled from Sichuan.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他发表抨击军阀的文章惹怒杨森，被礼送出川。

D6.en: At 22, he became close friends with a young woman who visited him at the newspaper office.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他与一位到报馆拜访他的年轻女子成了亲密朋友。

D16.en: At 25, he spoke at a Sun Yat-sen memorial rally at Tsinghua, promoting communism and denouncing imperialism and warlords.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他在清华园纪念孙中山逝世周年大会上发表演说，宣传共产主义，痛斥帝国主义和军阀。

D15.en: At 15-16, he was a famous football forward, known as one of the 'Five Tigers' in Chengdu student circles.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，他踢得一脚好球，是成都学界有名的‘五虎’之一。

D14.en: At 20, he was the only one who could write in vernacular, busy drafting declarations and bulletins all day.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他是同学中唯一能写白话文的人，整天忙着起草宣言和通讯。

D1.en: At 20, he said he returned to Shanghai in November 1921, still undecided about revolution and wavering again.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他说1921年11月回到上海，还没下定决心干革命，又经历了一次动摇。

D13a.en: At 20, he said he returned to Shanghai in November 1921, still undecided about revolution and wavering again.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他说1921年11月回到上海，还没下定决心干革命，又经历了一次动摇。

D20.en: At 22, he was appointed party branch secretary of the Sino-French University, one of 18 branches in Beijing's western district.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他担任中法大学支部书记，是北京地委西部区18个支部之一。

D11.en: At 23, he wrote poetry, essays, fiction, translations, and reviews for New Shu Newspaper and Beijing publications.
D11.zh: 23岁时，他为《新蜀报》和北京各报刊写诗歌、散文、小说、译作和评论。

D3.en: At 22-23, he read Marxist works like The Communist Manifesto and journals from China.
D3.zh: 22到23岁间，他阅读了《共产党宣言》等马克思主义著作和国内传来的《向导》等刊物。

D2.en: As a teenager, he carved a motto on his desk and studied late into the night by oil lamp.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他在课桌上刻下座右铭，每晚在小油灯下读书到深夜。

D4.en: At 14-18, he and others secretly smashed and removed the idols, leaving a note.
D4.zh: 14到18岁时，他和别人悄悄打碎搬走菩萨，并留了张字条。

D7.en: At 20, he encouraged classmates to break through the school gate, leading 200 to rush out.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他鼓励同学们冲出去，带领200多人冲出校门。

D9.en: At 20, he joined protests, distributed leaflets, and shouted slogans against Japanese goods.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他参加游行集会，撒传单，喊口号抵制日货。

D5.en: At 20, he endured a two-day storm in the Mediterranean with mines and waves.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他在地中海遭遇两天两夜大风暴，还有水雷。

D18.en: At 20, he raised 300 silver dollars for travel expenses with his uncle's help.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他在舅父帮助下筹足300银元路费。

M1.en: At 19, he said, 'We must take serving the country as our foundation and achieve glory for the rise of the Chinese nation.'
M1.zh: 19岁时，他说：“我辈要以报国为根本，为中华民族的崛起而建功立业。”

M4.en: At 20, he joined protests, distributed leaflets, and shouted slogans against Japanese goods.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他参加游行集会，撒传单，喊口号抵制日货。

M7.en: At 19, he declared his commitment to serve the nation and contribute to the rise of the Chinese people.
M7.zh: 19岁时，他宣称要以报国为根本，为中华民族的崛起建功立业。

D15.en: At 20, he encountered a severe storm in the Mediterranean that lasted two days and nights.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他在地中海遇到持续两天两夜的大风暴。

D1.en: At 22, he left France for Belgium to attend a preparatory class at the Charleroi Labor University.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他离开法国前往比利时，进入沙洛瓦劳动大学补习班。

D20.en: At 25, he was elected head of the Paris liaison office of the Chinese Nationalist Party.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他被选为国民党驻巴黎通讯处处长。

D12.en: At 8, he followed his mother around with a bucket and sponge.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他提着桶和海绵跟在妈妈后面。

D15.en: At 23, he broke both arms playing football.
D15.zh: 23岁时，他踢足球摔断了两条胳膊。

D2.en: At 23, he composed a list of traits of a future general.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他列出了一份未来将军的特质清单。

D13b.en: At 18-19, he spent a year at Virginia Military Institute preparing for West Point.
D13b.zh: 18到19岁间，他在弗吉尼亚军事学院待了一年，为西点军校做准备。

D1.en: At 19, he failed math and was turned back to the next class.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他数学没及格，被留级到下一班。

D6.en: At 24, he gave Beatrice a photo of himself in a first classman uniform.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他送给比阿特丽斯一张自己穿一等兵制服的照片。

D14.en: At 16, he was a water sprite in a play where Beatrice had the lead.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他在比阿特丽斯主演的剧里演水精灵。

D3.en: Between 11 and 17, he developed a photographic mind that compensated for his dyslexia.
D3.zh: 11到17岁间，他练出了照相式记忆，弥补了阅读障碍。

D7.en: At 21, he insisted on wearing his father's silk opera hat with a tuxedo to a dance.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他坚持戴着父亲的丝绸歌剧帽配燕尾服去舞会。

D9.en: At 24, he wrote a second letter to the adjutant, candidly acknowledging his mistake.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他给副官写了第二封信，坦率承认了自己的错误。

M1.en: At 19, he aspired to become the first general in his class.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他立志成为班上第一位将军。

D4.en: As a teenager, he developed a photographic memory that compensated for his dyslexia.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他培养了照相式记忆，弥补了阅读障碍。

D17.en: At 8, he followed his mother with a bucket and sponge, and recalled she fainted from the smell.
D17.zh: 8岁时，他提着桶和海绵跟着母亲，回忆说气味太臭她晕倒了。

D10.en: At 23, he sat still for an hour, then ran around the hills like a loon.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他一动不动坐了一小时，然后像疯子一样在山上跑。

D18.en: At age 10, his mother sold all family possessions to fund a journey.
D18.zh: 10岁时，母亲卖掉所有家产以筹措旅费。

M2.en: At 12, he said his mother let him take his father's full name, and he hoped to be worthy of it.
M2.zh: 12岁时，他说母亲让他用父亲的全名，他希望配得上它。

D13a.en: At 12, with his mother's approval, he changed his middle name from William to Smith.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，在母亲同意下，他把中间名从威廉改为史密斯。

D16.en: At 13, his considerable oratorical skills first became evident.
D16.zh: 13岁时，他出色的口才首次显现。

D5.en: At 23, he asked to try something because he was curious how it would feel.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他请求尝试某物，因为好奇感觉如何。

D11.en: As a teenager, he wrote essays about ancient warriors he learned about from his father and aunt.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他写文章关于从父亲和姑妈那里学到的古代战士。

M3.en: He worshipped his ancestor-heroes, inspired by his father and aunt.
M3.zh: 他崇拜祖先英雄，受父亲和姑妈启发。

D19.en: He developed a photographic mind that compensated for his dyslexia.
D19.zh: 他培养了照相式记忆，弥补了阅读障碍。

D20.en: He joined twenty-five other children of Southern California gentry.
D20.zh: 他加入了南加州上流社会的其他二十五个孩子。

D20X.en: At age 11-12, he joined twenty-five other children of Southern California gentry.
D20X.zh: 11到12岁时，他加入了南加州上流社会的其他二十五个孩子。

M4.en: He was driven and obsessed to prove himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M4.zh: 他受驱使并痴迷于证明自己配得上巴顿血统。

M5.en: He was driven and obsessed to prove himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M5.zh: 他受驱使并痴迷于证明自己配得上巴顿血统。

M6.en: He was driven and obsessed to prove himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M6.zh: 他受驱使并痴迷于证明自己配得上巴顿血统。

M7.en: He was driven and obsessed to prove himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M7.zh: 他受驱使并痴迷于证明自己配得上巴顿血统。

M8.en: He was driven and obsessed to prove himself worthy of his Patton heritage.
M8.zh: 他受驱使并痴迷于证明自己配得上巴顿血统。

D9.en: He showed clear leadership skills in team sports.
D9.zh: 他在团队运动中展现了清晰的领导能力。

D7.en: He set fire to a fellow cadet's shirt, causing burns.
D7.zh: 他点燃了同学的衬衫，导致对方烧伤。

D2.en: He eagerly learned all he could about his profession.
D2.zh: 他热情地学习所有关于他职业的知识。

D15.en: He played rugby for the XV against Woolwich in 1907.
D15.zh: 1907年，他代表橄榄球十五人队对阵伍尔维奇。

D5.en: He set fire to a fellow cadet's shirt, causing burns.
D5.zh: 他点燃了同学的衬衫，导致对方烧伤。

D13a.en: Frequent beatings with a cane did not deter him.
D13a.zh: 频繁的藤条殴打并没有吓倒他。

D1.en: He worked hard for six months, determined to pass high.
D1.zh: 他努力了六个月，决心以高分通过。

D6.en: He was very happy at St. Paul's School.
D6.zh: 他在圣保罗学校非常快乐。

D20.en: He became captain of the Rugby XV and Cricket XI.
D20.zh: 他成为了橄榄球十五人队和板球十一人队的队长。

M1.en: He passed into Sandhurst halfway up the list.
M1.zh: 他考进桑赫斯特军校，成绩排在中游。

M3.en: He worshipped his father as a saint.
M3.zh: 他崇拜父亲，视其为圣人。

D12.en: He learned early to stand or fall on his own.
D12.zh: 他很小就学会靠自己成败。

D3.en: He had long talks about the Army's flaws and mastering the military art.
D3.zh: 他与人长谈陆军的问题和如何掌握军事艺术。

D12.en: At 20, he became a teacher and felt a sense of social responsibility as an enlightener.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他当了先生，意识到自己作为启蒙者的社会责任。

D2.en: At 22-23, he diligently studied military manuals and took careful notes.
D2.zh: 22到23岁时，他认真听讲军事课程并细心作业。
