D4.en: At 10-13, he climbed a tree to break off pine cones to test if gods existed, and nothing happened.
D4.zh: 10到13岁时，他爬树掰松盔想试试有没有神灵，结果没事。

D3.en: As a teenager, he enjoyed reading Chinese folk novels like Romance of the Three Kingdoms.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他喜欢看《三国演义》等中国民间小说。

D5.en: At 10-13, he climbed a tree to break off pine cones to test if gods existed, and nothing happened.
D5.zh: 10到13岁时，他爬树掰松盔想试试有没有神灵，结果没事。

D6.en: At 22-23, he joined the Youth Military Union under Jiang Xianyun's influence.
D6.zh: 22到23岁时，他在蒋先云影响下参加了青年军人联合会。

D9.en: At 10-13, he often raced friends in tree climbing, choosing hard trees and leaving easy ones for opponents.
D9.zh: 10到13岁时，他常拉同伴比爬树，专选难爬的树，把容易的留给对手。

D7.en: At 22, he questioned the principal's ban on certain topics and was soon dismissed.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他质问校长为什么不许讲某些内容，不久被辞退。

D18.en: At 16, he earned 3 silver dollars from a year's labor, his first self-earned money.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他劳动一年得了3块大洋，第一次凭自己劳动换来。

D1.en: At 13-14, he worked hard to improve his arithmetic after a poor first year.
D1.zh: 13到14岁时，他第一年算术不好，第二年下了大功夫赶上去。

D11.en: As a teenager, he thought about learning martial arts to fight for the poor.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他曾想学武替穷苦百姓打不平。

D15.en: At 18-20, he took up tai chi in martial arts class and practiced it for over 60 years.
D15.zh: 18到20岁时，他学了太极拳并坚持了60多年。

D16.en: At 22-23, he joined others in questioning Dai Jitao about loyalty and righteousness, embarrassing him.
D16.zh: 22到23岁时，他和大伙质问戴季陶何为忠孝节义，把他哄得狼狈不堪。

D13b.en: At 19, his parents arranged his marriage; he went along with it.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，父母做主为他操办了婚事。

M4.en: At 25, he declared his wish to become a Communist Party member fighting for communism.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他表示愿做一个为共产主义奋斗的共产党员。

M7.en: At 22, he resolved to find a way to save the country and its people.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他决心寻找一条救国救民的路。

M1.en: At 20, he taught diligently, corrected every mistake, and wrote model characters for students.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他认真教书，批改作业，纠正错误，在作业本边上写示范字。

D17.en: At about 10, he sat by his sick sister, swatting flies, fanning her, and feeding her water or rice soup.
D17.zh: 10岁左右，他坐在生病的妹妹旁边，给她打苍蝇、扇扇子、喂水或米汤。

D6.en: At 23, he felt joy and enthusiasm leading soldiers against the enemy.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他感到带领士兵面对敌人的喜悦和热情。

D5.en: At 22, he led troops with a yell, charging forward.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他大喊着带领部队冲锋。

D2.en: At 23, he trained arduously on slopes from dawn to dusk with a heavy backpack.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他背着沉重的背包从早到晚在斜坡上艰苦训练。

D3.en: As a teenager, he discovered a taste for mathematics that lasted his life.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他发现了对数学的爱好，并持续一生。

D15.en: As a teenager, he enthusiastically applied himself to skiing, bicycling, and exploration.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他热衷于滑雪、骑自行车和探险。

D12.en: At 18, he had a hernia and underwent an operation.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他得了疝气并接受了手术。

D9.en: At 22, he commanded a platoon in the 49th Field Artillery Regiment.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他在第49野战炮兵团指挥一个排。

D20.en: At 25, he refused to come under another's command, citing his orders from a senior officer.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他拒绝接受他人指挥，称自己的命令来自更高级的军官。

M1.en: At 22, he decided to attack the enemy opposite rather than remain inactive.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他决定攻击对面的敌人，而不是无所作为。

D13b.en: At 22, he served on attachment with an artillery regiment at Ulm.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他在乌尔姆的一个炮兵团服役。

D17.en: At 22, he helped people regardless of friend or foe.
D17.zh: 22岁时，他不分敌我地帮助别人。

D7.en: He recorded no concern at having acted with such blithe independence in attacking.
D7.zh: 他记录了自己对如此轻率独立地发动进攻毫无担忧。

D8.en: At 22, he took command of the battalion machine guns himself.
D8.zh: 22岁时，他亲自指挥营里的机枪。

D1.en: At 23, his impatient temperament drove him to return before medical authorities approved.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他急躁的性格使他在未经医疗批准前就返回。

D4.en: At 24, he rehearsed carefully and wanted bad, preferably stormy, weather.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他仔细排练，并希望天气恶劣，最好是暴风雨。

M3.en: He admired Napoleon above all men.
M3.zh: 他最崇拜拿破仑。

D4.en: At 21-22, he helped organize a cooperative that produced daily necessities like gauze and soap.
D4.zh: 21到22岁时，他参与组织三民实业社，生产纱布、肥皂等日用品。

D2.en: At 25-26, he insisted on leading by example as a company party representative.
D2.zh: 25到26岁时，他坚持要求士兵做到的自己首先做到。

D3.en: At 25, he sought advice from veteran soldiers on how to be a good company party representative.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他向老兵请教如何当好连党代表。

D5.en: At 25, he shouted 'Follow me, take that hill!' and led the charge.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他大喊“跟我来，占领这个山头！”并带头冲锋。

D7.en: At 12, he refused to join his father in ancestral worship ceremonies.
D7.zh: 12岁时，他拒绝跟父亲参加祭祖等活动。

D11.en: At 13, he wrote an essay praising Yu the Great's noble character.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他作文颂扬大禹的高贵品德。

D13a.en: At 24, he announced the dissolution of his marital relationship.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他宣布解除夫妻关系。

D9.en: He explained the meaning of CCP and the oath, then asked Mao Zedong to lead the pledge.
D9.zh: 他解释了CCP的含义和誓词意义，然后请毛泽东带领宣誓。

D10.en: At 23, he remarked emotionally that China should have a strong navy.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他感慨地说中国应当有强大的海军。

D18.en: He helped organize the Sanmin Industrial Society, raising 200-300 yuan from classmates.
D18.zh: 他参与组织三民实业社，向同学集资二三百元。

D12.en: He told his wife she could decide whether to leave the Luo family.
D12.zh: 他告诉妻子，是否离开罗家由她自己决定。

D1.en: He saw women who refused to leave the Red Army despite being driven away with sticks.
D1.zh: 他看到妇女们用棍子赶也赶不走，坚持跟着红军。

D15.en: He struggled with poor eyesight on mountain roads but still helped carry weapons for sick soldiers.
D15.zh: 他眼神不好，走山路高一脚低一脚，仍坚持帮病号背枪。

D10.en: At 10, he cried into his pillow for an hour out of resentment and rage.
D10.zh: 10岁时，他因怨恨和愤怒把头埋在枕头里哭了一小时。

D13a.en: At 19, he said he very much wanted to enter Annapolis or West Point.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他说他很想进入安纳波利斯或西点军校。

D3.en: At 18-19, he read The Life of Hannibal, which ignited his great interest.
D3.zh: 18到19岁时，他读了《汉尼拔传》，这激发了他极大的兴趣。

M3.en: As a child, his first hero was Hannibal, then he admired George Washington.
M3.zh: 小时候，他的第一个英雄是汉尼拔，后来他崇拜乔治·华盛顿。

D6.en: At 23, he wrote a letter to Ruby Norman.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他给鲁比·诺曼写了一封信。

D1.en: At 10, he continually took on Edgar in frustration, determined to best him.
D1.zh: 10岁时，他不断沮丧地挑战埃德加，决心要胜过他。

D9.en: At 23-25, he coached junior varsity to success and became head cheerleader.
D9.zh: 23到25岁时，他执教校队二队取得佳绩，并成为陆军橄榄球赛的首席拉拉队长。

D7.en: At 20, he and another presented themselves clad only in tunics, naked underneath.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他和另一个人只穿着上衣，里面一丝不挂地出现。

M1.en: At 19-20, he said he very much wanted to enter Annapolis or West Point.
M1.zh: 19到20岁时，他说他很想进入安纳波利斯或西点军校。

D13b.en: At 21, he experienced West Point's autocratic method of turning civilians into soldiers.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他经历了西点军校将平民变成职业军人的专制方法。

D15.en: He won his 'A' as the most promising back in Eastern football, but broke his knee in the Tufts game.
D15.zh: 他被评为东部橄榄球最有前途的后卫获得'A'级，但在塔夫茨比赛中摔断了膝盖。

D20.en: His proudest achievement was writing a constitution he hoped would ensure the association's survival.
D20.zh: 他最自豪的成就是写了一部章程，希望确保协会的生存。

M2.en: He was determined to rise above humble beginnings and amount to something.
M2.zh: 他决心摆脱卑微的出身，将来有所成就。

M6.en: He was determined to rise above humble beginnings and amount to something.
M6.zh: 他决心摆脱卑微的出身，将来有所成就。

D20X.en: After 18 months, he dissolved the partnership and bought out Good.
D20X.zh: 18个月后，他解散了合伙关系，买断了古德的股份。

D5.en: He said it was all right with him; he had always had a curious ambition to go to Argentina.
D5.zh: 他说他没问题，他一直有个奇怪的愿望想去阿根廷。

M4.en: He anticipated the coming conflict with relish.
M4.zh: 他津津有味地期待即将到来的冲突。

D14.en: He shook the black player's hand before and after the game.
D14.zh: 他在比赛前后与黑人球员握手。

D11.en: He was busily making a toy.
D11.zh: 他正忙着做玩具。

D4.en: He played semipro baseball under an assumed name before West Point.
D4.zh: 他在进入西点前用假名打半职业棒球。

D17.en: At age 8, Dwight was busily making a toy while his brother played nearby.
D17.zh: 8岁时，德怀特忙着做玩具，弟弟在旁边玩。

D8.en: He told the orderly he would 'knock hell out of him' if he didn't hurry.
D8.zh: 他告诉勤务兵如果不快点就‘揍扁他’。

M8.en: At age 5, his mother Ida converted to the Bible Student movement.
M8.zh: 5岁时，母亲艾达改信圣经学生运动。

D16.en: He had to memorize the name of every classmate and upperclassman as a plebe.
D16.zh: 作为新生，他必须记住每个同学和高年级生的名字。

D19.en: He had poor handwriting.
D19.zh: 他字写得不好。

D2.en: He attended the Karlsruhe Cadet School with his brother from 1901 to 1903.
D2.zh: 1901到1903年，他和弟弟一起在卡尔斯鲁厄军校学习。

D13b.en: At 12-13, he and his brother decided to become army officers, endorsed by their father.
D13b.zh: 12到13岁时，他和弟弟决定成为军官，得到父亲支持。

D13a.en: He was persuaded to agree to a two-year cooling off period.
D13a.zh: 他被说服同意了两年的冷静期。

D3.en: He developed a delight in architecture, countryside, theatre, and dancing.
D3.zh: 他喜欢上了建筑、乡村、戏剧和舞蹈。

D15.en: He was a good rider with a strong character and charm.
D15.zh: 他骑术好，性格坚强，有魅力。

D7.en: He wrote a strongly critical report that reached the general.
D7.zh: 他写了一份措辞严厉的批评报告，传到了将军那里。

D1.en: He passed the Academy exam at the first attempt through frenetic application.
D1.zh: 他拼命努力，第一次就通过了军校考试。

M1.en: He passed the Academy exam at the first attempt, the youngest at 25 among 168 officers.
M1.zh: 他第一次就通过了军校考试，是168名军官中最年轻的，25岁。

D5.en: He did poorly in his final tactics exam by adopting a defensive posture instead of the prescribed attack.
D5.zh: 他战术期末考试考砸了，因为他采取了防御姿态，而不是规定的进攻方案。

D4.en: At 24, he felt the need to undertake technical training to broaden his professional knowledge.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他感到需要接受技术培训以拓宽专业知识基础。

D16.en: At 25, he became fluent in English.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他英语变得流利。

D11.en: At 20, his diary showed awakening criticism of the system and healthy skepticism.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他的日记显示出对体制的觉醒批评和健康的怀疑态度。

D18.en: At 20, he developed a delight in architecture, countryside, theatre, and dancing.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他培养了对建筑、乡村、戏剧和舞蹈的喜爱。

D19.en: At 20, with an outstanding memory, he could quote classical and military works by heart.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他记忆力超群，能背诵经典和军事著作。

M2.en: At 20, he said: 'I try to copy him.'
M2.zh: 20岁时，他说：‘我努力模仿他。’

M3.en: At 20, he said: 'I try to copy him.'
M3.zh: 20岁时，他说：‘我努力模仿他。’

M4.en: At 24, he felt the need to undertake technical training to broaden his professional knowledge.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他感到需要接受技术培训以拓宽专业知识基础。

M5.en: At 19, he did poorly in his final tactics exam by adopting a defensive posture instead of the prescribed attack.
M5.zh: 19岁时，他战术期末考试表现不佳，因为他采取了防御姿态而非规定的进攻方案。

M6.en: At 24, he felt the need for technical training to broaden his professional knowledge.
M6.zh: 24岁时，他感到需要接受技术培训以拓宽专业知识。

M7.en: At 24, he felt the need for technical training to broaden his professional knowledge.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他感到需要接受技术培训以拓宽专业知识。

M8.en: At 20, he developed a delight in architecture, countryside, theatre, and dancing.
M8.zh: 20岁时，他喜欢上建筑、乡村、戏剧和舞蹈。

D9.en: At 25, he wrote a strongly critical report that reached the general.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他写了一篇措辞严厉的批评报告，并送到了将军那里。

D7.en: At 24, he found his father's corpse and brother's bones, then swore revenge.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他找到父亲尸体和弟弟的骨头，发誓报仇。

D9.en: At 23, he said, 'Young men, follow me to form a band; women, go to your parents.'
D9.zh: 23岁时，他说：“年轻人跟我拉队伍，女人们投娘家。”

D18.en: As a teenager, he entered as a small muleteer, carrying grass and leading a mule.
D18.zh: 十几岁时，他背着一捆马草，牵一匹小花骡走进来。

D2.en: At 6-10, he practiced martial arts hard and learned from his father and an old man.
D2.zh: 6到10岁时，他苦练武功，跟父亲和谷兴楼老汉学拳术。

D15.en: At 10, he could fight his father to a draw.
D15.zh: 10岁时，他能和父亲打个平手。

D12.en: At 15, he was told to go to his brother-in-law's to drive mules.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他被叫去姐夫家赶马。

D3.en: At 15, he asked an old man about eating 'Guan Yin soil'.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他问一个老汉吃观音土有没有害。

D17.en: At 15, he gave his little money to beggars on the road.
D17.zh: 15岁时，他把兜里的钱给了路边乞讨的老人小孩。

D6.en: At 12, he said many brothers came to help him because the street was full of Hes.
D6.zh: 12岁时，他说许多哥哥兄弟出来帮他，因为满街全是姓贺的。

M1.en: At 10, he could fight his father to a draw.
M1.zh: 10岁时，他能和父亲打成平手。

M7.en: At 15, he gave his pocket money to beggars on the roadside.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他把兜里的钱给了路边乞讨的老人小孩。

D5.en: At 24, after finding his father's body and brother's bones, he swore revenge.
D5.zh: 24岁时，打捞到父亲尸体和弟弟骨头后，他砍树发誓报仇。

M4.en: At 20-21, he was tasked with bombing a house and carried a grenade, waiting for an opportunity.
M4.zh: 20到21岁间，他接受炸谭宅任务，怀揣手榴弹等待时机。

D4.en: At 20, he led 21 men with two chopping knives to raid a salt tax bureau, seizing 13 rifles.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他带21人用两把砍柴刀劈了盐税局，夺了13条枪。

D10.en: At 24, he wept bitterly when Lin Xiumei left.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他因林修梅离开而洒泪告别。

D2.en: At 17, he was drilled with a principle at Virginia Military Institute.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他在弗吉尼亚军事学院被灌输了一条原则。

D7.en: At 16, he never reported his tormentors.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他从未举报折磨他的人。

D14.en: At 14-15, he photographed a prize tomato with a Brownie camera and sent it to a seed company.
D14.zh: 14到15岁时，他用布朗尼相机给获奖番茄拍照，并寄给种子公司。

D13b.en: At 16, he suppressed a twinkle to be confirmed at St. Peter's.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，他忍住笑意在圣彼得教堂接受了坚信礼。

D12.en: As a small child, he hosed horse droppings from the street in front of his house.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他用软管冲洗家门前的马粪。

D5.en: At 16, he was ordered to squat over an unsheathed bayonet jammed into the floor.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他被命令蹲在插在地板上的刺刀上方。

D16.en: At 9, he feared standing in front of the class and being laughed at.
D16.zh: 9岁时，他害怕站在全班面前被嘲笑。

D3.en: At 14-15, he wrote a congressman for free seed packets to stimulate agriculture interest.
D3.zh: 14到15岁时，他写信给国会议员索取免费种子包以激发农业兴趣。

D1.en: At 24-25, he scored highest on Leavenworth tests twice but higher-ranking officers got the detail.
D1.zh: 24到25岁时，他两次在莱文沃思考试中得分最高，但高级军官得到了任务。

D4.en: At 24, he mapped 2,000 square miles of Texas desert.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他测绘了得克萨斯沙漠约2000平方英里的地图。

D18.en: At 14-15, he and a friend picked fruit and sold it to a grocer.
D18.zh: 14到15岁时，他和朋友摘水果卖给杂货店。

D9.en: At 18, he was appointed first sergeant for the following year.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他被任命为下一年的第一中士。

D15.en: At 21, he took up riding, borrowing cavalry mounts and acquiring a lifelong recreation.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他开始骑马，借骑兵的马，并养成了一生的爱好。

D8.en: At 21, he ordered troops to march through crocodile-infested water in proper military fashion.
D8.zh: 21岁时，他命令部队以正规军事方式穿过有鳄鱼的水域。

M1.en: He was competitive.
M1.zh: 他很好胜。

D10.en: He witnessed a female performer collapse twice after a few shows.
D10.zh: 他目睹一位女演员在几场演出后两次晕倒。

D12.en: At 19, he faced his family's collective thought of suicide due to hardship.
D12.zh: 19岁时，他面临家人因生活艰难而集体考虑自杀的情况。

D3.en: At 17, he was the only cadet who had a Bible and sometimes read it.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他是唯一拥有圣经并偶尔阅读的学员。

M1.en: At 17, he scored second from the top in a national competitive exam.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他在全国竞争性考试中排名第二。

D20.en: At 17, he won an appointment to the Imperial Naval Academy at Eta Jima.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他获得了江田岛海军兵学校的入学资格。

D9.en: As a student, he was a leader from the first.
D9.zh: 作为学生，他从一开始就是领导者。

D7.en: At 20, he kept a Bible on his desk, provoking arguments with friends.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他在桌上放了一本圣经，引发与朋友的争论。

D16.en: At 17, he fielded taunts gracefully, threw rowdies out, and resumed reading.
D16.zh: 17岁时，他优雅地回应嘲弄，把捣乱者赶出房间，继续读书。

D2.en: At 21, he was sent to gunnery school and promoted to sublieutenant.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他被派往横须贺海军基地的炮术学校，晋升为少尉。

D15.en: At 20, he graduated from the Imperial Japanese Naval Academy at Eta Jima.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他从江田岛海军兵学校毕业。

D10.en: At 21, he bound his hand with a handkerchief and stayed at his battle station until the fight ended.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他用手帕包扎手，坚守战斗岗位直到战斗结束。

D6.en: As a boy, he attended an American missionary's church in Nagaoka.
D6.zh: 他小时候在长冈上过美国传教士纽厄尔的教堂。

D1.en: He lost fingers and thigh flesh in battle but stayed at his post until the fight ended.
D1.zh: 他在战斗中失去手指和大腿肉，但坚守岗位直到战斗结束。

D4.en: He trained to fly solo at an age when most officers stopped flying.
D4.zh: 他在大多数军官停止飞行的年龄训练自己单飞。

D13a.en: He once got so drunk he fell into a ditch, then quit drinking for good.
D13a.zh: 他有一次喝醉掉进沟里，从此戒酒。

D5.en: He ate 47 mandarin oranges at once and got appendicitis.
D5.zh: 他一次吃了47个橘子，结果得了阑尾炎。

D14.en: He declared his belief that war would break out within a year.
D14.zh: 他宣称相信战争会在一年内爆发。

D12.en: As a small child, he lost his mother at age 3.5.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他3岁半就失去了母亲。

D3.en: At 22-23, he developed skill in assessing exquisite artworks under von Loewenfeld's critical eye.
D3.zh: 22到23岁间，他在冯·勒文费尔德的挑剔眼光下练就了鉴赏精美艺术品的能力。

D6.en: At 25, he was very well liked among his comrades.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他在战友中很受欢迎。

D2.en: At 19, he endured extraordinary physical demands as a cadet.
D2.zh: 19岁时，他作为学员承受了非凡的体能要求。

D1.en: At 22, he was overjoyed to be named a section leader.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他听到自己被任命为分队长时欣喜若狂。

D9.en: At 17-18, he founded a literary society with classmates.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他和同学创办了一个文学社团。

D4.en: At 17-18, he collected rock samples and fossils on excursions.
D4.zh: 17到18岁时，他远足收集岩石标本和化石。

D15.en: At 25, he called out from the foredeck for the captain to come aboard.
D15.zh: 25岁时，他在前甲板喊船长立即上船。

M3.en: At 19, he hero-worshipped a superior who thought highly of him.
M3.zh: 19岁时，他崇拜一位长官，而那位长官也对他评价很高。

M1.en: At 25, he was awarded the Iron Cross first class for his part in an encounter with a Russian dreadnought battleship.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他因参与与一艘俄罗斯无畏舰的交战而被授予一级铁十字勋章。

D5.en: At 25, he replied, 'I don't know that we should. Perhaps we'll escape again.'
D5.zh: 25岁时，他回答说：‘我不知道我们是否应该。也许我们还会再逃脱。’

D6.en: He formed a close personal friendship with fellow subaltern Wilhelm Dietrich von Ditfurth.
D6.zh: 他与同僚威廉·迪特里希·冯·迪特富特建立了亲密的个人友谊。

D7.en: He resented the dumb authority of school as a child.
D7.zh: 他小时候反感学校里愚蠢的权威。

D3.en: He took a four-week journey by train and steamer to Turkey, Greece and Italy.
D3.zh: 他乘火车和轮船进行了为期四周的旅行，去了土耳其、希腊和意大利。

D4.en: He fought many battles with his prized Heinrichsen tin soldiers on the dining-room table.
D4.zh: 他用他珍爱的海因里希森锡兵在餐桌上打了很多仗。

D15.en: He went cruising on summer Sundays on the lakes of the upper Spree near Berlin.
D15.zh: 他夏天周日在上施普雷河靠近柏林的湖上巡游。

D13b.en: He was promoted to Leutnant on 27 January 1907 with seniority backdated to 14 June 1905.
D13b.zh: 他于1907年1月27日晋升为少尉，资历追溯至1905年6月14日。

D2.en: He received education at the Royal Prussian Cadet Corps in Strasbourg and attended theatre visits as a page.
D2.zh: 他在斯特拉斯堡的皇家普鲁士士官学校接受教育，并作为侍童观看戏剧。

D12.en: At 12, he was apprenticed to a furrier and sent to work in Moscow.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他给一个皮货商当学徒，被送到莫斯科工作。

D2.en: At 7-10, he took the full three-year primary course instead of the usual two-year one.
D2.zh: 7到10岁间，他上了完整的三年制小学，而不是多数农民选的两年的。

D13b.en: At 11, he was apprenticed to his uncle, a successful Moscow furrier.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他给他舅舅当学徒，他舅舅是莫斯科一个成功的皮货商。

D1.en: At 22, he contracted typhus and could not join up for another six months.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他感染了斑疹伤寒，又过了六个月才能参军。

D18.en: At 12, he was apprenticed to a furrier and sent to work in Moscow.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他给一个皮货商当学徒，被送到莫斯科工作。

D3.en: At 11-18, he studied Russian, math, geography, popular science, and German with others.
D3.zh: 11到18岁间，他和别人一起学习俄语、数学、地理、科普读物和德语。

D13a.en: At 19, he insisted on serving despite being offered a medical deferment.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他拒绝了医疗延期入伍的机会，坚持服役。

D7.en: At 24, he was suspended from a course for a few days for a disciplinary infraction.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他因违反纪律被停课几天。

D9.en: At 24, he became secretary of his party cell at Ryazan.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他成为梁赞党组织书记。

D12.en: At age 9, his family moved to escape bandits and fell into debt.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他家为躲匪患迁入城内，欠债百多银元。

D11.en: As a child, he imagined becoming a sword-wielding hero to rid the people of evil.
D11.zh: 他幼时遐想长大后做为民除害的剑侠。

D2.en: At 17, he ran 5000 meters every morning, rain or shine.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他每天晨跑5000米，风雨无阻。

D4.en: At age 11-13, he helped bricklayers build a house, throwing and catching bricks skillfully.
D4.zh: 11到13岁时，他帮砖匠建房，抛砖接瓦很准。

D18.en: At age 11-13, he helped shopkeepers pack sugar and count money.
D18.zh: 11到13岁时，他帮店铺包糖包子、算账数铜板。

D7.en: At 16, he joined a student strike and outwitted soldiers with classmates.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他参加学生罢课，与同学智斗痞子兵。

D9.en: At 18, he persuaded students to transfer schools, nearly dissolving a missionary school.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他动员学生转学，使一所教会学校几乎瓦解。

D13a.en: At 16, he refused an arranged marriage to a bound-foot woman.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他坚决反对父亲包办的缠足亲事。

D5.en: At 21, he charged to the top of a hill and found only 9 men followed.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他冲上制高点，回头发现只跟上来9人。

D16.en: He spoke calmly and captivated the audience with his eloquent speeches.
D16.zh: 他演讲时态度从容，演说有声有色。

D3.en: He devoured revolutionary publications like The ABC of Communism.
D3.zh: 他废寝忘食地阅读《共产主义ABC》等革命书刊。

M4.en: He resolved to join the military to fight counter-revolutionaries.
M4.zh: 他决心投笔从戎，用武装革命反对武装反革命。

D1.en: He endured harsh training by seeing it as a chance to steel himself.
D1.zh: 他把艰苦生活当作磨炼自己的机会，咬牙坚持。

D10.en: She expressed fear of losing independence if they married.
D10.zh: 楚青坦言担心婚后失去独立，不适合做首长夫人。

M7.en: He drew resolve from Zhu De and Chen Yi's speeches.
M7.zh: 他听朱德、陈毅讲话后更坚定了斗志。

M1.en: He constantly studied and learned the art of command.
M1.zh: 他处处注意学习和研究指挥艺术。

D14.en: He ran away from home to join the New Fourth Army at age 15.
D14.zh: 他15岁时私自离家出走，加入新四军。

D12.en: As a small child, he became the eldest surviving son after two elder brothers died.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他因两个哥哥夭折而成为长子。

D15.en: He showed considerable personal courage and tenacity in seeing it through.
D15.zh: 他表现出了相当大的个人勇气和毅力，坚持完成了这件事。

D2.en: At 25, he applied himself grimly to his profession, working long hours with obsessional concentration.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他更加拼命地投入工作，长时间专注地工作。

D16.en: During school years, he was opinionated, obstinate, and quick to pick a fight.
D16.zh: 上学期间，他固执己见，好斗。

M1.en: At 15-18, he believed plain hard work was necessary to keep up with or beat other students.
M1.zh: 15到18岁时，他认为只有刻苦努力才能赶上或超过其他学生。

D13b.en: At 25, he married Katsu Ito.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他娶了伊藤胜。

D8.en: At 25, he took the initiative after first meeting, indicating to his parents that this was the girl he wanted to marry.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他在第一次见面后就主动向父母表示这是他想要娶的女孩。

D12.en: As a small child, he watched his mother toil for ten desperate years, her beauty fading and skin roughening.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他看到母亲辛苦劳作了十年，容颜憔悴，皮肤粗糙。

D2.en: He achieved extraordinary self-education despite discouraging handicaps.
D2.zh: 尽管困难重重，他仍通过自学取得了非凡成就。

D5.en: At 16, he went to every company commander begging to enlist.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他去找每个连长请求入伍。

D7.en: At 21, he sought a captain's commission but had to settle for less.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他想当上尉但只能接受较低的军衔。

D1.en: He achieved extraordinary self-education despite discouraging handicaps.
D1.zh: 尽管困难重重，他仍通过自学取得了非凡成就。

D9.en: At 24, he wrote a pamphlet on reconnaissance for the Philippine Constabulary.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他为菲律宾警察部队写了一本侦察手册。

D10.en: At 23, he admitted he was pale and shaky after his baptism of fire.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他承认经历战火洗礼后脸色苍白、浑身发抖。

D15.en: At 21, he was a weak hitter and barely adequate in right field, but he loved to play.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他击球弱，右外野也勉强合格，但他热爱打球。

D14.en: At 17, he won easily with his dramatic reading of a poem.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他凭一首诗的戏剧朗诵轻松获胜。

D6.en: At age 0, he was christened in a penthouse by Manila's Episcopalian bishop.
D6.zh: 0岁时，他在顶层公寓由马尼拉圣公会主教施洗。

D3.en: He heavily marked a book and set it aside for future reference.
D3.zh: 他在这本书上做了大量标记，留作日后参考。

D18.en: He fought with his fists for a chance to sell newspapers on a street corner.
D18.zh: 他靠拳头争取到在街角卖报纸的机会。

D17.en: He fished out a paper, signed Quinn's name, and handed it in.
D17.zh: 他捞出文件，签上奎因的名字，然后交了上去。

M1.en: He took his examination for promotion to first lieutenant in spring 1904.
M1.zh: 1904年春天，他参加了晋升中尉的考试。

D8.en: He demanded that his name be removed from the list.
D8.zh: 他要求把自己的名字从名单上拿掉。

D20.en: He was chosen first sergeant of A Company, the highest rank for a day student.
D20.zh: 他被选为A连第一中士，这是走读生能获得的最高军衔。

D11.en: He wrote a pamphlet on reconnaissance for the Philippine Constabulary.
D11.zh: 他为菲律宾警察部队写了一本侦察手册。

D16.en: He said he would round up signpainters, make warning signs, float them, pray, then fight.
D16.zh: 他说他会召集画匠做警告牌，漂在港口，然后祈祷，再拼命战斗。

M4.en: At 24, he wrote a pamphlet on reconnaissance for the Philippine Constabulary.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他为菲律宾警察部队写了一本侦察手册。

M7.en: He wrote a pamphlet on reconnaissance for the Philippine Constabulary.
M7.zh: 他为菲律宾警察部队写了一本侦察手册。

D13b.en: During his first two years at West Point, his mother lived in a nearby hotel.
D13b.zh: 他在西点军校的头两年，他母亲住在附近的旅馆里。

D4.en: He played with the heliograph as a small child.
D4.zh: 他幼年时玩过日光反射信号器。

M3.en: At 13, he was inspired by Arthur's example.
M3.zh: 13岁时，他受到阿瑟榜样的启发。

M2.en: As a child, he attended dynastic feasts presided over by Judge MacArthur.
M2.zh: 他小时候参加由麦克阿瑟法官主持的家族盛宴。

D4.en: At 12, his imagination was stirred by the great battles of Xerxes and Alexander the Great.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他的想象力被薛西斯和亚历山大大帝的伟大战役所激发。

D3.en: At 12, his imagination was stirred by the great battles of Xerxes and Alexander the Great.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他的想象力被薛西斯和亚历山大大帝的伟大战役所激发。

D2.en: He followed a strictly disciplined schedule from 6am to 9:30pm.
D2.zh: 他遵循严格的时间表，从早上6点到晚上9点半。

D13b.en: He endured hundreds of hours of confinement as instructors imposed strict order.
D13b.zh: 他忍受了数百小时的禁闭，教官们强加严格秩序。

D15.en: He was a brawny 6'3", 220-pound cadet who excelled in sports.
D15.zh: 他是个身高6英尺3英寸、体重220磅的强壮学员，擅长运动。

M1.en: At 17, he passed the West Point entrance examination.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他通过了西点军校入学考试。

D18.en: At 24, he went to work in the administrative offices of the L. Bamberger Company.
D18.zh: 24岁时，他去L.班伯格公司的行政办公室工作。

D13a.en: At 18, he decided to change his name eighteen years later.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他决定十八年后改名。

D5.en: At 3, he started walking around town by himself.
D5.zh: 3岁时，他开始独自在镇上走动。

D7.en: In January 1952, during Christmas vacation, he sent his parents to legally change his name to H. Norman Schwarzkopf.
D7.zh: 1952年1月，圣诞假期期间，他让父母去特伦顿合法地将他的名字改为H.诺曼·施瓦茨科普夫。

D9.en: At 21-22, if he saw someone becoming abusive in hazing first-year cadets, he would intervene to stop it.
D9.zh: 21到22岁时，如果他看到有人在欺负一年级学员，他会介入制止。

D1.en: At 12, he insisted on a stern photo for the yearbook, saying, 'When I become a general, I want people to know that I am serious.'
D1.zh: 12岁时，他坚持在年鉴上使用一张严肃的照片，说：“当我成为将军时，我要让人们知道我是认真的。”

M3.en: At 18-22, his model was Alexander the Great, who conquered much of Asia by 33.
M3.zh: 18到22岁间，他以亚历山大大帝为榜样，后者33岁前征服了亚洲大部。

D3.en: He crawled through the ship's engineering spaces, studying the steam plant.
D3.zh: 他爬进船舱的工程区域，研究蒸汽设备。

D4.en: He made a reading stand, an ironing board, and bookcases in manual training class.
D4.zh: 他在手工课上做了一个阅读架、一个熨衣板和几个书架。

D18.en: At 12-14, he worked in a fruit store and drove a horse-drawn wagon for deliveries.
D18.zh: 12到14岁时，他在水果蔬菜店打工，赶马车送货。

D7.en: He shouted at an officer three grades his senior while careening across the launch.
D7.zh: 他冲过小艇，朝一位比他高三级的长官大喊。

D2.en: He did a year of postgraduate study at Annapolis and another year at Columbia in electrical engineering.
D2.zh: 他在安纳波利斯读了一年研究生，又在哥伦比亚大学读了一年电气工程。

D5.en: He ordered the same number of shots the other man had already consumed and downed them one after another.
D5.zh: 他点了对方已经喝下的同样数量的烈酒，一杯接一杯地灌下去。

D9.en: He and his men ripped out old wiring and switchboards and installed new equipment.
D9.zh: 他和手下拆掉旧电线和配电盘，装上新设备。

D13a.en: He ignored the dietary prohibitions he had been brought up with.
D13a.zh: 他无视了从小被教导的饮食禁忌。

D6.en: He was one of the most popular members of his class.
D6.zh: 他是班上最受欢迎的人之一。

M1.en: His ship ranked first among all destroyers in engineering competition.
M1.zh: 他的舰在工程竞赛中在所有驱逐舰中排名第一。

D10.en: He reportedly had a few drinks and became tipsy.
D10.zh: 据报道他喝了几杯，微醺了。

D16.en: He freely gave his opinion of another's teaching ability.
D16.zh: 他坦率地评价了别人的教学能力。

D1.en: He urgently requested an operation, then attended torpedo school after discharge.
D1.zh: 他急切要求手术，出院后去了鱼雷学校。

D13b.en: He signed a register listing his religion upon admission to the Academy.
D13b.zh: 他入学时在登记簿上签了宗教信息。

D15.en: At 22, he calmly took photos while under fire, holding on by his toes.
D15.zh: 22岁时，他冷静地在炮火中拍照，仅靠脚尖支撑。

D12.en: At 23, he visited Herr Mauser and formally asked for his daughter's hand in marriage.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他拜访毛瑟先生，正式请求娶他女儿。

D11.en: As a small child, he was obsessed with medieval battles and knightly chivalry in his games.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他在游戏中痴迷于中世纪战斗和骑士精神。

D6.en: At 23, while convalescing, he had his first recorded love affair with a girl named Marianne Mauser.
D6.zh: 23岁休养期间，他有了第一次有记载的恋情，对象是玛丽安·毛瑟。

D5.en: At 22, he risked being charged with forging transfer papers and deserting his unit.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他冒着被指控伪造调令和擅离职守的风险。

D13b.en: At 12, he was sent to a military academy in Karlsruhe.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他被送到卡尔斯鲁厄的军校。

D14.en: At 24, he went to Berlin to receive his award from the Kaiser and enjoyed being a hero.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他去柏林从皇帝手中领奖，享受了英雄的待遇。

D9.en: At 22, he volunteered and spent three days cruising low over a chain of forts.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他自愿报名，花了三天低空飞越一连串堡垒。

D10.en: As a small child, he had fits of tears and tantrums, followed by gifts and affection.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他动不动就哭闹发脾气，之后总会得到礼物和关爱。

M1.en: He was awarded the Iron Cross first class by the Crown Prince.
M1.zh: 他被王储授予了一级铁十字勋章。

D20.en: At 25, he led the flight back and ordered crash-landings.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他带领飞行队返回并命令迫降。

D16.en: He spoke of the Richthofen squadron's history, Germany's bitter times, and the disgraceful attitude toward officers' sacrifice.
D16.zh: 他讲述了里希特霍芬中队的历史、德国的苦难时期，以及人民对军官牺牲的可耻态度。

D3.en: As a small child, he emulated his godfather in dress, manner, and speech.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他模仿教父的穿着、举止和言谈。

M3.en: At 11, he wrote an essay praising Ritter von Epenstein.
M3.zh: 11岁时，他写了一篇赞美埃彭斯坦骑士的文章。

D1.en: At 22, he crash-landed his plane near an emergency hospital.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他将飞机迫降在急救医院附近。

M4.en: At 25, he committed to being loyal to the Fatherland.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他决心效忠祖国。

D18.en: At about 15, he sold his violin to a fellow pupil to pay his fare home.
D18.zh: 15岁左右，他把小提琴卖给同学来付回家的路费。

D12.en: He was forced to drop out of upper secondary school at age 14-15.
D12.zh: 他14到15岁时被迫从高中辍学。

M4.en: He voluntarily joined the Red Guards in the Kargopol detachment as an ordinary cavalryman in October 1917 at age 21.
M4.zh: 他于1917年10月自愿加入卡尔戈波尔赤卫队支队，担任普通骑兵，时年21岁。

D18.en: He went to work at age 16-17, first as an assistant pastry chef, then as an assistant dentist.
D18.zh: 他16到17岁时开始工作，先是做糕点师助手，后来做牙医助手。

D3.en: From childhood he was interested in military history books about campaigns of Alexander the Great and Roman generals.
D3.zh: 他从小就对描写亚历山大大帝和罗马将军战史的军事书籍感兴趣。

D13a.en: His relatives took him and Maria away from their mother to raise them in a Polish spirit at age 8.
D13a.zh: 他8岁时，亲戚把他和玛丽亚从母亲身边带走，说要按波兰精神抚养他们。

D4.en: He engraved his initials into the tombstones that he made at age 15-20.
D4.zh: 他15到20岁时在自己制作的墓碑上刻下姓名首字母。

D14.en: He loved to sing and dance and played the harmonica rather well at age 15-20.
D14.zh: 他15到20岁时喜欢唱歌跳舞，口琴也吹得相当好。

D5.en: At 25, Rokossovsky saved an infantry battalion from destruction with a counterattack.
D5.zh: 25岁时，罗科索夫斯基用反攻挽救了一个步兵营免遭覆灭。

D9.en: He served as assistant commander of the Kargopol Cavalry Detachment from Dec 1917 to Mar 1918.
D9.zh: 他于1917年12月至1918年3月担任卡尔戈波尔骑兵支队的副指挥官。

D1.en: He took a severe wound in the leg but refused to leave the battlefield until the fighting ended.
D1.zh: 他腿部受重伤，但拒绝离开战场直到战斗结束。

D6.en: At 14, he and neighborhood guys rushed to slash a nettle with a parade sword.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他和邻居伙伴们冲去用阅兵剑砍荨麻。

D2.en: As a small child, he was raised accustomed to work and regimen.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他被培养成习惯工作和规律生活。

D10.en: At 14, he carried his sobbing 5-year-old brother, whose foot was bloodied, while he himself was totally pale.
D10.zh: 14岁时，他抱着哭泣的5岁弟弟，弟弟的脚在流血，他自己脸色惨白。

D7.en: At 6, he disagreed with a teacher and tried to leave through a locked door, then an open window.
D7.zh: 6岁时，他不同意老师的观点，试图从锁着的门离开，然后从开着的窗户出去。

D15.en: At 17-19, he worked at Buckeye Steel Casting Company until 3 A.M. six nights a week.
D15.zh: 17到19岁时，他在巴克艾钢铁铸造公司工作，每周六个晚上干到凌晨3点。

D18.en: At 18, he started work at Buckeye for $35/week, working 9-hour days, 6 days a week.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他在巴克艾开始工作，周薪35美元，每天工作9小时，每周6天。

D2.en: He worked at Buckeye Steel Casting Company, staying until 3 A.M. six nights a week at age 17-19.
D2.zh: 他17到19岁时在巴克艾钢铁铸造公司工作，每周六晚干到凌晨3点。

D6.en: At 18-22, he joined Theta Tau, an engineering fraternity.
D6.zh: 18到22岁间，他加入了工程兄弟会Theta Tau。

D8.en: At 11-13, he picked fights with smaller kids but sometimes misjudged.
D8.zh: 11到13岁时，他挑那些不太大的孩子打架，但有时判断失误。

D12.en: He worked at Buckeye Steel Casting Company until 3 a.m. six nights a week.
D12.zh: 他在巴克艾钢铁铸造公司工作，每周六晚都干到凌晨3点。

M1.en: At 18, he aimed to earn a civil engineering degree and win an ROTC commission to join the Army Air Service.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他目标是获得土木工程学位并赢得后备军官训练团委任，以便加入陆军航空队。

D1.en: At 21, he continued working under Peewee, nearly causing disaster.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他在皮威手下继续干，差点酿成大祸。

M4.en: At 21, he became obsessed with joining the Air Corps, despite having flown only once.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他执意要加入空军，尽管此前只坐过一次飞机。

D5.en: At 24, he took a young lady for a ride in a PT-3, showing her the countryside, when the engine quit.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他带一位年轻女士乘坐PT-3兜风，给她看乡村景色，结果发动机停了。

D19.en: At 20, he made mechanical connections and adjustments.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他进行机械连接和调整。

D13a.en: At 21, he resigned his Reserve commission to accept a National Guard commission, then soon got another Reserve commission.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他辞去预备役委任以接受国民警卫队委任，随后又获得另一个预备役委任。

D20X.en: At 21, he resigned his Reserve commission to accept a National Guard commission.
D20X.zh: 21岁时，他辞去预备役委任以接受国民警卫队委任。

M2.en: At 21, he became obsessed with joining the Air Corps, despite having flown only once.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他执意要加入空军，尽管此前只坐过一次飞机。

M6.en: At 21, he became obsessed with joining the Air Corps.
M6.zh: 21岁时，他痴迷于加入空军。

M7.en: At 21, he became obsessed with joining the Air Corps.
M7.zh: 21岁时，他执意要加入空军。

M3.en: At 5, he cherished the romantic image of the first flying machine he saw overhead.
M3.zh: 5岁时，他珍视第一次看到头顶飞过的飞行器的浪漫形象。

D10.en: At 21, his determination to get into the Air Corps became almost an obsession.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他加入空军的决心几乎成了执念。

D7.en: He refused to be tested, saying if they believed in his capability fine, if not then not.
D7.zh: 他拒绝被测试，说如果相信他的能力就好，否则就算了。

D5.en: He went out on an action with about seven of them and met eighty Arabs.
D5.zh: 他和大约七个人出去行动，遇到了八十个阿拉伯人。

D2.en: At 24, he was a prisoner allowed to work half a day and study the other half.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他作为囚犯被允许只工作半天，另外半天用来学习。

D18.en: He built his own grafting shed and apple nursery; his seedlings were in large demand.
D18.zh: 他建了自己的嫁接棚和苹果苗圃，他的树苗需求很大。

D9.en: He sponsored a meeting in Ein Ganim to support the moderately socialist Jewish newspaper Hapoel Hatzair.
D9.zh: 他在恩加尼姆赞助了一次会议，支持温和社会主义的犹太报纸《青年工人》。

D4.en: He built his own grafting shed and apple nursery; his seedlings were in large demand.
D4.zh: 他建了自己的嫁接棚和苹果苗圃，他的树苗需求很大。

D3.en: He realized the only way to fight was to seize initiative and surprise enemies on the move.
D3.zh: 他意识到唯一的战斗方式是掌握主动，攻击敌人基地并在他们移动时突袭。

D10.en: At age 18, he gave an ultimatum to marry or sleep together.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他发出最后通牒：要么结婚，要么同床。

M1.en: At age 10, he said he wanted to be among the most diligent pupils.
M1.zh: 10岁时，他说想成为最勤奋的学生之一。

M5.en: At age 10-12, he would challenge anyone who made him lose face to a fight.
M5.zh: 10到12岁时，他会向让他丢脸的人挑战打架。

D3.en: He read everything possible, from world commentaries to social analysis.
D3.zh: 他读了一切可能读的东西，从世界局势评论到社会分析。

D12.en: At 16, after six years of schooling, he went to work.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他完成了六年学业，开始工作。

D2.en: At 22-23, working with dedication and speed, he ranked first in philosophy.
D2.zh: 22到23岁间，他专注而快速地学习，哲学成绩名列第一。

D4.en: He rode his bicycle from Hanoi to Cam Pha and back to report on a miners' strike.
D4.zh: 他骑自行车从河内到锦普再返回，报道矿工罢工。

D6.en: At 16, he joined public ceremonies mourning the death of patriot Phan Chu Trinh.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他参加公祭，哀悼爱国志士潘周桢。

D15.en: As a small child, he played da kien, kicking a coin shuttlecock to keep it spinning.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他玩踢毽子，用脚踢硬币毽子让它旋转。

M1.en: At 14, when he retook the exam, he finished second among all tested.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他重考，在所有考生中排名第二。

D9.en: At 14, he defied the school administration and joined a student quit-school movement.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他违抗校方，参加了学生罢课运动。

M4.en: At 16-17, he was increasingly drawn to the Communist Party's organization and discipline.
M4.zh: 16到17岁间，他越来越被共产党的组织和纪律所吸引。

D7.en: At 14, he defied the school administration and joined a student quit-school movement.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他违抗校方，参加了学生罢课运动。

D16.en: He was known as an excellent lecturer on military history.
D16.zh: 他以军事史方面的出色讲课而闻名。

D18.en: He held a job at a French-owned textile mill.
D18.zh: 他在一家法资纺织厂工作。

D14.en: He published the first issue of the journal Hon Tre Tap Moi.
D14.zh: 他出版了《青年之魂新刊》第一期。

D1.en: He decided to circumvent colonial demands by printing a French-language newspaper.
D1.zh: 他决定绕过殖民当局的要求，出版一份法文报纸。

D20.en: He served as editor in chief of Le Travail's first issue.
D20.zh: 他担任《劳动报》第一期的总编辑。

D6.en: At 21, he sang Gospel songs and swayed along with the black congregation.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他跟着黑人会众唱福音歌并摇摆身体。

D11.en: As a small child, he was obsessed with WWII, tracking troop movements on maps and playing with toy soldiers.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他痴迷二战，在地图上追踪部队并用玩具兵打仗。

D15.en: At 21, he marched, did calisthenics and ran obstacle courses in the muggy Georgia heat.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他在闷热的乔治亚州行军、做体操、跑障碍赛。

D2.en: At 21, he was eighth in his class.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他在班上排名第八。

D13a.en: At 23, he registered to vote that fall, leaving blank the space indicating party affiliation.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他当年秋天登记投票，但空下了党派一栏。

D14.en: At 18-21, he developed a special expertise in solo rifle-twirling that put him in the spotlight.
D14.zh: 18到21岁间，他练就了独门步枪旋转特技，让他成为焦点。

M2.en: At 10-16, he believed bringing shame upon the family was the worst sin.
M2.zh: 10到16岁间，他认为给家族蒙羞是最大的罪过。

M1.en: At 24-25, Schwarzkopf considered Lt. Powell the most outstanding officer of his grade he had encountered.
M1.zh: 24到25岁间，施瓦茨科普夫认为鲍威尔中尉是他见过最出色的同级军官。

D7.en: At 22, stormed out after father rejected his marriage plan.
D7.zh: 22岁时因父亲反对婚事而冲出家门。

D5.en: At 21, he signed up for three more months of mountain marching after being accepted into Ranger and Airborne schools.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他被游骑兵和空降学校录取后，又报名参加了三个月的山地行军。

D1.en: He endured through his early years.
D1.zh: 他熬过了早年时光。

D12.en: At 24, he had a couple of children.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他有了几个孩子。

D4.en: He broke off a bough, checked Pat's breathing, then shoved him over the edge.
D4.zh: 他折下一根树枝，检查了帕特的呼吸，然后把他推下了边缘。

D18.en: At age 6-8, he sold blackberries for ten cents a gallon with Roy.
D18.zh: 他6到8岁时和罗伊一起卖黑莓，每加仑十美分。

D5.en: At 21, he hunched down and pushed off down a draw.
D5.zh: 他21岁时，压低身子，顺着冲沟滑下去。

D15.en: At 21, he stopped to check if Patterson was breathing while carrying him.
D15.zh: 他21岁时，多次停下来听帕特森是否还在呼吸。

D6.en: I saw three Focke-Wulf fighters diving at me and radioed a warning.
D6.zh: 我看到三架福克-沃尔夫战斗机向我俯冲，用无线电警告了奥布莱恩。

D2.en: At 23, he did as told and counted to three or four hundred.
D2.zh: 他23岁时，按要求做，数到三四百。

D13b.en: At 18, he enlisted for a two-year hitch.
D13b.zh: 他18岁时，应征入伍，服役两年。

D1.en: At 21, he decided to wait till dark and drag both of them back up the mountain into Spain.
D1.zh: 他21岁时，决定等到天黑，把两人拖回山上，进入西班牙。

D9.en: I saw three Focke-Wulf fighters diving at me and radioed a warning.
D9.zh: 我看到三架福克-沃尔夫战斗机向我俯冲，用无线电警告了奥布莱恩。

M1.en: I spotted a Messerschmitt Me109 below me, dove on him, and blew him to pieces.
M1.zh: 我发现一架梅塞施密特Me109在下方，俯冲过去把它炸成了碎片。

D16.en: I told them the Focke-Wulf 190 is a good fighter, but the Mustang with wing tanks can escort bombers deep into Germany.
D16.zh: 我告诉他们，福克-沃尔夫190是很好的战斗机，但挂载翼下副油箱的野马能护送轰炸机深入德国。

D10.en: At 21, he sat in a small, airless, pitch-dark room, drenched in sweat.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他坐在一个闷热漆黑的小房间里，浑身是汗。

D13a.en: We both agreed to see other people, but the only one special to me was in England.
D13a.zh: 我们都同意各自见别人，但对我来说唯一特别的人在英格兰。

M4.en: I knew I was going down; I barely unfastened my belt and crawled over the seat before my burning P-51 began to snap and roll.
M4.zh: 我知道我要坠毁了；我勉强解开安全带爬过座椅，然后燃烧的P-51开始翻滚。

D20.en: He was assigned as group leader and led three squadrons on a bomber escort mission over Germany.
D20.zh: 他被任命为组长，带领三个中队执行轰炸机护航任务。

D8.en: He did a barrel roll, came in behind an enemy, and fired a burst over his canopy.
D8.zh: 他做了一个桶滚，绕到敌机后面，朝他的座舱盖开火。

D3.en: He could see at great distances, a gift only he and Andy shared.
D3.zh: 他能看到很远的地方，这种天赋只有他和安迪有。

D14.en: He and Hoover began putting on air shows together in fall 1945.
D14.zh: 1945年秋天，他和胡佛开始一起做飞行表演。

M7.en: He told someone he would fly more cautiously because he had a family.
M7.zh: 他告诉别人，因为自己有家室，会比单身飞行员更谨慎地飞行。

D3.en: He decided to examine the German Army during his vacation.
D3.zh: 他决定在假期中考察德国军队。

D7.en: He obtained an interview without an appointment by overcoming the secretarial outpost.
D7.zh: 他没有预约，通过克服秘书岗哨获得了面试。

D13b.en: He was placed in St Philip's, a Catholic grammar school in Edgbaston.
D13b.zh: 他被安排到埃德巴斯顿的圣菲利普天主教文法学校。

D6.en: He cycled on weekend camping trips with two friends from Stewarts and Lloyds.
D6.zh: 他周末和两位斯图尔特与劳埃德的朋友骑自行车露营。

D18.en: He was taken on as a junior clerk by Stewarts and Lloyds.
D18.zh: 他被斯图尔特与劳埃德录用为初级职员。

D20.en: At 16, he co-founded a stamp-selling partnership with friends.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他和朋友合伙卖邮票。

D9.en: He persuaded them to let him into their fortress alone.
D9.zh: 他说服他们让他独自进入他们的堡垒。

D2.en: He practiced rifle exercises in the solitude of his bedroom.
D2.zh: 他在卧室里独自练习步枪操练。

D5.en: He kept pace with the charge and was bandaged by a Turkish medical orderly.
D5.zh: 他跟上冲锋，并在占领阵地后被一名土耳其医护兵包扎。

D15.en: He kept pace with the charge and was bandaged by a Turkish medical orderly.
D15.zh: 他跟上冲锋，并在占领阵地后被一名土耳其医护兵包扎。

D1.en: He kept pace with the charge and was bandaged by a Turkish orderly.
D1.zh: 他跟上冲锋，被一名土耳其卫生员包扎。

D4.en: He watched Turkish shells hitting a beach, calculated their timing, and predicted the fourth would land in his spot.
D4.zh: 他观察土耳其炮弹击中海滩的规律，计算出第四发会落在自己位置。

D8.en: He borrowed a sergeant's jacket to board a restricted train.
D8.zh: 他借了中士的夹克才登上受限制的火车。

D10.en: He knew he was dying.
D10.zh: 他知道自己快要死了。

M4.en: He witnessed one of the most gallant actions at Anzac.
M4.zh: 他目睹了安扎克最英勇的行动之一。

M7.en: He came together with others at the extreme spearpoint of the British thrust from Anzac.
M7.zh: 他与其他人一起到达英军从安扎克突击的最前沿。

D19.en: He organized everything relevant beautifully and lucidly, impressing Cowan.
D19.zh: 他把所有相关内容整理得漂亮清晰，令考恩印象深刻。

D16.en: He is a good speaker with command and not afraid to express his views.
D16.zh: 他善于演讲，有指挥能力，不怕表达自己的观点。

D12.en: He teaches in elementary school.
D12.zh: 他在小学教书。

D6.en: He accepted the running so good-naturedly that the upper classmen lost interest in hazing him.
D6.zh: 他非常和善地接受了跑步，以至于高年级学生失去了刁难他的兴趣。

D15.en: He kept up walking, running, and swimming when not in competitive sports.
D15.zh: 他不参加竞技运动时，坚持走路、跑步和游泳。

D4.en: He took turns going aboard the Navy's first commissioned submarine for training runs.
D4.zh: 他轮流登上海军第一艘服役潜艇进行训练航行。

D18.en: At age 8, he worked as a delivery boy at a meat market for a dollar a week.
D18.zh: 8岁时，他在肉店当送货员，每周赚一美元。

D10.en: He dreaded a summons that would end his naval career.
D10.zh: 他害怕被传唤，那会终结他的海军生涯。

D9.en: At 19, he commanded the 8th Company as a three-stripe midshipman.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他作为三条杠的见习军官指挥第8连。

M1.en: He stood seventh in overall achievement in his class of 114 graduates.
M1.zh: 他在114名毕业生中总成绩排名第七。

D1.en: At 16, he caught pneumonia, spent a month in hospital, and fell behind in his studies.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他得了肺炎，住院一个月，学习落后了。

D3.en: At 16, he astonished his instructor by solving a problem with his own method, not the book's.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他用自己发明的方法解题，让老师大吃一惊。

D7.en: At 22-23, he attended a beer party.
D7.zh: 22到23岁时，他参加了一个啤酒派对。

D5.en: At 24, he ran the Decatur aground, was court-martialed, and reprimanded.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他让迪凯特号搁浅，被军事法庭审判并受到训斥。

D12.en: At 7-8, he had severe bronchitis that frightened his mother.
D12.zh: 7到8岁时，他得了严重的支气管炎，把他母亲吓坏了。

D3.en: At 14, he wrote a letter to President McKinley.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他给麦金利总统写了一封信。

D7.en: At 18-19, he and a midshipman were discovered absent from quarters at taps.
D7.zh: 18到19岁时，他和一名海军学员在熄灯时被发现不在宿舍。

D2.en: At 17-18, he prepared and struggled through the cruel entrance examination.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他努力准备并艰难通过了残酷的入学考试。

D16.en: At 14, he wrote a letter to President McKinley.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他给麦金利总统写了一封信。

M1.en: At 14, he asked the President for a Naval Academy appointment.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他请求总统给他一个海军学院的任命。

M2.en: At 14, he said his father is a Naval officer.
M2.zh: 14岁时，他说他父亲是一名海军军官。

D5.en: At 9-11, he broke street light globes with a slingshot.
D5.zh: 9到11岁时，他用弹弓打碎了路灯的灯罩。

D15.en: At 21, he and five classmates forfeited graduation leave to secure billets in the battleship Missouri.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他和五名同学放弃了毕业休假，以确保在密苏里号战列舰上的职位。

D4.en: At 9-11, he broke street light globes with a slingshot.
D4.zh: 9到11岁时，他用弹弓打碎了路灯的灯罩。

D6.en: He was elected president of the athletic association and awarded the Thompson Trophy Cup.
D6.zh: 他被选为体育协会主席，并获得了汤普森奖杯。

D14.en: He took some pleasure in marching at the second inauguration of President McKinley.
D14.zh: 他乐于在麦金利总统的第二次就职典礼上列队行进。

D1.en: He stood practically every type of watch and served as assistant engineer, then was appointed division officer.
D1.zh: 他几乎值过所有类型的班，担任过助理工程师，随后被任命为部门军官。

D20.en: At 17, he was inducted into the Delta Psi fraternity and wore its emblem on his watch chain.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他加入德尔塔普赛兄弟会，并把会徽挂在表链上。

M7.en: He crammed for the Naval Academy entrance exam and was sworn in as a naval cadet on 7 July 1900.
M7.zh: 他拼命备考海军学院的入学考试，并于1900年7月7日宣誓成为海军学员。

D18.en: He was invited to parties and introduced to pretty girls, and he made the most of the situation.
D18.zh: 他被邀请参加派对并被介绍给漂亮女孩，他充分利用了这种情况。

D8.en: He ordered a man to drop his weapon, and the man refused.
D8.zh: 他命令一名男子放下武器，但对方拒绝了。

D13a.en: At age ~20, he was caught not chewing tobacco by Halsey and admitted it.
D13a.zh: 20岁左右时，他被哈尔西发现没嚼烟草并承认了。

D12.en: At 8, he was taken to see his father's body after his death.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他被带去看了父亲的遗体。

D4.en: At 19, he designed and submitted a timed percussion fuse to the War Office.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他设计并提交了一个定时碰炸引信给陆军部。

D11.en: At 25-30, he said The Choir Invisible was the only book he read three times.
D11.zh: 25到30岁间，他说《看不见的唱诗班》是他唯一读过三遍的书。

D2.en: At 24, he added up his game book and found satisfactory totals for his first year in India.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他统计了狩猎记录，发现第一年在印度的总成绩令人满意。

D3.en: At 19, he designed and submitted a timed percussion fuse to the War Office.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他设计并提交了一个定时碰炸引信给陆军部。

D6.en: At 25-30, he made a mark as likeable, witty, and an excellent companion.
D6.zh: 25到30岁间，他给人留下可爱、机智、好相处的印象。

D17.en: At 12, he was sent to a German-run school in Pau for sons of English families.
D17.zh: 12岁时，他被送到波城一所德国人办的学校，那里招收英国家庭的儿子。

D15.en: At 25-30, he amused himself by shooting natives on the backside as they stooped over.
D15.zh: 25到30岁间，他拿射土著人的屁股取乐。

M1.en: At about 16, he came in 65th out of 72, at the tail end.
M1.zh: 大约16岁时，他排名第65，倒数第8。

D1.en: At 25, he failed the Staff College exam due to Military Law, then set aside that goal.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他因军事法不及格而没通过参谋学院考试，之后放弃了考取的想法。

D14.en: At 19, he won a silver medal from the French Ambassador for a French language competition.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他赢得了法国大使颁发的法语竞赛银牌。

D5.en: At 23, he volunteered for service in India.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他自愿去印度服役。

D9.en: At 20, he helped someone join the Somaliland campaign, boosting that person's reputation.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他帮助某人参加了索马里兰战役，提升了那人的声誉。

D12.en: He left home in Novosibirsk to seek his fortune at aviation school.
D12.zh: 他离开新西伯利亚的家，去航校寻找出路。

D7.en: He wrote a personal letter to Reichsmarschall Goering in anger.
D7.zh: 他愤怒地给帝国元帅戈林写了一封私人信件。

D16.en: He wrote a personal letter to Reichsmarschall Goering in anger.
D16.zh: 他愤怒地给帝国元帅戈林写了一封私人信件。

D9.en: He requested a transfer back to his Gruppe in JG-52 on the Eastern Front.
D9.zh: 他请求调回东线JG-52联队自己的大队。

D10.en: He stood gazing at the fiery spectacle, fascinated by its drama and violence.
D10.zh: 他站着凝视那火焰景象，被它的戏剧性和暴力迷住了。

M4.en: He stayed with defenseless civilians, a decision that cost him over ten years of his life.
M4.zh: 他留在手无寸铁的平民身边，这个决定让他付出了十多年生命的代价。

M7.en: He stayed with defenseless civilians, a decision that cost him over ten years of his life.
M7.zh: 他留在手无寸铁的平民身边，这个决定让他付出了十多年生命的代价。

D6.en: He belonged to the Glider Gang, a group of boys from Frau Hartmann's gliding club.
D6.zh: 他属于滑翔机帮，那是哈特曼夫人滑翔俱乐部的一群男孩。

D5.en: He dove through fighters, briefly firing at selected targets on the way.
D5.zh: 他俯冲穿过战斗机群，途中短暂射击选中的目标。

D2.en: He completed basic flying training by 14 October 1941 and was ready for advanced course.
D2.zh: 他在1941年10月14日完成了基础飞行训练，准备进入高级课程。

D4.en: He took his first flight in military training on 5 March 1941 in a BT-NB trainer.
D4.zh: 他于1941年3月5日首次在军事训练中驾驶BT-NB教练机飞行。

D3.en: Every weekend he was taken by his mother to gliding meets and took his turn with the other boys.
D3.zh: 每个周末他都被母亲带去参加滑翔机集会，并和其他男孩轮流飞行。

D15.en: He completed a free ski jump off a chalet roof, eighteen feet above the professor's head.
D15.zh: 他从小木屋屋顶完成了一次自由滑雪跳跃，离教授头顶十八英尺。

D13b.en: He found that the aviation school was strictly for mechanics and not for flying training.
D13b.zh: 他发现航空学校严格只教机械，不教飞行训练。

D1.en: He retraced his steps to the security of his fairy-tale valley with its stream and trees.
D1.zh: 他沿原路返回，回到那个有溪流和树木的童话般安全的山谷。

M1.en: The Knight's Cross seemed a remote and unattainable goal to him in January and February 1943.
M1.zh: 在1943年1月和2月，骑士十字勋章对他来说似乎是一个遥远而无法达到的目标。

D14.en: On 24 August 1942, he flew to Zerbst and demonstrated aerobatics over the airfield, buzzing with snap rolls and Cuban eights.
D14.zh: 1942年8月24日，他飞到策尔布斯特，在机场上空表演特技，用快滚和古巴八字俯冲。

M3.en: He was inspired during his Siberian boyhood by the achievements of Russian pioneer aviator Valery Chkalov.
M3.zh: 他在西伯利亚童年时期受到俄罗斯先驱飞行员瓦列里·契卡洛夫成就的启发。

D7.en: As a child and teenager, he refused to participate in organized sport.
D7.zh: 8到17岁时，他拒绝参加有组织的体育运动。

D2.en: At 18-19, he studied Xenophon's Anabasis and Caesar's De Bello Gallico.
D2.zh: 18到19岁时，他学习了色诺芬的《长征记》和凯撒的《高卢战记》。

D19.en: At 21, he drew up a detailed expense statement of £71 8s. 6d.
D19.zh: 21岁时，他详细列出了71英镑8先令6便士的开支。

D3.en: At 25, he found night digging exciting: plunging down shafts and smashing stone doors.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他觉得夜间挖掘很刺激：跳下竖井，砸碎石门。

M4.en: At 24, he felt the call of Syria and returned to Carchemish.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他感到叙利亚的召唤，回到了卡赫美士。

D4.en: At 25, he described digging as exciting: plunging down shafts and smashing stone doors.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他描述挖掘很刺激：跳下竖井，砸碎石门。

M2.en: At 24, he admired Petrie but found him dogmatic and opinionated.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他钦佩皮特里，但认为他教条且固执己见。

M7.en: At 24, Arabs cheered and fired salvoes for him.
M7.zh: 24岁时，阿拉伯人向他欢呼并鸣枪致意。

D1.en: At 22, he felt the idea of digging on was glorious, with hope for several parts of the digs.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他觉得继续挖掘的想法很光荣，因为挖掘的几个部分都有希望。

D11.en: At 24, he wrote an essay for Isis magazine about the Qasr of Ibn Wardani.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他为《伊西斯》杂志写了一篇关于伊本·瓦尔达尼城堡的文章。

D6.en: At 24, he and another archaeologist cultivated friendship with local Kurdish leaders.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他和另一位考古学家努力与当地库尔德领袖建立友谊。

D5.en: At 25, he noted Kurds were quiet but would go all out if matters got bad, with Arabs and their men joining.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他注意到库尔德人很安静，但如果局势恶化就会全力行动，阿拉伯人和他们的人也会加入。

D8.en: As a teenager, he was the dominant partner and initiator of their schemes.
D8.zh: 十几岁时，他是他们计划中的主导者和发起人。

D9.en: At 22, he agreed to form a loose business partnership and made plans for setting up a press.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他同意组建一个松散的商业伙伴关系，并计划建立印刷厂。

M1.en: At 24, he felt great admiration for Petrie but thought him dogmatic and opinionated.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他对皮特里非常钦佩，但认为他教条且固执己见。

D16.en: At 19, he overheard the young landlady of the Pelican Hotel discussing him with her family.
D16.zh: 19岁时，他无意中听到鹈鹕酒店的女房东和家人谈论他。

D10.en: He was bellowed at and sent away by Lawrence in a blind rage for no apparent reason.
D10.zh: 他无缘无故被劳伦斯暴怒地大吼大叫并赶走。

D17.en: At 23, he gave Hammoudi a letter to his father stating that if he died, the Hoja was not to blame.
D17.zh: 23岁时，他给哈穆迪一封信，信中告诉父亲如果他死了，不要责怪霍贾。

D20X.en: At 24, he was tempted but the call of Syria proved too strong, and by end of February he was back at Carchemish.
D20X.zh: 24岁时，他虽受诱惑，但叙利亚的召唤太强烈，2月底他回到了卡赫美士。

M3.en: At 23, he was thinking increasingly of Doughty's example.
M3.zh: 23岁时，他越来越想到道蒂的榜样。

M5.en: At 24, he and Woolley decided to confront the Governor.
M5.zh: 24岁时，他和伍利决定去质问总督。

M6.en: At 23, he said the Arab life is the only one that still holds the early poetry which is the easiest to read.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他说阿拉伯生活是唯一仍保有最易读的早期诗歌的生活。

M8.en: At 24, he felt great admiration for Petrie but thought him dogmatic and opinionated.
M8.zh: 24岁时，他非常钦佩皮特里，但认为他教条且固执己见。

D3.en: As soon as he could read, he began reading the Davar newspaper to her during breakfast.
D3.zh: 他刚学会阅读，就开始在早餐时给她读《达瓦尔》报纸。

D13a.en: He turned to the infantry in the Haganah and abandoned his role as an instructor in the Gadna.
D13a.zh: 他转向哈加纳的步兵，放弃了在加德纳的教官角色。

D6.en: He started his military career in the Gadna, a paramilitary high school organization.
D6.zh: 他的军事生涯始于加德纳，一个准军事的高中组织。

D2.en: He had to wake up in the dark and work in the fields before taking the bus to school.
D2.zh: 他不得不在天黑时起床，在田里干活，然后坐公交车去上学。

D9.en: He crossed the border with a small group of soldiers and killed Samueli in his home.
D9.zh: 他带着一小队士兵越过边境，在塞缪利的家中杀死了他。

D13b.en: At his father's request, he registered at the Hebrew University's extension in Rehovot to study agriculture.
D13b.zh: 应父亲的要求，他在雷霍沃特的希伯来大学分校注册学习农业。

M2.en: He learned that family solidarity was all-important.
M2.zh: 他学到了家庭团结至关重要。

D14.en: At 19, he burst into a friend's house and woke him with a homemade stun grenade.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他冲进朋友家，用自制的眩晕弹把朋友弄醒。

D5.en: At 22, he volunteered for every complex and high-risk combat mission.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他自愿参加每一项复杂且高风险的战斗任务。

D8.en: At 24, he had no difficulty imposing his leadership on the soldiers.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他毫不费力地向士兵们施加他的领导力。

D10.en: At 19, he ran through crowds shouting with glee.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他跑过人群，高兴地大喊。

M1.en: At 25, he pressed the general staff to capture the police station at Latroun.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他敦促总参谋部攻占拉特伦警察局。

D1.en: At 20, he was hospitalized again and again hurried back to his unit prematurely.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他再次住院，又再次提前赶回部队。

D19.en: He attended classes regularly and kept orderly notes.
D19.zh: 他按时上课，笔记记得很整齐。

D11.en: He felt a deep sense of belonging while resting in an olive grove before battle.
D11.zh: 他在战斗前躺在橄榄树林里，感到与土地融为一体，有归属感。

D18.en: At age eight he began picking oranges and loading them onto the family's horse-drawn wagon.
D18.zh: 8岁时他开始摘橙子，并把它们装上家里的马车。

D3.en: At 20, he gave a concise proof to a problem he had pondered while reading.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他对自己看书时思考过的问题给出了一个简明的证明。

D12.en: At 18-19, he did chores for Mr. Wu, including cleaning, chopping wood, and massaging Mrs. Wu.
D12.zh: 18到19岁时，他替吴先生干家务，打扫、劈柴、挑水，还给师母捶背按摩。

D9.en: At 25, he turned down higher positions at other universities to take a lower one at Columbia.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他放弃了其他大学的高职，选择了哥伦比亚大学的较低职位。

D4.en: At 20, he gave a concise proof to a problem he had pondered while reading.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他对自己看书时思考过的问题给出了一个简明的证明。

D5.en: At 16-17, he decided to leave Ganzhou for Guizhou to take the college entrance exam as a non-graduate.
D5.zh: 16到17岁时，他决心离开赣州奔赴贵州，以同等学历报考浙大。

D2.en: At 22, he started two plans: losing weight and learning to dance.
D2.zh: 22岁时，他着手实施两项计划：减肥和学习跳舞。

D6.en: At 21, he took a road trip to the West with Yang Zhenning and Ling Ning in a used car.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他和杨振宁、凌宁开着二手车去西部旅行。

D20.en: At 22, he and Rosenbluth and Yang Zhenning decided to systematically study a problem.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他和罗森布鲁斯、杨振宁决定系统探讨一个问题。

M1.en: At 25, he chose Columbia's lower position over higher offers, citing its academic and management level.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他选择了哥伦比亚大学的较低职位，理由是该校学术和管理水平较高。

M6.en: As a child, he developed a strong character through war and poverty.
M6.zh: 他从小在战乱和穷困中养成了刚毅的性格。

D20X.en: At 24, he left Yerkes Observatory for UC Berkeley after half a year.
D20X.zh: 24岁时，他半年后离开约克斯天文台去伯克利加州大学工作。

D1.en: At 22, he anxiously awaited a reply, lovesick and losing weight.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他像热锅上的蚂蚁等回信，相思消瘦。

M4.en: At 25, he refused a position he saw as political discrimination.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他认为这是政治歧视，拒绝了职位。

M7.en: At 25, he was told by his partner she didn't want kids in a discriminatory West Coast.
M7.zh: 25岁时，秦惠对他说不愿下一代生活在歧视华人的西海岸。

D2.en: At 15, he taught himself analytic geometry in 3 weeks amid a noisy family funeral, then took the exam.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他在家里办丧事的环境下自学三周解析几何，然后去考试。

D3.en: As a teenager, he spent hours in the library browsing books until closing time.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他常泡在书库里一看就是几小时，直到关门才出来。

D13b.en: At 8, on his first day of school, he saw a teacher hit a student's palm with a ruler and refused to go back.
D13b.zh: 8岁时，入学第一天看到老师用戒尺打学生手心，他吓得第二天不肯上学。

D1.en: At 9, with his uncle's help, he overcame difficulties, finished primary school, and ranked first in class.
D1.zh: 9岁时，在姑丈帮助下克服困难完成初小，期末考全班第一。

M1.en: At 9, with his uncle's help, he overcame difficulties, finished primary school, and ranked first in class.
M1.zh: 9岁时，在姑丈帮助下克服困难完成初小，期末考全班第一。

D11.en: At 24, he completed his paper on invariant theory of triple nets, which became his doctoral thesis.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他完成了关于三重网不变理论的论文，后来成为博士论文。

D9.en: At 16-19, he was elected to the science committee and was a key academic member of the university weekly.
D9.zh: 16到19岁间，他被选为理科科学会委员，也是校刊学术组骨干。

D6.en: At 16-19, he and Wu Dayou were the center of a friendly, happy group in the science college.
D6.zh: 16到19岁时，他和吴大猷是理学院一个友好快乐集体的核心。

D20.en: At 19, he and Wu Daren both decided to take the exam and both passed.
D20.zh: 19岁时，他和吴大任决定报考，结果双双考中。

D14.en: At 19-20, he worked as a math exam grader, envied by fellow students.
D14.zh: 19到20岁间，他当了一回数学阅卷老师，让同龄学生羡慕不已。

D12.en: He grew up in a large family struggling on his father's civil servant salary.
D12.zh: 他家里人口多，全靠父亲当公务员的薪水维持生活，很艰苦。

M2.en: He resolved to study hard to repay his family's support.
M2.zh: 他暗下决心要好好读书，回报家人的支持。

D5.en: He applied to the University of Hamburg because he admired Brecht.
D5.zh: 他因景仰布莱希特而申请去了德国汉堡大学。

D7.en: He was angered by foreigners looking down on Chinese and vowed to prove himself.
D7.zh: 有些外国人瞧不起中国人，他十分生气，心想期末让他们看看谁才是强者。

D3.en: At 23-24, she firmly chose pharmacognosy and practiced it all her life.
D3.zh: 23到24岁时，她坚定地选择了生药学，并一生付诸实践。

D2.en: At 18, after a two-year break due to illness, she entered Ningbo Xiaoshi High School.
D2.zh: 18岁时，休学两年病情好转后，她进入宁波效实中学高中就读。

D13a.en: At 20-21, she decided on her own to apply to Peking University Medical School's pharmacy department.
D13a.zh: 20到21岁时，她自作主张给自己报了北京大学医学院药学系。

D4.en: At 23-24, she firmly chose pharmacognosy and practiced it all her life.
D4.zh: 23到24岁时，她坚定地选择了生药学，并一生付诸实践。

D6.en: At 18-21, he told stories to siblings and neighbor kids every weekend.
D6.zh: 18到21岁时，他每周末给弟妹和邻居小孩讲故事。

D3.en: At 25-26, he told Teller he solved a nuclear reaction problem using group theory.
D3.zh: 25到26岁时，他告诉泰勒他用群论解决了核反应问题。

D2.en: At 25-26, he told Teller he solved a nuclear reaction problem using group theory.
D2.zh: 25到26岁时，他告诉泰勒他用群论解决了核反应问题。

D12.en: At 21, he wore an old army uniform for his exam photo.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他穿着旧军装拍考试报名照。

D13b.en: As a small child, his mother trained him to use his right hand for eating and writing.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，母亲教他用右手吃饭写字。

D4.en: At 24-25, his lab work in Allison's lab was unsuccessful and had many mishaps.
D4.zh: 24到25岁时，他在艾立逊实验室的实验不成功，闹了很多笑话。

D5.en: At 25, he underestimated the Grand Canyon hike and walked nearly 20 hours.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他低估了大峡谷徒步距离，走了近20小时。

D11.en: At 18-21, he told his siblings stories he read in books.
D11.zh: 18到21岁时，他给弟妹讲书里看来的故事。

D16.en: At 20-22, he debated everything from history to movies with friends.
D16.zh: 20到22岁时，他和朋友争论从历史到电影的一切。

M1.en: At 21, his friends avoided competing against him in the physics exam.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他的朋友为免被他打败而避开物理科目。

D9.en: He told stories from books to his siblings and neighbor kids on weekends.
D9.zh: 他週末回家給弟妹和鄰居小孩講書裡的故事。

D7.en: He scolded his parents for arguing, angering his father.
D7.zh: 他直說父母吵架有失體統，惹父親大怒。

D10.en: At 23, he saw an anxious face and burst into tears after holding them in all morning.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他看到一张焦虑的脸，忍了一早晨的泪水终于爆发。

D13a.en: He decided to stop dating to focus on studying.
D13a.zh: 他覺得不該交女朋友，宜專心唸書，於是冷淡下來。

D17.en: He gave a pair of new shoes as a gift.
D17.zh: 他送了一雙新鞋子作為禮物。

D19.en: His notebooks from college, filled with neatly written quantum mechanics formulas, were preserved for over half a century.
D19.zh: 他在西南联大的笔记本上整齐写着量子力学公式，保存了半个多世纪。

M4.en: At 20-22, he admired French writer Victor Hugo for his humanitarian concerns.
M4.zh: 20到22岁时，他佩服法国作家雨果的人道主义关怀。

M6.en: He was determined to do an experimental thesis abroad.
M6.zh: 他決心出國做一篇實驗論文。

D7.en: As a child, he was content to go at his own speed and made no extra effort.
D7.zh: 他小时候很满足于按自己的节奏走，不再多努力。

D13a.en: At 24, he would do something a second time, knowing it meant the end of a dream.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他知道再做一次意味着梦想的终结，但他还是做了。

D4.en: At 6-10, he learned to mend shoes and make chairs in the workshop.
D4.zh: 6到10岁时，他在作坊里学会了修鞋和做椅子。

D9.en: At 22, as platoon commander, he had his own room doubling as headquarters.
D9.zh: 22岁时，作为排长，他拥有自己的房间，也兼作排指挥部。

D5.en: At 23, he led his assault force to within 100 yards of the camp fence.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他带领突击队到达距离营地围栏约100码的地方。

D13b.en: At 21-22, he volunteered for the Jewish Legion as part of a plan to settle in Palestine.
D13b.zh: 21到22岁时，他志愿加入犹太军团，作为定居巴勒斯坦计划的一部分。

M4.en: At 23, he decided he might have to kill the man who opened the way to Redemption in 1967.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他决定可能不得不杀死那个在1967年战争中开辟救赎之路的人。

D1.en: At 17-18, he managed to stay at the top of his class, barely beating his friend.
D1.zh: 17到18岁时，他设法保持班级第一，勉强击败了他的朋友。

D20.en: He was chosen to represent the Legion soldiers on Jabotinsky's committee.
D20.zh: 他被选为代表军团士兵参加雅博廷斯基的委员会。

D6.en: In 1944, he met a young high school girl from Tel Aviv named Leah Schlossberg.
D6.zh: 1944年，他遇到了一位来自特拉维夫的高中女生，名叫莉亚·施洛斯伯格。

D3.en: As a small child, he visited a synagogue across the street and got to know its rabbi well.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他去了街对面的犹太会堂，并和那里的拉比熟络起来。

D2.en: For two weeks he carried out intensive physiotherapy to strengthen his leg.
D2.zh: 他进行了两周的高强度理疗来加强腿部力量。

D15.en: He picked up a petrified child who could not control his bladder.
D15.zh: 他抱起一个吓得尿失禁的孩子。

M1.en: At 17-18, he managed to remain at the head of his class, barely beating out his friend Moshe Netzer.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，他设法保持班级第一，勉强击败了他的朋友摩西·内策尔。

D14.en: At 19, he addressed hundreds of kibbutz members in shorts and sandals, describing a military operation.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他穿着短裤和凉鞋向数百名基布兹成员讲述了一次军事行动。

D16.en: At 19, he gave a lengthy description of the operation, demonstrating precise and lucid military analysis.
D16.zh: 19岁时，他详细描述了行动，展现出精准清晰的军事分析能力。

M6.en: At 24, he had his application to UC approved and would study to be an irrigation engineer.
M6.zh: 24岁时，他申请加州大学被批准，将学习成为灌溉工程师。

M2.en: At 21, he wondered what his father would think and if he was honoring his mother's memory.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他想知道父亲会怎么看他，以及他是否在纪念母亲。

D11.en: At 24-25, he suggested schemes like booby-trapping Rabin's car or shooting him with a missile.
D11.zh: 24到25岁时，他提出了一些计划，比如在拉宾的车上设陷阱或用导弹射击他。

M5.en: At 24, he was sensitive about his Yemenite origin and tried to prove he was better than Ashkenazis.
M5.zh: 24岁时，他对自己的也门血统很敏感，总是试图证明自己比阿什肯纳兹人更聪明、更优秀。

D12.en: At 20, he graduated early to serve the nation's urgent needs.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他提前一年毕业，奔赴国家急需的岗位。

D2.en: At 15-17, he memorized a whole book after saying it was easy with effort.
D2.zh: 15到17岁时，他说多花功夫就能背下整本书，后来果然背出来了。

D18.en: As a small child, he diligently scooped water despite his small strength.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他力气小，但舀水干得一丝不苟。

D6.en: As a child, he played happily with his brother and friends using slingshots.
D6.zh: 小时候，他带着弟弟和同学玩弹弓，打麻雀和蝉，玩得很开心。

D17.en: At age 8-12, his stepmother fed him milk with a spoon when he couldn't eat due to swollen lips.
D17.zh: 8到12岁时，后母用汤匙喂他牛奶。

D3.en: At 15-17, he borrowed college-level books like Calculus and Physics while in middle school.
D3.zh: 15到17岁时，他借阅大学丛书《微积分学》《达夫物理学》等。

D5.en: At 16-17, he applied to Xiamen University as an equivalency candidate before graduating high school.
D5.zh: 16到17岁时，他以同等学力报考厦门大学，当时还没高中毕业。

D7.en: At 16-17, he insisted he would walk to Xiamen University if needed.
D7.zh: 16到17岁时，他倔强地说就是走路也要走到厦大去。

D1.en: At 21, he was rescued by President Wang who gave him a job in the math department library.
D1.zh: 21岁时，王亚南校长把他带回厦大，让他在数学系资料室工作，他获救了。

D9.en: At 23, he chose number theory as his breakthrough and studied Hua Luogeng's works.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他选择数论作为突破口，钻研华罗庚的著作。

M1.en: At 23, he chose number theory as his breakthrough and studied Hua Luogeng's works.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他选择数论作为突破口，钻研华罗庚的著作。

D16.en: At 23, he was assigned to the number theory and algebra group at Peking University.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他被分配到北京大学的数论代数分组。

D10.en: At 23, he felt lonely and panicked on stage under the gaze of 30 mathematicians.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他站在讲台上，被30多位数学家注视，感到孤独无助和惊慌。

D19.en: As a teenager, he accepted criticism and wrote neatly from then on.
D19.zh: 十几岁时，他虚心接受批评，从此把字写得工工整整。

D14.en: Around age 50, he sang songs and danced with his family.
D14.zh: 大约50岁时，他高兴起来会唱歌，一家三口又唱又跳。

D2.en: After closing the shop and eating a quick meal, he returned to his small wooden room to study math by lamplight.
D2.zh: 他关上店门，胡乱吃几口饭，就回小木板房里点起油灯攻读数学。

D3.en: At 14, he excitedly showed his teacher a two-page paper attempting to prove Fermat's Last Theorem.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他兴冲冲地给老师看一篇自己写的论文，试图证明费马最后定理。

D7.en: At 15, he defied his teacher by presenting his own new problem-solving method on the blackboard.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他走上讲台写出自己创造的新解题方法，老师却固执地认为不对。

D13a.en: At 15, he decided to drop out of school, giving up his diploma just one semester before graduation.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他决定退学，放弃了差一学期就毕业的机会，没拿到文凭。

D12.en: At 15-16, he silently opened the shop, cleaned, stood at the counter until 7 pm, and settled accounts in ten minutes.
D12.zh: 15到16岁时，他默默地下门板、扫地、站柜台到晚上七点，十分钟算清一天账。

D10.en: He was so distressed that he fainted on the spot.
D10.zh: 他心疼至极，当场晕倒了。

D18.en: At 15-19, the only math books he could find in Jintan were a few algebra, geometry, and calculus texts, plus two magazines.
D18.zh: 15到19岁时，他在金坛能见到的数学书只有几本代数、几何、微积分，还有两本杂志。

M1.en: He chose number theory as his initial research field due to his skill and love for problem-solving.
M1.zh: 他因擅长和喜爱解题技巧，选择了数论作为最初的研究领域。

M6.en: He declared, 'I will use a sound mind to replace my unsound legs!'
M6.zh: 他宣称：“我要用健全的头脑，代替不健全的双腿！”

D9.en: He was invited by Xiong Qinglai to work in Beiping and arrived at Tsinghua University in summer 1931.
D9.zh: 熊庆来邀请他来北平工作，他于1931年夏到达清华大学。

D11.en: He wrote several articles in elementary mathematics while self-studying in Jintan.
D11.zh: 他在金坛时期边自学，边写了几篇初等数学文章。

D14.en: He published a paper criticizing Su Jiaju's algebraic equation solution, impressing Xiong Qinglai.
D14.zh: 他发表了论文“苏家驹之代数的五次方程式不能成立之理由”，受到熊庆来赏识。

D19.en: He wrote several articles in elementary mathematics while self-studying in Jintan.
D19.zh: 他在金坛时期边自学，边写了几篇初等数学文章。

D13b.en: He was transferred to Tsinghua University's mathematics department as an assistant in 1931.
D13b.zh: 1931年，他被调到清华大学算学系任助理员。

M2.en: He felt deep gratitude toward his motherland, teachers, relatives, and friends who helped him.
M2.zh: 他对养育他的祖国、培养他的老师、帮助过他的亲友感恩至深。

M3.en: He felt deep gratitude toward his motherland, teachers, and friends who helped him.
M3.zh: 他对养育他的祖国、培养他的老师、帮助过他的亲友感恩至深。

M8.en: He felt deep gratitude to his motherland, teachers, and friends.
M8.zh: 他对祖国、老师和亲友感恩至深。

D6.en: He often dined and strolled with other students from Jintan, discussing math and more.
D6.zh: 他常与金坛来的学生一起晚餐、散步，谈论数学等。

D5.en: At 19, he contracted typhoid and arthritis, leaving him lame.
D5.zh: 19岁时他得了伤寒和关节炎，腿瘸了。

D3.en: At 7, he listened to his mother's vivid story of ancient times and imagined a farmer emerging on the wild land.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他听母亲讲述远古故事，想象着荒原上浮现出一个农人的身影。

D15.en: At 10, he dared to swim across the Yangtze River.
D15.zh: 10岁时，他敢横渡长江。

M1.en: At 18, he won first place in the men's 100m and 400m freestyle in Hankou.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他夺得汉口赛区男子100米和400米自由泳两个第一名。

D14.en: At 23-30, he played his violin under an ancient camphor tree, letting dreamy music flow.
D14.zh: 23到30岁间，他在古樟树下拉小提琴，让梦幻般的音乐流淌。

D7.en: At 23-30, he was not intimidated by books even though he loved them.
D7.zh: 23到30岁间，他虽爱书但不被书本吓倒。

D11.en: At 9-10, he wished the falling Japanese bombs were grains of rice.
D11.zh: 9到10岁时，他希望天上落下的日本炸弹是一粒粒谷子。

M7.en: As a child, his unsettled childhood fostered his patriotism and sympathy toward the people.
M7.zh: 幼年时，动荡的童年培养了他的爱国情怀和对人民的同情。

D6.en: As a child, he sat under a tree with siblings while his mother told stories.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他搬小板凳坐在树下听母亲讲故事。

D18.en: He dragged his brother into the river swimming, so they'd be punished together.
D18.zh: 他拉上弟弟一起下河游泳，这样挨打也一起挨。

D5.en: He competed with a stomachache after overeating.
D5.zh: 他吃坏肚子，忍痛参加了比赛。

D9.en: He joined the university choir.
D9.zh: 他加入了大学生合唱团。

M4.en: Seeing starving people made him believe agriculture is the most direct way to help.
M4.zh: 他看到饥民饿死街头，觉得农业是造福人类最直接的方式。

D16.en: He vowed never to write clichés like 'time flies' again.
D16.zh: 他发誓再也不写‘光阴似箭’这类成语。

D1.en: He tested hybrid sweet potato seeds but got no tubers in the second generation.
D1.zh: 他试验月光花和红薯嫁接的种子，第二代却不结红薯了。

D10.en: He blushed but stood tall when classmates laughed at him.
D10.zh: 同学们哄笑时，他倔强地挺着腰杆，脖子根都红了。

D13a.en: His parents respected his choice, and he entered the agriculture department.
D13a.zh: 父母尊重他的选择，他进了私立相辉学院的农艺系。

D20.en: He learned to play the violin from a Hong Kong classmate.
D20.zh: 他跟香港同学梁元冈学会了拉小提琴。

M2.en: At 20, he saw starving people die in the streets and felt agriculture was the most direct way to help humanity.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他看到饥民饿死街头，觉得农业是造福人类最直接的方式。

M6.en: At 20, he was admitted to the agronomy department of private Xianghui College.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他如愿进了私立相辉学院的农艺系。

M5.en: At 9-10, as the older brother, he had to maintain his dignity in front of his younger brother.
M5.zh: 9到10岁时，他是哥，在弟弟面前必须保持男子汉的尊严。

M8.en: At 9-10, he wished the falling objects were grains instead of Japanese bombs.
M8.zh: 9到10岁时，他希望天上落下的不是日本炸弹，而是一粒粒谷子。

D7.en: At 7, he boldly skipped school because he found classroom learning boring.
D7.zh: 7岁时，他因为觉得课堂学习枯燥而大胆逃学。

D2.en: At 7, he recited texts by the lake every morning, ignoring distractions.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他每天清晨在鹅湖旁背诵课文，从不凑热闹。

D9.en: At 16, he volunteered to try solving a problem despite not having finished.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他举手说愿意试试解题，尽管还没完全解出来。

D1.en: At 15, he returned to Suzhou High School after his uncle promised to pay his tuition.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他因四叔承诺负担学费而回到苏州高中。

D3.en: As a small child, he avidly read all kinds of books in the study.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他在藏书丰富的书斋里如饥似渴地读书。

D11.en: As a small child, he was asked by his uncle to write short titles for stories.
D11.zh: 幼年时，八叔让他为《小说月报》的短文另拟短题目。

D13a.en: At 18, he followed his uncle's advice.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他听从了叔父的建议。

D18.en: At 7, he learned to do embroidery to earn money.
D18.zh: 7岁时，他跟着母亲学会了挑花挣钱。

D4.en: At 5, he practiced writing with a water brush on a polished brick.
D4.zh: 5岁时，他用蘸水的毛笔在磨光的方砖上写字。

D15.en: At 7, he swam in the clear river in summer, catching snails, frogs, and shrimp.
D15.zh: 7岁时，他在夏天去小河里摸螺蛳、捉田鸡、捞小虾。

M4.en: At 18, seeing poorly written Chinese characters, he felt furious and humiliated as a patriot.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他看到歪歪扭扭的中国汉字，感到全身血液上冲，愤怒颤抖，觉得国家和自己受辱。

D19.en: At 19-22, he carried a small notebook to write down questions from his reading.
D19.zh: 19到22岁时，他随身带一个小本子，记下读书中发现的疑问。

D2.en: As a junior, he stubbornly memorized vocabulary word by word, repeating each word up to three times.
D2.zh: 大三时，他每天抱着砖头一样的复习资料一个词一个词地啃，一遍记不住就两遍，两遍不行就三遍。

M1.en: As a junior, he stubbornly memorized vocabulary word by word, repeating each word up to three times.
M1.zh: 大三时，他每天抱着砖头一样的复习资料一个词一个词地啃，一遍记不住就两遍，两遍不行就三遍。

D3.en: At 17, he saw many computer books in the provincial capital bookstore that were unavailable in Yangquan.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他在省城书店看到很多阳泉看不到的计算机书。

D11.en: As a small child, he loved playing Sun Wukong, wrapping a sheet as a skirt and wielding a stick as a staff.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他喜欢演孙悟空，把床单裹在身上当皮裙，挥舞木棍当金箍棒。

D19.en: He made a small notebook to record favorite articles and poems.
D19.zh: 他做了一个小摘抄本，记录喜欢的文章和诗句。

D1.en: At 24, he entered the University at Buffalo half a semester late, taking spring courses and facing great pressure.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他毕业近半学年后进入布法罗大学，直接学春季课程，承受巨大心理压力。

D14.en: As a small child, he joyfully applied to the Shanxi Jin Opera troupe and was admitted after performing.
D14.zh: 幼年时，他兴高采烈地报名山西晋剧团，表演后当场被录取。

D13a.en: As a small child, his father reasoned with him about the importance of studying, telling him it was the only way out.
D13a.zh: 幼年时，他父亲心平气和地和他讲道理，告诉他只有好好学习才是唯一的出路。

M2.en: At about 9, seeing his sister's name on the honor roll, he decided to study hard and go to college.
M2.zh: 9岁左右，他看到大姐的名字在光荣榜上，心中向往，决定读书考大学。

D9.en: At 8, he was appointed as the discipline committee member by his teacher.
D9.zh: 8岁时，他被老师看重，担任班级纪律委员。

D5.en: At 23, he was told his visa was denied because his offer lacked a scholarship, leading the officer to suspect he might immigrate.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他被告知签证被拒，因为他的offer不含奖学金，签证官怀疑他可能移民。

D4.en: In his spare time from age 26 to 29, he conceived the idea of using hyperlink analysis to improve search engines.
D4.zh: 26到29岁间，他利用业余时间想出了用超链分析改进搜索引擎的点子。

D7.en: At age 8-9, when bullied, he would fight back without hesitation.
D7.zh: 8到9岁时，遇到别人欺负他，他毫不示弱，该出手时就出手。

D6.en: As a teenager, he secretly visited each class and found that top students chose science.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他偷偷到各班转了一圈，发现每个班的第一名都选了理科。

D12.en: At 23, he took a market research job to support himself.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他随便找了一份广告市场调研的工作来养活自己。

D12.en: As a child, he worked alongside his father at farming tasks like herding cattle and transplanting rice.
D12.zh: 他小时候就和父亲一起干农活，放牛、插秧等。

D2.en: He balanced farm work with study while reading with his grandfather.
D2.zh: 他一边跟祖父读书，一边参加劳动，过着半耕半读的生活。

D3.en: In his aunt's attic, he learned many novel things that broadened his horizons.
D3.zh: 在姑妈的小阁楼里，他知道了很多新鲜事，大开眼界。

D7.en: At 23, he gave up railway engineering to study chemical engineering at Tsinghua.
D7.zh: 他23岁时放弃铁路工程，改学化工。

D5.en: At 21, he decided to quit his railway job to apply for Tsinghua despite friends' warnings.
D5.zh: 他21岁时不顾朋友劝阻，弃职报考清华留美预备学堂。

M1.en: At 21, he scored exactly 1000 points across ten subjects.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他十门功课正好考了一千分。

D13a.en: At 14, he married Zhang Shuchun as arranged by his parents.
D13a.zh: 他14岁时奉父母之命与张淑春结婚。

D1.en: At 16, he was expelled for participating in an anti-US strike, then studied at Chen Baochen's school for a year.
D1.zh: 他16岁时因参加反美罢课被开除，后在陈宝琛所办中学学习一年。

D4.en: At age 7, he launched a paper airplane he had made, which flew high and far, drawing cheers.
D4.zh: 7岁时，他放飞自己做的纸飞机，飞得又高又远，引来欢呼。

D3.en: As a child, he loved reading the magazine 'Kaiming Youth Monthly', which promoted national pride.
D3.zh: 少年时，他最爱读《开明少年月刊》，这本杂志宣扬民族自尊。

D6.en: He often organized family concerts on holidays, acting as conductor and performer.
D6.zh: 他常在节假日策划家庭音乐会，既当指挥又当表演者。

D9.en: At age 12-13, he became a key organizer in baseball and billiards due to his authority and skill.
D9.zh: 12到13岁时，他凭威信和球技成为棒球和桌球的重要组织者。

D2.en: At age 20-23, he planned experiments meticulously and repeated them many times for accuracy.
D2.zh: 20到23岁时，他每次实验都制订周详计划，为准确结果重复多次。

D14.en: He often organized family concerts on holidays, acting as conductor and performer.
D14.zh: 他常在节假日策划家庭音乐会，既当指挥又当表演者。

D15.en: At 13, he won the provincial table tennis championship.
D15.zh: 13岁时，他赢得了全省桌球比赛冠军。

D18.en: As a child, he saved his New Year's money and carefully checked if he could afford books he liked.
D18.zh: 小时候，他把压岁钱攒下来，看到喜欢的书先记下价钱，再回家数钱看是否买得起。

D7.en: At age 13, he told the principal, 'See, we won because of our skill, not divine blessing.'
D7.zh: 13岁时，他对校长说：'我们打赢了，是我们技术好，不是神灵保佑。'

D11.en: At age 13, he expressed in his autobiography his strong desire to become a scientist and save the nation through science.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他在自传中抒发了当科学家的热切愿望和科学救国的理念。

D13a.en: At 19, he agreed to the recommendation, respecting his parents' wishes.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他同意了保送，尊重父母的意愿。

D12.en: At 5, he started elementary school and also studied classics at a private tutor's home.
D12.zh: 5岁时，他上小学，还在私塾读《左传》《论语》等经典。

D2.en: At 24-25, he skipped lectures he already knew and used the time to study cutting-edge physics.
D2.zh: 24到25岁间，他一次也没去听过已会的课，把时间全用来钻研物理学前沿。

D3.en: As a teenager, he often visited secondhand bookstalls to find banned progressive books.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他常去旧书摊找被禁的进步书籍。

D7.en: As a teenager, after a meeting, he tore up his paper flag and stomped on it in anger.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，会后他扯碎纸旗，还扔在地上狠狠踩了一脚。

D6.en: As a teenager, he gathered with radical classmates to discuss world affairs and inspire each other.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他和一群思想激进的同学聚会，议论天下大事。

D11.en: At 17-21, he recited ancient Chinese poetry with Yang Zhenning under a tree by the school wall.
D11.zh: 17到21岁间，他和杨振宁在校园墙根树旁一起念古诗。

D15.en: At 17, while traveling to Chongqing for college exams, he narrowly escaped Japanese bombing near the Yangtze River.
D15.zh: 17岁时，去重庆考大学途中，在长江边险遭日军飞机轰炸。

D18.en: At 24-25, he could only afford simple meals like bread and sausage, and had to budget carefully.
D18.zh: 24到25岁间，他只能吃面包香肠，得计划着吃。

D16.en: At 11-13, he often quoted Turgenev to his brother: 'Don't be a giant of words, a dwarf of action.'
D16.zh: 11到13岁时，他常对弟弟说屠格涅夫的话：不要做言语的巨人，行动的矮子。

M1.en: At 23, he worked as a teaching assistant, studied hard, and passed the exam to study in the US.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他边做助教边学习，通过了赴美研究生考试。

M4.en: At 13, he felt national humiliation and began discussing China's future with friends.
M4.zh: 13岁时，他感到民族屈辱，开始和朋友讨论国家前途。

D10.en: At 22, he hugged his mother tightly and kissed her, calling her 'Mama'.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他紧紧拥抱母亲，亲吻她并叫着“姆妈”。

D4.en: At 17-21, he copied an entire rare book by hand to avoid delaying others.
D4.zh: 17到21岁间，他手抄了一本难得的书，以免耽误别人阅读。

D5.en: As a child, he accidentally fell into icy water and was fished out shivering.
D5.zh: 幼年时，他一个箭步掉进冰水里，被人捞上来时浑身哆嗦。

D9.en: At 22-24, he actively worked in the Peking University staff association, raising funds for poor students.
D9.zh: 22到24岁间，他积极参加北大讲助会工作，为贫困学生募集钱款物资。

D20.en: At 12-15, he became good friends with Yang Zhenning, who was two grades above him at Chongde Middle School.
D20.zh: 12到15岁间，他在崇德中学与高两班的杨振宁成为好友。

M7.en: At 22-24, he firmly believed the Communist-led liberation would succeed and a new China would be born.
M7.zh: 22到24岁间，他坚信共产党领导的人民解放事业会成功，新中国将诞生。

D1.en: At 23, he worked as a teaching assistant, studied hard, and passed the exam to study in the US.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他边做助教边学习，通过了赴美研究生考试。

D13a.en: At 16, he was sent to Jiangjin by his sister, entered senior year, and graduated from National No.9 High School in 1941.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他被大姐送到江津，插班高三，1941年从国立九中高中毕业。

D17.en: At 17-21, he generously helped younger students, not sparing his valuable time.
D17.zh: 17到21岁间，他热情帮助低年级同学，毫不吝惜自己的时间。

D19.en: He copied every important part of the book without missing a word.
D19.zh: 他把全书重要的地方一字不漏地抄了下来。

D13b.en: At 16, he was sent by his sister to Jiangjin, Sichuan, where he entered senior high and graduated in July 1941.
D13b.zh: 16岁时，大姐遵照父命把他送到四川江津，他插班入高三，于1941年7月在国立九中高中毕业。

D8.en: He said to his brother, 'Now I have only hatred, no tears.'
D8.zh: 他对槜先说：“现在我只有仇恨，没有眼泪。”

D14.en: He and Yang Zhenning recited ancient poems together by a tree at the school.
D14.zh: 他和杨振宁在树旁一起念古诗，一个人拿书对照，另一个人背诵。

D20X.en: He worked as a teaching assistant while studying hard and preparing for the exam to study in the US.
D20X.zh: 他一边当助教，一边勤奋学习，准备去美国留学的考试。

M5.en: The huge gap between US and Chinese technology hurt his national pride.
M5.zh: 美国与中国科技水平的巨大差距刺伤了他的民族自尊心。

M8.en: At 22-24, he believed the Communist Party would lead to a new China.
M8.zh: 22到24岁间，他坚信共产党会领导出一个新中国。

M2.en: He said he would return after finishing his studies because the motherland would need talent.
M2.zh: 他说学成一定回来，因为祖国建设需要人才。

D5.en: After earning his master's at MIT, he decided to transfer to Caltech.
D5.zh: 在麻省理工获得硕士后，他决定转学到加州理工。

D2.en: He took a course in 1933-34 and got the highest average score of 90 among 14 students.
D2.zh: 他1933年下半年选修这门课，两学期平均90分，是14名学生中成绩最好的。

D9.en: At age 23-24, he co-founded a rocket club with three others in 1936.
D9.zh: 23到24岁间，他与三人共同创立火箭俱乐部。

D4.en: He repeatedly calculated, drew blueprints, and test-fired rockets, with one explosion killing three people.
D4.zh: 他反复计算、画图、试制火箭，有一次爆炸炸死了三人。

D12.en: He was born in Shanghai on December 11, 1911, the same year as the Xinhai Revolution.
D12.zh: 他于1911年12月11日在上海出生，是辛亥革命的同龄人。
