D13a.en: He said he spent 20 years in the US, first studying then working, all to prepare to serve his country.
D13a.zh: 他说他在美国20年，前几年学习后十几年工作，都是为了回国为人民做事。

D6.en: In 1933, his name appeared on the rosters of the band, orchestra, poetry society, and harmonica club.
D6.zh: 1933年，他的名字出现在军乐队、管弦乐队、诗社和口琴会的名单上。

D14.en: At 21, he played the French horn (Euphony).
D14.zh: 21岁时，他演奏了圆号（Euphony）。

D19.en: He spotted a mistake on his perfect exam paper and asked the teacher to deduct points.
D19.zh: 他发现满分试卷上有个小错误，主动请求老师扣分。

D1.en: He collected references and wrote 450 pages of notes, correcting predecessors' errors.
D1.zh: 他收集阅读大量参考文献，写了450页笔记，改正前人不足。

D11.en: He designed the class emblem and alumni directory cover for his 1934 class.
D11.zh: 他设计了1934年级徽和校友通讯录封面。

M1.en: He graduated first in his class from Jiaotong University with an average of 89.10.
M1.zh: 他以89.10分毕业于交通大学机械工程学院，位列第一。

M4.en: He shifted his focus from trains to airplanes during university.
M4.zh: 他在大学期间将专业志向从火车转向飞机。

D7.en: He joined street protests against Japanese aggression after recovering from typhoid.
D7.zh: 他伤寒康复后参加上街游行，抗议日本侵略。

D15.en: He won third place in the 100m hurdles at the 1933 National University Games with 13.4 seconds.
D15.zh: 他在1933年全国大学生运动会100米栏中以13秒4获季军。

M6.en: He fantasized about traveling in space by rocket at age 16.
M6.zh: 他16岁时幻想乘坐火箭遨游太空。

D13b.en: At about 18-20, he had an arranged marriage to Zha Pinzhen by his father; she never had children.
D13b.zh: 他约18到20岁时，由父亲包办与查品珍结婚，她终生未育。

D12.en: At 24, she visited UC Berkeley and was so captivated by its vitality that she decided to stay instead of going to Michigan.
D12.zh: 24岁时，她到柏克莱探访同学，被那里的生机吸引，决定留下不去密西根了。

D2.en: As a teenager and young adult, she received extra encouragement from teachers due to her outstanding talent and hard work.
D2.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，她因资质出众又努力，得到师长特别多的鼓励和提携。

D3.en: At 17, she was deeply impressed by Hu Shih's erudition and eloquence in class.
D3.zh: 17岁时，她被胡适在课堂上的渊博学识和口才所倾倒。

D9.en: At 19-20, she led protest marches in a low-key manner, avoiding diplomatic areas and newspaper offices.
D9.zh: 19到20岁时，她领头游行请愿，采取低姿态，避开外交部和报馆地区。

D11.en: As a small child, she began learning poetry, characters, and arithmetic like other kids.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她和许多小孩一样，从背诵诗文、识方块字和算学起步学习。

D18.en: At 24, she bargained with a steamship ticket seller to get an empty first-class cabin for herself and a friend at a lower price.
D18.zh: 24岁时，她向轮船公司卖票的人说，把空的头等舱卖给她和朋友住，付二等舱的钱。

D20.en: As a small child, she went door-to-door with her father to persuade neighbors.
D20.zh: 幼年时，她跟着父亲挨家挨户去说服别人。

M2.en: As a small child, she felt the limitations of being female but aspired to achieve what men could.
M2.zh: 幼年时，她感受到女儿身的限制，但立志要做男子汉能做的事。

M7.en: At 19-20, she believed everyone had a responsibility for the nation's fate.
M7.zh: 19到20岁时，她认为国家兴亡，匹夫有责。

D14.en: She made a special trip to attend his lecture again.
D14.zh: 她特地又跑去听他的演讲。

D1.en: At 11-17, she struggled with a math problem, sometimes staying up late.
D1.zh: 11到17岁时，她为了数学难题拼命想，有时想不出来很晚不睡觉。

D16.en: At 18-22, she joked with a classmate, asking if she had read Dream of the Red Chamber.
D16.zh: 18到22岁时，她碰见同学严梅和，开玩笑问她有没有看过《红楼梦》。

M1.en: At 17, she received a perfect score from Hu Shih for her understanding of Qing thought.
M1.zh: 17岁时，胡适给她一百分，说她透彻理解清朝三百年思想史。

D5.en: At 19-20, she risked expulsion by joining protests and demonstrations.
D5.zh: 19到20岁时，她参加请愿示威，冒被开除之险。

M3.en: At 18-22, she often cited Marie Curie as her role model.
M3.zh: 18到22岁时，她经常提到居里夫人是她的典范。

D10.en: At 18-22, she advised Sun Duoci not to be too weak.
D10.zh: 18到22岁时，她直言劝告孙多慈不要太软弱。

D4.en: At 22-24, she worked on instrument setup, gas purification, and high vacuum for spectroscopy.
D4.zh: 22到24岁时，她花工夫进行仪器装置、气体净化和高真空工作，以测定气体光谱。

D7.en: At 24, she was surprised and disapproved of gender discrimination.
D7.zh: 24岁时，她对女性歧视感到意外和不以为然。

D2.en: He kept his mind running like a clock, storing thoughts when too weak to write.
D2.zh: 他大脑像上紧发条的钟表不停运转，身体差时把问题存脑中，好转再补记。

D3.en: He secretly searched for literature and went to Shanghai Library in near 40°C heat.
D3.zh: 他瞒着父母找文献，拖着虚弱身体冒近40度高温去上海图书馆看书。

D4.en: He innovated by changing serial carry to four-bit group carry method.
D4.zh: 他别出心裁地把一位位串联进位改成四位一组成组进位。

D9.en: He took on logic design, circuit design, and engineering tasks.
D9.zh: 他肩负逻辑设计，还承担部分电路设计及工程设计工作。

M3.en: He greatly respected his knowledgeable and talented teacher Zhang Shilong.
M3.zh: 他非常尊重知识渊博、才华横溢的张世龙老师。

D7.en: He insisted on listening to Voice of America and BBC despite suspicion of treason.
D7.zh: 他不顾里通外国嫌疑，坚持偷听美国之音和BBC英语广播。

M2.en: He said he would win glory for the motherland and for Peking University.
M2.zh: 他说要为祖国争光，为北大争光。

D1.en: He worked day and night, pushing his mental and physical limits to the extreme.
D1.zh: 他日夜兼程地工作，几乎把自己脑力和体力用到了极限。

M4.en: He linked his career and future with the nation's destiny.
M4.zh: 他把自己事业和前途同国家命运联系在一起。

D15.en: He stayed awake for 40 consecutive hours.
D15.zh: 他连续40个小时没有合过眼。

D10.en: He fell into extreme depression, confusion, and bewilderment.
D10.zh: 他陷入极度苦闷、困惑与迷茫之中。

D11.en: He even fantasized about piloting a spaceship through space.
D11.zh: 他甚至还产生了驾驶宇宙飞船畅游太空的遐想。

D18.en: He reduced his already insufficient food ration to 8 liang per day.
D18.zh: 他把本来就不够吃的口粮减到每天8两。

D16.en: He poured out his innermost thoughts without concealment.
D16.zh: 他毫不隐讳地吐露了自己的心声。

D13a.en: He felt he must confess all his thoughts to the Party before joining.
D13a.zh: 他怀着一颗赤诚之心，觉得入党前必须向组织说出所有思想。

D20X.en: He left the 'Red Flag' machine research group half a year earlier.
D20X.zh: 他离开‘红旗机’课题组半年后。

M5.en: He was named a 'Three Good' student activist at Peking University.
M5.zh: 他被评为了三好学生积极分子。

M6.en: He chose computational mathematics as his major.
M6.zh: 他选择了计算数学专业。

M8.en: He harbored a sincere heart for the Party and the people.
M8.zh: 他怀着一颗对党和人民的赤诚之心。

D12.en: At 13, she had already lost two close people.
D12.zh: 13岁时，她已经失去了两个亲近的人。

D10.en: At 23, she told someone she wouldn't teach them how to fix their situation.
D10.zh: 23岁时，她告诉某人她不会教他们如何解决自己的困境。

D4.en: At 18-19, she taught illiterate peasant children to read and write in Polish.
D4.zh: 18到19岁时，她教不识字的农家孩子读写波兰语。

D2.en: At 24, she deprived herself of all but bare necessities to live alone.
D2.zh: 24岁时，她为了独自生活的奢侈，除了最基本的需求外什么都不给自己。

D18.en: At 18, she went to work for the Zorawski family for 500 rubles a year.
D18.zh: 18岁时，她去佐拉夫斯基家工作，年薪500卢布。

D3.en: At 24, she deprived herself of all but bare necessities to live alone.
D3.zh: 24岁时，她为了独自生活的奢侈，除了最基本的需求外什么都不给自己。

D9.en: At 18-21, she added teaching local peasant children to her exhausting schedule.
D9.zh: 18到21岁时，她在已经很累的日程上又加了教当地农家孩子读书。

M1.en: At 24, she prepared for a licence ès sciences, equivalent to a master's degree.
M1.zh: 24岁时，她准备考取相当于硕士学位的科学学士学位。

D13a.en: At 19, Casimir begged her for patience but retreated from defying his parents.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，卡西米尔求她耐心等待，却退缩了，不敢违抗父母。

D14.en: At 16, Maria encouraged Curie and her sister to attend as many parties as possible.
D14.zh: 16岁时，玛丽亚鼓励居里和妹妹尽可能多参加聚会。

M4.en: She taught illiterate peasant children to read and write in Polish.
M4.zh: 她教不识字的农民孩子读写波兰语。

D11.en: At age 4, she picked up her sister's book and read aloud flawlessly.
D11.zh: 4岁时，她拿起姐姐的书，完美地朗读出来。

M7.en: At 25, she aimed to become fully qualified to help the Polish nation upon her return.
M7.zh: 25岁时，她立志要完全合格，以便最终回国帮助波兰民族。

D8.en: At 17, she directed surgeons exactly where to probe, succeeding where the surgeon failed.
D8.zh: 17岁时，她准确指导外科医生探入位置，成功完成手术。

D19.en: At 25, she classified and filed snapshots into albums.
D19.zh: 25岁时，她把快照分类并归档到相册里。

D15.en: At 24-25, she took lessons in an exotic art with Fred, Biquard, and Irène.
D15.zh: 24到25岁间，她与弗雷德、比卡尔和伊雷娜一起学习一门异域艺术。

D12.en: At 24, she heard her brother made his son Lin Jiatong skip college to work as a bank trainee in the Philippines.
D12.zh: 24岁时，她听说大哥让儿子林嘉通不读大学，去菲律宾当银行练习生。

D3.en: As a teenager, she read late into the night, befriending characters like Romeo, Juliet, and Jane Eyre.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她常常在灯下读到深夜，罗密欧、朱丽叶、简·爱成了她的朋友。

D6.en: At 19, she noted that classmates hadn't had such an open, face-to-face chat before.
D6.zh: 19岁时，她说全班同学还没像今天这样面对面开怀倾谈过。

D14.en: As a teenager, she actively participated in performances and was rarely absent.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，她积极参加演出，几乎每次都少不了她。

D13b.en: At 7, she was baptized.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，她参加了受洗礼。

D2.en: At 19-27, she often studied after midnight, wrapping a blanket when cold and eating roasted sweet potatoes when hungry.
D2.zh: 19到27岁间，她常在十二点后起来，罩上电灯看书，冷了披毛毯，饿了啃烤白薯。

D7.en: At 20, she declared, 'I will never marry, you'll see!'
D7.zh: 20岁时，她说：'我一辈子也不嫁人，你看着吧！'

D9.en: At 20, she quickly helped carry a classmate to shade, loosened her collar, applied a cold towel, and called her sister.
D9.zh: 20岁时，她迅速和同学把琼英抬到阴凉处，解开领扣，放凉毛巾，并给其姐姐打电话。

M2.en: At 24, while singing, she thought of her father's kindness and burst into tears.
M2.zh: 24岁时，她一边唱一边想起父亲的养育之恩，泪如雨下。

M1.en: At 19-27, she often studied after midnight, wrapping a blanket when cold and eating roasted sweet potatoes when hungry.
M1.zh: 19到27岁间，她常在十二点后起来，罩上电灯看书，冷了披毛毯，饿了啃烤白薯。

D4.en: At 17-18, she crocheted a black hairnet with fine thread and small mesh, her hands moving skillfully.
D4.zh: 17到18岁时，她用勾针勾织黑色发网，线细网小，但她动作娴熟，两手灵活飞针挑线。

D5.en: At 20, she said, 'I've brought all my belongings; if I fail the exam, I won't go back!'
D5.zh: 20岁时，她说：‘我的衣物都带齐了，考不上，我就不回去了！’

D20.en: Upon graduation, she wore the school pin and kept it all along.
D20.zh: 临毕业时，她胸前佩带了校别针，并一直保存着。

D1.en: At 19, she scored zero on a test, heard 'girls can't do it,' and resolved to break the prejudice.
D1.zh: 19岁时，她一门课得了零分，听到‘女生就是不行’，决心打破偏见。

D8.en: At 19, she challenged a boy: 'If you boys score 100, we girls will score 110!'
D8.zh: 19岁时，她反击男生：‘你们男生考一百分，我们就要考一百一十分！’

D15.en: As a young adult, she was active and played basketball well.
D15.zh: 她年轻时好动，篮球打得不错。

D17.en: At 24, she stroked her father's head and sang a hymn with her family.
D17.zh: 24岁时，她抚着父亲的头，和家人一起唱《睡主怀中歌》。

D13a.en: In her 20s, she suppressed her feelings and nipped a budding romance.
D13a.zh: 20多岁时，她克制感情，把爱情的萌芽掐掉了。

M4.en: At 34-35, she secretly bought and donated medicine five times for a friend.
M4.zh: 34到35岁时，她偷偷购买药品，捐献了五次。

D10.en: At 48-49, her heart stirred; she twirled her pen and stared at the patient's face.
D10.zh: 48到49岁时，她心怦然一动，转动蘸水笔，久久注视病人。

D7.en: At 16, he joined the Tokyo Chinese Students' Association in protesting Japan's restrictive school regulations.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他参加东京中国留日学生总会，反对日本政府的限制性学校章程。

M4.en: At 16, he was sworn into Sun Yat-sen's Tongmenghui as one of its first young members.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他宣誓加入孙中山组建的同盟会，成为首批年轻会员。

D9.en: At 16, he was sworn into Sun Yat-sen's Tongmenghui and told to 'study hard for the nation.'
D9.zh: 16岁时，他加入孙中山的同盟会，被勉励‘努力向学，蔚为国用’。

D2.en: At 24-25, he insisted on solving difficult math problems himself, refusing shortcuts.
D2.zh: 24到25岁间，他坚持自己演算繁难习题，拒绝走捷径。

D12.en: As a small child, he helped his mother with chores like chopping wood and carrying water.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他帮母亲打柴、舂米、推磨、提水。

D3.en: At 24-29, he developed a habit of collecting materials and studying the history of each new subject.
D3.zh: 24到29岁间，他养成每选新学科就搜集资料、了解发展史的习惯。

D4.en: At 18, he built a water filter using two jars, sand, and stones to purify pond water.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他用大缸小缸、沙石和白布制作过滤器，将塘水变清。

D17.en: At 6-11, he ran home to get clothes from his mother for a shivering old woman.
D17.zh: 6到11岁间，他跑回家找母亲要衣服，给冻得发抖的老婆婆穿。

D18.en: At 18, he returned from Japan and did small things that left a deep impression on locals.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他从日本回来做了一些小事，给乡亲们留下难忘印象。

D1.en: At 21-24, he worked hard on math in England to catch up after poor performance.
D1.zh: 21到24岁间，他在英国对数学很下功夫，努力赶了上去。

D6.en: As a teenager, he studied in Japan with classmate Chen Houfu, who shared his ambition.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他在日本留学，同学陈厚甫与他一样有报国抱负。

D12.en: As a child, he carried 10-gallon water buckets 800 meters uphill.
D12.zh: 他小时候要背十加仑水走八百米上山。

D3.en: At 13, he asked whether a triangle can be uniquely constructed from three given elements using only straightedge and compass.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他问能否只用直尺和圆规从三种给定数量唯一作出三角形。

D9.en: At 22, he gathered with acquaintances at Portsmouth Square in San Francisco's Chinatown.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他在旧金山唐人街的朴茨茅斯广场与相识的人集合。

D6.en: At 21, he was drawn into his mentor's social circle, enjoying dinners and socializing.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他被导师的社交圈吸纳，参加宴饮应酬。

D4.en: At 25, he attacked the Calabi conjecture, applying real Monge-Ampère techniques to the complex case.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他立即向卡拉比猜想发起进攻，把实蒙日-安培方程的方法套用在复蒙日-安培方程上。

D5.en: At 14, he threw rocks from a railroad track at others and scared them away.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他拿起铁轨上的石头向别人掷去，把他们吓跑了。

D18.en: At 15, he started tutoring math, a small step toward his future career.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他开始替别人补习数学，这是迈向未来事业的一小步。

D1.en: At 24, after two weeks trying to disprove the Calabi conjecture, he switched to proving it was true.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他花了两星期试图证明卡拉比猜想不对，然后一百八十度转变，倾注心力去证明它是对的。

D10.en: At 17, he saw his father cry for the first time, feeling a deep sense of loss.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他第一次看见父亲流泪，感到从未有过的失落。

D20.en: He collaborated with Saraf to write a book based on lecture notes.
D20.zh: 他和萨拉夫合作，以讲义为基础写了一本书。

M1.en: At 20, he took the GRE, TOEFL, and other exams, and fortunately scored well.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他考了GRE、托福和其他相关考试，幸好成绩都不错。

D7.en: At 22, he was advised by a prominent figure to stop political actions, saying life is only about fame and profit.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他被知名人士劝告停止政治行动，对方说人生不外名与利。

M6.en: At 20, he hoped to use mathematics as a starting point to guide his search for truth and beauty.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他希望以数学为出发点，依靠它的指引，照亮他寻找真和美的旅程。

M2.en: At 20, he promised his mother he would keep in close contact, write regularly, and send money monthly from over 10,000 km away.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他跟母亲保证，哪怕在一万多公里之外，也会密切联系，定时写家书，每月寄钱回来。

D13a.en: At 22, he obeyed advice and stopped asking, but didn't mention that IAS paid less than half of other schools.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他听从意见不再追问，但没提高研院薪水不到其他学校一半。

D11.en: As a child, he didn't like studying, played in the plains and hills, and even skipped school for half a year.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他不喜欢读书，在平原山海间嬉戏游玩，甚至逃学半年之久。

D3.en: At 16, he became curious about electromagnetic theory during his Pavia days.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他在帕维亚期间对电磁理论产生了好奇。

D7.en: At 17-21, he was not allowed to conduct an experiment on the earth's movement against the aether.
D7.zh: 17到21岁间，他不被允许进行关于地球相对于以太运动的实验。

M1.en: At 7, he received his grades and was again number one with a brilliant report card.
M1.zh: 7岁时，他拿到成绩单，又是第一名，成绩优异。

D2.en: At 13, he became a good violinist and continued to read science books voraciously.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他小提琴拉得不错，还继续大量阅读科学书籍。

D11.en: At 16, he wrote his first scientific essay, reflecting his interest in electromagnetic phenomena.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他写了第一篇科学文章，反映了他对电磁现象的兴趣。

D5.en: At 18-19, he wanted to construct an apparatus to accurately measure the earth's movement against the ether.
D5.zh: 18到19岁间，他想造一个装置来精确测量地球相对于以太的运动。

D13a.en: At 16, he planned to give up his German citizenship.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他计划放弃德国国籍。

D6.en: He often got together with friends to play music in small groups.
D6.zh: 他经常和朋友聚在一起，组成小团体演奏音乐。

D4.en: At 20-21, he spent much time in the physics lab, considering it the most time-consuming part of his studies.
D4.zh: 20到21岁间，他花大量时间在物理实验室，认为这是他学习中最耗时的部分。

D16.en: At 16-17, he expressed his personal opinion undauntedly, whether it offended or not.
D16.zh: 16到17岁时，他无畏地表达个人意见，不管是否冒犯他人。

D1.en: At 24, he found examining patents boring and continued looking for a better job.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他觉得审查专利无聊透顶，继续寻找更好的工作。

D14.en: At 16, he tried to impress her with his violin playing.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他试图用小提琴演奏给她留下印象。

D9.en: At 17, he seriously began engaging in philosophical and intellectually satisfying discussions.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他开始认真参与哲学和其他智力上令人满足的讨论。

D13b.en: At 17, he entered the Swiss Federal Polytechnical Institute in October.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他于十月进入瑞士联邦理工学院。

M6.en: He developed so slowly that he did not wonder about space and time until adulthood, then delved deeper than any other adult.
M6.zh: 他发育很慢，直到成年才开始思考时空问题，然后比任何其他成年人都钻研得更深。

M4.en: At 17, he decided to give up his German citizenship.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他决定放弃德国国籍。

D4.en: He crumpled up the paper and threw it into the wastepaper basket across the room.
D4.zh: 他把纸揉成一团，扔进了房间另一头的废纸篓。

M1.en: At 17, he achieved nearly perfect marks in the physics section of the entrance examinations.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他在入学考试的物理部分取得了近乎满分的成绩。

D5.en: He challenged Fred Hoyle in 1964, building his reputation.
D5.zh: 他在1964年挑战了弗雷德·霍伊尔，不断巩固自己的声誉。

M3.en: At 12, his classmates nicknamed him Einstein.
M3.zh: 12岁时，同学们给他起了个外号叫爱因斯坦。

M6.en: As a teenager, he wanted to fathom the depths of the universe.
M6.zh: 十几岁时，他想探索宇宙的深处。

D2.en: He spent little time on math homework but still got full marks.
D2.zh: 他花很少时间做数学作业，却总能得满分。

D9.en: He found a way to phone London for the cost of a local Cambridge call.
D9.zh: 他找到一种方法，用本地剑桥电话的费用打给伦敦。

D7.en: He regretted letting his father steer him away from math at Oxford.
D7.zh: 他后悔让父亲引导他远离牛津的数学专业。

M4.en: He goes on Ban the Bomb marches.
M4.zh: 他参加禁止核弹游行。

D13a.en: He realized he needed a job quickly to get married.
D13a.zh: 他意识到需要尽快找到工作才能结婚。

D15.en: He exerted himself rowing and coxing for Univ.
D15.zh: 他在大学划船和当舵手时非常卖力。

D10.en: He was deeply shocked and experienced deep depression.
D10.zh: 他深感震惊，经历了一段深深的抑郁。

D6.en: At 19, he joined the Rowing Club and trained hard all winter.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他加入赛艇俱乐部，整个冬天刻苦训练。

D20.en: He insisted on studying natural science and won the argument with his father.
D20.zh: 他坚持要学自然科学，并说服了父亲。

D12.en: He knew marriage required a job, and a job required a Ph.D.
D12.zh: 他知道结婚需要工作，而工作需要博士学位。

D13b.en: He did graduate work at Cambridge in multiple periods between 1959 and 1985.
D13b.zh: 他在1959到1985年间多次在剑桥做研究生工作。

D11.en: He invented board games of astounding complexity.
D11.zh: 他发明了极其复杂的棋盘游戏。

D1.en: He won the top award with minimal effort.
D1.zh: 他毫不费力地赢得了最高奖项。

D8.en: He confronted bullies with clenched fists.
D8.zh: 他握紧拳头面对欺负人的大孩子。

D16.en: At 20, he told his examiner he expected a First to go to Cambridge.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他告诉考官他预期得一等荣誉去剑桥。

D3.en: He asked for instruction in thermodynamics and was told to read Planck's book.
D3.zh: 他请求指导热力学，被告知去读普朗克的书。

D18.en: He haggled and used his weekly allowance to buy a treasure.
D18.zh: 他讨价还价后，用每周零花钱买下了这件宝贝。

D2.en: He wrote his first letter to Persico in German, surprising him.
D2.zh: 他给佩尔西科写了第一封信，用的是德语，让他惊讶。

D4.en: As a small child, he pounded screws and nails into his wooden toys.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他把螺丝和钉子敲进木制玩具里。

D1.en: He consoled himself with deeper and more difficult study.
D1.zh: 他用更深入、更困难的学习来安慰自己。

D6.en: He soon had a companion, Enrico Persico, on his expeditions.
D6.zh: 他很快有了一个同伴，恩里科·佩尔西科，一起探险。

D9.en: At 17, he received his diploma from the liceo, skipping the third year.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他从liceo获得文凭，跳过了第三年。

M1.en: At 21, he was eager to reach the top as fast as possible.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他渴望尽快达到顶峰。

D7.en: He risked being expelled forever from the university.
D7.zh: 他冒着被大学永久开除的风险。

D10.en: He showed extreme reluctance to show his feelings.
D10.zh: 他极度不愿表露自己的感情。

D5.en: At 24, he wondered if the joke about his group being called the 'suicide squad' held some truth.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他怀疑自己的团队被称为‘敢死队’这个玩笑是否有些道理。

D16.en: At 18, he planned to give a lecture on quantum theory before several magnates.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他计划在几位大亨面前做一场关于量子理论的讲座。

D11.en: At 20, he received a letter with a drawing of two hands clasped in a handshake.
D11.zh: 20岁时，他收到一封信，上面画着两只手紧握致意。

D14.en: At 20, he gave a public oral dissertation.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他进行了一场公开的口头答辩。

D8.en: At 24, he would overtake anyone ahead of him, unable to let them beat him to the top.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他会超过任何走在他前面的人，不能容忍别人先于他登顶。

D13b.en: As a child, he plunged into cold water every morning with unquestioned discipline.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他每天早晨毫无质疑地跳进冷水里。

D13a.en: At 3, he obeyed and stopped crying after being told.
D13a.zh: 3岁时，他听人吩咐后止住眼泪，不再吵闹。

D2.en: He studied physics under James Franck in Göttingen for five years.
D2.zh: 他在哥廷根跟随詹姆斯·弗兰克学习物理，待了五年。

D6.en: He began an almost daily correspondence with the senior physicist Landé.
D6.zh: 他开始与年长的物理学家兰代几乎每天通信。

D9.en: He responded with new diplomacy but even more outrageous radical assumptions.
D9.zh: 他以新的外交方式回应，但提出了更加离谱的激进假设。

D7.en: At 24, Pauli sent his manuscript to press without informing Copenhagen, probably fearing a rupture.
D7.zh: 24岁时，泡利未告知哥本哈根就寄出稿件，可能担心决裂。

D5.en: He was the only victim, returning home terribly sick with a high fever.
D5.zh: 他是唯一的受害者，回家时病得很重，发着高烧。

M4.en: He attributed the half-integer quantum numbers to borrowed half-integer momentum.
M4.zh: 他把半整数量子数归因于借来的半整数动量。

D1.en: He trained himself to ski excellently over difficult terrain, though not a natural skier.
D1.zh: 他自学滑雪，在困难地形上滑得很好，尽管他并非天生滑雪者。

D15.en: He worked from 6:00 AM until past sundown, as late as 10:00 PM.
D15.zh: 他从早上6点工作到日落后，有时直到晚上10点。

M6.en: He focused on his dissertation, Sommerfeld's lectures, and Wien's lab course.
M6.zh: 他专注于他的论文、索末菲的讲座和维恩的实验课。

D16.en: He set out to derive Reynolds's results from hydrodynamic equations.
D16.zh: 他着手从流体力学基本方程推导雷诺的结果。

D10.en: He broke into tears because he couldn't stand the pressure from Bohr.
D10.zh: 他哭了起来，因为他受不了玻尔给他的压力。

D8.en: At 25, he broke into tears from Bohr's pressure during a weeks-long argument.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他因波尔的压力在长达数周的争论中崩溃大哭。

D14.en: He eagerly returned to his Munich colleagues' circle.
D14.zh: 他急切地回到了慕尼黑同事的圈子。

D18.en: He focused on his dissertation, Sommerfeld's lectures, and Wien's lab course.
D18.zh: 他专注于他的论文、索末菲的讲座和维恩的实验课。

D11.en: He set out to derive Reynolds's results from hydrodynamic equations.
D11.zh: 他着手从流体力学基本方程推导雷诺的结果。

D12.en: He spent a year between high school and Harvard recovering from dysentery.
D12.zh: 他在高中和哈佛之间花了一年时间从痢疾中恢复。

D13b.en: Within a month, he started working for Max Born.
D13b.zh: 一个月内，他开始为马克斯·玻恩工作。

M1.en: At 20-22, he set out to derive Reynolds's results from hydrodynamics equations.
M1.zh: 20到22岁时，他着手从流体力学基本方程推导雷诺的结果。

D20.en: As a Neupfadfinder leader, he could expect to exercise absolute authority over his Gemeinschaft.
D20.zh: 作为新探路者领袖，他可能对他的团体行使绝对权威。

M8.en: At 21-22, he eagerly returned to the circle of his Munich colleagues.
M8.zh: 21到22岁时，他急切地回到了慕尼黑同事的圈子。

M2.en: He aimed to obtain a full professorship to achieve the goal his family had set for him.
M2.zh: 他立志成为正教授，以实现家庭为他设定的目标。

M7.en: At 20-22, he set out to derive Reynolds's results from hydrodynamics equations.
M7.zh: 20到22岁时，他着手从流体力学基本方程推导雷诺的结果。

D17.en: At 21-22, he eagerly returned to the circle of his Munich colleagues.
D17.zh: 21到22岁时，他急切地回到了慕尼黑同事的圈子。

D19.en: At 20-22, he set out to derive Reynolds's results from hydrodynamics equations.
D19.zh: 20到22岁时，他着手从流体力学基本方程推导雷诺的结果。

D20X.en: At 21-22, he eagerly returned to the circle of his Munich colleagues.
D20X.zh: 21到22岁时，他急切地回到了慕尼黑同事的圈子。

M3.en: At 21-22, he concentrated on his dissertation, Sommerfeld's lectures, and Wien's lab course.
M3.zh: 21到22岁时，他专注于自己的论文、索末菲的讲座和维恩的实验课。

M5.en: At 21-22, he eagerly returned to the circle of his Munich colleagues.
M5.zh: 21到22岁时，他急切地回到了慕尼黑同事的圈子。

D3.en: He used dark, chilly days to learn theoretical chemistry and leaf through journals for a forgotten clue to DNA.
D3.zh: 他利用阴暗寒冷的日子学习理论化学或翻阅期刊，希望找到关于DNA的被遗忘的线索。

D2.en: He used dark, chilly days to learn theoretical chemistry or leaf through journals.
D2.zh: 他利用阴暗寒冷的日子学习理论化学或翻阅期刊。

D13a.en: He cycled to Ole's lab daily, deceiving fellowship electors to avoid forcing Herman to discuss biochemistry.
D13a.zh: 他每天骑车去奥勒的实验室，欺骗奖学金评选人，以免迫使赫尔曼谈论生物化学。

D5.en: He decided no harm could come from spending a few days building backbone-out models, temporarily ignoring the bases.
D5.zh: 他决定花几天搭建骨架在外模型没有坏处，暂时忽略碱基。

D6.en: He went back to Clare after lunch to hammer out the final draft of his genetics manuscript.
D6.zh: 他午饭后回到克莱尔，敲定遗传学手稿的最终稿。

D20.en: He and Francis thought the angle between adjacent bases was 30-40 degrees.
D20.zh: 他和弗朗西斯认为相邻碱基之间的旋转角是30到40度。

M4.en: He wanted to abandon conventional biochemistry, believing X-ray crystallography was key to genetics.
M4.zh: 他想放弃传统生物化学，认为X射线晶体学是遗传学的关键。

M1.en: He would start playing with two-chain models.
M1.zh: 他会开始摆弄双链模型。

D4.en: At 24, he quickly strung together several short sections of the sugar-phosphate backbone.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他迅速将几段糖-磷酸骨架拼接在一起。

D8.en: He hurried to Bragg's office for an explanation after his contribution went unacknowledged.
D8.zh: 他冲进布拉格的办公室要求解释，因为他的贡献未获承认。

D10.en: At 24, he was back in a mood to worry about what was wrong.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他又陷入担心哪里出错的情绪中。

D1.en: At about 31, he lost all desire to stay in physics and decided to try biology instead.
D1.zh: 大约31岁时，他完全失去了留在物理学的愿望，决定转而尝试生物学。

D14.en: At about 35, he went to a costume party looking like G. B. Shaw in a full red beard.
D14.zh: 大约35岁时，他参加化装舞会，打扮成留着红胡子的萧伯纳。

D2.en: He received his doctorate in mathematics from the University of Budapest in spring 1926.
D2.zh: 他于1926年春天在布达佩斯大学获得了数学博士学位。

D9.en: At 15, he was asked his opinion on how to deal with less reliable classmates.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他被问及如何处理不可靠的同学。

D7.en: His father decided against his wishes that a career in biology was not for him.
D7.zh: 他父亲违背他的意愿，认为他不适合从事生物学。

D6.en: At 23-25, he became the mathematician admired by most scholars outside his field.
D6.zh: 23到25岁间，他成为本领域外最受学者钦佩的数学家。

D4.en: In 1926, he sailed into mathematical action as an axiomatizer for the Copenhagen school.
D4.zh: 1926年，他作为哥本哈根学派的公理化者投入数学行动。

D13a.en: At 17, he worked towards a degree and a doctorate in two subjects simultaneously.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他同时攻读两个不同学科的学位和博士学位。

M1.en: In 1926, he took final exams in Budapest to become a Ph.D. with highest honors.
M1.zh: 1926年，他在布达佩斯参加最终考试，以最高荣誉获得博士学位。

D5.en: At 14, he walked to school through them.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他步行穿过它们去上学。

D11.en: At 10-14, he played complicated war games he made up with his family.
D11.zh: 10到14岁间，他和家人玩他自己编的复杂战争游戏。

D1.en: At 19, he wrote a first draft of his doctoral thesis.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他写了博士论文初稿。

D12.en: At 15, his family fled the country due to safety threats.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他全家因安全威胁逃离了国家。

D15.en: As a child and youth, he was entirely unathletic and could not do anything physical.
D15.zh: 他从小到青年时期完全不擅长运动，做不了任何体力活。

D18.en: As a schoolboy, he was attracted to acquiring great riches before his father persuaded him to pursue mathematics.
D18.zh: 上学时，他向往发财，但父亲说服他改学数学。

M4.en: At 15, the 1919 Budapest commune taught him that communism does not work.
M4.zh: 15岁时，1919年布达佩斯公社让他明白共产主义行不通。

D16.en: At 12-13, he proved a theorem using only subsidiary theorems Wigner knew.
D16.zh: 12到13岁时，他仅用维格纳知道的辅助定理证明了一个定理。

D20.en: At 24, he was induced to accept the post of privatdocent at the University of Berlin.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他被说服接受了柏林大学的私讲师职位。

D14.en: At 25, he knew more history than most professors and had memorized guide books by heart.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他比多数教授更懂历史，并把导游手册背得滚瓜烂熟。

D8.en: At 19, he had a perpetual contest with another person over who would do the teaching.
D8.zh: 19岁时，他和另一个人总在争谁教谁。

D19.en: At 24-26, he wrote all his papers in German in a uniform, orderly Prussian style.
D19.zh: 24到26岁间，他用德语以统一、有序的普鲁士风格写下了所有论文。

D4.en: At age 10-12, he learned glass blowing and did dangerous chemistry experiments in a garden shed.
D4.zh: 10到12岁时，他学会了吹玻璃，并在花园棚里做危险的化学实验。

D7.en: At about age 12, he suddenly refused to go to church, briefly upsetting his mother.
D7.zh: 大约12岁时，他突然拒绝去教堂，让母亲短暂不快。

M1.en: At age 14, he won a scholarship from Northampton Grammar School to attend Mill Hill boarding school.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他获得北安普顿文法学校的奖学金，进入米尔希尔寄宿学校。

D15.en: At age 23, he spent the first weeks of the war playing squash with his brother at Mill Hill school.
D15.zh: 23岁时，战争最初几周他在米尔希尔学校与兄弟打壁球。

D16.en: At age 16-18, he gave a talk on how Bohr theory and quantum mechanics explained the periodic table.
D16.zh: 16到18岁时，他做了一次关于玻尔理论和量子力学如何解释元素周期表的演讲。

D6.en: At age 23, he had lunch with someone almost every day at the Eagle.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他几乎每天都在鹰酒吧与某人共进午餐。

D2.en: He decided to spend another year here.
D2.zh: 他决定在这里再待一年。

D3.en: At 25, he worked on new ideas in logic, calling them hopeful.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他研究逻辑学新想法，认为很有希望。

D15.en: At 23-24, he worked entirely alone on his machines, not discussing them with Newman.
D15.zh: 23到24岁间，他完全独自研究机器，未与纽曼讨论。

D20.en: He chatted with Richard Braithwaite at High Table about Gödel's theorem.
D20.zh: 他在高桌餐会上与理查德·布雷斯韦特聊了几句哥德尔定理。

D9.en: At 12, he gathered enough people to hold an event.
D9.zh: 12岁时，他召集了足够的人来举办活动。

D19.en: At 23-24, he wrote a long paper full of ideas with technical work and unpublished evidence.
D19.zh: 23到24岁间，他写了一篇长论文，充满想法，包含大量技术工作和未发表的证据。

D12.en: At 23-24, he worked entirely alone on his machines, not discussing them with Newman.
D12.zh: 23到24岁间，他完全独自研究机器，未与纽曼讨论。

D18.en: He found a ring and was rewarded with two shillings.
D18.zh: 他找到一枚戒指，得到了两先令的奖励。

D10.en: He was glad the stars shone on Saturday morning as a tribute to Chris.
D10.zh: 他很高兴周六早上星星闪耀，向克里斯致敬。

M3.en: He often thought how like he was to Chris and wondered if he was left to do something Chris was called away from.
M3.zh: 他常想自己与克里斯有多相似，并怀疑自己是否被留下做克里斯被召唤去做的事。

M6.en: At 18, he wondered if he was left to do something that another person had been called away from.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他怀疑自己是否被留下来做别人被叫走去做的事。

D17.en: He learned that Victor was caned so severely that his spine was damaged, which turned him against the beating system.
D17.zh: 他得知维克多被鞭打致脊柱受损，这让他反对体罚制度。

D14.en: He read a paper on 'Mathematics and logic'.
D14.zh: 他宣读了一篇关于‘数学与逻辑’的论文。

D5.en: At 25, he wielded a textbook with hammer and sickle, enjoying shocked German expressions.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他挥舞着印有锤子镰刀的教科书，享受德国人震惊的表情。

D20X.en: At 23-24, he worked entirely alone on his machines, never discussing them with Newman.
D20X.zh: 23到24岁间，他完全独自制造机器，从未与纽曼讨论过。

D13b.en: At 24, he would soon emerge as a forceful exponent of materialism and identify as an atheist.
D13b.zh: 24岁时，他即将成为唯物主义的坚定倡导者，并自认是无神论者。

M4.en: At 24, he would soon emerge as a forceful exponent of materialism and identify as an atheist.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他即将成为唯物主义的坚定倡导者，并自认是无神论者。

D16.en: At 23-24, he described his ideas to David Champernowne, who grasped the universal machine concept.
D16.zh: 23到24岁间，他向大卫·钱珀瑙恩描述自己的想法，对方理解了通用机的概念。

D8.en: At 23-24, he resolved a central question in mathematics, crashing in as an unknown outsider.
D8.zh: 23到24岁间，他解决了数学的一个核心问题，以一个无名局外人的身份闯入。

M5.en: At 23-24, he resolved a central question in mathematics, crashing in as an unknown outsider.
M5.zh: 23到24岁间，他解决了数学的一个核心问题，以一个无名局外人的身份闯入。

M7.en: At 24, he would soon emerge as a forceful exponent of materialism and identify as an atheist.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他即将成为唯物主义的坚定倡导者，并自认是无神论者。

M8.en: He would soon emerge as a materialist and identify as an atheist.
M8.zh: 他很快会成为唯物主义者，并自称无神论者。

D2.en: He took a course in mathematical physics by Edmund Whittaker from 1906 to 1908.
D2.zh: 1906到1908年间，他上了埃德蒙·惠特克的数学物理课程。

D3.en: At 25, he started from a recent paper by Pieter Debye on polar molecules.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他从彼得·德拜一篇关于极性分子的近期论文出发。

D10.en: As a small child, he often had tantrums.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他经常发脾气。

D15.en: He once guided young Erwin in an ascent of the Einser in the Sexten Dolomites.
D15.zh: 他曾在塞克斯滕多洛米蒂山脉的艾因瑟山攀登中为年轻的埃尔温做向导。

D6.en: At 21, he experienced his first mature love affair.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他经历了第一次成熟的恋爱。

D7.en: He learned his negative attitude from his father and reinforced it through reading and experience.
D7.zh: 他从父亲那里学到了消极态度，并通过阅读和经历强化了它。

M1.en: From entry till graduation in 1906, he was always first in his class.
M1.zh: 从入学到1906年毕业，他一直是班上的第一名。

D12.en: As a teenager, he had two small rooms of his own overlooking the courtyard.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，他拥有两间自己的小房间，俯瞰庭院。

D14.en: He loved the theater, going often and keeping an annotated record of performances.
D14.zh: 他热爱戏剧，经常去看，并保存了带注释的演出记录。

D19.en: He recorded his loves with a code for the outcome.
D19.zh: 他用密码记录了自己的恋情和结局。

D17.en: At 23, he stayed all night moving a man's foot to a more comfortable position.
D17.zh: 23岁时，他整夜守着一个人，移动他的脚到更舒服的位置。

D13b.en: He completed military training and became a Fähnrich in the reserves.
D13b.zh: 他完成了军事训练，成为预备役少尉。

D9.en: He worked furiously with two others on a joint paper.
D9.zh: 他和另外两人拼命合作写一篇论文。

D4.en: He had free access to instruments and made spectroscopic observations.
D4.zh: 他可以自由使用仪器，进行光谱和干涉测量。

D13a.en: He was told by Rudolf to stay with physics, not go into business.
D13a.zh: 他被鲁道夫告知要留在物理学领域，不要从商。

D5.en: He wanted to marry but saw no way to support a wife as a physicist.
D5.zh: 他想结婚，但觉得当物理学家养不起妻子。

D16.en: He gave a lecture on the magneton for his Kolloquium.
D16.zh: 他做了关于磁子的学术报告。

D18.en: He wanted to marry but saw no way to support a wife as a physicist.
D18.zh: 他想结婚，但觉得当物理学家养不起妻子。

D20X.en: At 25, he wanted to marry but saw no way to support a wife as a physicist.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他想结婚，但觉得当物理学家养不起妻子。

M3.en: At 25, he started from a recent paper by Pieter Debye on polar molecules.
M3.zh: 25岁时，他以彼得·德拜关于极性分子的近期论文为起点。

M4.en: At 25, he wanted to marry but saw no way to support a wife as a physicist.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他想结婚，但觉得当物理学家养不起妻子。

M5.en: At 25, he saw his parents' social standing as a greater obstacle because the Krauss family could use 'Von' before their surname.
M5.zh: 25岁时，他认为父母的社会地位是更大障碍，因为克劳斯家族能在姓氏前加‘冯’。

M7.en: At 25, he wanted to marry but saw no way to support a wife as a physicist.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他想结婚，但觉得当物理学家养不起妻子。

M8.en: At 25, he was nominally Protestant but actually a free-thinker who probably didn't believe in God.
M8.zh: 25岁时，他名义上是新教徒，实际上是自由思想者，可能不信上帝。

D3.en: He first got a math book in 1903, a few months before leaving Town High School.
D3.zh: 1903年，他第一次拿到一本数学书，就在离开镇高中前几个月。

D7.en: He returned to Kumbakonam after an unhappy six months in Madras.
D7.zh: 他在马德拉斯度过了不愉快的六个月后，回到了库姆巴科纳姆。

D12.en: On his 11th day of life, he was formally named.
D12.zh: 出生第11天，他正式被命名。

D18.en: At 24, he accepted a clerical job at Madras Port Trust on Feb 9, 1912.
D18.zh: 24岁时，他于1912年2月9日接受了马德拉斯港务局的文员职位。

D13a.en: At 21, he first declined membership, then was inducted into the society on Feb 19, 1898.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他先拒绝了会员资格，但最终于1898年2月19日被接纳进学会。

D2.en: At 12, he reached the sixth form and surpassed most in math, though younger.
D2.zh: 12岁时，他升入六年级，尽管年龄较小，但在数学上超过了大多数同学。

D4.en: At 17, he lost interest in everything else and focused solely on math.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他对其他一切失去兴趣，只专注于数学。

D14.en: At 24, he attended a Shakespeare Society meeting on May 23, 1901.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他于1901年5月23日参加了莎士比亚学会的会议。

D9.en: At age 9, he scored high on an examination.
D9.zh: 9岁时，他考试得了高分。

D16.en: At 21, he asked how the world would look before anyone existed.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他问世界在没有人观看时是什么样子。

D11.en: At 25, he gave meanings to fractional and negative terms in series and products.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他给级数和乘积中的分数项和负项赋予了意义。

M4.en: He never rebelled; he embraced the unseen realm of spirit.
M4.zh: 他从未反抗；他拥抱了不可见的精神领域。

D5.en: At 25, he wrote to Baker, Hobson, and Hardy.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他写信给贝克、霍布森和哈代。

D10.en: At 23, he stayed up exclaiming on astronomical wonders till late night.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他熬夜惊叹天文奇观直到深夜。

D17.en: He sometimes collected food for a mentor, leading some to think he was mad to indulge him.
D17.zh: 他有时为一位导师收集食物，有人觉得他这样纵容对方是疯了。

D15.en: He was a crack gymnast at school, played cricket, and later became an accomplished rock climber and skier.
D15.zh: 他在学校是顶尖体操运动员，打过板球，后来成为出色的攀岩者和滑雪者。

D20.en: He worked on mathematics with a colleague, often until late into the night.
D20.zh: 他和一位同事一起研究数学，常常工作到深夜。

D2.en: At 14-17, she revealed a passion for learning at Erasmus Hall high school.
D2.zh: 14到17岁时，她在伊拉斯谟霍尔高中展现出对学习的热爱。

D12.en: At about age 3, she was sent to live with an aunt and uncle in Massachusetts.
D12.zh: 大约3岁时，她被送到马萨诸塞州与姨妈和姨父同住。

D4.en: At 25, her first task was to locate markers of maize genetic traits on chromosomes.
D4.zh: 25岁时，她的首要任务是定位玉米遗传性状在染色体上的标记。

D6.en: At 17, she was elected president of the women's freshman class.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她被选为女子新生班班长。

D15.en: At 6-10, she showed interest in ice skating and her parents bought her skates and let her skip school to skate.
D15.zh: 6到10岁时，她对滑冰表现出兴趣，父母给她买了冰鞋，允许她逃学去滑冰。

D7.en: At 24, she left Randolph's group and completed her doctorate under Sharp.
D7.zh: 24岁时，她离开兰道夫的研究组，在夏普指导下完成了博士学业。

D13a.en: At 17, she overrode parental opposition and enrolled in Cornell's College of Agriculture.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，她不顾父母反对，进入康奈尔大学农学院就读。

D5.en: At 17, she took up smoking and bobbed her hair, risqué behavior for a woman then.
D5.zh: 17岁时，她开始吸烟并剪短头发，这在当时对女性来说是出格行为。

D1.en: She spent two years beginning to understand what that statement might mean.
D1.zh: 她花了两年时间才开始理解那句话可能意味着什么。

D8.en: As a child, she stood and stared at a girl, then turned and went home without a word.
D8.zh: 小时候，她站着盯着一个女孩看，然后转身直接回家，一句话没说。

M1.en: At 25, she wanted to link a specific linkage group to a chromosome in maize.
M1.zh: 25岁时，她想把特定的连锁群与玉米的某个染色体关联起来。

M7.en: As a teenager, she decided to take consequences for an activity that gave her pleasure, to keep her sanity.
M7.zh: 十几岁时，她决定为带给她快乐的活动承担后果，以保持理智。

D18.en: At 16, she worked at an employment agency for six months, interviewing people to place them in jobs.
D18.zh: 16岁时，她在职业介绍所工作了六个月，面试求职者并为他们安排工作。

D14.en: At 20-21, she joined a jazz combo and played banjo in cafés and bars.
D14.zh: 20到21岁时，她加入了一个爵士组合，在咖啡馆和酒吧弹班卓琴。

D13b.en: At 21, the plant breeding department didn't take women, so she couldn't study genetics there.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，植物育种系不收女生，所以她不能在那里学遗传学。

D10.en: At 19-20, she grew nervous for about twenty minutes until her name came to her.
D10.zh: 19到20岁时，她越来越紧张，大约二十分钟后才想起自己的名字。

D11.en: At 20-21, she took a harmony course and had to write musical compositions.
D11.zh: 20到21岁时，她上了和声课，必须创作音乐作品。

D16.en: At 20-21, she joined a jazz improvisation group playing tenor banjo at local places.
D16.zh: 20到21岁时，她加入了一个爵士即兴演奏组，在当地场所弹奏高音班卓琴。

D20.en: At 17, she was elected president of the women's freshman class.
D20.zh: 17岁时，她被选为女子新生班班长。

M4.en: At 25, she wanted to do something obvious to her but not to geneticists or plant breeders at Cornell.
M4.zh: 25岁时，她想做一些对她来说显而易见但当时康奈尔的遗传学家或植物育种家却不明白的事。

M6.en: At 21, she knew she just had to go on.
M6.zh: 21岁时，她知道她必须继续走下去。

D9.en: At 25, she assembled a small maize cytogenetics group with two graduate students.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她组建了一个小型玉米细胞遗传学小组，包括两名研究生。

D12.en: At 10-12, he walked four miles to school and back each day, in darkness during winter.
D12.zh: 10到12岁时，他每天步行四英里上学放学，冬天早晚都在黑暗中。

D13b.en: At 14, he accepted an offer to live with Tom, John, and Mary in London.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，他接受了汤姆的提议，去伦敦与汤姆、约翰和玛丽同住。

D2.en: At 19-20, he found a teacher to coach him evenings and sat for the exam in July 1901.
D2.zh: 19到20岁时，他找了老师晚上辅导，并在1901年7月参加了考试。

M1.en: At 24, he obtained excellent marks and was immediately offered a clerkship in the Admiralty.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他取得了优异的成绩，立即获得了海军部的文员职位。

D3.en: As a small child, he observed plover's eggs and made pocket money collecting them.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他观察鸻鸟蛋，并通过收集它们赚零花钱。

M4.en: At 23, he felt a driving ambition to do medical research.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他怀有从事医学研究的强烈抱负。

D6.en: As a small child, he attended combined Halloween and Christmas parties with other families.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他与其他家庭一起参加万圣节和圣诞节的联合派对。

D18.en: As a small child, he made pocket money by collecting plover's eggs, sold for 4d each to a grocer.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他通过收集鸻鸟蛋赚零花钱，每个以4便士卖给达弗尔的杂货商。

D13a.en: At 19-20, he agreed with Tom's suggestion to stop being a clerk and become a medical student.
D13a.zh: 19到20岁时，他同意汤姆的建议，不再做文员，而是成为医学生。

D15.en: At 21-23, he played tennis and football for Adelaide University.
D15.zh: 21到23岁间，他为阿德莱德大学打网球和踢足球。

D7.en: At 16, he showed independence by wearing a bow-tie, and never wore any other sort for the rest of his life.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他表现出独立性，系了领结，此后一生只系领结。

D14.en: At 14, he took the whole family to the theatre to see Charley's Aunt; the two youngest had to stand at the back of the gallery.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他带全家去看《查理的姑妈》，两个最小的孩子只能站在楼座后排。

D5.en: He and his brother enlisted as privates in the London Scottish.
D5.zh: 他和他的兄弟作为列兵加入了伦敦苏格兰团。

M6.en: At 22, the idea of scientific medicine attracted him.
M6.zh: 22岁时，科学医学的想法吸引了他。

D16.en: At 25, he replied, 'I'd rather have facts than a lot of hot air'.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他回答说：‘我宁愿要事实，也不要一堆空话。’

D14.en: At 13, he drew a pastel portrait of his mother.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他用粉笔画了一幅母亲的肖像。

D12.en: As a child aged 10-12, he took part in harvests and festivities.
D12.zh: 10到12岁时，他参加了收割和庆祝活动。

D1.en: At 25, his discovery came from a complex series of investigations.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他的发现源于一系列复杂的调查。

D5.en: At 19, he took but failed the entrance exam for École Polytechnique.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他参加了巴黎综合理工学院的入学考试，但没通过。

D2.en: From age 25 to 33, he filled notebooks with efforts to establish the law.
D2.zh: 25到33岁间，他在笔记本里写满了确立该定律的艰苦努力。

M1.en: At 18-19, he ranked second in class twice and first in physics once.
M1.zh: 18到19岁时，他两次在班上排名第二，一次物理第一。

D9.en: At 25, he summarized his findings before the Académie des Sciences.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他在法国科学院总结了自己的发现。

M4.en: At 25, he contributed his savings of 150 francs to the republican cause.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他把150法郎积蓄捐给了共和事业。

D13a.en: At 20, he was conscripted and found himself on battlefields in 1812-13.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他被征召入伍，1812到1813年上了战场。

D7.en: At 20-21, a physiological chemistry lab was created for him at the École normale.
D7.zh: 20到21岁时，巴黎高师为他创建了一个生理化学实验室。

D4.en: At 25, he bent over his bench examining crystals of tartrates.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他俯身在实验台前检查酒石酸盐晶体。

D11.en: At 15-16, he executed his first pastels.
D11.zh: 15到16岁时，他创作了第一幅粉彩画。

D2.en: He filled sheaves of scratch paper with his fast adolescent scrawl using a fountain pen.
D2.zh: 他用钢笔以快速的少年笔迹写满了一叠叠草稿纸。

D6.en: At 18, he sent a copy of his theme to his friend Elmer Heller at Purdue University.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他把主题的副本寄给了在普渡大学的朋友埃尔默·海勒。

D9.en: He was the group leader at the Manhattan Project in Los Alamos during the war years.
D9.zh: 他在战争期间担任洛斯阿拉莫斯曼哈顿计划的组长。

D20.en: He was vice-president in the fall of 1934.
D20.zh: 他在1934年秋季担任副主席。

D12.en: He was married to Arline, who was treated for tuberculosis at Deborah Hospital and later died at a clinic in Albuquerque.
D12.zh: 他与阿琳结婚，她在德博拉医院接受结核病治疗，后来在阿尔伯克基的一家诊所去世。

D1.en: He answered he did not know, and proceeded to work it out by drawing the curve for the index of refraction versus the wavelength.
D1.zh: 他回答说不知道，然后通过绘制折射率随波长变化的曲线来求解。

D4.en: He designed it, but because of his imprecise machine work it didn't function properly.
D4.zh: 他设计了它，但由于他的机械加工不精确，它没能正常工作。

D18.en: He would repair radios in the neighborhood to make a little money to buy things for his laboratory.
D18.zh: 他会在附近修理收音机赚点钱，为他的实验室买东西。

D7.en: He had scrapes with censors because he and Arline used codes.
D7.zh: 他和阿琳使用密码，因此与审查人员发生了多次冲突。

D11.en: As a child, he used his imagination to rationally understand accounts of miracles.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他运用想象力理性理解各种奇迹描述。

M1.en: At 21, he submitted his thesis for a Bachelor of Science degree.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他提交了学士学位论文。

M6.en: In high school, he felt that was one of the greatest things ever and loved playing with action.
M6.zh: 高中时，他觉得那是最棒的事之一，从此热爱在作品中玩动作。

D5.en: At 25-27, he had scrapes with censors for using codes with Arline.
D5.zh: 25到27岁间，他因与阿琳使用密码而与审查员发生冲突。

D10.en: At 21, he was hospitalized for a few days but recovered in time to graduate.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他住院几天，但及时康复毕业。

D19.en: At 21, he neatly arranged his chemical bottles and planned experiments.
D19.zh: 21岁时，他整齐摆放化学瓶并规划实验。

D13a.en: At 17-18, he changed to electrical engineering, then to physics.
D13a.zh: 17到18岁间，他转学电气工程，又转学物理。

D8.en: At 23, he told an assistant his time was more valuable and he was paid more.
D8.zh: 23岁时，他对助理说自己的时间更宝贵，薪水更高。

M4.en: Frightened that scientists under Hitler might develop the bomb first, he decided to attend the meeting.
M4.zh: 他担心希特勒手下的科学家可能先造出炸弹，于是决定参加会议。

D13b.en: Upon graduation after completing his thesis, he went back to doing war-related research.
D13b.zh: 完成论文毕业后，他回去做与战争相关的研究。

M7.en: He worked on the OSRD atomic bomb project at Princeton from December 1941 to March 1942, then continued for the Manhattan Project.
M7.zh: 他从1941年12月到1942年3月在普林斯顿为OSRD原子弹项目工作，之后继续为曼哈顿计划工作。

D15.en: At 23, he won a National Research Council Fellowship to study with Bohr in Copenhagen, traveling by freighter for $55.
D15.zh: 23岁时，他获得国家研究委员会奖学金，乘货船花55美元去哥本哈根跟玻尔学习。

D20X.en: At 24, he deliberately ended his collaboration with Wheeler.
D20X.zh: 24岁时，他刻意结束了与惠勒的合作。

D18.en: At 22, he started a stock-quotation service with 25 subscribers.
D18.zh: 22岁时，他创办了一个股票报价服务，有25个订阅者。

D11.en: At 25-27, he wrote and published poetry under the pseudonym Tom Sleeper.
D11.zh: 25到27岁间，他用笔名汤姆·斯利珀创作并发表诗歌。

D4.en: As a young adult, he walked behind mowers to observe them cutting grain.
D4.zh: 二十多岁时，他跟在割草机后面观察它们割谷物。

D2.en: At 23, he worked in the shop from 7 AM to 2 AM daily.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他每天从早上七点到凌晨两点在店里工作。

D5.en: At 16, he narrowly avoided a train collision.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他险些遭遇火车相撞。

D7.en: At 9-11, his behavior was criticized by the headmaster as inattentive and silly.
D7.zh: 9到11岁间，他的行为被校长批评为不专心和愚蠢。

D3.en: As a young adult, he walked behind mowers to observe them cutting grain.
D3.zh: 二十多岁时，他跟在割草机后面观察它们割谷物。

D9.en: At 22, he started a stock-quotation service with 25 subscribers.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他创办了一个股票报价服务，有25个订阅者。

D6.en: At 17, he joined coworkers to form a local chapter of the National Telegraphic Union.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他和同事一起成立了全国电报工会的地方分会。

D12.en: At 24, he returned home for his beloved mother's funeral.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他回家参加他深爱的母亲的葬礼。

D10.en: At 22, he let his anger break through and declared bitter alienation in a letter to his younger brother.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他让愤怒爆发，在给弟弟的信中表达了痛苦的疏远。

D1.en: At 15, he forced himself into the habit of grouping several words at once for efficiency.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他强迫自己养成一次看多个单词的习惯以提高效率。

M3.en: At 20, he listed Faraday's work as a research need, influenced by a role model.
M3.zh: 20岁时，他把法拉第的著作列为研究需求，受到榜样的影响。

D20.en: At 22, he replaced Franklin Pope as superintendent of the Gold & Stock Reporting Telegraph Company.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他接替富兰克林·波普担任黄金与股票报道电报公司的主管。

M1.en: He liked to win at Parcheesi and often emerged victorious.
M1.zh: 他喜欢在巴棋戏中获胜，并经常赢得比赛。

D15.en: At 18, he wandered lonely as a cloud and furtively killed red squirrels to make paintbrushes from their tails.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他像一朵云一样孤独地游荡，偷偷杀死红松鼠，用它们的尾巴做画笔。

M2.en: At 20, he blamed his father's genius for his doubtful thoughts and felt he could never please him.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他责怪父亲的才华导致他怀疑和不安，觉得自己可能永远无法取悦他。

M6.en: At 18, he quit studying because it was too hard, wanting to become a landscape artist.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他因学习太难而放弃，想成为风景画家。

M4.en: He states he is doing his duty.
M4.zh: 他说他在尽自己的责任。

M8.en: As a child, he read Shakespeare, Tennyson, Charles Lamb, and the Old Testament for moral development.
M8.zh: 幼年时他读莎士比亚、丁尼生、查尔斯·兰姆和旧约以修身。

D3.en: She planned to read during the holidays to improve her French.
D3.zh: 她打算假期里读点书，把法语巩固一下。

D10.en: She lived alone, felt bored with work, despised her professor, and was ignored by lab colleagues.
D10.zh: 她独自生活，对工作感到厌倦，讨厌教授，实验室的男同事也忽视她。

D9.en: At 18, she had several successes unconverting people.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她成功让几个人改变了想法。

D12.en: She was born as the second child and first daughter of Ellis and Muriel Franklin.
D12.zh: 她出生，是埃利斯和穆里尔·富兰克林的第二个孩子和第一个女儿。

D4.en: She spent 100 francs on fabric for a dress, enough for a dress and several carpets.
D4.zh: 她花100法郎买了做裙子的布料，布料多到能做一条裙子和好几块地毯。

D15.en: At 15, she walked up a glacier with a guide, 4,600 feet high.
D15.zh: 15岁时，她和向导一起走上冰川，海拔4600英尺。

D13a.en: She accepted a job in Kingston.
D13a.zh: 她接受了金斯顿的一份工作。

M1.en: She got the highest marks in chemistry and won a school leaving exhibition and a prize.
M1.zh: 她化学成绩最高，获得了毕业展览和一笔奖金。

M6.en: She felt a deep sense of vocation.
M6.zh: 她深感使命感。

D6.en: She went on an expedition with a delightful French party and reveled in their company.
D6.zh: 她参加了一次愉快的法国人探险，很喜欢和他们在一起。

D1.en: She struggled to focus on her research, knowing she might soon quit a 2-3 year job after only 2-3 months.
D1.zh: 她很难专心研究，因为知道自己可能干了2到3个月就放弃一个本来要干2到3年的工作。

D14.en: At 18, she participated in a debate at school.
D14.zh: 18岁时，她参加了学校的辩论。

D16.en: At 18, she participated in a debate at school.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她参加了学校的辩论。

D13b.en: At 9, she was sent to a boarding school in Sussex for a couple of years.
D13b.zh: 9岁时，她被送到苏塞克斯的一所寄宿学校待了几年。

M4.en: At 17, she witnessed her parents' total commitment to refugee relief work.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她目睹父母全身心投入难民救济工作。

D5.en: At 23-24, she climbed Crib Goch in mist with steep drops.
D5.zh: 23到24岁间，她在雾中爬克里布戈赫山，两边是陡坡。

D19.en: At 20, she said she knew exactly where everything was, though it looked confused.
D19.zh: 20岁时，她说她知道每样东西在哪，虽然看起来乱。

D8.en: At 22, she stood up to him and they had several first-class rows.
D8.zh: 22岁时，她顶撞他，他们大吵了几架。

M7.en: At 25, she agreed with her father.
M7.zh: 25岁时，她同意父亲的意见。

D20X.en: At 25, she talked herself out of returning to Cambridge.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，她说服自己不回剑桥。

D3.en: At 17-18, mounted a 4.5 cm prism on a college spectrometer and illuminated it obliquely.
D3.zh: 17到18岁间，将4.5厘米棱镜装在大学分光计上斜照。

D4.en: At 17-18, he mounted a 4.5 cm prism on a college spectrometer and illuminated it obliquely.
D4.zh: 17到18岁间，他将4.5厘米棱镜装在大学分光计上斜向照明。

D13a.en: At age 12, he lost his scholarship and struggled through a private school.
D13a.zh: 12岁时他失去奖学金，在私立学校艰难求学。

D6.en: He joined the Kapitza Club, a weekly physics discussion group.
D6.zh: 他加入了卡皮查俱乐部，每周讨论物理。

D14.en: He heard her playing the Tyagaraja keertana on the veena.
D14.zh: 他听到她用维纳琴弹奏蒂亚格拉贾的克提那。

D18.en: He scrutinized banknotes under a magnifying glass and instructed the treasurer to give him fresh ones.
D18.zh: 他用放大镜检查钞票，并指示出纳给他新钞。

M2.en: He defied all pressures and insisted on having his own way.
M2.zh: 他顶住所有压力，坚持按自己的方式行事。

M4.en: He joined a boycott organized on that occasion.
M4.zh: 他参加了那次组织的抵制活动。

D1.en: He published his first papers between ages 15 and 23.
D1.zh: 他在15到23岁间发表了首批论文。

M3.en: At 25, he wrote about Helmholtz.
M3.zh: 25岁时，他写了关于亥姆霍兹的文章。

D13b.en: At 20-22, the depression forced him to drop out and take a real job.
D13b.zh: 20到22岁间，经济萧条迫使他辍学并找了份正式工作。

D6.en: At 17-21, his female friends included Sandra, who invited him to dine with her family.
D6.zh: 17到21岁间，他的女性朋友包括桑德拉，她邀请他去她家吃饭。

D15.en: At age 6-11, he excelled in urban street games like stickball, handball, and basketball.
D15.zh: 6到11岁间，他擅长街头游戏如棍球、手球和篮球。

M1.en: At 18-22, made the dean's list and transferred to UCLA film school, graduating summa cum laude.
M1.zh: 18到22岁间，他上了院长名单，转学到加州大学洛杉矶分校电影学院，以最优等成绩毕业。

D5.en: He wrote to Secretary of State Dean Acheson on Indiana University stationery.
D5.zh: 他用印第安纳大学的信纸给国务卿迪安·艾奇逊写信。

D16.en: He placed second in the American Legion oratorical contest in his senior year.
D16.zh: 他在高中最后一年在美国退伍军人协会演讲比赛中获得第二名。

D7.en: He wrote to Secretary of State Dean Acheson on Indiana University stationery.
D7.zh: 他用印第安纳大学的信纸给国务卿迪安·艾奇逊写信。

M4.en: He fully concurred with order-of-magnitude estimates that the problem was real.
M4.zh: 他完全同意数量级估计，认为问题确实存在。

M6.en: He chose extraterrestrial life as his dissertation topic.
M6.zh: 他选择地外生命作为博士论文题目。

D11.en: At about age sixteen, he wrote a long essay on extraterrestrial life in a school notebook.
D11.zh: 大约16岁时，他在学校笔记本上写了一篇关于地外生命的长文。

D20.en: He fully concurred with order-of-magnitude estimates that the problem was real.
D20.zh: 他完全同意数量级估计，认为问题确实存在。

D18.en: He worked as a poorly paid usher at a movie theater.
D18.zh: 他在电影院当引座员，工资很低。

D12.en: At age 6, he began farm work, cutting grass and collecting pig feed with his sister.
D12.zh: 他六岁起就干农活，跟姐姐上山砍柴、捞猪饲料。

D3.en: At age 10, he questioned why China lacked laws to protect overseas Chinese earnings.
D3.zh: 他十岁时质问：为什么中国没有法律保护华侨挣来的钱？

D2.en: At ages 7-9, he quickly cut grass and helped his sister finish her load.
D2.zh: 他七八岁时割草很快，还帮姐姐割足了一担。

D7.en: At ages 16-17, he tore down a Guan Yu portrait and said gods can't cure illness.
D7.zh: 他十六七岁时扯下关帝像，说关云长不能降福治病。

D14.en: At age 17, he jumped on an altar and broke a god statue's hand, revealing mud and straw.
D14.zh: 他十七岁时跳上供桌，打碎神像手指，露出烂泥稻草。

D6.en: At ages 20-22, he formed a close group of three revolutionary classmates, known as the Four Bandits.
D6.zh: 他二十到二十二岁间，与三位同学结为革命小团体，人称四大寇。

D16.en: At age 20, he debated a teacher, arguing that doing is not hard but knowing is.
D16.zh: 他二十岁时反驳老师，说“行之非艰，知之惟艰”。

D18.en: At age 6, he went up the mountain to cut grass and collect pig feed with his sister.
D18.zh: 他六岁时和姐姐上山割草、拾柴、捞猪饲料。

D9.en: At ages 20-22, he formed a close group of three revolutionary classmates, known as the Four Bandits.
D9.zh: 他二十到二十二岁间，与三位同学结为革命小团体，人称四大寇。

M1.en: At age 16, he graduated second in English grammar and received a Chinese book from the king.
M1.zh: 他十六岁时毕业考得英文文法第二名，由夏威夷国王发奖。

D11.en: At 12, he imitated martial arts at home and told classmates he wanted to become a general to fight the Manchus.
D11.zh: 12岁时，他在家里模仿舞弄拳棒，对同学说想当大将去打满洲仔。

D15.en: At 6, he sometimes went to sea with his grandfather to collect oysters.
D15.zh: 6岁时，他有时随外祖父驾船出海取蚝。

D5.en: At 13, he rode an untamed horse, fell off, and was hospitalized.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他骑上一匹没鞍的烈马，摔下来晕倒，被送进医院。

D4.en: At 12, he made a kite from bamboo sticks and paper and flew it high.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他用竹扦和纸做风筝，放飞到云霄。

D13a.en: At 24, he resigned his English teaching post to take up a position in the Nanjing government.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他辞去英文教席，到南京政府履职。

M4.en: At 17, he told fellow travelers about corrupt officials and urged political reform in China.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他向旅客讲述清政府官吏的贪婪，宣传改造中国。

M7.en: At 10-11, he sold flowers to raise funds, going all over the city.
M7.zh: 10到11岁时，他卖花募捐，跑遍全市。

D20.en: At 24, he and three close friends were called the 'Four Bandits' for their constant talk of revolution.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他与三人亲密无间，常谈革命，被亲友称为‘四大寇’。

D17.en: At 24, he went with the Cantlies to a leper colony in Guangzhou and gave each patient five dollars and cigarettes.
D17.zh: 24岁时，他与康德黎夫妇前往广州麻风村，赠每位病人五元和香烟。

D3.en: At 14, he carefully marked and measured a diagram of Mercury's transit across the Sun.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他仔细标记并测量了水星凌日的图解。

D4.en: At 18, he began a six-month practicum at a locomotive factory.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他在机车厂开始了六个月的实习。

D11.en: At 14-15, he wrote three handwritten piano pieces.
D11.zh: 14到15岁时，他手写了三首钢琴曲。

D2.en: At 20, he produced an eleven-page primer for rocket calculations.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他编写了十一页的火箭计算入门指南。

D13b.en: At 20, he sent a contract offer to Army Ordnance.
D13b.zh: 20岁时，他向陆军军械部提交了合同提议。

D6.en: At 14, he eagerly shared his knowledge about rockets with schoolmates.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他急切地与同学分享火箭知识。

D9.en: At 22, he set off for Borkum with five others.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他和另外五人出发前往博尔库姆。

D14.en: At about age 10-12, he was accepted for piano lessons by Paul Hindemith.
D14.zh: 他大约在10到12岁时被保罗·欣德米特收为钢琴学生。

D20.en: At 24, he issued an order tightening up engine testing organization.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他发布命令加强发动机测试的组织。

M6.en: He aimed to create technical systems enabling human space travel.
M6.zh: 他的目标是创造能让人类进入太空的技术系统。

D15.en: At 23, he studied hard but demonstrated his manliness by drinking and dueling.
D15.zh: 23岁时，他努力学习，但也通过喝酒和决斗来展示男子气概。

D18.en: He worked as a taxi driver in Berlin for hours daily.
D18.zh: 他每天在柏林当几小时出租车司机。

D16.en: He interrupted to offer suggestions on a problem.
D16.zh: 他插话说可以提供一些建议。

D1.en: He built an A-1 rocket in half a year, then it blew up in half a second.
D1.zh: 他花了半年造A-1火箭，半秒就炸了。

D5.en: He started a fire in the woods from an experiment.
D5.zh: 他做实验时在树林里引起了火灾。

M1.en: He completed his C license with an 11-minute soaring flight.
M1.zh: 他完成了C级执照，滑翔飞行超过11分钟。

M4.en: He channeled his energy into meetings, experimentation, and analysis.
M4.zh: 他把精力投入到会议、实验和分析中。

D12.en: At age 8, he was followed back to the capital by Emmy and three little boys in fall 1920.
D12.zh: 8岁时，艾米和三个小男孩在1920年秋天跟着他回到首都。

M3.en: He wrote to Oberth and hoped to meet his idol.
M3.zh: 他写信给奥伯特，热切希望见到偶像。

D13a.en: He dropped out immediately after graduating in summer 1934.
D13a.zh: 他1934年夏天毕业后立即退学。

D20X.en: He agreed with Schneider that it was time to quit.
D20X.zh: 他同意施耐德的说法，是时候退出了。

D3.en: At 24, he began learning Latin from Ma Jianzhong, studying two hours daily.
D3.zh: 24岁时，他每天花两小时跟马建忠学拉丁文。

D2.en: He studied Latin two hours daily, expecting to read fluently in a year.
D2.zh: 他每天学两小时拉丁文，预计一年就能读书。

D12.en: As a small child, he learned the Four Books and Poetry from his grandfather and mother.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他跟着祖父和母亲学《四书》《诗经》。

D6.en: He attended the first meeting of the Protect the Nation Society at the Cantonese New Hall theater, with 127 registrants.
D6.zh: 他参加了保国会在粤东新馆戏院召开的第一次大会，当天有127人登记与会。

D14.en: At 6-7, he answered a teacher's couplet with 'Southern people miss the Duke of Shao's pear tree.'
D14.zh: 六七岁时，他对出塾师的对子：'南国人思召伯棠。'

D7.en: At 24, he wrote about popular rights, criticized Qing misrule, and attacked scholars from Xunzi onward.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他大谈民权，揭露清朝弊政，抨击从荀子以来的学者。

D13a.en: At 25, he proposed divorce to his wife, saying their loveless marriage should end.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他向妻子提出离婚，认为没有爱情的婚姻不该继续。

M4.en: At 25, he believed free divorce based on mutual respect would end suffering and bring happiness.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他认为自由离婚、彼此尊重人格，能止绝苦痛、始兆幸福。

D1.en: At 12, he passed the imperial exam and became a xiucai.
D1.zh: 12岁时，他考中了秀才。

D20.en: At 23, he founded the Shiwu Bao newspaper in Shanghai with help from comrades.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他得同志之助，在上海创办了《时务报》。

D13b.en: At 12, he left home alone to study under a master in Guangzhou.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他独自离开家乡，到广州拜师求学。

D11.en: At 8, he wrote a poem pondering the changes of nature.
D11.zh: 8岁时，他作诗思考日月四时的变幻。

M2.en: At 12, he asked a scholar to write a birthday tribute for his grandfather.
M2.zh: 12岁时，他请先生为祖父写寿序。

D4.en: At 24, he went to Germany to study military with two companions, funded by Zhao Erxun.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他得赵尔巽批准和资助，与张孝准、宁调元结伴赴德国学习军事。

D15.en: As a teenager, he hunted fiercely with falcon and bow alongside strong men.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他臂鹰腰弓矢，与健儿们争先逐猛兽。

D18.en: As a small child, he was bought by a human trafficker and resold four times.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他被人口贩子买去，被转卖了四次。

M7.en: At 24, he questioned how one could save China without mastering knowledge.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他质疑学问不成如何救中国。

D20X.en: At 24, he accepted an invitation to teach at Shiwu Academy in Changsha.
D20X.zh: 24岁时，他应邀去长沙担任时务学堂总教习。

D16.en: At 25, he loudly advocated revolution, destruction, and civil rights as necessary remedies for China.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他大声疾呼革命、破坏、民权、自由，称破坏为第一美德。

M5.en: At 23, he felt shame that the Self-Strengthening Society disbanded and did not revive.
M5.zh: 23岁时，他认为强学会解散后一蹶不振是一种耻辱。

M6.en: At 23, he sailed to England to study under Russell, even writing a悼诗 when he heard a false death report.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他买船票去英国跟罗素学习，误闻死讯时流泪作悼诗。

D10.en: At 22, he felt as if a knife stabbed his heart when news of the treaty reached Beijing.
D10.zh: 22岁时，条约消息传到北京，他感到心如刀割。

M8.en: At 12-17, he greedily absorbed new knowledge like Alibaba discovering a treasure cave.
M8.zh: 12到17岁时，他像阿里巴巴发现藏宝洞一样贪婪地汲取新知识。

M3.en: At 21-23, he was stirred to heroic passion by reading Liang Qichao's biography of the Three Italian Heroes.
M3.zh: 21到23岁间，他读梁先生的《意大利三杰传》而血气之勇始见。

D2.en: At 21, he focused on his studies, ignoring outside affairs.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他专心学业，不问窗外事。

D3.en: At 12, he most enjoyed reading Wang Yangming's Instructions for Practical Living.
D3.zh: 12岁时，他最喜欢读王阳明的《传习录》。

D9.en: At 24-26, he traveled across Southeast Asia raising substantial funds.
D9.zh: 24到26岁间，他奔走南洋各地，筹集了可观的款项。

D16.en: At 22-23, he published a series of influential articles in Minbao under the pen name Jingwei.
D16.zh: 22到23岁间，他用笔名“精卫”在《民报》上发表了一系列有分量的文章。

M1.en: At 19, he took the county exam and placed first with his eight-legged essay.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他参加番禺县试，八股文做得漂亮，取为第一名。

D18.en: At 19, he was hired as a tutor for the children of a naval commander.
D18.zh: 19岁时，他被水师提督聘为家庭教师。

M4.en: At 24, he helped publish the Zhongxing Daily in Singapore to debate royalists.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他在新加坡参与出版《中兴日报》，与保皇派论战。

D13a.en: At 22, he was engaged without his consent, then he joined the revolution and wrote for Minbao.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他被强订婚约，随后加入革命党，在《民报》上写文章。

D7.en: At 22, he wrote a letter severing ties with his family and renouncing his engagement.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他写信与家庭断绝关系，并解除婚约。

D6.en: At 22, he helped draft the charter of the Chinese United League.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他参与起草了同盟会的章程。

D12.en: As a teenager, he lost his mother at 13 and his father at 14, further impoverishing his family.
D12.zh: 他13岁丧母，14岁丧父，家庭更加贫薄。

D4.en: At 22, he participated in drafting the charter of the Chinese United League.
D4.zh: 22岁时，他参与起草了同盟会的章程。

D13b.en: At 21, his brother arranged an engagement for him without his consent.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他哥哥代他与刘氏女订了婚约。

M7.en: At 22-23, he defended anti-Manchu ideas in a debate, advocating national salvation.
M7.zh: 22到23岁间，他在笔战中捍卫排满思想，主张救亡。

M3.en: At 21-22, he admired Japanese figures Saigo Takamori and Katsu Kaishu.
M3.zh: 21到22岁间，他景仰日本伟人西乡隆盛和胜海舟。

D1.en: At 21, he stayed in Japan and did not join the feast before returning to China.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他留在日本，没有参加回国前的盛宴。

D11.en: At 22, he chose his name to signify perseverance for revolution.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他取这个名字表示坚持革命。

D19.en: At 22-24, he drafted documents for Sun Yat-sen, which were rarely altered.
D19.zh: 22到24岁间，他代孙中山起草文件，很少被改动。

D15.en: At 21, he joined a volunteer corps of Chinese students to fight Russia in Manchuria.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他加入留学生义勇军，准备回东三省与俄军作战。

D17.en: At 22, he urged his family to care for his widowed sister-in-law and orphaned nephew.
D17.zh: 22岁时，他嘱托家人善待寡嫂孤侄。

D20X.en: At 22, he cut ties with his family to avoid implicating them.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他与家庭断绝关系以免连累。

M2.en: He said he would rather sever ties with his family to shed blood for his country.
M2.zh: 他说他宁愿断绝家庭关系，也要为国流血，实现自己的志向。

M6.en: At 22, he chose the name Jingwei, meaning perseverance in revolution.
M6.zh: 22岁时，他取名精卫，寓意坚持不懈革命。

M8.en: At 21-22, he admired Japanese figures Saigo Takamori and Katsu Kaishu.
M8.zh: 21到22岁间，他景仰日本伟人西乡南洲与胜海舟。

D14.en: At 24-25, he gave impassioned speeches with gestures and varied tones.
D14.zh: 24到25岁时，他演说时口若悬河、手舞足蹈、抑扬顿挫。

D13b.en: In January 1910, on a cold morning, he stood before the commander of the 19th Field Artillery Regiment in Takada.
D13b.zh: 1910年1月一个寒冷的早晨，他站在高田的野战炮兵第十九联队长面前。

D2.en: He stood straight on the balcony for half an hour every morning, lips tight, arms crossed.
D2.zh: 他每天早起在阳台笔直站半小时，双唇紧闭，双臂抱胸

D6.en: He played war games as a child, always appointing himself commander-in-chief.
D6.zh: 他小时候玩打仗游戏，总是自己当总司令

D18.en: He worked as a stock broker in Shanghai from 1912 to 1922.
D18.zh: 1912到1922年他在上海做股票经纪人

D7.en: He broke a clod of dirt into eight pieces in defiance of military discipline, glaring at the instructor.
D7.zh: 他不顾军纪把土块掰成八块，怒视教员

D10.en: He flew into a rage when a movie offended him, ordering it stopped and overturning tables.
D10.zh: 他看电影被冒犯时暴跳如雷，掀翻茶几摔茶杯

D19.en: He knelt daily with his wife to pray for God's blessing.
D19.zh: 他每天与夫人双膝长跪祈祷上帝保佑

D4.en: As a small child, he jumped into a water vat as tall as an adult.
D4.zh: 幼年时他跳进一口成人高的大水缸

D5.en: He decided to abandon his military studies in Japan and return to join the revolution upon hearing of the Wuchang Uprising.
D5.zh: 听到武昌起义消息，他决定放弃日本学业回国参加革命

D15.en: He often bathed in the stream, sometimes forgetting to return.
D15.zh: 他常常跳入溪水洗澡，有时乐而忘返

D3.en: At 17, he studied under Gu Qinglian at a literature hall in Fenghua.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他在奉化跟顾清廉在文学馆学习经史子集。

M4.en: At 18, he joined the army to take action and serve his country.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他投笔从戎，想为国效力。

M3.en: At 18, he met Chen Qimei in Tokyo, who became his mentor.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他在东京结识了陈其美，后来成了他的良师益友。

D9.en: At 23, he wrote to his mother before leading a successful attack on the Zhejiang governor's office.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他在攻打浙江巡抚衙门成功前给母亲写了信。

D12.en: At 23, he wrote to his mother asking forgiveness for his unfilial behavior.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他写信给母亲，恳请她恕儿不孝之罪。

D11.en: At 24-25, he wrote in the first issue of Military Voice magazine about a utopian world.
D11.zh: 24到25岁时，他在《军声》杂志创刊号上撰文向往大同世界。

D14.en: At 12-15, she wore heavy makeup and when teased, retorted 'It's Chinese lacquer.'
D14.zh: 12到15岁时，她浓妆艳抹，被调侃后回答‘是中国漆’。

D3.en: At 15, she said she urgently wanted education to return to China and introduce their savior.
D3.zh: 15岁时，她说自己急欲接受教育，以便回到中国介绍救世主。

D11.en: At 16, she hung a curved sword on her dorm wall, scaring a freshman.
D11.zh: 16岁时，她在宿舍墙上挂了一把弯刀，吓坏了一名大一学生。

D6.en: At 10, she insisted on going with her sister despite parents' advice to wait.
D6.zh: 10岁时，她坚持要和姐姐同行，不顾父母劝她等几年。

D15.en: At 16-20, she seemed particularly fond of swimming and tennis.
D15.zh: 16到20岁时，她对游泳和网球似乎情有独钟。

D1.en: At 5, she counted to twenty then thirty, but playmates left; her sister came back for her.
D1.zh: 5岁时，她数到二十再三十，玩伴却跑了，姐姐回头找她。

D9.en: At 20-25, she worked in social service, film censorship, and became first Chinese woman on child labor committee.
D9.zh: 20到25岁时，她从事社会服务、电影审查，并成为童工委员会第一个中国妇女委员。

M2.en: At 22, Soong Ching-ling recalled the lingering pain of defying her father to marry Sun Yat-sen.
M2.zh: 22岁时，宋庆龄回忆违抗父命与孙结婚的余痛。

M1.en: At 19-20, she became a Durant Scholar, the highest honor for students.
M1.zh: 19到20岁时，她获得杜兰特学者头衔，这是学生最高荣誉。

D16.en: She noted that Chiang Kai-shek's shrill voice and coarse speech still captivated listeners with his oratory.
D16.zh: 她指出蒋介石嗓音尖、讲话粗鄙，但口才使听者动容。

M4.en: At 18-24, Han Jiashu promised a friend she would continue spreading the gospel even if leaving the church.
M4.zh: 18到24岁间，韩嘉树向朋友保证即使离开教会也会继续传福音。

M7.en: She was always very loyal to China.
M7.zh: 她对中国一直十分忠诚。

M3.en: She said it was hero-worship; she wanted to help save China by working for Sun Yat-sen.
M3.zh: 她说这是一种英雄崇拜，她想帮助救国，因此为孙博士工作。

D12.en: As a baby, he was nursed by his aunt because his mother had insufficient milk.
D12.zh: 他出生后因生母奶水不足，由婶母哺乳。

D2.en: At 15-16, he studied poetry, calligraphy, and eight-legged essays under three tutors, sleeping only 5-6 hours a night.
D2.zh: 15到16岁时，他请了三个老师教诗歌、书法和八股文，每天只睡五六个小时。

D1.en: At 17-20, he failed the provincial exam twice, burned his poems in anger, and decided to find another path.
D1.zh: 17到20岁时，他两次参加乡试都落榜，一怒之下烧掉所有诗文，决心另寻出路。

D7.en: He questioned whether as a daotai he should use official letters for higher-ranking officers.
D7.zh: 他质疑自己作为道员，是否应对实缺提督总兵用扎。

D9.en: At 23, he went to Korea with Wu Changqing, performed well in suppressing a mutiny, and was promoted to battalion manager.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他随吴长庆赴朝鲜平定兵变，因精明强干被升为营务总理。

D8.en: At 25, he was criticized for arrogance by Zhang Jian and others, and offended the Japanese by suppressing the Korean Gaehwa Party.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他因目中无人被张謇等人批评，镇压朝鲜开化派得罪了日本人。

D5.en: At 25, fearing capture before the Sino-Japanese War, he sent six telegrams begging to return, claiming illness, and fled back.
D5.zh: 25岁时，甲午战争前夕他担心被俘，九天连发六封电报要求回国，托病以死相挟，狼狈回国。

D6.en: At 19-20, when poor scholar Xu Shichang visited him, he treated him warmly, became sworn brothers, and gave him money.
D6.zh: 19到20岁时，穷困的徐世昌来访，他一见如故，结拜兄弟并慷慨赠银。

D18.en: At 20, he handled relief fundraising in Chenzhou for his uncle, raising a large sum alone.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他受姑丈委托分办陈州捐务，集款独巨。

D20.en: At 25, he was asked to oversee all Korean internal affairs, diplomacy, and military.
D20.zh: 25岁时，韩国内政、外交、军务悉请他主持调度。

D16.en: He was energetic, eloquent, and skilled in political writing, often composing quickly.
D16.zh: 他年轻气盛，精力充沛，能说会写，擅长演说和政论，下笔千言。

D13b.en: He graduated from Baoding Military Academy and was assigned as a platoon commander in Shaanxi.
D13b.zh: 1909年毕业于保定陆军速成学堂炮兵科，分至陕西新军任排长。

D4.en: He co-founded the Huaxing Association with Huang Xing and joined the Science Society in Wuchang.
D4.zh: 1904年与黄兴等创立华兴会，并加入武昌的科学补习所。

D3.en: He studied at Hosei University and Waseda University.
D3.zh: 入法政大学和早稻田大学学习。

D15.en: He went to Korea to suppress a rebellion and was received by the Korean king for bravery.
D15.zh: 随吴长庆赴朝鲜平定壬午兵变，因作战勇敢受朝鲜国王单独接见。

D11.en: He burned his poems and essays, declaring he would serve on the battlefield, not waste time writing.
D11.zh: 他烧掉诗文，说大丈夫当效命疆场，安内攘外，不能久困笔砚间。

D13a.en: After failing the imperial exams, he resolutely abandoned that path and sought achievement through deeds.
D13a.zh: 两榜落第后他断然放弃科举之路，立志从事功上出头。

M1.en: He resolved to make his mark through practical achievements.
M1.zh: 立志从事功上出头。

M2.en: His ancestors and father were role models for him.
M2.zh: 他的祖、父辈都是榜样。

M4.en: He laughed and told a frightened soldier not to panic but to gather the remnants.
M4.zh: 袁公笑道，不要乱我军心，赶快回营收集残兵。

M6.en: At 20, he resolved to make his mark through achievement.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他立志要在功业上出头。

D14.en: At 23-35, he handled diplomacy in Korea for 12 years, becoming a smooth talker.
D14.zh: 23到35岁间，他在朝鲜办了十二年外交，嘴上功夫没得说。

D3.en: At 15, she went to the US to study and was influenced by democratic and anti-feudal ideas.
D3.zh: 15岁时，她远渡美国求学，受到民主和反封建思想的洗礼。

D11.en: At 20, she wrote an essay condemning foot binding and child brides.
D11.zh: 20岁时，她写文章谴责缠足和童养媳。

D6.en: At 22, she translated Sun Yat-sen's anti-Yuan declaration into English.
D6.zh: 22岁时，她把孙中山的讨袁宣言译成英文。

M7.en: She felt her aspirations, emotions, and entire life belonged to China.
M7.zh: 她觉得她的志向、情感和整个生命都属于中国。

D14.en: At 19-20, she was literary editor of the school magazine and a member of drama and literary societies.
D14.zh: 19到20岁时，她担任校刊文学编辑，参加戏剧社和文学社。

D20.en: At 18-20, she frequently wrote for the college magazine and literary journal.
D20.zh: 18到20岁时，她经常为学院校刊和文学杂志撰稿。

D9.en: At 20, she spent two pleasant weeks at the home of the acting Chinese minister in Berkeley.
D9.zh: 20岁时，她在伯克利中国代理公使家里度过了愉快的两周。

D12.en: At 16-19, she took care of her younger sister for three years, acting as a parent.
D12.zh: 16到19岁时，她照顾小妹三年，实际上担起了父母的职责。

D4.en: At 18, she tore down the Qing dragon flag, stomped on it, and shouted for the republic.
D4.zh: 18岁时，她扯掉清朝龙旗，踩在脚下，大喊共和。

D17.en: As a small child, she cried seeing a chef kill a duck and never ate duck again.
D17.zh: 幼年时，她看见厨师宰鸭就哭了，从此不吃鸭子。

D10.en: At 22, she saw her mother crying and her father, ill with liver disease, pleading.
D10.zh: 22岁时，她母亲哭着，患肝病的父亲劝着。

M3.en: At 22, she said it was 'out of admiration for a hero'.
M3.zh: 22岁时，她说这是“出于对英雄的景仰”。

D6.en: At 25, he invited eight friends to his home for a New Year's dinner party.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他在家约了八位朋友聚餐过年。

D2.en: At 9, his father forced him to study Shuowen Jiezi, reading 10 characters daily.
D2.zh: 9岁时，他父亲硬要他读《说文解字》，每天认十个字。

D14.en: At 17, he gave a speech in Russian to three thousand people.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他用俄文对三千人发表演说。

D7.en: At 17, he denounced his father Chiang Kai-shek as a traitor to the revolution.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他谴责父亲蒋介石背叛革命。

D1.en: At 21, he greeted mocking farmers politely and accepted their offer to work together.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他客气地向讥笑他的农民问早安，并接受了一起耕田的提议。

D17.en: At 25, he sold his books and furniture to help someone with travel expenses to deliver a letter to his father.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他卖掉三箱书和家具帮人凑路费，只为托他带信给父亲。

D12.en: At 25, he married in March 1935; his son was born in December; he was jobless for a year, living on his wife's salary.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他于1935年3月结婚，同年12月长子出生；他约有一年没工作，靠太太工资生活。

D4.en: At 18, he was assigned as an apprentice at the Dinamo Electric Factory, experiencing Soviet labor life.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他被分配到狄拿马电气厂当学徒，开始体验苏联劳工生活。

D5.en: At 25, he worked tirelessly for his goals, ignoring opposition and personal difficulties.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他为达目的尽力宣传组织，不怕反对，不顾困难。

M4.en: At 17, he declared his father Chiang Kai-shek a traitor to the revolution and his enemy.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他宣布父亲蒋介石背叛革命，成为他的敌人。

D3.en: At 15, he was assigned to give a report on 'The Development of Human Society' in social science class.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他在社会科学课上被指定作关于“人类社会的发展”的报告。

D20.en: At 25, he was elected vice-chairman of a village soviet and effectively served as chairman due to the chairman's illness.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他被农民选为农村苏维埃副主席，因正主席生病，实际担任主席职务。

D13a.en: At 25, after four days, he yielded and agreed to a letter drafted for him.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，第四天他屈服了，同意了他们代拟的家信。

D10.en: At 18, he felt depressed in the hospital because only three Russian friends visited him, no Chinese.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他在医院里感到格外忧闷，因为只有三个俄国朋友来看他，没有一个中国人。

D13b.en: At 25, under Wang's pressure and after four days of debate, he finally agreed to the demand.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，在王的压力下，经过四天争论，他最终同意了这一要求。

D12.en: At 16, he arrived in a strange place with no relatives and did odd jobs to survive.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他举目无亲，靠干零活打短工维持生计。

D18.en: At 14-19, he sold buns but ate them, then sold trinkets but gambled away the profits.
D18.zh: 14到19岁间，他卖包子自己吃了，当货郎又输掉了担子。

D4.en: At 17, he used his stepfather's veterinary knowledge to treat a horse and succeeded.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他用继父教的兽医知识治好了油坊的大马。

D5.en: At 14, he grabbed money from the gambling table and ran, shouting that the wind blew it away.
D5.zh: 14岁时，他把桌上的钱搂过来就跑，嚷着风把钱吹跑了。

D3.en: At age 20-25, he noted that most students in Fengtian preferred old history books, but Guo Songling liked new publications.
D3.zh: 他20到25岁间注意到奉天读书人多读旧史，郭松龄爱看新书刊。

D7.en: At 14, he refused to work, talked back, and fought back when hit by his teacher.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他不动活、顶嘴，还手打了师傅。

D6.en: At 16, he judged a stranger as important and helped him with the cart and tea.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他判断此人非同寻常，殷勤地帮他套车端茶。

D1.en: At 16, he fell gravely ill, was abandoned in a ditch, and rescued by an old man.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他重病被扔到沟里，被樊老汉救回家。

D17.en: At 19, he was rescued and cared for by Yu Tianlang's mother for over three months.
D17.zh: 19岁时，他被于天朗的母亲救回并抚养三个多月。

D9.en: At 25, he recruited six men to expand his forces.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他招收了六个人来扩充势力。

M1.en: At 19-20, he ranked first in his first two months of school.
M1.zh: 19到20岁时，他头一个月就考了第一，第二个月又考了第一。

D8.en: At 20-25, he strictly disciplined students and even officers.
D8.zh: 20到25岁时，他严格管教学生，还约束队长和区队长。

D19.en: He noted that only the Joint Brigade had a proper promotion system ensuring no grievances.
D19.zh: 他指出只有联合旅有按制度晋升的体系，保证军无怨言。

D15.en: At 23, he went to the front line and faced danger.
D15.zh: 23岁时，他上过前线，经历过危险。

D12.en: At 17, he had to send money to support his mother.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他必须寄钱赡养母亲。

D13a.en: At 17, he firmly stopped his arranged marriage, citing studies.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他斩钉截铁地阻止了婚事，借口求学要紧。

D2.en: At 23, he decided to socialize with educated women to reduce his loneliness.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他决定与有教育的女子交际，以减孤冷之性。

D1.en: At 18, his jail experience shamed him into studying for a scholarship.
D1.zh: 18岁时，牢狱之耻使他发愤读书，考取庚款留美。

D18.en: At 15-25, he cultivated his own barren fields and helped others farm and sew.
D18.zh: 15到25岁间，他耕种自家的荒田，还帮人种田、做裁缝。

D19.en: In his 20s-30s, he cleared the old house foundation to build a new, dry house.
D19.zh: 他二十到三十岁时，扒开老屋基，准备建新屋。

D20.en: At 25, he wrote to Chen Duxiu proposing eight literary revolution conditions.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他写信给陈独秀，提出八个文学革命条件。

D10.en: At 19, he sighed, regretted, and felt sorry for his mother.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他忍不住叹气，百分懊悔，觉得对不住慈母。

D3.en: At 23, he wrote in his diary about Western political trends from laissez-faire to socialism.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他在日记里写西方政治从放任主义转向社会主义。

D6.en: At 23, he mused that the ideal woman is hard to find and one must compromise.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他感叹意中人难寻，最终不得不妥协。

D9.en: At 24, he dreamed of a journal with unified views, a dream he never realized.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他梦想办一份有一致主张的杂志，但这个梦想从未实现。

D11.en: At 8-9, he made a miniature temple from a swallow nest box and a medicine box.
D11.zh: 八九岁时，他用燕窝匣子和午时茶匣子做了一座圣庙模型。

M1.en: At 25, he resolved to practice writing poetry in vernacular Chinese for several years.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他决心用几年时间练习用白话作诗。

M6.en: At 23, he described a friend's refusal to serve in the army as an idealist's righteous act, like 'footsteps in an empty valley'.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他形容朋友拒绝参军是理想主义的义举，犹如‘空谷之足音’。

D7.en: At 18, a night in jail spurred him to study hard and later embrace prohibition.
D7.zh: 18岁时，一夜牢狱经历刺激他发愤读书，并接受了禁酒理念。

D14.en: At 15, he published a popular geography article under the pen name 'Qizishengsheng'.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他以笔名‘期自胜生’发表了一篇通俗地理学文章。

D5.en: At 25, he felt confident writing prose in vernacular but not poetry, and wanted to practice for years.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他自信能用白话作散文但尚未用于韵文，想用数年时间实地练习。

M4.en: At 25, he decided to stop writing classical poetry, making 'Qu Guo Ji' his last classical verse collection.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他决定不再作文言诗词，让《去国集》成为绝笔。

D13b.en: At 23, he became one of six rotating editors of 'New Youth' starting from volume 4 in 1918.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，他从1918年第4卷起成为《新青年》六位轮流编辑之一。

D12.en: As a small child, he fled with his parents during the anti-Yuan struggle, never leaving his mother's side until her death.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他随父母在反袁斗争中逃亡，寸步不离母亲，直到她去世。

D13b.en: At 20, he promised his stepmother to obey his father in the Northeast.
D13b.zh: 20岁时，他答应继母去东北后听父亲的话。

D5.en: During the Japanese invasion, he fled to Shanghai, Chongqing, and finally settled in Taizhou, Shandong under Japanese rule.
D5.zh: 在日本侵略期间，他辗转流亡上海、重庆，最后落脚在日本统治下的山东泰州。

D9.en: At 23, he spoke about joining the Lixhi Society and discussed forming a Youth Aspiration Society in Anqing.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他谈起参加励志社的事，并商议在安庆成立青年励志社。

D4.en: As a teenager, he worked alongside laborers, eating simple food and enduring harsh conditions.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他与工人同作工，食夸饼饮自来水，冬衣袷夏无盖。

D2.en: At 25, he carried a bundle and umbrella to Wuhu to launch the Anhui Vernacular Newspaper.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他背包袱拿雨伞到芜湖创办《安徽俗话报》。

D7.en: At 24, he gave speeches at a library that became a revolutionary secret society hub.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他在藏书楼发表演说，该地成为革命秘密结社的机关。

D3.en: At 25-26, he visited Cai Yuanpei, Hu Shi, and others, and interacted with Beida students like Fu Sinian.
D3.zh: 25到26岁间，他拜访蔡元培、胡适等人，并与傅斯年等北大学生交往。

D6.en: He transferred to Zhicheng Middle School in Beiping for senior year, working part-time.
D6.zh: 他转到北平致诚中学读高三，半工半读。

D11.en: As a small child, he and friends shouted 'Fire!' to trick a sleeping guard.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他和一群小朋友在门前喊‘失火’捉弄睡着的户差。

D13a.en: At 18, his mother arranged his marriage to Gao Xiaolan.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他母亲安排他与高晓岚完婚。

M2.en: He called his father 'comrade' due to resentment over his father's neglect.
M2.zh: 他因怨恨父亲冷漠而称父亲为‘同志’。

D1.en: Whenever politically frustrated, he devoted himself to research.
D1.zh: 每当政治上受挫，他就专心致志进行研究。

M7.en: He co-founded the China Revive Society with Huang Xing and others.
M7.zh: 他与黄兴等人共同成立了华兴会。

D10.en: During this period, he had a bad temper and often quarreled with Pan Lanzhen.
D10.zh: 这一阵他肝火重，常和潘兰珍吵架。

M3.en: As a youth, he admired the reformists led by Kang Youwei and Liang Qichao.
M3.zh: 青年时期，他倾慕康梁维新派。

D12.en: From age 6, he did chores and helped with accounts; at 14-15, he worked full days in the fields alongside hired laborers.
D12.zh: 他从6岁起做家务和农活，识字后帮父亲记账；14到15岁时，整天和长工一起在地里干活。

D2.en: He joined the Work-Study Plan and went to France in 1920.
D2.zh: 他参加了勤工俭学计划，并于1920年去了法国。

D13a.en: At 14, his father arranged a marriage for him, but he never recognized it and never lived with his wife.
D13a.zh: 十四岁时父亲包办娶了媳妇，他始终不承认，从未同居。

D18.en: At 11, he canceled a pig purchase his father had made after hearing the seller's complaint about the low price.
D18.zh: 十一岁时，他听卖猪农民抱怨后，退掉了父亲已付定金的买卖。

D3.en: As a child, he stayed up late reading the novel Water Margin by a tiny oil lamp, covering himself with a quilt to hide the light.
D3.zh: 幼年时他熬夜读《水浒传》，用被子遮住油灯和自己。

D11.en: He secretly read banned novels like Water Margin during class.
D11.zh: 他上课时偷读《水浒传》等禁书，老师一来就用经书遮住。

D5.en: He ran down Yuelu Mountain in a thunderstorm to test an ancient saying.
D5.zh: 他在雷电交加的夜晚从岳麓山跑下来，体验《书经》中的境界。

D9.en: At 24, he ordered police to fire and students to set off firecrackers, scaring off retreating troops.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他叫警察开枪，让学生放鞭炮，吓退了溃军。

D16.en: At 25-26, he asked Hu Shi a question, but Hu refused to answer upon learning he was an unregistered student.
D16.zh: 二十五到二十六岁时，他向胡适提问，胡得知他未注册后拒绝回答。

D4.en: He stayed up all night reading Water Margin by a tiny oil lamp, shielding the light.
D4.zh: 他熬夜读《水浒传》，用被子遮住油灯以免灯光外泄。

M1.en: He was ranked third among thousands of students by his teacher Yang.
M1.zh: 杨先生把他列为长沙教过的几千名学生中的第三位。

D6.en: At 21, helped form the Communist Youth League in France, later incorporated into the CCP.
D6.zh: 21岁时在法国协助组建共青团，后并入中共。

D15.en: He hiked, swam in cold ponds, sunbathed, and slept outside for months.
D15.zh: 他爬山、在凉水里游泳、晒日光浴，有半年睡在院子里。

M4.en: He called for a great philosophical revolutionary like Tolstoy to cleanse the nation's old ideas.
M4.zh: 他呼吁出现像托尔斯泰那样的大哲学革命家来洗涤国民旧思想。

D1.en: He persisted in running the night school, promising to improve physics class when it went poorly.
D1.zh: 他顽强地维持夜校，物理课效果不佳时向学员许诺改进。

D19.en: He cut margins from newspapers and stitched them into notebooks with place names.
D19.zh: 他把报纸空白处剪下钉成本子，上面写着地名。

D10.en: He felt ashamed and frustrated after being questioned by a guest, retreating awkwardly.
D10.zh: 他被客人责问后羞愧难当，狼狈而退。

D17.en: At 25, he arranged for his younger brother to attend primary school in Changsha.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他安排弟弟到长沙读小学。
