D8.en: He stormed out of a friend's house for interrupting political talk to order groceries.
D8.zh: 他因朋友打断政治讨论去吩咐买猪肉而愤然离去，不再来往。

D14.en: He became a contributor to New Youth under a pseudonym.
D14.zh: 他用笔名成为《新青年》的撰稿人。

M2.en: He wrote 'How to avenge? In us students!'
M2.zh: 他写道：何以报仇？在我学子！

M5.en: He said he only admired Zeng Guofan among modern people.
M5.zh: 他说近代人中他只佩服曾国藩。

M6.en: He said without finding truth for ten years, he would have no ambition; without it for life, no ambition at all.
M6.zh: 他说十年未得真理即十年无志，终身未得即终身无志。

M8.en: He hoped for the destruction of the old universe to create a new one.
M8.zh: 他盼望旧宇宙毁灭，从而得到新宇宙。

D20X.en: He resolutely withdrew from Hunan Provincial High School.
D20X.zh: 他断然从湖南全省高等中学校退学。

M7.en: He invited patriotic youth to contact him, seeking those ready to die for the country.
M7.zh: 他邀请有志爱国工作的青年联系他，要结交随时准备为国捐躯的青年。

M3.en: He once worshipped Kang Youwei and Liang Qichao.
M3.zh: 他一度崇拜康有为和梁启超。

D7.en: At 21, he called for overthrowing the Anfu faction, its leaders, warlords, and foreign forces.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他号召推倒安福派及其首领、军阀和外来势力。

D16.en: At 22, he argued in court that the prosecutor contradicted himself by deeming petitions legal yet suing under criminal law.
D16.zh: 22岁时，他在法庭上辩称检察官自相矛盾，既认为请愿合法又依刑律起诉。

D3.en: At 20, he read New Youth morning and night, strongly endorsing its anti-Confucian, celibacy, and literary revolution views.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他早晚读《新青年》，极端赞成其排孔、独身和文学革命主张。

D12.en: At age 10-11, he began managing household finances and social engagements due to family hardship.
D12.zh: 10到11岁时，他因家境艰难开始当家，照管柴米油盐和外出应酬。

D2.en: At 22, he organized a daily schedule of morning interrogation prep, afternoon reading, and evening discussion and recreation.
D2.zh: 22岁时，他组织制定日程：上午预备讯问，下午读书，晚上研究问题和娱乐。

D9.en: At 16, he co-founded the 'Dedication and Fellowship Society' at Nankai School.
D9.zh: 16岁时，他与同学发起组织了南开学校的‘敬业乐群会’。

D19.en: Between 15 and 19, his dormitory won awards for being tidy and clean.
D19.zh: 15到19岁间，他住的宿舍获得“整齐洁净”嘉奖。

M1.en: At 20, he hoped to pass the government scholarship exam to ease his mind and repay kindness step by step.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他希望考上官费，安心向上走以报恩。

M7.en: At 20, he believed those with great ambitions should save the country and serve society.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他认为有大志向的人应救国、尽力社会。

D20.en: At 24, he was elected propaganda commissioner of the Central Executive Committee of the Chinese Communist Party.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他被选为中央执行委员会宣传委员。

D14.en: At 16-17, he represented his school in speech contests and won first place two years in a row.
D14.zh: 16到17岁时，他代表学校参加演讲比赛，连续两年获得第一名。

M4.en: At 20, he said we should learn abroad to shape our nation like the West and East.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他说我们到国外求真学问，要造出一种泰东西的民族样子来主宰自己的民族。

D11.en: At 24-25, he published a series of articles in The Youth, including on communism and the October Revolution.
D11.zh: 24到25岁时，他在《少年》上发表了一系列文章，包括《共产主义与中国》和《十月革命》等。

D1.en: At 20, he felt extremely distressed after failing experiments two days in a row.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他因连续两天试验失败而心中难堪异常。

D5.en: At 22, he traveled to France as part of the 15th work-study group, staying in the steerage with 197 others.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他作为第15批赴法勤工俭学学生，和197人住在统舱里。

D6.en: At 17, he proposed that he and his roommate separate to live with others to unite more classmates.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他提议和同住两年的朋友分开，与其他同学同住以团结更多人。

D15.en: As a teenager, he persisted in running, playing soccer, and doing exercises in the bitter cold.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他坚持在凛冽寒风中跑步、踢球、做操。

D17.en: At 12-15, he visited a sick school worker and gave him his last eight coppers for medicine.
D17.zh: 12到15岁时，他去看望生病的校工，把仅有的八个铜元给了他治病。

M2.en: At 20, he hoped to pass the government-funded exam to ease his mind and repay kindness step by step.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他希望考上公费，这样心就安了，一步步向上走，或许有报恩的日子。

M6.en: At 19, he wrote a poem expressing his determination to study abroad and save the nation.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他写诗表达东渡求学、济世救国的决心。

D12.en: He persuaded his wife to let his mother raise their son and gave her 30 mu of land.
D12.zh: 他说服妻子让母亲抚养儿子，并给了她三十亩地。

D2.en: At 23-24, he systematically studied Marxist classics like the Communist Manifesto.
D2.zh: 23到24岁间，他系统学习了《共产党宣言》等马列经典。

D6.en: At 22, he was recommended by He Minfan to join the Socialist Youth League.
D6.zh: 22岁时，贺民范介绍他参加社会主义青年团。

D4.en: At 9, he threw a black object at a dog, which rolled on the ground and ran away.
D4.zh: 9岁时，他朝狗扔了个黑乎乎的东西，狗满地打滚后跑了。

D15.en: At 15, he practiced hitting a sandbag and could strike a 30-40 kg one.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他练习击打沙袋，能击打开三四十公斤重的沙袋。

D5.en: At 23, he crossed the dangerous Iman River border between Japanese and Soviet zones.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他穿越日占区与苏俄控制区之间危险的伊曼河边界。

D1.en: At 18-22, he faced repeated setbacks but his thinking kept advancing.
D1.zh: 18到22岁间，他屡遭挫折，但思想不断前进。

D9.en: At 21, he joined the student movement in Changsha and became a backbone member.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他参加长沙学生运动，成为骨干分子。

D7.en: At 24, he told a warlord that grinding him to pulp wouldn't change the strike demands.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他对军阀说，把他碾成肉泥也无济于事。

D20.en: At 24, he served as the full representative of the workers' club, living there to handle all affairs.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他担任工人俱乐部全权代表，长住俱乐部应付一切。

M4.en: At 23, he joined the Chinese Communist Party, a turning point in his life.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他加入中国共产党，这是他一生中的重要转折点。

M7.en: At 24, he was passionately preparing to return to China to pursue the cause of saving the nation.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他满怀激情地准备回到祖国去干一番救国救民的壮丽事业。

M3.en: At 18-19, he deeply admired national heroes like Su Wu, Ban Chao, and Yue Fei, moved by their patriotic deeds.
M3.zh: 18到19岁时，他十分崇敬苏武、班超、岳飞等民族英雄，并为他们的爱国壮举所感动。

D13b.en: At 18, he was sent to the Soviet Union to study aircraft radio instrumentation.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他被派去苏联学飞机无线电仪表专业。

D13a.en: He recognized his mistakes and obeyed the organization's arrangement after patient education.
D13a.zh: 他在耐心教育下认识到自己的缺点错误，最后服从了组织的安排。

D11.en: At 11-12, whenever he had time, he would go to the study with Hong Gengyang and read book after book.
D11.zh: 11到12岁时，他一有时间就和洪赓扬结伴钻进书房，读了一本又一本。

D14.en: At 24, he performed on stage with Xiao Jinguang at a New Year party, though he had never acted before.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他和萧劲光等一起上台表演文艺节目，尽管他从来没有演过戏。

D12.en: At 15, he was among the Red Army troops transferring from Jinggangshan to southern Jiangxi.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他随红军部队从井冈山转移至赣南。

D4.en: At age 15, Hu Yaobang was among the Red Army troops moving to southern Jiangxi after Jinggangshan fell.
D4.zh: 十五岁时，胡耀邦随红军转移赣南。

D9.en: At 19-20, he organized and participated in propaganda activities, writing slogans and giving speeches.
D9.zh: 19到20岁时，他组织并参与宣传活动，写标语、发表演说。

D7.en: At 23, he confronted a KMT official, accusing him of undermining unity.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他当面质问国民党官员，指责其破坏军民关系。

D20.en: At 24, he was appointed head of the General Political Department's Organization Department.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他被任命为军委总政治部组织部部长。

D2.en: At 18-19, he devoured every book and newspaper in the office to raise his revolutionary consciousness.
D2.zh: 18到19岁时，他如饥似渴地读机关里所有的书报，提高革命觉悟。

D3.en: At 16, he curiously flipped through a Marxist pamphlet and learned about class struggle.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他好奇地翻过一本马克思著作的小册子，知道了阶级斗争。

D6.en: At 22, he led everyone in singing during recreational activities.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他在娱乐活动中指挥大家唱歌。

D14.en: At 11, he enthusiastically taught revolutionary songs and painted slogans on walls.
D14.zh: 11岁时，他热情地教唱革命歌曲，在墙上刷写标语。

M4.en: At 11, he was introduced to revolutionary ideas by a teacher, awakening his class consciousness.
M4.zh: 11岁时，老师向他介绍革命思想，唤醒了他的阶级意识。

D15.en: As a teenager, he walked a hilly 20 km round trip daily for two years, totaling over 25,000 li.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他每天走20公里崎岖山路往返，两年累计两万五千里。

D16.en: He wrote that executing discipline, like expelling a bad element, strengthens the party.
D16.zh: 他写道，执行纪律也是教育，开除坏分子会使队伍更强健。

D11.en: At 15, he wrote a play titled 'Down with Chai Shanhu' for the propaganda team.
D11.zh: 15岁时，他自编剧本《打倒柴山虎》给宣传队。

D5.en: At 17, he learned from panicked pedestrians that enemy troops were approaching the town.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他从惊慌的行人处得知国民党部队正开向白兔潭镇。

D10.en: At 18, he burst into a leader's room, sobbing that he was not an 'AB团' member.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他扑到顾书记面前哭着说：我不是AB团。

M7.en: At 19, his work centered on recruiting soldiers, collecting grain, and supporting the front.
M7.zh: 19岁时，他的工作围绕扩红、征粮和支前展开。

D1.en: At 19, despite illness, he forgot his pain while busy mobilizing peasants and workers to join the Red Army.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他支撑病体，忙于动员贫苦农民和修路工人参加红军。

D17.en: At 20, he and his team rescued and transported comrades who collapsed from hunger and cold along the road.
D17.zh: 20岁时，他和收容队员沿路救助因饥寒倒下的战友。

D19.en: At 22, he meticulously handled every step of publishing, from writing to distribution, ensuring high quality.
D19.zh: 22岁时，他兢兢业业，从组稿到发送精雕细刻，使刊物文章质量高、形式清新。

D3.en: At 13, he wrote an essay praised by his teacher.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他写了一篇受到老师褒奖的文章。

D7.en: At 20, he stammered out a refusal.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他结结巴巴地表示拒绝。

D13a.en: At 20, he had no choice but to return to the army.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他走投无路，只好返回部队。

D5.en: At 20, he led a charge that routed the enemy.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他大喊“冲啊”，率排冲锋，迫使敌军溃散。

D9.en: At 23, he ordered burning oxen to charge the enemy.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他下令点燃牛尾，驱牛冲敌阵。

M1.en: At 20, he was promoted to company commander for his decisiveness.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他因机智果断被提升为连长。

D20.en: At 21, he was appointed commander of the 28th Regiment.
D20.zh: 21岁时，他被任命为红28团团长。

D8.en: At 22, he wrote Mao a letter attacking Zhu De.
D8.zh: 22岁时，他给毛泽东写信攻击朱德。

D2.en: At 25, he kept a notebook recording his battles.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他随身携带小本子记录战役数据。

D11.en: At age 5, he began studying Confucian classics at a relative's home, then transferred to a larger private school.
D11.zh: 5岁时，他在亲戚家学习儒家经典，第二年转入更大的私塾。

D3.en: At 16, while sailing to France, he was impressed by the unequal relationship between white masters and local laborers.
D3.zh: 16岁时，在去法国的船上，他对白人与当地劳工的不平等关系印象深刻。

D12.en: At 16-17, he worked as a waiter, porter, construction laborer, and cleaner.
D12.zh: 16到17岁时，他当过招待、搬运工、建筑工和清洁工。

M1.en: At 11, he passed a competitive exam and became a boarder at a county high school.
M1.zh: 11岁时，他通过竞争激烈的考试，进入县高级小学并成为寄宿生。

D6.en: At 14, he participated in a demonstration as part of the May Fourth Movement.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他参加了五四运动的示威活动。

D14.en: At 14, he participated in a demonstration as part of the May Fourth Movement.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他参加了五四运动的示威活动。

D2.en: At 22-23, he wrote in his resume that his father hoped he would become a high official.
D2.zh: 22到23岁时，他在简历中写道父亲盼他当大官。

D5.en: At 21, he chaired a Kuomintang mass meeting in Paris, commemorating Wang Jingqi.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他在巴黎主持国民党群众大会，纪念王京歧。

D18.en: At 6-11, he secretly gave five silver dollars to a classmate to treat his sister.
D18.zh: 6到11岁时，他悄悄塞给同学五个银元，让他给妹妹治病。

D1.en: He insisted on going to school in all weather, refusing his parents' advice to stay home.
D1.zh: 他风雨无阻去上学，拒绝父母劝他留在家里的建议。

M2.en: He bore his parents' expectation to carry on ancestral ambitions and bring honor to the family.
M2.zh: 他背负着父母“承祖志、耀门庭”的期望。

D9.en: At 21, he chaired a Kuomintang mass meeting in Paris to commemorate Wang Jingqi and denounce imperialism.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他在巴黎主持国民党群众大会，纪念王京歧并揭露帝国主义迫害。

M4.en: At 21, he chaired a Kuomintang mass meeting in Paris to commemorate Wang Jingqi and denounce imperialism.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他在巴黎主持国民党群众大会，纪念王京歧并揭露帝国主义迫害。

D13a.en: At 15, he was excited about his father's decision, as it matched his own ideas of saving China through science and industry.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他对父亲的决定非常兴奋，因为这与他科学救国、工业救国的想法一致。

D16.en: At 22, he excelled in debates, earning the nickname 'Little Steel Cannon' for his sharp reasoning and eloquence.
D16.zh: 22岁时，他在辩论中表现出色，因推理严密、口才出众被称作“小钢炮”。

D19.en: At 15, he planned his mother's daily chores to eliminate repetitive work.
D19.zh: 15岁时，他为母亲的日常劳动做了合理规划，废除了重复性劳动。

D4.en: At 16, he ran along the beach shouting, 'Hurry up! Let's pick seashells before the tide rises!'
D4.zh: 16岁时，他边跑边喊：“快点儿啊！捡贝壳去啰，海潮涨满了可就捡不着了！”

M7.en: At 15, he began to develop a strong sense of responsibility for his country and nation.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他心中逐渐萌发对国家、民族强烈的责任感。

D20.en: At 22, he was assigned as group leader of the 'theorist squad' due to his above-average education and revolutionary experience.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他因文化水平中上且有革命经历，被编入理论家班并任小组长。

D8.en: At 22, he was nicknamed 'Little Cannon' for his sharp debating skills.
D8.zh: 22岁时，他因辩论犀利被同志们称为‘小钢炮’。

M6.en: At 22, he resolved to dedicate himself to the Party and the proletariat.
M6.zh: 22岁时，他决心把身子交给党，为无产阶级利益斗争。

D2.en: At 5-7, he learned the Hundred Family Surnames and Three Character Classic under his strict father's daily lessons.
D2.zh: 5到7岁时，他在严厉父亲的要求下每天学五个字，学完了《百家姓》和《三字经》。

D11.en: At 15, he wrote a seven-character regulated verse titled 'Frost Cold, Heavy Dew, Wild Geese Across the Sky'.
D11.zh: 15岁时，他作了一首七律《霜寒初重雁横空》。

D14.en: At 21, he organized a drama performance at Dawang Temple to raise funds for the May 30th Movement.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他为支援五卅运动组织会员在大王庙演文明戏。

D16.en: At 15, he matched a couplet with the line 'In Ziwu Valley, birds fly and rabbits run; know that days and months pass easily.'
D16.zh: 15岁时，他对上了李少山出的上联，下联为：子午谷鸟飞兔走，须知日月易蹉跎。

D13a.en: At 25, the Party allowed him to stay in Shanghai and assigned him to propaganda work in the Huxi District Committee.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，党组织接受他的要求，派他到沪西区委做宣传工作。

D6.en: At 21, he was elected as an officer of the Wuchang Student Union and the Hubei Youth League Executive Committee.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他被选为武昌学生联合会干事和湖北青年团联合会执行委员。

D9.en: At 20, he co-founded the Henan-Anhui Youth Society and was elected director of affairs.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他和同学发起成立豫皖青年学会，被推选为事务部主任。

M1.en: At 16-20, he was often the first to receive the monthly stipend, along with Chen Shaoyu.
M1.zh: 16到20岁间，每次发放的第一本不是他的就是陈绍禹的。

M4.en: At 21, he joined the Chinese Communist Party, driven by his belief in Marxism and willingness to fight for communism.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他因信仰马克思主义并愿为共产主义奋斗，加入了中国共产党。

M7.en: At 16, he wrote a poem calling on compatriots to awaken and fight for survival.
M7.zh: 16岁时，他写诗呼吁同胞觉醒奋战以图存。

D4.en: At 20, during winter break, he visited farmers and wrote a poem titled 'Visiting a Farmhouse'.
D4.zh: 20岁寒假回家时，他访问农民并写了一首《访农家》诗。

D13b.en: At 21, he joined the Chinese Communist Party in the summer of 1925.
D13b.zh: 21岁那年夏天，他加入了中国共产党。

D3.en: At 21, he wrote an article titled 'A Simple Explanation of Society, Sociology, Social Science, Social Problems, and Socialism'.
D3.zh: 21岁时，他写了《社会、社会学、社会科学、社会问题、社会主义底浅释》一文。

D5.en: At 21, he argued with someone at the provincial party department, threatening to report to the police if not allowed to go.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他在省党部与人争吵，威胁说如果不同意就去报告巡捕房。

D20.en: At 22, he was elected chairman of the student commune (equivalent to student council president).
D20.zh: 22岁时，他被选为学生公社主席（相当于学生会主席）。

D2.en: At 25, he studied over 10 courses including principles of state, imperial constitution, and applied economics.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他修了国家学原理、帝国宪法、应用经济学等10多门课。

D6.en: At 25, he befriended Tang Hualong through Sun Hongyi.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他经孙洪伊介绍结交汤化龙。

D11.en: At 22-23, he wrote a poem: 'Nine generations' revenge can be reported...'
D11.zh: 22到23岁间，他写下诗句：九世仇堪报，十年愿未违。

M4.en: At 19, he went to Tianjin to take school entrance exams, driven by a desire to study politics to save the nation.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他深感国势衰微，萌生深入研究政治以挽救民族的想法，于是和几个同学趁暑假去天津投考学校。

D9.en: At 21, he was elected as a representative to lead students in a rally.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他被推选为代表率领全校学生参加集会游行。

D7.en: At 23, he translated a book and wrote a rebuttal against Japan's aggression.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他翻译《支那分割之运命》并出版驳议，痛斥日本侵略言论。

D16.en: At 25, he published multiple articles and poems in a journal.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他在《言治》月刊发表《原杀》等多篇诗文。

D3.en: At 25-26, he resonated with Abe Isoo's lecture on urban problems.
D3.zh: 25到26岁间，他听安部矶雄的选修课并产生共鸣。

D10.en: At 25, he stood up angrily and said, 'You didn't listen carefully!'
D10.zh: 25岁时，他激动地站起来说：'这说明你根本就没有好好听。'

D13a.en: At 11, he married Zhao Renlan, who was six years older, as arranged by Li Ruzhen.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他在李如珍安排下与比他大六岁的赵纫兰结婚。

D20.en: At 23, he served as editorial department head with Yu Xianzhang.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他和郁宪章同任编辑部部长。

D4.en: At 25, he went to live at Han Yu Temple on Mount Wufeng with classmate Guo Xujing.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他和同学郭须静到昌黎西五峰山韩文公祠山居。

D13b.en: At 10, he married Zhao Renlan from his village.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他与本村赵纫兰结婚。

D14.en: At 24, he published over 30 articles and poems in that journal.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他在该刊发表文章、诗作30余篇。

D18.en: At 24, he published a book refuting Nakajima's work, co-translated with classmates.
D18.zh: 24岁时，他出版与同学合译并驳议的《〈支那分割之命运〉驳议》一书。

D1.en: At 13, after failing the child exam, he entered a private school run by Song Senyin.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他童试失利后进入宋森荫的学馆继续攻读。

M1.en: At 16, he took the child exam again and entered Yongpingfu Middle School.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他再次参加童试，得以进入永平府中学堂读书。

D5.en: At 19, he felt no fear in dangerous situations, experiencing a sense of adventurous beauty.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他在险境中几乎不畏惧，感到一种冒险的美趣。

D15.en: At 14, he ran down the stairs and out to the tennis courts.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他跑下楼梯，冲向网球场。

D3.en: He knew languages and had traveled to Europe eight times.
D3.zh: 他懂多门语言，并去过欧洲八次。

D2.en: At 11, he won one of five top prizes out of 240 boys.
D2.zh: 11岁时，他在240个男孩中赢得五个最高奖之一。

D4.en: At 12, his gun accidentally fired, but pellets hit the ground harmlessly.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他的枪意外走火，但弹丸无害地打在地上。

D19.en: He collected over a million stamps in 150 matching albums.
D19.zh: 他收集了超过一百万张邮票，放在150本配套的册子里。

D6.en: At 19, he took guests and his mother for a dinner cruise on the Half Moon.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他带客人和母亲乘坐半月号游船晚餐。

D9.en: At 18-21, he led several college clubs and was chosen as Harvard newspaper editor.
D9.zh: 18到21岁间，他领导了几个大学社团，并被选为哈佛校报编辑。

D13b.en: He was one of 84 remaining out of 106 in Columbia Law School.
D13b.zh: 他是哥伦比亚法学院106人中坚持到第三年的84人之一。

D1.en: He won a prize at Prize Day and would win it twice more before graduating.
D1.zh: 他在颁奖日获奖，并在毕业前又赢了两次。

D16.en: He won a prize in Latin and was one of the school's star debaters.
D16.zh: 他获得了拉丁语奖，还是学校的明星辩手之一。

M2.en: At 18, he wondered how he could let them down.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他想着自己怎么能让他们失望。

M1.en: He stood fourth in a class of nineteen, a position he more or less maintained.
M1.zh: 他在十九人的班级中排名第四，并大致保持了这个位置。

D7.en: He slept in the berth above hers and woke her each morning by dangling a string onto her face.
D7.zh: 他睡在她上铺，每天早上用一根绳子垂到她脸上把她弄醒。

D13a.en: He promised to wait until after graduating before marrying Eleanor.
D13a.zh: 他承诺等到毕业后才与埃莉诺结婚。

D20.en: He led several clubs in college and was chosen as editor of the Harvard newspaper.
D20.zh: 他在大学领导了几个俱乐部，并被选为哈佛校报的编辑。

D5.en: He was a Democrat but chose the Republican candidate, Theodore, for this vote.
D5.zh: 他是民主党人，但这次投票选了共和党候选人西奥多。

M4.en: He was a Democrat but chose the Republican candidate, Theodore, for this vote.
M4.zh: 他是民主党人，但这次投票选了共和党候选人西奥多。

D11.en: He drew a picture of the ceiling to be copied in the addition to the Hyde Park house.
D11.zh: 他画了一张天花板图样，用于海德公园房屋扩建时的复制。

D8.en: He told his mother, "If I didn't give the orders, nothing would happen!"
D8.zh: 他对妈妈说：“如果我不发号施令，什么都不会发生！”

D18.en: At age 20, he gave a necklace made from claws of a tiger he shot in India.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他送了一条用他在印度射杀的老虎爪子制成的项链。

M3.en: He carefully photographed the ground over which his hero had led a charge.
M3.zh: 他仔细拍摄了他的英雄带领冲锋的那片土地。

D12.en: At 14, she passed a railway recruitment exam and became a train attendant.
D12.zh: 14岁时，她考上铁路局，成为列车员。

D13b.en: She was restricted to being a housewife and assistant, barred from party office.
D13b.zh: 她被规定只能当家庭主妇和助手，不得担任党内职务。

D6.en: At 11, she gathered neighborhood kids to play a drama game.
D6.zh: 11岁时，她召集邻居孩子一起玩戏段。

D7.en: At 11-12, she was punished by a teacher and later expelled after more conflicts.
D7.zh: 11到12岁时，她被老师打手心，后来因多次冲突被开除。

D13a.en: At 19, she held a small informal wedding with only a few close friends.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，她办了简单的婚礼，只请了几位好友。

D2.en: At 19-20, she practiced scripts alone for hours, memorizing lines perfectly.
D2.zh: 19到20岁时，她关上门反复背台词、对镜练习，从早练到晚。

D8.en: At 25, she raged at a cook, threatening to make him eat a cockroach.
D8.zh: 25岁时，她对厨师发火，威胁让他吃掉虫子。

D18.en: At 19, she learned to smoke and drink, spending beyond her means to dress fashionably.
D18.zh: 19岁时，她学会抽烟喝酒，为打扮入时花钱超支。

D9.en: At 21, she socialized actively at a party, toasting and chatting with everyone.
D9.zh: 21岁时，她在聚会上穿梭敬酒，与众人谈笑。

D17.en: As a teenager, she helped a vomiting friend by patting her back and cleaning up.
D17.zh: 十几岁时，她照顾呕吐的朋友，拍背、漱口、清理。

D10.en: At 19, she was slapped, then cried loudly and challenged the man to hit her again.
D10.zh: 19岁时，她挨了一巴掌，先一愣，然后号啕大哭，喊着让对方打死自己。

D14.en: At 21, she wore a white striped silk dress and canvas shoes, with no makeup.
D14.zh: 21岁时，她穿白色条子纺绸旗袍和白帆布鞋，脸上不擦粉不施胭脂。

D3.en: At 20, she bought a radio and a phonograph, and spent half days learning popular songs.
D3.zh: 20岁时，她买了收音机和留声机，每天用半天时间听流行歌曲并跟着哼唱。

D5.en: At 20, she was arrested after being spotted by spies while fleeing with a companion.
D5.zh: 20岁时，她与同伴分头逃跑时被特务截住，被捕了。

M4.en: At 23, she felt that as a revolutionary artist and Chinese youth, she should go to Yan'an for the anti-Japanese cause.
M4.zh: 23岁时，她认为作为革命文艺工作者和中国青年，应该投奔延安参加抗日救亡。

M1.en: At 21, politically frustrated, she pinned her hopes on the play Nora to make a name on stage.
M1.zh: 21岁时，政治上失意，她巴望通过《娜拉》在舞台上出人头地。

D11.en: At 21, she walked barefoot on the beach, looking at her footprints, with rocks and sea around.
D11.zh: 21岁时，她赤脚在海滩漫步，低头看脚印，左边岩石右边大海。

D4.en: At 20, she invited a man who played erhu to accompany her singing, and they started interacting.
D4.zh: 20岁时，她邀擅长拉胡琴的人伴奏，她唱戏他拉琴，彼此有了来往。

M5.en: At 24, she became famous for acting and stood out in class, earning newfound respect.
M5.zh: 24岁时，她靠演戏出了名，在课堂上又出了风头，让人刮目相看。

D20X.en: At 23, unable to stay in Shanghai, she wanted to slip away.
D20X.zh: 23岁时，她无法在上海立足，想溜了。

D15.en: At 22-23, she grabbed Tang Na's hair and banged his head against the wall.
D15.zh: 22到23岁间，她抓住唐纳的头发，把他的头往墙上撞。

D19.en: At 23, she scooped up a pile of photos from a drawer and tossed them into a small suitcase.
D19.zh: 23岁时，她从抽斗里捧出一堆照片，扔进小皮箱。

M7.en: At 23, she got very excited about it; reportedly, she had left Shanghai for northern Shaanxi two months earlier.
M7.zh: 23岁时，她为之大大兴奋；据说两个月前，她已离沪赴陕北。

D16.en: At 19-20, his debating and public speaking won classmates' awed respect.
D16.zh: 19到20岁时，他的辩论和公开演讲赢得了同学们的敬畏。

D3.en: At 19, he discovered he had a mind.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他发现自己有头脑。

D2.en: As a child aged 9-14, his father drilled him on observations and challenged his expression.
D2.zh: 9到14岁时，他父亲就所见之物训练他，并挑战他的表达方式。

D11.en: At 22, he composed a political essay based on themes from his debating club.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他根据辩论俱乐部的主题写了一篇政治文章。

D14.en: At 24, in spare moments he returned to his father's pulpit to recite great speeches.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他在空闲时回到父亲的讲坛背诵著名演说。

D9.en: At 22, he made a pact with a classmate during senior year.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他在大四那年与同学达成了一项协议。

D20.en: At 22, in his senior year he became managing editor.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他在大四那年升任主编。

D13a.en: At 25, he thought about law only in mornings, keeping afternoons for his own subjects.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他只在上午思考法律，下午留给自己喜欢的科目。

M1.en: At 22, he graduated in the top third of his class with a 90.3% average.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他以90.3分的平均分在班级前三分之一毕业。

D1.en: At 17-18, he gained marked skill through painstaking and inexhaustible practice.
D1.zh: 17到18岁时，他通过刻苦不懈的练习获得了显著技能。

D18.en: At 25, he began his law practice in Atlanta in May 1882.
D18.zh: 25岁时，他于1882年5月在亚特兰大开始律师执业。

D7.en: At 22, he resisted a suggestion and moved from a boarding-house into a regular student room.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他拒绝了一项建议，从寄宿公寓搬进了普通学生宿舍。

D12.en: At age 10, his family set out for Canada with aunt Isabella caring for the children.
D12.zh: 10岁时，全家在姑母伊莎贝拉照顾下前往加拿大。

D5.en: At 24, he wrote to his cousin about taking risks.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他写信给表姐谈论冒险。

D8.en: His first reaction was to refuse the prize.
D8.zh: 他的第一反应是拒绝奖品。

D19.en: At 18, he wrote his diary in shorthand.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他用速记写日记。

D13b.en: At 7, he was taken out of local school and enrolled in a private school.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他被从当地学校转学到一所私立学校。

D3.en: At 20, he told others about abuses suffered by a group and how Germans hated Russians.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他告诉别人某个群体遭受的虐待以及德国人如何仇恨俄国人。

D12.en: At 18, he withdrew from university for medical examinations and treatment.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他退学接受检查和治疗。

D1.en: At 23, he had trouble finding a publisher; established publishers were not interested.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他找不到出版商；大出版社不感兴趣。

D6.en: At 20, he found a note from friends and spent the evening with them.
D6.zh: 20岁时，他找到朋友留的纸条，并和他们一起度过当晚。

M1.en: At 25, he dreamed of entering politics and reaching the White House.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他梦想进入政界甚至入主白宫。

D15.en: At 18, he raced in Joe's boat from Edgartown to Nantucket and back to Hyannis.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他乘坐乔的船从埃德加敦到楠塔基特再返回海恩尼斯比赛。

D13a.en: At 24, after speaking to his father, he finally decided it was time to act.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，和父亲谈过后，他终于决定是时候行动了。

D5.en: At 20, he decided to take a swim and it took over two hours to get the car out of the sand.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他决定去游泳，结果花了两个多小时才把车从沙子里弄出来。

D20.en: At 25, he won the presidency of Columbia after fending off a takeover.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他成功抵御收购后赢得了哥伦比亚大学校长职位。

D9.en: At 22, he proposed breaking the country into two autonomous districts with self-government.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他提议将国家分成两个自治区，给予自治权。

M2.en: At 23, he wrote an apologia for his father's life, enclosing it in a letter to his father.
M2.zh: 23岁时，他在给父亲的信中附上了一篇为父亲一生辩护的文字。

M5.en: At 16, he felt he could never win the approval his parents lavished on his elder brother.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他觉得自己永远无法赢得父母对哥哥那样的赞赏。

D16.en: At 18, he adopted a mock-heroic, self-deflating, witty style as his own.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他采用了一种戏谑英雄、自嘲、机智的风格。

M4.en: At 22, he asked how to avoid a world war if people felt that way.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他问如果人们那样感觉，如何避免世界大战。

D14.en: At 23, he protested in print against those who opposed rearmament in America.
D14.zh: 23岁时，他通过印刷品抗议那些反对美国重新武装的人。

D11.en: At 25, he began drafting an article he had long wanted to write but never dared publish.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他开始起草一篇他早就想写但一直不敢发表的文章。

D18.en: At 22, he and Big Arthur Goldsmith played the stock market with pretty good success.
D18.zh: 22岁时，他和阿瑟·戈德史密斯炒股赚了不少钱。

D8.en: At 25, he apologized when Rosen proved the allegation untrue.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他在罗森证明指控不实后道歉。

D17.en: At 19, he took her to a dance, danced with her, and had friends cut in so she felt popular.
D17.zh: 19岁时，他带她去俱乐部跳舞，和她跳舞开玩笑，还让朋友插进来让她觉得自己受欢迎。

M3.en: At 19, after reading about Francis I, he had a new hero.
M3.zh: 19岁时，读完弗朗西斯一世的传记后，他有了新偶像。

D19.en: He never hung up anything, just dropped it.
D19.zh: 他从不挂东西，直接扔下。

D15.en: At 21, he felt twice the man after three days of good shooting.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他打了三天好猎，觉得自己强壮了一倍。

D7.en: At 24, he called something sheer nonsense.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他说那完全是胡说八道。

D12.en: At 11, he had too little breath to blow out his candle when putting himself to bed.
D12.zh: 11岁时，他上床睡觉时喘不过气，吹不灭蜡烛。

D6.en: At 10-11, he listened to his mother tell stories of her early life and ancestors.
D6.zh: 10到11岁时，他听妈妈讲她早年的生活和祖先的故事。

D1.en: At 25, despite lack of success, he didn't consider calling off the hunt.
D1.zh: 25岁时，尽管没成功，他也没想过放弃打猎。

M4.en: At 21, he aimed to help the cause of better government in New York City.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他打算帮助改善纽约市的政府。

D2.en: At 18, he found it not as hard as expected to avoid drinking and smoking.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他发现不喝酒不抽烟没想象中那么难。

D10.en: At 19, he valued his marks mainly for his father's pride in them.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他看重成绩主要是因为父亲会为此骄傲。

D5.en: At 23, he climbed a mountain steeper than the Jungfrau.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他爬了一座比少女峰还陡的山。

D9.en: He won a victory in the House, getting his Reform Charter Bill to a third reading.
D9.zh: 他在众议院赢得胜利，使他的改革宪章法案进入三读。

D11.en: He wrote a letter to Edith Carow during his family's first tour abroad.
D11.zh: 他在家庭首次出国旅行期间给伊迪丝·卡罗写了一封信。

D3.en: At age 9, he wrote a letter describing his mouse collection to his sister.
D3.zh: 9岁时，他写信给姐姐描述他的老鼠收藏。

D4.en: He recorded the total number of birds he killed.
D4.zh: 他记录了他杀死的鸟的总数。

M1.en: He said he would study law and work hard for his wife.
M1.zh: 他说他将学习法律，并为他的妻子努力工作。

M7.en: He called for future work by all who wish their country well.
M7.zh: 他呼吁所有希望国家好的人未来要努力。

D14.en: At 9, he jumped with delight when he found you heard the mockingbird, and said get some of its feathers if you can.
D14.zh: 9岁时，他听说你听到了模仿鸟，高兴得跳起来，说如果你能弄到它的羽毛就弄一些。

D13b.en: At 18, he was told to prioritize morals, health, then studies.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他被告知要先顾道德，再顾健康，最后才是学业。

D13a.en: At 24, he abandoned the law as his political life grew.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他放弃了法律，因为政治生活日益扩大。

D8.en: At 18-22, he leaped up during roll call to protest mispronunciation of his name.
D8.zh: 18到22岁间，他在点名时跳起来抗议名字被念错。

D18.en: At 13, he said he had not had a respectable suit since leaving home.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他说离家后就没穿过体面的西装。

D19.en: At 10-11, he alone kept methodically at his record, never missing a day.
D19.zh: 10到11岁时，他独自坚持记录，一天不落。

D17.en: At 20-25, he spent every other Sunday evening at the Lodging House talking with boys.
D17.zh: 20到25岁间，他每隔一个周日晚去寄宿所和男孩们聊天。

M3.en: As a child and young man, he read his favorite books many times over.
M3.zh: 幼年到青年时，他反复阅读最爱的书。

M6.en: As a child and young man, a book was the only thing that could make him sit still.
M6.zh: 幼年到青年时，只有书能让他安静坐着。

D12.en: At 5-6, he attended to his baby sister, fixing her hair and rocking her to sleep.
D12.zh: 他五六岁时照顾妹妹，给她梳头、摇她入睡。

D6.en: In the 1890s, his house with its big yard and farm animals was a gathering place for neighborhood children.
D6.zh: 19世纪90年代，他家的大院子和农场动物成了邻里孩子们的聚集地。

D4.en: At 6-11, his goat was bald with a black face.
D4.zh: 他6到11岁时，他的山羊秃头黑脸。

D5.en: At 17, he traveled like a hobo, grabbing rides on freight cars.
D5.zh: 他17岁时像流浪汉一样扒货运火车旅行。

D2.en: At 14, his family bought an upright piano from the Kimball Piano Company of Chicago.
D2.zh: 他14岁时，家里从芝加哥金博尔钢琴公司买了一架立式钢琴。

D18.en: He hoped to tap investment opportunities that might bring profit to both of them.
D18.zh: 他希望能挖掘投资机会，可能给双方带来利润。

D3.en: At 16, he copied down and saved lines of a favorite poem by Tennyson, 'Locksley Hall'.
D3.zh: 他16岁时抄下并保存了丁尼生《洛克斯利大厅》中喜欢的诗句。

D9.en: At 16, he, Charlie Ross, Tasker Taylor, and others launched a first yearbook for the school.
D9.zh: 他16岁时和查理·罗斯、塔斯克·泰勒等人为学校创办了第一本年鉴。

D11.en: At 22-32, he named two sows Sarah Bernhardt and Carrie Nation; one cow, Nellie Bly.
D11.zh: 他22到32岁间给两头母猪取名莎拉·伯恩哈特和卡丽·纳辛，一头母牛取名内莉·布莱。

D13a.en: At 19, he obeyed superiors and cheerfully complied with rules.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他服从上级并愉快遵守所有规章制度。

D19.en: At 22, he kept books listing crop sales and buyers.
D19.zh: 22岁时，他记账，列出作物卖给谁以及价格。

M1.en: He was elected to receive Masonic degrees at Belton Lodge in 1909, and by March he passed to Master Mason.
M1.zh: 1909年，他当选在贝尔顿会所接受共济会等级，到3月时他通过了共济会大师傅的考核。

D20.en: At 24-26, he organized a lodge in Grandview and became its first Master.
D20.zh: 24到26岁间，他在格兰德维尤组织了一个分会并成为首任大师。

D14.en: At 21-22, he worked as an usher at a vaudeville house and saw famous acts.
D14.zh: 21到22岁时，他在一家杂耍剧场当引座员，看了所有著名表演。

D16.en: As a teenager, he would stand clear of his desk and square his shoulders before speaking in class.
D16.zh: 十几岁时，他在课堂上发言前会站离书桌并挺直肩膀。

D1.en: At 21-22, he refused another raise, quit the Bank of Commerce, and went to work for Union National Bank.
D1.zh: 21到22岁时，他拒绝再次加薪，辞去商业银行的工作，去了联合国民银行上班。

D13b.en: At 17, he took the job at John Truman's urging.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他在约翰·杜鲁门的催促下接受了那份工作。

M2.en: He noted that both his grandfathers had been Masons and his father said he always meant to join.
M2.zh: 他指出他的两位祖父都是共济会成员，他父亲说他一直想加入。

M4.en: At 25, he felt uplifted by brotherhood in an order claiming great antiquity, joined by Mozart and Andrew Jackson.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他因加入一个自称历史悠久的兄弟会而感到振奋，莫扎特和安德鲁·杰克逊也曾加入。

D7.en: He set up ten more tables in defiance and sold his last copies of the Pt. Huron Statement.
D7.zh: 他违抗命令又摆了十张桌子，卖掉了最后几份休伦港宣言。

D14.en: He was accepted after reading character sketches from scripts.
D14.zh: 他朗读了几个剧本中的人物速写后被录取了。

D19.en: He meticulously notched a tree branch by the river.
D19.zh: 他在河边的一根树枝上仔细刻了凹痕。

D2.en: He worked every vacation as a lifeguard and during school washing dishes or as a swimming instructor.
D2.zh: 他每个假期都在洛厄尔公园当救生员，上学期间洗碗或当游泳教练。

D18.en: He earned most of his money washing dishes at Lyda's Wood.
D18.zh: 他大部分钱是在莱达伍德女生宿舍洗碗挣的。

D1.en: He made his twenty-fifth rescue.
D1.zh: 他完成了第二十五次救援。

D15.en: He was active in athletics and dramatics during high school.
D15.zh: 他高中时积极参加体育和戏剧活动。

M1.en: His team placed third out of twelve, and he was recognized individually.
M1.zh: 他的队在十二支队伍中排名第三，他个人也获得了认可。

D9.en: At 21, he set up ten more tables in defiance and sold his last copies of the Pt. Huron Statement.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他违抗命令又摆了十张桌子，卖掉了最后几份《休伦港宣言》。

M3.en: At 21, he followed Tom Mix and Mary Pickford and imagined himself on screen beside them.
M3.zh: 21岁时，他追随汤姆·米克斯和玛丽·毕克馥，想象自己和他们一起出现在银幕上。

D4.en: At 25, he seemed determined to emulate Damon Runyon as a coiner of original slang.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他似乎决心效仿达蒙·鲁尼恩，创造原创俚语。

D5.en: At 17, he dived in and sought someone out in inky darkness.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他跳进水里，在漆黑中寻找某人。

D10.en: At 21, he felt the wave building, lifting him up, dark and angry yet exultant.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他感到浪潮在积聚，将他托起，黑暗愤怒却又狂喜。

D13a.en: At 11, he came back to her and said, "I want to declare my faith and be baptized."
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他回到她身边说：“我想宣告我的信仰并受洗。”

D11.en: At 16, he illustrated each card with silhouette drawings of himself as an authority figure.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他在每张卡片上画了自己的剪影，把自己画成权威人物。

D3.en: At 5, he read aloud to his parents a newspaper account of a notorious bombing.
D3.zh: 5岁时，他给父母大声朗读报纸上关于一起臭名昭著的爆炸案的报道。

D16.en: He pretended to be a sports announcer, using a broomstick as a microphone to create imaginary football games.
D16.zh: 他假装成体育播音员，用扫帚当麦克风，编造出整场虚构的足球比赛。

M6.en: At 21, he played out of habit rather than competitive spirit.
M6.zh: 21岁时，他打球是出于习惯而非竞争精神。

M4.en: At 21, his latest passion was pacifism.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他最新的热情是和平主义。

M7.en: At 19-21, he served two years on the student Senate and one year as its president.
M7.zh: 19到21岁时，他在学生参议院任职两年，其中一年担任主席。

D6.en: At 13, he arranged for a photographer to come to the ranch to record the occasion.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他安排摄影师来牧场记录这一场合。

D12.en: At 20-21, he hoped to relieve his parents' hardships and said he would begin on a small scale right now.
D12.zh: 20到21岁间，他希望减轻父母的艰辛，并说现在就要从小事做起。

D7.en: At 13-15, he ate with loud, slurping noises, violently cramming huge spoonfuls of food into his mouth.
D7.zh: 13到15岁间，他吃饭时发出响亮的吸吮声，暴力地把大勺食物塞进嘴里。

D2.en: At 21, he said her letters gave him strength, renewed courage, and bulldog tenacity.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他说她的信给了他力量、新的勇气和斗牛犬般的坚韧。

D3.en: At 10, he hid under the dining room table all through the meal to listen to the talk.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他躲在餐桌下听完整顿饭的谈话。

D4.en: At 20, he used his first paycheck to buy a volleyball and a softball.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他用第一张工资支票买了一个排球和一个垒球。

D10.en: At 20-21, he said her letters gave him strength and courage, and when one was delayed he felt sadness and disappointment.
D10.zh: 20到21岁间，他说她的信给他力量和勇气，信迟了就会感到悲伤和失望。

D9.en: He wrote editorials for the College Star in 1927.
D9.zh: 他1927年为《大学之星》写社论。

D5.en: At 16, he decided, without asking or telling anyone, to go with them and jumped into the car.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他决定不告诉任何人就和他们一起走，跳上了车。

M2.en: He said a letter gave him strength, courage, and tenacity.
M2.zh: 他说一封信给了他力量、勇气和坚韧。

D20.en: At 23, he became the youngest Speaker of the Little Congress.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他成为小议会最年轻的议长。

D8.en: He used his longer reach to take the biggest pork chop and last biscuit.
D8.zh: 他利用臂长抢到最大的猪排和最后一块饼干。

D16.en: He caucused the White Stars day and night to determine blocs.
D16.zh: 他日夜召集白星党团会议，判断各派倾向。

D1.en: He tramped from boardinghouse to boardinghouse all night courting votes.
D1.zh: 他整夜挨家挨户走访寄宿公寓拉票。

M7.en: At 21, he declared the world needs men who are not for sale, who master themselves, and who serve others.
M7.zh: 21岁时，他宣称世界需要不妥协、能自控、服务他人的人。

D14.en: At 19, he watched Franklin Roosevelt deliver the nominating speech as the excited leader of the White Stars.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他作为白星队的兴奋领袖观看了富兰克林·罗斯福的提名演讲。

M3.en: At 10, he wanted to be like his father, getting pensions for old people.
M3.zh: 10岁时，他想要像他爸爸一样，为老人争取养老金。

D18.en: At 18, he couldn't make enough money and faced leaving school unless he got a loan.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他挣不够钱，除非借到贷款否则得退学。

D15.en: At 20, he took a night job stuffing envelopes for 25 cents an hour.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他找了份夜工装信封，每小时25美分。

D19.en: At 24-27, he insisted every correspondent be listed on a file card with personal details.
D19.zh: 24到27岁间，他坚持每个通讯员都登记在档案卡上，附上个人细节。

D13b.en: Johnson's parents lived on a farm for 14 years, raising nine children.
D13b.zh: 约翰逊的父母在农场住了14年，养育了9个孩子。

D17.en: At 20, he told Coronado to learn English and bought him a book with his own money.
D17.zh: 20岁时，他让科罗纳多学英语，并用自己的钱给他买了本书。

M5.en: At 20, he hinted he wanted to find a girl with a lot of money.
M5.zh: 20岁时，他暗示想找个有钱的女孩。

M4.en: At 20-21, he had won believing in nothing, without a reform or principle he cared about.
M4.zh: 20到21岁间，他赢了却什么都不信，没有想推动的改革或在乎的原则。

D2.en: He learned and practiced speaking and debating techniques in high school.
D2.zh: 他在高中学到了演讲和辩论技巧并加以练习。

D18.en: He drove the invalid wife of one of his law professors on occasion, earning a little more.
D18.zh: 他偶尔开车送一位法学教授的病妻，多赚一点钱。

D9.en: He campaigned for friends in at least one service club national convention, unsuccessfully.
D9.zh: 他在至少一次服务俱乐部全国大会上为朋友助选，但没有成功。

D5.en: He was job hunting and made the rounds of all the top law firms in the city.
D5.zh: 他正在找工作，跑遍了城里所有顶级律师事务所。

D7.en: He successfully championed the amendment of campus rules to allow for dances, convincing his elders.
D7.zh: 他成功推动修改校园规则允许跳舞，说服了长辈们。

D1.en: Harold's death while he was in college led him to resolve to try even harder, work harder.
D1.zh: 哈罗德在他上大学时去世，这让他决心更加努力、更加勤奋。

D15.en: At age three, he was thrown from a horse-drawn wagon and struck by its metal wheel.
D15.zh: 三岁时，他从一辆马车里被甩出来，被金属车轮撞到。

D3.en: He especially liked James W. Riley's poems and asked to have them read over and over.
D3.zh: 他特别喜欢詹姆斯·W·赖利的诗，要求一遍又一遍地读给他听。

D13b.en: Hannah forced him to practice, against his wishes, every afternoon.
D13b.zh: 汉娜强迫他每天下午练习，尽管他不愿意。

D16.en: He stood his ground against a classmate in a torts class.
D16.zh: 他在侵权法课上与同学对峙，毫不退让。

M1.en: He was on the honor society for the fourth straight year and ranked second in his class.
M1.zh: 他连续第四年进入荣誉协会，班级排名第二。

D6.en: He made extensive connections and became a sought-after luncheon and dinner speaker.
D6.zh: 他建立了广泛的人脉，成为很受欢迎的午餐和晚餐演讲者。

D14.en: He joined the local little theater group after returning to Whittier.
D14.zh: 他回到惠蒂尔后加入了当地的小剧场团体。

M4.en: At 13, he went through a public conversion and spiritual reawakening.
M4.zh: 13岁时，他经历了一次公开的皈依和精神觉醒。

D20.en: He emerged unscathed from a melee with the title of Student Body President.
D20.zh: 他在混战中毫发无损，获得了学生会主席的头衔。

D8.en: At 20-21, he prepared his own elevation by arranging a division of electoral spoils.
D8.zh: 20到21岁时，他通过安排选举战利品分配来为自己铺路。

D11.en: He was dismissed from Columbia University for blasphemous writings.
D11.zh: 他因亵渎性文章被哥伦比亚大学开除。

D4.en: He played with defused artillery shells as a child.
D4.zh: 他小时候玩过拆掉引信的炮弹。

D20X.en: He could not find work with a Wall Street firm and applied unsuccessfully to the FBI.
D20X.zh: 他没能找到华尔街的工作，申请联邦调查局也失败了。

D13a.en: His mother got him a desk in a local law office, and he slunk back.
D13a.zh: 他母亲帮他在当地律所找了个位子，他灰溜溜地回去了。

D10.en: At 20, he sat stoically, lost in grief and locked in agony.
D10.zh: 20岁时，他面无表情地坐着，沉浸在悲痛和痛苦中。

D2.en: She stuck at her duty conscientiously and was more than competent.
D2.zh: 她尽职尽责，非常胜任。

D3.en: She devoured Hayek's book in 1945.
D3.zh: 她在1945年如饥似渴地读了哈耶克的书。

D18.en: She taught at a Grantham school during her first year's Long Vacation.
D18.zh: 她在第一年暑假期间在格兰瑟姆一所学校教书。

D19.en: At 23, she said if she had a £50 overdraft it would be her own fault for not knowing how to spend money.
D19.zh: 23岁时，她说如果自己透支了50英镑，那只能怪她不会花钱。

D9.en: She joined OUCA as soon as she went up to Oxford and threw herself into activities.
D9.zh: 她一进牛津就加入了OUCA，并积极参与活动。

D13b.en: As a child, she was forbidden from all pleasures on Sundays, even newspapers.
D13b.zh: 她小时候，星期天所有娱乐都被禁止，连报纸也不行。

D16.en: She began elocution lessons at age 10.
D16.zh: 她10岁时开始上演讲课。

M1.en: She won a scholarship to Kesteven and Grantham Girls' School at age 10.
M1.zh: 她10岁时获得凯斯特文和格兰瑟姆女子学校的奖学金。

D6.en: She and Kay went to a party with actors from the local rep.
D6.zh: 她和凯与当地剧团的演员一起参加了舞会。

M2.en: She wrote a letter to Muriel on 19 Apr 1945.
M2.zh: 她于1945年4月19日给穆里尔写了一封信。

D14.en: At 12, she won a prize for a piano duet at the eisteddfod.
D14.zh: 12岁时，她在艺术节上赢得钢琴二重奏奖。

D20.en: At 24-27, she became the first woman elected to its executive committee.
D20.zh: 24到27岁间，她成为首位当选其执行委员会的女性。

D13a.en: At 23, she quit her plastics job and joined Lyons ice-cream.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，她辞去塑料工作，加入莱斯冰淇淋。

D11.en: At 13, she wrote: 'Smile a while and when you smile another smiles and then there’s miles and miles of smiles.'
D11.zh: 13岁时，她写道：‘笑一会儿，当你笑时别人也笑，然后就有数英里的微笑。’

D1.en: At 24, she cheerfully set out to assail it.
D1.zh: 24岁时，她愉快地开始攻击它。

M4.en: At 24, she was utterly convinced that Labour policies could not work.
M4.zh: 24岁时，她完全相信工党政策行不通。

D4.en: She taught her daughters sewing and how to adapt material for new uses.
D4.zh: 她教女儿们缝纫和如何改造旧物新用。

D8.en: She always got her way, securing equipment and materials for experiments.
D8.zh: 她总能如愿以偿，搞到实验所需的设备和材料。

D5.en: She spoke outside factory gates to all-male workers, defying union officials.
D5.zh: 她在工厂门外对全是男工的工人讲话，违抗工会官员。

M3.en: At 19, wrote a letter to Muriel on 19 Apr. 1945.
M3.zh: 19岁时于1945年4月19日给穆里尔写信。

M7.en: She said women are intensely patriotic and worried about Britain's prestige.
M7.zh: 她说女性非常爱国，对英国威望下降深感忧虑。

D15.en: At 19, wrote a letter to Muriel on 19 Apr. 1945.
D15.zh: 19岁时于1945年4月19日写信给穆里尔

M5.en: At 19, wrote a letter to Muriel on 19 Apr. 1945.
M5.zh: 19岁时于1945年4月19日给穆里尔写信。

M8.en: At 19, wrote a letter to Muriel on 19 Apr. 1945.
M8.zh: 19岁时于1945年4月19日写信给穆里尔。

D2.en: At 25, while convalescent, he studied Vol. III of Capital in German.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他在康复期间学习了德文版《资本论》第三卷。

D3.en: At 25-26, he planned a vast work on the development of capitalism in Russia and began writing it.
D3.zh: 25到26岁间，他计划写一部关于俄国资本主义发展的大部头著作并开始动笔。

D6.en: At 17, in Kazan, he established contact with revolutionaries and joined a students' circle.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他在喀山与革命者建立联系并加入了一个学生小组。

D7.en: At 24, he said: 'I do not recognize the court! I do not recognize the Tsar! I do not recognize the laws!'
D7.zh: 24岁时，他说：“我不承认法庭！我不承认沙皇！我不承认法律！”

M4.en: At 24, he deliberately provoked the court, acting out his role as a dedicated revolutionary contemptuous of all laws.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他故意挑衅法庭，扮演一个蔑视一切法律的坚定革命者角色。

D16.en: At 17, he said: 'A wall, yes, but a rotten one. It only wants a push, and it will collapse.'
D16.zh: 17岁时，他说：“墙，是的，但是一堵烂墙。只需一推，它就会倒塌。”

D9.en: As a teenager, in Samara, he took an active part in revolutionary circles for the first time.
D9.zh: 十几岁时，他在萨马拉第一次积极参与革命小组活动。

D4.en: As a teenager, he would chin himself on the horizontal bars he set up near his lime-tree bower.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他会在椴树凉亭附近设置的单杠上做引体向上。

D12.en: His mother was sentenced to eternal hard labor.
D12.zh: 他母亲被判终身苦役。

D13b.en: At 22, he was hired as an assistant to barrister Khardin.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他被聘为律师哈尔金的助手。

D18.en: At 19-23, he sued peasant neighbors for cattle trespass.
D18.zh: 19到23岁间，他起诉农民邻居的牛闯入他的庄稼。

D8.en: At 23, he launched a withering attack on Narodnaya Volya.
D8.zh: 23岁时，他对民粹派发起了猛烈抨击。

M3.en: As a small child, he looked up to his elder brother as a hero.
M3.zh: 幼年时，他崇拜哥哥，视其为英雄。

D10.en: At 17, he wrote a letter with intense concentration, relishing it.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他全神贯注地写信，显然乐在其中。

D14.en: As a child, he was given roles to play and acted well.
D14.zh: 小时候，他常被分配角色，而且演得很好。

D19.en: He played the flute well and had some knowledge of music.
D19.zh: 他笛子吹得很好，也懂一些音乐。

M7.en: He described the key to the box containing forces that destroy the state.
M7.zh: 他描述了装有摧毁国家力量的盒子的钥匙。

D1.en: At 15, he threw himself more fiercely into his work to deaden his grief.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他更加拼命地投入工作以麻痹悲伤。

M1.en: At 23, he launched a withering attack on the Narodnaya Volya and its defenses.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他对民粹派及其辩护论点发起了猛烈抨击。

D15.en: As a child of 10-11, he could swim across to the other bank.
D15.zh: 10到11岁时，他能游到对岸。

D17.en: At ages 40-66, he looked over his peasants in their sicknesses.
D17.zh: 40到66岁间，他照料生病的农民。

M2.en: Before age 32, he rose from poverty by his own efforts and became an educator.
M2.zh: 32岁前，他靠自身努力摆脱贫困，一生从事教育。

M8.en: At ages 40-66, he believed in water's healing powers and wrote a book on it.
M8.zh: 40到66岁间，他相信水疗的功效，还写了一本书。

D13a.en: At 17, he gave up smoking and never resumed it.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他戒了烟，再没抽过。

D3.en: At 22, he came into contact with science and was attracted again.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他接触到科学，再次被吸引。

D13b.en: At 23, he became a graduate student under Igor Tamm at FIAN.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，他成为伊戈尔·塔姆在物理研究所的研究生。

D20.en: At 20-21, he founded a little group to study general relativity.
D20.zh: 20到21岁时，他成立了一个小组学习广义相对论。

D6.en: At 22, he said: 'I am marrying for love.'
D6.zh: 22岁时，他说：“我是为爱结婚。”

M1.en: At 20-21, he founded a little group to study general relativity.
M1.zh: 20到21岁时，他成立了一个小组学习广义相对论。

M6.en: At 23, he asked to take graduate exams, calling theoretical physics his calling.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他请求参加研究生考试，称理论物理是他的天职。

D4.en: At 12-14, he built a crystal radio based on his father's design.
D4.zh: 12到14岁时，他根据父亲的图纸制作了一台矿石收音机。

D10.en: At 22, he saw Bolshevism as demagogic anarchism and unruliness.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他亲眼看到布尔什维克主义只是煽动性的无政府主义和混乱。

D7.en: At 22, he read mysterious books instead of Lenin or Stalin.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他读了一些完全神秘的书，而不是列宁或斯大林的作品。

M4.en: At 20, he joined the Menshevik-Internationalist Party, spoke at rallies, and wrote pamphlets.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他加入了孟什维克国际主义党，在集会上演讲并撰写关于工人兄弟情谊的小册子。

D9.en: At 21, he was assigned to work in a munitions factory in Ulyanovsk.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他被分配到乌里扬诺夫斯克的军工厂工作。

D13a.en: At 22-23, he accepted an invitation to leave Ulyanovsk, urged by Klava and her father.
D13a.zh: 22到23岁间，在克拉娃和她父亲的坚持下，他接受了离开乌里扬诺夫斯克的邀请。

D2.en: At 21, he accepted the exhausting assignment with cheerful fatalism.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他带着乐观的宿命论接受了这份极其累人的工作。

D5.en: At 21, he accepted the exhausting assignment with cheerful fatalism.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他带着乐观的宿命论接受了这份极其累人的工作。

D18.en: At 23, he attached a patent and three manuscripts to an admissions request.
D18.zh: 23岁时，他在入学申请中附上了一项发明专利和三篇手稿。

D15.en: At 20-21, he came down with severe, life-threatening dysentery.
D15.zh: 20到21岁间，他患上了严重的痢疾，在当时条件下危及生命。

D8.en: He was difficult to intimidate, unbullyable, and proud of it, with a streak of the devil.
D8.zh: 他很难被吓倒，不受欺负，并为此自豪，还有点调皮。

M3.en: He admired Pushkin so much that he adopted one of Pushkin's mannerisms.
M3.zh: 他非常崇拜普希金，甚至模仿了普希金的一个习惯。

M7.en: At 22, his work was accepted for use in the war effort.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他的工作被接受用于战争努力。

D19.en: He began numbering all his letters and postcards to Klava with pedantic regularity and convinced her to do the same.
D19.zh: 他开始以刻板的规律给克拉娃的所有信件和明信片编号，并说服她也这样做。

D12.en: At 13, he wrote that his holidays were much chopped about but he did not wish to complain.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他写道假期被打乱了很多，但不想抱怨。

D6.en: At 22, he was invited by Duchess Lily to a weekend party for the prince.
D6.zh: 22岁时，莉莉公爵夫人邀请他参加为王子举办的周末派对。

D17.en: At 21, he visited her several times during her final illness.
D17.zh: 21岁时，他在她临终期间多次探望她。

D2.en: At 7-13, he had a capacity for memorizing huge amounts of prose and verse.
D2.zh: 7到13岁时，他有能力记住大量散文和诗歌。

D5.en: At 23, he fired a few shots at them from the saddle.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他从马鞍上向他们开了几枪。

D15.en: At 23, he fired a few shots at them from the saddle.
D15.zh: 23岁时，他从马鞍上向他们开了几枪。

D3.en: At 22-23, he asked his mother to send him copies of The Annual Register.
D3.zh: 22到23岁时，他让母亲寄给他《年度记录》的副本。

M6.en: At 22-23, he wrote he would devote his life to preserving the Empire.
M6.zh: 22到23岁时，他写道将毕生致力于维护帝国。

D9.en: At 24, he was proclaimed as a Conservative candidate.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他被宣布为保守党候选人。

D1.en: At 24, he won 11,477 votes but lost narrowly to two Radical Liberals.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他赢得11477票，但以微弱差距输给两位激进自由党人。

D16.en: At 23-24, he ended a letter to his mother with 'I end ere I weary you … Adieu'.
D16.zh: 23到24岁间，他在给母亲的信结尾写道：“我结束以免你厌倦……再见。”

M1.en: At 13-16, he imagined his father's pride if he were elected to Parliament.
M1.zh: 13到16岁时，他想象如果自己当选议员并发表精彩演说，父亲会多么自豪。

M4.en: At 22, he declared Tory Democracy as the standard under which he would range himself.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他宣称托利民主是他将加入的旗帜。

D11.en: At 25, his work of 250,000 words in two volumes was published in November 1899.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他25万字的作品分两卷于1899年11月出版。

D14.en: At 17, he traveled to Dieppe, had coffee, waited four hours, and declared his cigarettes at the station.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他去了迪耶普，喝了咖啡，等了四小时，在车站申报了香烟。

D10.en: At 17, he wrote to his mother: 'I am so wretched. Even now I weep. Please my darling Mummy be kind to your loving son'.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他写信给母亲：“我太痛苦了。现在还在哭。请我亲爱的妈妈对爱你的儿子好一点。”

D7.en: At 17, he reminded his mother of her promise of an extra week at home if he gave up Christmas, and asked her to keep it.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他提醒母亲她曾承诺如果他放弃圣诞节就多给他一周在家，并请求她兑现。

D18.en: At 24.5, he made a journalistic arrangement with the Morning Post to be paid £2.
D18.zh: 24岁半时，他与《晨邮报》达成新闻协议，报酬为2英镑。

D4.en: At 17, he wrote that he bought French and Russian infantrymen, artillerymen, and black cannon toy soldiers for 7 francs.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他写信说买了法国和俄罗斯步兵、炮兵和黑色大炮玩具兵，花了7法郎。

D19.en: At 25, he kept meticulous accounts of his net profits down to shillings and pence.
D19.zh: 25岁时，他详细记录了自己的净利润，精确到先令和便士。

D8.en: At 21, he dominated the conversation at dinner, overriding his host.
D8.zh: 21岁时，他在餐桌上主导谈话，压过了主人。

D13b.en: At 19-20, he went to Sandhurst due to poor academic record.
D13b.zh: 19到20岁时，他因学业不佳进入桑赫斯特皇家军事学院。

M2.en: At 24, he said he was trading on his father's name in his election speech.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他在竞选演讲中说自己在利用父亲的名声。

M3.en: At 20-21, he found a father-figure and political role-model in Cockran.
M3.zh: 20到21岁时，他把科克伦视为父亲般的人物和政治榜样。

D12.en: He grew up in a kommunalka, a communal flat, sharing one room with his family.
D12.zh: 他在公社公寓里长大，全家住一间房，共用厨房和厕所。

D6.en: He went to a Georgian restaurant with a friend, eating chicken in walnut sauce and drinking sweet liqueur.
D6.zh: 他和朋友去了一家格鲁吉亚餐厅，吃了核桃酱鸡肉，还喝了甜利口酒。

D15.en: He became a master in the sport in 1973 and competed in championships.
D15.zh: 他在1973年成为运动健将，并参加了多项比赛。

D2.en: He wrote a thesis on most-favored-nation status in international trade.
D2.zh: 他写了一篇关于国际贸易中最惠国待遇的论文。

D13b.en: He began attending School No. 193 in Leningrad in September 1960.
D13b.zh: 他于1960年9月开始在列宁格勒第193学校上学。

D3.en: He pursued humanities, literature, history, and continued German lessons.
D3.zh: 他学习了人文学科、文学、历史，并继续上德语课。

D5.en: He was recruited by the KGB in 1974 during his fourth year.
D5.zh: 他在1974年大四时被克格勃招募。

D1.en: He was intrigued by the discipline and rigor of sambo and judo.
D1.zh: 他对桑搏和柔道的纪律和严谨产生了兴趣。

D4.en: He and his friends would chase with sticks.
D4.zh: 他和朋友们会拿着棍子追逐。

D7.en: He walked into the local KGB headquarters and volunteered his service.
D7.zh: 他走进当地克格勃总部，自愿提供服务。

M3.en: At 16, he hero-worshipped fictional spies, imagining himself as Major Belov cum Johann Weiss.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他崇拜虚构间谍，想象自己是马约尔·别洛夫兼约翰·韦斯。

M4.en: At 16, he walked into the local KGB office and volunteered his service.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他走进当地克格勃办公室，自愿提供服务。

D13a.en: At 16, he decided to study law at university against his parents' wishes.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他违背父母意愿，决定上大学学习法律。

D10.en: At 16, he furiously exclaimed, 'I'm not going to be a cop!'
D10.zh: 16岁时，他愤怒地喊道：“我才不当警察！”

D9.en: At 21, he interned with the criminal division of the Transportation Ministry, investigating an airplane crash.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他在交通部刑事部门实习，参与调查一起飞机失事。

M6.en: At 23, he fulfilled his dream of joining the KGB in the summer of 1975.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他于1975年夏天实现了加入克格勃的梦想。

D12.en: He found a bed in a doss-house and moved to a hostel for men.
D12.zh: 他在廉价客栈找到床位，年底搬进了男子宿舍。

D2.en: At 12-13, he improved after a scolding at home.
D2.zh: 12到13岁时，他在家里挨骂后成绩有所提高。

D7.en: As a teenager, he ridiculed the state his father had served.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他嘲笑父亲效忠的国家。

D10.en: At 5, he showed tantrums when he didn't get his way.
D10.zh: 5岁时，他不如意就发脾气。

D13b.en: At 15-16, he transferred to a school in Steyr and finished at 16.
D13b.zh: 15到16岁时，他转学到施泰尔，16岁毕业。

D3.en: At 20-24, he spent much time in the public library, reading indiscriminately.
D3.zh: 20到24岁时，他常去公共图书馆，读书杂乱无章。

D5.en: At 21, he sued Hanisch, who spent a week in prison.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他起诉哈尼施，后者坐了一周牢。

D11.en: At 19-24, he fantasized ambitious pipe-dreams that quickly dissolved.
D11.zh: 19到24岁时，他幻想宏大计划，但很快破灭。

D6.en: At 23-24, he was a central figure among the 'intelligentsia' in the writing-room.
D6.zh: 23到24岁时，他是写作室“知识分子”圈的核心人物。

D14.en: At 19, he regaled Kubizek for hours on architectural history.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他给库比策克大谈建筑史，一讲就是几小时。

D1.en: He reapplied the following year for admission to the painting school.
D1.zh: 他第二年重新申请了绘画学校的入学资格。

D13a.en: He had a conflict with his father over a civil service career.
D13a.zh: 他与父亲在公务员职业上发生了冲突。

D18.en: He preferred to sell his pictures to Jewish dealers.
D18.zh: 他更喜欢把自己的画卖给犹太商人。

D4.en: He bought cards, ink, and paints, and produced little copies of views of Vienna.
D4.zh: 他买了卡片、墨水和颜料，制作了维也纳风景的小复制品。

D16.en: His violent attacks on the Social Democrats caused trouble with some inmates.
D16.zh: 他对社会民主党的猛烈攻击引起了一些室友的麻烦。

M4.en: He was prepared to learn from his second Austrian political hero, Karl Lueger.
M4.zh: 他准备向他的第二位奥地利政治英雄卡尔·卢格学习。

D15.en: He worked as a dispatch runner in shifts of three days at Fromelles followed by three rest days at Fournes.
D15.zh: 他作为传令兵轮班工作，在弗罗梅勒工作三天，然后在富尔内休息三天。

M1.en: He left to take the admission examination for the Academy of Fine Arts in Vienna.
M1.zh: 他离开去参加维也纳美术学院的入学考试。

D8.en: He was domineering with his classmates and a leading figure in immature pranks.
D8.zh: 他对同学很霸道，是幼稚恶作剧中的领头人物。

M2.en: He rejected everything his father had stood for, and with that, his father himself.
M2.zh: 他拒绝了他父亲所代表的一切，也因此拒绝了他父亲本人。

M3.en: He was prepared to learn from his second Austrian political hero, Karl Lueger.
M3.zh: 他准备向他的第二位奥地利政治英雄卡尔·卢格学习。

M6.en: He wanted to live like a Wagnerian hero and become a new Wagner.
M6.zh: 他想像瓦格纳英雄一样生活，并成为新的瓦格纳。

D9.en: He left the co-residents and set off for Munich.
D9.zh: 他离开了同住的伙伴，动身前往慕尼黑。

D19.en: He was anxious to keep his clothes and underclothes spotlessly clean.
D19.zh: 他急于保持衣服和内衣一尘不染。

D12.en: He had to leave childhood behind when barely into his teens to survive the war.
D12.zh: 他为了在战争中生存，刚十几岁就不得不告别童年。

D6.en: He chatted with a lot of young specialists.
D6.zh: 他和很多年轻专家聊天。

D9.en: At 19, he applied to Moscow State University.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他申请了莫斯科国立大学。

D7.en: He challenged a local party official: 'What’s with you? Do you distrust my wife ideologically?'
D7.zh: 他质问当地党官员：“你怎么了？你是不是在意识形态上不信任我妻子？”

D8.en: He defended his Jewish friend in a university meeting and the persecution ended.
D8.zh: 他在大学会议上为犹太朋友辩护，迫害停止了。

D1.en: He won the Red Labor Banner for helping his combine-driver father break harvesting records.
D1.zh: 他因帮助开联合收割机的父亲打破收割纪录而获得劳动红旗勋章。

D5.en: The conductor might have thrown him off the train if other passengers hadn't come to his aid.
D5.zh: 要不是其他乘客帮忙，列车员可能已经把他赶下火车了。

D13a.en: He was invited to research collective farm law but turned it down, having divined it was a non-subject.
D13a.zh: 他被邀请研究集体农庄法，但拒绝了，因为他已预感到这是个伪课题。

D20.en: He became komsorg for the entire Law School in 1952.
D20.zh: 他于1952年成为整个法学院的团组织负责人。

D20X.en: He was invited to research collective farm law but turned it down.
D20X.zh: 他被邀请研究集体农庄法，但拒绝了。

M4.en: He was already a candidate member of the Communist Party.
M4.zh: 他已经是共产党的预备党员。

M2.en: He wondered how to explain that both his grandfathers were imprisoned as enemies of the people.
M2.zh: 他不知如何解释他的两个祖父都被关押过，被定为人民公敌。

M7.en: He served on the commission that assigned graduates to their first jobs.
M7.zh: 他参加了分配毕业生工作的委员会。

D3.en: He chatted with many young specialists who were all very dissatisfied.
D3.zh: 他和很多年轻专家聊天，他们都很不满。

D4.en: He spent five summers helping his father operate a combine harvester.
D4.zh: 他连续五个夏天帮父亲操作联合收割机。

D16.en: He defended his Jewish friend at a university meeting.
D16.zh: 他在大学会议上为他的犹太朋友辩护。

D18.en: He and his family sold grain and earned over 1,000 rubles including salaries.
D18.zh: 他和家人卖掉了挣来的粮食，加上工资，收入超过1000卢布。

D19.en: He graduated with distinction in 1955.
D19.zh: 他于1955年以优异成绩毕业。

D10.en: He confessed he was 'depressed' and found his surroundings 'disgusting'.
D10.zh: 他承认自己感到‘沮丧’，并觉得周围环境‘令人厌恶’。

D11.en: He became an enthusiastic amateur actor.
D11.zh: 他成为一名热情的业余演员。

D2.en: He said, 'I am normally at my desk till late.'
D2.zh: 他说：‘我通常伏案工作到很晚。’

D13b.en: At 21, he was elevated to full party membership.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他成为正式党员。

M1.en: At 19, he applied to Moscow State University, the most important university of all.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他申请了莫斯科国立大学，这所最重要的大学。

M5.en: He proudly said he received a silver medal while Raisa got gold.
M5.zh: 他自豪地说他获得了银牌，而赖莎获得了金牌。

M8.en: He saw the university as a temple of learning that opened undreamed vistas.
M8.zh: 他认为大学是学习的殿堂，打开了做梦都想不到的视野。

D11.en: At 17-18, he played John Travolta in a Saturday Night Fever skit.
D11.zh: 17到18岁时，他扮演了《周末夜狂热》中的约翰·特拉沃尔塔。

D5.en: At 25, a police officer struck him on the head with a club.
D5.zh: 25岁时，一名警察用警棍击中了他的头部。

M4.en: At 15, he began organizing against the Vietnam War and for racial equality.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他开始组织反越战和争取种族平等的活动。

D1.en: At 24-25, he felt discouraged by slow progress but planned to persist for another year.
D1.zh: 24到25岁时，他对进展缓慢感到沮丧，但打算再坚持一年。

D9.en: At 20, he transferred to Columbia University.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他转学到了哥伦比亚大学。

D10.en: At 24, he admitted staying embittered and sullen towards someone for a week and a half.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他承认对某人保持了一周半的怨恨和闷闷不乐。

D6.en: At 14-15, he started pot-smoking trends as a member of the Choom Gang.
D6.zh: 14到15岁时，他作为楚姆帮成员开创了一些吸大麻的风气。

D3.en: He wrote an article titled 'Breaking the War Mentality' at age 21-22.
D3.zh: 他21到22岁时写了一篇题为《打破战争心态》的文章。

D18.en: He interviewed for a job with Business International Corporation in late summer of 1983.
D18.zh: 1983年夏末，他参加了商业国际公司的面试。

D16.en: His reply wove its way through literature, politics, and personal philosophy.
D16.zh: 他的回答贯穿了文学、政治和个人哲学。

D14.en: He took on the role of John Travolta in Saturday Night Fever as a senior.
D14.zh: 高中最后一年，他扮演了《周末夜狂热》中的约翰·特拉沃尔塔。

D4.en: At 17, he applied for a job at Baskin-Robbins with a friend.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他和朋友一起去Baskin-Robbins应聘工作。

D19.en: He recorded in his journal a sense of exhilaration and fear at age 24.
D19.zh: 他24岁时在日记中记录了兴奋和恐惧的感觉。

M6.en: At 23, he felt a desire for greater purpose.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他感到渴望更大的目标。

D20.en: He co-wrote a satirical letter to the college newspaper inviting submissions.
D20.zh: 他和麦克尼尔合写了一封讽刺信给校报，邀请投稿。

M3.en: He became a loyal follower of Martin Luther King Jr.
M3.zh: 他成了马丁·路德·金的忠实追随者。

D20X.en: He was talked out of something by his grandmother, a rare occurrence.
D20X.zh: 他被外婆说服留在家中，这是少有的情况。

M5.en: He dismissed the entire event, saying it made no difference.
M5.zh: 他否定了整个事件，说那毫无影响。

M8.en: He said Chicago offered 'the promise of redemption.'
M8.zh: 他说芝加哥提供了‘救赎的希望’。

D8.en: He dominated the game and led his roommates to victory.
D8.zh: 他主导了比赛，带领室友们获胜。

M2.en: He was expected to do good.
M2.zh: 他被期望做好事。

D13b.en: She began her scientific career after graduating in 1978.
D13b.zh: 她1978年毕业后开始了科学生涯。

D12.en: As a small child, she was late learning to walk and prone to falls.
D12.zh: 幼年时，她学走路晚，容易摔倒。

D6.en: At age 10-14, she joined the Young Pioneers.
D6.zh: 10到14岁时，她加入了少先队。

D7.en: At 14-15, she chose Lutheran confirmation over a state ceremony.
D7.zh: 14到15岁时，她选择了路德宗坚信礼而非国家仪式。

D3.en: As a teenager, she practiced English by reading a UK communist newspaper.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，她通过阅读英国共产党报纸练习英语。

D5.en: At 17-18, she risked losing her university place.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，她冒着失去大学名额的风险。

D2.en: At 15, she was best in Russian, the most time-consuming subject.
D2.zh: 15岁时，她俄语最好，这门课花时间最多。

D15.en: At age 7, she aspired to become a figure skater.
D15.zh: 7岁时，她梦想成为花样滑冰运动员。

D10.en: At 19-24, she seemed to have a strong inner life.
D10.zh: 19到24岁时，她似乎有丰富的内心世界。

M3.en: At 10-14, she read biographies and found her role model: Marie Curie.
M3.zh: 10到14岁时，她读传记，找到了榜样：玛丽·居里。

D13a.en: At 14-17, she joined the FDJ, excelled in Russian, and was a model Socialist pupil.
D13a.zh: 14到17岁时，她加入自由德国青年团，俄语成绩优异，是模范社会主义学生。

D18.en: At about 12-14, she was the first in her school to wear blue jeans, received from Hamburg relatives.
D18.zh: 大约12到14岁时，她是学校里第一个穿蓝色牛仔裤的人，牛仔裤是汉堡亲戚寄来的。

M1.en: At 7, she was the best in her class but did not win Pupil of the Year.
M1.zh: 7岁时，她是班上最好的学生，但没有获得年度学生奖。

D14.en: At 14, she began telling the story about the stamp-collecting boy in Czechoslovakia who tore up stamps.
D14.zh: 14岁时，她开始讲一个捷克斯洛伐克集邮男孩撕毁邮票的故事。

D4.en: At 19-20, when using lab equipment, the men went for burners while she took time to think first.
D4.zh: 19到20岁时，使用实验室设备时，男生们直接去拿燃烧器，而她先花时间思考实验。

D9.en: At 17-18, she had Peter Bliss as a classmate and FDJ chairman.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，她的同学彼得·布利斯是学校自由德国青年团主席。

D17.en: At about 10-13, she was always willing to help less gifted students.
D17.zh: 大约10到13岁时，她总是愿意帮助天赋不如她的同学。

D20.en: She and her 70 freshmen (63 men) were relatively sheltered from ideological influence.
D20.zh: 她和70名新生（63名男性）相对免受意识形态影响。

D4.en: As a child, he helped with hay-making and harvest and found an eagle-owl nest in the church loft.
D4.zh: 他小时候帮忙晒干草和收割，在教堂阁楼发现了一个雕鸮巢。

D12.en: At 18, he decided to become a career soldier after completing military service.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他决定在完成兵役后成为职业军人。

D1.en: At 6, his father set him tasks so difficult that even adults couldn't solve them.
D1.zh: 6岁时，他父亲给他布置了连大人都解不出的难题。

D15.en: Between ages 6 and 12, he spent six weeks in a plaster cast and later wore steel braces up to his hips.
D15.zh: 6到12岁间，他打了六周石膏，后来还戴了到臀部的钢支架。

M1.en: At 18, he had to answer one of three questions for his Abitur exam.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他必须从三个问题中选一个回答才能通过高中毕业考。

D6.en: At 18-19, he joined a Catholic student corporation called Brisgovia.
D6.zh: 18到19岁时，他加入了一个叫布里斯戈维亚的天主教学生社团。

D9.en: At 25, he joined a Cologne tennis club known as a kind of brides' fair.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他加入了一个科隆网球俱乐部，那里被称为一种新娘市场。

D7.en: At 25, he was referred to the Home Reserve and did not serve.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他被编入本土后备军，但未服役。

D13a.en: At 23, he announced to his parents his desire to convert to Catholicism.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他向父母宣布自己想改信天主教。

D18.en: At 18-19, when money ran out, he and a friend went hungry.
D18.zh: 18到19岁时，钱花光了，他和一个朋友挨饿。

D3.en: As a child and young man, he kept books dotted with notes and underlining, always with him into old age.
D3.zh: 幼年到青年时，他保留着满是笔记和下划线的书，直到老年都随身携带。

D13b.en: At 18, he enlisted as a three-year volunteer in the 4th Company of the Second Rhenish Infantry Regiment 28.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他作为三年志愿兵加入第28莱茵步兵第2团第4连。

D19.en: As a young man, he kept detailed accounts of all his expenditures.
D19.zh: 年轻时，他详细记录所有开支。

M8.en: As a child, he attended family prayers and Sunday mass at St. Apostle church.
M8.zh: 幼年时，他参加早晚家庭祈祷和周日在圣使徒教堂的弥撒。

D11.en: At 23-24, he wrote a volume of poems.
D11.zh: 23到24岁时，他写了一卷诗。

D5.en: At 25, he was referred to the Home Reserve and did not serve.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他被编入本土后备军，但未服役。

D12.en: At 17, he might not have left India if his father had not died in 1886.
D12.zh: 17岁时，如果父亲在1886年没有去世，他可能不会离开印度。

D2.en: At 19, he felt joy and thankfulness for not needing meat or liquor despite the cold.
D2.zh: 19岁时，尽管天冷，他不需要肉或酒，心中充满喜悦和感激。

D3.en: At about 22, he read 'The Truth about Women' and found it interesting though he disagreed with the conclusion.
D3.zh: 大约22岁时，他读了《女性的真相》，觉得有趣但不同意作者结论。

D6.en: At 25, he visited the Askews and was told not to chat about vegetarianism or Buddhism.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他去阿斯科家，被要求别聊素食或佛教。

D7.en: At 25, he said he would stop visiting them altogether.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他说最好不再去拜访他们。

D5.en: He chose imprisonment over paying a fine.
D5.zh: 他选择坐牢而不是交罚款。

D13b.en: At 13, he married when he was a high school sophomore.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他上高中二年级时结婚了。

D8.en: At 13, he lost no time in assuming the authority of a husband.
D8.zh: 13岁时，他立刻摆出丈夫的权威。

M4.en: At 24, zeal for a cause dissolved his timidity and loosened his tongue.
M4.zh: 24岁时，对事业的热情消除了他的胆怯，让他畅所欲言。

D14.en: At 24-25, he wrote to The Natal Mercury on behalf of the Esoteric Christian Union.
D14.zh: 24到25岁时，他代表神秘基督徒联盟给《纳塔尔信使报》写信。

D1.en: At 20-21, he failed the Latin exam the first time but passed the second time.
D1.zh: 20到21岁时，他第一次拉丁语考试没通过，但第二次通过了。

D9.en: At 25, his farewell party became a working committee to plan resistance to a bill.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他的告别会变成了一个工作委员会，计划抵制一项法案。

D16.en: At 24, he delivered his first public speech, on white discrimination.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他发表了第一次公开演讲，关于白人歧视。

D4.en: At 20, he came across a book on vegetarianism and read it cover to cover.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他偶然看到一本关于素食的书，从头到尾读完了。

M1.en: At 18, he did best in English and Gujarati, averaging about 45% in each.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他英语和古吉拉特语成绩最好，每科平均约45分。

D15.en: He took long walks through the city and calculated his distance.
D15.zh: 他长途步行穿过城市，并计算了距离。

D11.en: At 21-24, he wrote his first substantial piece, unpublished, covering 55 pages.
D11.zh: 21到24岁时，他写了第一篇重要文章，未发表，共55页。

D20.en: At 24, he became the leader of the Natal Indians.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他成为了纳塔尔印度人的领袖。

D10.en: At 25, he said he could not make her believe he was sincere and had no wish to force himself on her as a companion.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他说他无法让她相信自己是真诚的，也不想强迫自己成为她的伴侣。

D18.en: At 23-37, he sent his brothers more than Rs 60,000 from South Africa.
D18.zh: 23到37岁间，他从南非给兄弟们寄了超过6万卢比。

D12.en: He was born in his grandparents' house in Lille on 22 November 1890.
D12.zh: 他1890年11月22日出生在里尔的外祖父母家。

D13b.en: He entered the Ecole Spéciale Militaire, his first ambition.
D13b.zh: 他进入了梦寐以求的圣西尔军事专科学校。

D2.en: Confined to barracks, he decided to study German and read Greek and Roman history.
D2.zh: 不准外出时，他决定学德语并读希腊罗马史。

D3.en: He read a great deal of history, natural history, and German.
D3.zh: 他大量阅读历史、自然史和德语。

D1.en: He was escorted out of captivity by Ducret disguised as a German nurse.
D1.zh: 杜克雷扮成德国男护士，庄严地护送他逃出俘虏营。

M1.en: He could hope to quickly earn his captain's stripes.
M1.zh: 他有望很快获得上尉军衔。

D15.en: He distinguished himself as an aggressive full-back for Antoing's first team.
D15.zh: 他在安托万一线队踢后卫，表现凶猛。

D9.en: He was posted to the 1st Battalion and put in charge of the 1st platoon.
D9.zh: 他被派到第一步兵营，负责指挥第一排。

D10.en: He grew angry at politicians and wrote that Parliament was hateful and stupid.
D10.zh: 他对政客越来越愤怒，写道议会可恨又愚蠢。

D11.en: At 23, he wrote an unpublished autobiographical story called Le Baptême.
D11.zh: 23岁时，他写了一篇未发表的自传体故事《洗礼》。

D4.en: At 25, he was wounded in the thigh by a bayonet thrust that pierced his map case.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他被刺刀刺穿地图盒并伤及大腿。

D7.en: At 23, he was told not to repeat an action that could cause conflagrations.
D7.zh: 23岁时，他被警告不要再做可能导致战火蔓延的事。

D5.en: At 25, he fell into enemy hands near Douaumont, wounded by a bayonet in the thigh but recovered.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他在杜奥蒙附近被俘，大腿被刺刀刺伤但已痊愈。

D6.en: At 25, he found camaraderie among officers of all nationalities and planned escapes.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他感受到各国军官间的战友情，并策划越狱。

M4.en: At 24, he angrily wrote that Parliament was becoming hateful and stupid.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他愤怒地写道议会变得可憎又愚蠢。

M7.en: At 25, he wrote that only France should dictate the terms of absolute victory and peace.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他写道只有法国才能决定绝对胜利与和平的条件。

M2.en: At 18, he told his mother she was first to add Saint-Cyrien to his name, owing his exam success to her.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他告诉母亲她是第一个在他名字前加上圣西尔军校生的人，并将考试成功归功于她。

D17.en: At 16, he went to Lourdes as a volunteer stretcher-bearer.
D17.zh: 16岁时，他去卢尔德当志愿担架员。

D16.en: He gave a speech quoting Paul Déroulède to his men in 1913.
D16.zh: 1913年，他向部下发表演讲，引用了保罗·德鲁莱德的话。

M6.en: At 20-24, he established a personal code of ethics based on France's mission and his destined role.
M6.zh: 20到24岁间，他建立了一套个人道德准则，基于法国的使命和他注定要扮演的角色。

D8.en: He asked his men, 'Were you frightened, gentlemen?' in 1915.
D8.zh: 1915年，他问部下：“先生们，你们害怕了吗？”

D12.en: At 17, he offered to help his mother en route or at the sanitorium.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他提出在路上或疗养院帮妈妈忙。

D6.en: At 15, he joined the Chess Club.
D6.zh: 15岁时，他加入了国际象棋俱乐部。

D3.en: At 16, he came across Meredith Townsend's Asia and Europe and was receptive.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他读到梅雷迪思·汤森德的《亚洲与欧洲》，思想很开放。

D14.en: At 15, he nearly got a singing part but didn't.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他差点被分到唱歌任务，但没分到。

D7.en: At 22, he said he didn't want to stay in England under those conditions.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他说不想在这种条件下在英国多待一天。

D16.en: At 22-23, he lost assurance when speaking in open court.
D16.zh: 22到23岁间，他在公开法庭发言时失去自信。

D15.en: At 15, he had to turn on footpans for sixth formers after football.
D15.zh: 15岁时，他得在足球赛后给高年级生开洗脚盆。

D13a.en: At 19-21, he gave up a degree exam after sitting the first paper.
D13a.zh: 19到21岁间，他考完第一场就放弃了学位考试。

D13b.en: At 18, he had to go to London to apply for admission to the Inner Temple.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他得去伦敦申请进入内殿律师学院。

D18.en: He was often in debt and had to pawn his watch and chain.
D18.zh: 他经常负债，不得不典当自己的表和表链。

D9.en: He rejected a Rs 500/month offer to write a column on international affairs.
D9.zh: 他拒绝了每月500卢比撰写国际事务专栏的邀请。

D8.en: He apologized to his father within a week after an argument.
D8.zh: 他在一周内向父亲道歉。

D4.en: At 17, Nehru said he would have dearly liked to have been there.
D4.zh: 17岁时，尼赫鲁说他非常想去那里。

D11.en: As a small child, he was dressed like an Indian prince for photos, then given a Lord Fauntleroy outfit.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他拍照被打扮成印度王子，后来穿上劳德·方特勒罗伊式服装。

M1.en: He was top of his form and expected a prize.
M1.zh: 他是班上第一名，听说会得到奖品。

D10.en: He was withdrawing into himself and limiting his incursions into the outer world.
D10.zh: 他越来越封闭自己，减少对外界的接触。

M4.en: He would not write for pro-British papers.
M4.zh: 他不会为亲英报纸撰稿。

D2.en: He had work to do for the next morning.
D2.zh: 他第二天早上还有工作要做。

M2.en: His father promised him a 'real gem' of a girl from a large field of selection.
M2.zh: 他父亲答应从众多人选里给他找一个‘真正的宝贝’女孩。

M7.en: At 23, he wanted Indian liberties and hated British rule passionately.
M7.zh: 23岁时，他想要印度人享有自由，对英国统治恨之入骨。

D20X.en: At 22, he pleaded to delay a decision until he could see a girl.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他恳求推迟决定，直到他能见那个女孩。

D19.en: At 22, he bought a 20-volume Thackeray and a 40-volume Dickens.
D19.zh: 22岁时，他买了20卷的萨克雷和40卷的狄更斯。

D17.en: At 22, he asked his mother if she wanted him to marry someone he might not like.
D17.zh: 22岁时，他问母亲是否想让他娶一个可能不喜欢的女孩。

M5.en: At 22, his father's disappointment meant the Nehru name would be honored by a nephew.
M5.zh: 22岁时，父亲的失望意味着尼赫鲁的名声将由侄子来维护。

M6.en: At 23, he wanted Indian liberties and hated British rule passionately.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他想要印度人享有自由，对英国统治恨之入骨。

M8.en: At 23, he admired British commitment to freedom and patriotism.
M8.zh: 23岁时，他钦佩英国人对自由和爱国主义的承诺。

D12.en: He grew up in an orphanage with little formal education.
D12.zh: 他在孤儿院长大，没受过多少正规教育。

D3.en: At Healdtown, he first heard about the African National Congress.
D3.zh: 在希尔德镇，他第一次听说非洲人国民大会。

D6.en: He thinks about 100 people attended the Youth League's formation.
D6.zh: 他估计约有100人参加了青年联盟的成立大会。

D13b.en: At 24, Mandela noted Ruth left teaching due to Bantu education.
D13b.zh: 24岁时，曼德拉提到鲁思因班图教育离职。

D7.en: At 15, he rushed at a girl who made a remark, wanting to choke her.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他冲向一个说了话的女孩，想掐死她。

D13a.en: At 24, he returned to Johannesburg determined to follow his own calling.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他回到约翰内斯堡，决心走自己的路。

D1.en: At 23, he rescued his friend by confessing the gun was his.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他承认枪是自己的，救了朋友。

D2.en: At 23-25, he studied by candlelight at night to complete his BA by correspondence.
D2.zh: 23到25岁间，他晚上点蜡烛学习，通过函授完成学士学位。

D4.en: As a small child, he helped fix a white man's motorbike and got paid.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他帮一个白人修摩托车，得到了报酬。

D9.en: At 25, he marched with thousands in the Alexandra bus boycott.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他参加了亚历山德拉巴士抵制游行，与数千人同行。

D15.en: He took up school sports, especially boxing and long-distance running.
D15.zh: 他积极参加学校运动，尤其是拳击和长跑。

M1.en: He won a Healdtown prize in 1938 for the best Xhosa essay.
M1.zh: 1938年他赢得了希尔德镇最佳科萨语作文奖。

D14.en: He practiced ballroom dancing, styling himself on Victor Sylvester.
D14.zh: 他练习交谊舞，模仿维克多·西尔维斯特的风格。

D20.en: He stood firm as a matter of principle, refusing to serve.
D20.zh: 他坚持原则，拒绝服役。

D5.en: At 16, he was tense, hoping not to cry out or flinch at the cuts.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他紧张地希望自己不会因割礼而叫喊或退缩。

M4.en: He identified the ANC as his natural political home.
M4.zh: 他认为非国大是他自然的政治归宿。

D10.en: He told Fatima Meer that Winnie's eyes brimmed with tears at their parting.
D10.zh: 他告诉法蒂玛·米尔，温妮在他们分别时泪眼汪汪。

M7.en: He marched with thousands of protesters in the Alexandra bus boycott.
M7.zh: 他参加了亚历山德拉巴士抵制运动，与数千人一起游行。

D17.en: He was left in charge of children, reading stories and playing games with them.
D17.zh: 他被留下来照看孩子，给他们读故事、玩游戏。

D16.en: He urged her to think beyond beauty and admirers and complete her education.
D16.zh: 他劝她不要只想着美貌和追求者，要完成学业。

M6.en: They awakened in him a new sense of purpose, a calling he embraced.
M6.zh: 它们唤醒了他新的使命感，一种他接受并拥抱的召唤。

M3.en: He was thrilled when famous Xhosa poet Krune Mkwayi visited campus.
M3.zh: 著名科萨诗人克鲁内·姆夸伊访问校园时，他非常兴奋。

D20X.en: At 22, his wife complained that they didn't need to be taught how to speak, having suffered much.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他的妻子抱怨说他们不需要被教如何说话，已经受了很多苦。

D7.en: At 12, she wrote to the principal demanding promotion by her own power.
D7.zh: 12岁时，她写信给校长要求凭自己的能力升班。

D15.en: At 19-22, she found time for swimming, skiing, and mountain climbing.
D15.zh: 19到22岁时，她抽空游泳、滑雪和爬山。

D5.en: At 16, she was among those thrashed by police in a swoop.
D5.zh: 16岁时，她在警察突袭中挨了打。

D3.en: At 8, she spent hours collecting all the greens in nature.
D3.zh: 8岁时，她花几个小时收集自然界所有的绿色。

D16.en: At 17, she wrote cheerfully about Dr Atal's hapless German attempts.
D16.zh: 17岁时，她开心地写信描述阿塔尔博士学德语的窘态。

D13a.en: At 24, she made known her intentions despite solid family opposition.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，她不顾家人强烈反对宣布了自己的打算。

M4.en: At 23, she felt her place was with Feroze in bombed London.
M4.zh: 23岁时，她觉得该去被轰炸的伦敦陪费罗兹。

D9.en: At age 5, she organized a Bal Charkha Sangh where children learned to spin and weave.
D9.zh: 5岁时，她组织了一个儿童纺车协会，让孩子们学习纺纱织布。

D10.en: At 18, she woke up with a queer singing feeling in her heart, as she wrote to her father.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她醒来时心中有一种奇怪的歌唱感，她在给父亲的信中写道。

D14.en: At 16-17, her great passion was Indian classical dance of the Manipuri School.
D14.zh: 16到17岁时，她热衷于曼尼普尔学派的印度古典舞。

D13b.en: At 14, Gandhiji suggested she join the People's Own School in Poona.
D13b.zh: 14岁时，甘地建议她加入浦那的人民学校。

D17.en: At 23, she was visited by someone who traveled by boat and train to see her as often as he could.
D17.zh: 23岁时，有人乘船和火车尽可能频繁地来看望她。

D20.en: At 14-16, the school's mock parliament chose her as its prime minister.
D20.zh: 14到16岁时，学校的模拟议会选她担任总理。

D11.en: At about 6, she loved to fantasize, create plays, design costumes, and dress up.
D11.zh: 大约6岁时，她喜欢幻想、编戏、设计服装和打扮。

M6.en: She said he completely changed her life.
M6.zh: 她说他完全改变了她的生活。

D4.en: At 16-17, she joined Kala Bhavan and came under the influence of painter Nandlal Bose.
D4.zh: 16到17岁时，她加入卡拉巴万，受到著名画家南德拉尔·博斯的影响。

M7.en: At 22, she condemned British arms support for Finland compared to their refusal to help Republican Spain.
M7.zh: 22岁时，她谴责英国对芬兰的武器支持，而此前英国拒绝帮助西班牙共和派。

D1.en: At 12, she asked to take exams for two classes after being denied promotion.
D1.zh: 12岁时，她请求参加两个年级的考试，因为之前被拒绝升级。

D8.en: At 5, she snatched it away from me.
D8.zh: 5岁时，她从我手里抢走了它。

M2.en: At 19, she hoped to succeed at Oxford to gain her father's approval.
M2.zh: 19岁时，她希望在牛津取得成功以获得父亲的认可。

D19.en: In her mid-20s, she carried a notepad to meetings and showed precision and order.
D19.zh: 20多岁时，她开会时带笔记本记东西，做事精确有序。

D18.en: At 16, almost all of her jewelry was sold.
D18.zh: 16岁时，她几乎所有的珠宝都被卖掉了。

D2.en: In his second year at the War College, he began hard work and hard thinking.
D2.zh: 他在军事学院的第二年开始了刻苦学习和思考。

D13a.en: As a boy, he contrived to enter the Military School in Salonika.
D13a.zh: 他小时候设法进入了萨洛尼卡的军事学校。

D7.en: Refusing to accept loss of status, he left home and lodged with a relative.
D7.zh: 他不接受地位下降，离开家寄宿在亲戚家。

D13b.en: He was posted to the provinces for unit training after passing out.
D13b.zh: 他毕业后被派往各省进行部队训练。

D5.en: He made up his mind that he must get to Salonika.
D5.zh: 他下定决心必须去萨洛尼卡。

D3.en: He is said to have read the French newspapers Le Matin and Le Petit Parisien.
D3.zh: 据说他读了法国报纸《晨报》和《小巴黎人报》。

D16.en: In his orations, he displayed a forcible capacity for convincing his audience.
D16.zh: 在他的演讲中，他展现了说服听众的强力能力。

M1.en: He passed out of military high school ranked second in his class.
M1.zh: 他以班级第二名的成绩从军事高中毕业。

D14.en: He went home, changed into rough clothes, and returned to join them in Western pleasures.
D14.zh: 他回家换上粗布衣服，回去加入他们的西方娱乐活动。

D9.en: At 14, he threw himself into the swirling world of Monastir and thrived on it.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他投身于莫纳斯提尔的纷繁世界，并在此茁壮成长。

D11.en: At 21-24, he fantasized himself as the reigning hero and savior of the troubled country.
D11.zh: 21到24岁间，他幻想自己是主宰人物，是未来冒险的英雄和受困国家的救星。

M4.en: He was moved by the patriotism in another's work.
M4.zh: 他被他人作品中的爱国精神所打动。

D4.en: At 24, he served in the 30th cavalry regiment making sallies from Damascus to chase Druze tribesmen.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他在大马士革的第30骑兵团出击追击德鲁兹部落成员。

D10.en: At 24, he continued to be plagued by depressive moods and insomnia.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他仍被抑郁情绪和失眠困扰。

D6.en: At 24, he and his brother officer struck up a friendship with Mustafa Efendi.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他和战友与穆斯塔法·埃芬迪建立了友谊。

D20.en: At 25, he read out the three articles of the society he had jotted down on a card.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他宣读了写在卡片上的社团三条章程。

D15.en: As a teenager, he disliked running and jumping games, preferring to walk fast.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他不喜欢跑跳游戏，更喜欢快步走。

D8.en: From an early age, he dominated his immediate circle.
D8.zh: 他从小就在自己的小圈子里占主导地位。

D1.en: At 24, when refused, they went nevertheless and were eventually admitted after promising to keep quiet.
D1.zh: 24岁时，被拒绝后他们仍去了，最终在承诺保密后被接纳。

M2.en: He was exceedingly anxious to grow up to be like him.
M2.zh: 他极其渴望长大后像他一样。

D12.en: He went to Amherst College to sit the entrance exam in September.
D12.zh: 他九月份去阿默斯特学院参加入学考试。

D13b.en: At 15, he was registered in Black River Academy in Ludlow.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，他在拉德洛的黑河学院注册。

D9.en: He began to speak publicly, starting at boardinghouse meals.
D9.zh: 他开始公开演讲，从寄宿公寓的餐食开始。

M1.en: He was cowinner of the J. Wesley Ladd prize for best oration.
M1.zh: 他共同获得J.韦斯利·拉德最佳演讲奖。

D4.en: At 10, he stitched a quilt top of Tumbling Blocks pattern.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他缝制了翻滚方块图案的被子面。

D6.en: He joined a canoe club and went out on the lake on Sundays.
D6.zh: 他加入独木舟俱乐部，周日去湖上划船。

D16.en: At 21, he demanded that the owner produce every dog he owned in the dining room before he would eat.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他要求店主把他所有的狗都带到餐厅来才肯吃饭。

D18.en: He tried to get his father to invest in a railroad, even working out the math.
D18.zh: 他试图说服父亲投资一条铁路，甚至算好了账。

D19.en: He kept his own books rigidly and noticed when others were lax.
D19.zh: 他严格记账，并注意到别人松懈的时候。

D10.en: He said, 'It is lonesome here without Abbie.'
D10.zh: 他说：“没有阿比，这里很寂寞。”

M6.en: He gained an affection for Cicero's orations and saw he might use oratory in his own life.
M6.zh: 他喜欢上西塞罗的演说，并意识到自己可以在生活中运用演说术。

D15.en: He weighed 119.5 pounds and was 68.9 inches tall, below average weight but above average height.
D15.zh: 他体重119.5磅，身高68.9英寸，体重低于平均水平，身高略高于平均水平。

D13a.en: He did not join in the small-time looting; he deemed himself as much town as gown.
D13a.zh: 他没有参与小规模的抢劫；他认为自己既是市民也是学生。

D20.en: He joined the Republican Committee for Ward 2 in 1897.
D20.zh: 他于1897年加入了第二选区的共和党委员会。

M3.en: As a boy, he was drawn to Lincoln, who became a country lawyer like he would.
M3.zh: 他小时候就崇拜林肯，林肯后来成了乡村律师，就像他一样。

D2.en: At 18, he cycled 60 km from Lübeck to Hamburg to recruit speakers.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他骑车60公里从吕贝克到汉堡邀请著名演讲者。

D12.en: Brandt was raised solely by women until age five.
D12.zh: 布兰特幼年时完全由女性抚养长大。

D3.en: At 14, he wrote frequent contributions to a paper and decided to become a journalist.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他经常给报纸投稿，并决定成为一名记者。

D6.en: At 19-21, he joined the elitist left-wing Mot Dag group of writers and artists.
D6.zh: 19到21岁间，他加入了精英左翼团体“前进之路”，成员包括作家和艺术家。

D15.en: As a child, he was a good swimmer and won a 5000 m race as the only contestant.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他游泳很好，并在5000米比赛中因唯一参赛者而获胜。

D9.en: At 17, he and half of the Socialist Youth organization joined enthusiastically.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他和大约一半的社会主义青年组织成员热情地加入了。

D20.en: At 19, he became chairman of SAP's Oslo Office and head of the SJV's Central Office Abroad.
