D20.zh: 19岁时，他成为SAP奥斯陆办事处主席，并负责SJV海外中央办公室。

D14.en: At 16, he was a contributing editor of the local Social Democrat daily, the Volksbote.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他是当地社民党日报《人民信使》的撰稿编辑。

M3.en: At 16, he saw Leber as a father figure.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他把莱伯当作父亲般的人物。

D5.en: At 19, he attended a clandestine meeting of the illegal SAP in Dresden on March 11-12, 1933.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他于1933年3月11日至12日在德累斯顿参加了非法的SAP秘密会议。

D4.en: He collaborated on a Norwegian translation of Marx's Das Kapital at age 19-21.
D4.zh: 他19到21岁时参与将马克思的《资本论》翻译成挪威语。

M4.en: He went to Norway to work for the SAP, absorbed in political work.
M4.zh: 他去挪威为SAP工作，全身心投入政治活动。

D3.en: At 16, he began to fancy himself an intellectual.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他开始自认为是知识分子。

D11.en: At 17, he was already writing poems, one dedicated to her.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他已经在写诗，其中一首献给了她。

D2.en: At 17, he wrote a 28-page assessment of its performance.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他写了一篇28页的绩效评估。

D5.en: At 21, he deliberately gave up being a lab assistant and studying chemistry.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他故意放弃了实验室助理的工作和化学学习。

D14.en: At 20, his first public performance as a man of letters produced explosions.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他作为文人的首次公开表演引起了轰动。

D6.en: At 16, he quickly established himself as the group's unrivalled convenor.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他迅速成为团队无可匹敌的组织者。

D9.en: At 20, he began to hammer away at the presumption that art and truth are a happily married couple.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他开始抨击艺术与真理是幸福伴侣的假设。

D12.en: At age 6, his mother decided to move him to a country school.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他母亲决定送他去乡村学校。

D13b.en: As a small child, he began attending school in the first week of September 1942.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，他在1942年9月的第一周开始上学。

D13a.en: He was expelled from school after two and a half years.
D13a.zh: 他在那里只读了两年半就被学校开除了。

D20.en: He established himself as the group's unrivalled convenor.
D20.zh: 他确立了自己作为团体无可争议的召集人的地位。

D16.en: He began to challenge the idea that art and truth are a happy couple.
D16.zh: 他开始质疑艺术与真理是幸福伴侣的假设。

M4.en: He came to the group with a prepared ethical system called 'optimalism'.
M4.zh: 他带着一套精心准备的名为“最优主义”的伦理体系来到团体。

D7.en: At 13-18, he knocked on Eric Anderson's door asking why he had to obey school rules.
D7.zh: 13到18岁时，他敲开埃里克·安德森的门，问为什么他必须遵守校规。

D12.en: At 11, his father Leo was deprived of speech for three years by a stroke.
D12.zh: 11岁时，他父亲利奥因中风失语三年。

D14.en: At 21, he appeared as lead singer in the rock band Ugly Rumours.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他作为摇滚乐队Ugly Rumours的主唱亮相。

D6.en: At 22, he moved to Earl's Court, London, and joined Chelsea Labour Party.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他搬到伦敦伯爵宫，加入切尔西工党。

D3.en: At 20-21, he delivered a paper on Marx's 'Economic and Philosophical Manuscripts'.
D3.zh: 20到21岁时，他提交了一篇关于马克思《经济学哲学手稿》的论文。

D9.en: At 22, he voted in the referendum for Britain to stay in the EEC.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他投票支持英国继续留在欧共体。

M8.en: At 20-21, he asked to be confirmed in the Church of England.
M8.zh: 20到21岁时，他请求在英格兰教会接受坚信礼。

M4.en: As a young man, he was greatly stimulated by Macmurray's writing.
M4.zh: 他年轻时，麦克默里的作品极大地激励了他。

D16.en: At 15, he had a 15-minute argument about changing something at school.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他花了15分钟争论学校应该改变某些做法。

D8.en: At 17, he ensured everyone worked purposefully on the play.
D8.zh: 17岁时，他确保每个人都认真地排练话剧。

D11.en: At 21-22, he was lead singer in the student rock band Ugly Rumours.
D11.zh: 21到22岁间，他是学生摇滚乐队Ugly Rumours的主唱。

D13b.en: At 19-22, he read law at St John's College, Oxford, earning an Upper Second.
D13b.zh: 19到22岁间，他在牛津圣约翰学院读法律，获得二等上学位。

D1.en: At 11, his father's stroke was the most important event in his first 18 years.
D1.zh: 11岁时，他父亲的中风是他前18年中最重要的事。

D17.en: He lived through his mother Hazel nursing his father Leo back to health over three years.
D17.zh: 他经历了母亲黑泽尔三年间照顾父亲利奥康复。

D15.en: At 15-16, he lost sight in one eye from a rugby injury, delaying his university start.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，他因橄榄球受伤导致一只眼睛失明，推迟了大学入学。

D13a.en: He persuaded someone to stay, warning that storming out would hurt his father.
D13a.zh: 他说服别人留下，警告说摔门而出会伤害他父亲。

M1.en: At 18, he achieved AAC grades in English, History and French.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在英语、历史和法语中取得了AAC的成绩。

D18.en: At 18, he hatched a plan to sell a recipe to Beecham.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他策划了一个向比彻姆出售配方的计划。

D4.en: At 18, he bought a decrepit blue Ford Thames van for £50 to ferry bands.
D4.zh: 18岁时，他花50英镑买了一辆破旧的蓝色福特泰晤士货车，用来运送乐队。

D20.en: At 22, he won a place on Labour's Scottish Executive.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他赢得了工党苏格兰执行委员会的一个席位。

M2.en: At 24, he noted his mother voted Conservative in memory of her father.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他提到母亲为纪念父亲投了保守党的票。

M7.en: At 15, Leo became secretary of the Govan branch of the Scottish Young Communist League.
M7.zh: 15岁时，利奥成为苏格兰青年共产主义联盟戈万支部的秘书。

D19.en: At 19, he looked after the accounts, recording them in a joint diary with a ledger page each month.
D19.zh: 19岁时，他负责记账，记录在与科勒内特共同保管的日记中，每月有一页分类账。

D12.en: At 16, she dropped out of school and went to work at a laundry.
D12.zh: 16岁时，她辍学去洗衣店工作。

M4.en: At 15-16, she was intrigued by discussions about Zionism and socialism.
M4.zh: 15到16岁时，她对关于犹太复国主义和社会主义的讨论很感兴趣。

D6.en: At 23, she was invited to a room with a group of Americans and others on the kibbutz.
D6.zh: 23岁时，她被邀请到房间，和一群美国人及其他人在基布兹。

D13a.en: At 19, she told him she would not compromise; she was going to Palestine with or without him.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，她告诉他她不会妥协；不管有没有他，她都要去巴勒斯坦。

D4.en: At 23-26, she raised chickens, cleared stones, planted trees, and cooked in the kitchens.
D4.zh: 23到26岁间，她养鸡、清理石头、种树，并在厨房做饭。

D9.en: At 20, she took part in the Poalei Zion conference from December 24 to 29.
D9.zh: 20岁时，她参加了12月24日至29日的锡安工人会议。

D20.en: At 23-26, the kibbutz elected her to committees and sent her to represent Merhavia at conferences.
D20.zh: 23到26岁间，基布兹选她进入各种委员会，并派她代表梅尔哈维亚参加会议。

D14.en: At 23, she felt part of the inner circle of pioneers for the first time.
D14.zh: 23岁时，她第一次感到自己是先驱核心圈子的一员。

D18.en: At 16, she found a better job in a department store, taking measurements for skirt linings.
D18.zh: 16岁时她在百货商店找到更好的工作，为裙衬量尺寸。

D3.en: At 15-17, her father compiled reading lists and explained music subtleties.
D3.zh: 15到17岁时，父亲为她列书单并讲解音乐细节。

M1.en: Graduated as class valedictorian at age 14 in 1912.
M1.zh: 14岁时在1912年以班级第一名毕业。

M2.en: At 15, she moved to live with Sheyna to study.
M2.zh: 15岁时，她搬去与谢娜同住以便学习。

D16.en: At 25, she defended women doing kitchen work in kibbutzim.
D16.zh: 25岁时，她为在基布兹做厨房工作的女性辩护。

D10.en: At 25, she left the kibbutz in tears, hoping to return permanently when Morris recovered.
D10.zh: 25岁时，她含泪离开基布兹，希望莫里斯康复后能永久返回。

D11.en: As a teenager and young adult, she wrote witty poetry and contributed articles to intellectual Jewish publications.
D11.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，她写诙谐诗歌，为犹太知识刊物撰稿。

D6.en: He led an antifraternity element against campus swells at Stanford.
D6.zh: 他在斯坦福领导反兄弟会势力对抗校园精英。

D7.en: At 14-16, he shut off the gas, leaving angry businessmen in total darkness.
D7.zh: 14到16岁时，他关掉煤气，让愤怒的商人陷入一片漆黑。

D9.en: He arranged regular cash transfers to support May, Harriette, and Theodore.
D9.zh: 他安排定期汇款以支持梅、哈里特和西奥多。

D15.en: At age 24, Herbert Hoover wrote a letter to his brother Theodore on February 15, 1898.
D15.zh: 24岁时，赫伯特·胡佛于1898年2月15日写信给兄弟西奥多。

D4.en: He made a name for himself in the rich goldfields of the Australian outback.
D4.zh: 他在澳大利亚内陆的富金矿中为自己赢得了名声。

D18.en: At age 23, Herbert Hoover wrote a letter to his brother Theodore in August 1897.
D18.zh: 23岁时，赫伯特·胡佛于1897年8月写信给兄弟西奥多。

D12.en: He arranged regular cash transfers to support May, Harriette, and Theodore.
D12.zh: 他安排定期汇款以支持梅、哈里特和西奥多。

M1.en: He knew he was destined for something better than competing with a mule.
M1.zh: 他知道自己注定要做比跟骡子竞争更好的事。

D3.en: At 20, he wrote a letter to Nell May Hill.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他给内尔·梅·希尔写了一封信。

D2.en: At 18, he wrote a letter to Nell May Hill.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他给内尔·梅·希尔写了一封信。

D14.en: At 22-23, he spoke to a reporter and got his appointment mentioned in a local paper.
D14.zh: 22到23岁间，他对记者讲话，当地报纸报道了他的任命。

D11.en: At 21, he wrote a letter to Nell May Hill.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他给内尔·梅·希尔写了一封信。

M6.en: At 23, he wrote a letter to his brother Theodore Hoover.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他给哥哥西奥多·胡佛写了一封信。

D8.en: At 23, he wrote a letter to his brother Theodore Hoover.
D8.zh: 23岁时，他给哥哥西奥多·胡佛写了一封信。

M3.en: Around 21, he took Lindgren as a role model and considered graduate studies at Columbia or Johns Hopkins.
M3.zh: 大约21岁时，他以林德格伦为榜样，考虑去哥伦比亚或约翰·霍普金斯读研究生。

D20.en: At 19, he served as Branner's right hand and two-time president of the Stanford geology club.
D20.zh: 19岁时，他担任布兰纳的得力助手，并两次担任斯坦福地质俱乐部主席。

M4.en: At age 23, Herbert Hoover wrote a letter to his brother Theodore in August 1897.
M4.zh: 23岁时，赫伯特·胡佛于1897年8月给兄弟西奥多写信。

D10.en: At 23, he wrote that May had alienated his regard for her, leaving only a duty basis.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他写道梅已经疏远了他对她的好感，只剩下义务关系。

D17.en: At about 23, he rolled bandages as a volunteer in the Spanish-American War effort.
D17.zh: 大约23岁时，他作为志愿者为美西战争卷绷带。

D20X.en: At age 23, Herbert Hoover wrote a letter to his brother Theodore Hoover on September 27, 1897.
D20X.zh: 23岁时，赫伯特·胡佛于1897年9月27日写信给其兄西奥多·胡佛。

M7.en: At 23, Herbert Hoover wrote to Theodore Hoover in August 1897.
M7.zh: 23岁时，赫伯特·胡佛于1897年8月写信给西奥多·胡佛。

D6.en: After arriving in Gdansk, he met an attractive girl named Lala.
D6.zh: 到格但斯克后不久，他认识了一个叫拉拉的美女。

D7.en: He walked through the shipyard with a boy, shouting, 'Look what they are doing to our children!'
D7.zh: 他带着一个男孩走过船厂，喊道：'看看他们对我们的孩子做了什么！'

D13a.en: Until about age ten, he called his stepfather 'dad'.
D13a.zh: 大约十岁前，他管继父叫'爸爸'。

D15.en: He preferred to be a goalkeeper because his short legs couldn't keep up with other boys.
D15.zh: 他更喜欢当守门员，因为他的短腿跟不上其他男孩。

D10.en: He felt anger as a teenager in the 1950s.
D10.zh: 他十几岁时在1950年代感到愤怒。

D13b.en: He followed the rule: respect elders even if they don't fully deserve it.
D13b.zh: 他遵守规则：要尊重长辈，即使他们不完全配得上。

D3.en: At thirteen, he began to listen more carefully to words for the first time.
D3.zh: 十三岁时，他第一次开始更仔细地听别人说话。

D9.en: In the dormitory, he was a kind of gang leader and managed to keep order.
D9.zh: 在宿舍里，他有点像帮派头目，成功维持了秩序。

D2.en: At 24, he studied the worker's role diligently instead of falling into destructive drinking.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他勤奋研究工人角色，没有像同事那样酗酒。

D4.en: At 25, he walked through the shipyard with a boy shouting, 'Look what they're doing to our children!'
D4.zh: 25岁时，他带着一个男孩走过造船厂，喊道：“看他们对我们孩子做了什么！”

D5.en: He burned a banner reading 'The Party is the guiding force of the nation' in Gdańsk in the late 1970s.
D5.zh: 他在1970年代末在格但斯克烧毁了一条写着‘党是民族的领导力量’的横幅。

D2.en: He played war games as early as fourth or fifth grade.
D2.zh: 他早在小学四五年级就开始玩战争游戏。

D15.en: He celebrated his coming of age at eighteen in spring 1919.
D15.zh: 1919年春天，他十八岁举行了成人礼。

D7.en: He was sharply reprimanded by Prince Saionji for a rowdy lapse of decorum.
D7.zh: 他因行为失礼被西园寺公望严厉训斥。

D3.en: He asked detailed questions about World War I fighting.
D3.zh: 他详细询问了第一次世界大战中的战斗情况。

D9.en: The government announced sending him overseas on 8 February 1921.
D9.zh: 1921年2月8日，政府宣布派他出国。

D6.en: He and his brothers enacted the Russo-Japanese War in play as small children.
D6.zh: 他幼年时和兄弟们玩日俄战争游戏。

D14.en: He stepped onto the platform as a bright, glorious figure.
D14.zh: 他走上讲台，光彩照人。

D5.en: He departed for Europe on a tour to further his education and counter decline.
D5.zh: 他启程前往欧洲游学，以增长见识并扭转皇室颓势。

D13a.en: He had to conform to the ritual role expected of the sovereign.
D13a.zh: 他不得不遵守君主应有的礼仪角色。

D12.en: He was removed from the court and placed in the care of the Kawamura family.
D12.zh: 他被带出宫廷，交由川村家抚养。

D4.en: He was given a special tour of the warship Katori.
D4.zh: 他获得了一次参观战舰香取的特别机会。

M2.en: His visits to shrines and imperial mausoleums deepened his sense of the importance of his ancestors.
M2.zh: 他参拜神社和皇陵的经历加深了他对祖先重要性的认识。

D20.en: He became regent for his father on November 25, 1921.
D20.zh: 1921年11月25日，他成为父亲的摄政。

D10.en: He was overcome with emotion upon hearing of Nogi's death, with tears in his eyes.
D10.zh: 听到乃木的死讯，他情绪激动，泪流满面。

D11.en: He wrote a school essay in 1920 that aped the viewpoints of the elders surrounding him.
D11.zh: 1920年，他写了一篇模仿周围长辈观点的学校作文。

D18.en: He continued to operate on the black market, acting as a broker for anything tradeable.
D18.zh: 他继续在黑市上经营，充当各种可交易物品的经纪人。

D3.en: He was curious and went up to see for himself, and it was quite a sight.
D3.zh: 他很好奇，亲自上去看，那景象真壮观。

D7.en: He was caught in the laboratory drawing the back of his head with its bald patch.
D7.zh: 他在实验室里画他后脑勺的秃斑时被抓住了。

D13b.en: At 7, he was double-promoted from primary 1 to standard 1, skipping primary 2.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他从小学一年级跳级到标准一年级，跳过了二年级。

D6.en: He joined the Cambridge University Labour Club and regularly attended their meetings.
D6.zh: 他加入了剑桥大学工党俱乐部，并定期参加他们的会议。

D14.en: At age 4, he was a page boy at his aunt's wedding, dressed in traditional costume.
D14.zh: 4岁时，他在姑姑的婚礼上当小傧相，穿着当时的传统服装。

D2.en: At 7, he had to sit with his father for an hour three evenings a week before dinner.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他每周有三个晚上要在晚饭前和父亲坐在一起一个小时。

M1.en: He came first in school and won a place in Raffles Institution, which took only top students.
M1.zh: 他在学校考了第一名，进入了只收顶尖学生的莱佛士书院。

D16.en: At 23, he impressed by speaking grammatical English without a strong accent, explaining his war-interrupted education.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他因能说语法正确、不带浓重口音的英语而给人留下印象，解释了自己因战争中断学业的情况。

D20.en: He joined the Cambridge University Labour Club and regularly attended their meetings.
D20.zh: 他加入了剑桥大学工党俱乐部并定期参加他们的会议。

D9.en: He discussed with his mother and decided to move to the Cameron Highlands.
D9.zh: 他和母亲商量后决定搬到金马仑高原。

D15.en: He had a broken nose from boxing in his younger days.
D15.zh: 他年轻时打拳击导致鼻子骨折。

D10.en: He felt a deep chill and braced himself for interrogation.
D10.zh: 他感到一阵寒意，做好了接受审讯的准备。

D5.en: He applied for leave and went to reconnoitre Penang and the Cameron Highlands.
D5.zh: 他请假去侦察槟城和金马仑高原。

M4.en: He felt disillusioned as a fellow Asiatic during the war.
M4.zh: 他作为亚洲同胞感到幻灭。

M7.en: He was filled with disillusionment as a fellow Asiatic.
M7.zh: 作为亚洲同胞，他充满了幻灭感。

D1.en: He endured this cat-and-mouse game for some eight weeks.
D1.zh: 他忍受了这场猫鼠游戏大约八周。

D4.en: He bought a full-size billiard table, restored it, and installed it in his flat.
D4.zh: 他买了一张标准尺寸的台球桌，修复后安装在自己的公寓里。

M2.en: She tried to ensure he would commit to a Chinese girl and not return with an English one.
M2.zh: 她尽力确保他会对某个中国女孩负责，以免带回一个英国女孩。

D13a.en: He was sent to an English school to prepare for an English university.
D13a.zh: 他被送到一所英文学校，为将来上英国大学做准备。

D2.en: At 17, he asked a friend to buy many seals and engraving tools, saying the more the better.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他托朋友买大量图章和铁笔，说越多越好。

D3.en: At 17, he planned to learn arithmetic and foreign languages after the Dragon Boat Festival.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他打算过五月节后学算术和洋文。

D4.en: As a small child, he found a painting manual and traced it on paper with joy.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他翻到《芥子园画谱》喜不自禁，趴在地上用纸印描。

D7.en: At 25, he broke tradition by having his mother's coffin placed in the main hall and held a modern-style funeral.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他破例将母亲灵柩停于前院大厅，按新式礼仪开吊。

D11.en: At age 5-10, he imitated Buddhist monks, draping a blanket as a robe and directing other children.
D11.zh: 5到10岁时，他模仿和尚做佛事，披被单当袈裟，指挥其他孩子。

M4.en: At 19, he lamented that China must reform and carved a seal declaring Kang Youwei his teacher.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他慨叹中国非变法不可，刻印“南海康君是吾师”明志。

D13a.en: At about 43, he decided to return gifts to avoid attachment, thinking of himself as already dead.
D13a.zh: 约43岁时，他认为出家应做已死想，退还礼物以免挂碍。

D9.en: At 20, he co-founded the Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Association with other artists.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他与任伯年等人组织海上书画公会。

D6.en: At 17, he hosted a literary society at his home, meeting monthly for essay exchanges.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他在宅中设文社，每月会课交流心得。

D14.en: From age 25 to 38, he excelled in many roles: artist, teacher, actor, monk, and more.
D14.zh: 25到38岁间，他胜任贵公子、金石家、演员、教师、和尚等多种角色。

D5.en: He performed difficult acrobatic and martial old-man roles after suffering hardships.
D5.zh: 他吃苦后表演了难度大的靠把戏及武老生戏。

D12.en: As a small child, his parents gave him a branch as a keepsake, which also held his father's omen.
D12.zh: 幼年时，父母送他一根树枝作纪念，其中也包含父亲视为异兆的因素。

M1.en: From age 25 to 38, he excelled as an actor, artist, musician, teacher, and monk.
M1.zh: 25到38岁间，他胜任演员、艺术家、音乐家、教师和和尚等多种角色。

D19.en: He sent rubbings to over 30 celebrities for poems and printed a two-volume collection.
D19.zh: 他将拓片寄给30多位名人征求诗词，印成《汉甘林瓦砚题辞》二卷。

D20.en: He co-founded the Shanghai Calligraphy and Painting Association with several artists.
D20.zh: 他和任伯年等人组织“海上书画公会”。

D15.en: He experienced authentic Edo-style morning baths, kimonos, and long braziers.
D15.zh: 他尝过地道的江户趣味：早浴、和服、长火钵。

M7.en: He wrote two long articles on art theory for the monthly magazine Awakening Lion.
M7.zh: 他写了两篇美术理论长文在《醒狮》月刊上发表。

D13b.en: He founded the Music Little Magazine in 1905.
D13b.zh: 1905年，他创办《音乐小杂志》。

D18.en: He opened Dongjisheng pharmacy to practice medicine.
D18.zh: 他开办东济生药店行医。

M2.en: As a small child, his mother stopped him from eating at a crooked table, citing Confucius.
M2.zh: 幼年时，母亲因桌子不正制止他吃饭，引用孔子的话。

D10.en: At about 43, his cheeks flushed as if angry, then he suddenly clasped his hands and chanted 'Amitabha' repeatedly, weeping.
D10.zh: 约43岁时，他两颊泛红像有怒色，接着合掌连声念‘阿弥陀佛’，流着泪。

M6.en: At age 6-10, he felt impermanence and emptiness, and later shaved his head at Hupao Temple due to past karma.
M6.zh: 七八岁时即有无常苦空之感，后到虎跑寺披剃，全靠宿有之因。

D12.en: At 13, he was sent as a shop apprentice, but fell ill and returned to his grandmother.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他被送到百货店当学徒，因病退业，回到外祖母身边。

D7.en: At 20, he wrote an angry letter to Master Jicang for not rescuing a jailed monk, then fled before the ceremony ended.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他致函寄老，责怪其不营救被拘的圆映，随后不等法会结束就避走他寺。

D6.en: At 19, he studied at the Zhiyuan Seminary in Nanjing with classmates including Mei Guangxi and Ouyang Jian.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他在南京祗垣精舍学习，同学有梅光羲、欧阳渐等人。

D9.en: At 24, he co-founded the Buddhist Progressive Association at Jinshan Temple, sparking the 'Great Uproar at Jinshan'.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他与仁山等在镇江金山寺成立佛教协进会，引发“大闹金山”事件。

D13b.en: At 14, he returned to his ancestral home to pay respects but failed to claim property rights, then hurried back to Haining.
D13b.zh: 14岁冬，他回崇德祭扫祖坟，但未能取得祖产继承权，随即返回海宁。

D3.en: At 22, he established a Buddhist study hall at Shizilin and began lecturing and writing, producing his first works.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他在狮子林设立佛学精舍，开始讲学著述，编出《教观诠要》和《佛教史略》。

D14.en: At 9, he insisted on accompanying his grandmother on a pilgrimage to Jiuhua Mountain, visiting temples along the way.
D14.zh: 9岁秋，他坚持随外祖母去九华山进香，途中瞻礼平望小九华寺和镇江金山寺。

D18.en: At 15, he became an apprentice at a department store but was too weak for chores and longed for Buddhist freedom.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他入朱万裕百货店当学徒，但因体弱不堪繁务，未能安心，向往佛门自在。

D2.en: At 14, he recovered from malaria at his grandmother's nunnery, rested, and began learning to write.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他因疟疾退业，在外祖母的庵中养息温读，开始学写文章。

D5.en: He said his Buddhist revolutionary reputation spread, earning respect, fear, disgust, or pity.
D5.zh: 他说自己的佛教革命名声从此传开，受人尊敬、惊惧、厌恶或怜惜。

D16.en: At 20, he debated Master Di about the nature of the seventh and eighth consciousnesses, leaving Di speechless.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他与谛老辩论七识无体、八识有体，把谛老问得目瞪口呆。

D4.en: At 22, he founded a Buddhist study hall and began writing his first works, including 'Jiaoguan Quanyao'.
D4.zh: 22岁时，他在狮子林组建佛学精舍，开始讲学著述，编出《教观诠要》等。

M4.en: At 24, he explored various socialist ideologies and approached anarchist Liu Shifu's group.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他研究各种社会主义，并接近刘师复的无政府党。

D11.en: At 24, he published essays like 'On Private Evil' and 'The Truth of the Universe' in a Buddhist monthly.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他在佛教月报上发表《致私篇》、《宇宙真相》等文章。

D20.en: At 24, he joined the Chinese Unified Nationalist Party, whose constitution still exists.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他参加中华民国统一国民党，该党党章至今保存。

D13b.en: At 24, she had deep friendship with a nun in Fengyuan and discussed Buddhist teachings daily.
D13b.zh: 24岁时，她和丰原师父交情很深，每天聚谈探讨佛法道理。

D9.en: At 23, she led siblings in theater business and suggested selling two theaters; her mother agreed.
D9.zh: 23岁时，她带领弟弟妹妹熟悉戏院业务，建议卖掉两家戏院，母亲依她做主。

D12.en: At 24, she carried water buckets on mountain paths daily with another nun.
D12.zh: 24岁时，她每天挑着水桶往返于崎岖的山路上。

D1.en: At 24, she survived on wild vegetables when food was scarce.
D1.zh: 24岁时，她靠挖野菜度日。

D7.en: At 23-25, she ran away three times to study Buddhism; her mother cried and brought her back each time.
D7.zh: 23到25岁间，她三次离家出走自行学佛，母亲哭着把她找回。

D15.en: At 24, she carried water buckets on mountain paths daily.
D15.zh: 24岁时，她每天挑着水桶往返于崎岖的山路上。

M4.en: At 24, she aspired to be a nun who is self-reliant and serves all beings equally.
M4.zh: 24岁时，她希望出家后自力更生，为众生服务。

M6.en: At 24, she felt a vague thought gradually clarify: she had always wanted to become a Buddhist nun, but what was the purpose?
M6.zh: 24岁时，她一道朦胧的心念渐渐清晰：一直向往出家学佛，但学佛的目的是什么？

D10.en: At 23, her eyes were wide but tearless, her face pale as wax, and her lips bloodless.
D10.zh: 23岁时，她眼睛睁得很大却没有眼泪，脸色惨白如蜡，嘴唇毫无血色。

D13a.en: At 23-25, she insisted on her choice despite her mother's tears.
D13a.zh: 23到25岁间，她不顾母亲含泪劝阻，坚持自己的选择。

D12.en: After his father's death, he left law to work full-time for socialism.
D12.zh: 父亲去世后，他放弃法律工作，全职投身社会主义事业。

D14.en: He gave the Chichele Lectures at Oxford.
D14.zh: 他在牛津大学做了奇切利讲座。

D15.en: He played rugby passionately and loved cricket as a child.
D15.zh: 他小时候热爱橄榄球和板球。

D16.en: He was an argumentative boy who loved political debate.
D16.zh: 他是个好争论的男孩，喜欢辩论政治。

D6.en: He visited the Fabian Society headquarters and became a member.
D6.zh: 他参观了费边社总部并成为会员。

D18.en: At 17, he earned his first income as a superintendent at a summer camp.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他在夏令营当管理员赚到第一笔收入。

D19.en: He won a prize for the best-kept tent.
D19.zh: 他因帐篷最整洁而获奖。

D9.en: At 18-21, he debated imperial protection in his college debating society.
D9.zh: 18到21岁时，他在学院辩论社就帝国保护问题进行了辩论。

D2.en: At 23, he was at the club almost every evening and refereed football on Saturdays.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他几乎每晚都去俱乐部，周六还担任足球裁判。

M7.en: At 23, he threw himself into the local area and got to know the families of the lads he trained.
M7.zh: 23岁时，他投身当地社区，结识了他训练的年轻人的家庭。

M2.en: As a child and young man, he was close to his siblings and family.
M2.zh: 他从小到大与兄弟姐妹和家人关系亲密。

M3.en: As a young man, he had various influences on him.
M3.zh: 年轻时，他受到了各种影响。

D13b.en: He was betrothed to two girls by his grandmother and uncle.
D13b.zh: 祖母和叔父为他定了两门亲事。

D6.en: He followed his uncle to visit monasteries at Nan-yue.
D6.zh: 他跟随叔父去南岳参观寺院。

D7.en: He refused a post from the abbot and was beaten as punishment.
D7.zh: 他拒绝方丈的职务，因此被杖责。

D13a.en: He was forced to marry two women and placed under house arrest.
D13a.zh: 他被强迫成婚并遭软禁。

D2.en: He hid in a grotto to perform penitential offerings to Buddhas.
D2.zh: 他躲在山洞里向诸佛忏悔供养。

D1.en: At 23, he completed three years of observing the rules for repentance and reform.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他完成了三年的忏悔和改过规则的遵守。

D9.en: He returned to the mountain temple and was given work.
D9.zh: 他回到山寺，被安排了工作。

D3.en: He perceived a great brightness and saw everything clearly.
D3.zh: 他眼前突现大光明，内外洞彻。

D14.en: At 25, he wept and declared he would rather die than break his vow to burn off a finger for his mother.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他痛哭流涕，说宁死也不违背为母亲烧指还愿的决定。

D10.en: At 25, he wept and declared he would rather die than break his vow to burn off a finger for his mother.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他痛哭流涕，说宁死也不违背为母亲烧指还愿的决定。

M2.en: At 25, he vowed to burn off a finger to repay his debt to his mother.
M2.zh: 25岁时，他发誓烧掉一根手指来报答母亲。

M6.en: At 25, he recalled leaving home, his wanderings, illness by the Yellow River, and the layman who saved him.
M6.zh: 25岁时，他想起离家出走、流浪、黄河边生病以及救他的居士。

M8.en: At 25, he cut off his last doubt about the Mind-root and rejoiced at realizing his cherished aim.
M8.zh: 25岁时，他断除了对心性的最后疑惑，为达成夙愿而欢喜。

D12.en: At 15, she boarded a plane for Delhi, beginning her years of exile.
D12.zh: 15岁时，她登上了前往德里的飞机，开始了多年的流亡生活。

D6.en: At 11, she transferred to the more prestigious Methodist English High School.
D6.zh: 11岁时，她转学到更著名的卫理公会英语高中。

M2.en: As a small child, Aung San was imbued with the spirit of revolt and independence.
M2.zh: 幼年时，昂山就被灌输了反抗和独立的精神。

D14.en: As a child, she learned piano with enthusiasm and aptitude.
D14.zh: 她小时候学钢琴，充满热情且很有天赋。

D13a.en: At 19, she left Lady Sri Ram College after only one year.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，她在拉姆夫人学院只读了一年就离开了。

D3.en: At 15-18, she examined her countrymen from her new perspective and saw how right May Oung had been.
D3.zh: 15到18岁间，她从新视角审视同胞，发现梅翁说得对。

D9.en: At 22, she encouraged student activists to go out amongst the people and meet peasants and workers.
D9.zh: 22岁时，她鼓励学生活动家走到民众中去，接触农民和工人。

M6.en: At 20, she felt she must one day do something for her people.
M6.zh: 20岁时，她觉得总有一天必须为人民做些什么。

D4.en: At 24, she was led to a hollowed-out tree trunk full of water heated by hot stones.
D4.zh: 24岁时，她被带到一根挖空的树干旁，里面装满用热石头加热的水。

M4.en: At 20, she was genuinely absorbed by the ideals of Mahatma Gandhi and Pandit Nehru.
M4.zh: 20岁时，她真正被圣雄甘地和潘迪特·尼赫鲁的理想所吸引。

D17.en: At 7, she was handed a gun by her brother and told to guard it.
D17.zh: 7岁时，她哥哥把枪递给她，让她保管。

D10.en: She stiffened her spine and pursed her lips whenever something untoward occurred.
D10.zh: 每当有不顺心的事发生时，她就会挺直脊背、抿紧嘴唇。

D15.en: As a child aged 10-15, she was taken to the Presidential Bodyguard grounds for riding lessons.
D15.zh: 10到15岁时，她被带到总统卫队训练场学骑马。

D8.en: At 20-21, she insisted that rebirth in a broad sense was an actual phenomenon.
D8.zh: 20到21岁时，她坚持认为广义的转世是真实存在的现象。

D12.en: At 21, he worked as a secretary while recovering from illness, handling newspapers and drafting documents.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他一边养病一边做秘书，收发报纸、起草文件。

D7.en: At 3, an elder grabbed his ear and pulled him down for offending ancestors.
D7.zh: 3岁时，一位长辈揪住他耳朵把他扯下来，说他冲撞了祖宗。

D2.en: At 21, he studied Buddhist scriptures and gradually mastered his uncle's preferences, needing fewer edits over time.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他开始研究佛经，摸清了舅舅的口味，关纲之一改得越来越少。

D4.en: At 7, he used a bamboo pole to carefully remove a spider web and rescue a dragonfly.
D4.zh: 7岁时，他找来竹竿挑开蜘蛛网，救出了蜻蜓。

D3.en: At 7, he picked up a thin copy of the Hundred Parables Sutra to read.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他拿起一本薄薄的《百喻经》看。

D16.en: At 7, he said, 'Guanyin Pavilion's Guanyin Buddha protects the people's peace.'
D16.zh: 7岁时，他说：“观音阁观音大佛保佑黎民平安。”

D11.en: At 19, he described the scenery around Soochow University and his longing for home in the poem 'Jiangnan Hao'.
D11.zh: 19岁时，他在《江南好》中描写了东吴大学周围的景色和怀念家乡的心情。

M7.en: At 18, he introduced a fundraising method for students to save money by eating vegetarian for a week.
M7.zh: 18岁时，他介绍了学生吃素一周省钱的募捐办法。

D9.en: At 18, he continued serving as class monitor.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他继续当班长。

D6.en: At 21-23, he often gathered with Mei Dajun and others at the Gongdelin vegetarian restaurant.
D6.zh: 21到23岁时，他常和梅达君等在功德林素菜馆小聚。

D1.en: At 21, he revised Guan Gangzhi's drafts less and less until he stopped.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他改关纲之的稿子越改越少，最后不改了。

D13a.en: At 21, he was valued by Guan Gangzhi.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他被关纲之看重。

D13b.en: At 24, he married Wang Dihua.
D13b.zh: 24岁时，他和汪棣华结婚。

M2.en: He was born into a scholarly family.
M2.zh: 他出生书香门第。

M3.en: At 18, he enjoyed attending Su Xuelin's lectures.
M3.zh: 18岁时，他喜欢听苏雪林的课。

D12.en: At 24, he experienced his father's death after two months of illness and managed funeral affairs.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他经历了父亲病两个多月后去世，并办理丧事。

D7.en: At 24, he decided to become a monk due to depression and Buddhist influence.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他因内心沉闷抑郁和佛法熏习，决心出家。

D13a.en: At 24, he decided to become a monk due to depression and Buddhist influence.
D13a.zh: 24岁时，他因内心沉闷抑郁和佛法熏习，决心出家。

D5.en: At 25, he left home to become a monk, embarking on an uncertain journey.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他离家出家，踏上充满光明但前途未知的旅程。

D2.en: At 25, he resolved to practice diligently after becoming a monk.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他出家后决心好好精进修行。

D3.en: At 20, he purchased several Buddhist texts from bookstores.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他从商务印书馆等处购得多种佛教典籍。

D19.en: At 20, he extracted Buddhist terms from the Ciyuan dictionary.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他从《辞源》中摘录佛教术语。

D13b.en: At 25, he became a monk under Master Qingnian at Fuquan Temple, receiving the name Yinshun.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他在福泉庵依清念和尚落发出家，法名印顺。

D9.en: At 20, he was invited to host young men for instruction.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他受邀接待前来受训的年轻人。

D2.en: At 21, he devoted himself to village work and teaching new believers.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他投身乡村工作，教导新信徒。

D13a.en: At 20, he felt uneasy about accepting missionary help, trusting God would work.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他对接受宣教士帮助感到不安，相信神会亲自做工。

D7.en: As a teenager, he aroused resentment by pointing out a boy who took something.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他指认了拿东西的男孩，引起公愤。

M4.en: At 21, he felt he must abandon thoughts of her to follow the Lord utterly.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他感到必须放弃对她的念想，才能完全跟随主。

D5.en: He faced expulsion that would ruin his scholarship and study abroad hopes.
D5.zh: 他面临开除，这会毁掉他的奖学金和出国留学希望。

D10.en: At 24, he joyfully jumped up and ran downstairs to tell a young brother he had died.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他欢欣地从椅子上跳起来，跑下楼对青年弟兄说：『你知道我已经死了吗？』

D18.en: He had only about thirty dollars, but the boat fare was at least eighty.
D18.zh: 他手头上只有三十几块钱，而乘汽艇的费用最少也要八十块钱。

D20.en: He shared responsibility with two brothers, one two years older and one seven years older.
D20.zh: 他和两位弟兄共同承担责任，一位比他大两岁，一位比他大七岁。

D15.en: He shared his vision of spreading the life-giving Word across China with her.
D15.zh: 他把自己期待将生命之道传遍中国的异像与她交通。

D11.en: He expounded Daniel and Revelation with enthusiasm and great effect.
D11.zh: 他满怀热情地讲解但以理书和启示录，效果显著。

D19.en: After months, he finished the first four-volume set of 'The Spiritual Man', a systematic work.
D19.zh: 几个月以后，他完成了第一部包括四卷内容的《属灵人》，这是一本很有系统的作品。

M1.en: He shared his vision of spreading the life-giving Word across China with her.
M1.zh: 他把自己期待将生命之道传遍中国的异像与她交通。

M2.en: He shared his vision of spreading the life-giving Word across China with her.
M2.zh: 他把自己期待将生命之道传遍中国的异像与她交通。

M6.en: He pledged himself to serve God wholly and utterly, and never went back on his word.
M6.zh: 他立誓完全彻底地侍奉神，并且从未食言。

M7.en: He received an invitation to conduct a series of evangelistic meetings.
M7.zh: 他收到邀请，主持一系列布道会。

M8.en: He shared with her his vision of spreading the word of life throughout China.
M8.zh: 他向她分享了自己将生命之道传遍中国的异象。

D1.en: He and five others resolved to spend the holiday preaching Christ to villagers.
D1.zh: 他和另外五人决定假期去村里向村民传讲基督。

D20X.en: He left the Presbyterian mission after a few months, not feeling God wanted him there permanently.
D20X.zh: 他与长老会的关系只有短短几个月，他不认为神要他永远在此机构服事。

D8.en: He shared with her his vision of spreading the word of life throughout China.
D8.zh: 他向她分享了自己将生命之道传遍中国的异象。

M3.en: He shared with her his vision of spreading the word of life throughout China.
M3.zh: 他向她分享了自己将生命之道传遍中国的异象。

M5.en: He shared with her his vision of spreading the word of life throughout China.
M5.zh: 他向她分享了自己将生命之道传遍中国的异象。

D12.en: At 18, he felt obligated to quit school to help his mother at home.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他觉得有责任辍学回家帮助母亲。

D3.en: As a teenager, he received solid Christian grounding and showed unusual promise.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他接受了扎实的基督教基础训练，并表现出非凡的潜力。

D2.en: At 24, the experience drove him to more intense Bible study at home.
D2.zh: 24岁时，这次经历促使他在家更刻苦地研读圣经。

D10.en: At 24, he was so joyful at a discovery that he jumped up and shouted, 'Praise the Lord, I am dead!'
D10.zh: 24岁时，他因一个发现欣喜若狂，跳起来喊道：‘赞美主，我死了！’

D9.en: At 24, he decided to start holding Bible classes in his home on Precious Rain Street.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他决定在自己宝雨街的家中开始举办查经班。

D13b.en: As a small child, his parents dedicated him to God's service.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，父母将他奉献给神使用。

D11.en: At about age 7-8, he was full of ideas for games to play.
D11.zh: 七八岁时，他满脑子都是玩游戏的点子。

D14.en: At 24, growing numbers were attracted to hear the eloquent young preacher.
D14.zh: 24岁时，越来越多的人被吸引来听这位口才出众的年轻传道人。

D6.en: He was gratified to meet people who had read and appreciated Revival.
D6.zh: 他很高兴遇到读过并欣赏《复兴》的人。

M4.en: At 15, he determined to become a politician.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他决心成为政治家。

M6.en: He saw his oneness with Christ and that when Christ died, he died.
M6.zh: 他看见自己与基督合一，基督死时他也死了。

M1.en: At age 9-12, he kept first in his class and examinations.
M1.zh: 9到12岁时，他保持班级第一和考试第一。

M3.en: He read the life of Abraham Lincoln, who became his hero.
M3.zh: 他读了林肯传记，林肯成了他的英雄。

D16.en: He boldly argued publicly against a speaker's errors in 1924.
D16.zh: 1924年，他公开大胆反驳一位讲员的错误。

D12.en: He was not afraid of the Communists because his family were proletariat tenant farmers.
D12.zh: 他因为家里是无产阶级佃农，所以不怕共产党。

D10.en: He kept asking himself questions until he became a big ball of doubt.
D10.zh: 他不断问自己这些问题，直到自己变成一个巨大的疑团。

D3.en: He secretly read books on Marxism and felt it conflicted with Buddhism.
D3.zh: 他偷偷读马克思主义书籍，觉得与佛教不符。

D6.en: He corresponded with a former monk named Mantao Zhang studying in Japan.
D6.zh: 他与在日本留学的前僧张曼涛通信。

D7.en: He bumped into his mother when he was supposed to be in school.
D7.zh: 他本该在学校，却意外撞见母亲。

D13a.en: He was grateful his mother wasn't going to force him to marry her.
D13a.zh: 他感激母亲不强迫他娶她。

D2.en: He grew to love chanting and would chant while doing chores or walking.
D2.zh: 他渐渐喜欢诵经，做家务或走路时也常念。

D9.en: He and two classmates enlisted in the communication corps.
D9.zh: 他和两个同学一起报名参加了通信兵。

D17.en: His mother took in girls like a mother hen with chicks.
D17.zh: 他母亲像母鸡护小鸡一样收留了那些女孩。

D5.en: At 18-19, he risked arrest by the Nationalists.
D5.zh: 18到19岁时，他冒着被国民党逮捕的风险。

D13b.en: At 19, he enrolled in Quiet Calm Monastery in Shanghai.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，他进入上海静安寺佛学院就读。

M1.en: At 11-12, he felt motivated to study for his future.
M1.zh: 11到12岁时，他感到不学习就没有未来。

M7.en: At age 10-12, his mother took in girls like a mother hen.
M7.zh: 他10到12岁时，母亲像母鸡护小鸡一样收留了女孩们。

D14.en: At 20-25, he published novels, poems, and essays under a pen name.
D14.zh: 20到25岁时，他用笔名行世将军发表小说、诗歌和散文。

D11.en: At 14, he excitedly dreamed of his future on Wolf Mountain.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他兴奋地在狼山上编织未来的美梦。

M6.en: At 19, he wanted a life of learning and Dharma transmission.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他想要一种学习、研究和传法的生活。

M2.en: At 19, he wanted a life of learning and Dharma transmission.
M2.zh: 19岁时，他想要一种学习、研究和传法的生活。

D15.en: At 19, he learned Taijichuan and Shaolin boxing from a Shaolin teacher.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他跟少林寺的老师学习太极拳和少林拳。

D4.en: At 25-31, he pleaded to spare a snake caught in his room.
D4.zh: 25到31岁时，他请求不要杀死在他房间里的蛇。

D19.en: He copied notes from the board and discussed them with classmates.
D19.zh: 他抄下黑板上的笔记，和同学讨论。

M3.en: He admired Taixu's efforts in Buddhist education, which influenced him lifelong.
M3.zh: 他佩服太虚办佛教教育的努力，这影响了他一生。

M4.en: He vowed to preserve his country's dignity and fight for Buddhism before joining the army.
M4.zh: 他参军前发誓维护国格、为衰微的佛教争光。

M5.en: He scored at least 90% on exams and graduated top of his class.
M5.zh: 他考试至少得九十分，以全班前几名毕业。

M8.en: He wanted a life of learning and transmitting the Dharma.
M8.zh: 他想要过学习、研究和传法的生活。

D1.en: He prayed to Guanyin and passed the officer exam despite lacking qualifications.
D1.zh: 他祈求观音菩萨，虽然不够资格还是通过了军官考试。

D16.en: He was well-known in Taiwan's Buddhist circle for his articles published since his army days.
D16.zh: 他因从军时就开始发表文章而在台湾佛教界知名。

D12.en: At age 5-6, he cleaned the house and collected cow dung to sell for coins.
D12.zh: 五六岁时，他打扫屋子，捡牛粪换铜钱。

D18.en: At age 5-6, he proactively cleaned the house and picked cow dung to help his parents financially.
D18.zh: 五六岁时主动打扫内外，捡牛粪换钱帮补家用。

D17.en: At 12-20, he saw his mother help a wounded soldier by carrying him on a door panel.
D17.zh: 12到20岁间，他看到母亲用门板帮助受伤的士兵。

D15.en: At 9, he tried to drown himself after his pet pigeon disappeared, but swam to safety.
D15.zh: 9岁时，他因小白鸽飞失而投河自杀，却游到了对岸。

D2.en: At 22-24, he drew 600 buckets of water daily from a well and walked 15 li to buy groceries.
D2.zh: 22到24岁间，他每天从井里打600桶水，走15里路买菜。

D7.en: At 10, he refused to become a monk when told his family couldn't join him.
D7.zh: 10岁时，他因家人不能同去而拒绝出家。

D13a.en: At 12, he insisted on keeping his promise to become a monk despite his mother's objection.
D13a.zh: 12岁时，他坚持出家承诺，不顾母亲反对。

D1.en: At 17, he vowed to dedicate his life to Buddhism after receiving half a bowl of pickled vegetables from his master while ill.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他病中收到师父半碗咸菜，发愿奉献佛教。

D10.en: He recalled that was the most anxious he ever saw his mother.
D10.zh: 他回忆说那是母亲最着急的一次。

D9.en: Between ages 18-25, he edited literary supplements for newspapers and co-edited a magazine.
D9.zh: 18到25岁间，他编辑报纸副刊，合编《怒涛月刊》。

D5.en: At 22, he was arrested by police and detained in a warehouse, rescued by General Sun Liren's wife and others.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他被警察逮捕关在仓库，靠孙立人夫人等人营救才脱险。

D3.en: As a teenager, he managed the library and read books that teachers and seniors had borrowed.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他管理图书馆，专看老师和学长借过的书。

D11.en: At 18, he created his own magazine titled 'My Garden' and published it monthly.
D11.zh: 18岁时，他编了一份专属杂志《我的园地》，每月出版。

D4.en: At 22-23, while riding a bicycle, he shouted at children and fell into a deep ditch.
D4.zh: 22到23岁时，他骑脚踏车喊小孩让开，结果掉进深沟。

D16.en: Between ages 15-25, he practiced speeches repeatedly and reflected on his words at night.
D16.zh: 15到25岁间，他反复练习开示讲稿，深夜反省说话得失。

D14.en: At 18, he submitted articles to Zhenjiang newspapers, with his first piece titled 'The Words of Banknotes'.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他投稿镇江报纸，第一篇文章叫《钞票的话》。

D20.en: At 25, he became the editor-in-chief of the magazine 'Life'.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他主编《人生》杂志。

D13b.en: At 12, he became a monk at Qixia Mountain due to the war.
D13b.zh: 12岁时，他因战争在栖霞山剃度出家。

M6.en: At 17, he vowed to propagate Buddhism and repay his teacher's kindness.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他心中默默发愿将来要弘法利生，以报师恩。

D8.en: At 12-20, he saw his mother acquitted for her calm demeanor in court.
D8.zh: 12到20岁间，他目睹母亲因神态自若、对答清楚而被判无罪。

M5.en: At 12-15, he accepted a comment about his potential, thinking time would tell.
M5.zh: 12到15岁间，他承担了那句话，心想有没有出息不急于一时分辨。

D20X.en: At 12, he became a novice monk.
D20X.zh: 12岁时，他出家当沙弥。

M1.en: At 12-15, he thought time would show his worth in 20-30 years.
M1.zh: 12到15岁间，他心想时间会给他力量，二十、三十年后谁知道呢。

M8.en: At 12-15, he worshipped Guanyin Bodhisattva daily.
M8.zh: 12到15岁间，他每日礼拜观音菩萨。

D3.en: At 13-14, he was enthroned as abbot due to precocious learning.
D3.zh: 13到14岁时，他因早慧被立为住持。

D7.en: At 11, he was urged to side with the Communist party.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他被要求与共产党站在一起。

D13a.en: In his teens, he supported land reform and praised Party-built schools.
D13a.zh: 十几岁时，他支持土地改革，赞扬党建的学校。

D12.en: He was born to the village headman's wife, Sonam Dolma.
D12.zh: 他出生了，母亲是村长夫人索南多玛。

D13b.en: At 5, he was flown to Beijing and filmed exchanging khatas with Jiang Zemin.
D13b.zh: 5岁时，他被带到北京，与江泽民互献哈达并被拍摄。

D2.en: He taught himself Chinese using a small, worn dictionary.
D2.zh: 他靠一本破旧的小字典自学了中文。

D9.en: At 7, he was confirmed as Panchen in a ceremony with 100,000 people.
D9.zh: 7岁时，他在十万人参加的仪式上被确认为班禅。

D15.en: He shared his enthusiasm for riding, fencing and target practice.
D15.zh: 他分享了自己对骑马、击剑和射击的热情。

M7.en: He supported the vision of a socialist society respecting Tibetan traditions.
M7.zh: 他支持尊重西藏传统的社会主义社会愿景。

D11.en: He wrote a book.
D11.zh: 他写了一本书。

D3.en: She wrote, 'Jesus, it is for you and for souls!'
D3.zh: 她写道：‘耶稣，这是为你和灵魂！’

D6.en: She first attended elementary school at Sacred Heart Church with two other children.
D6.zh: 她最初和另外两个孩子一起在圣心教堂上小学。

D13b.en: Her first photo as novice Sister Teresa was taken on February 22, 1929.
D13b.zh: 1929年2月22日，她作为初学生特蕾莎修女的第一张照片被拍下。

D5.en: She made landfall in Colombo en route to Bengal on December 25, 1928.
D5.zh: 1928年圣诞节后不久，她的船在科伦坡靠岸，前往孟加拉的洛雷托传教区。

M6.en: By age 18, she was convinced her calling was to be a missionary.
M6.zh: 18岁时，她确信自己的使命是成为传教士。

D9.en: She was a born organizer and a driving force in her activities.
D9.zh: 她天生是个组织者，是她所有活动中的推动力。

D11.en: She composed poems and had two articles published in a local newspaper.
D11.zh: 她创作诗歌，并在当地报纸上发表了两篇文章。

D2.en: She learned Hindi and Bengali from 1929 to 1931.
D2.zh: 1929到1931年间，她学习印地语和孟加拉语。

D14.en: She sang Christmas carols around a paper crib under stars on December 25, 1928.
D14.zh: 1928年圣诞节，她在星空下围着纸做的马槽唱圣诞颂歌。

D4.en: At 18, she prayed her weight was light enough not to hurt a man.
D4.zh: 18岁时，她祈祷自己的体重轻到不会让那个男人太痛苦。

D17.en: At 25, she found joy placing her hand on each dirty child's head.
D17.zh: 25岁时，她发现把手放在每个脏兮兮的孩子头上带来极大的快乐。

D15.en: At 15, she had a diving accident.
D15.zh: 15岁时，她遭遇了一次跳水事故。

D7.en: At about 15, she admonished her brother: 'It is your duty to love him; he is Christ's priest.'
D7.zh: 大约15岁时，她告诫哥哥：‘你有责任爱他、尊重他，他是基督的司铎。’

D3.en: At 11-12, he memorized key passages to quote to friends back in Kim Lien.
D3.zh: 11到12岁时，他背下关键段落，以便向金莲的朋友们引用。

D2.en: At 20, he was assigned as instructor of martial arts.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他被指派担任武术教官。

D13b.en: At 21, his application to the Colonial School was rejected.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他申请殖民学校被拒。

D9.en: At 11-12, he helped serve guests who came to lecture.
D9.zh: 11到12岁时，他帮忙招待来演讲的客人。

D12.en: At 21, he took a job selling newspapers with a fellow villager named Huang.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他和同村人黄一起卖报纸。

D15.en: At 10-11, he climbed hills, including Mount Chung.
D15.zh: 10到11岁时，他爬山，包括钟山。

D4.en: He took his pupils on walks in the woods and along the shoreline.
D4.zh: 他带学生去树林里和海边散步。

M4.en: At 11-12, he memorized key passages to quote to friends back in Kim Lien.
M4.zh: 11到12岁时，他背下关键段落，以便向金莲的朋友们引用。

D10.en: At 10-11, he joined others in weeping bitterly over their sacrifice.
D10.zh: 10到11岁时，他和大家一起为牺牲痛哭。

D7.en: He turned his latania hat upside down as a sign of the need to overthrow the status quo.
D7.zh: 他把他的拉塔尼亚帽子倒过来戴，作为需要推翻现状的标志。

D18.en: He took a job selling newspapers with a fellow villager from Kim Lien named Huang.
D18.zh: 他和一个来自金莲村、名叫黄的同乡找了份卖报纸的工作。

M1.en: He tried to memorize key passages so that he could quote them to friends.
M1.zh: 他试图记住关键段落，以便能向朋友们引用。

D16.en: He tried to memorize key passages so that he could quote them to friends.
D16.zh: 他试图记住关键段落，以便能向朋友们引用。

D19.en: He tried to memorize key passages.
D19.zh: 他试图记住关键段落。

D13a.en: His father assigned to him the new name Nguyen Tat Thanh.
D13a.zh: 他父亲给他起了新名字阮必成。

D1.en: At 18, he arrived and took his assigned seat when the bell rang.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他在铃声响起时到达并坐到了自己的座位上。

D8.en: An older student who had been instructed to keep an eye on him found it necessary to tie a string to his charge.
D8.zh: 一个被吩咐照看他的年长学生觉得有必要用绳子拴住他。

M5.en: At 11-12, he was irritated that most classical texts available dealt with Chinese, not Vietnamese history.
M5.zh: 11到12岁时，他恼火地发现能找到的经典大多是中文的，而非越南历史。

D14.en: At 18, he cut classes and gave haircuts to passersby, sometimes unsolicited.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他逃课和朋友一起给路人理发，有时不请自来。

D11.en: At 23-24, he wrote a poem on a postcard about fighting for one's compatriots.
D11.zh: 23到24岁时，他在明信片上写了一首诗，说要为同胞而战。

D19.en: At 14, he began a journal and kept it for life.
D19.zh: 14岁时，他开始写日记，并坚持了一辈子。

D12.en: At 7, he served early morning Mass daily.
D12.zh: 7岁时，他每天清晨帮忙做弥撒。

D13b.en: At 11, he arrived as a boarder at a seminary.
D13b.zh: 11岁时，他作为寄宿生进入神学院。

D14.en: At 5-6, he learned his first poem by heart.
D14.zh: 五六岁时，他背下了第一首诗。

D2.en: At 17, he prayed for his will to align perfectly with God's.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他祈祷自己的意志与天主完全一致。

D6.en: At 18-19, he could join Radini's study and action group.
D6.zh: 18到19岁时，他可以加入拉迪尼的学习和行动小组。

D7.en: At 17, he was rebuked for alleged arrogance and had to humble himself against his will.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他因被指傲慢而受训斥，不得不违心地谦卑。

D4.en: At 19, he asked permission to read Augusto Conti's History of Philosophy.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他请求允许阅读奥古斯托·孔蒂的《哲学史》。

D5.en: At 8, he crept up a creaking staircase to see a dead old woman.
D5.zh: 8岁时，他蹑手蹑脚地走上吱吱作响的楼梯，去看一位死去的老妇人。

D1.en: At 9, he translated Caesar's Gallic War and was slapped or made to kneel for errors.
D1.zh: 9岁时，他翻译凯撒的《高卢战记》，犯错就被打耳光或罚跪。

D10.en: At 9, he felt his heart endangered and had to leave quickly to hide his emotion.
D10.zh: 9岁时，他感到内心危险，必须尽快离开以免流露感情。

D9.en: At 18-19, he organized mass pilgrimages for the Holy Year of 1900.
D9.zh: 18到19岁时，他组织1900圣年的群众朝圣。

M1.en: At 14, his aim in life was to become a holy priest.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他的人生目标是成为圣洁的神父。

M6.en: At 21, he read both visits as Providential events.
M6.zh: 21岁时，他将两次访问视为天意事件。

D17.en: At 17, he felt orphaned by the death, writing 'I am left an orphan'.
D17.zh: 17岁时，他因死亡感到成了孤儿，写道‘我成了孤儿’。

D15.en: At 20, he scored high marks on the rifle range.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他在射击场获得高分。

D11.en: At 25, he edited thirty-nine volumes of St Charles Borromeo.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他编辑了圣查尔斯·博罗梅奥的39卷著作。

D13a.en: At 21, he chose to keep silent on doubtful points to avoid differing from orthodoxy.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他选择对有疑问之处保持沉默，以免偏离正统。

D16.en: At age 25, he repudiated an attitude and modified his motto to 'I will bend, but not break'.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他摒弃了一种态度，并将座右铭改为‘我弯而不折’。

D14.en: At 24, he constantly makes speeches and preaches in English.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他不断在各种场合演讲和用英语布道。

D17.en: At 19-21, he took over a children's religious class.
D17.zh: 19到21岁间，他接手了一个儿童宗教班。

D3.en: At 19, he wrote to his parents about reading Dostoevsky's speech on Pushkin.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他写信给父母说读了陀思妥耶夫斯基关于普希金的演讲。

D13a.en: At 23, he plunged into systematic theology, ignoring a suggestion to do historical work.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他埋头研究系统神学，无视了研究历史问题的建议。

D2.en: At 17, he spends almost the entire day sitting in the theological college.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他几乎整天坐在神学院里。

D4.en: At 12, he builds a subterranean cave and passageway.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他建造了一个地下洞穴和通道。

D11.en: At 22, he freely dramatized scenes or put himself in the position of the psalmist.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他自由地戏剧化场景或把自己代入诗篇作者的位置。

D15.en: As a small child, he got used to being the strongest and most agile of the three youngest children.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他习惯了成为三个最小孩子中最强壮最灵活的。

D10.en: At 16, he recalls his friend's passionate indignation and deep, spontaneous anger.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他回忆起朋友的激烈愤慨和深沉的、自发的愤怒。

D18.en: At 12, he gleaned often and successfully, gathering quite a lot.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他经常成功拾穗，收获颇丰。

D7.en: At 16, he was very narrow-mindedly right-wing, almost like he forgot his swastika.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他非常狭隘地右翼，几乎忘了自己的纳粹标志。

D16.en: At 16, he asked what would become of Germany if its best leaders were killed.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他问如果德国最优秀的领袖被杀，德国会怎样。

D13b.en: At 17, he followed family tradition by beginning university studies with a year in Tübingen.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他遵循家族传统，在蒂宾根开始大学第一年。

D5.en: At 17, he said he would go for a few days until receiving a reply, but would return if objected.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他说会先去几天直到收到回复，如果反对就回来。

M7.en: At 25, he based his entire class on the idea of a worshipping community.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他将整个班级建立在敬拜团体的理念上。

D20.en: At 25-26, he noted youth secretaries were usurping issues and duplicating work from Geneva.
D20.zh: 25到26岁时，他指出青年干事篡夺议题并重复日内瓦的工作。

D19.en: As a small child, his day followed a disciplined pattern; he always knew where he was.
D19.zh: 幼年时，他的日常遵循有纪律的模式；他总知道自己在哪里。

D1.en: At 6, he wanted to do everything the older children could.
D1.zh: 6岁时，他想做所有大孩子能做的事。

D9.en: He received an invitation from Memorial Methodist Church.
D9.zh: 他收到了纪念卫理公会的邀请。

M1.en: He wanted to do everything the older children could.
M1.zh: 他想做所有大孩子能做的事。

M2.en: He found theology attractive because it offered a chance to achieve something special.
M2.zh: 他觉得神学有吸引力，因为它提供了取得特殊成就的机会。

M6.en: He discovered Barth during his studies in 1925, deepening his thought.
M6.zh: 他在1925年学习期间发现了巴特，思想得到首次决定性深化。

D20X.en: He had no special friendships until his student years.
D20X.zh: 他在学生时代之前没有特别的友谊。

M5.en: He found theology attractive because it offered a chance to achieve something special.
M5.zh: 他觉得神学有吸引力，因为它提供了取得特殊成就的机会。

M8.en: He may have heard his mother sing Beethoven's Gellert Lieder with great enthusiasm.
M8.zh: 他可能听到母亲热情地唱贝多芬的格勒特歌曲。

D8.en: He won particular respect by fending off unreasonable demands.
D8.zh: 他通过拒绝不合理的要求赢得了特别的尊重。

M3.en: He found theology attractive because it offered a chance to achieve something special.
M3.zh: 他觉得神学有吸引力，因为它提供了取得特殊成就的机会。

M4.en: The peace question and the new wave of defamation in Germany assured his loyalty to the organization.
M4.zh: 和平问题与德国新的诽谤浪潮确保了他对该组织的忠诚。

D3.en: At 22, he avidly listened to Herrmann's lectures on Dogmatics I and ethics.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他如饥似渴地听赫尔曼讲授教义学I和伦理学。

D12.en: At 15-16, he underwent confirmation instruction under Robert Aeschbacher.
D12.zh: 15到16岁间，他在罗伯特·艾施巴赫指导下接受坚信礼教导。

D13a.en: At 20, he compromised and went to Berlin.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他妥协了，去了柏林。

D6.en: At 22-23, he acquired a small group of lifelong close friends.
D6.zh: 22到23岁间，他结交了一小群终身亲密的朋友。

D9.en: At 22, he was chosen as editorial assistant of Die Christliche Welt.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他被选为《基督教世界》的编辑助理。

D18.en: At 18-20, he earned money by giving private violin lessons.
D18.zh: 18到20岁间，他通过私人小提琴课赚钱。

M4.en: At 25, he took the lead in his village to force the local textile industry to unionize.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他在村里带头迫使当地纺织业成立工会。

D16.en: At 25, he responded with his own 'Open Letter'.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他用自己的公开信回应。

D11.en: At 15, he wrote his longest play, 'Leonardo of Montenuova, or Freedom and Love'.
D11.zh: 15岁时，他写了最长的剧本《蒙特努奥瓦的莱昂纳多，或自由与爱》。

D14.en: At 13, he shouted, shrieked, and barked loudly in solemn silence, startling everyone.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他在肃静中大喊、尖叫、狂吠，让所有人都看向他。

D13b.en: As a university student, he rebelled against his father's views and became a committed liberal.
D13b.zh: 大学期间，他反抗父亲的观点，成为一个坚定的自由派。

D7.en: At 13, his teacher Kacher pulled out two of his hairs, which he now keeps in his museum.
D7.zh: 13岁时，老师卡彻拔了他两根头发，现在他保存在自己的博物馆里。

M1.en: He wrote two versions of a commentary on the Epistle to the Romans that made his name.
M1.zh: 他写了两个版本的《罗马书》注释，这让他成名。

M2.en: As a teenager and young adult, he rebelled against his father's views.
M2.zh: 十几岁到二十多岁时，他反抗父亲的观点。

D5.en: At 25, he was notorious as the 'red pastor'.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他因‘红色牧师’的名声而臭名昭著。

D1.en: In his mid-20s to mid-30s, he wrote two versions of a commentary on the Epistle to the Romans that made his name.
D1.zh: 25到35岁间，他写了两个版本的《罗马书》注释，这让他成名。

M6.en: In his mid-20s to mid-30s, he wrote two versions of a commentary on the Epistle to the Romans.
M6.zh: 25到35岁间，他写了两个版本的《罗马书》注释。

D20.en: As a child and young adult, he maintained a wide circle of friends and acquaintances.
D20.zh: 幼年到青年时期，他维持着广泛的交友圈。

D15.en: At 18, he climbed the Titlis and the Urirotstock on a school trip.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他在学校旅行中爬了铁力士山和乌里罗斯托克山。

D19.en: He wrote two versions of a commentary on the Epistle to the Romans.
D19.zh: 他写了两个版本的《罗马书》注释。

D4.en: He pursued interests from music to military history.
D4.zh: 他追求从音乐到军事史的各种兴趣。

M7.en: At 25, he supported local workers and trade unions.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他支持当地工人和工会。

M8.en: At 25-35, he wrote two versions of a commentary on the Epistle to the Romans.
M8.zh: 25到35岁间，他写了两个版本的《罗马书》注释。

M3.en: He was a committed liberal.
M3.zh: 他是一个坚定的自由派。

D10.en: He was still sore about the punishment.
D10.zh: 他对惩罚仍然耿耿于怀。

D12.en: At 13, he was shocked to hear his father announce it was time for him to start work.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他震惊地听到父亲宣布他该开始工作了。

D18.en: At 15-16, he got a job in a food laboratory.
D18.zh: 15到16岁时，他在一个食品实验室找到工作。

D2.en: At 6-12, he rose hours before classmates to serve Mass for a Salesian priest.
D2.zh: 6到12岁时，他比同学早起床几个小时，为一位慈幼会神父做弥撒。

D11.en: At 13, he gave catechesis on the sacrament.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他讲授圣事要理。

D15.en: As a child, he liked to kick around with friends but wasn't much good due to flat feet.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他喜欢和朋友踢球，但因为扁平足踢得不太好。

D14.en: As a teenager, he loved to dance and was good at the tango.
D14.zh: 十几岁时，他喜欢跳舞，尤其擅长探戈。

D7.en: At 22-23, he asked to be sent to Japan as a missionary.
D7.zh: 22到23岁时，他请求被派往日本做传教士。

D1.en: At 21, he recovered but impaired breathing ended his hope of working overseas.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他康复了，但呼吸受损使他无法去海外工作。

D13b.en: At 22, he entered the Society as a novice on 11 March 1958.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他于1958年3月11日进入修会成为初学生。

D3.en: At 25-28, he discussed the Council with the future provincial of Chile, Fernando Montes.
D3.zh: 25到28岁时，他与未来的智利省会长费尔南多·蒙特斯讨论了大公会议。

D4.en: He started a six-year vocational course at the Escuela Nacional de Educación Técnica, earning a diploma as a chemical technician.
D4.zh: 他在国立技术教育学校开始六年的职业课程，获得化学技术员文凭。

D6.en: He studied with Argentine Jesuits at Casa Loyola in Santiago, Chile, in 1960.
D6.zh: 1960年，他在智利圣地亚哥的洛约拉之家与阿根廷耶稣会士一起学习。

D5.en: He nearly died from a lung infection in 1957.
D5.zh: 1957年，他因肺部感染差点死去。

M6.en: At 17, he had an experience in confession that unlocked his vocation.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他在告解中经历了一次启发他圣召的体验。

M4.en: He asked to be considered for a mission there, enraptured by the idea.
M4.zh: 他深受吸引，请求被考虑派往那里传教。

D13a.en: At 19, his parents acquiesced, but it took some years for Regina to accept.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他父母默许了，但里贾纳花了好几年才接受。

D9.en: He became determined to stay and lead the change.
D9.zh: 他下定决心留下来领导变革。

D10.en: Delirious with pain, he begged visitors to explain what was happening.
D10.zh: 他痛得神志不清，恳求访客解释发生了什么。

D11.en: At 20, wrote a meditation on justice in history in spring 1940.
D11.zh: 20岁时于1940年春撰写关于历史正义的沉思录。

D2.en: At 22-25, he secretly studied philosophy and theology at the chemical factory where he worked.
D2.zh: 22到25岁间，他在工作的化工厂偷偷学习哲学和神学。

D14.en: At age 13, he had a nonspeaking part in a summer pageant.
D14.zh: 13岁时，他在夏季庆典中扮演了一个无声角色。

D1.en: At 21, his father died and he pursued a priestly vocation.
D1.zh: 21岁时父亲去世，他走上神职道路。

D6.en: At 19, he danced and drank wine with classmates at a celebration.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他和同学在庆祝活动中跳舞喝酒。

D3.en: At 19, he passed matriculation exams to study Polish philology.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他通过了波兰语文学的毕业考试。

D5.en: At 23-24, he was struck by a German truck and hospitalized.
D5.zh: 23到24岁时，他被一辆德国卡车撞伤并住院。

D7.en: At 19, he began clandestine studies and underground cultural resistance.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他开始秘密学习和地下文化抵抗活动。

M1.en: At 19, he passed two exams to continue matriculation in Polish philology.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他通过了两场考试，得以继续波兰语文学的学业。

M6.en: At 19, he was seen deep in prayer before the tomb of Jagiello.
M6.zh: 19岁时，有人看到他在雅盖沃墓前虔诚祈祷。

M4.en: At 19, he began clandestine studies and underground cultural resistance.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他开始秘密学习和地下文化抵抗活动。

D13a.en: At 22, he enrolled in the clandestine seminary run by the archbishop.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他进入大主教秘密开办的神学院。

D18.en: At 19-20, he worked as a store messenger for a restaurant.
D18.zh: 19到20岁时，他在一家餐馆做店铺信差。

D13b.en: At age 14, converted to Catholicism and was baptized on August 25, 1940.
D13b.zh: 14岁时改信天主教，于1940年8月25日受洗。

D20.en: At 25, elected vice president of university's Students' Fraternal Aid Society.
D20.zh: 25岁时当选大学学生互助会副主席。

D19.en: He wrote his first essays and poems in the 1940s at age 20-25.
D19.zh: 他在20到25岁间写了首批散文和诗。

D20X.en: At 19-25, he engaged in clandestine cultural resistance.
D20X.zh: 在19到25岁间，他参与秘密文化抵抗。

M8.en: At age 21, he read works of St. Louis Grignon de Montfort.
M8.zh: 21岁时，他阅读了圣路易·格里尼翁·德蒙福的著作。

D3.en: As a small child, he held out his hands to feel the wind and talked to 'Monsieur Wind'.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他伸出手感受风，并和‘风先生’说话。

D11.en: As a small child, he played a game answering his own question with a drawn-out 'Oo-oo-oo' as the wind's voice.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他玩自问自答的游戏，用拖长的‘呜-呜-呜’模仿风声。

D14.en: Before he could talk, he chanted 'Dah-hou!' imitating church bells.
D14.zh: 还不会说话时，他就吟唱‘哒-呼！’模仿教堂钟声。

D10.en: At 24, he was overwhelmed with disgust for the life he was leading.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他对自己的生活感到极度厌恶。

D6.en: At 19, he joined the Young Communist League under the name Frank Swift.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他以弗兰克·斯威夫特为化名加入共青团。

D4.en: At 21, he worked at Radio City on the 86th floor between 11 AM and 8 PM.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他在无线电城86楼工作，从上午11点到晚上8点。

D13b.en: At 25, he was told he could not write his doctoral thesis on Gerard Manley Hopkins.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他被告知不能以杰拉德·曼利·霍普金斯的诗歌作为博士论文题目。

D5.en: At 18-19, he threw a brick through a shop window and got arrested for riding on a car's running board.
D5.zh: 18到19岁时，他扔砖头砸碎商店橱窗，并因站在汽车踏脚板上被逮捕。

D2.en: At 23, he decided to attend Corpus Christi without receiving the sacrament.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他决定去科珀斯克里斯蒂但不领圣体。

D17.en: At 11, he was fattened up with butter and milk and given love and care.
D17.zh: 11岁时，他被黄油和牛奶养得胖胖的，还得到了关爱。

D15.en: At 20-21, he took track, running, swimming, and rowing.
D15.zh: 20到21岁间，他参加了田径、跑步、游泳和划船。

D9.en: At 24, he was choosing the form and language and selecting models.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他在选择形式和语言，并挑选和拒绝模型。

M1.en: As a schoolboy, he had an uncanny knack of coming first and winning prizes.
M1.zh: 作为学生，他有一种不可思议的能力，总是得第一并获奖。

M8.en: He prayed at Santa Sabina after a personal crisis.
M8.zh: 他在个人危机后在圣萨比娜祈祷。

D1.en: At 20, he walked with a purpose, having shed his Cambridge lethargy.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他走路有目的，摆脱了在剑桥的懒散。

M6.en: In his 20s, his sense of duty to his own history was too strong and urgent.
M6.zh: 20多岁时，他对自身历史的责任感太强太紧迫。

D19.en: He began a journal in 1939, starting a lifelong habit.
D19.zh: 1939年他开始写日记，养成了终身习惯。

M4.en: He imagined he belonged in England and had responsibilities there.
M4.zh: 他想象自己属于英国，在那里有责任。

D16.en: He said confessions are only valid in literature if they confess God.
D16.zh: 他说文学中的忏悔只有忏悔上帝才有效。

D20.en: He became editor of the Columbia yearbook for 1937.
D20.zh: 他成为1937年哥伦比亚大学年鉴的编辑。

D20X.en: At 21-22, he attended a meeting where Berryman read his own long poem, after which the club disbanded.
D20X.zh: 21到22岁时，他参加了一次会议，会上贝里曼朗读了自己的长诗，之后俱乐部解散了。

D8.en: He wrote an article titled 'Wählt Hitler' in 1932.
D8.zh: 1932年他写了一篇题为《选择希特勒》的文章。

M5.en: He wrote an article titled 'Wählt Hitler' in 1932.
M5.zh: 1932年他写了一篇题为《选择希特勒》的文章。

M7.en: At 17, he wrote a poem titled 'Wählt Hitler' for his school magazine.
M7.zh: 17岁时，他为校刊写了一首题为《选择希特勒》的诗。

D2.en: At 13-14, he had private confirmation instruction three times, twice with siblings and once alone.
D2.zh: 13到14岁时，他接受了三次私人坚信礼指导，前两年与兄弟姐妹一起，最后一年独自一人。

D12.en: At 21, he spent the summer filling the vacancy at St. John's after his father's death.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他夏天在父亲去世后填补了圣约翰教堂的空缺。

D6.en: At 22, he made contacts in the Divinity School and the more intimidating College.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他在神学院和更令人畏惧的学院里建立了联系。

D14.en: At 23, he shook with pride when editor Ellery Sedgwick accepted two of his manuscripts in 1916.
D14.zh: 23岁时，1916年编辑埃勒里·塞奇威克接受了他的两篇手稿，他激动得发抖。

D9.en: At 15, he requested admission to the Evangelical Pro-Seminar, promising to obey all instructions.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他申请进入福音派预备神学院，承诺遵守所有指示和要求。

D13b.en: At 22, he preached several times at Saint John's and at the First Methodist Church.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他在圣约翰教堂和第一卫理公会教堂讲道数次。

D3.en: At 22, he invoked William James's pragmatism to justify the act of belief.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他引用威廉·詹姆斯的实用主义来为信仰行为辩护。

M1.en: At 23, he valued influence for his ideas and notice for his person more than money.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他认为思想的影响力和个人的关注比金钱更重要。

D20.en: At 19, he edited the Keryx.
D20.zh: 19岁时，他编辑《凯里克斯》。

D18.en: He wrote a barrage of prose to augment his minuscule income.
D18.zh: 他写了一大堆文章来增加微薄的收入。

D16.en: He preached twice that day, in German and in English, taking John 17:21 as his text.
D16.zh: 他那天用德语和英语各讲道一次，以约翰福音17章21节为经文。

D1.en: He took a pastorate instead of a Yale doctorate due to obligation and financial responsibility for his widowed mother.
D1.zh: 他因教职义务和赡养寡母的经济责任，选择了牧师职位而非耶鲁的博士学位。

M2.en: He had financial responsibility for his widowed mother.
M2.zh: 他承担了赡养寡母的经济责任。

M6.en: After a sermon silenced him, he announced his intention to study for the ministry.
M6.zh: 一次讲道让他完全沉默，之后他宣布打算学习神职。

M3.en: He exhibited a special affinity for James.
M3.zh: 他对詹姆斯表现出特别的亲近。

D10.en: As a small child, he reached for cheese with his left hand, was rapped on the knuckles by his father, and ran upstairs enraged.
D10.zh: 幼年时，他用左手去拿奶酪，被父亲敲了指关节，愤怒地跑上楼。

D7.en: As a teenager, he had no interest in following his father's path into the ministry.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他对继承父业从事神职毫无兴趣。

D4.en: He worked for a time in a sewing-machine factory.
D4.zh: 他曾在一家缝纫机厂工作过一段时间。

D15.en: He had a run-in with a bull at a relative's farm in Alhambra, Illinois.
D15.zh: 他在伊利诺伊州阿尔罕布拉亲戚家的农场与一头公牛发生了冲突。

D11.en: At 24, he analyzed Billy Sunday's revival in Detroit.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他分析了比利·桑戴在底特律的复兴运动。

M4.en: At 23, he wrote letters to prominent German-Americans asking for their reactions.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他写信给著名的德裔美国人，恳请他们阅读并回复意见。

D6.en: At 21, he began going out regularly to preaching services.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他开始定期外出参加布道活动。

D13b.en: At 10, he was required to memorize the Westminster Shorter Catechism.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，他被要求背诵《威斯敏斯特小教理问答》。

D12.en: At 20, he took charge of a run-down 400-acre farm after Crook died.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他在克鲁克去世后接管了一个破败的400英亩农场。

D7.en: At 15, he said the people using the place were just fanatics who talked Dad into it.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他说那些使用场地的人只是说服了爸爸的狂热分子。

D3.en: Despite never finishing high school, he was eager for more Christian education.
D3.zh: 尽管没读完高中，他仍渴望更多基督教教育。

D15.en: At 13-15, he played first base on his high school team and a few innings with a semi-pro team.
D15.zh: 13到15岁间，他在高中队打一垒，还和半职业队打过几局。

D14.en: At 25, he preached at the inaugural rally on May 27, 1944, to 3,000 young people.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他在1944年5月27日的就职集会上向3000名年轻人布道。

D2.en: At 19-22, he was willing to practice his sermons in an empty auditorium.
D2.zh: 19到22岁间，他愿意在空礼堂里练习布道。

D9.en: At 20, he was elected class president and named outstanding evangelist.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他被选为班长并被提名为杰出布道者。

D18.en: At 18, he spent the summer selling Fuller Brush products in the Carolinas.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他夏天在卡罗来纳州推销富勒刷具产品。

D13a.en: At 18, his deep reluctance to challenge authority made it hard for him to act on his inclinations.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他极度不愿挑战权威，这使他难以按自己的意愿行事。

D1.en: At 18, when he lost all the money he made during the first week, he refused to seek help from home.
D1.zh: 18岁时，当他把第一周赚的钱全赔光时，他拒绝向家里求助。

D16.en: At 17-18, he was introduced as a new convert who wanted to tell what Jesus had done for him.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，他被介绍为一名新信徒，想讲述耶稣为他做的事。

D5.en: At 16, he drove a car right up on the sidewalk in downtown Charlotte.
D5.zh: 16岁时，他在夏洛特市中心把车开上了人行道。

D4.en: He worked as a bellhop, waiter, caddy, and high-speed dishwasher.
D4.zh: 他当过门童、服务员、球童和高速洗碗工。

M3.en: He wrote home telling his mother he longed to be like certain people.
M3.zh: 他写信回家告诉母亲他渴望像某些人一样。

D20.en: His peers elected him president of the eleven-member senior class.
D20.zh: 他被同学选为十一名毕业班的主席。

D2.en: At 14, he received his diploma and started to teach.
D2.zh: 14岁时，他获得毕业证书并开始教书。

D3.en: At 19-20, he learned modern sciences, unprecedented among eastern ulama.
D3.zh: 19到20岁时，他学习现代科学，这在东部学者中前所未有。

D9.en: At 19-20, he first taught modern physical sciences alongside religious sciences.
D9.zh: 19到20岁时，他首次同时教授现代物理科学和宗教科学。

D7.en: As a child, he challenged older students or corrected scholars.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他挑战年长学生或纠正学者。

M6.en: At 14-15, he dreamed of the Prophet, who promised knowledge of the Qur'an.
M6.zh: 14到15岁时，他梦见先知，先知承诺赐予古兰经知识。

D16.en: At 14-16, he answered difficult questions from Shaikh Emin without hesitation.
D16.zh: 14到16岁时，他毫不犹豫地回答了谢赫·艾敏的难题。

M4.en: He answered doubts raised about Islam.
M4.zh: 他回答了关于伊斯兰教的质疑。

D14.en: At about 30-31, he delivered public speeches to inform people.
D14.zh: 大约30到31岁时，他发表公开演讲以告知民众。

D1.en: After about a year, he set off again to continue his full-time studies.
D1.zh: 大约一年后，他再次出发继续全日制学习。

D13a.en: He rejected an offer to annoy the sultan and tell him the truth.
D13a.zh: 他拒绝了一个提议，以激怒苏丹并告诉他真相。

D6.en: He frequented Tahir Pasha's residence where intellectuals gathered.
D6.zh: 他常去塔希尔帕夏的住所，那里是知识分子聚集的地方。

D20X.en: He declined positions as parliament member, chief preacher, and religious affairs officer.
D20X.zh: 他拒绝了议员、首席传教士和宗教事务官员的职位。

M7.en: As a youth, he worked to secure the unity and progress of the Ottoman Empire and Islamic world.
M7.zh: 青年时，他致力于维护奥斯曼帝国和伊斯兰世界的团结与进步。

M1.en: At 14, he received his diploma and started teaching.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他获得了文凭并开始教书。

D5.en: He showed proverbial courage and boldness in his youth.
D5.zh: 他在年轻时表现出众所周知的勇气和大胆。

D11.en: He imagined visiting the Prophet Muhammad by the bridge of Sirat.
D11.zh: 他想象着去西拉特桥上拜访先知穆罕默德。

D17.en: He dipped his bread in soup and gave crumbs to ants.
D17.zh: 他把面包蘸汤，然后把面包屑喂给蚂蚁。

D12.en: At 13, he ran errands for Nursi.
D12.zh: 13岁时，他为努尔西跑腿。

D10.en: At 4, he felt infinite deprivation and loss from not seeing his mother.
D10.zh: 4岁时，他因见不到母亲而感到无尽的失落和缺失。

D3.en: He developed a lifelong interest in Chinese philosophy.
D3.zh: 他培养了对中国哲学的终身兴趣。

D2.en: From ages 3 to 10, he received private lessons in English, French, German, and Jewish subjects.
D2.zh: 3到10岁时，他接受英语、法语、德语和犹太科目的私人课程。

M3.en: At 20, he had a sophomoric hero worship of socialist Ferdinand Lassalle.
M3.zh: 20岁时，他对社会主义者费迪南德·拉萨尔有种幼稚的英雄崇拜。

D7.en: At 16, he felt raging hatred for the official morality and education around him.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他对周围的官方道德和教育感到强烈憎恨。

D13a.en: As long as he lived with his grandparents, he was externally well rooted in Judaism.
D13a.zh: 他和祖父母住在一起时，至少在表面上深深扎根于犹太教。

D4.en: In his mid-20s, his interest in Hasidism gained momentum with his wife's support.
D4.zh: 25岁左右，他在妻子支持下对哈西德主义的兴趣增强。

M4.en: At 18-19, he actively participated in a secret conference of Polish students.
M4.zh: 18到19岁时，他积极参与波兰学生的秘密会议。

D11.en: At 24-25, he published a cycle of three poems affirming cultural rebirth.
D11.zh: 24到25岁时，他发表了三首关于文化重生的组诗。

D14.en: At 21, he gave an impassioned speech before the committee.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他在委员会前发表了慷慨激昂的演讲。

D16.en: At 23, he called upon readers of Die Welt to prepare joyously with thoughtful debate.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他呼吁《世界报》的读者通过深思熟虑的辩论来愉快地准备。

M1.en: At 18, he published four essays on Vienna's avant-garde poets in a Warsaw journal.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他在华沙一家文学杂志上发表了四篇关于维也纳先锋派诗人的文章。

D20.en: At 23, he co-founded an organization with Chaim Weizmann and others.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他与哈伊姆·魏茨曼等人共同创立了一个组织。

D6.en: At 23, he was appointed editor-in-chief of Die Welt by Herzl.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他被赫茨尔任命为《世界报》主编。

D20X.en: After four months as editor of the weekly, he suddenly resigned.
D20X.zh: 他担任周刊主编四个月后突然辞职。

D15.en: As a teenager, he wandered through mountains and valleys with woods and lakes.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他漫游于山林溪谷之间，沿途有树林和湖泊。

D3.en: As a small child, he was fascinated by astronomy and had a map of the universe in his bedroom.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他对天文学着迷，卧室里挂着一张宇宙地图。

D6.en: At 21, he traveled to Kislovodsk to meet his distant cousin, the Sixth Lubavitcher Rebbe.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他前往基斯洛沃茨克会见他的远房表亲，第六代卢巴维奇拉比。

D13b.en: At 25-26, his wedding was held in Warsaw, Poland.
D13b.zh: 25到26岁间，婚礼在华沙举行。

D5.en: In his 30s, Tillich coordinated a transatlantic banking arrangement to move funds to help Jews in Stalinist Russia.
D5.zh: 30多岁时，蒂利希协调跨大西洋银行安排，转移资金帮助斯大林治下的犹太人。

D7.en: At 25, he repeatedly emphasized to three officials that he would under no conditions give up his religious practices.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他向三名官员反复强调，他绝不会放弃自己的宗教习俗。

D13a.en: He was told he would regret giving up his profession.
D13a.zh: 他被告知会后悔放弃自己的职业。

D16.en: At age 25-29, he engaged in cynical and provocative debates with Hasidim who entered the store.
D16.zh: 25到29岁间，他与进入商店的哈西德派进行愤世嫉俗且挑衅性的辩论。

D18.en: He briefly opened a clothing store that quickly went out of business.
D18.zh: 他短暂开了一家服装店，但很快就倒闭了。

D9.en: He was challenged by the Rebbe about strengthening Jewish life at Cambridge.
D9.zh: 他被拉比质问在剑桥加强犹太生活的事。

M4.en: At 25, he insisted to officials he would never give up his religious practices.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他向官员强调绝不放弃宗教实践。

D11.en: As a small child, the vision of future Redemption began to form in his imagination.
D11.zh: 幼年时，未来救赎的愿景开始在他想象中形成。

D1.en: As a small child, he became very ill with polio.
D1.zh: 幼年时，他得了小儿麻痹症，病得很重。

D5.en: As a small child, his flannel pajamas caught fire while warming himself.
D5.zh: 幼年时，他烤火时法兰绒睡衣着火了。

D7.en: As a small child, he stubbornly refused to stop warming himself by the brazier.
D7.zh: 幼年时，他倔强地不肯离开火盆取暖。

D6.en: He organized the Literary and Dramatic Society and chaired the Cultural and Debating Society.
D6.zh: 他组织了文学戏剧社，并主持了文化与辩论社。

D4.en: He invited Trevor Huddleston to speak at the college.
D4.zh: 他邀请特雷弗·赫德尔斯顿来学院演讲。

D3.en: Encouraged by his father, he developed a love of reading.
D3.zh: 在父亲鼓励下，他养成了阅读爱好。

D2.en: He was among a handful promoted to Standard Six.
D2.zh: 他是少数升到六年级的学生之一。

D18.en: He took his first university degree for the extra money it would earn him as a teacher.
D18.zh: 他攻读第一个大学学位是为了当老师能多赚钱。

D15.en: As a teenager, he was taught jogging and boxing by an older friend.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他被一位年长的朋友带着跑步和学拳击。

D10.en: At about 14, he said it was one of the worst nights of his life.
D10.zh: 大约14岁时，他说那是他一生中最糟糕的夜晚之一。

M4.en: At about 10-12, he and his friends waved goodbye to war convoys.
M4.zh: 大约10到12岁时，他和朋友们向开赴战场的车队挥手告别。

M1.en: At 20-22, he took his first university degree for extra teacher pay.
M1.zh: 20到22岁时，他为了教师加薪而获得了第一个大学学位。

M7.en: At about 10-12, he and his friends waved goodbye to war convoys.
M7.zh: 大约10到12岁时，他和朋友们向开赴战场的车队挥手告别。

D17.en: He gave away one of his two sweaters to a boy with none and swore his sister to silence.
D17.zh: 他把自己穿的两件毛衣中的一件送给了一个没毛衣的男孩，并让妹妹格洛丽亚保密。

D14.en: He was fascinated by movies starring African-Americans, especially Stormy Weather.
D14.zh: 他对非裔美国人主演的电影着迷，尤其是《暴风雨天气》。

D16.en: He joined the debating team and won the school's prize for best speaker in 1949.
D16.zh: 他加入了辩论队，并在1949年获得了学校的最佳演讲者奖。

D9.en: For two years he organized the Literary and Dramatic Society and chaired the Cultural and Debating Society.
D9.zh: 他组织了两年文学戏剧协会，并主持了文化与辩论协会。

D13a.en: At 25, he was told by the education supervisor he was in line for a principalship.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，教育主管告诉他，他有望成为校长。

M6.en: He now believes he has a vocation to the priesthood.
M6.zh: 他现在相信自己有成为神职人员的使命。

D12.en: At 15, he was forced to earn a living.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他被迫谋生。

D2.en: At 21-23, he resumed studying dars-i nizami with two Deobandi ulama.
D2.zh: 21到23岁间，他重新学习尼扎米课程，师从两位德奥班德学者。

D3.en: At 22-23, he started learning German but quit after a few months.
D3.zh: 22到23岁间，他开始学德语，但几个月后放弃了。

D11.en: At 11, he translated Qasim Amin's book from Arabic to Urdu.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他把卡西姆·阿明的书从阿拉伯语翻译成乌尔都语。

D13a.en: At 25, he left Al-Jam'iat.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他离开了阿尔贾米亚特。

D9.en: At 22, he became editor of Al-Jam'iat.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他担任了《贾米亚特报》的编辑。

D7.en: At 22, his clean-shaven face caused an uproar among ulama.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他刮净的脸在学者中引起轩然大波。

D14.en: At 21, he wrote a book on Hyderabad titled Dawlat-i Astfiyah wa hukumat-i Britaniyah.
D14.zh: 21岁时，他写了一本关于海得拉巴的书，名为《阿西菲亚政府与英国政府》。

D4.en: At 23-24, he set out to provide a rational exposition of jihad.
D4.zh: 23到24岁间，他开始对圣战进行理性阐述。

M4.en: At 24, he became convinced his calling was not journalism but a higher vocation.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他确信自己的使命不是新闻业，而是更高的天职。

D3.en: He was predisposed to mystical life when he began his studies in Qom.
D3.zh: 他开始在库姆学习时，就倾向于神秘生活。

D2.en: At 15, he began seriously learning Arabic grammar with Morteza.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他认真跟莫尔特扎学习阿拉伯语语法。

D9.en: At 17, after consulting his brother, it was decided he would go to Isfahan.
D9.zh: 17岁时，与哥哥商量后，决定他去伊斯法罕。

D14.en: He chose Qom's Feyziyeh Seminary for its large audience of locals and pilgrims.
D14.zh: 他选择了库姆的菲齐耶神学院，因为那里有大量当地人和朝圣者听众。

M6.en: He prayed for total separation from other-than-God and attachment to God.
M6.zh: 他祈求完全脱离非主之物，依附于主。

M1.en: In 1928, he won a government scholarship to study in France.
M1.zh: 1928年，他获得了政府奖学金去法国学习。

D6.en: At 18-22, he joined the Association of Muslim Students of Esfahan University.
D6.zh: 18到22岁间，他加入了伊斯法罕大学穆斯林学生协会。

D4.en: At about 22-25, he obtained a master's degree in pharmacy at Tehran University.
D4.zh: 大约22到25岁间，他在德黑兰大学获得了药学硕士学位。

D15.en: He could beat other boys in wrestling and was the local champion at leapfrog.
D15.zh: 他摔跤能赢其他男孩，跳背游戏是当地冠军。

D13b.en: At age seven, he was sent to a local maktab run by Akhund Mollah Abolqassem.
D13b.zh: 七岁时，他被送到阿洪德·毛拉·阿博尔加塞姆办的私塾。

D11.en: He wrote many books, including The Ascension of the Traveller and the Prayer of the Mystics.
D11.zh: 他写了很多书，包括《旅行者的升霄与神秘主义者的祈祷》。

D1.en: He told Ha'eri politely but firmly to speak more softly while studying Suyuti.
D1.zh: 他在学习苏尤蒂时，礼貌但坚定地请哈埃里说话小声些。

D5.en: He was described as an energetic, dare-devil and intelligent companion.
D5.zh: 他被描述为一个精力充沛、胆大妄为且聪明的伙伴。

D12.en: At 15, he dropped out of school after his father died and began working as a journalist.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他因父亲去世而辍学，开始做记者。

D3.en: At 18-22, he attended courses taught by Taha Husayn, who persuaded him to study in both disciplines.
D3.zh: 18到22岁间，他听了塔哈·侯赛因的课，对方说服他学习两个学科。

D5.en: At 20, a shattered love affair pushed him to leave and travel to Istanbul and later London to resume medical studies.
D5.zh: 20岁时，一次破碎的恋情促使他离开，先去了伊斯坦布尔，后来到伦敦继续学医。

D7.en: At 6, he rejected a choice and attended the kuttab instead.
D7.zh: 6岁时，他拒绝了一个选择，转而去了库塔布学校。

D18.en: At 15, he dropped out of school after his father died and began working as a journalist.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他因父亲去世而辍学，开始做记者。

D15.en: As a boy of about 8-12, he and his companions would playfully jump into waters from the school rooftop.
D15.zh: 大约8到12岁时，他和玩伴们会从学校屋顶跳进水里玩。

D10.en: He felt relief when his mother vetoed an idea, preferring to leave his injury to God.
D10.zh: 他感到庆幸，因为母亲否决了一个想法，宁愿将他的伤势交给真主。

D13b.en: In 1926, he received a certificate in religious training in the Deobandi (or Hanafi) legal tradition.
D13b.zh: 1926年，他获得了迪奥班德（或哈乃斐）教法传统的宗教培训证书。

D16.en: At 23 in 1949, he wrote to a friend that he found America extremely materialistic and alienated.
D16.zh: 1949年23岁时，他写信给朋友说，他觉得美国极其物质主义和疏离。

M2.en: At 13, his father died.
M2.zh: 13岁时，他的父亲去世了。

M4.en: At 16, he joined the Khilafat movement.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他加入了哈里发运动。

M6.en: At 15, he acted with a sense of destiny and mission.
M6.zh: 15岁时，他带着使命感和宿命感行事。

D19.en: As a child aged 6-12, he kept his books in a special tin box to protect them.
D19.zh: 6到12岁时，他把书放在一个特制的铁盒里以防虫防尘。

D2.en: At 20-22, he resumed medical studies and received a diploma in bacteriology.
D2.zh: 20到22岁间，他重新学医并获得了细菌学文凭。

D9.en: At 23, he decided to abandon his literary career for Egyptian politics.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他决定放弃文学生涯，更积极地参与埃及政治。

D14.en: At 15, he began publishing poems in local literary magazines.
D14.zh: 15岁时，他开始在当地文学杂志上发表诗歌。

D17.en: At about 28, he married a French woman, his loyal reader.
D17.zh: 大约28岁时，他娶了一位法国女子，他的忠实读者。

M3.en: He found a mentor whose life encapsulated everything he aspired to be.
M3.zh: 他找到了一位导师，其生活体现了他渴望成为的一切。

D11.en: His sermons were published in a groundbreaking treatise called al-Jihad fi al-Islam.
D11.zh: 他的布道被出版成开创性论文《伊斯兰中的圣战》。

D13a.en: At age 6, his parents debated whether to send him to a modern school or a traditional kuttab.
D13a.zh: 6岁时，他的父母争论是否送他去现代学校还是传统学堂。

D20.en: At age 12, he was elected president of the school's literary society.
D20.zh: 12岁时，他被选为学校文学社社长。

D1.en: At age 6, he rejected his parents' choice and attended the kuttab, then returned to public school.
D1.zh: 6岁时，他拒绝了父母的选择，去了学堂，后又回到公立学校。

M7.en: At 16, he held anti-imperialist, anti-colonial, and anti-British ideas.
M7.zh: 16岁时，他持有反帝国主义、反殖民主义和反英思想。

M8.en: At age 6, he was drawn to mysticism.
M8.zh: 6岁时，他受到神秘主义吸引。

D14.en: At 23, he spoke about the Cause, clarifying points for scholars.
D14.zh: 23岁时，他谈论圣道，为学者澄清了一些要点。

D3.en: At 16, he showed keen interest in American travel magazines.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他对来自美国的旅行杂志表现出浓厚兴趣。

D2.en: At 24, he engaged day and night in perfecting his translation art.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他日夜致力于完善翻译技艺。

D12.en: As a small child, he lived in the prison-city of Akka in Syria.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他住在叙利亚的监狱城市阿卡。

D6.en: At 13, he sat disconsolate on the steps of the Master's house.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他沮丧地坐在大师家的台阶上。

D4.en: He waited impatiently for photographs he had sent to arrive.
D4.zh: 他焦急地等待他寄出的照片到达。

D13a.en: At 9-13, he was sent to a Catholic boarding school in Beirut.
D13a.zh: 9到13岁间，他被送到贝鲁特的一所天主教寄宿学校。

D10.en: At 24, he lay in bed for days, senseless and greatly agitated.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他卧床数日，神志不清且极度不安。

M6.en: At 23, he hoped to acquire the best and recast the truths of the Faith.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他希望获取精华，重塑圣道真理。

M3.en: He idolized his grandfather and made serving him his life's object.
M3.zh: 他崇拜他的祖父，并以服务祖父为人生目标。

D15.en: At age 10, he wore short pants, long stockings, a fez, a jacket, and a huge sailor collar.
D15.zh: 10岁时，他穿着短裤、长袜、戴土耳其帽、穿夹克，肩上披着巨大的水手领。

D5.en: At 25, he bought a bicycle and cycled over many passes.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他买了一辆自行车，骑过了许多山口。

D9.en: At 23, he said he came in close touch with eminent professors and Orientalists at Oxford or London University.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他说他通过他们与牛津或伦敦大学的著名教授和东方学者密切接触。

D19.en: At 25, being methodical, he kept complete records and copies of letters sent.
D19.zh: 25岁时，他天性有条理，保留了完整的记录和寄出信件的副本。

M1.en: At 17-18, he won first prize in the Freshman-Sophomore Prize Contest in February 1915.
M1.zh: 17到18岁时，他在1915年2月的新生-大二竞赛中获得一等奖。

M4.en: At 22, his avowed purpose was to perfect his English to better translate the Tablets of 'Abdu'l-Bahá.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他宣称的目标是完善英语，以便更好地翻译阿博都-巴哈的书简。

D16.en: At 23-24, he said he would send a paper on the Movement which he read at a leading society in Oxford.
D16.zh: 23到24岁时，他说他会寄一份关于该运动的论文，他曾在牛津的一个主要社团宣读。

M7.en: At 22, he aimed to better translate the Tablets of 'Abdu'l-Bahá and other Sacred Writings.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他旨在更好地翻译阿博都-巴哈的书简和其他圣作。

D17.en: At 24, he comforted others despite his own agony.
D17.zh: 24岁时，他在痛苦中安慰他人。

D15.en: At 20-25, he experienced what seemed like utter collapse with even the heartbeat stopping.
D15.zh: 20到25岁间，他经历了似乎彻底崩溃、连心跳都停止的感觉。

D2.en: At 17-18, he single-handedly worked from dawn to dusk day after day until the path was finished.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他独自一人从早干到晚，日复一日，直到把路修好。

D3.en: He listened as the Swami summarized the books' teachings for him.
D3.zh: 他听斯瓦米为他概括了那些书的核心教义。

D11.en: At 17, he dramatized death by lying stiff as if rigor mortis had set in and imitating a corpse.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他戏剧化地表现死亡，四肢僵硬地躺着，模仿尸体。

D10.en: At 17, a sudden violent fear of death overtook him.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他突然被一种对死亡的强烈恐惧笼罩。

M6.en: At 17, he felt he had to solve the problem himself, there and then.
M6.zh: 17岁时，他觉得自己必须当场亲自解决这个问题。

D4.en: At 17-18, he single-handedly worked from dawn to dusk day after day until the path was finished.
D4.zh: 17到18岁时，他独自一人从早干到晚，日复一日，直到把路修好。

D14.en: At 16, he hastened straight to the great Temple, and all gates and doors stood open in mute welcome.
D14.zh: 16岁时，他径直赶往大庙，所有大门和门扉都无声敞开以示欢迎。

D7.en: At 16, he threw away his remaining money and never handled money again.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他扔掉了剩下的钱，从此再没碰过钱。

D13a.en: At 16, he was given an exercise to copy out three times for not learning it, and it was the forenoon.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他因没学会而被罚抄写三遍，时间是上午。

D9.en: At 20-25, he tried to be free a third time after his mother's death.
D9.zh: 20到25岁间，他在母亲去世后第三次尝试获得自由。

D6.en: At 19-21, he had drawn devotees and an Ashram formed around him.
D6.zh: 他19到21岁时吸引了追随者，道场随之形成。

D18.en: At 16, he took off his shoes to raise money for a train journey.
D18.zh: 16岁时，他脱下鞋子换钱以完成火车旅行。

D1.en: At 16, he stayed in a dark vault where insects caused sores on his thighs that left permanent marks.
D1.zh: 16岁时，他待在一个黑暗的地下室里，虫子在他大腿上咬出疮口，留下永久疤痕。

D13b.en: He went with the Swami to Gurumurtam in February 1897.
D13b.zh: 他于1897年2月与斯瓦米一同前往古鲁穆尔塔姆。

D12.en: At 9, he ran away from home alone in a mood of enthusiasm, unknown to his parents.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他一时兴起独自跑出家，父母不知情。

D16.en: At 22, in 1900, he attended a pandit meeting and won the title Kavyakanta for impromptu versification and disputation.
D16.zh: 22岁时，1900年，他参加学者会议，因即兴作诗和辩论赢得卡维亚坎塔称号。

D5.en: He had a scorpion climb up his front and another down his back.
D5.zh: 他有一只蝎子爬上他的前胸，另一只爬下他的后背。

M8.en: At 20-25, he said he never did any sadhana and didn't even know what it was.
M8.zh: 20到25岁间，他说自己从未做过修行，甚至不知道修行是什么。

D4.en: At 12-13, he learned to make doors and tables.
D4.zh: 12到13岁时，他学会了做门和桌子。

D13a.en: At 12-13, his father apprenticed him to a carpenter against his wish to study.
D13a.zh: 12到13岁时，他父亲不顾他继续学习的愿望，让他跟当地木匠当学徒。

D2.en: At 20, he served two or three masses each morning for priests on board.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他每天早上为船上的神父们服务两到三次弥撒。

D6.en: At 13, he spoke with someone about his desire to go to the seminary.
D6.zh: 13岁时，他和别人谈了自己想上神学院的愿望。

D15.en: He became the unofficial instructor for those who could not swim.
D15.zh: 他成了那些不会游泳的人的非正式教练。

D18.en: At 12-13, he was apprenticed as a letter carrier and later a telegraph operator.
D18.zh: 12到13岁时，他先当信差学徒，后来当电报员学徒。

M6.en: At 13, his desire to be a priest may have arisen from the mayor's suggestion.
M6.zh: 13岁时，他想当神父的愿望可能来自镇长的建议。

M8.en: At 23-24, he felt God inspired a great desire for holiness in him.
M8.zh: 23到24岁时，他感到天主在他内激发了对圣德的强烈渴望。

D9.en: At 24-25, he organized catechism classes, visited jails, and worked with radio and newspaper.
D9.zh: 24到25岁时，他组织要理班和初领圣体，探访乡村和监狱，并参与教区电台和报纸工作。

D13b.en: At 13, he left Ciudad Barrios for the minor seminary in San Miguel.
D13b.zh: 13岁时，他离开巴里奥斯城前往圣米格尔的小修院。

D11.en: As a teenager, he wrote a poem reflecting his view of the priesthood.
D11.zh: 十几岁时，他写了一首诗，反映了他对司铎职的看法。

D5.en: At 20-24, he challenged others to swim to a rock and sat on a sea urchin nest.
D5.zh: 20到24岁间，他挑战别人游到一块岩石上，结果坐在了海胆窝上。

M2.en: At 20, he wrote: 'My father is dead! Dear Father, I who each evening turned my gaze to the distant east.'
M2.zh: 20岁时，他写道：'我父亲去世了！亲爱的父亲，我每晚都遥望东方，寄去我遥远的思念。'

D16.en: After 25, he grew famous as a preacher; five radio stations broadcast his Sunday mass.
