D16.zh: 25岁后，他成为著名布道者，五家电台同时直播他的周日弥撒。

D7.en: After 25, he offended many by refusing cathedral use for a Masonic ceremony and denying Christian burial to Masons.
D7.zh: 25岁后，他因拒绝让共济会在主教座堂举行仪式并拒绝为他们举行基督徒葬礼而得罪了很多人。

D4.en: He moved around Baroda in an old ramshackle carriage drawn by a big, plodding nag.
D4.zh: 他乘坐一辆由大笨马拉着的破旧马车在巴罗达四处走动。

D2.en: He entered the Calcutta Medical College and in 1865 became a licentiate in medicine and surgery.
D2.zh: 他进入加尔各答医学院，并于1865年成为内外科执业医师。

M6.en: At 18, he had a mental realization of the Self.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他有了对自我的精神觉悟。

D1.en: Between ages 16 and 17, his performance in every subject improved.
D1.zh: 16到17岁间，他每门课的成绩都提高了。

D5.en: At 21, he applied unsuccessfully for the post of principal of a college in another state.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他申请另一个州的大学校长职位，但未成功。

D7.en: At 20, he offended his mother by marrying as he did.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他因自己的婚事冒犯了母亲。

D11.en: At 10-11, he wrote a poem 'Light' and published it in an English family magazine.
D11.zh: 10到11岁时，他写了一首诗《光》，并在一本英国家庭杂志上发表。

M1.en: At 19, he was named joint winner of the Rawley Prize for best composition in Greek iambics.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他获得罗利奖联合得主，表彰其希腊抑扬格最佳作品。

D16.en: At 20, he submitted the first of a series of essays titled 'New Lamps for Old'.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他提交了系列文章《新灯换旧灯》的第一篇。

M4.en: At 20, he vowed to work for the liberation of India.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他发誓为印度解放而工作。

D10.en: At 24, he fell ill in November–December 1906.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他在1906年11月至12月间生病。

D13a.en: He joined the French Reformed Church but then quit the cloth to study law.
D13a.zh: 他加入了法国归正会，但后来放弃神职去学法律。

D9.en: At 23, he was a founding member of the National Council of Education.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他是国民教育委员会的创始成员。

D19.en: He governed his life by the clock, frequently consulting a cheap pocket watch.
D19.zh: 他按钟表生活，经常查看口袋里的廉价手表。

D20.en: At 24, he became editor of Bande Mataram.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他成为《母亲颂》的编辑。

D11.en: As a small child, she heard voices like her own in the well.
D11.zh: 幼年时，她听见井里有像自己的声音。

D14.en: At 17, she won a newspaper subscription contest and her picture was published.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她赢得报纸订阅比赛，照片被刊登。

D6.en: At 17, she went to costume skating carnivals, play rehearsals, and college balls.
D6.zh: 17岁时，她参加化妆滑冰嘉年华、话剧排练和大学舞会。

D7.en: At 22, she defended her marriage against followers, newspapers, and her rebellious husband.
D7.zh: 22岁时，她为自己的婚姻辩护，对抗追随者、报纸和叛逆的丈夫。

D3.en: At 17, she argued with a preacher about evolution and made a fool of him.
D3.zh: 17岁时，她与牧师争论进化论，让他出丑。

D16.en: At 15-16, she wrote a letter to the editor that was published in a Montreal newspaper.
D16.zh: 15到16岁时，她写信给编辑，发表在蒙特利尔报纸上。

D12.en: At 20, she and Robert arrived in Hong Kong via the Suez Canal and Ceylon.
D12.zh: 20岁时，她和罗伯特经苏伊士运河和锡兰抵达香港。

D1.en: At 17, she prayed for days in the snow to be emptied of self and filled with heavenly power.
D1.zh: 17岁时，她在雪中祈祷数日，求神倒空自己、充满天上的能力。

D20.en: At about 30, she countersued for divorce in California.
D20.zh: 大约30岁时，她在加州反诉离婚。

M4.en: At 17, she converted, received Spirit baptism, and spoke in tongues.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她悔改信主，受圣灵洗并说方言。

M7.en: In her early 30s, she and her students planned to spread the Foursquare gospel worldwide.
M7.zh: 30岁出头时，她和学生计划将四方福音传到地极。

D13b.en: At 21, she married Harold McPherson in Chicago.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，她在芝加哥与哈罗德·麦克弗森结婚。

D10.en: At 18, she hobbled in pain to the train back to Chicago.
D10.zh: 18岁时，她忍着剧痛一瘸一拐地上了回芝加哥的火车。

D17.en: As a small child, she befriended animals and frantically guarded her guinea pigs from visitors.
D17.zh: 幼年时，她与动物为友，疯狂保护豚鼠不让客人碰。

D8.en: At 7, she jumped and grabbed it, and other children fled from her with bloody noses and barked shins.
D8.zh: 7岁时，她跳起来抓住了它，其他孩子被她吓得流着鼻血、擦伤小腿逃走了。

M1.en: At 11-12, she won a gold medal in a public speaking contest.
M1.zh: 11到12岁时，她在演讲比赛中赢得金牌。

M8.en: At 17, she felt the pardoning blood of Jesus flowing over her.
M8.zh: 17岁时，她感到耶稣的赎罪之血流过她身上。

D18.en: At 17, she sold newspaper subscriptions with great skill.
D18.zh: 17岁时，她卖报纸订阅，技巧娴熟。

D2.en: At 21-22, she stayed up all night scripting sermons.
D2.zh: 21到22岁间，她通宵写讲道稿。

D15.en: At 18, she tumbled down stairs and broke her ankle.
D15.zh: 18岁时，她从楼梯上摔下来，摔断了脚踝。

M5.en: At 25, she spoke in a deep melodious voice for over an hour.
M5.zh: 25岁时，她用深沉悦耳的声音讲了一个多小时。

M3.en: As a teenager, Anthony Quinn was in awe of her and dared not look her in the eyes.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，安东尼·奎因敬畏她，不敢直视她的眼睛。

D12.en: After his father's death, he assumed full responsibility for his family.
D12.zh: 父亲去世后，他承担了家庭的全部责任。

D3.en: At 11, he felt intense longing for God and was drawn to the Himalayas.
D3.zh: 11岁时，他强烈渴望上帝，被吸引到喜马拉雅山。

D14.en: At 25, he gave his first lecture in America at the International Congress of Religious Liberals in Boston.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他在波士顿的国际宗教自由大会上做了在美国的第一次演讲。

D11.en: At 11, he had a clear picture of himself roaming India as a monk.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他清晰地想象自己作为僧侣在印度漫游。

D10.en: At 16-17, after two hours of tears, he felt transformed as if by an alchemical cleanser.
D10.zh: 16到17岁时，哭了两个小时后，他感觉像被炼金术净化了一样转变了。

D7.en: At 20-25, he refused to give his father a third chance to ridicule his plans for Kashmir and decided to go anyway.
D7.zh: 20到25岁时，他拒绝再给父亲第三次机会嘲笑他的克什米尔计划，决定无论如何都要去。

D5.en: At 11, he fled toward Naini Tal in the Himalayan foothills.
D5.zh: 11岁时，他逃往喜马拉雅山麓的奈尼塔尔。

D6.en: At 20-25, he invited three friends.
D6.zh: 20到25岁时，他邀请了三位朋友。

D9.en: At 24, he determined to found a school for boys.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他决心创办一所男校。

M4.en: At 22, he became a monk of the Swami Order.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他成为斯瓦米教团的僧侣。

M7.en: At 25, he had to play father-mother to little children and cope with organizational difficulties.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他不得不扮演小孩子们的父母角色，并应对许多组织上的困难。

M8.en: As a child to young adult, he cognized the center of the empyrean as a point of intuitive perception in his heart.
M8.zh: 从幼年到青年，他认识到苍穹的中心是他心中直觉感知的一个点。

D1.en: At 16-17, he was in near-collapse, his young stomach protesting deprivation.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他几乎崩溃，年轻的胃因匮乏而剧烈抗议。

D13a.en: At 22, he definitely refused it.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他明确拒绝了它。

D15.en: As a teenager, he excelled in swimming and manual skills with a powerful body.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他凭借强壮的身体在游泳和许多手工技艺上表现出色。

D8.en: In silent mutiny, the other disciples continued to seek him out for daily counsel.
D8.zh: 其他门徒暗中违抗，仍每天找他咨询。

D13b.en: At 18, he was married to Srimati Kashi Moni.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他与斯里玛蒂·卡什·莫尼结婚。

D4.en: At 23, he reasoned that the human will could directly recharge energy.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他推论人的意志可以直接补充能量。

D17.en: At 25, he placed a lifeless pet on his lap and prayed for its life.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他把看似无生命的宠物放在腿上，祈祷它活过来。

M1.en: At 20, he said he would be disappointed if he failed to outwit the instructor.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他说如果没能智胜导师会很失望。

D16.en: At 25, he gave his first lecture in America at the International Congress of Religious Liberals in Boston.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他在波士顿的国际宗教自由大会上做了在美国的首次演讲。

M5.en: At 16, he made his first flight.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他进行了首次飞行。

D10.en: At 25, he said he was in a most rebellious mood and didn't want to belong to anything he was ashamed of.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他说自己处于最叛逆的情绪中，不想属于任何让他感到羞耻的东西。

D15.en: At 14, he said he had ridden 31½ miles and would add more that evening.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他说自己已经骑了31.5英里，晚上还要再加一些。

D9.en: At 25, he said he would write the editorial every month and it would be very difficult for him.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他说自己每个月都要写社论，对他来说会非常困难。

D4.en: At 8, he took his father's clock to pieces and refused to go to school or eat until he put it together.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他把父亲的钟拆了，不肯上学或吃饭，直到重新装好。

D17.en: At 7, he would give his pencils, slate, or books to poorer boys on his way home from school.
D17.zh: 7岁时，他放学回家路上常把铅笔、石板或书送给更穷的男孩。

D11.en: At 17, he had written a new book called Education as Service to be published in London.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他写了一本新书《教育即服务》，将在伦敦出版。

D2.en: At 25, he said he must work hard at his studies and go to elocution classes in Paris.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他说自己必须努力学习，并在巴黎上演讲课。

D13a.en: At 18, he declared he would not return to his father.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他宣称不会回到父亲身边。

D7.en: At 18, he showed a spirit of independence in a letter.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他在一封信中表现出独立精神。

D18.en: At 17, he started collecting money for Mrs. Besant's work.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他开始为贝桑特夫人的工作募捐。

D1.en: Between ages 17-19, he would study for days but leave exams blank.
D1.zh: 17到19岁间，他学习数日却在考试时交白卷。

M6.en: At 19, he accepted his position but derived no satisfaction from it.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他接受了职位但未从中获得满足。

M3.en: As a child, he was deeply affected by a painting of Buddha.
M3.zh: 幼年时，他被一幅佛陀画像深深打动。

M7.en: At 18, he felt sympathy for Mrs. Besant's failed efforts.
M7.zh: 18岁时，他同情贝桑特夫人的努力失败。

D8.en: At 18, he decided to take his affairs into his own hands.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他决定自己掌管自己的事务。

D5.en: At 18, he was determined to make clear he knew what he was doing.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他决心表明自己清楚在做什么。

D20X.en: At 18, he thought all would go well if Raja were relieved of his duties.
D20X.zh: 18岁时，他认为如果拉贾被解除职务，一切都会顺利。

D12.en: At 6, he felt the impact of the West directly.
D12.zh: 6岁时，他直接感受到西方的影响。

D2.en: He never let go of a research question until he found a satisfying answer.
D2.zh: 他对自己研究的问题，不得到确实心安的主见就不放松。

D13a.en: At 17-19, he joined revolutionary activities despite his father's differing views, forming independent thinking.
D13a.zh: 17到19岁时，他加入京津同盟会参加革命活动，父亲不干涉，他逐渐养成独立思考习惯。

D7.en: At 24, he took a detour through Hunan on his way back to Peking.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他绕道湖南回北京。

D3.en: At 9-11, he used five million words of journals as his earliest self-study textbooks.
D3.zh: 9到11岁时，他把五百多万字的《新民丛报》和《新小说》当作最早的自学课本。

D4.en: At 8, he furtively burned all his English textbooks.
D4.zh: 8岁时，他偷偷烧掉了所有英语课本。

D6.en: At 14, he was close friends with three other boys.
D6.zh: 14岁时，他和三个男孩很要好。

D18.en: At 19-20, he worked as a reporter, going everywhere from the president's office to street corners.
D18.zh: 19到20岁时，他当外勤记者，上至总统府下至街头巷尾无处不去。

D1.en: At 7, his school closed due to the Boxer Rebellion and foreign troops, so he had to suspend studies.
D1.zh: 7岁时，闹义和团八国联军进城，学校关门，他因此休学。

D5.en: At 18, he took his first trip outside Peking on secret revolutionary business.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他第一次出北京城，去昌平从事秘密革命工作。

M1.en: At 18, he held to his constitutionalist stand until the Hsin-hai year (1911).
M1.zh: 18岁时，他坚持立宪立场直到辛亥年。

M2.en: At 25, he said his father's suicide was inseparable from his family's generations of serving the Qing dynasty.
M2.zh: 25岁时，他说父亲的自杀离不开他家几代在清王朝做官这个背景。

M4.en: As a teenager, he was obsessed by the desire to make China strong against its oppressors.
M4.zh: 十几岁时，他痴迷于让中国强大以对抗压迫者的愿望。

D9.en: At 18, he was chosen to run a coal shop as a front for revolutionaries' lair.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他被选去经营一家煤铺作为革命党人秘密据点的掩护。

D14.en: At 19, he dashed off to Nanking to join the revolutionary government and begin his journalism career.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他赶往南京加入革命政府，开始担任党报《国民报》记者。

D20.en: At 24-26, his closest friends at the university were central figures of the May Fourth and New Culture movements.
D20.zh: 24到26岁间，他在大学里的挚友是五四和新文化运动的核心人物。

D15.en: As a small child, he fell from a pole at school and was revived with medicine, never daring to try again.
D15.zh: 幼年时，他在小学爬杆子摔下来，用药后才苏醒，以后不敢再试。

D10.en: At 19, he attempted suicide again at the end of the year.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他在年底再次尝试自杀。

D11.en: At 25, he wrote his 1918 book, A General Discussion of Indian Thought.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他写了1918年的书《印度哲学概论》。

M6.en: At 23, he used Western thought to argue for Wei-shih metaphysics as key to understanding the world.
M6.zh: 23岁时，他用西方思想论证唯识形而上学是理解世界的关键。

D13b.en: He collaborated in redrafting the Church's laws as a brilliant young Vatican lawyer.
D13b.zh: 他作为杰出的年轻梵蒂冈律师，合作修订了教会法律。

D11.en: He was an actor and poet in his youth.
D11.zh: 他年轻时当过演员和诗人。

D3.en: He strived to emulate the eloquence of French bishop Bossuet.
D3.zh: 他努力模仿法国主教博叙埃的雄辩口才。

D2.en: His only recreation was a daily walk in the Villa Borghese with a breviary.
D2.zh: 他唯一的消遣是每天在博尔盖塞别墅散步，手拿祈祷书。

D14.en: He composed spiritual bouquets as a child.
D14.zh: 他小时候制作过精神花束。

D15.en: He was a broad-shouldered skier and mountain walker in his youth.
D15.zh: 他年轻时是个宽肩膀的滑雪者和山地徒步者。

D7.en: He informed his parents that he wanted to become a priest.
D7.zh: 他告诉父母他想成为神父。

D13a.en: His father got him permission to live at home while studying.
D13a.zh: 他父亲为他争取到在家居住学习的许可。

D4.en: As a youth, he was more used to physical action than to scholarship.
D4.zh: 年轻时，他更习惯体力活动而非学术研究。

D12.en: At 21, he replaced Xu Mo'an at the Shiwu Bao office, but received only 12 yuan instead of the expected 20.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他代替许默庵在时务报馆任职，但领薪时只拿到12元而非20元。

D18.en: At 22, he worked as a clerk and proofreader for Shiwu Bao, earning very little.
D18.zh: 22岁时，他为《时务报》做书记和校雠，所得很少。

D3.en: At 24-28, he noted that some scholars used Western theories to transform ancient Chinese doctrines.
D3.zh: 24到28岁间，他指出有学者用西洋学说改造古代学说。

D7.en: At 21, he was furious hearing townspeople blame exiles for current affairs, saying they were blind to danger.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他听到同乡指责亡人，气得说危亡在即却不知病。

D6.en: At 19, he bought and read Shiwu Bao from issue one, and promoted reform ideas to friends and his father.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他购阅《时务报》并宣传变法思想给同学和父亲。

D11.en: At 21-22, he wrote a poem about Zhang Qian and Gan Ying, marveling at the view of Daqin from the Black Sea.
D11.zh: 21到22岁时，他作诗咏张骞甘英，感叹黑海东头望大秦的壮观。

D13a.en: At 21, he failed the provincial exam and ended his imperial examination career, seeking his own path.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他乡试不中，从此结束科举生涯，转而寻找自己愿走的路。

D4.en: As a small child, he heard his father discuss reform policies at home, which he later saw implemented.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他听父亲谈论经世之学和新政，这些后来都得以实行。

D9.en: At 21, he remarked that unity is the hardest thing in the world, due to people's low quality, agreeing with Spencer.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他感叹合群是天下第一难事，因民质未进，深以为然。

M2.en: At 16, he felt it unlikely to fulfill the family's hope of bringing glory as the eldest son.
M2.zh: 16岁时，他觉得作为长房长子光宗耀祖的愿望很难实现。

M4.en: At 19, he bought and read the Shiwu Bao, became a loyal reader, and promoted reform ideas.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他购阅《时务报》，成为忠实读者，并宣传变法思想。

M1.en: At 16, he considered himself the top talent of Haining but failed the exam due to neglecting the eight-legged essay.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他自恃海宁四才子之首，不用功科举时文，结果名落孙山。

D20X.en: At 21, after failing the provincial exam, he ended his imperial examination career and sought his own path.
D20X.zh: 21岁时，他乡试不中，从此结束科举生涯，转而摸索自己愿意走的道路。

D17.en: At 16, he helped his elder sister's wedding, as she had cared for him after their mother's death.
D17.zh: 16岁时，他姐姐出阁，他鞍前马后出力奔走，因为母亲去世后姐姐常照顾他。

D15.en: At age 21, Wang Guowei took the provincial exam in Hangzhou, which started on the sixth day of the eighth lunar month in 1897.
D15.zh: 21岁时，王国维在杭州参加乡试，始于1897年农历八月初六。

D1.en: From 16 to 21, he failed two prefectural exams, but his father insisted he must take the provincial exam to decide his future.
D1.zh: 16到21岁间，他两次府试失败，但他父亲认为他必须参加一次乡试才能决定前途。

D16.en: At 19, he promoted reform ideas to his friends and father.
D16.zh: 19岁时，他向同学朋友和父亲宣传变法思想。

D10.en: At 16, he lost interest in imperial exams and became passionate about studying the Book of Han.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他对科举冷淡下来，对研究《汉书》却热情起来。

D14.en: At 19, he promoted reform ideas to his friends and father.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他向同学朋友和父亲宣传变法思想。

M5.en: At 16, he considered himself the top of the Four Talents of Haining and failed the exams due to neglect.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他自恃海宁四才子之首，不用功科举时文，结果名落孙山。

M6.en: At 21, after failing the provincial exam, he ended his imperial exam career and sought his own path.
M6.zh: 21岁时，他乡试不中，从此结束科举生涯，转而摸索自己愿意走的道路。

M7.en: At 19, he subscribed to and faithfully read the Chinese Progress, and promoted reform ideas.
M7.zh: 19岁时，他购阅《时务报》，一直是忠实读者，并宣传变法思想。

M8.en: At 19, he subscribed to and faithfully read the Chinese Progress, and promoted reform ideas.
M8.zh: 19岁时，他购阅《时务报》，一直是忠实读者，并宣传变法思想。

D13b.en: At 20, he married the granddaughter of a fellow merchant, Mo Yinsheng.
D13b.zh: 20岁时，他娶同邑商人莫寅生孙女为妻。

D3.en: At 16, he wrote in his diary about rewriting 'The World and Its People' into paragraphs.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他在日记里说要把《The World and Its People》改写成段落。

D4.en: At 23, he assembled a bicycle and it was stolen the next day.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他把自行车装好，第二天就丢了。

D15.en: At 24, he exercised at the YMCA and hiked Grizzly Peak and Mount Tamalpais.
D15.zh: 24岁时，他在青年会做体操，爬灰熊峰和塔马尔帕斯山。

D12.en: At 11-12, after his mother fell ill, he loafed around for about two years with little schooling.
D12.zh: 11到12岁时，母亲病了后，他差不多有两年都在外面闲逛。

D18.en: As a small child, he collected old coins with other kids, untying strings of cash to find them.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他和小孩们攒古钱，解开一吊一吊的钱找古钱。

D10.en: At 23, he felt more sensitive and indecisive than ever, unable to concentrate.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他比以往更敏感、拿不定主意，没法集中精力。

D19.en: At 14, he started his first diary on April 15, 1906.
D19.zh: 14岁时，他在1906年4月15日开始记第一条日记。

D11.en: At 20-23, his one-act comedy 'Registered Letter' was performed at annual meetings more than once.
D11.zh: 20到23岁时，他写的独幕笑剧《挂号信》在年会上上演不止一次。

D2.en: At 21-23, he practiced Beethoven's Ninth Symphony on piano for days before performing it.
D2.zh: 21到23岁时，他在钢琴上练了好几天贝多芬第九交响曲才登场演奏。

D5.en: At 23, he walked from Cambridge to Pittsfield, staying in cheap inns and riding freight cars between cities.
D5.zh: 23岁时，他从剑桥一路走到皮茨菲尔德，沿途住便宜旅店，还坐过城市间的货车。

D7.en: At 16, he laughed during a required mourning ritual for the emperor and empress dowager.
D7.zh: 16岁时，他在为光绪皇帝和慈禧太后哭灵的仪式上大笑。

D1.en: At 18, he passed the Boxer Indemnity exam, ranking 2nd among 72, and went to the US.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他考取庚子赔款留美考试第二名，去了美国。

M1.en: At 18-22, he scored 100 in math twice, 99 once, and 100 in astronomy.
M1.zh: 18到22岁间，他数学得了两个100分和一个99分，天文学得了一次100分。

D6.en: At 20-23, he was active in philosophy, math, cosmopolitan, and Chinese student clubs.
D6.zh: 20到23岁间，他热心参加哲学、数学、世界主义和中国学生俱乐部。

D9.en: At 14, he organized a youth group to buy books and build a library.
D9.zh: 14岁时，他组织了一个青年集益社，买书建图书馆。

M4.en: At 14, he secretly hoped for revolution, believing the Qing dynasty would fall.
M4.zh: 14岁时，他暗中期盼革命，觉得满清王朝快亡了。

D13a.en: At 14, he was told of an arranged marriage and wrote in his diary that he was sad.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，他被安排了一桩亲事，在日记里写自己很伤心。

M6.en: At 16-18, he decided to become a world citizen, fascinated by the idea of a world nation.
M6.zh: 16到18岁间，他决定加入世界国家，做一个世界公民。

D12.en: At 9, he received permission from his parents to enter a monastery.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他得到父母允许进入修道院。

D6.en: At 18, he fell in love with Jai, Phut's step-sister, and courted her at Phut's house.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他爱上了普特的继妹杰，并在普特家追求她。

D7.en: He began a 240 km hike northwards from Bahn Kor.
D7.zh: 他从班科出发，开始向北徒步240公里。

D13a.en: At 9, his parents took him to the local village monastery.
D13a.zh: 9岁时，他父母带他去当地村寺。

M6.en: At 20, he saw that life as a monk might resolve his lack of peace and meaning.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他看出僧侣生活可能解决他缺乏平静和意义的问题。

D10.en: At 20-27, for the first seven years of monkhood, he found it impossible to let go of thoughts of this young woman.
D10.zh: 20到27岁间，出家头七年，他发现自己无法完全放下对这个年轻女子的思念。

D13b.en: At 9, he received permission from his parents to enter a monastery.
D13b.zh: 9岁时，他得到父母允许进入修道院。

D2.en: At 13-16, he spent time daily walking up and down in the shade, memorizing Pali chants.
D2.zh: 13到16岁间，他每天在阴凉处走来走去，背诵巴利文唱诵。

D15.en: He practiced walking meditation in the rain, even as puddles formed around his feet.
D15.zh: 他在雨中练习行禅，即使脚下积起水洼也不停。

M2.en: He no longer saw his practice as just making merit for his parents.
M2.zh: 他不再认为自己的修行只是为了给父母积德。

D17.en: He helped nurse his father for the thirteen days and nights he lingered.
D17.zh: 他照顾了父亲临终前的十三个日夜。

D4.en: At 20-25, he sewed a set of robes flat out for Luang Pu Kinaree.
D4.zh: 20到25岁间，他拼命为龙普基那里缝制了一套僧袍。

D5.en: He set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor before hiking 240 km north.
D5.zh: 他出发长途步行返回伊桑，在班科尔中途休息，然后向北跋涉240公里。

D3.en: He heard Dhamma talks about letting go but couldn't understand much.
D3.zh: 他听法师讲放下，但还不太明白。

M4.en: The suffering he witnessed in the cremation forest spurred him to greater efforts.
M4.zh: 他在火葬林目睹的苦难激励他更加努力。

M7.en: He felt joy and rapture in the Dhamma, and resolved to put his knowledge into practice.
M7.zh: 他感受到法喜，决心将所学付诸实践。

D18.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor.
D18.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，途中在班科尔停留。

D19.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking at Bahn Kor before hiking 240 km northwards.
D19.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，在班科尔停留后向北徒步240公里。

D20X.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor before hiking 240 km northwards.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，在班科休息后向北徒步240公里。

D8.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor before hiking 240 km northwards.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，在班科休息后向北徒步240公里。

D11.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor.
D11.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，途中在班科尔停留。

D14.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking at Bahn Kor before hiking 240 km northwards.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，在班科休息后向北徒步240公里。

M1.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，途中在班科尔休息。

M5.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor.
M5.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，途中在班科尔停留。

M8.en: At age 25, he set off on a long walk back to Isan, breaking the journey at Bahn Kor.
M8.zh: 25岁时，他启程长途步行返回伊桑，途中在班科尔停留。

D2.en: At 16, he learned to wake up without an alarm clock, which was a revelation.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他学会了不用闹钟起床，这让他恍然大悟。

D3.en: At 23, he was curious about Miss Ransom's approach to Christianity, a subject he knew little about.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他对兰塞姆小姐的基督教方式感到好奇，这是他知之甚少的领域。

D11.en: As a small child, he told his siblings about his plans to build a zoo next to the temple with a train from town.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他告诉兄弟姐妹他计划在寺庙旁建一个动物园，并让火车从镇上开上来。

M6.en: At 22, he knew he would practice Zen his whole life.
M6.zh: 22岁时，他知道自己会终生修习禅宗。

D9.en: At 21, he said, 'I want to establish a branch temple of Rinso-in in Manchuria.'
D9.zh: 21岁时，他说：“我想在满洲建立临松院的分寺。”

D10.en: He jerked and screamed, dropped the melon, quickly got his balance and reached up.
D10.zh: 他猛地一抖，尖叫起来，手里的瓜掉了，但他很快稳住身体，伸手去够。

D1.en: At 21, he was told by Shunryu Suzuki to just continue, that there would be a way.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他听铃木俊隆说，只要继续下去，就会有办法。

D13a.en: His father had agreed in correspondence to let him give up and go back to kendo.
D13a.zh: 他父亲在信中同意让他放弃，回去练剑道。

M1.en: He graduated from Komazawa University, second in his class.
M1.zh: 他从驹泽大学毕业，成绩全班第二。

D5.en: It seemed like a rash, even an irresponsible thing to do. Those close to him were worried they’d never see him again.
D5.zh: 这看起来是个鲁莽甚至不负责任的做法。亲近他的人都担心再也见不到他了。

D15.en: He walked all the way, about forty miles, arriving that night.
D15.zh: 他一路走了大约四十英里，当晚到达。

D20.en: He had students who called themselves the High Grass Mountain Group.
D20.zh: 他的学生自称高草山会。

M4.en: At 19, he didn't oppose the war but wanted it to end.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他不反对战争，只希望战争结束。

D12.en: At 14, he heard his father say he could not live if he died.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他听到父亲说自己若死了父亲活不成。

D7.en: At 21, he wrote a 3000-word essay arguing the Six Classics were not exclusive to Confucius.
D7.zh: 21岁时，他写了一篇三千多字的《丧文论》，说《六经》不是孔子专有。

D13a.en: At 7-8, he bought snacks with a maid but his grandmother threw them on the roof.
D13a.zh: 七八岁时，他和女佣买了糖食，结果被祖母扔到屋瓦上。

D14.en: At 12, he served as a nominal matchmaker at a relative's wedding, wearing borrowed formal attire.
D14.zh: 12岁时，他代做名义上的媒人，穿了借来的小礼服去男家做大媒。

D2.en: At 15-16, he spent a year compiling a 20-volume work on Qing dynasty authors based on bibliographies.
D2.zh: 15到16岁时，他花一年时间根据书目编成二十册《清代著述考》。

D3.en: At 23, he studied logic and evolution books, deciding to major in philosophy.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他细读《名学浅说》和《天演论》，决定考进哲学系。

D18.en: At 11, he frequented bookstores, buying hundreds of books yearly with his mother's allowance.
D18.zh: 11岁以后，他天天出入书肆，一年买五六百册书，母亲每月给他书款。

D9.en: At 16-19, he organized a 'National Studies Research Society' and printed unpublished works.
D9.zh: 16到19岁间，他组织国学研究会，油印未刊著作作为丛书。

D5.en: At 20-21, he watched daytime plays and stayed in the theater eating flatbread to see Lao Tan's night performance.
D5.zh: 20到21岁时，他白天看戏后带烧饼在池子里等老谭夜戏。

D4.en: At 20-21, he started a 'Drama Table' analyzing each play's details and a 'Drama Notes' journal.
D4.zh: 20到21岁时，他动笔写《戏剧表》分析每出戏，并写《论剧记》记录心得。

M1.en: At 23, he ranked fifth on the list and became an undergraduate.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他名列第五，成了本科生。

D11.en: At 20-21, he read Liu Zhiji's Shitong and planned to write a book called Xitong in the same style.
D11.zh: 20到21岁间，他读了刘知几的《史通》，想照这个方式写一部《戏通》。

D2.en: At 20-25, he studied Sanskrit and Pali under Lanman at Harvard and under Lüeders in Berlin, also visiting Müller's lectures.
D2.zh: 20到25岁间，他在哈佛随蓝门学梵文和巴利文，在柏林随鲁斗继续研究，并游于缪勒之门。

D3.en: At 20-25, he studied Sanskrit and Pali under Lanman at Harvard and under Lüeders in Berlin, also visiting Müller's lectures.
D3.zh: 20到25岁间，他在哈佛随蓝门学梵文和巴利文，在柏林随鲁斗继续研究，并游于缪勒之门。

D11.en: He wrote an ancient-style poem praising the beauty of snow-covered mountain peaks while studying at the University of Zurich.
D11.zh: 他在苏黎世大学读书时，曾作古诗描写山顶雪景之美。

D6.en: He became a student at Tsinghua's Guoxue Research Institute, studying under Liang Qichao and Chen Yinke.
D6.zh: 他成为清华国学研究院学生，师从梁启超和陈寅恪。

M4.en: He held a belief in breaking with the current order and fought for it most of his life.
M4.zh: 他持有与现时秩序决裂的信仰，并为之奋斗了大半生。

M6.en: He held a belief in breaking with the current order and fought for it most of his life.
M6.zh: 他持有与现时秩序决裂的信仰，并为之奋斗了大半生。

M8.en: In his 20s, he held a belief in breaking with the existing order.
M8.zh: 20多岁时，他存有与现时秩序决裂的信仰。

D9.en: He organized villagers into a militia to defend Yining for several years.
D9.zh: 他组织乡人为团练，防守义宁达数年之久。

D7.en: At 29, he saw the Old Summer Palace on fire and wept loudly in a restaurant.
D7.zh: 29岁时，他看到圆明园起火，在酒楼拍桌号啕大哭。

D8.en: At 33, he mediated a conflict between Zeng Guofan and Shen Baozhen.
D8.zh: 33岁时，他调和了曾国藩和沈葆桢之间的冲突。

M1.en: During 19-35, he studied in Europe and America for years without earning any degree.
M1.zh: 19到35岁间，他在欧美留学多年，没有拿任何学位。

D2.en: At 24, he memorized vocabulary day and night, even reading under a streetlamp after lights out.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他日夜背单词，熄灯后还在路灯下读。

D3.en: At 14, he stayed home self-studying, building on his strong primary school foundation.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他呆在家里自学，基础好，进步快。

D12.en: At 14, he worked in his father's grocery, then tried weaving but failed due to nearsightedness, so studied at home.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他在父亲杂货店打杂，后学织布因近视未成，居家自学。

D1.en: From age 11 to 14, he endured scorn and poor seating, studying hard without giving up.
D1.zh: 11到14岁间，他忍受冷眼和屈辱座位，默默用功不气馁。

D7.en: At 25, he joined a student protest and co-signed a manifesto to oust an official.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他参加学潮，联名发表驱江文告。

D11.en: At 24-25, he planned a novel with a protagonist named Wang Luanzhu, but never wrote it.
D11.zh: 24到25岁间，他计划写长篇小说，主人公叫王鸾珠，但未写成。

D14.en: At 18-19, he entered couplet contests almost every time and won repeatedly.
D14.zh: 18到19岁时，他几乎每次参加对联比赛都获奖。

D9.en: At 25, he actively joined a student movement and co-published a statement against the dean.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他积极参加学潮，联名发表驱江文告。

D5.en: At 25, encouraged by his principal and colleagues, he resolved to burn his bridges to fulfill his dream.
D5.zh: 25岁时，在校长和同事鼓励下，他决心破釜沉舟以偿宿愿。

M4.en: At 25, he joined a student protest and co-signed a manifesto to expel Jiang.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他参加学潮，联名发表驱江文告。

D20.en: He co-signed a public statement with professors and classmates to expel Jiang.
D20.zh: 他和一些教授、同学联名发表文告，要求驱逐江某人。

D13a.en: At 16, he married a stranger in an arranged family wedding.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他听从家庭安排，和一个陌生农村姑娘结婚。

D13b.en: He obeyed his parents and married Qin Zuying from Longshutang village.
D13b.zh: 他遵父母之命，与同县龙树堂村的秦祖瑛结婚。

D12.en: At 19, he decided to join the construction of New China and worked as an accountant in Hunan.
D12.zh: 19岁时，他决定投身新中国建设，在湖南担任会计。

D2.en: At 21-25, he completed a lot of reading in the dawn before others woke up.
D2.zh: 21到25岁间，他常在晨曦中完成大量阅读。

D3.en: At 21-25, he studied peasant socialism and co-translated economic works by Herzen and Chernyshevsky.
D3.zh: 21到25岁间，他研究农民社会主义，并与好友合译了赫尔岑和车尔尼雪夫斯基的经济学著作。

D11.en: At 21, he saw boats pulled by trackers against the current and wrote a poem to the tune of 'Dreamlike Song'.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他见到纤夫拉船逆流而上，填了一首《如梦令》。

D14.en: At 17-18, inspired by a chemistry teacher and a factory visit, he resolved to save China through industry.
D14.zh: 17到18岁间，受化学老师和化工厂参观影响，他立下实业救国的志向。

D18.en: At 19, he worked as an accountant at a consumer cooperative in Yuanling, Hunan.
D18.zh: 19岁时，他在湖南沅陵教育用品消费合作社担任会计。

M1.en: At 18, he graduated with excellent grades from the high school affiliated to Jinling University and was recommended for admission.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他以优异成绩从金陵大学附中毕业，并被保送进入金陵大学。

M6.en: At 17-18, he set the ambition to save the country through industry.
M6.zh: 17到18岁间，他立下了实业救国的志向。

D1.en: At 21, he found his knowledge lacking and resolutely quit his job to take the college entrance exam.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他发现自己知识贫乏，毅然离职参加高考。

D9.en: At 19, he decided to join the construction of New China and started working in Hunan.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他决定投身新中国建设，在湖南参加了工作。

D20.en: At 24, he co-translated a book with Zhao Huijie.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他与赵辉杰合译了一本书。

D12.en: At 12-13, he helped his mother care for siblings and do housework.
D12.zh: 12到13岁时，他帮母亲照顾弟妹和做家务。

D2.en: At 18-21, he followed advice to improve his English for three years.
D2.zh: 18到21岁间，他听从建议努力加强英语水平三年。

D3.en: At 17, he sought to find China's ills in history and learn from past leaders.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他希望从历史中找出现代中国的病根并了解过去领袖的得失。

D6.en: At 18, he often consulted his teacher after class and was invited for weekly talks.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他常课后向老师请教，并被邀请每周四下午详谈。

D9.en: From age 5-17, he was often assigned or recommended as class leader or student council president.
D9.zh: 5到17岁间，他经常被老师指派或同学推荐担任班长或学生会主席。

D7.en: At 14-17, he was dissatisfied with the school's tracking system that devalued humanities students.
D7.zh: 14到17岁时，他对学校将文史班学生降为次等的分班方法非常不满。

D5.en: At 14-17, he was forced to play in a soccer match and felt clumsy and miserable.
D5.zh: 14到17岁时，他被老师要求上场踢足球，在场中痛苦不堪、笨手笨脚。

D4.en: At 21, he published an article translating Russell's views on Chinese and Western civilizations, revised by Mr. Yin.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他发表了自己翻译并由殷先生修改的关于罗素对中西文明看法的文章。

M4.en: At 18, he heard Yin Haiguang's teaching and saw a direction to realize his patriotic feelings.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他听到殷海光的教诲，看到了落实爱国情怀的方向。

M7.en: At 17, he hoped to find the root of China's problems in history to prepare for serving the nation.
M7.zh: 17岁时，他希望从历史中找出中国问题的根源，以备将来为国家服务。

D12.en: He was born with a disability and couldn't walk well, so during the war he couldn't attend school.
D12.zh: 他生而残疾，不良于行，抗战期间未能入学。

D3.en: He studied at NTU and Academia Sinica, and compiled genealogies of figures from the Zuo Zhuan.
D3.zh: 他在台大与中研院学习时，将《左传》中的人物排列谱系。

D2.en: At 25, he selected the Rites of Zhou and Zuo Zhuan from pre-Qin classics and read them sentence by sentence with commentaries.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他从先秦典籍中选取《周礼》与《左传》，连本文加注疏，一句一句仔细点读。

D9.en: He organized a picnic and garden party for classmates on the lawn during his master's program.
D9.zh: 他在台大读硕士班时，组织同学郊游，在草坪上举行园游会。

D11.en: He started reading wuxia novels at age 8, learning to recognize characters as he read.
D11.zh: 他从八岁开始读武侠小说，从识字不多到认识得差不多了。

D7.en: He was drawn into political debates among classmates during the civil war in high school.
D7.zh: 他在高一下学期被卷入同学间的政治辩论中。

D10.en: He repeatedly shattered and rebuilt his religious faith over five years.
D10.zh: 那五年中，他一次又一次把自己的宗教信仰打碎又重建。

D13b.en: He applied to and was admitted to NTU's foreign language department in 1948.
D13b.zh: 他1948年报考台大外语系并被录取。

D13a.en: The dean told him to switch to history, and he transferred the next semester.
D13a.zh: 校长建议他读历史系，他第二学期即转入历史系。

D6.en: He organized a picnic and garden party for classmates on the lawn during his master's program.
D6.zh: 他在台大读硕士班时，组织同学郊游，在草坪上举行园游会。

D20.en: He entered the Institute of History and Philology with three other assistant researchers.
D20.zh: 他与潘武肃、张春树、管东贵三位助理研究员同时进入史语所。

D16.en: At 19-20, he formed a team to participate in a school debate competition.
D16.zh: 19到20岁时，他组队参加学校的辩论比赛。

D1.en: At 20-25, he felt disheartened by repeated setbacks, and Mr. Shen once specially called him for a long talk.
D1.zh: 20到25岁时，他屡遭横逆，颇为心灰意冷，沈先生有一次特别召他长谈。

D15.en: At 22-25, he climbed stairs to class, finding it tiring but easier than now.
D15.zh: 22到25岁时，他爬楼梯去上课，觉得累但比现在好。

D7.en: At 17, he refused to stay home and insisted on going to a big city to study.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他不愿意待在家里，坚持要去大城市读书。

D12.en: At 25, he returned to his hometown for several months to reunite with family before studying abroad.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他去美国留学前回嵊县老家度假数月，与父母妻女团聚。

D2.en: At 25, he decided to switch from engineering to economics and resolved to exercise more.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他决定弃工改行攻读经济学，并决心加紧锻炼身体。

M1.en: At 16-19, he ranked first in every semester exam.
M1.zh: 16到19岁间，他每学期考试总得第一名。

D15.en: At 25-28, he used swimming and cold baths as means to build his physique.
D15.zh: 25到28岁间，他把游泳和冷水浴作为锻炼身体的手段。

D3.en: At 25-28, he discussed fitness ideas with an American doctor and asked for health secrets.
D3.zh: 25到28岁间，他同一位美国医生谈锻炼想法，并询问强身秘诀。

D13a.en: At 19, he married a local woman arranged by his parents during the Spring Festival.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他在父母包办下同本县姑娘张团妹结婚。

D3.en: At 23, he read Hume's works in college.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他在大学读了休谟的书。

D1.en: At 21, he entered Peking University penniless, arriving over a month late, and considered selling blood.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他没钱考进北大，迟到一个多月，曾想卖血。

D6.en: In his 20s-30s, he loved to argue with friends but remained friends after.
D6.zh: 20到30岁时，他爱争辩，从不让步，但争论完还是朋友。

D12.en: At 12, his father died.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他父亲死了。

D2.en: As a child, he learned that his surname was given by Zeng Guofan five generations ago.
D2.zh: 他小时候得知，五代以前姓是曾国藩给定的。

D11.en: Around age 10-13, he read new novels and poetry, imitating Ai Qing's poems and Ai Wu's novels.
D11.zh: 10到13岁时，他看新小说、新诗，模仿艾青的诗和艾芜的小说。

D9.en: He said he has administrative and organizational talent.
D9.zh: 他说他有行政和组织才能。

D15.en: At a Michigan venue, he danced to music and suddenly received applause from the crowd.
D15.zh: 在密歇根有一次，他随着音乐跳舞，突然获得场内一片掌声。

M1.en: At 26, he published many articles in top national journals.
M1.zh: 26岁时，他在国家一级刊物上发表了很多文章。

D4.en: At 18-19, he risked his life to deliver Mao's mimeographed proclamations.
D4.zh: 18到19岁时，他冒着生命危险传送毛泽东的油印文告。

D7.en: At 24-25, he confessed his relationship with his cousin to the organization and was punished.
D7.zh: 24到25岁时，他主动向组织坦白与表姐的恋情，因此受处分。

D5.en: At 18-19, he carried Mao's items while police and soldiers were everywhere, knowing it would be disastrous if caught.
D5.zh: 18到19岁时，他随身带着毛的东西，周围军警林立，查出来就不得了。

D10.en: During the Korean War, he gave passionate speeches in his department.
D10.zh: 抗美援朝时，他在系里激情演讲。

D18.en: In the 1950s, he wore a watch but didn't know the various brands others had.
D18.zh: 50年代他戴表时，不知道别人戴的各种牌子。

D13a.en: At 17, his mother thought letting another woman go to the countryside first would allow him to stay in the city.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他母亲觉得先让另一个女性下乡，这样他就能留城工作。

D12.en: As a child, he experienced the hardships of war and felt the nation's weakness.
D12.zh: 他年少时经历了抗战的苦难，体会到国家贫弱的不幸。

D3.en: He learned about relativity and non-Euclidean geometry, which changed his worldview.
D3.zh: 他了解了相对论和非欧几何，彻底改变了他的世界观。

D2.en: His grandmother rewarded him with a coin for each well-written character, so he practiced hard.
D2.zh: 祖母奖励他练字，写得好就给一个铜板，他练得很卖力。

D4.en: He helped train drivers use kinetic energy to climb slopes.
D4.zh: 他帮助机车驾驶员制定利用动能闯坡的方法。

D7.en: He took the entrance exam for Nankai twice and was admitted as a transfer student in his final year.
D7.zh: 他考了两次南开，最后一年被录取为插班生。

D16.en: He gave speeches on the street to promote the government's policies.
D16.zh: 他上街演说，宣传人民政府的政策。

D13b.en: At age 11, he left home to board at Hanmin Middle School in Guilin.
D13b.zh: 他十一岁时离开家到桂林的汉民中学住校上学。

D5.en: He experienced several close bombings by Japanese planes, with deafening explosions.
D5.zh: 他几次遇到日机投弹，爆炸声震耳欲聋。

D15.en: He ran the 5000 meters at a school sports meet and later participated in a torch relay.
D15.zh: 他参加过五千米长跑和全国运动会的火炬接力赛。

D11.en: He wrote down his thoughts in a notebook titled 'One Hundred Whys'.
D11.zh: 他把思考结果写在笔记本上，起名叫‘一百个为什么’。

D18.en: He tutored to earn extra money.
D18.zh: 他当家教赚外快。

D9.en: He and two classmates started an English wall newspaper.
D9.zh: 他和同学沈逢吉、张若衡一起办了英文壁报。

D6.en: He and two classmates started an English wall newspaper.
D6.zh: 他和同学沈逢吉、张若衡一起办了英文壁报。

D1.en: He took the Nankai entrance exam twice, admitted as a transfer student in senior year.
D1.zh: 他考了两次南开，第二次在高三被录取为插班生。

M1.en: He took the Nankai entrance exam twice, admitted as a transfer student in senior year.
M1.zh: 他考了两次南开，第二次在高三被录取为插班生。

D14.en: He invited girlfriends to watch his parallel bars performance.
D14.zh: 他邀请女朋友观看他的双杠表演。

D20.en: He and two classmates started an English wall newspaper.
D20.zh: 他和同学沈逢吉、张若衡一起办了英文壁报。

M4.en: He gave street speeches to promote government policies.
M4.zh: 他上街演说，宣传人民政府的政策。

M7.en: He chose to go to the distant cold northeast instead of staying in Shanghai.
M7.zh: 他选择了遥远寒冷的东北，没留上海。

M6.en: He believed he must learn to think independently and see things differently.
M6.zh: 他相信必须学会独立思考，用不同眼光看事物。

D3.en: At 18, he decided to become an economist.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他决定成为一名经济学家。

D14.en: At 12-15, he won a medal in a national oratorical contest sponsored by the New York Times.
D14.zh: 12到15岁时，他在《纽约时报》主办的全国演讲比赛中获奖并得到一枚奖章。

D1.en: At 11, he was moved to seventh grade in the middle of sixth grade, making him two years younger than his peers.
D1.zh: 11岁时，他在六年级中途跳级到七年级，比同学小两岁。

D9.en: At 16-20, he served as copyeditor of the student newspaper.
D9.zh: 16到20岁时，他担任学生报纸的编辑。

D18.en: He saved several hundred dollars during his undergraduate years and was effective at generating income.
D18.zh: 他大学期间存了几百美元，而且很会赚钱。

D4.en: At 18-19, he started entrepreneurial ventures in college, including a summer school for high school students.
D4.zh: 18到19岁时，他在大学开始创业，包括为高中生办暑期学校。

M4.en: At 20, he had no hesitation to study economics because of the urgent problem of unemployment.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他毫不犹豫地选择学习经济学，因为失业问题紧迫。

D6.en: He lectured part-time at Columbia and socialized with the Columbia crowd.
D6.zh: 他在哥伦比亚大学兼职讲课，并与哥大圈子社交。

D16.en: As a child, he spent a lot of time arguing about everything.
D16.zh: 小时候，他花很多时间争论各种事情。

D12.en: At 11-13, his father died, leaving him with early responsibility.
D12.zh: 11到13岁间，他父亲去世，他早早承担起责任。

D3.en: At 25, he noticed passenger traffic exceeded freight on the highway and pondered its meaning.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他注意到公路上客运多于货运，并思考其意义。

D12.en: At 20, he switched to sociology mid-stream.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他半路出家进入社会学领域。

D2.en: At 24-25, he was sent to measure soldiers at a military camp near Beijing, escorted by cavalry.
D2.zh: 24到25岁时，他被派到北京郊外的兵营测量士兵，由骑兵护送。

D9.en: At 16-17, his brother ran a school for workers and peasants, and he helped with the work.
D9.zh: 16到17岁时，他哥哥办了一所面向工农的学校，他协助办学。

D13b.en: At 10, his mother took him to the principal, and he became one of the few male students at Zhenhua Girls' School.
D13b.zh: 10岁时，母亲带他去见校长，他成了振华女校少数男学生之一。

D6.en: At 20-23, he often talked for hours with classmates by the lake, being very talkative.
D6.zh: 20到23岁间，他常和同学临湖长谈，很健谈。

D11.en: At 14, he published a piece encouraging a friend to struggle against difficulties.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他发表作品鼓励朋友在阻力中猛斗。

D10.en: At 20-23, he felt unsatisfied and even depressed, unable to find a way out.
D10.zh: 20到23岁间，他感到不满足甚至苦闷，一时找不到出路。

D4.en: At 25, he set up three bricks by his bed to cook food himself.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他在床头支了三块砖烧火煮东西吃。

D18.en: He and Fei Dasheng and others each invested 500 yuan to co-found a silkworm egg farm in Wujiang.
D18.zh: 他和费达生等5人每人入股500元在吴江合办友声蚕种场。

D16.en: He argued that sociology must engage with Chinese society, not just theory.
D16.zh: 他主张社会学不能离开中国社会实际。

D5.en: He risked sneaking alone to copy 36 god names, trembling as if stealing.
D5.zh: 他冒险独自偷抄神名，浑身打颤像做贼。

D15.en: He climbed 500 meters up a mountain, step by step, as if endless.
D15.zh: 他一步一级爬到500米高处，像走无尽路程。

D7.en: He opposed deceptive statements, advocating honesty and truth.
D7.zh: 他不赞成欺骗，主张诚实坦白实话实说。

D1.en: He calmly and effectively advanced research despite great difficulties.
D1.zh: 他在重重困难中平心静气推进研究计划。

M2.en: At 25, he hoped to set Chinese culture on the right path.
M2.zh: 25岁时，他希望让中国文化走上正确道路。

D14.en: He started submitting articles to the magazine Youth.
D14.zh: 他开始向《少年》杂志投稿。

D13a.en: He disliked philosophy class and read his own books during lectures.
D13a.zh: 他不喜欢哲学课，在课堂上看自己有兴趣的书。

D17.en: He caught pneumonia during a protest and spent over a month in hospital.
D17.zh: 他在游行中受寒得肺炎，住院一个多月。

D20X.en: He disliked Zhang Dongsun's philosophy class and read his own books during it.
D20X.zh: 他不喜欢张东荪的哲学课，在课堂上看自己的书。

M8.en: He defined religious fervor as focusing one's will entirely on a goal or person and serving with one's whole life.
M8.zh: 他说宗教热忱是一门心思把意志着眼于目标或人物，全生命来为他服务。

M4.en: He volunteered to be a pathfinder for developing Chinese humanities.
M4.zh: 他自愿要做发展中国人文学科的探路人。

M7.en: He offered objective opinions on treating special ethnic groups for adoption.
M7.zh: 他以客观态度贡献对待特种民族的意见，以备采纳。

M6.en: He expressed this idea clearly in the afterword of "The Social Organization of the Hualan Yao."
M6.zh: 他在《花蓝瑶社会组织》后记中把这个意思表达得清楚明白。

M1.en: He valued degrees, calling himself a scholar-official, and strove for an unattained degree before liberation.
M1.zh: 他一直重视学位，自称科甲出身，解放前还在为一个没得到的学位努力。

D4.en: At 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an radio in bed and had it published via a foreign news agency.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，然后由关在汉以外国通讯社的名义发表出去。

D3.en: At 16-17, he read Marx's Capital, Ai Siqi's Popular Philosophy, Gorky's In the World, and Lu Xun's complete works.
D3.zh: 16到17岁间，他读了马克思的《资本论》、艾思奇的《大众哲学》、高尔基的《在人间》以及《鲁迅全集》等。

D2.en: At 20, he resumed studies at Jinling University in Nanjing, majoring in economics.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他在南京金陵大学复学，就读经济系。

D15.en: At 20, he was busy all day and never tired.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他整天忙碌，不知疲倦。

D12.en: At age 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an radio daily in bed and had Guan Zaihan publish it under a foreign news agency's name.
D12.zh: 17岁时他天天躺在床上偷听延安广播，让关在汉以外国通讯社名义发表

D7.en: At 17, he opposed collaborating with reactionary forces.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他反对与反动势力同流合污。

D16.en: At 22, he would go to the podium to criticize teachers if their lectures contradicted Capital.
D16.zh: 22岁时，他发现老师的讲授有悖《资本论》的理论，就会走上台去开展大批判。

D13a.en: At 18, he obediently chose the College of Liberal Arts.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他乖乖地选了文学院。

D9.en: At 17, he lay in bed secretly listening to Yan'an radio, and Guan Zaihan published it under a foreign news agency.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，由关在汉以外国通讯社的名义发表出去。

D20.en: At age 22, he would go to the podium to criticize teachers if their lectures contradicted Das Kapital.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他会走上讲台批判与《资本论》相悖的讲授。

M4.en: At 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an broadcasts in bed and had them published via a foreign news agency.
M4.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，然后由关在汉以外国通讯社的名义发表出去。

D6.en: At 22, he joined the Chinese Communist Party.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他加入了中国共产党。

D5.en: At 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an broadcasts in bed and had them published via a foreign news agency.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，然后由关在汉以外国通讯社的名义发表出去。

D11.en: At 16-17, the novel 'What Is to Be Done?' influenced his worldview.
D11.zh: 16到17岁时，小说《怎么办？》影响了他的世界观。

D18.en: At 17, he secretly listened to Yan'an broadcasts in bed and had them published via a foreign news agency.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他天天躺在床上秘密收听延安广播，然后由关在汉以外国通讯社的名义发表出去。

D19.en: At 17, he recorded the broadcasts word by word and copied them for distribution.
D19.zh: 17岁时，他一字一句地记录下来，复写多份在熟人中传播。

D20X.en: At 17, he and Guan Zaihan opposed collaborating with reactionary forces.
D20X.zh: 17岁时，他和关在汉坚决反对与反动势力同流合污。

M2.en: At 24, he told his mother not to sit on the sofa because the proletariat sits on stools.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他对母亲说：“你以后不要坐沙发了，无产阶级都坐凳子。”

M3.en: At 16-17, the protagonist Rakhmetov became his idol.
M3.zh: 16到17岁时，主角拉赫美托夫成了他的偶像。

M6.en: At 16-17, he believed his mother's constitutional ideals were illusory and that only revolution could save China.
M6.zh: 16到17岁时，他认为母亲的宪政理想是虚幻的，只有革命才能拯救中国。

M8.en: At 16-17, he was first influenced by the revolutionary novel 'What Is to Be Done?'.
M8.zh: 16到17岁时，他第一次被革命小说《怎么办？》影响。

D13b.en: At 24, he was assigned to the Institute of Economics of the Chinese Academy of Sciences.
D13b.zh: 24岁时，他被分配到中国科学院经济研究所。

D5.en: At 17-18, he entered the army and was sent to the Italian front after training.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他参军并在训练后被派往意大利前线。

D12.en: At 4, his father died.
D12.zh: 4岁时，他父亲去世了。

D13b.en: His widowed mother remarried into a wealthy Viennese family, allowing him to mix with high society.
D13b.zh: 他丧偶的母亲再婚嫁入维也纳富裕贵族家庭，使他得以接触上流社会。

D3.en: At 24, he wrote a thesis on imputation for Weiser.
D3.zh: 24岁时，他为维塞尔写了一篇关于归算的论文。

D4.en: At 24, he wrote 'Das Wesen und der Hauptinhalt der theoretischen Nationalökonomie' in Cairo.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他在开罗写了《理论国民经济学的本质与主要内容》。

D2.en: After working in the math department for two years, he switched to economics.
D2.zh: 在数学系工作两年后，他转到了经济学。

D6.en: In his 20s, he participated in Mises' private seminar.
D6.zh: 20多岁时，他参加了米塞斯的私人研讨会。

D9.en: At 22-23, while finishing his law degree, he formed a discussion group called the Geistkreis.
D9.zh: 22到23岁时，在完成法律学位期间，他成立了一个名为Geistkreis的讨论小组。

D1.en: He recruited over 500 of 3,000 students, then worked less successfully to enlist graduate students at the University of Chicago.
D1.zh: 他招募了3000名学生中的500多人，随后在芝加哥大学招募研究生时成效较差。

M1.en: At 24, he wrote a thesis for Weiser on imputation.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他为韦泽写了一篇关于归因的论文。

M4.en: At 19, he read his first systematic books on economics, given by a fellow officer.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他读了第一本系统的经济学书籍，是一位军官同事给他的。

D11.en: At 24, he wrote a letter to the New York Times published as 'Germany’s Finance'.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他给《纽约时报》写了一封信，于1923年8月19日以“德国的财政”为题发表。

D3.en: At 17-18, he asked his teacher whether truth itself changes, stumping him.
D3.zh: 17到18岁时，他问老师真理本身变不变，老师答不上来。

D11.en: At 11, he learned the four tones by memorizing 'tian zi sheng zhe'.
D11.zh: 11岁时，他背「天子圣哲」学四声。

D16.en: At 15-16, he overheard a poem being revised and learned how to polish verses.
D16.zh: 15到16岁时，他旁听改诗，懂得诗句要这样推敲。

D2.en: From age 7 to 16, he read selected passages from classics like Shiji and Guwen Guanzhi, not full recitation.
D2.zh: 7到16岁间，他选读《史记》《古文观止》等，不全篇背诵。

D12.en: At 14, he witnessed a dead body and felt sorrow.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他亲眼看到一具尸体，感到悲伤。

D6.en: At 19, he went ice skating on a lake at night with classmates.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他晚上跟同学去湖上溜冰。

D14.en: At 25, he attended a lecture, got a signature, and reported it in a publication.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他听了演讲并排队请演讲者签名，报道刊登在《海澜》上。

D10.en: At 19, he paced the dormitory corridor for days, lost in thought, noticed by friends.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他在宿舍走廊来回走动苦想，被同学看出有心事。

D13a.en: At 25, he had to apply for a passport through Taiwan's Ministry of Education and Ministry of Foreign Affairs.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他必须通过台湾的教育部和外交部申请出国护照。

D15.en: At 19, he went ice skating on a lake at night with classmates.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他晚上跟同学去湖上溜冰。

M1.en: At 20, he believed only China was his home and academic research his ideal life.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他以为只有中国本土才是他安身立命的所在，而学术研究是他最向往的人生。

M4.en: At 19, he vehemently refuted someone's factual claims using propaganda clichés he had just picked up.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他声色俱厉驳斥对方，理由来自刚捡来的宣传八股。

D7.en: At 25, Taiwan's underground agents reported him as anti-KMT 'third force' without investigation.
D7.zh: 25岁时，台湾地下人员未经调查就密报他是反国民党的「第三势力」。

D9.en: At 24, he was invited to join a group and agreed to do editing and proofreading only.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他受邀加入，声明只参与审稿编辑。

D4.en: At 23-24, he collected all changes in society, economy, politics, and thought from Hou Han Shu onto cards.
D4.zh: 23到24岁间，他把《后汉书》中社会、经济、政治、思想变动都记在卡片上。

D19.en: From age 23 to 25, he accumulated over a thousand cards, laying the foundation for his Chinese history research.
D19.zh: 23到25岁间，他积累了一千多张卡片，奠定中国史研究基础。

M2.en: At 20, he felt too selfish for pursuing personal interests without considering his father's situation.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他觉得自己太自私，只考虑个人兴趣，没顾及父亲的处境。

M7.en: At 20, he insisted on returning to mainland China to serve his country.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他一意要回中国本土，为国家尽力。

M3.en: At 19-20, he deeply admired Mr. Qian.
M3.zh: 19到20岁时，他对钱先生景仰之至。

D3.en: He took the train to Berlin and explored the university library there.
D3.zh: 他坐火车去柏林，探索了那里的大学图书馆。

D12.en: From 1887, he and his mother relied on each other for love and support.
D12.zh: 从1887年起，他和母亲互相依靠，给予爱和支持。

D13b.en: In fall 1907, he courted and married Gladys Ricardo Seaver on November 5.
D13b.zh: 1907年秋，他追求并娶了格拉迪斯·里卡多·西弗，11月5日结婚。

D2.en: He met the challenge of the Faculty of Law's hard work day and night from 1903 to 1906.
D2.zh: 1903到1906年，他日夜努力，应对法学院的严格要求。

M1.en: He established his mastery of combining hard work with a good life.
M1.zh: 他确立了自己将艰苦工作与美好生活相结合的本领。

D9.en: His three papers were published in the Austrian statistical bulletin in 1905.
D9.zh: 他的三篇论文于1905年在奥地利统计公报上发表。

D14.en: He conversed with wit and facility, finessing into higher circles in 1906-1907.
D14.zh: 1906到1907年，他机智健谈，巧妙进入更高阶层。

D6.en: On July 3, 1925, he dined at Kobenzl, walked to Grinzing, and talked to his mother about getting engaged.
D6.zh: 1925年7月3日，他在科本茨尔用餐，步行到格林津，与母亲谈论订婚。

D15.en: He hunted foxes and rode his private horse in Hyde Park in 1906-1907.
D15.zh: 1906到1907年，他在海德公园猎狐并骑自己的马。

D5.en: He arranged a position with a law firm in Egypt and sailed there with Gladys in 1907.
D5.zh: 1907年，他在埃及一家律所谋得职位，与格拉迪斯乘船前往。

D10.en: At 22, he was in a tense atmosphere likely discussing G. and perhaps her abortion.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他身处紧张气氛中，可能是在讨论G.和她的堕胎。

M6.en: He knew he wanted to be an economist.
M6.zh: 他知道自己想成为经济学家。

D18.en: He found a position in Egypt and became an associate in an Italian law firm in Cairo.
D18.zh: 他在埃及找到一份工作，成为开罗一家意大利律师事务所的合伙人。

D1.en: His book sold fewer than a thousand copies and German scholars took little notice.
D1.zh: 他的书卖了不到一千本，德国学者们几乎没有注意。

D11.en: He probably kept a diary and may have recorded pessimism and depression.
D11.zh: 他可能记过日记，也许记录过悲观和抑郁。

D4.en: He decided while in Berlin to write a book explaining economic theory to Germans.
D4.zh: 他在柏林时决定写一本书向德国人解释经济理论。

D19.en: He gave himself grades as if writing report cards from a dutiful son to his mother.
D19.zh: 他给自己打分，就像在写一个孝顺儿子给母亲的成绩单。

D7.en: He decided while in Berlin to write a book explaining economic theory to Germans.
D7.zh: 他在柏林时决定写一本书向德国人解释经济理论。

M4.en: He especially wanted to bring peace between theorists and the historical school.
M4.zh: 他特别希望让理论家和历史学派和解。

D20.en: He organized a demonstration against a professor and led students invading the class with a seltzer bottle.
D20.zh: 他组织了一次反对教授的示威，带领学生拿着苏打水瓶冲进教室。

D16.en: He decided in Berlin to write a book explaining economic theory to Germans.
D16.zh: 他在柏林决定写一本向德国人解释经济理论的书。

D13a.en: He became engaged to Gladys Ricarde Seaver in August 1907.
D13a.zh: 1907年8月，他与格拉迪斯·里卡德·西弗订婚。

D20X.en: He submitted the book to the Faculty of Law.
D20X.zh: 他把书提交给了法学院。

M5.en: He was seen on terms of equality in houses above the smart set.
M5.zh: 他被视为与上流社会之上的家庭平等相处。

M8.en: He learned to excel in diverse circumstances, like successful economic players in capitalism.
M8.zh: 他学会了在各种环境中脱颖而出，就像资本主义体系中的成功经济参与者一样。

D2.en: At 13, he entered Changzhou Prefectural Middle School and, encouraged by seniors, passed the exam to skip a grade.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他考入常州府中学堂，在高年级同学鼓励下报考二年级并被录取。

M1.en: At 16, he passed the county exam and became a xiucai (scholar) with the top rank.
M1.zh: 16岁时，他县试入泮，以案首第一名成为秀才。

D9.en: After failing the provincial exam repeatedly, he set up a private school at home and taught over forty students.
D9.zh: 多次乡试不中后，他在家设馆授徒，前后有四十余人来求学。

D7.en: He demanded absolute freedom over school administration and curriculum as a condition for taking a job.
D7.zh: 他要求对学校行政和课程编排有绝对自由，否则不去。

D3.en: He treasured his late brother's annotated book set and carried it everywhere, rereading it for new insights.
D3.zh: 他珍惜长兄留下的圈点本，走到哪里都带着，每读一次都有新体会。

D11.en: At 16, he read Tan Sitong's 'Renxue' so intently that he finished it outdoors in the dark and cut off his queue the next day.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他读谭嗣同的《仁学》入迷，在院子里读到天黑，第二天就剪了辫子。

D14.en: At 25-26, he directed a student to practice a fisherman song for a school performance, preparing costume and props.
D14.zh: 25到26岁时，他指导一名学生练习《老渔翁歌》并准备蓑笠，让其在游艺会上独唱。

D16.en: At 8, he performed as both Zhuge Liang and Zhang Zhao in a recitation with acting.
D16.zh: 8岁时，他背诵兼表演，一人分饰诸葛亮和张昭。

D13a.en: His brother arranged his marriage to the Shen family when he was 16.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他哥哥替他定婚沈家。

D12.en: At 18, he told his teacher he needed to support his family and chose a tuition-free normal school.
D12.zh: 18岁时，他告诉老师因家贫需自立养家，选择免学费的师范学校。

D1.en: At 17, after recovering from illness, he returned to school and learned of the revolution, staying to join.
D1.zh: 17岁时，病愈返校途中得知革命军起义，决定留校等待投效。

D5.en: At 25, he stood up and asked a friend if he would hire him, insisting it was not a joke.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他站起来问朋友是否愿意聘他，并强调绝非戏言。

D6.en: At 12, he and his brother were among eight students sent by boat to Changzhou Middle School.
D6.zh: 12岁时，他和哥哥等八名学生被派船送到常州中学。

D15.en: At 17, after three months of illness, he ate meat for the first time and wanted to return to school the next day.
D15.zh: 17岁时，病三月后首次进荤食，次日即欲返校。

M4.en: At 19, he decided overnight to switch to science because he thought China's technology was weak.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他因觉得中国科技不行，一夜之间决定改学理科。

M7.en: At 17, he served as drill instructor for the village militia, training his uncles.
M7.zh: 17岁时，他担任团练教官，训练伯叔父们。

D10.en: At 11, he angrily asked the supervisor what format to fill in.
D10.zh: 11岁时，他愤慨地大声问监督该填什么格式。

D4.en: At 25-26, he played the qin while a student sang, then stopped abruptly, leaving the student singing on.
D4.zh: 25到26岁间，他弹琴让学生跟唱，突然停琴，学生歌声仍续。

D19.en: He meticulously verified citations, once spending days with a clerk to find a Zhu Xi quote's volume number.
D19.zh: 他仔细核对引文出处，曾与书记翻查数日才找到朱子一条的卷数。

D2.en: At 24, he spent Sunday talking to someone who made many important criticisms.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他花了一个周日与某人交谈，对方提出了许多重要批评。

D3.en: At 17, he investigated the comparative lengths of long poems.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他调查了一些长诗的长度比较。

D10.en: At 7, he said he had been preparing for days for a great effort.
D10.zh: 7岁时，他说自己为一次巨大努力准备了多日。

D6.en: At 18, he attended the first meeting of the Literary Society.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他参加了文学社的第一次会议。

D9.en: At 24, he had an interesting time at Cambridge and heard everything from Pigou.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他在剑桥度过有趣时光，从皮古那里听说了所有事。

D15.en: At 16, he went out on the river every day and enjoyed rowing immensely.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他每天去河边，非常享受划船。

M1.en: At 14, he came out top of the division in the fortnightly order.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他在双周排名中位列分部第一。

D19.en: At 18, he found that when appointed to a committee, he was made to do all the work.
D19.zh: 18岁时，他发现被任命到委员会后，所有工作都落在他身上。

D20.en: At 18, he was elected to the Committee of Management of the School Stores.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他被选入学校商店管理委员会。

D1.en: At age 24, he said he thought it was sheer bad luck and a little wickedness on their part.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他说他认为这纯粹是运气不好，加上对方有点坏心眼。

D14.en: He sang a song before an audience of nearly fifty last night.
D14.zh: 他昨晚在近五十名观众面前唱了一首歌。

D4.en: He nailed art muslin to a goal post and launched it from a tower window.
D4.zh: 他把艺术棉布钉在球门柱上，从塔楼窗户发射出去。

D11.en: He escaped into the fantasy world of Victorian melodrama, comedy, pantomime, and opera each weekend.
D11.zh: 他每个周末都逃进维多利亚时代情节剧、喜剧、哑剧和歌剧的幻想世界。

D5.en: He felt black and aimless while doing his papers.
D5.zh: 他做论文时感到沮丧且漫无目的。

M4.en: He was with Moore absolutely on all things, even secondary qualities.
M4.zh: 他在所有事情上都完全支持摩尔，甚至包括次要性质。

M2.en: As an only son, he carried the whole burden of his parents' hopes.
M2.zh: 作为独子，他承担了父母全部的希望。

M3.en: He struggled to fulfill his father's hopes, with Fawcett's moral example before him.
M3.zh: 他努力实现父亲的期望，以福塞特的道德榜样为指引。

D18.en: He defended free trade in a speech.
D18.zh: 他在一次演讲中为自由贸易辩护。

M7.en: Free trade was his only political cause before the First World War.
M7.zh: 第一次世界大战前，自由贸易是他唯一的政治事业。

D12.en: At 12, he became an apprentice at Pan Xulun's accounting firm.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他进入立信会计师事务所当练习生。

D2.en: He practiced writing 3000 characters nightly and studied English.
D2.zh: 他每晚刻3000字，还自学英文。

D9.en: At 22-23, he creatively proposed using clubs to unite people.
D9.zh: 22到23岁间，他创造性地提出用联谊会、俱乐部团结群众。

D11.en: At 17-19, he wrote accounting textbooks, published at 19.
D11.zh: 17到19岁间，他编写会计讲义，19岁出版《银行会计》。

M4.en: At 20, he joined the Communist Party and did underground work.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他加入中国共产党，从事地下工作。

D7.en: At 21-22, he published essays attacking the Kuomintang.
D7.zh: 21到22岁间，他发表杂文抨击国民党。

D6.en: At 12-13, he hiked to Labor University every Saturday.
D6.zh: 12到13岁时，他每周六远足到劳动大学。

D3.en: From 12 to 20, he read widely and pondered social changes.
D3.zh: 12到20岁间，他大量阅读，思考社会变迁。

D5.en: At 25, he was transferred to the New Fourth Army base.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他被调往苏南抗日根据地。

D4.en: At 14, he engraved copper plates at night, 1500 characters each.
D4.zh: 14岁时，他夜间刻铜版，每张1500字。

D16.en: He published political essays attacking the Nationalists for not resisting Japan, posing as a centrist.
D16.zh: 他以中间派身份发表时论杂文，抨击国民党不抗日反共。

D1.en: He was driven out of class within a week of teaching, but a year later he held his ground as a teacher.
D1.zh: 他上课不到一周被轰下台，一年后再当老师就站住了脚。

M7.en: He gradually accepted Marxism to find an effective way to save the country and the people.
M7.zh: 他为了救国救民逐渐接受了马克思主义。

D18.en: As an apprentice, he handled reception, bank deposits, and formal business like company registration and trademark applications.
D18.zh: 他当练习生时负责接待、送款，还做公司登记和商标注册等正式业务。

D15.en: At 12-13, he went on long hikes every Saturday from 5 PM to the Labor University.
D15.zh: 12到13岁时，他每周六下午五点就远足到劳动大学。

D13b.en: He had to stop schooling after junior high because his family could no longer afford tuition.
D13b.zh: 他因家庭无力承担学费，初中毕业后不得不停止学业。

D19.en: He read widely and took copious notes, filling his notebooks densely.
D19.zh: 他看得多记得勤，笔记本上记得密密麻麻。

D17.en: At just over ten years old, he took on the responsibility of supporting his family.
D17.zh: 他年仅十多岁就承担起赡养家庭的重任。

M1.en: He was intelligent and diligent, quickly mastering accounting and reaching academic heights.
M1.zh: 他聪颖好学，很快掌握会计学并攀上学术高峰。

M2.en: He had to study and work hard to improve his family's finances.
M2.zh: 他必须勤奋学习和工作来改善家庭经济条件。

M5.en: He taught at night school and managed correspondence courses, gaining a reputation in Shanghai's accounting and education circles.
M5.zh: 他在夜校兼课并负责函授行政工作，在上海会计界和教育界小有名气。

M6.en: His lofty ideals made him willing to give up a comfortable life.
M6.zh: 崇高的理想使他愿意放弃优越的生活条件。

M1.en: At 17, he was admitted to Tsinghua University in late summer 1934.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他1934年夏末考取了清华大学。

D6.en: He ranked 13th among over 1,000 candidates, while a friend ranked 9th.
D6.zh: 一千多人投考，他考了第十三名，朋友考了第九名。

D3.en: At 25, he discovered 16 rare Yunnan gazetteers at the University of Washington's Far Eastern Library.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他在华盛顿大学远东图书馆发现了16种国会所无的云南方志。

D9.en: At 23, he privately resolved to test his postdoc research by trying to publish in top Western history and social science journals.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他私下决心把博士后国史研究打进西方一流历史社科期刊。

D14.en: At 11-12, he practiced the difficult role of Sun Wukong, leaping and crashing onto a bed with a prop spear.
D14.zh: 11到12岁时，他主动扮孙悟空，接枪摔在床上啪嚓作响。

D15.en: At 11-12, he built basic skills that later let him perform as Zhang Fei in a play, winning audience applause.
D15.zh: 11到12岁时，他自练基本功，后来扮演张飞唱做武打博得观众欢迎。

D11.en: At 11-12, he sang and played the huqin by himself, with a clear voice tinged with slight hoarseness, in the style of Tan Xinpei.
D11.zh: 11到12岁时，他自拉自唱，嗓音清亮略带沙哑，唱的是谭派腔调。

D13b.en: At 11.5, he passed the entrance exam and entered Nankai Middle School in summer 1928.
D13b.zh: 11岁半时，他于1928年夏秋之际考进南开中学。

D13a.en: At 16, he had to forge a transfer certificate in Beijing in early 1933.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，他于1933年初在北平不得不假造一张转学证书。

D1.en: At 13, he won first place in the 100m dash in fall 1932, then quit sports to focus on preparing for Tsinghua.
D1.zh: 13岁时，他于1932年秋获百米第一，之后挂钉鞋不再运动，专心准备投考清华。

D4.en: At 21, his second paper 'Zhang Yinhuan's Life' was published in Tsinghua Journal in 1940.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他的第二篇习作论文《张荫桓事迹》于1940年刊于《清华学报》。

M7.en: At 19, he got angry at those who only knew the Third International and lacked national consciousness.
M7.zh: 19岁时，他大怒，认为那些只知第三国际的人没有国家观念。

D10.en: At 25, he rushed back to hear Sima Tan's deathbed will and resolved to become Grand Historian.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他于前110年春遄返河洛亲聆司马谈病床遗嘱，立志继父为太史令。

D3.en: At 20, he reviewed a brochure titled The Christ by pastor Pierre Jeannet.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他评论了牧师皮埃尔·雅内写的小册子《基督》。

D4.en: As a schoolboy, he was curator of molluscs at the Natural History Museum in Neuchâtel.
D4.zh: 他上学时在纳沙泰尔自然历史博物馆担任软体动物馆长。

D6.en: He belonged to the Club of the Friends of Nature from June 1910 to September 1915.
D6.zh: 他从1910年6月到1915年9月属于自然之友俱乐部。

D9.en: He presented a paper to the Neuchâtel group of the Swiss Christian Students' Association.
D9.zh: 他向瑞士基督教学生协会纳沙泰尔小组提交了一篇论文。

D13b.en: He finished his doctoral dissertation on the classification of mollusks.
D13b.zh: 他完成了关于软体动物分类的博士论文。

D12.en: He wrote a letter of resignation from the Friends of Nature, dated 25 September 1915.
D12.zh: 他写了一封退出自然之友的辞职信，日期是1915年9月25日。

M1.en: He received the highest grade of six in all but two of his courses and for his dissertation.
M1.zh: 他在除两门课外的所有课程和论文中都获得了最高分六分。

D14.en: Tradition demanded that he give a talk; he began by quoting Paul Godet.
D14.zh: 传统要求他做演讲；他引用保罗·戈代开始。

D16.en: At 15, his first professional article opened with an anecdote about his relationship with Godet.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他的第一篇专业文章以一则关于他与戈代关系的轶事开头。

D15.en: At 14-17, he gathered mollusks at different altitudes from lake shores to mountain heights.
D15.zh: 14到17岁间，他在不同海拔采集软体动物，从湖岸到山巅。

M6.en: At 19-20, he had an exalted acceptance of a redeeming mission to regenerate humanity and Christianity.
M6.zh: 19到20岁间，他崇高接受了拯救人类和基督教的使命。

D8.en: At 16-18, he was involved in a debate about classification of deep-water Limnaea from Lake Geneva.
D8.zh: 16到18岁间，他参与了一场关于日内瓦湖深水椎实螺分类的辩论。

M4.en: At 19-20, he revolted against political and religious orthodoxies deemed responsible for the war.
M4.zh: 19到20岁间，他反抗被认为应对战争负责的政治和宗教正统。

D5.en: At 19, he suppressed his most openly anti-German statements from Mission at the secretary's behest.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他应秘书要求从《使命》中删除了最公开的反德言论。

D13a.en: At age 19, Piaget suppressed anti-German statements from Mission at a secretary's request.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，皮亚杰应秘书要求从《使命》中删除了最公开的反德言论。

D7.en: At 19-20, he revolted against political and religious orthodoxies blamed for WWI.
D7.zh: 19到20岁时，他反抗被认为导致一战的政治和宗教正统。

M7.en: At age 21, he stated that socialism and human sentiment are one.
M7.zh: 21岁时，他说社会主义和人类情感是一体的。

D13b.en: He received his graduation record from a Jewish gymnasium in Gomel in 1913.
D13b.zh: 1913年他在戈梅利的犹太中学拿到了毕业证书。

D2.en: As secretary of the society, he made meticulous notes of meetings.
D2.zh: 作为学会秘书，他仔细记录了会议内容。

M1.en: He graduated with excellent grades and a gold medal in 1913.
M1.zh: 1913年他以优异成绩毕业并获得金质奖章。

D13a.en: Within months of admission, he transferred to the Faculty of Jurisprudence.
D13a.zh: 入学后仅几个月，他就转到了法学系。

D6.en: In 1923, he received a letter from the founder of psychoanalysis.
D6.zh: 1923年，他收到了精神分析创始人的一封信。

D9.en: He founded the Kazan Psychoanalytic Society in late summer 1922.
D9.zh: 1922年夏末，他创立了喀山精神分析学会。

D20.en: He founded the Kazan Psychoanalytic Society in late summer 1922.
D20.zh: 1922年夏末，他创立了喀山精神分析学会。

D4.en: He set up a small psychology lab and performed his first experiments on dominant reactions and respiration.
D4.zh: 他建立了一个小型心理学实验室，并进行了关于优势反应和呼吸的首次实验。

D12.en: At 24, he fell seriously ill for the first time and was sent to a sanatorium.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他第一次病重，被送进了疗养院。

D1.en: Expecting to die, he asked a former professor to publish his writings posthumously, but recovered.
D1.zh: 他以为自己要死了，请一位前教授在他死后出版他的作品，但后来康复了。

D16.en: At age 20, Luria gave a talk on psychoanalysis of dress.
D16.zh: 20岁时，卢里亚做了关于着装心理分析的演讲。

D5.en: At 20, he took the liberty of informing Freud about a major event in psychological history.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他擅自通知弗洛伊德心理学史上的一个重大事件。

D8.en: At 15, he disapproved of his sister reading Balzac and Dumas as too risqué.
D8.zh: 15岁时，他反对妹妹读巴尔扎克和大仲马，认为太露骨。

D20.en: At 18, he led a students' strike over religion just before graduating.
D20.zh: 18岁时，他在毕业前领导了一场关于宗教的学生罢课。

D9.en: As a child and teenager, he helped his siblings with lessons and lectured them about the world.
D9.zh: 他小时候和十几岁时，帮兄弟姐妹辅导功课，还给他们讲世界知识。

D20X.en: At 42-43, Rank kept sending him sharp criticisms on how he edited the Journal.
D20X.zh: 42到43岁时，兰克不断对他编辑期刊的方式提出尖锐批评。

D14.en: As a child and teenager, he excelled in classical literature and history at school.
D14.zh: 他小时候和十几岁时，在学校古典文学和历史成绩优异。

D7.en: At 10-12, his father began taking him along on walks.
D7.zh: 10到12岁时，他父亲开始带他一起散步。

D12.en: At 24, he began smoking, first cigarettes then only cigars.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他开始吸烟，先是香烟，后来只抽雪茄。

M6.en: As a teenager, he became enthralled with Freud's writings.
M6.zh: 十几岁时，他迷上了弗洛伊德的著作。

M1.en: As a schoolboy, he was imagined to have a glorious career.
M1.zh: 他上学时，别人想象他会有辉煌的事业。

D16.en: At 17, his professor told him he had an idiotic style, which Herder called correct and characteristic.
D16.zh: 17岁时，教授告诉他，他有赫尔德所说的那种既正确又独特的白痴风格。

D1.en: At age 3-4, he resisted his nurse and tried to win her over.
D1.zh: 三四岁时，他反抗保姆，试图讨好她。

M4.en: He led a students' strike over religion after a row with a teacher.
M4.zh: 他与宗教老师争吵后，领导了学生罢课，起因是宗教问题。

M3.en: As a teenager, he identified closely with his hero Hannibal.
M3.zh: 十几岁时，他深深认同自己的英雄汉尼拔。

D10.en: At 16-18, he experienced depression, irrational hostility, and suicidal thoughts.
D10.zh: 16到18岁时，他经历了抑郁、非理性的敌意，甚至想过自杀。

D15.en: He took up tennis, swimming, and later his favorite sport, mountain climbing.
D15.zh: 他打网球、游泳，后来还登山，这是他最爱的运动。

D18.en: At 15, he arrived alone from Austria-Hungary with three dollars.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他独自一人从奥匈帝国来到这里，口袋里只有三美元。

D5.en: At age 16, a girl slapped him.
D5.zh: 16岁时，一个女孩打了他一巴掌。

D2.en: He believed mathematics was key to economic theory, a significant departure.
D2.zh: 他认为数学是经济理论的关键，这是一个重大转变。

D3.en: He worked out relationships between reserves and deposits and liquidity for a system.
D3.zh: 他算出了整个系统中储备与存款以及流动性的关系。

D12.en: At 24, he lost his father to death.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他父亲去世了。

D6.en: He was born on May 15, 1915, above his parents' drugstore.
D6.zh: 他于1915年5月15日出生在父母药店楼上。

D4.en: He considered a diplomatic career and sent for the entrance booklet.
D4.zh: 他考虑从事外交工作，并索取了入门手册。

D7.en: He was expelled and arrested for radical political activity in December 1930.
D7.zh: 1930年12月，他因激进政治活动被开除并逮捕。

D11.en: He wrote a short story that appeared in The Scroll.
D11.zh: 他写了一篇短篇小说，发表在《卷轴》上。

D15.en: He was in track for three years and in basketball and fencing in his senior year.
D15.zh: 他参加了三年田径，高年级时还参加了篮球和击剑。

D9.en: At 25, he signed a contract with Prentice-Hall to write a textbook.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他与普伦蒂斯-霍尔签约编写一本教科书。

M1.en: At 17, he won a prize for a political science essay.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他因一篇政治学论文获奖。

D16.en: At 18, he became increasingly skeptical about political legislation.
D16.zh: 18岁时，他对政治立法越来越怀疑。

D5.en: At 18, he was expelled and arrested for radical political activity.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他因激进政治活动被开除并逮捕。

D10.en: At 19, he felt sympathy and emotion, but less than the average Jew.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他感到同情和情绪，但程度低于普通犹太人。

D14.en: At 23-24, he attended burlesque shows at the Old Howard in Boston.
D14.zh: 23到24岁间，他在波士顿的老霍华德看脱衣舞表演。

M4.en: At 18, he was expelled and arrested for radical political activity.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他因激进政治活动被开除并逮捕。

M6.en: At 19, he secretly intended to transfer to Germany because his German was better.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他暗中打算转到德国，因为他德语更好。

D13a.en: At 25, he decided to accept and moved immediately, weeks into the academic year.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他决定接受并立即搬家，那时开学才几周。

D1.en: At 24, he worried he did not have long to live.
D1.zh: 24岁时，他担心自己活不长了。

D20.en: At 25, he planned to co-author a work with Erich Roll.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他计划与埃里希·罗尔合著一部作品。

D3.en: At 23, he filled his diaries with questioning and searching.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他在日记中写满了质疑和探索。

D5.en: At age 3, he sat with one leg dangling beneath a rickety railing, mesmerized by the water.
D5.zh: 3岁时，他坐在栏杆边，一条腿悬在摇摇欲坠的栏杆下，被水迷住了。

D6.en: At 19, he was invited to join Zofingia, the fraternity his father had belonged to.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他被邀请加入他父亲曾加入的兄弟会Zofingia。

D2.en: As a child and young man, he became consumed with learning to deflect dreariness.
D2.zh: 幼年和青年时期，他埋头学习以逃避生活的沉闷。

D1.en: At 25, he openly chose the most despised branch of medicine and refused a post in Munich.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他公开选择了最受鄙视的医学分支，并拒绝了慕尼黑的职位。

M6.en: At 18-19, he felt it was his fate to become a doctor, with little choice.
M6.zh: 18到19岁时，他觉得成为医生是他的命运，几乎别无选择。

D13a.en: He rejected relatives' advice to quit medical studies and sought a way to continue.
D13a.zh: 他拒绝了亲戚让他放弃学医的建议，设法继续学业。

D12.en: He was the fourth-born and first-surviving child of a poor country parson and his unhappy wife.
D12.zh: 他是一个贫穷乡村牧师和他不快乐妻子的第四个孩子，也是第一个存活下来的。

D13b.en: He enrolled in the medical curriculum at the University of Basel on 18 April 1895.
D13b.zh: 他于1895年4月18日注册了巴塞尔大学的医学课程。

D14.en: He divided December 1-9, 1900 between visiting art museums in Munich and Stuttgart.
D14.zh: 他在1900年12月1日至9日间，分别在慕尼黑和斯图加特参观艺术博物馆。

D16.en: At 23-24, he asked a question he would repeat throughout his life.
D16.zh: 23到24岁时，他提出了一个他一生中反复问的问题。

D9.en: He invited three persons to a séance on August 18, 1897.
D9.zh: 他于1897年8月18日邀请了三个人参加降神会。

D15.en: He entered the Swiss Citizens' Army as a soldier in 1895, attending infantry school in Aarau.
D15.zh: 他于1895年作为士兵加入瑞士公民军，在阿劳参加步兵学校。

D19.en: At 20, he kept careful accounts of all household spending.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他仔细记录家庭的所有开支。

D20.en: At 22-23, he was president of the group.
D20.zh: 22到23岁间，他担任该组织的主席。

D20X.en: At 9, he hid a box on an attic beam, feeling safe because no one went there.
D20X.zh: 9岁时，他把盒子藏在阁楼的梁上，觉得安全，因为没人去那里。

D17.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler and bedded it in a pencil box.
D17.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小人，然后把它放在铅笔盒里。

D18.en: At 19-20, he sold his old aunt's antique collection on commission.
D18.zh: 19到20岁时，他代售老姑妈的古董收藏。

M2.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler and bedded it in a pencil box.
M2.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小人，然后把它放在铅笔盒里。

M3.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler and bedded it in a pencil box.
M3.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小人，然后把它放在铅笔盒里。

M5.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler and bedded it in a pencil box.
M5.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小人，然后把它放在铅笔盒里。

M7.en: At 9, he carved a manikin on a ruler and bedded it in a pencil box.
M7.zh: 9岁时，他在尺子上刻了一个小人，然后把它放在铅笔盒里。

D5.en: He crashed a Citroën 5CV given by his childhood friend Pierre Dreyfus.
D5.zh: 他撞毁了童年好友皮埃尔·德雷福斯送他的雪铁龙5CV。

D12.en: He had to put most of his modest earnings into the family coffers.
D12.zh: 他不得不把大部分微薄收入交给家里。

D3.en: At 21-22, he read widely, visited galleries, and pondered art and aesthetics.
D3.zh: 21到22岁时，他广泛阅读、参观画廊并思考艺术与美学。

D4.en: He helped his father decorate the Madagascan pavilion for the Exposition coloniale.
D4.zh: 他帮父亲装饰殖民博览会的马达加斯加展馆。

D2.en: As a teenager, he took violin lessons at the Opéra.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他在歌剧院上小提琴课。

D18.en: He crashed a Citroën 5CV given by his childhood friend Pierre Dreyfus.
D18.zh: 他撞毁了童年好友皮埃尔·德雷福斯送他的雪铁龙5CV。

D6.en: He built a lively social life around contacts with local socialist groups.
D6.zh: 他通过与当地社会主义团体接触，建立了活跃的社交生活。

D9.en: He became president of a left-wing think tank called the Groupe des onze.
D9.zh: 他成为左翼智库“十一人小组”的主席。

M1.en: At 23, he passed the agrégation on his first try, ranking third.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他第一次参加会考就通过了，排名第三。

D20.en: He became president of the left-wing think tank Groupe des onze.
D20.zh: 他成为了左翼智库十一人集团的主席。

D16.en: He wrote in a special edition of L’Étudiant socialiste.
D16.zh: 他在《社会主义学生》特刊上撰文。

D15.en: He would unwind by hiking into the mountains, following fault lines and scrambling up escarpments.
D15.zh: 他常徒步进山，沿着断层线攀爬陡坡。

M4.en: He spent eight years in meetings, talks, and publications with youthful indignation.
M4.zh: 他花了八年时间开会、演讲和发表文章，充满年轻时的愤怒和理想主义热情。

D11.en: He dreamed since childhood of being a composer or conductor.
D11.zh: 他从小梦想成为作曲家或指挥家。

D3.en: At age 9-10, he wanted to prepare duck, goose, and pigeon heads to compare them.
D3.zh: 9到10岁时，他想准备鸭、鹅和鸽子的头来比较它们。

D7.en: At 22, he said he would not be shy to defend his affairs with the sword.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他说他不怕用剑捍卫自己的事情。

D5.en: At 22, he brought home cuts, even on the nose, due to quarrels over the Jewish situation.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他带回家几处伤口，甚至鼻子上也有，因为犹太局势引发的争吵。

D6.en: At 25, he became a frequent guest at the Boases', often dining twice a week.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他成了博厄斯家的常客，经常一周吃两次晚饭。

D2.en: At 20, he scheduled himself for a daunting twenty-six hours of classes in summer semester 1878.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他在1878年夏季学期给自己安排了吓人的26小时课程。

D15.en: At 19, he swam in the North Sea and skated on Weser-flooded fields in winter.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他在北海游泳，冬天在威悉河泛滥的田野上滑冰。

D4.en: At 22, he used a pewter tube coated inside with nitrate of saltpeter oxide.
D4.zh: 22岁时，他使用了一个内部涂有硝酸盐氧化物的锡管。

D9.en: At age 19-20, he served as secretary for one semester; the minute book is in his handwriting.
D9.zh: 19到20岁时，他担任了一学期秘书；会议记录本是他的笔迹。

D12.en: At 23, he thought about how practical she must be now that her mother was gone.
D12.zh: 23岁时，他想她母亲去世后她一定很务实。

D13b.en: At 19, on November 30, 1877, he was locked in the student jail until Monday.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，1877年11月30日，他被关进学生监狱直到周一。

M4.en: At 23, he supported the failed revolution.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他支持了失败的革命。

D13a.en: At 22, he promised to avoid situations where as a Jew he would be insulted.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他承诺远离所有作为犹太人可能受辱的场合。

D1.en: At 19, he believed his duty to himself was higher than his duty to you, and so he did it.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他认为对自己的责任高于对你的责任，于是他做了。

M1.en: From age 20 to 28, he achieved in education at ages 20, 22, 24, 26, 27–28.

M6.en: At 15-16, he wanted to devote his life to botany.
M6.zh: 15到16岁时，他想献身于植物学。

M3.en: At 15-16, he felt he had to dare to do what he wanted, like Ulrich von Hutten.
M3.zh: 15到16岁时，他觉得自己必须敢于做想做的事，就像乌尔里希·冯·胡滕一样。

D20.en: At 22, he was the only student enrolled in Fischer's seminar.
D20.zh: 22岁时，他是菲舍尔研讨班上唯一的学生。

D17.en: At 25, he provided turpentine, quinine, ammonia, and opium for her illness.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他给她提供了松节油、奎宁、氨水和鸦片来治病。

D3.en: At 16, she decided to major in theology at university.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她决定在大学主修神学。

D9.en: At 15, she led her classmates in boycotting a teacher's classes.
D9.zh: 15岁时，她带领同学抵制老师的课。

D16.en: At 24, she defended herself.
D16.zh: 24岁时，她为自己辩护。

D12.en: As a small child, she was lively, moved by sad stories, and loved cheerful things.
D12.zh: 幼年时，她非常活泼，被悲伤的故事打动，喜欢一切快乐的事物。

D6.en: At 23-24, she attended musical evenings at Leopold Furtwängler's mother's home.
D6.zh: 23到24岁间，她参加了莱奥波德·富特文格勒母亲家的音乐晚会。

D11.en: At 19, she wrote something after her first year as a university student.
D11.zh: 19岁时，她在大学第一年后写了些东西。

M1.en: At 22, she completed a doctorate in philosophy at Heidelberg.
M1.zh: 22岁时，她在海德堡大学完成了哲学博士学位。

D7.en: At 14, she left home through her bedroom window and took the streetcar to Stolp alone.
D7.zh: 14岁时，她从卧室窗户离开家，独自坐有轨电车去了斯托尔普。

D13b.en: As a small child, she was sent to synagogue with her grandparents.
D13b.zh: 幼年时，她被送去和祖父母一起上犹太教堂。

D2.en: At 23, she finished revising her doctoral dissertation for publication by Springer.
D2.zh: 23岁时，她完成了博士论文的修改，准备由斯普林格出版。

D14.en: At 23, she attended a Marxists' masquerade ball in Berlin.
D14.zh: 23岁时，她参加了柏林一场马克思主义者举办的化装舞会。

D17.en: She played cards with him all summer, forbidding anyone to speak harshly to him.
D17.zh: 她整个夏天陪他打牌，不许任何人对他恶语相向。

D10.en: At 7, she watched the funeral procession from the window, proud of the crowd.
D10.zh: 7岁时，她从窗户看着送葬队伍，为这么多人送爷爷感到骄傲。

D5.en: At 14, she left through her bedroom window and took the streetcar to Stolp alone.
D5.zh: 14岁时，她从卧室窗户溜出去，独自坐电车去了斯托尔普。

M4.en: At 19, she joined the Spartacist party.
M4.zh: 19岁时，她加入了斯巴达克派。

D15.en: As a small child, she had childhood illnesses.
D15.zh: 幼年时她患有儿童疾病。

M6.en: She wrote her dissertation on Augustine's concept of love.
M6.zh: 她写了关于奥古斯丁爱的概念的论文。

D5.en: At 18-19, he got into a double-decker bus from the northern end of town near his home.
D5.zh: 18到19岁时，他上了一辆从镇北端他家附近开来的双层巴士。

D3.en: At 18, he used the University Library to look up popular books on cancer to quieten his mind.
D3.zh: 18岁时，他去大学图书馆查了几本关于癌症的通俗读物来让自己安心。

D14.en: At 25-29, some essays he wrote were accepted for publication in standard philosophy journals.
D14.zh: 25到29岁时，他写的一些文章被标准哲学期刊接受发表。

D7.en: At 22-23, there was a predictable storm of protest at his appointment.
D7.zh: 22到23岁时，他的任命引发了一场可预见的抗议风暴。

D13a.en: At age 9-10, he refused to leave his new school because he loved it.
D13a.zh: 9到10岁时，他拒绝离开新学校，因为他喜欢那里。

D9.en: He led the founding of an economics department and served as its head.
D9.zh: 他领导创立了一个经济系并担任系主任。

D16.en: He went to Baharampur for an all-Bengal debating competition and had some success.
D16.zh: 他去了巴哈拉姆普尔参加全孟加拉辩论赛，取得了一些成功。

D17.en: He gave a cigarette tin full of rice to anyone who begged for food.
D17.zh: 他把一铁罐米给任何讨饭的人。

M4.en: He became determined to do what he could to prevent famines from occurring again.
M4.zh: 他下定决心尽己所能防止饥荒再次发生。

D10.en: At 18, he was terribly dejected but also triumphant.
D10.zh: 18岁时，他极度沮丧，但也感到得意。

M1.en: He applied only to Trinity College, so certain was his choice.
M1.zh: 他只申请了三一学院，对自己的选择如此确定。

D20.en: He led the founding of an economics department and served as its head.
D20.zh: 他领导创立了一个经济系并担任系主任。

D9.en: He asked Peter Hilferding to arrange interviews for him.
D9.zh: 他请彼得·希尔弗丁为他安排采访。

D2.en: He earned money by guiding American students around the university and city.
D2.zh: 他靠带美国学生参观大学和城市赚钱。

D3.en: He systematically explored the psychology of learning in dialogue with Bühler's work.
D3.zh: 他通过与比勒作品的对话，系统探索了学习心理学。

D12.en: He left home to live with an eccentric group of political outcasts and wayward students.
D12.zh: 他离家与一群古怪的政治流亡者和任性学生同住。

D13a.en: He staged a rebellion against his parents and left school.
D13a.zh: 他反抗父母并离开了学校。

D7.en: He challenged a teacher who vowed eternal hatred to the Allies.
D7.zh: 他挑战了一位发誓永远仇恨协约国的老师。

D11.en: For two years, he thought seriously of becoming a musician.
D11.zh: 有两年时间，他认真考虑过成为音乐家。

D17.en: He could only ask his parents for a few coppers to give the poor.
D17.zh: 他只能向父母要几个铜板给穷人。

D10.en: At 13, he fell ill with rheumatic fever while depressed.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他抑郁中患上了风湿热。

D4.en: At 16, he went around Vienna in an old military uniform, eager to join the revolution.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他穿着旧军装在维也纳四处走动，渴望参与革命。

D5.en: At 16-17, he attacked a police station with others to free arrested leaders; police fired, killing 12.
D5.zh: 16到17岁间，他和数百人袭击警察局以解救被捕领袖，警察开枪打死12人。

M4.en: At 16-17, he was 'converted' to communism by Popper.
M4.zh: 16到17岁时，他被波普尔‘转变’为共产主义者。

D18.en: At 16-17, he worked as an office boy at communist headquarters.
D18.zh: 16到17岁间，他在共产党总部当勤杂工。

D1.en: At 23, he ended his involvement with socialist educational reform.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他结束了参与社会主义教育改革。

D20X.en: At 19, he left the Schoenberg circle.
D20X.zh: 19岁时，他离开了勋伯格圈子。

D16.en: At 23, his first published article echoed school-reform debates.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他发表第一篇文章，呼应学校改革辩论。

M2.en: He was excluded from Heimat on ethnic grounds and responded by subjecting ethnic and national identities to a universal humanity.
M2.zh: 他因种族原因被排除在故乡之外，并以将种族和民族身份置于普遍人性之下作为回应。

D2.en: He kept trying and eventually ended up in Philosophy.
D2.zh: 他不断尝试，最终进入了哲学领域。

D3.en: He experienced a sharp criticism that deepened his interest in philosophy.
D3.zh: 他受到尖锐批评，逐渐加深了对哲学的兴趣。

D19.en: He took clearly structured notes on what he read and memorized these summaries.
D19.zh: 他对自己读的内容做了条理清晰的笔记，并记住了这些摘要。

D12.en: With most of his class going to war, he decided to accelerate his studies.
D12.zh: 班上多数人参战，他决定加速学业。

D4.en: He owned an experimental chemistry set and produced smells and explosions with additional chemicals.
D4.zh: 他有一套化学实验器材，用教父提供的额外化学品制造气味和爆炸。

D9.en: He volunteered to go deep into the jungle to lead the General out.
D9.zh: 他自愿深入丛林，带领将军出来。

D6.en: He noticed black children attended separate schools and made friends with a black boy, visiting his home.
D6.zh: 他注意到黑人孩子上隔离学校，并与一个黑人男孩交朋友，去他家做客。

D1.en: He learned he could overcome his stammer when speaking in rhyme.
D1.zh: 他发现自己用押韵说话能克服口吃。

D15.en: He was accepted onto the freshmen football team.
D15.zh: 他被选入新生足球队。
