M8.en: He rekindled a latent interest in religion, wrote his senior thesis on it, and considered studying for the priesthood.
M8.zh: 他重燃对宗教的潜在兴趣，以此写毕业论文，并考虑去神学院学习成为牧师。

D7.en: He refused to punish a soldier as ordered by a first lieutenant.
D7.zh: 他拒绝执行中尉命令惩罚一名士兵。

M1.en: He passed the state bar exam and became a practicing lawyer at age 22.
M1.zh: 他22岁时通过州律师考试，成为执业律师。

D5.en: He drank from a stream without his helmet and an enemy bullet grazed his head, leaving a scar.
D5.zh: 他摘下头盔喝溪水时，一颗敌军子弹擦过他的头，留下疤痕。

D11.en: At 21, he wrote his undergraduate honors thesis on religious problems of sin and community.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他写了关于罪与共同体的宗教问题的本科荣誉论文。

D3.en: At 21, he read Channing or Spinoza before starting work.
D3.zh: 21岁时，他在开始工作前读了钱宁或斯宾诺莎。

D2.en: At 21, his captain was extremely satisfied with his diligence.
D2.zh: 21岁时，他的船长对他的勤奋非常满意。

D6.en: At 19, he spent Sundays at relatives' houses, calling them the bright spots of his existence.
D6.zh: 19岁时，他周日去亲戚家，称那是他生活的亮点。

D5.en: At 19, he fought customary duels in his third semester and received the ribbon.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他在第三学期参加了常规决斗并获得了绶带。

D15.en: At 20, he had a week and a half crowded with exercises and field practice.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他有一周半的时间忙于训练和野外演习。

D9.en: At 21, he hoped to be promoted in two or three weeks and experience the pleasant side of the military.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他希望在两三周内晋升，体验军队中愉快的一面。

D12.en: At 25, he considered factory inspectorate one of the hardest jobs in state administration.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他认为工厂监察是州行政中最难的工作之一。

D7.en: At 20, he was infuriated by Bismarck, but not mainly by his militarism and autocratic bearing.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他对俾斯麦感到愤怒，但主要不是因其军国主义和专制态度。

M1.en: At 25, he received his doctorate magna cum laude for his dissertation.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他以优等成绩获得博士学位。

D13b.en: At 18, he passed his Abitur and studied in Heidelberg, then switched to law and economics in Berlin.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他通过了高中毕业考试，在海德堡学习，随后转到柏林学习法律和经济学。

D4.en: At 4, he played with trains for a long time.
D4.zh: 4岁时，他玩了很久的火车玩具。

D10.en: At 20, he guiltily admitted his inability to communicate orally with those closest to him.
D10.zh: 20岁时，他愧疚地承认自己无法与最亲近的人口头交流。

D19.en: As a teenager and young adult, he drew up a precise daily plan from morning to night.
D19.zh: 十几岁到二十几岁时，他每天从早到晚制定精确的计划。

D18.en: At 18-19, he thought running up debts was the only thing to do, continuing an old tradition.
D18.zh: 18到19岁时，他认为欠债是唯一的选择，延续了旧传统。

D11.en: At 14, he wrote a letter to his mother from Erfurt, giving news about them.
D11.zh: 14岁时，他从埃尔福特给母亲写信，告知他们的消息。

M8.en: At 24, he said he owed it largely to someone if he had come closer to finding God again.
M8.zh: 24岁时，他说很大程度上多亏了某人，他才重新接近上帝。

M4.en: At 6, the outbreak of the War of 1870 made a lasting impression on him.
M4.zh: 6岁时，1870年战争的爆发给他留下了深刻印象。

D16.en: At 23, he wrote a letter to his girlfriend reflecting his thinking.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他给女友写了一封信，反映他的想法。

D17.en: At 24, he showed great solicitude and sympathy for Helene's situation.
D17.zh: 24岁时，他对海伦的处境表现出极大的关心和同情。

D2.en: At 21-23, he entered graduate school and became a Harvard Society of Fellows member.
D2.zh: 21到23岁间，他进入研究生院并成为哈佛研究员协会成员。

D6.en: He belonged to clubs and was well liked by peers as a teenager.
D6.zh: 他十几岁时加入多个俱乐部，很受同龄人喜欢。

D3.en: He was reading anarchist material by Diego Abad de Santillán at age 8-12.
D3.zh: 他8到12岁时阅读迭戈·阿瓦德·德桑蒂良等无政府主义作品。

D11.en: He published his first article at age 10, an editorial on the fall of Barcelona.
D11.zh: 他10岁时发表第一篇文章，关于巴塞罗那陷落的社论。

D7.en: He did not celebrate the end of WWII at age 16-17, unable to identify with the jubilation.
D7.zh: 他16到17岁时没有庆祝二战结束，无法认同周围的欢庆。

D5.en: He began traveling alone by train to New York City at age 13, visiting bookstores.
D5.zh: 他13岁时开始独自乘火车去纽约，逛二手书店。

M4.en: He was moving toward anarchism and away from Marxism at age 12-13.
M4.zh: 他12到13岁时逐渐转向无政府主义，远离马克思主义。

D9.en: He decided to plunge into public life, though he hated the decision.
D9.zh: 他决定投身公共生活，尽管他讨厌这个决定。

D6.en: At 25, she talked with someone for 30 hours straight.
D6.zh: 25岁时，她和一个人连续聊了30个小时。

D2.en: At 23, she hurried to learn Samoan geography and language to leave Pago Pago.
D2.zh: 23岁时，她急着学习萨摩亚地理和语言，以便离开帕果帕果。

D3.en: At 23-24, she studied house and canoe building and began writing her thesis.
D3.zh: 23到24岁时，她研究了房屋和独木舟的建造，并开始写论文。

D4.en: At 23-25, she studied the Serrano people under Alfred Kroeber at Berkeley.
D4.zh: 23到25岁时，她在伯克利跟随阿尔弗雷德·克罗伯研究了塞拉诺人。

D11.en: At 21, she wrote mediocre undergraduate poems and exchanged verses with friends.
D11.zh: 21岁时，她写了平庸的本科诗歌，并与朋友交换诗作。

D15.en: At 25, she was the only regular bather in the village.
D15.zh: 25岁时，她是村里唯一经常洗澡的人。

D14.en: At 22, she decorated the seminar room with enlarged reproductions of Polynesian designs.
D14.zh: 22岁时，她用放大的波利尼西亚图案装饰了研讨室。

D10.en: At 23-24, she felt Stanley found her sexually undesirable.
D10.zh: 23到24岁时，她觉得斯坦利认为她没有性吸引力。

D9.en: At 23, she bent all her energies toward persuading Isabel Gordon to go to the South Seas with her.
D9.zh: 23岁时，她全力说服伊莎贝尔·戈登和她一起去南太平洋。

D1.en: At 23-25, her manuscript was rejected by Harper Brothers but taken by William Morrow.
D1.zh: 23到25岁间，她的手稿被哈珀兄弟拒绝，但被威廉·莫罗接受。

M1.en: At 11-14, she won scholarships to St. Margaret's School for education.
M1.zh: 11到14岁间，她获奖学金入读圣玛格丽特学校。

M7.en: At 16, she danced for joy in an uncharacteristic outburst.
M7.zh: 16岁时，她罕见地高兴得跳起舞来。

M4.en: At 23, she traveled 9,000 miles to American Samoa to learn a new language from scratch.
M4.zh: 23岁时，她旅行9000英里到美属萨摩亚，从零开始学习一门新语言。

D8.en: She arranged ceremonies and insisted on directing this one.
D8.zh: 她一直安排仪式，这次也不愿失去指挥权。

M6.en: She was baptized and called it one of the happiest days of her life.
M6.zh: 她接受了洗礼，称这是她一生中最快乐的日子之一。

M2.en: She said she would act that way because of her upbringing.
M2.zh: 她说因为家庭教养，她也会这么做。

D16.en: She was enthusiastic about the debate squad.
D16.zh: 她对辩论队充满热情。

M8.en: She raced with her atheist parents to the baptism she insisted on.
M8.zh: 她拉着不信教的父母赶到她坚持要参加的洗礼。

D12.en: He grew up in a close-knit, orthodox Jewish family.
D12.zh: 他在一个关系紧密、正统的犹太家庭中长大。

D7.en: He was disappointed by the school's education, finding it too humanistic and hostile to science.
D7.zh: 他对学校的教育感到失望，认为它太人文学科化且敌视科学态度。

D13a.en: He decided as a schoolboy not to follow the family tradition.
D13a.zh: 他还在上学时就决定不遵循家族传统。

D3.en: He read Renan at the École Normale and found the experience distasteful.
D3.zh: 他在巴黎高师读了勒南的作品，觉得这次体验令人反感。

D2.en: After his first year at the École, he gained his licence with maximum honours.
D2.zh: 在巴黎高师第一年后，他以最高荣誉获得了学士学位。

D1.en: After two unsuccessful attempts, he was admitted to the École on his third try in 1879.
D1.zh: 经过两次失败后，他于1879年第三次尝试才被巴黎高师录取。

D9.en: He determined to be a teacher, scholar, and apostle to raise public spirit after defeat.
D9.zh: 他决心成为教师、学者和使徒，以提振战败后的公共精神。

D16.en: He discussed for hours with logical fervor that amazed his hearers.
D16.zh: 他连续数小时以逻辑热情讨论，令听者惊叹。

D6.en: He sought out discussions, often starting them and throwing himself in with passion.
D6.zh: 他主动寻找讨论，经常发起并全身心投入其中。

D14.en: He spent the entire day demonstrating in the Paris streets on July 14, 1880.
D14.zh: 1880年7月14日，他整天在巴黎街头示威。

D10.en: He groaned and vented indignation over Latin verses he couldn't handle.
D10.zh: 他对着自己搞不定的拉丁文诗句呻吟和发泄愤怒。

M1.en: He came second to last in his year.
M1.zh: 他在年级里排名倒数第二。

D12.en: At 25, he used his money to send his younger sister Catherine to college.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他用自己的一部分钱供妹妹凯瑟琳上大学。

D18.en: At 18, he enrolled in the Ontario Agricultural College.
D18.zh: 18岁时，他进入安大略农学院学习。

D2.en: At 22-25, he had academic articles published while at Berkeley.
D2.zh: 22到25岁间，他在伯克利时已经发表了一些学术文章。

D9.en: At 25, he decided to go see for himself on his first visit.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他决定亲自去看看，那是他第一次去。

D4.en: At 22, he manned an exhibit at the Canadian National and people asked why the clock had no hands.
D4.zh: 22岁时，他在加拿大国家展览会上亲自看管展品，人们问他为什么钟没有指针。

M1.en: At 25, he applied for a Royal Society of Canada fellowship for postgraduate work at Harvard.
M1.zh: 25岁时，他申请了加拿大皇家学会的奖学金，用于在哈佛大学读研。

D3.en: At 25, he thought knowledge from a class he wasn't in might be useful one day.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他觉得从一堂他没上的课上学到的知识可能有一天会有用。

D6.en: At 17, he took a train ride with two other local boys, marking the start of a fresh life.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他和另外两个当地男孩坐火车，开始了新生活。

D15.en: As a teenager, he played intramural football, track, and hockey without distinction, once knocking the puck into his own goal.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他参加校内足球、田径和冰球，表现平平，有一次不小心把球打进了自家球门。

D14.en: He manned an exhibit himself and faced annoyed questions about a clock with no hands.
D14.zh: 他自己看展台，人们生气地问他为什么钟没有指针。

D10.en: He skipped the social events and felt he wouldn't be missed.
D10.zh: 他没参加社交活动，觉得没人会想念他。

D5.en: He drove from Ohio to California in a broken old car that guzzled gas and oil.
D5.zh: 他开着一辆破旧耗油的汽车从俄亥俄到加利福尼亚。

D7.en: He found the school atmosphere repellent under a tyrannical principal.
D7.zh: 他觉得学校氛围令人反感，因为校长很专制。

D13a.en: His father arranged for him to repeat his senior year elsewhere.
D13a.zh: 他父亲安排他去别处重读高三。

M4.en: He concluded that agricultural markets were terribly flawed.
M4.zh: 他得出结论，农业市场存在严重问题。

M6.en: He felt something more challenging and broader was calling him.
M6.zh: 他感到有更挑战、更广阔的事在召唤他。

D18.en: At 4, I started going to kindergarten alone every day.
D18.zh: 4岁时，我开始每天自己上幼儿园。

D5.en: At 15, she heard a loud bang in the air right after her jump.
D5.zh: 15岁时，她起跳后听到空中一声巨响。

D4.en: At 16, I did something unprecedented: I asked a water polo player for a cap.
D4.zh: 16岁时，我干了一件前无古人的事：跟水球队队员要了一顶水球帽。

D7.en: At 15, she nodded and climbed the 10-meter platform again, legs trembling with fear.
D7.zh: 15岁时，她点点头又爬上10米台，双腿发抖。

D10.en: At 15, I bit my lip and tried hard to hold back tears, staring at the wall.
D10.zh: 15岁时，我咬着牙，拼命睁大眼睛，想把泪水咽下去。

D1.en: She was scared to do flips on the net because she worried his shoulder couldn't hold her.
D1.zh: 她吓得不敢在网上做翻腾，担心他的肩膀拉不住她。

D9.en: At 16, she decided to watch 30-60 minutes of game footage daily after training.
D9.zh: 16岁时，她决定每天训练后花30到60分钟看技术录像。

D19.en: At 16, she decided to watch 30-60 minutes of game footage daily after training.
D19.zh: 16岁时，她决定每天训练后花30到60分钟看技术录像。

D16.en: At 17, she told herself the trees would have sprouted by the time she returned.
D16.zh: 17岁时，她对自己说：回来时这些树就该发芽了。

D11.en: At 17, she told herself the trees would have sprouted by the time she returned.
D11.zh: 17岁时，她对自己说：回来时这些树就该发芽了。

D13a.en: At 15, Coach Liu told her to leave the team, but she persevered through the ordeal.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，刘指导让她退队，但她挺过了这次血的教训。

D14.en: At 17, she won the women's 3m springboard international championship with 540 points.
D14.zh: 17岁时，她以540分赢得女子3米板国际比赛冠军。

D20X.en: At 16, she decided to watch 30-60 minutes of game footage daily after training.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，她决定每天训练后花30到60分钟看技术录像。

M2.en: At 17, she was told not to disgrace the Chinese team.
M2.zh: 17岁时，她被告知不要丢中国队的脸。

M4.en: At 17, she was told China is a world diving power and not to disgrace the team.
M4.zh: 17岁时，她被告知中国是世界跳水强国，不要丢中国队的脸。

M7.en: At 17, she was told not to disgrace the Chinese team.
M7.zh: 17岁时，她被告知不要丢中国队的脸。

M5.en: At 17, she wondered if she would disgrace the Chinese team.
M5.zh: 17岁时，她担心自己会不会丢中国队的脸。

M6.en: At 17, she told herself the trees would sprout by the time she returned.
M6.zh: 17岁时，她对自己说：回来时这些树就该发芽了。

M8.en: At 17, she told herself the trees would sprout by the time she returned.
M8.zh: 17岁时，她对自己说：回来时这些树就该发芽了。

D3.en: At 25, she kept asking 'why' after each dive, distracting my attention.
D3.zh: 25岁时，她每跳一次就问各种'为什么'，弄得我的注意力都跑到了她身上。

M3.en: At 14, she saw Louganis on TV and he became her idol.
M3.zh: 14岁时，她在电视上看到洛加尼斯，他成了她的偶像。

D4.en: She started raising chickens, but the income was too low, so she considered raising pigs instead.
D4.zh: 她先养鸡，收入少，就琢磨养猪怎么养得好。

D3.en: She thought about how to raise pigs better after finding chicken farming unprofitable.
D3.zh: 她琢磨怎么养猪才养得好。

D15.en: She rode her bicycle back and forth every day, struggling financially like everyone else.
D15.zh: 她每天骑自行车来回跑，那时候经济上手紧。

D12.en: She lived with her large family in a remote mountain ridge, leading a primitive self-sufficient life.
D12.zh: 她全家十几口人独门独户住在大山梁子上，过着近乎原始的自给自足生活。

D2.en: She wrapped textbooks and flatbread in a cloth around her waist and walked a long way to school.
D2.zh: 她每天在腰里缠一个长形小包袱，里头是课本和饼子，走很远的路来学校。

D18.en: She asked village officials to let her learn carpentry outside, hoping to get enough to eat.
D18.zh: 她向村干部提出要外出学做木匠活，心想可以混几顿饱饭。

D7.en: She said, 'I formally tell you, I quit. I want to retire.'
D7.zh: 她说：“我很正式地向你提出，我不干了，我要退役。”

D5.en: She walked alone, determined to go where the village officials could not find her.
D5.zh: 她孤身奋然前行，要走到村干部们再也找不到的地方去。

D8.en: She spoke first, saying, 'I formally tell you, I quit. I want to retire.'
D8.zh: 她首先说话：“我很正式地向你提出，我不干了，我要退役。”

D10.en: Two lines of miserable tears streamed down her thin face.
D10.zh: 两行凄楚的眼泪从她削瘦的脸上流下。

D12.en: At 14, he was taken to Hong Kong and forced to fend for himself.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他被带到香港，被迫自食其力。

D2.en: As a child aged 6-8, he practiced table tennis after school and during holidays.
D2.zh: 6到8岁时，他放学和假日都练球，扒几口饭就回学校。

D9.en: At 20-21, he explained Beethoven's symphonies to teammates, getting them into music.
D9.zh: 20到21岁时，他向队友讲述贝多芬交响乐的内涵，让他们着迷。

D5.en: At 22, he lazily told the dean that playing with low-level students was boring.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他懒洋洋地对院长说，和水平低的学生打球没意思。

M4.en: At 18, he angrily retorted to his boss, 'Patriotism is not a crime; I won't write a confession.'
M4.zh: 18岁时，他反驳老板说：“爱国无罪，人头落地也不写悔改书。”

D1.en: At 19, he decided to master serving and blocking to improve his defense.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他下决心学好发球和推挡功夫。

D7.en: At 18, he defied his boss, refusing to write a confession for being patriotic.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他顶撞老板，拒绝写悔改书。

D4.en: At 6, he set up a makeshift table tennis table with a bed board and bamboo pole.
D4.zh: 6岁时，他用床板和竹竿搭成球桌，和父亲对攻。

D3.en: At 20-21, he seriously read military theory works by Marx, Engels, Stalin, and Mao, and studied classic military texts.
D3.zh: 20到21岁时，他认真阅读马克思、恩格斯、斯大林和毛泽东的军事理论著作，并研究古今军事名著。

D6.en: At 18, he met Yu Junming at a table tennis friendship match and became close.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他在一次乒乓球友谊赛中认识了余骏明，彼此过从甚密。

D13b.en: At about 10, he had to transfer to a lower-quality school, Tongji Primary.
D13b.zh: 约10岁时，他只好转到师资水平较低的同济小学就读。

D14.en: At 18, his performances drew full houses; clubs competed to hire him as coach.
D14.zh: 18岁时，凡有他出场表演几乎全场满座，多家俱乐部争相请他当教练。

D15.en: As a teenager, he started each day with jump rope, shuttlecock kicking, and calisthenics.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他每天起床后先跳绳、踢毽和做柔软体操。

D13a.en: At 18, he quit his job with his father, refusing to compromise his principles.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他决心不为五斗米折腰，跟随父亲辞职并取回行李。

M1.en: As a teenager, he represented his team and won a group runner-up.
M1.zh: 十几岁时，他代表工联队参加比赛，取得了团体亚军。

D10.en: At 22, he reveled in honors and declared his lifelong ambition fulfilled.
D10.zh: 22岁时，他沉浸在荣誉中，公开表示夺得世界冠军，平生壮志已酬。

D20.en: At 20-21, he wrote big-character posters and examined his thoughts with classmates.
D20.zh: 20到21岁时，他和同学们一起写大字报，检查自己的思想问题。

D11.en: At 20-21, he wrote poetry and calligraphy but never showed his poems to others.
D11.zh: 20到21岁时，他临贴写诗词，但诗作从不给别人看。

D16.en: He published an article on his forehand serve in Yangcheng Evening News with illustrations.
D16.zh: 他在《羊城晚报》上发表了《谈谈我的正手发球》的文章并附图。

D17.en: He helped vendors at the market carry goods, clean, and run errands.
D17.zh: 他帮市场摊贩搬菜、搬肉、拣鱼、挑水、打扫垃圾和跑腿。

M2.en: He was told to seize the rare opportunity and fight for the Chinese nation.
M2.zh: 他被鼓励把握千载难逢的机会，为中华民族争气。

M7.en: He resolved to fight for national honor and break the barrier to world championship.
M7.zh: 他暗下决心为祖国荣誉而战，打开世界冠军的大门。

M5.en: Someone observed him as a thin boy with slightly bowed legs and a crew cut.
M5.zh: 有人打量他，见他身体瘦削、罗圈腿、花旗装小分头。

M6.en: He wanted to confide in General He Long his long-held wish to return and serve China.
M6.zh: 他很想向贺龙老总倾吐自己回国效力的心愿。

M8.en: At 17-18, he was captivated by music, sitting still as if possessed by its spirit.
M8.zh: 17到18岁时，他听音乐听得入迷，安静得像被乐魂附身。

D8.en: He defeated the three top players in a row.
D8.zh: 他连续打败三巨头。

D19.en: He received many letters of consolation from citizens.
D19.zh: 他收到不少市民的慰问信。

D13a.en: At 14, his mother enrolled him in Collège Saint-Stanislas, a religious secondary school.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，母亲送他进入宗教中学圣斯坦尼斯拉斯学院。

D1.en: At 25, he finally passed the agrégation in philosophy on his second try.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他终于在第二次尝试中通过了哲学教师资格考试。

D9.en: At 24-27, he participated in a series of lectures at the Maison des Lettres on Rue Férou.
D9.zh: 24到27岁间，他参加了在费鲁街文学之家的一系列讲座。

M1.en: At 17, he passed his baccalauréat with better than average results.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他以高于平均水平的成绩通过了中学毕业会考。

D6.en: At 25-29, he became the center of a small band of Communist students at the Ecole.
D6.zh: 25到29岁间，他成为学校里一小群共产主义学生的核心。

D19.en: At 17-19, he not only procured but copied and was willing to lend them.
D19.zh: 17到19岁间，他不仅弄到了这些书，还抄写并愿意借出。

D2.en: As a small child, he had homework in reading and spelling exercises from a very early age.
D2.zh: 幼年时，他很小就开始有阅读和拼写练习的家庭作业。

D8.en: He subjected disliked peers to constant putdowns, scorn, and insulting nicknames in public.
D8.zh: 他对自己不喜欢的人不断贬低、嘲笑，并公开给他们起侮辱性绰号。

D10.en: He was seen chasing another student with a dagger in the middle of the night.
D10.zh: 他半夜被人看到拿着匕首追赶另一个学生。

D5.en: He was seen chasing another student with a dagger in the middle of the night.
D5.zh: 他半夜被人看到拿着匕首追赶另一个学生。

D20X.en: He wanted to join the party cell but not the students' syndicate, and was rejected.
D20X.zh: 他想加入党支部但不加入学生会，因此被拒绝。

D4.en: He helped prepare and read experiments, and sometimes served as a subject.
D4.zh: 他帮忙准备和读取实验，有时也作为实验对象。

D15.en: As a teenager, he was a good cyclist and regularly rode to visit his grandmother.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他是个好骑手，经常骑车去看望祖母。

D12.en: At 19, he received food parcels from home that made conditions more bearable.
D12.zh: 19岁时，他收到家里寄来的食物包裹，让处境好过些。

M2.en: At age 24, his decision to join the PCF infuriated Dr. Foucault.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他加入法共的决定激怒了福柯博士。

D12.en: She was raised by her maternal grandparents in Greenwood, Mississippi.
D12.zh: 她在密西西比州格林伍德由外祖父母抚养长大。

D7.en: At 9, she took off her brace and walked into church, surprising parishioners.
D7.zh: 9岁时，她取下支架走进教堂，让教友们大吃一惊。

D1.en: At 16, she thought: 'Four years from now, I'm going to bring home the gold for America.'
D1.zh: 16岁时，她想：四年后，我要为美国拿回金牌。

D2.en: As a teenager, she was coached by Marion Armstrong-Perkins at David T. Howard High School.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，她在戴维·T·霍华德高中接受玛丽昂·阿姆斯特朗-珀金斯的指导。

D14.en: At 21, she equaled her world record in the 100 meters and anchored the U.S. relay team to victory.
D14.zh: 21岁时，她平了100米世界纪录，并带领美国接力队获胜。

D9.en: She led the team with motivational speeches, and they were all ready to face the competition.
D9.zh: 她通过激励演讲带领团队，大家都准备好迎接比赛。

D15.en: At 11, with family support, she was able to walk without a brace.
D15.zh: 11岁时，在家人支持下，她能够不戴支架走路了。

D6.en: In tenth grade, she and Robert, who had liked each other all through school, grew closer.
D6.zh: 十年级时，她和罗伯特（两人一直互相喜欢）关系更近了。

M1.en: At 16, she thought: 'Four years from now, I'm going to bring home the gold for America.'
M1.zh: 16岁时，她想：四年后，我要为美国拿回金牌。

D3.en: At 16, she learned that winter is not everywhere at once.
D3.zh: 16岁时，她明白了冬天不是同时出现在所有地方。

D10.en: As a sophomore, she often quit the team but returned the next day.
D10.zh: 高二时，她常退出队伍，但第二天又回来训练。

D16.en: At 16, she said making a speech scared her more than running in the Olympics.
D16.zh: 16岁时，她说演讲比参加奥运比赛更让她害怕。

D13a.en: At 21, she surprised many by marrying William Ward on October 14, 1961.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她于1961年10月14日与威廉·沃德结婚，令许多人惊讶。

D4.en: At 12, she started playing basketball in the yard with her brothers.
D4.zh: 12岁时，她开始在院子里和兄弟们打篮球。

M7.en: At 15, her mother told her to be the best runner and never give up for a college chance.
M7.zh: 15岁时，母亲让她专心跑步争取上大学，永不言弃。

M3.en: At 16, she admired Robinson and felt thrilled meeting him.
M3.zh: 16岁时，她崇拜罗宾逊，见到这位著名球员时非常激动。

D13b.en: At 18-19, she decided to attend Tennessee State on a scholarship covering room, board, and tuition.
D13b.zh: 18到19岁时，她决定拿着覆盖食宿和学费的奖学金去田纳西州立大学。

D11.en: He had a sudden idea to collect the whole team's signatures on his racket bag.
D11.zh: 他突发奇想，要在球包上征集全队的签名。

D2.en: He started extra training on his own to prepare for the Thomas Cup.
D2.zh: 为了备战汤姆斯杯，他开始主动给自己加练。

D7.en: He sneaked out after his father fell asleep, but his father pretended to sleep and caught him.
D7.zh: 他趁父亲睡着后偷偷溜出去，但父亲装睡，在他摸到门口时喊住他。

D4.en: He estimated the rackets he smashed could form a platoon.
D4.zh: 被他摔掉的球拍大概可以编成一个排。

D15.en: His footsteps got faster and faster.
D15.zh: 他的脚步越来越快。

D12.en: At 9, his mother left him behind after 10 days, and he felt helpless and uncared for.
D12.zh: 9岁时，妈妈10天后丢下他回上杭，他感到无助，没人洗衣服也没人关心他。

D6.en: Ji Xinpeng, his neighbor, came to his room every day to play cards.
D6.zh: 吉新鹏住他隔壁，每天都来他房间打牌。

M1.en: He won consecutive men's singles titles at the Hong Kong and China Opens after the World Championships.
M1.zh: 世锦赛后他连续拿下香港公开赛、中国公开赛等站的男单冠军。

D9.en: He hoped to join Bao Chunlai as China's two sharp knives in men's singles, meeting in the final.
D9.zh: 他希望和鲍春来一起充当中国男单的两把尖刀，会师决赛。

D14.en: He saluted the audience after winning for the first time in Denmark.
D14.zh: 在丹麦赛赢球后，他第一次向观众行军礼。

M7.en: He saluted after thinking of national honor.
M7.zh: 想到国家荣誉，他敬了一个军礼。

D10.en: He called his coach and asked to be sent home because he couldn't bear it.
D10.zh: 他打电话给高主任，问能不能把他弄回去，不想待了。

M4.en: He wanted to win honor for his country by representing China.
M4.zh: 他代表中国参赛，要为祖国争得荣誉。

M2.en: He visited Mao's family ancestral grave again.
M2.zh: 他特意再次去祭拜了毛家祖坟。

D8.en: He shouted at his opponent, who argued back.
D8.zh: 他喊了一声，对方也跟他吵了起来。

D19.en: He trained very devotedly every day.
D19.zh: 他每一天都练得非常投入。

D3.en: At 25, he hoped to gather the strength of teammates, coaches, and staff to help him through a challenge.
D3.zh: 25岁时，他希望集合所有队友、教练和工作人员的力量来帮他闯过一关。

M5.en: At 25, he said he didn't give those waiting to see him fail a chance.
M5.zh: 25岁时，他说他没有给那些等着看他笑话的人机会。

M6.en: At 25, he deliberately visited the Mao family ancestral grave again.
M6.zh: 25岁时，他特意再次去祭拜了毛家祖坟。

M8.en: At 25, he deliberately visited the Mao family ancestral grave again.
M8.zh: 25岁时，他特意再次去祭拜了毛家祖坟。

M3.en: As a child, his idol was Danish badminton player Peter Gade.
M3.zh: 小时候，他的偶像是丹麦羽毛球选手皮特·盖德。

D2.en: At 15-16, she split her day between school and tennis training at the sports school.
D2.zh: 15到16岁时，她每天上午上课，下午训练或打友谊赛。

D15.en: She told a doctor about her neck stiffness, and he cracked her neck, miraculously healing it.
D15.zh: 她告诉医生自己脖子不舒服，医生一掰，脖子神奇地好了。

D12.en: At 14, after her father died, her mother consulted her on every family decision.
D12.zh: 14岁时，父亲去世后，妈妈凡事都和她商量。

D4.en: As a small child, she used her feet to mark lines and pushed a cart to paint them with lime water.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她用脚蹭出线，再推小车用石灰水画线。

D6.en: At 15, her American roommate talked to her constantly in English, whether she understood or not.
D6.zh: 15岁时，她的美国室友每天噼里啪啦对她说一大通英文。

D3.en: At 15-16, she focused on learning English and playing friendly matches, as math was easy.
D3.zh: 15到16岁时，她主要精力放在攻克语言关和打友谊赛上。

D13a.en: At 20, her mother firmly opposed her continuing training.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，妈妈坚决不同意她继续训练。

D18.en: At 14, she wanted to play more matches to earn prize money and pay off family debts.
D18.zh: 14岁时，她希望多打比赛，多拿奖金还清家里的债。

D1.en: At 9, she stopped training for a month to prepare for the middle school entrance exam.
D1.zh: 9岁时，她为准备小升初考试，整整一个月没去训练。

M1.en: At 15, she won her first national championship, becoming the youngest adult singles champion.
M1.zh: 15岁时，她拿到人生中第一个全国冠军，成为年龄最小的成人组全国单打冠军。

D5.en: At 15, she mustered the courage to ask Asian-looking strangers for help despite not knowing English.
D5.zh: 15岁时，她鼓起勇气向亚洲面孔的人求助，尽管不懂英语。

D10.en: At 15, she sat watching the sky darken, tears silently falling.
D10.zh: 15岁时，她坐着看天空变黑，眼泪无声地掉下来。

D11.en: At 15-16, she wrote letters when she missed home.
D11.zh: 15到16岁时，她想家时就写信。

D7.en: At 19, she felt upset when a promise was broken again.
D7.zh: 19岁时，她因承诺再次被违背而感到不舒服。

M7.en: At 22, she decided to play for Hubei team in the National Games to repay a favor.
M7.zh: 22岁时，她决定为湖北队打全运会以回报恩情。

D9.en: At 25, she insisted the national team assign Jiang Shan as her personal coach.
D9.zh: 25岁时，她一再要求国家队调姜山担任她的专职教练。

D8.en: At 22, she won a match easily and called Jiang Shan to report the score.
D8.zh: 22岁时，她轻松赢得比赛，打电话给姜山报比分。

D14.en: At 19, she got a tattoo of a heart and a rose on herself.
D14.zh: 19岁时，她在自己身上文了一颗心和一朵玫瑰花。

D16.en: At 22, she felt unable to argue and told others to handle the issue.
D16.zh: 22岁时，她觉得自己不善言辞，让别人出面解决。

D17.en: At 22, she hurried to find a doctor from the Beijing team.
D17.zh: 22岁时，她赶紧去找北京队的大夫。

D19.en: She researched opponents online and noted their traits days before matches.
D19.zh: 她比赛前几天上网查资料，记下对手的特点。

D20.en: She partnered with a Tianjin teammate and won the championship.
D20.zh: 她和天津队友搭档，打到了冠军。

M4.en: At 22, she told him she wouldn't give up and could still play.
M4.zh: 22岁时，她告诉他她不弃权，还能打。

M2.en: After her father's death, she couldn't go against his last wish.
M2.zh: 爸爸离世之后，她更不能悖逆他的遗愿。

D13b.en: As a teenager, she played because her coach and team needed her to fight for her hometown and country.
D13b.zh: 青少年时期，她打球是因为教练和队伍需要她为家乡和国家而战。

D15.en: He won the broad jump and the 100-meter dash.
D15.zh: 他赢得了跳远和100米短跑。

M1.en: He won the broad jump and the 100-meter dash, slightly ahead of Ralph Metcalfe.
M1.zh: 他轻松赢得跳远和100米短跑，略微领先拉尔夫·梅特卡夫。

D6.en: He sat around listening to a Victrola and having a bull session with some of the boys.
D6.zh: 他坐下来听留声机，和几个兄弟闲聊。

D12.en: At 21, he pumped gas on the night shift at Wright's Sohio station.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他在赖特的Sohio加油站上夜班加油。

D9.en: At 21, he was elected captain of the Ohio State track squad for the next year.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他被选为俄亥俄州立大学田径队下一年的队长。

M4.en: At 22, he stood up to the Nazis at their own Olympics, refuting their theories with his deeds.
M4.zh: 22岁时，他在纳粹的奥运会上挺身而出，用行动驳斥了他们的理论。

D20.en: At 21, he became the first black ever to captain a team in the Big Ten.
D20.zh: 21岁时，他成为十大联盟中第一位黑人队长。

D1.en: At 15, embarrassed, he could not face his coach for almost thirty minutes, then finally approached.
D1.zh: 15岁时，他感到尴尬，近半小时不敢面对教练，最后才走上前。

D3.en: At 14, he watched horses gallop effortlessly down the stretch.
D3.zh: 14岁时，他观看马匹轻松地沿赛道奔驰。

M3.en: At 15, he knew all about Paddock, the first great sprinter to turn Olympic gold into fame.
M3.zh: 15岁时，他熟知帕多克，首位将奥运金牌转化为国际声誉的伟大短跑选手。

D10.en: At 22, he squeezed his wife's hand and said, "From here on out, everything for us is going to change."
D10.zh: 22岁时，他紧握妻子的手说：“从现在起，我们的一切都将改变。”

D5.en: At 22, he was arrested for speeding and reckless driving, pleaded guilty, and was fined.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他因超速和鲁莽驾驶被捕，认罪并被罚款。

D13b.en: He chose Ohio State University for 1934-37.
D13b.zh: 他选择了俄亥俄州立大学，1934到37年。

D18.en: At 9, he packed up with his family and boarded a train for Cleveland.
D18.zh: 9岁时，他随家人收拾行李，登上前往克利夫兰的火车。

M7.en: At 22, he visited four colleges and ten high schools for National Negro Health Week.
M7.zh: 22岁时，他为全国黑人健康周访问了四所大学和十所高中。

D7.en: At 22, he was suspended indefinitely by Avery Brundage for refusing to proceed to Stockholm.
D7.zh: 22岁时，他因拒绝前往斯德哥尔摩被埃弗里·布伦戴奇无限期禁赛。

D13a.en: At 21, he married Ruth in her parents' living room.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他在露丝父母家的客厅与她结婚。

D8.en: At 22, he insisted on rerunning a race against his friend Peacock.
D8.zh: 22岁时，他坚持与朋友皮科克重赛。

D15.en: He won the gold medal, running 100 meters in 9.99 seconds.
D15.zh: 他赢得了金牌，100米跑了9.99秒。

D11.en: He went into his announcer voice and said, 'Here it is, folks, Carl Lewis is one jump away from the world record.'
D11.zh: 他模仿解说员的声音说：‘各位观众，卡尔·刘易斯离世界纪录只差一跳了。’

D2.en: Two or three days a week, he worked in practice on nothing but his jump.
D2.zh: 每周有两三天，他训练时只练跳远。

D1.en: He came home and complained to his family that he was tired of losing, but the only thing he could do was keep trying.
D1.zh: 他回家跟家人抱怨说输腻了，但唯一能做的就是继续努力。

D18.en: Adidas had given him five thousand dollars to wear their stuff exclusively.
D18.zh: 阿迪达斯给了他五千美元，让他只穿他们的装备。

M1.en: At 18-19, he beat the world's top-ranked sprinter in a 100m invitational meet.
M1.zh: 18到19岁时，他在一场100米邀请赛中击败了世界排名第一的短跑选手。

M3.en: At 20, he was paired with his childhood idol Steve Williams.
M3.zh: 20岁时，他与童年偶像史蒂夫·威廉姆斯搭档。

D5.en: At 19, he strained a hamstring while long-jumping.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他在跳远时拉伤了腿筋。

D13a.en: At 19, he skipped a history test to avoid failing, planning a makeup test after break.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他逃了一次历史考试，打算假期后补考。

M8.en: At about 18, he faced difficult questions and felt something might be missing in his life.
M8.zh: 大约18岁时，他第一次面对这么难的问题，觉得生活里可能缺了点什么。

D13b.en: At 18-20, he started studying business at Houston, then switched to radio/TV after sophomore year due to a speech teacher.
D13b.zh: 18到20岁时，他在休斯顿大学开始学商科，大二后因为一位演讲老师转到了广播电视专业。

D10.en: At 23, he was alone in a hotel room, wondering where everything went wrong despite being at the top of his sport.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他独自在酒店房间里，想不通哪里出了问题，尽管他已是世界顶尖运动员。

D18.en: At 12, a circus hired him as a strong man, capitalizing on his freakish build.
D18.zh: 12岁时，马戏团雇他当大力士，利用他奇特的体格。

D12.en: At 12, he left home and drifted through the Alps into France.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他离家出走，穿越阿尔卑斯山进入法国。

D6.en: At 23, he boarded the Cunard liner Berengaria for a cruise to New York.
D6.zh: 23岁时，他登上丘纳德公司的贝伦加利亚号邮轮前往纽约。

D15.en: At 18, his body had hardened through constant workouts and exercise.
D15.zh: 18岁时，他的身体通过持续锻炼变得结实。

D13b.en: At 21, he knew his managers wanted to sign with Jacobs, and he rubber-stamped their decision.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，他知道经理们想和雅各布斯签约，就盖章同意了。

D13a.en: He took only one or two violin lessons before finding a better use for the weekly dollar.
D13a.zh: 他只上了一两节小提琴课，就把每周的一美元花在了别处。

D4.en: As a teenager, he studied cabinet making at the Bronson School, where boxing was part of recreation.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他在布朗森学校学习家具制作，那里的娱乐活动包括拳击。

D3.en: As a teenager, he studied cabinet making at the Bronson School, where boxing was part of recreation.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他在布朗森学校学习家具制作，那里的娱乐活动包括拳击。

D1.en: A white man punched him, breaking a tooth; the pain nearly made him quit.
D1.zh: 一个白人打了他一拳，打碎了一颗牙，疼痛几乎让他放弃。

D9.en: At 21, he insisted on marrying before the fight, wanting a bride to come home to.
D9.zh: 21岁时，他坚持在比赛前结婚，希望回家时有个新娘。

D10.en: At 13, he developed a stammer and became reticent with strangers.
D10.zh: 13岁时，他患上了口吃，对陌生人变得沉默寡言。

M1.en: As a heavyweight contender, he saw being rich as vital to his self-image.
M1.zh: 作为重量级拳手，他认为富有对自己的形象至关重要。

D7.en: He defied his trainer for the first and last time in his career.
D7.zh: 他职业生涯中第一次也是最后一次违抗了教练。

M4.en: Louis knocked out Schmeling in the first round; the fight was remembered as a symbolic defeat of Nazism.
M4.zh: 路易斯在第一回合击倒了施梅林；这场比赛被铭记为对纳粹主义的象征性击败。

M7.en: At 21-22, he contributed to every black cause as part of his self-image.
M7.zh: 21到22岁间，他为每一个黑人事业出力，这是他自我形象的一部分。

D14.en: He had his picture holding a Bible published in newspapers nationwide the day before the Carnera fight.
D14.zh: 他在与卡尔内拉比赛前一天，手拿圣经的照片登上了全国报纸。

D2.en: At 19-25, he lived like a monk, only interested in football, no smoking or drinking.
D2.zh: 19到25岁间，他像和尚一样生活，只对足球感兴趣，不抽烟不喝酒。

D12.en: At 20, he got married and had two children, always living for football.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他结婚并有了两个孩子，一直为足球而活。

D10.en: He declared himself the greatest and said he shook up the world.
D10.zh: 他宣称自己是最伟大的，并说他震撼了世界。

D5.en: He bought a parachute from an army surplus store and wore it on the plane.
D5.zh: 他从军需商店买了一个降落伞，并在飞机上穿着它。

D15.en: He threw punches into the gray floating haze and waited for the bell.
D15.zh: 他向灰色的浮雾中出拳，等待铃声。

M4.en: He said he can't be a Christian seeing colored people fighting for integration get blown up.
M4.zh: 他说看到有色人种为融合而战被炸死，他不能做基督徒。

D7.en: He said he doesn't believe in forced integration and knows where he belongs.
D7.zh: 他说他不相信强制融合，他知道自己属于哪里。

D12.en: At age 8, Sonny Liston was sent to work in the fields instead of school.
D12.zh: 8岁时，桑尼·利斯顿被送去田里干活，没上学。

D13a.en: He voluntarily withdrew from Central High School on March 31, 1958.
D13a.zh: 1958年3月31日，他自愿从中央高中退学。

D9.en: He signed to fight Doug Jones at Madison Square Garden in March 1963.
D9.zh: 他签约于1963年3月在麦迪逊广场花园与道格·琼斯比赛。

M1.en: He signed to fight Doug Jones at Madison Square Garden in March 1963.
M1.zh: 他签约于1963年3月在麦迪逊广场花园与道格·琼斯比赛。

D4.en: At 20, he accepted Islam's dietary restrictions, and the Nation provided him with cooks.
D4.zh: 20岁时，他接受了伊斯兰教的饮食限制，伊斯兰民族组织为他安排了厨师。

D6.en: He bought a parachute at an army surplus store and wore it on the plane.
D6.zh: 他在军需商店买了一个降落伞，并在飞机上穿着它。

D13b.en: He entered Central High School in the 10th grade on September 4, 1957.
D13b.zh: 他于1957年9月4日进入中央高中读十年级。

D14.en: He declared, 'I am the greatest! I shook up the world! I’m the greatest thing that ever lived.'
D14.zh: 他宣称：“我是最伟大的！我震撼了世界！我是有史以来最伟大的。”

D16.en: He said, 'I’m making money, the popcorn man making money, and the beer man, and you got something to write about.'
D16.zh: 他说：“我在赚钱，爆米花小贩在赚钱，卖啤酒的也在赚钱，而你们有了可写的东西。”

D18.en: He helped his father paint signs on weekends or after school.
D18.zh: 他周末或放学后帮父亲画招牌。

D1.en: He was determined to carry his opponent and redeem himself as a prognosticator of rounds.
D1.zh: 他决心坚持到对手倒下，并挽回自己作为回合预测者的声誉。

D3.en: He asked question after question about training, other fighters, and technique.
D3.zh: 他一个接一个地问关于训练、其他拳手和技术的问题。

M6.en: By age fifteen, he had a sure sense of his own destiny.
M6.zh: 到十五岁时，他对自己命运有了确定的感知。

D11.en: He imbibed Muslim tales with fascination but had a casual grasp of the specifics.
D11.zh: 他着迷地吸收穆斯林故事，但对细节掌握得比较随意。

M2.en: He found a father substitute in Elijah Muhammad, who explained the world of black goodness and white evil.
M2.zh: 他在以利亚·穆罕默德身上找到了父亲替代者，后者向他解释了黑人的善良和白人的邪恶。

D8.en: He confronted Malcolm X, saying he made the wrong decision by leaving Elijah Muhammad.
D8.zh: 他质问马尔科姆·X，说他离开以利亚·穆罕默德是错误的选择。

D20.en: He made a rematch agreement with Sonny Liston for November 16, 1964.
D20.zh: 他与索尼·利斯顿达成协议，于1964年11月16日进行复赛。

M7.en: He felt he was born to do something for his people.
M7.zh: 他觉得自己生来就是要为他的人民做些什么。

D17.en: He took him to the hospital, paid his bills, and made sure he was taken care of.
D17.zh: 他带他去医院，支付了账单，并确保他得到妥善照顾。

D12.en: He never forgot the social humiliation of being born into a shanty.
D12.zh: 他从未忘记出生在棚户区的社会屈辱。

M2.en: At 21, he pursued his dream of bringing happiness to his family.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他追求为家人带来幸福的梦想。

D4.en: He could kill the ball and lift it with both feet.
D4.zh: 他能停球并用双脚轻松挑起。

D18.en: He got the key to his first apartment, large enough for his family.
D18.zh: 他拿到了第一套公寓的钥匙，足够容纳家人。

D2.en: He shouted to the terraces, 'I'm not leaving', and struggled to the end.
D2.zh: 他向看台大喊‘我不走’，并坚持到比赛结束。

D15.en: He celebrated by looking up at the sky, giving thanks to God and blessing himself.
D15.zh: 他仰望天空庆祝，感谢上帝并为自己祈福。

D9.en: He led his team through a successful qualifying round for the Youth World Championship.
D9.zh: 他带领球队成功晋级世青赛预选赛。

D6.en: He escaped for a night of pizza and cards, safe from publicity.
D6.zh: 他逃出去吃了一夜披萨打牌，远离公众视线。

D14.en: His ball control and pace brought the Olympic stadium to its feet.
D14.zh: 他的控球和速度让奥林匹克体育场观众起立欢呼。

M1.en: He became the youngest player in football history to play in a premier-division match.
M1.zh: 他成为足球史上最年轻的顶级联赛出场球员。

D5.en: At 15, he complained to the referee, claiming a hand ball and demanding a penalty.
D5.zh: 15岁时，他向裁判抱怨，声称对方手球并要求判罚点球。

D3.en: At 10, he could kill the ball and lift it with both feet.
D3.zh: 10岁时，他能停球并用双脚将球挑起。

D7.en: At 17, he vowed never to forgive Menotti and to quit football.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他发誓绝不原谅梅诺蒂并退出足坛。

D10.en: At 20, he screamed with eyes shut and face contorted, as if struck by the devil.
D10.zh: 20岁时，他紧闭双眼、面部扭曲地尖叫，仿佛被魔鬼击中。

D13b.en: At 19, he complied by cutting his hair, putting on a uniform, and swearing allegiance before being drafted.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，他服从要求，剪了头发、穿上制服并宣誓效忠，随后应征入伍。

D13a.en: From age 14, he increasingly skipped school.
D13a.zh: 从14岁起，他越来越频繁地逃学。

D1.en: At 19-20, he could only keep playing after a doctor gave him a strong painkiller.
D1.zh: 19到20岁时，他只能在医生注射强效止痛药后继续比赛。

M3.en: At 16, he said, 'My idol, Bochini.'
M3.zh: 16岁时，他说：“我的偶像，博奇尼。”

D16.en: At 18-21, he refused to lend his name to a wine ad, fearing it might encourage alcoholism.
D16.zh: 18到21岁时，他拒绝为葡萄酒广告代言，担心会助长酗酒。

M6.en: At 22, he said, 'Because God makes me play well. That is why I always make the sign of the cross.'
M6.zh: 22岁时，他说：“因为上帝让我踢得好。所以我上场时总是画十字。”

D20.en: He and his agent caused the sacking of coach Lattek and his replacement by Menotti in March 1983.
D20.zh: 1983年3月，他和经纪人导致拉特克被解雇，梅诺蒂接任教练。

D8.en: He let Schuster's bad relationship with coach Lattek worsen on purpose.
D8.zh: 他故意让舒斯特尔与教练拉特克的关系恶化。

D11.en: He wrote a simple letter saying he came to Esquina for its beauty, family, and friendly women.
D11.zh: 他用简单的词汇写信说，他来埃斯基纳是因为那里美丽、有亲戚朋友，还有热情的女人。

D6.en: She played cards with her mother while waiting in airports or hotel lobbies.
D6.zh: 她和妈妈在机场或酒店大堂等车时打牌。

M1.en: At 16, she faced Chris Evert in the Family Circle Magazine Cup finals.
M1.zh: 16岁时，她在家庭圈杯决赛中对阵克里斯·埃弗特。

D15.en: She glides to her right, reaches back, and swings so hard she lifts off the ground.
D15.zh: 她滑向右侧，向后挥拍，用力一击，身体离地。

D7.en: If she disagrees with a referee's call, she does not argue or throw a tantrum.
D7.zh: 如果不同意裁判的判罚，她不会争辩或发脾气。

D4.en: At age 3, her father sawed off inches from an old racket so she could hold it.
D4.zh: 3岁时，父亲锯短旧球拍，让她能握住。

D9.en: At 13 years and 4 months, she turned professional in fall 1982.
D9.zh: 13岁零4个月时，她于1982年秋季转为职业球员。

D5.en: At 17, she took a rare trip to the net and missed.
D5.zh: 17岁时，她罕见地冲到网前，但没打中。

D13a.en: At 16, her father forbade her from playing to prevent burnout.
D13a.zh: 16岁时，她父亲不让她打球，怕她失去对网球的热爱。

D18.en: At 17, she decided to give flowers to the next player who beat her.
D18.zh: 17岁时，她决定给下一个打败她的球员送一束花。

D12.en: He was raised by his mother after his parents divorced.
D12.zh: 父母离婚后，他由母亲抚养长大。

D4.en: He began to keep a pigeon coop.
D4.zh: 他开始养鸽子。

D1.en: He forced himself past his doubts and insecurities.
D1.zh: 他强迫自己克服怀疑和不安全感。

D7.en: He angrily splattered milk containers against the walls.
D7.zh: 他愤怒地把牛奶盒砸在墙上。

D18.en: He began seeking out and buying old and rare boxing films.
D18.zh: 他开始寻找并购买老旧稀有的拳击影片。

D5.en: At 11, he was the bagman, loading up the gang's spoils and running.
D5.zh: 11岁时，他负责装赃物并逃跑。

M3.en: At 12, he saw Ali and said to himself, 'I want to be champ of the world.'
M3.zh: 12岁时，他看到阿里，对自己说：“我想成为世界冠军。”

D3.en: He asked another boy to help him with his reading skills.
D3.zh: 他请另一个男孩帮他提高阅读能力。

D15.en: At 13, he bench-pressed 250 pounds ten times on his first try.
D15.zh: 13岁时，他第一次卧推就举起了250磅，做了十次。

D9.en: He obeyed and then talked things out with Bordick.
D9.zh: 他服从了，然后和博迪克把事情谈开了。

D20.en: At 16, he was partners in the gym with the dream of developing fighters for six years.
D20.zh: 16岁时，他和别人在健身房合伙，梦想培养拳手，已经一起干了六年。

M1.en: At 14-15, he became the national junior olympics heavyweight champion.
M1.zh: 14到15岁时，他成为全国少年奥运会重量级冠军。

D13a.en: At 15-16, his formal schooling ended after his freshman year in high school.
D13a.zh: 15到16岁时，他高中一年级后正式学业就结束了。

D20X.en: At 16, he felt that Cus walked out on him.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他觉得库斯抛弃了他。

D11.en: At 14-16, he trained himself to be wicked, snapping at everyone on his walk to school.
D11.zh: 14到16岁时，他训练自己变得凶狠，上学路上见人就骂。

D17.en: At 11-16, he worked weekends at a home for the elderly, taking wheelchair-bound residents for walks.
D17.zh: 11到16岁时，他周末在养老院工作，推轮椅带老人散步。

M5.en: At 7-8, he observed that having the newest fashion gave status.
M5.zh: 7到8岁时，他注意到拥有最新时尚能带来地位。

D14.en: At 20, he turned to face them, put his gloves through the ropes, and saluted gladiator-style.
D14.zh: 20岁时，他转身面对他们，把手套穿过绳索，掌心向上，以角斗士的方式致敬。

M6.en: At 14-16, he wanted someone to talk about him like that and to be part of that world.
M6.zh: 14到16岁时，他渴望有人那样谈论他，想成为那个世界的一部分。

D16.en: At 13-14, he said he would do anything to win and give his life to be champion.
D16.zh: 13到14岁时，他说他会不惜一切取胜，愿为冠军付出生命。

D19.en: At 13-14, he wore a plastic suit with Albolene for weeks to sweat off weight, only removing it at night for a hot bath.
D19.zh: 13到14岁时，他涂上阿尔博林、穿塑料服数周，只在晚上洗热水澡时脱下，以大量出汗减重。

M7.en: At 13-14, he said he would do anything to win and give his life to be champion.
M7.zh: 13到14岁时，他说他会不惜一切取胜，愿为冠军付出生命。

M8.en: At 13-14, someone saw something in him, though he didn't know how.
M8.zh: 13到14岁时，有人在他身上看到了什么，他也不知道怎么回事。

D4.en: At 9-10, she played tennis on the first indoor court in Prague.
D4.zh: 9到10岁时，她在布拉格第一个室内网球场打网球。

D3.en: At 9-10, she did crosswords with her grandmother.
D3.zh: 9到10岁时，她和外婆一起做填字游戏。

D11.en: As a child, she imagined she was Rod Laver.
D11.zh: 小时候，她想象自己是罗德·拉沃尔。

D12.en: At 23, she bought her parents a house in Texas on the same street as hers.
D12.zh: 23岁时，她给父母在德克萨斯买了房子，和她同一条街。

D2.en: At 20, she lost about thirty pounds in eight months.
D2.zh: 20岁时，她在八个月内减了大约三十磅。

D15.en: At 24, she was building muscles from hard training with Lieberman.
D15.zh: 24岁时，她跟着利伯曼刻苦训练，练出了肌肉。

D7.en: At 24, she told them she was buying a second house near Charlottesville.
D7.zh: 24岁时，她告诉他们她要买第二栋房子，在夏洛茨维尔附近。

D5.en: At 21, she dated Rita Mae, who was not discreet about her private life.
D5.zh: 21岁时，她与里塔·梅约会，后者对她的私生活并不谨慎。

D6.en: At 16, she knelt to touch the grass at a holy place for tennis players.
D6.zh: 16岁时，她跪下来触摸草地，那是网球选手的圣地。

D9.en: At 21-23, she and Rita Mae hosted gatherings where she learned from literary friends.
D9.zh: 21到23岁时，她和丽塔·梅一起举办聚会，从文学朋友那里学习。

M1.en: At 18-19, she lost to Chris Evert in the French Open finals.
M1.zh: 18到19岁时，她在法网决赛输给了克里斯·埃弗特。

D1.en: Two years later, she climbed from that abyss to victory on Wimbledon Centre Court.
D1.zh: 两年后，她从那个深渊中爬出来，在温布尔登中央球场取得了胜利。

D14.en: At 19, she accused line judges of cheating, widely reported.
D14.zh: 19岁时，她指责司线员作弊，被广泛报道。

M3.en: At 16, she played terribly trying to impress her heroine she finally met.
M3.zh: 16岁时，她终于见到偶像，想表现却打得很糟。

D10.en: At 18-19, she slammed her racket and sarcastically OK'd the lineswoman.
D10.zh: 18到19岁时，她摔拍子，讽刺地向女司线员比OK。

D18.en: At 18-19, she was earning thousands of dollars.
D18.zh: 18到19岁时，她赚着几千美元。

M4.en: At 18, she won her childhood grudge match against the Czech government.
M4.zh: 18岁时，她赢了童年与捷克政府的恩怨对决。

D16.en: At 18, she said, 'I wanted my freedom.'
D16.zh: 18岁时，她说：‘我想要我的自由。’

D20.en: At 22, she jointly owned a house with an oak library and nine acres.
D20.zh: 22岁时，她与人合拥有一栋带橡木图书馆和九英亩地的房子。

D18.en: At 10, she did odd jobs to collect eight dollars.
D18.zh: 10岁时，她做零工攒了8美元。

D15.en: At age 2-3, she underwent fitness training using the Richard Williams method.
D15.zh: 2到3岁时，她接受了理查德·威廉姆斯方法的体能训练。

M1.en: At 10, she won her 17th straight singles title.
M1.zh: 10岁时，她赢得了第17个连续单打冠军。

D11.en: At 14, she stared happily through the window as the world zipped by with the speed of her passing thoughts.
D11.zh: 14岁时，她开心地望着窗外，世界以她思绪的速度飞驰而过。

D10.en: At 6, she was so concerned for her father that she didn't leave the sofa and kept looking out the window.
D10.zh: 6岁时，她非常担心父亲，没有离开沙发，一直望着窗外。

M4.en: At 14, she felt she had to find a way to beat the class system and develop her gifts without seed money.
M4.zh: 14岁时，她觉得必须找到办法打败阶级制度，在没有启动资金的情况下发展自己的天赋。

M3.en: At 14, she lapped up Alice's history and longed to become a champion like her.
M3.zh: 14岁时，她如饥似渴地了解爱丽丝的历史，渴望成为像她那样的冠军。

D13a.en: At 11, she reluctantly stepped aside.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，她不情愿地让开了。

D17.en: At 14, she heard Alice coughing through the night while hooked to an oxygen tank.
D17.zh: 14岁时，她听到爱丽丝夜里咳嗽，连着氧气罐。

D19.en: She studied tennis as closely as a sleuth inspects a fingerprint.
D19.zh: 她像侦探检查指纹一样仔细研究网球。

D8.en: At 14, she beat the top player, Kathy Chabot.
D8.zh: 14岁时，她击败了顶尖选手凯西·查博特。

M2.en: She saw her mother's wounded look and tried to mend her heart by staying upbeat.
M2.zh: 她看到母亲受伤的表情，试图通过保持乐观来安抚母亲。

M5.en: She thought, 'One day I'm going to eat at that counter and not worry about cost.'
M5.zh: 她想：‘总有一天我会在那个柜台吃饭，不用担心价格。’

D6.en: He married a Japanese-American woman and had two children in the United States.
D6.zh: 他娶了一位日裔美国女性，在美国生了两个孩子。

M1.en: At 14, he was already an incredible 6'9" and the best young prospect in the country.
M1.zh: 14岁时，他已经有6英尺9英寸高，是全国最好的年轻苗子。

D14.en: At 14, he was already an incredible 6'9" and the best young prospect in the country.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他已经有6英尺9英寸高，是全国最好的年轻苗子。

D4.en: He picked up a ball and started shooting around at the basket on the other end of the gym.
D4.zh: 他拿起一个球，开始在体育馆另一端的篮筐投篮。

D18.en: At 14, Yao Ming was 6'9" and the best young prospect in China.
D18.zh: 14岁时，姚明身高2.06米，是中国最佳青年苗子。

M2.en: At 25, he reassured his mother he was doing everything to make her proud.
M2.zh: 25岁时，他安慰母亲说自己正竭尽全力让她骄傲。

D15.en: As a child, he showed fearless intensity inspired by watching NBA games on TV.
D15.zh: 小时候，他看电视上的NBA比赛，表现出无畏的激情。

D13a.en: He was the first Chinese basketball player to abandon the national team.
D13a.zh: 他是第一个退出国家队的中国篮球运动员。

D3.en: At 11, he loved reading books about foreign lands and China's imperial history.
D3.zh: 11岁时，他喜欢读关于外国和中国历史方面的书。

D19.en: At 14, he was 6'9" and the best young prospect in the country.
D19.zh: 14岁时，他身高6英尺9英寸，是全国最好的年轻新秀。

M3.en: At 14, Yao Ming was 6'9" and considered the best young prospect in China.
M3.zh: 14岁时姚明身高2.06米，被视为全国最佳青年苗子。

M5.en: At 14, Yao Ming was 6'9" and the best young prospect in China.
M5.zh: 14岁时，姚明身高2.06米，是中国最好的年轻苗子。

M6.en: At 14, Yao Ming was 6'9" and considered the best young prospect in China.
M6.zh: 姚明14岁时身高2.06米，被视为全国最佳青年苗子。

D20.en: He married a Japanese-American woman.
D20.zh: 他娶了一位日裔美国女性。

D17.en: He married a Japanese-American woman and had two children in the US.
D17.zh: 他与一位日裔美国女性结婚，在美国有了两个孩子。

M4.en: At 23, he wore a little red Mao pin given by his aunt as a good-luck charm.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他别了一枚阿姨送的小红毛主席像章当护身符。

D8.en: At 24, he stormed off the court screaming at teammates, waving arms and stomping feet.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他冲下场朝队友大喊，挥舞手臂，用力跺脚。

D15.en: In his first season at Michigan State, the Spartans finished 25-5.
D15.zh: 在密歇根州立大学的第一个赛季，斯巴达人队取得了25胜5负的战绩。

D2.en: He attended study hall every night after practice to maintain a 2.0 GPA.
D2.zh: 他每晚训练后都去自习室，以保持2.0的平均绩点。

D12.en: As a rookie, he carried equipment on the road without minding.
D12.zh: 作为新秀，他外出比赛时帮忙搬运器材，并不介意。

D6.en: He talked with teammates in the locker room about teaching opponents manners.
D6.zh: 他在更衣室里和队友讨论要去教训对手。

D18.en: He worked as a DJ at a student disco two nights a week during college.
D18.zh: 大学期间，他每周两晚在学生迪斯科舞厅当DJ。

D7.en: He told UCLA to forget it because he didn't want to play second fiddle.
D7.zh: 他告诉加州大学洛杉矶分校别想了，因为他不想当配角。

D11.en: At 18-19, he held up his hand and a teammate slapped it, starting a ritual they repeated in every game.
D11.zh: 18到19岁时，他举起手，队友拍了一下，从此每场比赛都这样做。

D3.en: He majored in telecommunications with a minor in education and taught a fourth-grade class.
D3.zh: 他主修电信，辅修教育，并曾给四年级学生上课。

D1.en: He made the team because he practiced weak-hand shooting a lot.
D1.zh: 他因为大量练习非惯用手投篮而入选了球队。

M3.en: He watched basketball greats like Wilt Chamberlain and Kareem Abdul-Jabbar with his father.
M3.zh: 他和父亲一起观看了威尔特·张伯伦、卡里姆·阿卜杜尔-贾巴尔等篮球巨星。

D10.en: At 19, he looked in the mirror as a Laker and cried.
D10.zh: 19岁时，他看着镜子里身为湖人队员的自己，泪流满面。

D9.en: At 18, he decided to attend Michigan State University.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他决定去密歇根州立大学上学。

D5.en: At 20-21, he said nobody thought his team had a prayer in Game Six without Kareem.
D5.zh: 20到21岁时，他说没人认为没有贾巴尔的他们能在第六场有胜算。

M1.en: At 18, he cared only about the future and saw an NCAA championship for Michigan State.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他只在乎未来，看到了密歇根州立的NCAA冠军。

D20.en: He played on the same team at Michigan State and was roommates with someone.
D20.zh: 他在密歇根州立大学和某人同队并同住一间宿舍。

D13a.en: At 19, he hesitated at a private dining room, so the other ordered for both.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他对包间犹豫，对方就替两人点了菜。

D8.en: At 19, he grabbed Cookie and said, 'Get your coat. You’re coming with me.'
D8.zh: 19岁时，他抓住库奇说：“穿上外套，跟我走。”

D14.en: In college, he was E.J. the DeeJay at Bonnie & Clyde's, loving to mix music and lights.
D14.zh: 大学时，他在邦妮与克莱德当DJ E.J.，喜欢混音和灯光。

D13b.en: At 19, he felt the decision of who he'd play for in the NBA wasn't his.
D13b.zh: 19岁时，他觉得为谁打NBA的决定不是他的。

M2.en: At 19, he felt he could begin to repay his parents.
M2.zh: 19岁时，他觉得可以开始回报父母了。

D17.en: At 21-25, he saw Andre every summer in Lansing and spent time with him.
D17.zh: 21到25岁间，他每年夏天回兰辛看安德烈并一起度过。

D6.en: He greeted an American athlete and offered a brocade souvenir.
D6.zh: 他向前来打招呼的美国运动员赠送了织锦留念。

D2.en: He lost his paddle before a crucial match against his master.
D2.zh: 他在与师傅的关键比赛前丢了拍子。

M1.en: He gritted his teeth and resolved to fight ahead of his opponent.
M1.zh: 他一咬牙，决心拼在对手前面。

D15.en: He retreated two meters and engaged in a rally.
D15.zh: 他边打边后退到两米处展开对攻。

D14.en: He was deeply impressed by a book about Sorge as a young man.
D14.zh: 他年轻时看了一本描写佐尔格的书，印象很深。

D3.en: At six, he was sent to a martial arts school and fell in love with boxing and weapons.
D3.zh: 他六岁时被送到武术社，爱上了拳术和刀枪剑棍，练了六年。

D4.en: At 24, he changed his stroke structure, not for smallness but to prepare for the next shot.
D4.zh: 24岁时，他改了动作结构，不是为了动作小，而是为下一板做准备。

D5.en: He sometimes sneaked into government offices to practice table tennis.
D5.zh: 他有时大胆闯进机关去练球。

D7.en: His father broke his paddle, and he cried on his bed.
D7.zh: 他爸爸踩断了他的球拍，他扑在床上哭。

D9.en: He designed the blackboard newspaper for his class.
D9.zh: 他负责班里黑板报的美术设计。

D18.en: He ate bark and wild vegetables and drank rainwater with cattle and horses during the famine.
D18.zh: 他吃过树皮、野菜，与牛马共饮雨水。

M7.en: He vowed to use the hand shaken by Chairman Mao to win glory for Mao.
M7.zh: 他要用毛主席握过的手为毛主席争光。

D20.en: He and two teammates took the top three spots in the monthly round-robin matches.
D20.zh: 每月大循环比赛前三名被他、周兰荪、李富荣包了。

M4.en: He believed working with people from hostile countries truly embodies 'friendship first'.
M4.zh: 他认为做敌对国家人民的工作才体现'友谊第一'。

D10.en: At 24, he felt heavy-hearted and feared he would lose if things continued.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他心情沉重，觉得再这样下去非输不可。

D11.en: At age 10, he presented flowers to the Dalai Lama and Panchen Lama, who touched his head.
D11.zh: 他十岁时给达赖和班禅献花，他们摸了他的头顶。

D19.en: He wrote in his diary: 'Eight years of hard training produced a unique attacking style.'
D19.zh: 他在日记中写道：'八年伤心筋骨痛，育出奇花两面攻。'

D17.en: He smashed hard while Zhang Xielin floated like a butterfly to save difficult balls.
D17.zh: 他重板扣杀，张燮林飘然救回险球。

M2.en: At 19, he vowed to use the hand shaken by Chairman Mao to win glory for him.
M2.zh: 19岁时，他发誓要用毛主席握过的手为毛主席争光。

M3.en: At 19, he vowed to use the hand shaken by Chairman Mao to win glory for him.
M3.zh: 19岁时，他发誓要用毛主席握过的手为毛主席争光。

M5.en: At 19, he vowed to use the hand shaken by Chairman Mao to win glory for him.
M5.zh: 19岁时，他发誓要用毛主席握过的手为毛主席争光。

M6.en: At 19, he vowed to use the hand shaken by Chairman Mao to win glory for him.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他发誓要用毛主席握过的手为毛主席争光。

M8.en: At 19, he vowed to use the hand shaken by Chairman Mao to win glory for him.
M8.zh: 19岁时，他发誓要用毛主席握过的手为毛主席争光。

D13b.en: At about 25, he married Luo Fuzhen in a lavish Chinese-Western wedding at the Hardoon Garden.
D13b.zh: 大约25岁时，他和罗馥贞在哈同花园举行了豪华的中西合璧婚礼。

D12.en: She was born in Romania on November 12, 1961, and named Nadia Elena Comaneci.
D12.zh: 她于1961年11月12日在罗马尼亚出生，取名纳迪亚·埃琳娜·科马内奇。

D15.en: She executed each skill with the extension and movements expected of her, and dismounted.
D15.zh: 她按照要求的伸展和动作完成了每个技巧，然后下器械。

D10.en: She sat on a mat and watched Bela run around the arena demanding to see individual scores and yelling for justice.
D10.zh: 她坐在垫子上，看着贝拉在赛场里跑来跑去要求看个人分数并大喊要公正。

D2.en: At 12, she ate dinner after second practice, did homework, and had lights out by 10 PM.
D2.zh: 12岁时，她第二次训练后吃晚饭，做作业，10点熄灯。

D7.en: She started going to practices late, and Bela was completely unused to any defiance on her part.
D7.zh: 她开始训练迟到，贝拉完全不习惯她有任何反抗。

D4.en: She used to tape foam to her heels because they got so bruised from constantly whacking the bar.
D4.zh: 她过去常把泡沫贴在脚后跟上，因为脚后跟因不断撞击杠子而淤青。

D3.en: She learned by creating and figuring out how to make things work.
D3.zh: 她通过创造和弄清楚如何让东西运作来学习。

D13b.en: At 20-23, she felt humiliated by the expectation to keep sacrificing.
D13b.zh: 20到23岁间，她觉得被要求继续牺牲很屈辱。

M1.en: At 12, she heard Bela say that if a kid didn't want to work hard, that was fine.
M1.zh: 12岁时，她听到贝拉说，如果孩子不想努力，那也没关系。

M7.en: At 14, she performed, made her country proud, and left by bus, not limousine.
M7.zh: 14岁时，她表演了，让国家骄傲，然后坐巴士离开，不是豪华轿车。

M6.en: At 14, she had a secret personal goal for 1976: to create her own dream.
M6.zh: 14岁时，她有一个秘密的个人目标：创造自己的梦想。

M4.en: At 12, she would have followed him through fire if it made her a better gymnast.
M4.zh: 12岁时，如果跟着他穿过火堆能让她成为更好的体操运动员，她会做。

D19.en: As a child, she kept blue pens in one place, black in another at school.
D19.zh: 幼年时，她在学校把蓝笔放一处，黑笔放另一处。

D13a.en: At 17, she agreed to go to Deva on condition she no longer lived in the dorm with younger gymnasts.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，她同意去德瓦，条件是不再和年轻体操运动员住宿舍。

D16.en: At 18, she wrote letters to her mom about being homesick and disliking spicy food.
D16.zh: 18岁时，她给妈妈写信说想家，不喜欢辣的食物。

D18.en: At 19-20, she traded fruits and vegetables daily to plan dinner.
D18.zh: 19到20岁时，她每天用水果和蔬菜换东西来计划晚餐。

D17.en: As a teenager, she invited him to meals despite having little.
D17.zh: 十几岁时，她尽管自己也不富裕，仍邀请他一起吃饭。

D15.en: He cut to the left and shot across his body, the puck went through the mesh.
D15.zh: 他向左切入，横身射门，球穿过了网。

D4.en: As a small child, he bounced impatiently at the farmhouse door in winter gear.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他穿着冬装在家门口不耐烦地蹦跳。

D2.en: Between practices and games, he was on the ice four or five nights a week at age 9-10.
D2.zh: 9到10岁时，他在训练和比赛之间每周上冰四五个晚上。

D1.en: At 14, he was booed when he skated out; not many years later, everyone loved him.
D1.zh: 14岁时他上场被嘘；没几年后，人人都爱他。

D6.en: At age 8, he wore the A as Brantford's alternate captain.
D6.zh: 8岁时，他戴着A标志担任布兰特福德队的副队长。

D10.en: At age 10-11, he ran home, locked his bedroom door, and cried all night.
D10.zh: 10到11岁时，他跑回家，锁上卧室门，哭了一整夜。

M1.en: At 18, he read the standings and said, 'Third. I've got to finish at least third.'
M1.zh: 18岁时，他看了排名说：'第三。我必须至少拿到第三。'

D13b.en: He learned from his dad that stardom carries responsibility.
D13b.zh: 他从父亲那里学到，明星身份意味着责任。

D7.en: At 14, he told his dad, 'I'm not going back. Regardless, I'm not going back.'
D7.zh: 14岁时，他对父亲说：'我不回去了。无论如何，我不回去。'

M3.en: At 11, he listened when his hero spoke.
M3.zh: 11岁时，他的英雄说话时他认真听。

D5.en: At 17, he carefully took down the new wording of the deal that would make him rich.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他仔细记下了能让他致富的新协议条款。

D9.en: At 16-17, every time he yelled for a centre, he'd be over the boards and gone.
D9.zh: 16到17岁时，每次他喊需要中锋，他就会翻过板墙冲出去。

D18.en: At 17, he used some of his bonus money to buy a new Pontiac Trans-Am.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他用部分奖金买了一辆新的庞蒂亚克Trans-Am。

D20.en: At 17, he was shipped to the Edmonton Oilers with Mio and Peter Driscoll.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他和米奥、彼得·德里斯科尔一起被交易到埃德蒙顿油人队。

D2.en: He improved year by year, adding to his game, especially his defense.
D2.zh: 他逐年进步，给自己的比赛增添内容，尤其是防守。

D13b.en: His parents were determined their children not be burdened by race.
D13b.zh: 他父母决心不让孩子们受种族困扰。

D1.en: He leaped from the other side of the lane and ferociously smacked down the ball.
D1.zh: 他从球场另一侧跳起，凶狠地将球拍下。

D6.en: He began a friendship with Jordan that first week.
D6.zh: 他在第一周就和乔丹开始了友谊。

D15.en: He stole the ball, had a breakaway, and dunked it.
D15.zh: 他断球后快攻，扣篮得分。

D9.en: Williams separated him from the others and suggested he stay for a second session.
D9.zh: 威廉姆斯把他和其他人分开，建议他留下参加第二节课。

D7.en: His father wanted him to play baseball, but now he wants him to pursue basketball.
D7.zh: 他父亲希望他打棒球，但现在希望他追求篮球。

D4.en: He mugged for the cameras, leaning forward to measure his hands.
D4.zh: 他对着镜头做鬼脸，前倾测量自己的手。

M1.en: At 21, he aimed to win one more national championship to settle unfinished business.
M1.zh: 21岁时，他决心再赢一次全国冠军来了结未完成的事。

D3.en: He asked Mr. Gibbons what he needed to do to be a better player.
D3.zh: 他问吉本斯先生，他需要做什么才能成为更好的球员。

D14.en: He was MVP of the league and MVP of the all-star game, beginning to make his name.
D14.zh: 他是联赛MVP和全明星赛MVP，开始打响名声。

M3.en: Like his idol Magic Johnson, he spent much time around the basket defensively.
M3.zh: 像他的偶像魔术师约翰逊一样，他花了很多时间在篮下防守。

M6.en: After attending Dean Smith's camp, Carolina was his first choice.
M6.zh: 参加迪恩·史密斯的训练营后，卡罗来纳是他的首选。

D8.en: He drove to the basket and unleashed one of the most thunderous dunks of his career.
D8.zh: 他带球冲向篮筐，完成了职业生涯最震撼的扣篮之一。

D10.en: He wanted them to lose to prove he could help them.
D10.zh: 他希望他们输，以证明他能帮助他们。

D13a.en: His father wanted him to play baseball, but now he wants him to pursue basketball.
D13a.zh: 他父亲想让他打棒球，但现在想让他追求篮球。

M2.en: His mother dreamed of him and his sister graduating from Chapel Hill on the same day.
M2.zh: 他母亲梦想他和妹妹同一天从教堂山毕业。

D11.en: He wanted to end his traveling show and play in Chicago with Michael Jordan.
D11.zh: 他想结束巡回表演，去芝加哥和迈克尔·乔丹一起打球。

D16.en: He became an unbearable trashtalker after the shot.
D16.zh: 那一投之后，他变成了让人受不了的垃圾话大王。

D17.en: He delighted in children, sweeping them up in his arms as toddlers.
D17.zh: 他喜欢孩子，把他们抱在怀里逗乐。

D20X.en: He wrote to Virginia but only received an admission form, no one watched him.
D20X.zh: 他写信给弗吉尼亚，但只收到一张入学表格，没人来看他。

D6.en: He played baseball before age seven.
D6.zh: 他七岁前打过棒球。

D4.en: At age 7-16, he learned carpentry and rolling cigars.
D4.zh: 7到16岁间，他学了木工和卷雪茄。

D18.en: At 19, he received $5 pocket money from Dunn, more than he had ever had at once.
D18.zh: 19岁时，他从邓恩那里得到5美元零花钱，比他以往任何一次都多。

D17.en: At 19, he got Baltimore press coverage as a basketball player.
D17.zh: 19岁时，他作为篮球运动员获得了巴尔的摩媒体报道。

D3.en: At 19, he wanted to learn what ballplayers did on and off the field.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他想知道球员在场上和场下都做什么。

D15.en: At 19, he started at shortstop, played the whole game, and got three hits.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他担任游击手打满全场，击出三支安打。

D14.en: At 19, he hit a ball over the left fielder's head to score the winning run.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他击出一球越过左外野手头顶，送回制胜分。

D1.en: At 23, he returned to the lineup as a pitcher and hit a home run.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他回到投手位置并击出本垒打。

D8.en: At 24, he threatened to punch someone for entering his room.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他威胁要揍闯进他房间的人。

M3.en: At 19, he would never change his style, which owed much to Brother Matthias.
M3.zh: 19岁时，他绝不会改变自己的风格，这很大程度上归功于马蒂亚斯修士。

D16.en: At 19, he got Baltimore press coverage as a basketball player.
D16.zh: 19岁时，他作为篮球运动员获得了巴尔的摩媒体报道。

D11.en: At 19, he learned to operate it and nearly killed himself starting it up with his head out the door.
D11.zh: 19岁时他学会操作，头探出门外启动时差点丧命。

D19.en: At 19, he kept his style influenced by Brother Matthias and was considered fast.
D19.zh: 19岁时，他保持受马蒂亚斯兄弟影响的风格，被认为速度快。

D20X.en: At 19, he was rated a fast man and would never change his style, which owed much to Brother Matthias.
D20X.zh: 19岁时，他被认为速度快，且从不改变受教于马蒂亚斯兄弟的风格。

M2.en: At 19, he was rated a fast man and would never change his style, which owed much to Brother Matthias.
M2.zh: 19岁时，他被认为速度快，且永不改变风格，这很大程度上归功于马蒂亚斯兄弟。

M4.en: At 19, he was properly rated a fast man and would never change his style, which owed much to Brother Matthias.
M4.zh: 19岁时，他被认为是快攻手，且从不改变受教于马蒂亚斯兄弟的风格。

M7.en: At 19, he was fast but would never change his style, influenced by Brother Matthias.
M7.zh: 19岁时，他速度快但不会改变风格，受马蒂亚斯兄弟影响。

M8.en: At 19, he was fast and would never change his style, influenced by Brother Matthias.
M8.zh: 19岁时他速度快，风格受马蒂亚斯兄弟影响，永不改变。

D6.en: He spent hours talking with friends at the club or restaurant, and occasionally went to a movie with Dorval or others.
D6.zh: 他花数小时在俱乐部或餐厅与朋友聊天，偶尔和多瓦尔或其他人去看电影。

M1.en: He swore he would do it as soon as he grew up.
M1.zh: 他发誓长大后一定要做到。

D15.en: He ran up and down the field, kicking, dribbling, and tackling until dark.
D15.zh: 他在场上跑来跑去，踢球、带球、铲球，直到天黑。

D1.en: He locked the door, lay on the bed, and cried as he hadn't in a long time.
D1.zh: 他锁上门，躺在床上，哭了好久没哭过的那种哭。

M7.en: At 15, he was told to take his opportunities when they come and not end up like his father.
M7.zh: 15岁时，他被告知要抓住机会，不要像他父亲那样。

D4.en: He started shining shoes; a friend helped him build a box like a shoe-shine kit.
D4.zh: 他开始擦鞋，朋友帮他做了一个像擦鞋箱的盒子。

D12.en: He lived in a family struggling on meager earnings.
D12.zh: 他生活在靠微薄收入勉强度日的家庭中。

D18.en: He earned over two cruzeiros and went home arm in arm with his friend, two breadwinners.
D18.zh: 他赚了两克鲁赛罗多，和朋友挽着手回家，两个养家糊口的人。

D3.en: At 7, he started shining shoes with a box built by a friend.
D3.zh: 7岁时，他用朋友做的鞋箱开始擦鞋。

D7.en: At 7-8, he refused to obey someone who needed constant watching.
D7.zh: 7到8岁时，他拒绝服从一个需要时刻看管的人。

D13a.en: At 7, he obeyed Dona Celeste and went door to door for a week.
D13a.zh: 7岁时，他听从多娜·塞莱斯特，挨家挨户走了一周。

D11.en: At 7-8, he dreamed of flying a plane with goggles, jacket, and boots.
D11.zh: 7到8岁时，他梦想戴着护目镜、穿着夹克和靴子开飞机。

D10.en: At 17, he cried like a baby while teammates pummeled him.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他哭得像个孩子，队友们捶打着他。

D9.en: At 20, he helped his company expand into building rental apartments.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他帮助公司扩展到建造出租公寓。

D5.en: At 14, a friend talked him into visiting a local prostitute.
D5.zh: 14岁时，一个朋友说服他去见当地妓女。

D14.en: At 15-16, he scored four goals in a 6-1 win.
D14.zh: 15到16岁时，他在一场6比1的胜利中攻入四球。

D17.en: At 12-14, he promised to share his food so no one would have less.
D17.zh: 12到14岁间，他承诺分享食物，不让别人少一份。

D16.en: At 15, he wanted to show friends how grown-up he looked.
D16.zh: 15岁时，他想向朋友展示自己有多成熟。

D19.en: At 15, he wanted to show friends how grown-up he looked.
D19.zh: 15岁时，他想向朋友展示自己有多成熟。

M2.en: At 15, he wanted to eliminate his mother's worry of paying rent forever.
M2.zh: 15岁时，他想永远消除母亲付房租的忧虑。

M4.en: At 15, he was told not to end up like his father.
M4.zh: 15岁时，他被告诫不要像他父亲那样。

M5.en: At 15, he wanted to show friends how grown-up he looked.
M5.zh: 15岁时，他想向朋友展示自己有多成熟。

M8.en: At 15, he couldn't believe he was leaving home.
M8.zh: 15岁时，他不敢相信自己要离开家了。

D2.en: At age 2, he practiced golf for two hours daily under his father's supervision.
D2.zh: 他两岁时，每天在父亲监督下练习两小时高尔夫。

D3.en: At 1.5, his father worked long to teach him a good swing.
D3.zh: 1岁半时，他父亲长时间教他好的挥杆。

M3.en: At 16-17, he told his caddie, 'Got to be like Nicklaus.'
M3.zh: 他16到17岁时对球童说：‘要像尼克劳斯一样。’

M1.en: At age 20, he shot the lowest score in the qualifying tournament, becoming top seed.
M1.zh: 他20岁时在资格赛中打出最低杆数，成为头号种子。

D6.en: At age 16, he loved being with the Gravell family and feeling their warmth.
D6.zh: 他16岁时喜欢和格拉维尔一家在一起，感受他们的温暖。

D15.en: At age 16, he became the youngest player ever in a PGA Tour event.
D15.zh: 他16岁时成为PGA巡回赛史上最年轻的参赛选手。

D14.en: At 20-25, he took questions from the grandstands while on the practice tee.
D14.zh: 他20到25岁时在练习场接受看台提问。

D9.en: At age 20, he ordered security to ban media from the locker room, and it was done.
D9.zh: 他20岁时命令保安禁止媒体进入更衣室，并照做了。

D1.en: At age 20, he made a series of semimiraculous pars and chipped away at Kribel's lead.
D1.zh: 他20岁时打出连续半神奇标准杆，逐步缩小与克里贝尔的差距。

D5.en: At age 16, he became the youngest player ever in a PGA Tour event.
D5.zh: 他16岁时成为PGA巡回赛史上最年轻的参赛选手。

D12.en: He became an orphan before age 16 and was taken in by an older sister's family.
D12.zh: 他不到16岁就成了孤儿，被一个姐姐的家庭收养。

D7.en: He refused to fight, even for his great love.
D7.zh: 他不愿意去争斗，即使是为了他最爱的人。

D8.en: He wanted to beat everything Trip stood for and prove he was better than the country club clique.
D8.zh: 他想打败Trip代表的一切，向乡村俱乐部的小圈子证明他更优秀。

D18.en: He and others devised a scheme to exploit his fame by charging rich country club members to golf with him.
D18.zh: 他和别人想出一个计策，利用他的名气，让有钱的乡村俱乐部会员付费和他打高尔夫。

D16.en: When asked by The New York Times if racism motivated him, he answered with one word: no.
D16.zh: 当《纽约时报》问他种族主义是否给他额外动力时，他只说了一个字：不。

D10.en: One of the unspoken rules at his home was to keep their feelings to themselves.
D10.zh: 他家的一条不成文规矩是把感情藏在心里。

D20.en: He founded Tiger Woods, Inc., a nonprofit, with himself as chairman and his father as director.
D20.zh: 他成立了非营利组织泰格·伍兹公司，自己担任主席，父亲担任董事。

M4.en: Together with others, he devised a scheme to exploit his fame by having rich country club members pay to golf with him.
M4.zh: 他和别人一起想出一个计策，利用他的名气让乡村俱乐部有钱会员付费和他打高尔夫。

D20X.en: At 20, he didn't even inform his captain, Eri Crum.
D20X.zh: 20岁时，他甚至没通知队长埃里·克拉姆。

D4.en: At age 4, his father built him his first hand-built model car with a one-horsepower engine.
D4.zh: 4岁时，他父亲为他造了第一辆手工模型车，配一马力引擎。

D3.en: At 22, he absorbed information from reading, watching, and exercising his curious mind.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他通过阅读、观察和锻炼好奇心吸收信息。

D2.en: At age 20-21, he lived in a rented bungalow in Norfolk for weeks preparing.
D2.zh: 20到21岁间，他在诺福克租平房住了几周做准备。

D7.en: At age 20, he dropped out and persuaded his father to fund a year in Formula Ford in Britain.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他辍学并说服父亲资助他在英国参加一年福特方程式。

D11.en: At age 21, he announced his retirement, feeling success and talent counted for little.
D11.zh: 21岁时他宣布退役，觉得成功和才华无足轻重。

D1.en: At age 18, placing sixth motivated him to aim for victory in Europe.
D1.zh: 18岁时，第六名的成绩激励他更想在欧洲获胜。

M1.en: At 23, he won the first nine races of the season, beating Piquet's record of eight.
M1.zh: 23岁时，他赢得了赛季前九场比赛，打破了皮奎特八场的纪录。

D13a.en: At 20, he dropped out after persuading his father to back him financially for a year in Formula Ford in Britain.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他说服父亲资助他在英国参加一年福特方程式，然后辍学了。

D9.en: At 25, he swung critical elements of the McLaren team behind him.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他让迈凯伦车队的关键成员支持他。

D20.en: At 23, he reached an agreement with West Surrey Racing.
D20.zh: 23岁时，他与西萨里车队达成了协议。

D8.en: At 23, he tried a manoeuvre that Brundle covered, and he spun into a barrier.
D8.zh: 23岁时，他尝试了一个动作，布伦德尔封住了路线，他打转撞上了护栏。

D16.en: At 21, he said talent is not important in racing because money buys good teams.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他说赛车中天赋不重要，因为钱能买到好车队。

D10.en: He told the press he was frustrated and disillusioned with motor racing.
D10.zh: 他向媒体表示自己对赛车运动感到沮丧和幻灭。

D12.en: At 21, he went to work for his father managing a building supplies business.
D12.zh: 21岁时，他去为父亲工作，管理一家建材公司。

D18.en: At 21, he went to work for his father managing a building supplies business.
D18.zh: 21岁时，他去为父亲工作，管理一家建材公司。

D19.en: At 22, he wanted to stay a free agent, feel right about moving to Formula One, and make his own decisions.
D19.zh: 22岁时，他想保持自由身，对进入一级方程式感觉合适，并自己做决定。

M2.en: At age 22, his father left him to decide between business and racing; he chose racing.
M2.zh: 22岁时，父亲让他选择经商或赛车，他选了赛车。

D5.en: At 18, he got leave to go to Sydney with his father.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他获准和父亲一起去悉尼。

D15.en: As a teenager, he won 100, 200, quarter-mile, and half-mile races.
D15.zh: 十几岁时，他赢得了100码、200码、四分之一英里和半英里赛跑。

D18.en: At 21, he was offered a PR job at Mick Simmons Ltd.
D18.zh: 21岁时，他得到了米克·西蒙斯公司的公关工作。

D12.en: At 20, he realized he needed steady employment to support himself.
D12.zh: 20岁时，他意识到需要稳定工作来养活自己。

D11.en: At 22, he composed a piano piece and made a record in London.
D11.zh: 22岁时，他在伦敦创作了一首钢琴曲并录制了唱片。

D4.en: As a small child, he threw a golf ball at a tank stand using a laundry door as wicket.
D4.zh: 幼年时，他用洗衣房门当三柱门，把高尔夫球扔向水箱架。

D3.en: At 19, he learned by watching and asking other players on his first Shield tour.
D3.zh: 19岁时，他在第一次盾赛巡演中通过观察和询问其他球员学习。

D2.en: At 18, he found time for tennis and a new love: motoring.
D2.zh: 18岁时，他抽空打网球，并爱上了开车。

D6.en: At 17, he enrolled as a regular member of the Bowral Town Club.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他成为鲍勒尔镇俱乐部的正式成员。

M1.en: At 20-21, his season aggregate of 1690 was the record for an Australian season.
M1.zh: 20到21岁间，他单赛季总得分1690分，创下澳大利亚赛季纪录。

D9.en: At 17, his team Bowral won the match and the Tom Mack Cup by an innings and 338 runs.
D9.zh: 17岁时，他所在的鲍勒尔队以一场比赛和338分的优势赢得汤姆·麦克杯。

D14.en: At 23, he played the piano remarkably well, playing most tunes by ear or from music.
D14.zh: 23岁时，他钢琴弹得非常好，能凭耳朵或乐谱演奏大多数曲子。

D10.en: At 20, he slept so soundly that he was late for work the next morning.
D10.zh: 20岁时，他睡得太沉，第二天早上上班迟到了。

D13a.en: At 25, he had to establish a residential qualification to play for South Australia in the 1934–35 season.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他必须取得居住资格才能代表南澳大利亚参加1934-35赛季。

D13b.en: As a teenager, he survived the rigours of a Winchester education.
D13b.zh: 十几岁时，他熬过了温彻斯特教育的严苛。

D16.en: At 24, he answered objections with intelligence and deliberation.
D16.zh: 24岁时，他聪明而审慎地回答了这些反对意见。

M2.en: At 17, he was promised a new cricket bat by his mother if he scored a century against Bowral's enemies.
M2.zh: 17岁时，他母亲承诺如果他面对鲍勒尔的敌人拿到百分，就给他买一个新板球拍。

M6.en: At 20, he believed that his future lay in his bat.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他相信他的未来就在他的球拍中。

D19.en: As a teenager and young adult, he applied the same discipline to keeping his diary as on the cricket field.
D19.zh: 十几岁到二十出头时，他把在板球场上同样的纪律用在了记日记上。

D6.en: As a teenager, he hung out with a multiracial gang.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他和一个多种族的帮派混在一起。

D7.en: At 24, he angrily asked a man if he knew how close his wife had been to a nigger.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他愤怒地问一个人是否知道他妻子曾离黑鬼有多近。

D15.en: At 21, he scored three touchdowns in a Bruins win.
D15.zh: 21岁时，他在棕熊队的一场胜利中完成了三次达阵。

D4.en: At 19, he made his record 25' 6½" leap on his final jump.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他在最后一次试跳中跳出了25英尺6.5英寸的纪录。

D5.en: At 24, he angrily asked a man if he knew how close his wife had ever been to a nigger.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他愤怒地问一个男人是否知道他妻子曾离黑鬼有多近。

D14.en: At 18, he returned a kickoff for a touchdown after reversing field three times.
D14.zh: 18岁时，他三次变向后回攻开球达阵。

D9.en: At 22, he set up regular events and calisthenics for youngsters.
D9.zh: 22岁时，他为年轻人安排了常规活动和体操。

D2.en: At 19, he made his record 25' 6½" leap on his final jump.
D2.zh: 19岁时，他在最后一次试跳中跳出了25英尺6.5英寸的纪录。

D3.en: He made his record 25' 6½" leap on his final try.
D3.zh: 他在最后一次试跳中跳出了25英尺6.5英寸的记录。

M1.en: He helped lead his team to victory in the California State Amateur Baseball championship.
M1.zh: 他帮助带领球队赢得了加州业余棒球锦标赛冠军。

D8.en: At 25, he relentlessly attacked a player named Ivory Fox, who couldn't escape or apologize.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他猛烈攻击一个叫艾弗里·福克斯的球员，对方无法逃脱或道歉。

D1.en: He competed in and won a table tennis competition, becoming champion in the U.S. Army.
D1.zh: 他参加并赢得了一场乒乓球比赛，成为美国陆军冠军。

M4.en: His patriotism was sorely tried by the humiliations facing blacks in the military.
M4.zh: 他的爱国心因黑人在军队中受到的羞辱而备受考验。

D20.en: He and Ray Bartlett, black and white players, became a unit.
D20.zh: 他和雷·巴特利特，黑人和白人球员，成了一个整体。

D16.en: He said, 'I hear you were lucky in your last game. How are you gonna go ninety-eight yards for a touchdown against us?'
D16.zh: 他说：‘听说你上场比赛运气好。你打算怎么对我们跑98码达阵？’

D10.en: He hated to see how some grandchildren conducted themselves at Mallie's house on Pepper Street.
D10.zh: 他讨厌看到一些孙辈在佩珀街的马利家中的行为举止。

D11.en: At 19, he tossed dust into the air, which the wind blew into the pitcher's eyes, then stole the base.
D11.zh: 19岁时，他把尘土抛向空中，风把尘土吹进投手眼里，然后他盗垒成功。

D19.en: At 19, he made his record 25' 6½" leap on his final competitive jump.
D19.zh: 19岁时，他在最后一次试跳中跳出25英尺6.5英寸的记录。

M2.en: At 19, he broke Mack's national junior-college AAU record in the broad jump.
M2.zh: 19岁时，他打破了麦克的全国大专AAU跳远纪录。

M5.en: At 18, he heard an opponent say, 'How are you gonna go ninety-eight yards for a touchdown against us?'
M5.zh: 18岁时，他听到对手说：‘你怎么能对我们跑98码达阵？’

M6.en: At 19, he broke Mack's national junior-college AAU record in the broad jump.
M6.zh: 19岁时，他打破了麦克的全国大专AAU跳远纪录。

M8.en: At 19, he broke Mack's national junior-college AAU record in the broad jump.
M8.zh: 19岁时，他打破了麦克的全国大专AAU跳远纪录。

D13a.en: At 25, he gave an ultimatum to Rachel: leave the Nurse Cadet Corps or end their engagement; she chose the latter.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他向瑞秋发出最后通牒：离开护士学员队或解除婚约；她选择了后者。

D13b.en: At 18, he enlisted in the Army on March 30, 1964.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他于1964年3月30日入伍。

M3.en: He had not met Mr. Hogan yet, but learned enough to know how intimidating he could be.
M3.zh: 他还没见过霍根先生，但已了解到他有多吓人。

D14.en: He decided to make a learning visit to Britain's pro circuit.
D14.zh: 他决定去英国职业巡回赛学习。

D1.en: He tried for the first time to qualify for the U.S. Open, didn't make it, was second alternate.
D1.zh: 他第一次尝试参加美国公开赛资格赛，没成功，是第二替补。

D2.en: He holed an eight-foot putt to win the 59th United States Amateur Golf Championship.
D2.zh: 他推进八英尺推杆，赢得第59届美国业余高尔夫锦标赛。

M1.en: He did better the next year at Purdue, defeating a teammate in the final.
M1.zh: 他第二年表现更好，在普渡大学决赛中击败了一名队友。

D5.en: He tried for the first time to qualify for the U.S. Open, didn't make it, was second alternate.
D5.zh: 他第一次尝试参加美国公开赛资格赛，没成功，是第二替补。

D15.en: He won the Trans-Mississippi Amateur Championship final 3 and 2.
D15.zh: 他以赢3洞剩2洞赢得跨密西西比业余锦标赛决赛。

D9.en: In summer 1962, he organized a Jack Nicklaus Day in Columbus after Nicklaus's U.S. Open win.
D9.zh: 1962年夏天，他在哥伦布市为杰克·尼克劳斯组织了杰克·尼克劳斯日，庆祝尼克劳斯赢得美国公开赛。

D20.en: He played practice rounds with Deane Beman, with a five-dollar Nassau bet each time.
D20.zh: 他和迪恩·贝曼打练习轮，每次赌五美元纳索。

D18.en: At age 11, he clerked in a drugstore operated by Fred J. Mebs.
D18.zh: 11岁时他在Mebs药店做店员。

D7.en: He found an official, said thanks but he was pulling out, and went back to Augusta.
D7.zh: 他找到一位官员，说谢谢但我要退出，然后回了奥古斯塔。

D13a.en: He agreed to bet on himself after his father suggested it, based on how well he was playing.
D13a.zh: 他父亲建议后，他同意赌自己赢，因为自己打得很好。

D12.en: He was raised by his father and elder brother after losing his mother at age eight.
D12.zh: 他八岁丧母后，由父亲和哥哥抚养。

D10.en: He was really burned thinking about that Tuesday.
D10.zh: 想到那个星期二，他真的很恼火。

M2.en: His father and Jack Grout inspired him by talking about Bob Jones's finish at the 1920 Open.
M2.zh: 他父亲和杰克·格劳特用鲍勃·琼斯在1920年公开赛的表现激励他。

D8.en: At 20, he relieved Deane of about forty dollars.
D8.zh: 20岁时，他从迪恩那里拿了大约四十美元。

D17.en: He had learned about Mr. Hogan's intimidating presence from Jack Grout and other sources.
D17.zh: 他从杰克·格劳特等处了解到霍根先生令人畏惧的气场。

D19.en: At 20, he avoided looking at leader boards to focus on his shotmaking.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他刻意不看记分板，以便集中精力打球。

M4.en: At 20, he led the Open by one stroke over four players including Ben Hogan.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他在公开赛上领先包括本·霍根在内的四名选手一杆。

M5.en: At 20, he was burned up thinking about that Tuesday.
M5.zh: 20岁时，他一想起那个星期二就火大。

M6.en: At 20, he led the Open by one stroke over four players including Ben Hogan.
M6.zh: 20岁时，他在公开赛上领先包括本·霍根在内的四名选手一杆。

M7.en: At 20, he led the Open by one stroke over four players including Ben Hogan.
M7.zh: 20岁时，他在公开赛上领先包括本·霍根在内的四名选手一杆。

M8.en: At 20, he had not met Hogan but learned he could be intimidating.
M8.zh: 20岁时，他还没见过霍根，但听说他可能很吓人。

D13b.en: He had to prove he had enough money in the bank to sustain himself for a year before joining the tour.
D13b.zh: 他必须证明银行里有足够的钱维持一年生活才能加入巡回赛。

D11.en: At 21, he recalled a story about him by an Australian golf writer.
D11.zh: 21岁时，他想起一位澳大利亚高尔夫作家写他的报道。

D16.en: At 23, he went to the press room after missing the cut and talked openly, earning an ovation.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他未晋级后去新闻发布室坦诚交谈，赢得了掌声。

D15.en: At 19, he performed a reverse dive pike on the ten-meter platform.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他在十米跳台上做了一个反身抱膝跳水。

D14.en: At 24, he gave two diving exhibitions.
D14.zh: 24岁时，他做了两场跳水表演。

D4.en: At 19, he brought people to the top of the ten-meter platform to show his view.
D4.zh: 19岁时，他把人带到十米跳台顶端，让他们看看他眼中的景象。

D6.en: At 24, he was welcomed warmly as an openly gay athlete for the first time.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他第一次作为公开的同性恋运动员受到热烈欢迎。

D7.en: At age 25, a fellow diver harassed him with homophobic slurs and signs.
D7.zh: 25岁时，一名跳水运动员用恐同言论和标语骚扰他。

D1.en: Three days after returning to the University of Miami, he was back on the platform.
D1.zh: 回到迈阿密大学三天后，他又站上了平台。

D10.en: At 16, he fell for someone like a boulder off the ten-meter platform.
D10.zh: 16岁时，他像一块巨石从十米跳台上坠落一样爱上了某人。

D13a.en: At 11, he put on a wet-suit jacket and went down to the pool.
D13a.zh: 11岁时，他穿上泳衣和短袖湿衣夹克，走下楼梯去了泳池。

D12.en: At 17, he became the first in his family to go to college.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他成为家里第一个上大学的人。

D18.en: At 17-18, he took two more part-time jobs to save for college.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他又打了两份工攒大学学费。

D5.en: At 19, he hit his head on the platform and was knocked unconscious.
D5.zh: 19岁时，他后脑勺重重撞在跳台上，昏了过去。

D9.en: At 24, he decided to talk about Cobb County and the Gay Games in his speech.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他决定在获奖感言里提到科布县和同性恋运动会的经历。

M4.en: At 24, he decided to talk about Cobb County and the Gay Games in his speech.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他决定在获奖感言里提到科布县和同性恋运动会的经历。

D16.en: At 16, he took someone aside and told him he thought he was gay.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他把一个人拉到一边，说自己可能是同性恋。

D11.en: As a small child, he was attracted to an older cousin and tried to spend time with him.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他被一个表哥吸引，想方设法和他待在一起。

M2.en: At 17, he was the first in his family to go to college; his parents didn't have the chance.
M2.zh: 17岁时，他成为家里第一个上大学的人，他父母没这个机会。

D12.en: At 15, he watched helplessly as Tendulkar and Kambli set a world-record partnership against his school.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他无助地看着坦杜卡尔和坎布利对阵他的学校创下世界纪录的搭档。

D3.en: At 17, he told his coach he wanted to play West Indian fast bowlers after facing local ones.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他告诉教练，在打过本地快速投手后，他想面对西印度群岛的快速投手。

D5.en: At 17, he told his coach he wanted to play West Indian fast bowlers after facing local ones.
D5.zh: 17岁时，他告诉教练，在打过本地快速投手后，他想面对西印度群岛的快速投手。

D14.en: At 17, he took the car around Mumbai, drove it to match venues, and showed it proudly to friends.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他开车在孟买转悠，开到比赛场地，并自豪地向朋友展示。

D6.en: At 11, he became a member of Acharekar's Kamat Memorial Cricket Club.
D6.zh: 11岁时，他加入了阿查雷卡的卡马特纪念板球俱乐部。

D2.en: At 16, he stayed at the crease for 195 minutes and, along with Sidhu, saw India through a critical stage.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他在击球区待了195分钟，与西杜一起帮助印度度过关键阶段。

M1.en: At 22, he became one of the youngest in the world to get 3,000 runs in ODIs in Sharjah.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他在沙迦成为世界上在ODI中达到3000分的最年轻球员之一。

D20.en: At age 21, he served as vice-captain and backbone of the lineup.
D20.zh: 21岁时，他担任副队长和阵容核心。

D10.en: At 17, he was self-conscious, out of his depth, shy, but anxious to co-operate.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他害羞、不自在，但渴望合作。

D18.en: At 17, he did his first ad, possibly the only one for free.
D18.zh: 17岁时他做了第一个广告，可能是唯一免费的。

D13b.en: At 15-16, he appeared successfully for his Class X exams.
D13b.zh: 15到16岁间，他成功通过了十年级考试。

D20X.en: At age 16, he was dropped for the other two one-dayers after a duck.
D20X.zh: 16岁时，他因得零分被排除在另外两场一日赛之外。

D11.en: At 16, he sleepwalked to the corridor asking about a man from Malik Sports who was to deliver his bats.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他梦游到走廊，询问那个要送他球棒的马利克体育的人。

D17.en: At 16, he and Manjrekar often stayed up late playing soft-ball cricket.
D17.zh: 16岁时，他和曼杰雷卡经常熬夜打软式板球。

D19.en: At 16, he tried to make the near-impossible possible.
D19.zh: 16岁时，他试图让几乎不可能的事成为可能。

M2.en: At 16, he tried to achieve the near-impossible.
M2.zh: 16岁时，他试图实现近乎不可能的事。

M3.en: At 16, he and Manjrekar often stayed up late playing soft-ball cricket.
M3.zh: 16岁时，他和曼杰雷卡经常熬夜打软式板球。

M4.en: At 16, he tried to make the near-impossible possible.
M4.zh: 16岁时，他试图让几乎不可能的事成为可能。

M5.en: At 16, he took on Qadir in a manner that Qadir and all who saw the game would never forget.
M5.zh: 16岁时，他以一种卡迪尔和所有观众都难忘的方式挑战了卡迪尔。

M8.en: At 16, he tried to make the near-impossible possible.
M8.zh: 16岁时，他试图让几乎不可能的事成为可能。

D12.en: He was born into post-war Munich, where hunger and need roamed the streets.
D12.zh: 他出生在战后的慕尼黑，饥饿和贫困在街头游荡。

D6.en: He lifted him onto the roof of a construction shack among the ruins.
D6.zh: 他把他举到废墟间一座工棚的屋顶上。

D14.en: He felt the first hint of football fascination.
D14.zh: 他感受到了足球魅力的第一丝气息。

D10.en: He went through valleys of self-doubt like all virtuosos.
D10.zh: 他像所有大师一样经历过自我怀疑的低谷。

M1.en: He said Germany had to win to open the door to the 1966 World Cup.
M1.zh: 他说德国必须赢才能打开1966年世界杯的大门。

D3.en: At 13, he watched Garrincha's magic on TV during the 1958 World Cup.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他在电视上观看了1958年世界杯加林查的魔幻表演。

D13a.en: In the middle of his apprenticeship to FC Bayern, he followed the ball's call over his father's wish for insurance.
D13a.zh: 他在拜仁慕尼黑学徒期间，追随足球的召唤，违背了父亲希望他做保险的意愿。

D15.en: He surged forward and delivered a sharp, low cross.
D15.zh: 他冲上前，送出一记又急又低的传中。

D13b.en: After primary school, he became an apprentice at Allianz to be an insurance salesman.
D13b.zh: 小学毕业后，他去安联当学徒，想成为保险推销员。

D7.en: At 19, he refused to be led like a dancing bear by an arrogant show-off.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他拒绝被一个傲慢的炫耀者像跳舞的熊一样牵着走。

D18.en: At 17, he signed his first professional contract with local rival Bayern for a base salary of DM 160.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他与当地对手拜仁签下第一份职业合同，基本工资160德国马克。

D1.en: In the 1966 World Cup final, he encountered referee bias when the Wembley goal was awarded to England.
D1.zh: 1966年世界杯决赛，他遭遇了裁判不公，温布利进球被判给英格兰。

D5.en: At 8, he started smoking and also spat into vodka glasses like a Moscow street kid.
D5.zh: 8岁时，他像莫斯科街头小孩一样开始抽烟，还往伏特加杯里吐口水。

D2.en: As a teenager, he became a goalkeeper with heart and soul.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他全身心投入成为守门员。

D4.en: At 13, he watched Garrincha's magical dribbling on TV during the 1958 World Cup in Sweden.
