D4.zh: 13岁时，他在电视上观看了1958年瑞典世界杯上加林查的魔幻盘带。

D10.en: At 17, he missed a short putt and hurled his putter in frustration.
D10.zh: 17岁时，他推短杆失误，气得把推杆扔了出去。

D13a.en: At 22, he joined the Coast Guard as a yeoman, puzzling Pap but with Doris's understanding.
D13a.zh: 22岁时，他加入海岸警卫队当文书，让帕普不解，但得到了多丽丝的理解。

M1.en: At 24, he won a series of tense, nail-biting matches in the early rounds of the U.S. Amateur.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他在美国业余锦标赛前几轮赢下了一系列紧张刺激的比赛。

D4.en: At 3, he started playing golf with a cut-off shortened hickory-shaft brassie and a sawed-off iron given by his father.
D4.zh: 3岁时，他用父亲给的截短的山胡桃木杆身铜头球杆和锯短的铁杆开始打高尔夫。

D14.en: At 14, he became aware of his desire to show off and please people.
D14.zh: 14岁时，他意识到自己想炫耀并取悦他人。

M4.en: At 14, he became aware of his own desire to show off and please people.
M4.zh: 14岁时，他意识到自己想要炫耀和取悦他人。

D5.en: He and his wife borrowed money to honeymoon on the PGA Tour circuit.
D5.zh: 他和妻子借钱去PGA巡回赛度蜜月。

D12.en: At 18, his father struggled to afford his college education due to many mouths to feed.
D12.zh: 他18岁时，父亲因家口众多难以负担他的大学费用。

D15.en: He won tense matches in the early rounds of the U.S. Amateur.
D15.zh: 他在美国业余赛早期轮次中赢得了紧张的比赛。

D16.en: At 18-21, he was called Crow by opponents for always yakking and boasting.
D16.zh: 18到21岁间，他因总是喋喋不休和吹嘘而被对手称为乌鸦。

M3.en: At 24, he was obsessed with becoming a better player than his hero, Bobby Jones.
M3.zh: 24岁时，他痴迷于成为比他的英雄鲍比·琼斯更好的球员。

M2.en: At 24, he wanted most to show his father that he was the best amateur golfer.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他最想向父亲证明他是最好的业余高尔夫球手。

D11.en: He played golf in his head as a teenager.
D11.zh: 他十几岁时在脑子里打高尔夫。

D15.en: At 19, he hit an inside-the-park home run.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他打出了一个场内本垒打。

D6.en: As a teenager, he occasionally participated in track and field at high school.
D6.zh: 十几岁时，他偶尔参加高中的田径项目。

D14.en: At 19, he trotted to the batter's box holding his cap.
D14.zh: 19岁时，他拿着帽子小跑到击球区。

D4.en: As a teenager, he squeezed a hard rubber ball to strengthen his forearms.
D4.zh: 十几岁时，他捏硬橡胶球来锻炼前臂。

D12.en: At 13, his father died; at 16, he was hailed as a baseball prodigy.
D12.zh: 13岁时父亲去世；16岁时，他被誉为棒球神童。

D18.en: His father gave him round-trip bus fare and sent him to San Juan to buy lottery tickets.
D18.zh: 他父亲给他往返车费，派他去圣胡安买彩票。

D9.en: At 15, he was starring at shortstop in a softball league.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他在垒球联赛中担任游击手主力。

D2.en: At 18-19, he listened to advice and applied it.
D2.zh: 18到19岁时，他听从建议并照做。

D1.en: At 25, he said he played most of the season sick because no one believed his arm was sore.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他说自己带病打了大半个赛季，因为没人相信他手臂酸痛。

D3.en: As a teenager, he crouched by the radio to hear his idol Monte Irvin.
D3.zh: 十几岁时，他蹲在收音机旁听偶像蒙特·欧文的比赛。

M3.en: He idolized Monte Irvin, San Juan's graceful outfielder.
M3.zh: 他崇拜圣胡安优雅的外野手蒙特·欧文。

D5.en: He wrenched his neck and spine but insisted he did not need treatment.
D5.zh: 他扭伤了脖子和脊椎，但坚持不需要治疗。

D7.en: He fidgeted with the hat and then flung it out an open window.
D7.zh: 他摆弄帽子，然后把它扔出窗外。

D8.en: At 25, he scored from first on a single.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他从一垒跑回本垒得分，靠一个一垒安打。

M4.en: As a young man, he always defended the cause of the Latins.
M4.zh: 他年轻时总是为拉丁人的事业辩护。

D17.en: At 20, he reached the hospital and learned Luis's tumor was malignant and advanced.
D17.zh: 20岁时，他到达医院，得知路易斯的肿瘤是恶性的且已晚期。

D13b.en: Since he was a minor, his father signed the contract for him.
D13b.zh: 因为他未成年，合同由他父亲代签。

D13a.en: At 19, he said if you gave your word, you keep your word.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他说如果你许下诺言，就要遵守诺言。

M1.en: At 19, he led the Royals to a win, as per a headline.
M1.zh: 19岁时，他带领皇家队获胜，如标题所示。

D19.en: At 20, he would spend hours studying outfield barrier angles.
D19.zh: 20岁时，他会花几个小时研究外场围栏的角度。

D10.en: He often became angry when he struck out and hurled his helmet to the ground.
D10.zh: 他三振出局时常生气，把头盔摔在地上。

D16.en: He said, 'Nonetheless, I play like Roberto Clemente.'
D16.zh: 他说：‘尽管如此，我打得像罗伯托·克莱门特。’

M2.en: He said he was trying to pay his parents back for giving him so much.
M2.zh: 他说他在努力回报父母给予他的一切。

D20.en: At fifteen, he was starring at shortstop in a softball league on a team sponsored by Sello Rojo.
D20.zh: 15岁时，他在一个由Sello Rojo赞助的垒球联赛中担任游击手主力。

M5.en: At 18, he imagines becoming famous and giving his hat to someone who will tell everyone it was his.
M5.zh: 18岁时，他想象自己成名后把帽子送人，那人会告诉大家这是他的帽子。

D6.en: At 14, she played for the North Texas State Team at an Olympic Development tournament in Louisiana.
D6.zh: 14岁时，她代表北得克萨斯州队参加路易斯安那州的奥运发展锦标赛。

D15.en: As a child aged 10-12, she played soccer with boys in Modesto, California.
D15.zh: 10到12岁时，她在加州莫德斯托和男孩们一起踢足球。

D2.en: At 10, she played soccer with boys in Modesto, California.
D2.zh: 10岁时，她在加州莫德斯托和男孩们一起踢足球。

D3.en: At 14, she watched the 1986 World Cup on TV, following games and players.
D3.zh: 14岁时，她在电视上观看1986年世界杯，关注比赛和球员。

M3.en: As a child, she wanted to be like Carin Gabarra.
M3.zh: 小时候，她想成为卡琳·加巴拉那样的人。

D1.en: At 20-25, she saw Michelle tackle a Brazilian player hard.
D1.zh: 20到25岁时，她看到米歇尔凶狠地铲倒一名巴西球员。

D5.en: At 20-25, she tried to jump clear of a Brazilian player but got hit in the legs and driven into the bench.
D5.zh: 20到25岁时，她试图跳开躲避巴西球员，但被踢中腿部，撞到替补席上。

D4.en: At 10, she played soccer with boys in Modesto, California.
D4.zh: 10岁时，她在加州莫德斯托和男孩们一起踢足球。

D2.en: She watched a golfer from ten feet away, then practiced for hours.
D2.zh: 她站在十英尺外观察高尔夫球手，然后练习数小时。

D6.en: At a reception, she was given the key to the City of Beaumont.
D6.zh: 在招待会上，她被授予博蒙特市钥匙。

D5.en: As a child, she soaped trolley tracks to make streetcars slide and halt.
D5.zh: 幼年时，她在电车轨道上涂肥皂，让电车打滑停下。

D14.en: She ran onto the field alone, waving her arms wildly amid the roar.
D14.zh: 她独自跑进场内，在欢呼声中疯狂挥舞手臂。

D7.en: She asked Sigrid to raid the pantry for her after being denied supper as punishment.
D7.zh: 她因逃学被罚不准吃晚饭，让西格丽德帮她去食品储藏室偷吃的。

D18.en: At 14-15, she sewed burlap bags at a potato-sack factory for a penny each.
D18.zh: 14到15岁时，她在土豆麻袋厂缝麻袋，一个一分钱。

D4.en: As a child, she soaped trolley tracks to make streetcars slide and halt.
D4.zh: 幼年时，她在电车轨道上涂肥皂，让电车打滑停下。

D8.en: At 12, she knocked a boy to the floor without hesitation.
D8.zh: 12岁时，她毫不犹豫地把一个男孩打倒在地。

D16.en: At 17-18, she was so argumentative that others couldn't win against her.
D16.zh: 17到18岁时，她特别能争辩，别人都说不过她。

D15.en: At 21, she became co-holder of the high-jump world record.
D15.zh: 21岁时，她成为跳高世界纪录的共同保持者。

D9.en: At 18, she scored 26 points, leading her team to a state championship.
D9.zh: 18岁时，她独得26分，带领球队赢得州冠军。

D13a.en: At 21, she announced she would give up amateur status and turn professional.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她宣布放弃业余身份，转为职业选手。

D12.en: As a teenager, she bought her mother a refrigerator and stove for Mother's Day.
D12.zh: 十几岁时，她在母亲节给妈妈买了冰箱和炉灶。

D1.en: At 21, she conquered her anxiety and changed clothes in the car on the way to the stadium.
D1.zh: 21岁时，她克服焦虑，在去体育场的车上换了衣服。

D10.en: At 21, she threw up her arm and yelled, 'Well, I won again!'
D10.zh: 21岁时，她举起手臂大喊：“我又赢了！”

M6.en: At 21, she decided to cash in on her fame and turned professional.
M6.zh: 21岁时，她决定利用名气赚钱，转为职业选手。

M7.en: At 21, she said she had only played basketball for three years and wasn't ready to quit as an amateur.
M7.zh: 21岁时，她说自己只打了三年篮球，还没准备好放弃业余身份。

D20.en: At 21, she was entered as a one-woman team in the 1932 national AAU championship.
D20.zh: 21岁时，她作为单人队伍参加1932年全国AAU锦标赛。

D20X.en: After one week as a harmonica-playing vaudeville star, she canceled her advance bookings.
D20X.zh: 当了一周口琴杂耍明星后，她取消了后续预订。

D19.en: At 22, she saved enough money to take time off and decided to pursue a new interest.
D19.zh: 22岁时，她攒够了钱休息一阵，决定追求新爱好。

D13b.en: At 21, she was hired by Chrysler to promote the Dodge coupe that had caused trouble.
D13b.zh: 21岁时，她被克莱斯勒雇佣推广惹麻烦的道奇双门车。

M2.en: At 19, she bought her mother a refrigerator and a stove for Mother's Day.
M2.zh: 19岁时，她母亲节给妈妈买了冰箱和炉灶。

M4.en: At 21, she decided to cash in on her fame and announced she would turn professional.
M4.zh: 21岁时，她决定利用名气赚钱，宣布转为职业选手。

M3.en: At 22, she felt that with professional coaching and practice, she could become an outstanding golfer.
M3.zh: 22岁时，她觉得通过专业训练和练习，她能成为杰出高尔夫球手。

D12.en: At 8, he was sent for by his mother Theresa.
D12.zh: 8岁时，他被母亲特蕾莎接走。

D7.en: He was the first professional football player to use an agent, a controversial decision.
D7.zh: 他是第一个使用经纪人的职业橄榄球运动员，这个决定当时很有争议。

D2.en: He would do the same thing after practice on the street outside his home.
D2.zh: 他会在训练后在家外的街道上做同样的事。

D5.en: During his NFL career, he often went entire games and hours of practice without drinking a drop.
D5.zh: 在NFL生涯中，他经常整场比赛和数小时训练不喝一滴水。

D9.en: At 23, he jumped at the chance and took the job.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他抓住机会接受了这份工作。

M4.en: In his early NFL years, his awareness of race and pro football grew steadily.
M4.zh: 在NFL早期，他对种族和职业橄榄球的认识逐渐加深。

D6.en: At 11-13, he joined a group of tough kids called the Gaylords.
D6.zh: 11到13岁时，他加入了一个叫盖洛德的硬核孩子帮。

D20.en: At 23-32, he rose to athletic director; eventually nothing happened without his knowledge.
D20.zh: 23到32岁间，他升任体育主任；最终没有他不知道的事。

D1.en: At 19, after being moved to second string, he angrily left practice, preparing to quit.
D1.zh: 19岁时，被降为替补后，他愤怒地离开训练场，准备放弃。

D3.en: At 13-15, he picked up a lacrosse stick out of curiosity and played gracefully despite never having seen one before.
D3.zh: 13到15岁时，他出于好奇拿起长曲棍球杆，尽管从未见过，却打得优雅自如。

D4.en: He picked up a lacrosse stick out of curiosity and played with other boys at school.
D4.zh: 他出于好奇拿起一根长曲棍球棒，和学校里的其他男孩一起玩。

D16.en: As a child, he questioned his grandmother about a white Jesus picture.
D16.zh: 幼年时，他问祖母为什么挂白人的耶稣像。

D15.en: At 20, he led Syracuse to an 8-6 win with 1 goal and 3 assists.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他带领锡拉丘兹以8-6获胜，贡献1球3助攻。

D14.en: At 16-18, he scored 53 points on a Tuesday and 55 on a Friday, breaking state records.
D14.zh: 16到18岁间，他周二得53分，周五得55分，打破州纪录。

D13b.en: At 17, he arrived on the Syracuse campus in fall 1953.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他于1953年秋到达锡拉丘兹校园。

M1.en: At 20, he had 132 yards, scored 21 points, and was named MVP.
M1.zh: 20岁时，他跑出132码，得21分，被评为MVP。

D10.en: At 21, he declined to help raise his child, fearing it would hurt his athletic career.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他拒绝抚养孩子，担心影响运动生涯。

D12.en: At 13-15, he resisted his father's efforts to join the family fishing business.
D12.zh: 13到15岁间，他拒绝父亲让他加入家族渔业生意的努力。

D14.en: At age 22, he had a cameo as a would-be singer in Manhattan Merry-Go-Round.
D14.zh: 22岁时，他在《曼哈顿旋转木马》中客串饰演想当歌手的人。

D4.en: At age 21, he instinctively knew where the ball was hit and retrieved it.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他本能地知道球被打到哪里并去捡回。

D16.en: At 21-22, he spoke to youth clubs and Rotary Clubs.
D16.zh: 21到22岁时，他向青年俱乐部和扶轮社发表演讲。

D7.en: As a teenager, he chose not to follow the family tradition, disappointing his father.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他选择不遵循家族传统，让他父亲失望了。

D20.en: At 21, he and Tom planned to open a restaurant and looked for a partner.
D20.zh: 21岁时，他和汤姆计划开一家餐厅，正在寻找合伙人。

M3.en: At 24, he said Gehrig was the greatest Yankee, citing RBIs as key.
M3.zh: 24岁时，他说格里克是最伟大的洋基队员，关键看打点。

D18.en: At 17-18, he became a hitter for hire, playing weekends and weekdays into evening.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他成了受雇击球手，周末和大多数工作日都打到傍晚。

M1.en: At 15, he had to win; that was why he played.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他必须赢；那是他打球的原因。

M2.en: At 21, he used his World Series share for a down payment on a house for his parents.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他用世界大赛的奖金为父母付了房子的首付。

M6.en: At 22, he dreamed of hitting a home run in the Series at Yankee Stadium or the Polo Grounds.
M6.zh: 22岁时，他梦想在洋基体育场或马球场的世界大赛中打出本垒打。

D13a.en: At 23, he sent a telegram accepting contract terms and announcing his travel plans.
D13a.zh: 23岁时，他发电报接受合同条款并告知行程。

D13b.en: At 15-16, he dropped out of Galileo High after a year.
D13b.zh: 15到16岁时，他在伽利略高中读了一年后辍学。

D11.en: At 23, he tweaked his nose and said, 'Don't I sound like a banjo?'
D11.zh: 23岁时，他捏着自己的鼻子说：‘我听起来不像班卓琴吗？’

D19.en: At 21-22, he wrote down numbers in a system of his own devising.
D19.zh: 21到22岁间，他用自己设计的一套系统记录数字。

D5.en: At 21, he received hate mail calling him an uppity nigger and a traitor after a passive gesture.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他因一个被动姿态收到仇恨邮件，被骂作傲慢的黑鬼和叛徒。

D1.en: He kept silent to the coach, then helped his team win the city championship and earn All-American honors.
D1.zh: 他对教练没再说什么，然后和球队一起赢得市冠军，自己再次入选全美最佳阵容。

D12.en: At 17, he got a job at HARYOUACT's journalism workshop, earning $35 a week in Harlem.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他在哈莱姆青年行动项目新闻工作坊找到工作，每周赚35美元。

D2.en: At 13, he practiced his jump shot the entire afternoon the day before.
D2.zh: 13岁时，他前一天整个下午都在练习跳投。

D3.en: At 21, he recited memorized Qur'an portions and summarized his college reading.
D3.zh: 21岁时，他背诵了记忆的古兰经段落，并总结了他的大学阅读内容。

D13a.en: At 17, he made a major individual decision to smoke pot.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他做了一个重大个人决定：吸大麻。

D6.en: At 21, he was invited on the Today show to publicize Operation Sports Rescue.
D6.zh: 21岁时，他受邀参加《今日秀》宣传体育救援行动。

D4.en: At 10, he studied a photo for months, having never seen anything like it.
D4.zh: 10岁时，他研究一张照片好几个月，从未见过类似的东西。

D7.en: At 20, he fully supported the idea, feeling no part of the country.
D7.zh: 20岁时，他完全支持这个想法，觉得自己与国家无关。

D17.en: At around age 10-11, he helped a girl at the pool by grabbing her under the arm.
D17.zh: 大约10到11岁时，他在泳池边抓住一个女孩的胳膊帮她到池边。

D15.en: At 14, he graduated from St. Jude's, standing six feet eight inches tall.
D15.zh: 14岁时，他从圣犹大毕业，身高六英尺八英寸。

M1.en: At age 13, he was driven by the desire for a display-case distinction.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他渴望获得展示柜里的荣誉。

D9.en: At 14, Mr. Donohue gave him a varsity uniform with number 33.
D9.zh: 14岁时，多诺霍先生给了他一件校队球衣，号码是33。

D20.en: At 17, he and the editorial board realized they could speak for the community.
D20.zh: 17岁时，他和编委会意识到他们可以为社区发声。

D11.en: At age 18-19, he wrote an essay bringing the Village Vanguard to life.
D11.zh: 18到19岁时，他写了一篇文章，让乡村先锋俱乐部栩栩如生。

D8.en: At 21, he told someone it wasn't his function to save the state.
D8.zh: 21岁时，他告诉别人他的职责不是拯救国家。

D14.en: At 17, he covered MLK's press conference and appeared in Jet magazine.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他报道了马丁·路德·金的新闻发布会，并出现在《Jet》杂志上。

D18.en: At age 19-20, he started working for Don Kirshner in the record division.
D18.zh: 19到20岁时，他开始为唐·基什纳的唱片部门工作。

D19.en: He won the postseason Iona tournament with five straight victories at age 14.
D19.zh: 他14岁时在Iona锦标赛中五连胜夺冠。

D20X.en: He tried to fight them but was sentenced to three weeks in the country.
D20X.zh: 他试图反抗他们，但被判在乡下待三周。

M2.en: His parents were joyous in their understated way, and his confidence rose.
M2.zh: 他的父母以低调的方式感到高兴，他的信心上升了。

M4.en: He fully supported the idea, feeling no part of the country.
M4.zh: 他完全支持这个想法，觉得自己不属于这个国家。

M6.en: At 14, he wanted to learn pointers and win at Power, not thinking about pro ball.
M6.zh: 14岁时，他只想学点技巧，在力量游戏中获胜，没想过打职业。

M7.en: He instilled pride in hundreds of inner-city black kids by teaching them.
M7.zh: 他通过教学给数百名市中心黑人孩子注入了自豪感。

D16.en: His lecturer took an interest in him because he spoke well in class.
D16.zh: 他的讲师对他感兴趣，因为他在课堂上发言很好。

D13b.en: He didn't appreciate his major career move being decided by a coin flip.
D13b.zh: 他不喜欢自己的重大职业变动由抛硬币决定。

D5.en: He skipped his senior year, defying the taboo of leaving college early.
D5.zh: 他跳过大四，挑战了提前离开大学的禁忌。

D11.en: He felt a warm surge of self-pride while waking up or walking down a hall.
D11.zh: 他在醒来或走过走廊时感到一阵温暖的自豪。

D15.en: He ran a quarter mile in 49.6 seconds for the mile relay team.
D15.zh: 他为一英里接力队跑完四分之一英里，用时49.6秒。

D9.en: As chief petty officer in charge of recreation, he organized countless sports tournaments.
D9.zh: 作为负责娱乐活动的海军士官长，他组织了无数体育比赛。

D2.en: He bought a suit in September and outgrew it by January.
D2.zh: 他九月买了套西装，到一月就穿不下了。

D1.en: He scored ten straight first-half field goals on the way to twenty-nine points.
D1.zh: 他上半场连续投进十个球，最终得到29分。

M1.en: He lost the NCAA tournament honor to Hal 'King' Lear of Temple.
M1.zh: 他把NCAA锦标赛的荣誉输给了坦普尔的哈尔·“国王”·李尔。

M6.en: He had his basketball epiphany in the winter of 1952.
M6.zh: 他在1952年冬天顿悟了篮球的真谛。

D3.en: He studied basketball by devouring sports magazines to learn how to defend top players.
D3.zh: 他通过大量阅读体育杂志来研究篮球，学习如何防守顶尖球员。

D10.en: He said, 'Don't you think I read the papers? It's like knives. It hurts.'
D10.zh: 他说：“你以为我不看报纸吗？就像刀子一样，很伤人。”

D13a.en: He gave Sears a warm, gracious speech.
D13a.zh: 他给西尔斯做了一场热情、得体的演讲。

D16.en: He asked Coach Woolpert, 'Can you hold these for me?'
D16.zh: 他问伍尔珀特教练：“你能帮我拿着这些吗？”

M5.en: He burned with hurt and blamed Woolpert.
M5.zh: 他内心受伤，责怪伍尔珀特。

M3.en: From that day on, he idolized Mikan.
M3.zh: 从那天起，他崇拜迈肯。

D8.en: He waited a few minutes, then landed his own cheap shot while the referees looked elsewhere.
D8.zh: 他等了几分钟，然后趁裁判不注意，给了对方一记暗招。

D14.en: He touched Rose's hand, stared into her eyes, spun her around, and planted a theatrical kiss.
D14.zh: 他触碰罗斯的手，凝视她的眼睛，让她转了一圈，然后戏剧性地吻了她。

M4.en: At 21, he understood image politics from media attention and listened to criticism of the Globetrotters.
M4.zh: 21岁时，他从媒体关注中理解形象政治，并听取了对哈林篮球队的批评。

D13a.en: At 18, he dropped out of St. John's University mid-semester to find work.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他辍学离开圣约翰大学，另找谋生途径。

D2.en: At 17-18, he was a university honor student, top in academics and athletics, with a scholarship and monthly stipend.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他是大学荣誉生，功课和体育都名列前茅，获得奖学金和每月十元补助。

D4.en: At 25, he was sent to design a cost accounting system at a mine and told to inspect underground.
D4.zh: 25岁时，他被派去矿场设计成本会计制度，并被告知必须下矿井实地考查。

D3.en: At 21-23, he often brought masons to design or modify kilns when selling coal.
D3.zh: 21到23岁间，他推销煤炭时常带着泥水匠设计或改装炉窑。

D5.en: At 25, he was transferred to Xuzhou coal mine during Xi'an Incident; his father told him to take risks.
D5.zh: 25岁时，他被调到徐州煤矿，正值西安事变，父亲对他说做大事要敢于冒大险。

D18.en: At 21-23, he sold Kaiping coal to kilns, transported by wooden boat, and accepted lime and bricks as payment.
D18.zh: 21到23岁间，他向窑户推销开平煤，用木船运煤，并收取石灰砖瓦抵煤价。

D1.en: At 21-22, he did not lose heart after failure; he analyzed causes and made new plans.
D1.zh: 21到22岁间，他毫不灰心，分析失败原因并重订计划。

D9.en: At 25, he drafted a firm English letter requesting his father's resignation on health grounds, signed by him and his six brothers.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他起草了一封措辞坚决的英文信，请求父亲辞职休养，由他和六个兄弟共同署名。

D7.en: At 18, he went to the principal's office and stammered, 'My mother disagrees!'
D7.zh: 18岁时，他走进校长办公室，嗫嚅地说：“我妈不同意！”

D12.en: At 25, after returning from UK studies, he was assigned as a regular accountant at China Terminal Company.
D12.zh: 25岁时，他留学回国后被派在中华码头公司当一名普通会计员。

D15.en: At 25, he descended into a mine with six guards and two police dogs the day after arriving.
D15.zh: 25岁时，他到后第二天就带着六个警卫和两只警犬下井了。

D6.en: At 25, he joined the Chongqing Stone Masons Association (MA Sonic Lodge).
D6.zh: 25岁时，他参加了重庆石匠协会 (MA Sonic Lodge)。

D2.en: At 15, he studied under his elder brother, who beat him when he slacked, so he worked day and night and improved greatly.
D2.zh: 15岁时，他在长兄的严厉督促下读书，不用功就挨打，于是日夜用功，文思大进。

D3.en: At 23, he studied algebra and discussed historical geography with Li Zhuanke.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他跟李专客学代数，还讨论了历史地理。

D9.en: At 19, he followed his father's order to join a literary society in Tianjin, where Yan Xiu often ranked first, giving him a model.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他奉父命与天津士人联文课，严修常居前列，得以观摩。

D13a.en: At 20, he followed his father's order to present his writings to Li Ciming as a student.
D13a.zh: 20岁时，他奉父命随两兄执贽李慈铭门下，呈所业文字。

D13b.en: At 22, he served as a candidate langzhong in the Ministry of Works, taking up his post in August.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他以候补郎中在工部都水司主稿上行走，八月到部。

D1.en: At 22, he took the autumn provincial exam and was selected as a copyist.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他参加秋闱乡试，被录取为誊录。

D18.en: As a small child, he liked to eat Longtan cooked half, and would ask his mother for a few coins to buy it.
D18.zh: 幼年时，他喜食龙潭熟半，常向母亲要一两文钱买来吃。

D16.en: At 23, he discussed Jin-Yuan territories and Northeast border history clearly with Li Ciming.
D16.zh: 23岁时，他与李慈铭谈论金元疆域及东北边地沿革之略，也能了了。

M3.en: He admired Fan Zhongyan and took benefiting people's livelihood as his duty.
M3.zh: 他仰慕范仲淹，以造福民生为己任。

M4.en: He devoted his life to industrial construction due to concern for national decline.
M4.zh: 他因忧心国势衰微，一生致力于工业建设。

M7.en: He once took benefiting people's livelihood as his own duty.
M7.zh: 他曾以造福民生为己任。

D3.en: He visited Xiehe University and saw the Peking Man skull and a dinosaur model.
D3.zh: 他参观了协和大学，看到了周口店猿人头盖骨和恐龙模型。

D11.en: He wrote math problem-solving books under the name Lu Si and filed them for publication.
D11.zh: 他以卢思名义编写了代数、三角等习题解答书，在成都立案准备出版。

D14.en: His first book 'New Solutions to Applied Math Problems' was published in 1914.
D14.zh: 1914年，他的《应用数题新解》出版，这是他第一部正版著作。

D7.en: At 17, he joined the Tongmenghui with revolutionary passion.
D7.zh: 17岁时，他满腔革命热情地加入了同盟会。

M4.en: At 18, he joined Sun Yat-sen's Revolutionary Alliance and became active in the Sichuan Railway Protection Movement.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他加入孙中山领导的同盟会，成为四川保路运动的积极分子。

D13a.en: He declined the post of Kuiguan supervisor and continued teaching and revolutionary activities in Chengdu.
D13a.zh: 他谢绝了夔关监督的任命，继续在成都教书和从事革命活动。

D16.en: As a journalist for 'Qun Bao', he criticized political corruption and advocated for the people.
D16.zh: 他担任《群报》记者兼编辑，抨击政治腐败，为人民疾苦呼吁。

D2.en: In harsh conditions, he completed all math courses in half a year and studied English hard.
D2.zh: 在简陋条件下，他半年内学完数学课程并刻苦攻读英语。

D18.en: He recruited math students, taught middle school math, and authored problem-solving books under the pen name Lu Si.
D18.zh: 他招收数学补习生，边学边教，并以卢思笔名编著了难题解准备出版。

D6.en: He noted the school had a bank, post office, consumer cooperative, clothing store, and shoe shop.
D6.zh: 他看到校中有银行、邮局、消费合作社、衣庄和鞋铺。

D9.en: He reported three things about Sichuan's situation and enterprises.
D9.zh: 他报告了川中情形和事业的三件事。

D1.en: At 23, he was framed and imprisoned, then bailed out by public figures.
D1.zh: 23岁时，他被诬陷入狱，后经各界人士联名保释。

D5.en: He wrote a letter exposing the truth of his arrest and had it smuggled out.
D5.zh: 他疾书一封揭露被捕真相的《告全县各界人士书》托狱卒带出。

D12.en: As a small child, he helped his mother with chores and cared for his younger brother.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他帮母亲分担家务，照管弟弟。

D13b.en: Yun Daiying introduced Lu Zuying to join the Chinese Kuomintang.
D13b.zh: 恽代英介绍卢子英加入国民党。

D8.en: He represented the left, was bold in fights, and feared no one.
D8.zh: 他代表左派，要打架他就去，胆量很大，不怕任何人。

M7.en: As a young man, he wished to drive out foreign capital from the Chuan River.
M7.zh: 青年时代，他就有将列强资本逐出川江的愿望。

M6.en: As a young man, he wished to drive out foreign capital from the Chuan River.
M6.zh: 青年时代，他就有将列强资本逐出川江的愿望。

M2.en: Lu Zuofu willingly endured poverty and hardship, driven by Confucian education to pursue national rejuvenation.
M2.zh: 卢作孚甘于清贫，以民族复兴为志，源于儒家教育。

D12.en: As a small child, he had a seaside villa named after him.
D12.zh: 他小时候，父亲为他建了一栋以他名字命名的海边别墅。

D7.en: At 13, he swore to his mother that he would become successful within 10 years.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他向母亲发誓10年内要出人头地。

D2.en: At 13-14, he studied hard, got first in class, and won a scholarship at Queen's College.
D2.zh: 13到14岁间，他发奋用功，考了D班第一，获得奖学金。

M1.en: At 18, he entered the University of Hong Kong with excellent grades, majoring in science.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他以优异成绩考入香港大学，专修科学。

D9.en: At 20, he was assigned as a telephone operator in a British air raid shelter.
D9.zh: 20岁时，他被安排到英军防空警报室当电话接线生。

D5.en: At 21, he carried 300,000 Hong Kong dollars cash on a midnight sea rendezvous, waiting until 4 am.
D5.zh: 21岁时，他身揣30万港元现金，午夜出海交易，等到凌晨4点。

M2.en: At 20, he told himself he went to Macau to make money, not to flee, and to support his mother.
M2.zh: 20岁时，他对自己说去澳门是去赚钱，不是逃难，要让母亲过好日子。

D10.en: At 21, he felt anxious and sleepy but forced himself to stay alert.
D10.zh: 21岁时，他忐忑不安，犯困但强迫自己打起精神。

D4.en: At 21, he tore a rag into squares and painted a Japanese flag with red paint.
D4.zh: 21岁时，他撕烂汗衫画红膏药，做出一面日本国旗。

D16.en: At 21, he spoke fluent Japanese and persuaded a Japanese officer with his eloquence.
D16.zh: 21岁时，他日语流畅，口才了得，打动了日军头目。

D18.en: At 21-22, he bid 100 yuan for 40 machines, resold them before payment deadline, earning 22,000 yuan profit.
D18.zh: 21到22岁时，他看中40台机器，用100元竞标，中标后不等付款限期倒卖，赚得2.2万元利润。

D6.en: In 1942 at age 21, he fell in love with a woman, proposed, and married her.
D6.zh: 1942年21岁时，他爱上一位女子，向她求婚，不久两人结婚。

D1.en: In the 1940s at age 20-25, he never gave up despite setbacks; he found Liu Cai to discuss strategies.
D1.zh: 20到25岁间，他永不言败，虽受挫折却不放弃，找来刘财共商对策。

D20.en: In 1942 at age 21, after a year as a clerk at Lianchang Company, he became a partner due to his outstanding performance.
D20.zh: 1942年21岁时，他在联昌公司做职员一年，成绩斐然，被吸收为合伙人。

D3.en: At age 20-21 in 1941-1942, he learned simple daily language quickly, achieving more in a month than others in a year.
D3.zh: 20到21岁时，他富有语言天赋，一个月学会简单日常用语，比一般人学一年收获还大。

D13a.en: At 13, he was sent by his father to study in Canada.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他被父亲安排去加拿大留学。

D20X.en: Ye Han proposed retirement on condition that his son join the board to manage the casino.
D20X.zh: 叶汉提出退休，但要求让儿子进入董事局参与管理赌场。

M4.en: Ye Han allied with tycoon Cheng Yu-tung to bid for the casino monopoly by offering higher taxes.
M4.zh: 叶汉拉拢郑裕彤，联手向澳府提出增加赌税，企图夺取赌场专营权。

D13b.en: He spent his childhood in Zhuangshi Town and attended Ye's Zhongxing School.
D13b.zh: 他在庄市镇度过童年，就读于叶氏中兴学校。

D9.en: At 18, he assisted in film distribution and learned filmmaking at Tianyi Film Company.
D9.zh: 18岁时，他在天一影片公司协助发行影片并学习拍摄。

D4.en: At 19-20, he operated the camera for 'Sun Wukong vs. Golden Leopard', his breakout film.
D4.zh: 19到20岁时，他主执机器拍摄《孙行者大战金钱豹》，成为成名作。

D2.en: At 17-19, he worked as a general handyman at Tianyi, doing cleaning and prop management.
D2.zh: 17到19岁时，他在天一干后勤，打扫卫生、管理道具。

D7.en: At 13, he told his brother he wanted to change his name because it sounded ugly.
D7.zh: 13岁时，他对大哥说想改名，因为原名难听。

D11.en: At 13, after a sleepless night, he announced his new name: Renleng, styled Yifu.
D11.zh: 13岁时，他熬了一夜后宣布新名：仁楞，号逸夫。

D15.en: At 21-22, he trudged through mud in a storm.
D15.zh: 21到22岁时，他在狂风暴雨中泥泞跋涉。

D1.en: At 21, he persuaded small Chinese theater owners to screen 'Lidi Chengfo'.
D1.zh: 21岁时，他游说华人小戏院老板上映《立地成佛》。

D6.en: At 7, he entered the Hong Kong Chinese Drama Academy, interested in martial arts.
D6.zh: 7岁时，他进入香港中国戏剧学院，爱好武术。

D20.en: He and his brothers worked together to emerge from the shadow of Liuhe.
D20.zh: 他和兄弟们齐心协力，走出了六合的阴影。

M1.en: At 21-22, he wrote film details on posters and pasted them around villages.
M1.zh: 21到22岁时，他拿着笔在海报上写好影片名称内容，拎着浆糊桶走街串村张贴。

D12.en: At 15, he told his mother he was already an adult and could take care of himself.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他对母亲说自己已经是大人了，能照顾自己。

D13a.en: At 37, he persuaded his father to let him enter the shipping business instead of clashing with him.
D13a.zh: 37岁时，他说服父亲同意自己投身航运业，没有正面冲突。

D2.en: As a teenager, he felt that school in Ningbo only taught classics and couldn't satisfy his curiosity.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他觉得在宁波读书只教四书五经，满足不了他的好奇心。

D9.en: At 24, he became head of the insurance department at Central Trust and manager of a bank branch.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他担任中央信托局保险部主任和工矿银行分行经理。

D3.en: At 12-15, he often sat by the window watching ships and wondered about the outside world.
D3.zh: 12到15岁时，他常坐在窗前看商船，想知道外面的世界。

M1.en: At 13, he said he would return only after making a great achievement.
M1.zh: 13岁时，他说干出一番事业才回来见母亲。

D8.en: At 7-10, he stood up to correct a wrong judgment made by another boy named Wang Ergong.
D8.zh: 7到10岁时，他站出来纠正了王尔功做出的一个错误判断。

D14.en: At 10-12, as a flag bearer, he marched with head high and the flag at a 45-degree angle.
D14.zh: 10到12岁时，他当旗手，昂首挺胸，将校旗保持45度角前进。

D5.en: At 13, he traveled alone, and someone promised to put him on a boat to Hankou.
D5.zh: 13岁时，他独自出门，有人保证送他上到汉口的船。

M2.en: At 13, he believed that bringing honor to his family depended on these things.
M2.zh: 13岁时，他认为光宗耀祖全靠这些了。

M7.en: At 13, he declared that as a future master, he should serve the country with passion and knowledge.
M7.zh: 13岁时，他说作为未来的主人翁，应以热血报国，以知识救国。

D7.en: At 13-14, he told his mother he wanted to go to Hankou to study with his father.
D7.zh: 13到14岁时，他对母亲说想去汉口念书，和爹在一起。

M4.en: At 66, he chose Beilun Port and decided to develop it.
M4.zh: 66岁时，他相中了北仑港，并决定把它开发出来。

D1.en: At 19-27, his dream of college was dashed by the war.
D1.zh: 19到27岁时，他因抗日战争大学之梦成泡影。

D4.en: As a child, he loved reading.
D4.zh: 他从小喜欢读书。

D11.en: In his 30s-40s, he named his four daughters after the first four letters of the alphabet.
D11.zh: 30到40岁时，他用英文字母A、B、C、D的顺序为四个女儿命名。

D16.en: At 37, he avoided conflict with his father and explained the shipping prospects.
D16.zh: 37岁时，他没有跟父亲正面冲突，而是陈述了航运的前景。

D18.en: As a child, he loved reading.
D18.zh: 他从小喜欢读书。

D19.en: He named his daughters in alphabetical order to show seniority.
D19.zh: 他用英文字母A、B、C、D的顺序为女儿命名，以示长幼有序。

D20X.en: He insisted on canceling those invitations.
D20X.zh: 他硬是把这些邀请取消了。

M3.en: As a child, he loved reading.
M3.zh: 他从小喜欢读书。

M5.en: He did not confront his father directly but explained the situation and shipping prospects.
M5.zh: 他没有跟父亲发生正面冲突，而是把自己所掌握的情况以及航运的前景一一向父亲陈述。

M6.en: As a child, he loved reading.
M6.zh: 他从小喜欢读书。

M8.en: As a child, he loved reading.
M8.zh: 他从小喜欢读书。

D13b.en: At 13, he became an intern at the insurance department of British firm Arnhold & Co.
D13b.zh: 他13岁时进英商安利洋行保险部做实习生。

D12.en: As a small child, he followed his mother to pick up fallen wood and coal from passing carts to sell or use for fuel.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他跟着母亲在台车道上捡拾掉落的木材和煤块，好的拿去卖，差的生火做饭。

D18.en: At 15, he borrowed 200 yuan from his father and opened a rice shop in Chiayi, starting his business.
D18.zh: 15岁时，他靠父亲借来的200元在嘉义开了家米店，迈出事业第一步。

D2.en: After college, he left his business to brothers and went to Japan to study finance and management at Tokyo Imperial University.
D2.zh: 大学毕业后，他把事业交给兄弟，自己去日本帝国大学学财政和工商管理。

D1.en: In his early 20s, he tried brick, lumber, and rice milling businesses, facing hardship but persevering.
D1.zh: 20岁出头时，他尝试过砖厂、木材业和碾米厂，虽然落魄但坚持忍耐。

M4.en: At 9, he vowed to build the largest hospital in Taiwan to treat all people, especially the poor.
M4.zh: 9岁时，他发誓要建台湾最大的医院，救治所有人，特别是穷苦病人。

D5.en: At 15, he borrowed 200 yuan from his father and opened a rice shop in Chiayi, starting his business.
D5.zh: 15岁时，他靠父亲借来的200元在嘉义开了家米店，迈出事业第一步。

D9.en: He called a meeting with technical, production, and management staff to discuss making plastic chairs.
D9.zh: 他通知技术人员、生产人员和管理人员开会，讨论塑料椅子的制作。

D4.en: At 16, he and his brothers picked out debris from the rice before selling it to customers.
D4.zh: 16岁时，他和兄弟们在闲时把米里的杂物拣出来再卖给顾客。

D3.en: At 15, while cleaning, he observed how the boss kept accounts and calculated costs, a habit that later helped him.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他边收拾边观察老板记账和核算成本，这个好学习惯让他后来开店受益匪浅。

D6.en: At 15-16, while working at a rice shop, he built good relationships with regular customers.
D6.zh: 15到16岁时，他在米店打工，和常客搞好关系，渐渐熟识。

D15.en: As a child, he sometimes walked 20 li of mountain roads to see a movie.
D15.zh: 小时候，他有时要走20里山路去看一场电影。

D13b.en: At 7, he was sent to Xindian National Elementary School by his parents.
D13b.zh: 7岁时，他被父母送到新店国民小学读书。

D2.en: He repeatedly failed the provincial exam over eight years, despite passing lower-level exams.
D2.zh: 他八年间乡试屡次落第，尽管通过了低级别考试。

D7.en: He fraudulently registered under a false name in Rugao and passed all three levels of exams.
D7.zh: 他冒籍如皋张氏，改名张育才，连过县州院试。

D1.en: He repeatedly failed the provincial exam over eight years, despite passing lower-level exams.
D1.zh: 他八年间乡试屡次落第，尽管通过了低级别考试。

D4.en: He realized that knowledge extends beyond the eight-legged essay to practical affairs.
D4.zh: 他发现学问不限于八股制艺，实际事务中还有更多深奥道理。

D3.en: He realized that knowledge extends beyond the eight-legged essay to practical affairs.
D3.zh: 他发现学问不限于八股制艺，实际事务中还有更多深奥道理。

D9.en: He expanded his social circle in Jiangning, meeting many prestigious friends and teachers.
D9.zh: 他在江宁扩大社交圈，结识了许多有声望的师友。

D12.en: He brought home 100 taels of silver from his salary to repay debts.
D12.zh: 他回家探亲时带回俸银一百两用于还债。

D18.en: He was deeply aware of the harm of likin taxes due to his small merchant family background.
D18.zh: 他因出身小商人家庭，对厘金的危害感受深切。

D11.en: He began practicing eight-legged essays and writing poems at the Xiting Poetry Society.
D11.zh: 他开始习试帖制艺，在西亭诗社分题作诗或限字诗钟。

D13a.en: He reported himself and requested to be stripped of his scholar status and return to his original hometown.
D13a.zh: 他自行检举，请求褫夺衣顶并归还原籍。

M1.en: At 15, he passed the imperial examination to become a shengyuan.
M1.zh: 15岁时，他考取了附学生员。

D12.en: At 14, he left school to support his family.
D12.zh: 14岁时，他辍学养家。

D2.en: At 12, he studied secondary school courses at night after 14-hour workdays and set three alarm clocks to ensure punctuality.
D2.zh: 12岁时，他每天工作14小时后仍坚持夜间自修中学课程，并设三个闹钟防迟到。

D18.en: At 13, he bought used textbooks from bookstores that sold old books.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他想到买旧教材的办法。

M1.en: At 22, he decided to start his own business instead of working for others.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他决定自己独立经营。

M2.en: He remembered his father's stories about prodigies who wasted their talent.
M2.zh: 他牢记父亲讲的关于神童不思勤勉的故事。

D13a.en: At 14, he refused his uncle's offer to fund his schooling and chose to work.
D13a.zh: 14岁时，他谢绝舅舅资助，决定辍学挣钱养家。

D3.en: At 13, he bought books on lung disease treatment and care, and feared he had all symptoms.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他买来肺病治疗书籍，发现自己似乎有所有症状。

D11.en: As a child aged 5-10, he often read avidly in a small study after school.
D11.zh: 5到10岁时，他放学后常在小书房如醉如痴地读书。

D15.en: At 14-15, he woke before 5am, carried heavy teapots, and worked 16 hours daily.
D15.zh: 14到15岁时，他每天不到5点起床，拎着沉重茶壶工作16小时。

D1.en: At 14, he lost his father and struggled to find work, but never gave up.
D1.zh: 14岁时丧父，他挨家挨户找工作，虽屡遭闭门羹仍坚持不懈。

D6.en: At 12-13, he learned Cantonese from his cousin to communicate in Hong Kong.
D6.zh: 12到13岁时，他跟着表妹学广州话，很快就能和香港人交流了。

D5.en: At 18, he left a hardware factory to join a small plastic belt factory.
D5.zh: 18岁时，他离开五金厂，去了一家小型塑胶裤带制造公司。

M6.en: As a small child, he dreamed of becoming an educator.
M6.zh: 幼年时，他的理想是当一名教育家。

M4.en: At 11, he felt a surge of patriotic passion.
M4.zh: 11岁时，他心中涌动着拳拳爱国热情。

D7.en: At 14, he stopped grave diggers and walked away, refusing to pay them.
D7.zh: 14岁时，他阻止挖坟的人，说钱当施舍，然后头也不回地下山了。

D20.en: At 18, he became a department manager; two years later, general manager.
D20.zh: 18岁时他成为部门经理，两年后升任塑胶公司总经理。

D20X.en: At 22, he raised funds to start his own business.
D20X.zh: 22岁时，他筹资建立自己的企业。

D19.en: At 17-22, he divided Hong Kong into districts and recorded prices and consumer habits in a notebook.
D19.zh: 17到22岁时，他把香港分成许多区域，把物价和消费习惯记在本子上。

D4.en: At 17, he carried a big bag and traveled door to door on foot to sell his goods, walking over ten li to save fare.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他背着大包挨家挨户推销，为了省车费走十来里路。

D8.en: At 18, he was promoted to manager.
D8.zh: 18岁时，他被提拔为经理。

D10.en: At 12, he sat on the bed hugging his knees, staring out the window into the deep night sky.
D10.zh: 12岁时，他抱着腿坐在床上，凝望着窗外深沉的夜空。

D14.en: At 14-18, he worked hard, studied diligently, and remained optimistic about life, actively pursuing the future.
D14.zh: 14到18岁间，他勤奋工作，努力学习，对生活充满乐观，积极追求未来。

M7.en: At 17-22, he assisted the boss in boosting sales and production, making the plastic company thrive.
M7.zh: 17到22岁间，他协助老板以销促产，使塑胶公司生意红火。

M5.en: As a small child, he was told to study hard, stand out, and achieve great things.
M5.zh: 幼年时，他被要求好好学习，出人头地，做出一番事业。

M3.en: As a small child, he deeply admired those loyal and patriotic people of insight.
M3.zh: 幼年时，他对那些精忠报国的有识之士敬佩不已。

D17.en: At 13-14, he diligently cared for his father in the hospital, visiting even in bad weather and reporting his studies daily.
D17.zh: 13到14岁间，他尽心伺候住院的父亲，再恶劣的天气也去探望，每天汇报学业。

D3.en: At 8-12, he applied a Confucian quote to real life after his teacher asked him to.
D3.zh: 8到12岁时，他在老师要求下将一句儒家名言应用到现实生活例子中。

D4.en: At 12, he learned gold assaying, refining, and melting, then became head clerk at Tianbaorong.
D4.zh: 12岁时，他学了看金、化金、熔金技术，出任天宝荣金铺头柜。

D5.en: At 10, he slipped away from a crowd to escape bandits.
D5.zh: 10岁时，他悄悄从人群中逃出去躲避贼匪。

D1.en: At 10, after a robbery, he felt distressed but worked hard with his father to restart.
D1.zh: 10岁时，被洗劫后他感到难堪，但和父亲一起变卖杂物从头苦干。

D18.en: At 12, he washed old banknotes with bleach, coated them with egg white, and ironed them to look new.
D18.zh: 12岁时，他把旧纸币泡水加漂白粉洗刷，涂蛋白风干熨平。

D9.en: At 15, his father entrusted him to manage two businesses in Shunde.
D9.zh: 15岁时，他父亲把顺德永生银号和天宝荣金铺交给他掌管。

D12.en: At 15, his father left him in charge of Yongsheng Bank and Tianbaorong Gold Shop.
D12.zh: 15岁时，他父亲让他掌管永生银号和天宝荣金铺。

D6.en: At 25, he regularly lunched with business peers at Xiawei Restaurant in Central to exchange market info and discuss investments.
D6.zh: 25岁时，他常在中环夏蕙餐厅与商贾行家午膳茶叙，交流市场资料，研究投资机会。

D20.en: At 25, he joined seven others to jointly invest in real estate.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他与另外七人合资经营地产。

D2.en: At 14-15, he started as a handyman at a gold shop, sweeping, dusting, and cleaning.
D2.zh: 14到15岁时，他在金铺从杂役干起，扫地、抹灰、倒痰盂、洗厕所。

D13b.en: At 18, he married the boss's daughter, Zhou Cuiying, becoming the son-in-law.
D13b.zh: 18岁时，他与老板的千金周翠英圆房，正式成为东家女婿。

D13a.en: At 18, he married Zhou Cuiying in a wedding ceremony.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他与周翠英洞房花烛，结为连理。

D6.en: At 15-18, he worked diligently but also sneaked out to observe other shops without permission.
D6.zh: 15到18岁时，他勤勤恳恳，却又未经老板同意就跑去逛街睇铺。

D9.en: Before completing 3 years, he was promoted to manager of the gold shop at age 17-18.
D9.zh: 17到18岁时，他未满3年就荣升为金铺掌管，负责铺面日常经营。

D12.en: At 14-15, he began his apprenticeship.
D12.zh: 14到15岁时，他开始了学徒生涯。

D4.en: In his 20s-30s, he bought a prime triangular lot in Causeway Bay and built a luxury building.
D4.zh: 20到30多岁时，他买下铜锣湾三角地，盖了一幢豪华级香港大厦。

D3.en: At 15-16, he had a sudden idea and approached someone to offer currency exchange at Chow Tai Fook.
D3.zh: 15到16岁时，他灵机一动，趋前搭讪说周大福金铺可兑换，价格最公道。

D5.en: At 23, after a plane crash, he took over gold transport, reducing quantity and avoiding flights to minimize risk.
D5.zh: 23岁时，空难发生后，他包揽携带黄金的任务，减少数量并尽量不坐飞机以降低风险。

D18.en: At 14, he came to Guangzhou to work at a salt warehouse, then moved to a gold shop as an apprentice.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他来广州盐仓打工，后转入金铺做学徒。

D7.en: At 19, he staged a fake funeral to quit school after one year.
D7.zh: 19岁时，他让同学把躺在棺材里的他抬出校门，以此告别学校。

D12.en: At 24, he was responsible for developing new germanium transistors.
D12.zh: 24岁时，他负责开发新的锗晶体管。

D3.en: At 24, he began self-studying semiconductors using Shockley's classic book.
D3.zh: 24岁时，他开始自修半导体，课本是夏克雷的经典作。

D2.en: At 24, he read slowly, word by word, sometimes only two pages a night.
D2.zh: 24岁时，他一个字一个字慢慢读，有时一晚只能读两页。

D1.en: At 23-24, he failed two doctoral qualifying exams but recovered in a week.
D1.zh: 23到24岁间，他两次博士资格考试落第，但只花了一星期就恢复过来。

D11.en: As a child, he naively dreamed of becoming a writer for six or seven years.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他有六七年痴心想以写作为终身工作。

D13a.en: At 16-17, his father dissuaded him from writing, saying it might not make a living.
D13a.zh: 16到17岁时，父亲劝他写作不易谋生，他渐渐打消了作家野心。

D6.en: At 24, he had a colleague at the hotel who became his first semiconductor teacher.
D6.zh: 24岁时，同住旅馆的一位同事成了他第一个半导体教师。

D5.en: At 11-12, he traveled from Xuzhou to Luoyang using various transport, even walking across the front line.
D5.zh: 11到12岁时，他从徐州到洛阳用尽各种交通工具，甚至步行穿越前线。

D9.en: At 24, he trained two experienced operators who reached 80-90% speed with new method in a couple of days.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他训练两位经验最丰富的作业员，一两天后她们新方法速度达八九成。

D15.en: At 25-26, he worked night shifts and sometimes stayed up all night for his thesis.
D15.zh: 25到26岁时，他常上夜班赶工作，有时彻夜不眠写论文。

D7.en: He sneaked out after lights-out to buy snacks and was caught by the dean, feeling embarrassed.
D7.zh: 他熄灯后偷溜出去买宵夜被训导主任抓到，觉得很没面子。

D20.en: He co-created a school newspaper called 'Jian Bao' and wrote for the literature section.
D20.zh: 他和同学合办壁报《健报》，负责文艺栏并每周写稿。

M1.en: He developed nearly 10 types of germanium transistors in 1956-1957.
M1.zh: 他在1956到1957年间开发了近10种锗晶体管。

M2.en: His father gently discouraged his ambition to become a writer, warning it might not pay.
M2.zh: 父亲委婉地劝他打消作家梦，说写作可能饿肚子。

M7.en: He saw himself as a future leader of China, with victory and rising status assured.
M7.zh: 他自诩为中国未来主人，认为胜利在望且国际地位提升。

D14.en: He bought a season ticket to the Boston Symphony Orchestra and attended weekly.
D14.zh: 他买了波士顿交响乐团的季票，每周去听音乐会。

D10.en: He was devastated when his name was not on the list, feeling his self-esteem crushed.
D10.zh: 他榜上无名，自尊心和自信心瞬间消失，感到巨大打击。

D4.en: He tested an indirect heating method by letting the welder touch only the copper wire.
D4.zh: 他试验间接加热法，让焊接器只接触铜丝而不直接碰电极。

D12.en: He fell critically ill after his mother's death and recovered by eating watermelon.
D12.zh: 母亲去世后他病危，吃西瓜转机。

D2.en: At 16, he wrote the shop sign and spring couplets for others.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他写庄招牌并代人写小春联。

D4.en: He planned the mill layout himself without engineers or architects.
D4.zh: 他靠自己计划厂屋，没有工程师或建筑师。

D3.en: At 16, he cured his nearsightedness by looking at distant green or roof tiles daily.
D3.zh: 16岁时，他每天望远处青绿色或屋瓦，治好了近视。

D6.en: He attended a welcome meeting for Sun Yat-sen in Shanghai and was in a group photo.
D6.zh: 他在上海参加欢迎孙中山的会议，并合影留念。

D18.en: He faced his most difficult business situation with debts and no funds.
D18.zh: 他遇到入市以来最困难的局面，欠款无着。

D19.en: He traveled to Shanghai every morning and returned by evening train over 30 times.
D19.zh: 他每早去上海，晚车返回，共三十余次。

D20.en: At 25, he became general manager, with his brother as chairman and other directors elected.
D20.zh: 25岁时，他任总经理，其兄任董事长，并选出其他董事。

M1.en: He and his brother decided to focus on profitable mills and work hard.
M1.zh: 他与兄弟决定专心走赚钱的路，发愤用力。

D9.en: He served as general manager with his brother as chairman.
D9.zh: 他担任总经理，兄任董事长。

D5.en: He had no money, only his own labor.
D5.zh: 他财力全无，只靠人力。

D1.en: At 25, he and his brother decided to focus on the two factories and work hard.
D1.zh: 25岁时，他和兄弟商量，还是向赚钱的路走，专心两厂，从此发愤用力。

D7.en: At 25, he agreed to repay 8,000 taels yearly with land deeds as collateral, but the other party still refused.
D7.zh: 25岁时，他答应每年付八千两，用田单和苏州的契据作抵押，但对方还是不答应。

M2.en: At 25, he felt heartbroken because the business was founded by his father.
M2.zh: 25岁时，他十分痛心，因为那是他父亲创下的。

M7.en: At 25, he proposed three motions at a meeting, including expanding textiles and building a machine tool factory.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他在会上提出三案，包括扩充纺织和设母机厂。

D14.en: At 25, he won two third-class medals and felt honored.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他得了两块三等奖牌，一时荣幸。

D10.en: At 25, he stayed up all night with the bank manager who wanted to withdraw goods late.
D10.zh: 25岁时，他和信成银行经理坐到天亮，一夜没睡。

D15.en: At 25, he traveled to Shanghai every morning and returned by evening train, over 30 times.
D15.zh: 25岁时，他每天早上去上海，晚上坐车回来，共三十多次。

D16.en: At 25, he proposed three motions at a meeting with over 80 proposals.
D16.zh: 25岁时，他在有八十多件提案的会上提出了三案。

D17.en: At 25, he considered buying Ruan Gong Dun but decided against it due to difficulty in filling.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他想买阮公墩，但因填高不易而作罢。

D13a.en: At 25, he was appointed by the county magistrate as the director of Kaiyuan Fan and concurrently as an education promoter.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他被知县任命为开原扇董，兼任劝学事。

D13b.en: At 25, he was appointed as a fan director and education promoter by the county magistrate.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，他被知县照会担任扇董兼劝学事。

D20X.en: At 25, he found the board of Zhenxin difficult to cooperate with.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他觉得振新董事会难以合作。

M4.en: At 25, he proposed three motions including expanding textiles and building a machine factory.
M4.zh: 25岁时，他提出三案，包括扩充纺织和设母机厂。

M5.en: At 25, his Maoxin brand was praised and outperformed others.
M5.zh: 25岁时，他的茂新牌子被称赞，销路俏过老车。

M6.en: After the burial, he felt joyful, believing he had found a wealthy land and would succeed within a few years.
M6.zh: 安葬后，他心中大悦，认为得到了一块富地，几年内必能成功。

M8.en: At 25, after burial, he felt joyful, believing a rich land would bring success.
M8.zh: 25岁时，安葬后他心中大悦，认为得一大富地必有成功之日。

D7.en: At 18, he got angry and became more determined to pass math.
D7.zh: 18岁时，他感到愤怒，更加决心要通过数学考试。

D6.en: At 21-24, he met his future wife and many friends who later became business partners.
D6.zh: 21到24岁间，他结识了未来的妻子张瑛和许多朋友，其中一些人后来成为创业伙伴。

D2.en: At 16, he took study courses, bicycling between home and school in summer 1980.
D2.zh: 16岁时，他1980年夏天参加学习课程，骑自行车往返于家和学校。

D1.en: At 20, he took the college entrance exam a third time and was admitted to Hangzhou Normal University.
D1.zh: 20岁时，他第三次参加高考，考上了杭州师范学院。

D9.en: As a small child, he created a cohesive bond among a select group of peers.
D9.zh: 幼年时，他就能在选定的同龄人中建立紧密联系。

D16.en: At 24-31, he taught English at a night school; his lectures were so exciting that students from other classes came to watch.
D16.zh: 24到31岁间，他在夜校兼职教外语，讲课精彩，其他班学生都跑来看他表演。

D14.en: At 24-31, he taught English at a night school, where his lively lectures drew students from other classes.
D14.zh: 24到31岁间，他在夜校兼职教外语，讲课精彩，其他班的学生都跑来看他表演。

D3.en: At 15, he practiced English with foreigners on the streets of Hangzhou whenever he had a chance.
D3.zh: 15岁时，他一有机会就在杭州街上跟外国人练英语。

D11.en: As a small child, he immersed himself in martial arts novels.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他沉浸在武侠小说的浪漫中。

D18.en: At 13, he used his pedicab to serve as an English-speaking guide for foreigners.
D18.zh: 13岁时，他蹬三轮车为外国人当英语导游。

D5.en: As a child, he was badly wounded and had to be stitched up without anesthesia.
D5.zh: 他小时候受伤严重，没有麻醉就缝了针。

M2.en: He grew up fighting for his family name.
M2.zh: 他从小为家族名声而打架。

D13a.en: At 20, he didn't do well in college entrance exams and entered Hangzhou Normal University.
D13a.zh: 20岁时他高考没考好，进了杭州师范学院。

D12.en: At 18, he pedaled a heavy tricycle daily to earn one yuan, rain or shine.
D12.zh: 18岁时他每天蹬三轮车赚一元钱，风雨无阻。

D15.en: At age 5, he was in charge of watching the flag during a spring outing and planted it first on the hilltop.
D15.zh: 5岁时，他在春游中负责看管旗帜，并第一个把旗插到山头上。

D1.en: At 17, he challenged rural soldiers to a wheat-cutting contest, refusing to give up despite bleeding hands.
D1.zh: 17岁时，他和农村兵比赛割麦，手磨出血也不服输。

D6.en: At age 7-9, he stole melons alone and became the village children's leader.
D6.zh: 7到9岁时，他独自偷了一袋香瓜，成了孩子王。

D7.en: As a student, he climbed a tree for bird nests and got stuck, requiring the fire department.
D7.zh: 学生时代，他爬树掏鸟窝下不来，惊动了消防队。

D5.en: At 17-19, he climbed a mountain alone without telling anyone and got stranded overnight.
D5.zh: 17到19岁时，他独自登山未打招呼，困在半山腰一夜。

D2.en: At 23, he studied English and political economy on his own after being admitted to a railway college.
D2.zh: 23岁时，他被选送入兰州铁路学院，课余自学英语和政治经济学。

D3.en: At 22, he chose a boiler repair job over driving because it offered a chance to attend university.
D3.zh: 22岁时，他放弃开车，选择锅炉大修工，因为有机会上大学。

D12.en: At 17, he enlisted and became a truck driver in the air force logistics.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他入伍当汽车兵，在空军后勤系统。

D13b.en: At 17, he enlisted and became a truck driver in the air force logistics.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他入伍当汽车兵，在空军后勤系统。

M1.en: At 17, he challenged rural soldiers to a wheat-cutting contest, refusing to give up despite bleeding hands.
M1.zh: 17岁时，他和农村兵比赛割麦，手磨出血也不服输。

M6.en: He chose mountaineering as a lifestyle, unable to stop.
M6.zh: 他选择登山作为一种生活方式，欲罢不能。

M3.en: He admired General Patton, a star from West Point.
M3.zh: 他崇拜巴顿将军，西点军校的璀璨将星。

D6.en: He organized a Marxist study group with local friends and later founded a Marxist research association.
D6.zh: 他组织了马列主义学习小组，后来建立了马列主义研究会。

D11.en: He withdrew from mass movements to study Marxism in his study.
D11.zh: 他从群众运动退回书斋，向马克思主义寻求真理。

D3.en: He formed a Marxist research association with several local friends.
D3.zh: 他约了几位同窗好友，组成了马列主义研究会。

D2.en: He studied and practiced hard for two to three years, becoming a popular teacher.
D2.zh: 他拼命学练，两三年后成了学生欢迎的教师。

D16.en: At 19, he climbed Taibai Rock with eight others and wrote a poem about its decay.
D16.zh: 19岁时，他与八人重登太白岩，作诗感叹破败。

D5.en: At 20, he braved the foggy Three Gorges for the first time on a boat to Wuhan.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他第一次闯雾峡，坐船出三峡去武汉。

D18.en: At 20, short of money for a boat ticket, he sold his bedding to a scrap station for 30 yuan.
D18.zh: 20岁时，他钱不够，把被盖当废品卖了30元买船票。

D9.en: He became the propaganda minister of the student union, organizing cultural and sports activities.
D9.zh: 他进校当上学生会宣传部长，组织文体活动和晚会。

D12.en: After his father died, he had to shoulder the family's livelihood.
D12.zh: 父亲去世后，他挑起一家生活的担子。

D7.en: He defied authority by choosing the latter, showing unconventional thinking.
D7.zh: 他毅然选择了后者，表现出与常人不同的思维。

D15.en: At 22, he struggled as a furnace worker, lifting heavy steel rods in extreme heat, sweating profusely but persevering.
D15.zh: 22岁时，他当炉前工，举着几十斤钢钎在三千多度高温下搅拌，汗如雨下，咬牙拼命干。

D4.en: In his 20s, he carried a large bag of beef home, feeling it was a great success.
D4.zh: 20多岁时，他扛着一大袋牛肉回家，觉得这是一次了不起的成功。

D1.en: At 20-22, he learned that even a low-paying job required skill, which spurred him to learn and practice relentlessly.
D1.zh: 20到22岁时，他明白了即使一天八角工钱的泥饭碗，没本领也端不稳，于是拼命学、拼命练。

D20.en: At 24, he was brought into the factory's service group and, somewhat confused, pushed into a leadership position.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他被拉进厂里的勤务组，在几分糊涂中被推上了掌权的宝座。

M4.en: At 24, he was passionate about using his pen to fight against capitalist-roaders.
M4.zh: 24岁时，他怀着一腔热血要用手中的笔和走资派血战到底。

M6.en: At 28, he wanted to understand the Cultural Revolution theoretically, so he started studying Marxist-Leninist works.
M6.zh: 28岁时，他想从理论上弄清文革，于是响应毛主席号召，开始关注地学习马列原著。

D10.en: At 24, he woke up, looked at his nearly disabled hand, and felt an inexplicable hatred.
D10.zh: 24岁时，他苏醒过来，望着差点残废的手，平添了一种莫名的仇恨。

D13b.en: At 22, he chose to be a furnace worker over a Chinese teacher, surprising everyone.
D13b.zh: 22岁时，他毅然选择了炉前工，而不是语文教师，让所有人意外。

M1.en: At 22, he believed that with his talent, he could stand out in the factory and maybe become a leader or even factory director.
M1.zh: 22岁时，他凭才华在工厂苦干几年容易脱颖而出，说不定会被选为干部，弄得好还当厂长。

M5.en: In his 20s, he wanted to buy something to bring home to prove he wasn't a failure and save face.
M5.zh: 20多岁时，他想买点东西带回家，表明自己不是失败者，捞回一点面子。

M7.en: At 24, he was eager to fight the capitalist-roaders with his pen.
M7.zh: 24岁时，他怀着一腔热血要用手中的笔和走资派血战到底。

D8.en: At 20, he struggled to maintain order in the classroom.
D8.zh: 他20岁时在课堂上很难管住学生。

D17.en: At 20-22, he volunteered for public work, not afraid of dirt or fatigue, and competed to carry manure.
D17.zh: 20到22岁间，他乐于公益活动，不怕脏和累，为公家种地抢着挑粪。

D20X.en: At 24-25, he experienced a process of self-negation and began a turning point in his life.
D20X.zh: 24到25岁间，他经历了一个对自己否定之否定的过程，人生之路开始转折了。

D19.en: At 25-27, he retreated from the noisy mass movement to his study to seek truth from Marxism.
D19.zh: 25到27岁间，他从喧闹的群众运动退回书斋，向马克思主义寻求真理。

M8.en: At 25-27, he retreated from the noisy mass movement to his study to seek truth from Marxism.
M8.zh: 25到27岁间，他从喧闹的群众运动退回书斋，向马克思主义寻求真理。

D12.en: He prided himself on being able to sweep, clean, and make beds as well as anyone.
D12.zh: 他自豪于自己扫地、打扫和铺床不输任何人。

D11.en: He imagined all kinds of situations and how he would handle them.
D11.zh: 他想象各种情况以及自己会如何应对。

D18.en: At 14, he opened the Ray Kroc Music Emporium.
D18.zh: 14岁时，他开了雷·克罗克音乐商店。

D15.en: He played grand neighborhood games in the alley behind his house.
D15.zh: 他在屋后小巷里玩盛大的邻里游戏。

D2.en: At 20, he sold paper cups by day and played piano for a radio station by night.
D2.zh: 20岁时，他白天卖纸杯，晚上为当地电台弹钢琴。

D16.en: He loved being the center of attention, persuading the audience his side was right.
D16.zh: 他喜欢成为焦点，说服观众自己是对的。

D5.en: At 15-16, he was eager to become an ambulance driver for the Red Cross in WWI.
D5.zh: 15到16岁时，他渴望在第一次世界大战中为红十字会开救护车。

D6.en: He partied and skipped classes in his fraternity, nearly becoming president.
D6.zh: 他在兄弟会里参加派对、逃课，差点当上会长。

D13a.en: At 21, he started selling Lily brand paper cups.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，他开始销售百合牌纸杯。

D1.en: He stuffed cash into paper bags and carried it home when his registers overflowed.
D1.zh: 他把塞满现金的纸袋带回家，因为收银机装不下了。

M1.en: In his 20s, he was driven by ambition and hated to be idle.
M1.zh: 20多岁时，他充满雄心，讨厌闲着。

D10.en: At 23, he was steaming mad.
D10.zh: 23岁时，他气得要命。

D4.en: In his late 20s, he sold soft drink cups to concessions.
D4.zh: 20多岁时，他向各场所销售软饮杯。

D3.en: He scouted a McDonald's for three hours, counting customers in the cold.
D3.zh: 他花了三小时侦察麦当劳，数着寒风中排队的顾客。

D13b.en: As a teenager, he dropped out of high school after his sophomore year.
D13b.zh: 十几岁时，他读完高二就退学了。

D12.en: As a small child, his father was a financial professional and his mother came from a wealthy family.
D12.zh: 幼年时，父亲从事金融，母亲出身大户。

D3.en: He was more interested in national affairs than in books.
D3.zh: 他对国家大事的兴趣比书本更大

D11.en: After reading Pu Yi's autobiography, he let his imagination run wild.
D11.zh: 看了溥仪的《我的前半生》后，他浮想联翩

D7.en: He was deemed unqualified because his uncle was a 'rightist'.
D7.zh: 他因舅舅是右派分子而被宣布为不合格

D2.en: At 17-18, he carried a manure bucket door to door during winter break to train his connection with workers and peasants.
D2.zh: 17到18岁时，他寒假背着粪桶挨家挨户淘粪，苦练工农感情。

D18.en: He used competing suppliers for computer parts and had them bid against each other.
D18.zh: 他利用相互竞争的供应商提供配件，让他们相互竞标

D1.en: He insisted there was still profit in the chassis and packaging foam when others said margins were squeezed.
D1.zh: 当别人说油水挤得差不多了，他坚持说还有机箱和包装箱里的泡沫塑料

D14.en: He hand-painted the words 'Lenovo E-Series Computer' on the packaging box.
D14.zh: 他亲手在包装箱上画出了'联想E系列电脑'那几个字

M4.en: He dreamed of making his microcomputers cheaper to meet the needs of ordinary Chinese.
M4.zh: 他怀抱着梦想：让自己的微机更便宜，迎合普通中国人的需求

M7.en: At 25, he aimed to meet ordinary Chinese people's needs.
M7.zh: 25岁时，他致力于满足普通中国人的需求。

D7.en: He defied his father and left the old system to make his own way.
D7.zh: 他为了跟父亲赌气，抛弃旧体制约束，要自己闯出一番天地。

D15.en: He broke his arm while working in the countryside at age 16.
D15.zh: 他16岁插队时摔断了胳膊。

D3.en: He provided equipment and wrote software programs to teach others.
D3.zh: 他提供大量设备，还编写软件程序教别人使用。

D18.en: He earned a high income as a manager at the agricultural academy at age 22.
D18.zh: 他22岁在农科院当经理时收入很高。

D9.en: At 24, his talent exchange company trained, educated, outsourced, and hired talent.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他的人才交流开发公司通过培训、教育、外包、外聘方式组织人才活动。

D5.en: He agreed to pay 3000 yuan to transfer someone, taking a risk.
D5.zh: 他一口答应由公司支付3000元也要把人调来。

D4.en: He worked as a porter at night, moving expensive goods upstairs.
D4.zh: 他半夜当搬运工，把贵重商品从一楼搬到三楼。

D1.en: After two failed party applications, he persisted and was accepted on the third try.
D1.zh: 他两次入党申请被拒后没退缩，第三次申请终于在1973年初被批准。

D10.en: His father's criticism left him speechless for a long time.
D10.zh: 父亲说他立功没什么可吹的，他呆立当场半天说不出话。

D13b.en: He did not apply for the Communist Youth League, focusing on academics instead.
D13b.zh: 他初中时没申请入团，因为心思在学好数理化上。

D6.en: He became close friends with several girls, who confided in him as a parental figure.
D6.zh: 他和几个女孩子成了好朋友，她们把他当家长，把不敢对家长说的话说给他听。

D12.en: At 16, he broke his arm while working in the countryside and recuperated for over 80 days.
D12.zh: 16岁时，他插队时摔断胳膊，休养了八十多天。

D2.en: After being promoted in the military, he saved dozens of yuan every month.
D2.zh: 在部队提干后，他每月固定存几十元。

D19.en: In the 1980s, he sorted coins by denomination, rolled them in paper strips, and counted daily income.
D19.zh: 80年代，他把硬币按面值分开，十个一叠用纸条卷好，捆起来数出总收入。

M4.en: At 18, he joined the army with the belief of serving and protecting the country.
M4.zh: 18岁时，他当兵是抱着保家卫国尽义务的理念。

M2.en: At 18, he joined the army with the belief of serving and protecting the country.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他当兵是抱着保家卫国尽义务的理念。

M6.en: At 18, he joined the army with the belief of serving and protecting the country.
M6.zh: 18岁时，他当兵是抱着保家卫国尽义务的理念。

M8.en: At 18, he joined the army with the belief of serving and protecting the country.
M8.zh: 18岁时，他当兵是抱着保家卫国尽义务的理念。

D13a.en: At 23-25, his father compared his own past rank to his achievement, leaving him speechless.
D13a.zh: 23到25岁间，他父亲拿自己当年的级别和他的功劳比，让他半天说不出话。

D20.en: He engaged in revolutionary propaganda, organized activities and armed fights, and published newspapers.
D20.zh: 他参与宣传革命、组织武斗、出版报刊，还刻蜡版、油印刷、撒传单、大游行。

M1.en: He was often the first to submit exams and won many championships.
M1.zh: 他几乎每场竞赛都最先交卷并取得好名次，拿过很多冠军。

D17.en: As a small child, his mother had swollen hands and feet from malnutrition.
D17.zh: 幼年时，他妈妈手脚浮肿，营养不良。

D11.en: His father bought many opera videotapes for self-entertainment when children were away.
D11.zh: 他父亲购买大量京剧录像带自娱自乐。

D14.en: His father was more fashionable than his mother and went to dance at unit or street cultural center parties.
D14.zh: 他父亲比母亲新潮，去单位或街道舞会跳舞。

M3.en: He regarded his father as a role model and hoped to forge his own path like the older revolutionaries.
M3.zh: 他把父亲当作榜样，希望能像老一辈革命家一样闯出自己的路。

D8.en: He embarrassed Deputy Commander Guo in front of the entire 8th Company.
D8.zh: 他当着八连全体官兵的面，让郭副团长很下不来台。

D16.en: He composed a poem inspired by the scene.
D16.zh: 他为此情此景赋诗一首。

D20X.en: He began to think about leaving the military.
D20X.zh: 他开始产生退伍的念头。

D3.en: At 16-17, he attended evening classes at the Chicago Academy of Fine Arts three times a week.
D3.zh: 16到17岁时，他每周三次去芝加哥美术学院上夜校。

D11.en: As a child and teenager, he was most interested in cartooning, though his drawings were uninspired.
D11.zh: 他从小到十几岁时最喜欢漫画，但画得并不出彩。

D4.en: At 4-8, he herded pigs and rode on their backs.
D4.zh: 4到8岁时，他放猪并骑在猪背上。

D17.en: At 25, he had his mother-in-law move in with them shortly after they took possession of the house in December.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他在12月搬进新家后不久就让岳母搬来同住。

D15.en: At 15-16, he was convinced to compete on the school's seventy-pound relay team and won in 1917.
D15.zh: 15到16岁时，他被说服参加学校70磅接力队并在1917年获胜。

D14.en: At 25, he made a point of buying an ice cream for the dog, standing on the curb and feeding it to her.
D14.zh: 25岁时，他特意给狗买冰淇淋，站在路边喂它。

D6.en: At 22, he rented 4651 Kingswell.
D6.zh: 22岁时，他租下了金斯韦尔4651号。

D10.en: He couldn't keep a straight face and left, clearly very upset.
D10.zh: 他绷不住脸，转身离开，显然非常难过。

D7.en: He complained to Mintz, starting an angry exchange of letters.
D7.zh: 他向明茨抱怨，引发了一场愤怒的信件往来。

D16.en: At 20-21, he was called 'quite a salesman' by Rudy Ising.
D16.zh: 20到21岁时，他被鲁迪·伊辛称为‘相当有推销能力’。

D1.en: He said, 'We need money.'
D1.zh: 他说：“我们需要钱。”

D18.en: He aged helmets with shellac, earning five francs each.
D18.zh: 他用虫胶做旧头盔，每个赚五法郎。

D13a.en: His mother forged his passport, and he altered his birthdate to 1900.
D13a.zh: 他母亲伪造了护照，他把出生日期改成1900年。

D20.en: He told his brother Roy the studio would be called Walt Disney Studio.
D20.zh: 他告诉哥哥罗伊，工作室将更名为华特迪士尼工作室。

M1.en: At 16-17, he attended evening classes at the Chicago Academy of Fine Arts three times a week.
M1.zh: 16到17岁时，他每周三次去芝加哥美术学院上夜校。

M4.en: At 20, he was too independent-minded to think of himself as someone else's employee for long.
M4.zh: 20岁时，他太独立了，不认为自己会长期给别人打工。

M5.en: At 21, he wanted to be inside the studios, directing movies.
M5.zh: 21岁时，他想进入电影公司内部执导电影。

M7.en: At 21, he wanted to be inside the studios, directing movies.
M7.zh: 21岁时，他想进入电影公司内部执导电影。

D8.en: At 18-19, he kept asking Cauger until he gave in.
D8.zh: 18到19岁时，他不断请求考格，直到对方让步。

D13b.en: At 20-21, he left home for good to work as a bank teller.
D13b.zh: 20到21岁时，他永久离开家，去当银行出纳员。

M2.en: At 21, he wanted to be inside the studios, directing movies.
M2.zh: 21岁时，他想进入电影公司内部执导电影。

M3.en: At 21, he rode the trolley to Culver City, gazed at Ben-Hur's set, and walked past Chaplin's studio hoping to glimpse his idol.
M3.zh: 21岁时，他坐电车去卡尔弗城，惊叹于《宾虚》的布景，又走过卓别林的工作室，希望能瞥见偶像。

M6.en: At 21, he wanted to be inside the studios, directing movies.
M6.zh: 21岁时，他想进入电影公司内部执导电影。

M8.en: At 21, he wanted to be inside the studios, directing movies.
M8.zh: 21岁时，他想进入电影公司内部执导电影。

D20X.en: At 23, when Mintz rejected his demand, he broke off negotiations.
D20X.zh: 23岁时，明茨拒绝了他的要求，他就中断了谈判。

D14.en: He was born on February 24, 1955, in San Francisco, California.
D14.zh: 他于1955年2月24日出生在加利福尼亚州旧金山。

D13b.en: He made sure his deal with Chrisann Brennan was signed before the IPO.
D13b.zh: 他确保他与克里斯安·布伦南的协议在IPO前签署。

D4.en: He dropped by Atari to consult with Alcorn, who knew old-fashioned electrical engineering.
D4.zh: 他顺道去雅达利咨询阿尔康，后者懂老式电气工程。

D7.en: He refused to go to the classes he was assigned and instead went to the ones he wanted.
D7.zh: 他拒绝上被分配的课程，而是去上他想上的课。

D9.en: He was the public face of the IPO and helped choose the two investment banks handling it.
D9.zh: 他是IPO的公众面孔，并帮助选择了处理IPO的两家投资银行。

D2.en: He started with two-day fasts and eventually tried to stretch them to a week or more.
D2.zh: 他从两天禁食开始，最终试图延长到一周或更久。

D3.en: He was already thinking about how he and Wozniak could profit from this new field.
D3.zh: 他已经在思考他和沃兹尼亚克如何从这个新领域获利。

D6.en: He shipped the product to thirty people for testing, mostly youngsters he met through chat rooms.
D6.zh: 他把产品寄给三十人测试，大多是他通过聊天室认识的年轻人。

D15.en: He switched from swimming team to water polo because swimming took too much time.
D15.zh: 他因为游泳队训练太耗时，转而参加水球。

D10.en: At 22-23, he relaxed by immersing his feet in the toilet bowl and flushing.
D10.zh: 22到23岁时，他把脚泡在马桶里冲水来放松。

D5.en: At 24, he said: 'I will let you invest a million dollars in Apple if you will open the kimono at Xerox PARC.'
D5.zh: 24岁时，他说：‘如果你愿意在施乐帕克敞开大门，我就让你投资苹果一百万美元。’

D8.en: At 25, he intentionally kept one of his oldest friends from receiving any stock.
D8.zh: 25岁时，他故意不让一位老朋友得到任何股票。

D18.en: At 17-18, he decided to sell his IBM Selectric typewriter to raise cash.
D18.zh: 17到18岁时，他决定卖掉IBM Selectric打字机来换钱。

D13a.en: At 17, he dropped out of school and got a tuition refund, yet remained on campus.
D13a.zh: 17岁时，他退学并拿回了学费，但还留在校园里。

D1.en: At 18, he bought a programming book on Amazon and charged ahead, captivated by the project.
D1.zh: 18岁时，他在亚马逊买了一本编程书，被项目吸引，继续前进。

D20.en: At 21, Wozniak signed a document giving him and Steve Jobs equal shares in the company.
D20.zh: 21岁时，沃兹尼亚克签署文件，让他和史蒂夫·乔布斯在公司拥有平等股份。

M1.en: At 24, he projected the feeling that he would succeed with or without us and we'd better get on board.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他表现出无论有没有我们他都会成功的感觉，我们最好加入。

M4.en: At 23, he still considered drugs an essential element in gaining perspective.
M4.zh: 23岁时，他仍然认为毒品是获得洞察力的关键因素。

D11.en: At 16-17, he created light shows for parties by bouncing lasers off mirrors attached to his stereo speakers.
D11.zh: 16到17岁时，他用立体声音箱上的镜子反射激光，为派对制作灯光秀。

D17.en: At 19, he covered a friend's food expenses and bus ticket back to Delhi.
D17.zh: 19岁时，他为一个朋友支付了食物费用和回德里的巴士票。

D19.en: At 24, he bought a house in Los Gatos hills but left it mostly barren due to obsessive furnishing choices.
D19.zh: 24岁时，他在洛斯加托斯山买了一栋房子，但因为对家具过于挑剔，房子里基本空荡荡的。

D18.en: At 10, he signed a contract with his sister Kristi.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他和姐姐克里斯蒂签了一份合同。

D9.en: At 19, he stopped attending classes and gave up poker.
D9.zh: 19岁时，他不再上课，甚至放弃了心爱的扑克。

D7.en: As a teenager, he snuck out to take a bus back to C-Cubed for more programming.
D7.zh: 十几岁时，他偷偷溜出家，坐十分钟公交回C-Cubed继续编程。

D1.en: At 17, after Evans died, he asked Allen to help with the class scheduling project.
D1.zh: 17岁时，埃文斯去世后，他请艾伦帮忙做课程安排项目。

M1.en: At 9-10, he said: 'I want to get back to division because last year I was the best.'
M1.zh: 9到10岁时，他说：“我想回到除法，因为去年我是最好的。”

D6.en: At 18, he played bridge, chess, and argued in dorms till late.
D6.zh: 18岁时，他打桥牌、下棋，在宿舍争论到深夜。

D15.en: At 17-18, he water-skied gracefully and fearlessly on one ski.
D15.zh: 17到18岁时，他单脚滑水，动作优雅且无畏。

D2.en: At 19, he stopped attending classes and gave up poker.
D2.zh: 19岁时，他不再上课，甚至放弃了心爱的扑克。

D5.en: At 22, he worked on the 8086 project on spec to get ahead of the hardware curve.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他冒险做8086项目，试图领先硬件发展。

D13a.en: He returned to the Aiken Computer Center while Allen went back to Honeywell.
D13a.zh: 他回到了艾肯计算机中心，而艾伦回到了霍尼韦尔工作。

D12.en: He spent three weeks as a legislative page in Olympia, Washington.
D12.zh: 他在华盛顿州奥林匹亚市当了三个星期的立法会议服务员。

D10.en: He suffered a severe case of ulcerative colitis and was hospitalized for over a week.
D10.zh: 他得了严重的溃疡性结肠炎，住院超过一周。

D20.en: His confrontational management style helped Microsoft maintain its edge.
D20.zh: 他那种对抗式的管理风格帮助微软保持了优势。

D16.en: At 21-22, he expanded on features, advantages, and value line by line.
D16.zh: 21到22岁时，他逐行阐述功能、优势和价值。

D19.en: He and two others spent most of the last year documenting, improving, and adding features to BASIC.
D19.zh: 他和另外两人去年大部分时间都在记录、改进和增加BASIC的功能。

D13b.en: He drew up a written agreement to formalize the entity over Christmas vacation.
D13b.zh: 他在圣诞假期起草了一份书面协议来正式化这个实体。

D14.en: He landed leading roles in two school plays: The Night the Bed Fell and Black Comedy.
D14.zh: 他在两部校园剧中担任主角：《床塌之夜》和《黑色喜剧》。

D8.en: He can be extremely vocal and persuasive in arguing one side of an issue.
D8.zh: 他在争论问题时可以非常直言不讳且具有说服力。

M5.en: He wrote a letter to gain fame and notoriety in the microcomputer community.
M5.zh: 他写了一封信，旨在在微机社区中获得名声和关注。

M4.en: He used a confrontational management style to maintain Microsoft's edge.
M4.zh: 他用对抗性的管理风格来保持微软的优势。

D11.en: At age 7-8, he read the entire encyclopedia from beginning to end.
D11.zh: 7到8岁时，他把百科全书从头到尾读了一遍。

D20X.en: At age 20, Gates noted MITS hadn't licensed Altair BASIC to others.
D20X.zh: 20岁时，盖茨指出MITS未将Altair BASIC授权给他人。

D17.en: He spent most of the last year documenting, improving, and adding features to BASIC with two others.
D17.zh: 他和另外两人花了去年大部分时间记录、改进并增加BASIC的功能。

M3.en: He wrote a letter to gain fame and notoriety in the microcomputer community.
M3.zh: 他写了一封信，旨在在微机社区中获得名声和关注。

M6.en: He wanted to hire ten programmers and deluge the hobby market with good software.
M6.zh: 他想雇佣十个程序员，用优质软件淹没业余市场。

M7.en: He wanted to hire ten programmers and deluge the hobby market with good software.
M7.zh: 他想雇佣十个程序员，用优质软件淹没业余市场。

M8.en: He wrote a letter to gain fame and notoriety in the microcomputer community.
M8.zh: 他写了一封信，旨在在微机社区中获得名声和关注。

D3.en: At 13, he saw a self-powered steam engine on a trip to Detroit.
D3.zh: 13岁时，他在去底特律的路上看到了一台自行驱动的蒸汽机。

D2.en: At 7, he started attending the one-room Scotch Settlement School.
D2.zh: 7岁时，他开始去只有一间教室的苏格兰移民学校上学。

D6.en: At 17, he went to a Ford banquet with about fifty people.
D6.zh: 17岁时，他参加了一个福特宴会，大约有五十人。

D12.en: At 12, he sometimes came after school to stamp letters.
D12.zh: 12岁时，他有时放学后来盖邮戳。

D7.en: He flung his office keys at the tycoon and resigned.
D7.zh: 他把办公室钥匙扔向大亨并辞职。

D18.en: At 17, he worked as a news butcher on the Erie and Huron Railroad.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他在伊利和休伦铁路上当报童。

D13a.en: At 19, he decided to skip college and join his father's business; he later regretted it.
D13a.zh: 19岁时，他决定不上大学，加入父亲的公司；后来他后悔了。

D15.en: He learned to skate as a teenager and became quite proficient.
D15.zh: 他十几岁时学会了滑冰，并且变得相当熟练。

M1.en: He saw the Challenge Cup as a chance to overtake his rival.
M1.zh: 他把挑战杯视为超越对手的机会。

M4.en: He heard about wonders in Philadelphia and resolved to leave the farm.
M4.zh: 他听说费城的奇迹后，决心离开农场。

D1.en: He worked for half an hour to clear a trough for a heavy unit.
D1.zh: 他工作了半小时，清出一条沟槽让重物滑下。

D10.en: He wrote to Clara about the pleasure of finding someone loving and kind.
D10.zh: 他写信给克拉拉，表达找到如此温柔善良之人的喜悦。

D14.en: He sat out two dances with a woman and showed her a watch he made.
D14.zh: 他和一位女士坐了两支舞，并给她看他做的手表。

D5.en: He was seriously injured when a runaway horse collapsed his wagon.
D5.zh: 他因马受惊拉垮马车而受重伤。

D20X.en: He ended his business relationship with Cooper by selling his interest in the race cars.
D20X.zh: 他卖掉了赛车的股份，结束了与库珀的合作关系。

D8.en: He spoke bluntly to coworkers and customers.
D8.zh: 他对同事和顾客说话直率。

D18.en: He dabbled in selling Easter eggs door-to-door in the neighborhood.
D18.zh: 他曾在邻里间尝试挨家挨户卖复活节彩蛋。

D11.en: At 13, he taught himself to code and wrote the video game Blastar.
D11.zh: 他13岁时自学编程，编写了游戏《Blastar》。

D14.en: At 12-13, his game coverage in a magazine earned him $500.
D14.zh: 他12到13岁时，杂志报道他的游戏让他赚了500美元。

D3.en: At 23, he wrote a paper jubilant about ultracapacitors for energy storage.
D3.zh: 23岁时，他写了一篇关于超级电容器的论文，对此感到欣喜。

D6.en: At 25, he formed a video-game team to compete in Quake tournaments.
D6.zh: 他25岁时组建了一支电玩队伍参加雷神之锤比赛。

D4.en: At 24, he wrote a program merging maps with business listings.
D4.zh: 他24岁时写了一个程序，将地图与商家列表合并。

D5.en: At 8, he was determined to get a motorcycle.
D5.zh: 他8岁时决心要一辆摩托车。

D9.en: At 24, he talked his brother into moving to Silicon Valley to conquer the Web.
D9.zh: 他24岁时说服弟弟搬到硅谷，一起征服网络。

D1.en: From 19 to 21, he studied business, competed in speaking contests, and showed intensity.
D1.zh: 他19到21岁间学习商业，参加演讲比赛，展现出强烈的好胜心。

D15.en: He won a local skeet-shooting contest but was too young to accept the whiskey prize.
D15.zh: 他赢得了当地飞碟射击比赛，但因年龄太小无法领取威士忌奖品。

D16.en: He gained attention for railing against fossil fuels in favor of solar power.
D16.zh: 他因抨击化石燃料、支持太阳能而受到关注。

M6.en: He started fantasizing about colonizing other planets in high school.
M6.zh: 他在高中时开始幻想殖民其他星球。

D20.en: He and Kimbal were chatting during a class break when Wood interrupted them.
D20.zh: 他和金巴尔课间聊天时被伍德打断。

D13b.en: He wanted to avoid joining the military despite South Africa's required service.
D13b.zh: 尽管南非要求服兵役，他想避免参军。

D8.en: He talked his way into getting back two lost points and got a hundred.
D8.zh: 他说服教授要回了两分，得了满分。

M1.en: He would stand next to his father's chair and make his case, over and over.
M1.zh: 他站在父亲椅子旁，一遍又一遍地陈述自己的理由。

D7.en: Neither of them liked following rules, so they rented a house in a sketchy part of West Philadelphia.
D7.zh: 他们俩都不喜欢守规矩，于是在西费城一个不太安全的区域租了房子。

D12.en: At 17, he did chores on a Saskatchewan farm.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他在萨斯喀彻温省的农场做杂务。

D10.en: He was so angry that he started hitting them.
D10.zh: 他气得动手打他们。

D18.en: At 17, he started his career with sixty dollars.
D18.zh: 17岁时，他用60美元开始了职业生涯。

D5.en: At 24, he became obsessed with eliminating R.F.K., saying he must be killed.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他执意要除掉R.F.K.，说他必须被杀死。

D2.en: At 17, he took an intensive three-week course on telephone company operations.
D2.zh: 17岁时，他参加了为期三周的密集电话公司内部课程。

D4.en: At 17, he signed on as a deckhand.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他签约当了甲板水手。

D9.en: He assumed the duties of this position with seriousness and dedication.
D9.zh: 他认真负责地承担了这个职位的职责。

D3.en: At 17, he worked on his Spanish, read newspapers, and listened in on business calls.
D3.zh: 17岁时，他学习西班牙语、读报纸，并偷听商业电话。

D6.en: At 11-13, he often found time to meet a favorite, buy her a sweet, and perhaps walk her home.
D6.zh: 11到13岁时，他常抽空见一个喜欢的人，给她买糖，可能还送她回家。

D13b.en: At 25, after law school, he took a job in the Criminal Division of the Department of Justice.
D13b.zh: 25岁时，法学院毕业后，他在司法部刑事司找到一份工作。

D13a.en: At 15-17, he was banned from car or boat rides and faced stern discipline.
D13a.zh: 15到17岁时，他被禁止乘车或乘船，并受到严厉管教。

D15.en: At 20-25, he earned a pilot's license and flew his own planes.
D15.zh: 20到25岁时，他获得了飞行员执照，驾驶自己的飞机。

D12.en: At 17, he accompanied his sisters to school and brought them home daily.
D12.zh: 17岁时，他每天陪妹妹上学并接她们回家。

D14.en: At 17, he danced with Callas to rhythms he mastered in Argentina.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他和卡拉斯跳舞，用的是他在阿根廷学会的节奏。

D1.en: At 16-17, he tried to get his father released from prison.
D1.zh: 16到17岁时，他试图让父亲出狱。

M1.en: At 18, he was determined to build a business as successful as his father's.
M1.zh: 18岁时，他决心建立和父亲一样成功的生意。

M4.en: Before 24, he expressed fierce Arab nationalism and hatred of Zionists.
M4.zh: 24岁前，他表现出强烈的阿拉伯民族主义，憎恨犹太复国主义者。

M7.en: At 16-17, he worked to reunite his family in mainland Greece.
M7.zh: 16到17岁时，他努力让家人在希腊本土团聚。

M2.en: At 18, he was determined to build a business as successful as his father's.
M2.zh: 18岁时，他决心建立和父亲一样成功的生意。

D19.en: At 18-20, he cautiously maintained his inexpensive lodgings.
D19.zh: 18到20岁时，他小心地维持着他那廉价的住处。

D4.en: As a child, he dismantled an electric bell and rigged a telegraph to a friend's house.
D4.zh: 他小时候拆了一个电铃，给邻居朋友家装了电报系统。

D3.en: As a child, he dismantled an electric bell and rigged a telegraph to a friend's house.
D3.zh: 他小时候拆了一个电铃，给邻居朋友家装了电报系统。

D5.en: At 22-24, he failed the employment exam for Tokyo-Shibaura Electric Company.
D5.zh: 22到24岁间，他没能通过东京芝浦电气公司的录用考试。

D9.en: He got two companies to join under him to form a network competing with Yamaha, later Sony Sales.
D9.zh: 他让两家公司加入他的旗下，组成一个与雅马哈竞争的网络，后来成为索尼销售公司。

D6.en: At 23-24, he met Ibuka and later visited him in Nagano to study thermal-guidance and noctovision devices.
D6.zh: 他23到24岁时遇到井深，后来去长野拜访他，研究热导系统和夜视设备。

D13a.en: At 24, he decided to teach but agreed to work part time in Ibuka's shop.
D13a.zh: 他24岁时决定教书，但同意在井深的店里兼职。

D7.en: He, a former naval lieutenant, was forced to leave the classroom.
D7.zh: 他，一名前海军中尉，被迫离开教室。

D20.en: He co-founded Tokyo Telecommunications Engineering Co., Ltd. in 1946.
D20.zh: 1946年，他共同创立了东京通信工业株式会社。

D2.en: He put them all in a hotel room for ten straight days, working on two problems.
D2.zh: 他把所有人都关在酒店房间里整整十天，解决两个问题。

D11.en: At 17, he played Al Jolson records day and night.
D11.zh: 17岁时，他日夜播放阿尔·乔尔森的唱片。

D3.en: At 20, he wrote a letter to Barron's that was published.
D3.zh: 20岁时，他给《巴伦周刊》写了一封信并发表。

D12.en: He was mentioned in Frank D. Buffett's will filed in 1949.
D12.zh: 1949年，他被列入弗兰克·D·巴菲特的遗嘱。

D18.en: At 12-14, he swept floors and delivered groceries part-time in summer.
D18.zh: 12到14岁时，他暑假兼职扫地、送杂货。

D2.en: At 19-20, he wrote a letter to his father Howard Buffett.
D2.zh: 19到20岁时，他给父亲霍华德·巴菲特写了一封信。

D16.en: At 20, he wrote a letter to Barron's that was published.
D16.zh: 20岁时，他给《巴伦周刊》写了一封信并发表。

D5.en: At 22, he flew to Cleveland for an annual meeting of Cleveland's Worst Mill.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他飞往克利夫兰参加克利夫兰最差工厂的年会。

D7.en: At 17-18, he criticized the Marshall Plan.
D7.zh: 17到18岁时，他抨击马歇尔计划。

D20.en: In his partnerships, he was always the senior partner.
D20.zh: 在他的合伙企业中，他一直是高级合伙人。

D6.en: At 10-11, he enjoyed being vied over by his uncles.
D6.zh: 10到11岁时，他喜欢被叔叔们争抢。

D1.en: At 21-24, he pestered Ben Graham for a job, even offering to work for free.
D1.zh: 21到24岁时，他缠着本·格雷厄姆要工作，甚至提出免费干活。

D14.en: At 17, he wore a raccoon fur coat to high school football games for fun.
D14.zh: 17岁时，他穿着浣熊毛大衣去高中橄榄球赛，就为了好玩。

D13a.en: At 13, he moved back to Omaha to live with his grandfather and aunt.
D13a.zh: 13岁时，他搬回奥马哈，和祖父及姑姑同住。

D19.en: At 25, he ran his partnership from his bedroom sun porch to avoid rent.
D19.zh: 25岁时，他在卧室外的阳光房经营合伙公司，省下租金。

D13b.en: At 18-20, he studied photography, physics, and music at Stanford for two years.
D13b.zh: 18到20岁时，他在斯坦福学了两年摄影、物理和音乐。

D15.en: At 8, he took one boxing lesson and never put the gloves on again.
D15.zh: 8岁时，他上了一节拳击课，再也没戴过手套。

D17.en: At 7-9, he rescued his sister's doll from the wastebasket and returned it.
D17.zh: 7到9岁时，他从废纸篓里救出妹妹的娃娃并还给她。

M3.en: At 10, he knew he was in the presence of a big shot in Sidney Weinberg's office.
M3.zh: 10岁时，他在西德尼·温伯格的办公室里意识到自己面对的是个大人物。

M2.en: At 20, he asked, 'What am I going to tell my dad?'
M2.zh: 20岁时，他问：‘我该怎么跟我爸说？’

D20X.en: At 25, he turned the offer down.
D20X.zh: 25岁时，他拒绝了那个提议。

M7.en: At 17-18, he lashed the Marshall Plan.
M7.zh: 17到18岁时，他抨击马歇尔计划。

D12.en: He grew up in a subsistence economy where food and clothing were critical.
D12.zh: 他在自给自足的经济中长大，食物和衣服至关重要。

D3.en: He tinkered with abandoned car parts and built a racing model.
D3.zh: 他摆弄废弃的汽车零件，组装了一辆赛车模型。

D2.en: After six months of baby-sitting, he was ordered to assist with auto repairs.
D2.zh: 当了六个月保姆后，他被命令协助汽车修理。

D13b.en: At 15, he went to Tokyo to apprentice at Art Shokai.
D13b.zh: 15岁时，他去东京在亚图商会当学徒。

D7.en: He decided to douse tax officials with a fire engine hose.
D7.zh: 他决定用消防水带浇税务官员。

D18.en: At 24, he found a job with a small steel commission merchant.
D18.zh: 24岁时，他在一家小型钢铁佣金商那里找到了工作。

D1.en: He was miserable but stayed because he was ashamed to face his parents.
D1.zh: 他很痛苦，但因为羞于面对父母而留了下来。

D4.en: He tinkered with abandoned car parts and built a racing model.
D4.zh: 他摆弄废弃的汽车零件，组装了一辆赛车模型。

D14.en: At 8, he made aviation goggles from cardboard and bamboo propellers, terrorizing the village.
D14.zh: 8岁时，他用纸板做航空护目镜，在自行车前装竹螺旋桨，在村里横冲直撞。

D5.en: Drunk, he drove his car off a bridge into a river.
D5.zh: 他喝醉了，把车从桥上开进了河里。

D10.en: At 22-25, he flew into a rage, picked her up, and threw her out of the window.
D10.zh: 22到25岁间，他勃然大怒，把她抱起来扔出了窗外。

D15.en: At 22-25, he made himself a speedboat which he raced on Lake Hamana.
D15.zh: 22到25岁间，他给自己造了一艘快艇，在滨名湖上比赛。

D13a.en: At 18, he agreed to stay on another year as an apprentice.
D13a.zh: 18岁时，他同意再当一年学徒。

D9.en: At 25, he was employing fifty men, one of the youngest entrepreneurs about.
D9.zh: 25岁时，他雇了五十个人，是当时最年轻的企业家之一。

M1.en: At 22-25, he won first place from time to time.
M1.zh: 22到25岁间，他时不时赢得第一名。

D12.en: At age nine, he was sent to Osaka to work 16 hours a day and live with his employer.
D12.zh: 9岁时，他被送到大阪每天工作16小时，并住在雇主家。

D18.en: At age nine, he was sent to Osaka to work 16 hours a day and live with his employer.
D18.zh: 9岁时，他被送到大阪每天工作16小时，并住在雇主家。

D10.en: At 12-13, he begged Godai for a 10% discount, breaking into tears in his eagerness.
D10.zh: 12到13岁时，他哭着恳求五代给他打九折。

D14.en: At age nine, he felt immense satisfaction hearing Mr. Murakami's praise when he would win.
D14.zh: 9岁时，他听到村上先生的表扬时感到极大的满足。

D2.en: He worked eighteen hours a day, seven days a week with Toshio and Mumeno.
D2.zh: 他和俊雄、武野一起每天工作18小时，每周工作7天。

D1.en: At 22, he worked long hours seven days a week to overcome lack of technological know-how.
D1.zh: 22岁时，他每周工作七天，每天长时间工作以克服技术不足。

D15.en: At 19, he coughed up blood coming home from an installation at a bathing place in Hamadera, Osaka.
D15.zh: 19岁时，他从大阪滨寺的一个浴场安装工作回家时咳血。

D13a.en: At 15, he sent himself a fake telegram saying his mother was ill and he was needed at home.
D13a.zh: 15岁时，他给自己发了一封假电报，说母亲生病需要他回家。

D7.en: At 15, he decided to break out of a relationship that had spanned nearly half his life.
D7.zh: 15岁时，他决定摆脱一段占据他近半生的关系。

D4.en: At 23, he tried to improve his attachment plug and invent more products.
D4.zh: 23岁时，他尝试改进自己的插头并发明更多产品。

D9.en: At 24, he introduced basic specialization to boost productivity and cut costs.
D9.zh: 24岁时，他引入基本分工以提高生产力并降低成本。

D13b.en: At 20, he agreed to marry and the wedding was held on September 4, 1915.
D13b.zh: 20岁时，他同意结婚，婚礼于1915年9月4日举行。

M7.en: His boarding house became the headquarters of the Junagadh Students' League.
M7.zh: 他住的宿舍成了朱纳加德学生联盟的总部。

D6.en: He found accommodation in a caste-funded boarding house.
D6.zh: 他在一个种姓资助的宿舍找到了住处。

D3.en: At school, he was infected by the independence mood.
D3.zh: 在学校，他受到了独立氛围的感染。

D13b.en: He was taken back by his natural parents against his will.
D13b.zh: 他被亲生父母强行带回。

D12.en: At age five, he was given in adoption to the childless Raja of Manda.
D12.zh: 五岁时，他被过继给没有孩子的曼达王公。

D8.en: At 16, he argued with the police commissioner to defend Shah.
D8.zh: 16岁时，他与警察局长争论为沙阿辩护。

D18.en: He and Chambaklal called their new business Reliance Commercial Corporation.
D18.zh: 他和钱巴克拉尔将新公司命名为信实商业公司。

D2.en: He won entrance to a prestigious school and immersed himself in it.
D2.zh: 他考入了名校并全身心投入其中。

D15.en: He took a bet that he could dive off and swim to shore.
D15.zh: 他打赌自己能跳下水游到岸边。

D1.en: He suffered a financial squeeze and had to borrow from colleagues.
D1.zh: 他遭遇财务困难，不得不向同事借钱。

D13a.en: He pulled out of the graduate program six months short and returned to Mumbai.
D13a.zh: 他在毕业前六个月退出研究生项目，返回孟买。

M1.en: He won entrance to a prestigious school and immersed himself in it.
M1.zh: 他考入了名校并全身心投入其中。

D4.en: He bought rials, melted them down, and sold the silver ingots.
D4.zh: 他购买里亚尔，熔化后出售银锭。

M2.en: At 24, he left graduate school six months short of completion to return to Mumbai.
M2.zh: 24岁时，他差半年没读完研究生就回到孟买。

D15.en: At 16, he rode competitively.
D15.zh: 16岁时，他参加马术比赛。

D2.en: At 19, he focused solely on passing exams for Ecole Polytechnique.
D2.zh: 19岁时，他一心只想通过考试进入巴黎综合理工学院。

D3.en: As a small child, he dreamed of a family of soldiers and adventurers.
D3.zh: 幼年时，他梦想着一个军人冒险家家族。

D6.en: In his 20s, he headed to the US, a country he loved from holidays.
D6.zh: 20多岁时，他前往美国，一个他度假时爱上的国家。

D11.en: As a small child, he dreamed of a family of soldiers and adventurers.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他梦想着一个军人冒险家家族。

M1.en: At 6-11, he was an excellent pupil, top in all subjects including PE.
M1.zh: 6到11岁时，他是个优秀学生，所有科目包括体育都名列前茅。

M6.en: As a small child, he dreamed of a family of soldiers and adventurers.
M6.zh: 幼年时，他梦想着一个军人冒险家家族。

D20.en: At 24, he became head of Ferret-Savinel and set up Férinel.
D20.zh: 24岁时，他成为Ferret-Savinel的负责人，并成立了Férinel。

D13b.en: At about 18-20, he was a son at Ecole Polytechnique.
D13b.zh: 18到20岁左右，他是巴黎综合理工学院的学生。

D1.en: At 19, he focused solely on passing exams for Ecole Polytechnique.
D1.zh: 19岁时，他一心只想通过考试进入巴黎综合理工学院。

D3.en: At 18-21, he took courses at the mathematics school in the Faculty of Sciences.
D3.zh: 他18到21岁间在理学院数学系上过课。

D4.en: At 10, he accompanied his father to the historical center to help with work and learn the ropes.
D4.zh: 他10岁时跟父亲去历史中心帮忙干活、学门道。

D13a.en: At 25, he married Soumaya Domit Gemayel on April 28, 1966, with no party due to her father's death a month earlier.
D13a.zh: 他25岁时于1966年4月28日与苏玛雅·多米特·杰马耶尔结婚，没有派对，因为她父亲一个月前去世了。

D18.en: At 12, he was buying stock in the National Bank of Mexico.
D18.zh: 他12岁时就在买墨西哥国家银行的股票。

D20.en: At 16-17, he was asked to administer family matters.
D20.zh: 他16到17岁时被要求管理家庭事务。

D6.en: His sisters appeared in Lebanese socialite magazines.
D6.zh: 他的姐妹们出现在黎巴嫩社交杂志上。

D7.en: At 11, he stood up and hit him.
D7.zh: 11岁时，他站起来打了他一下。
