D2.en: At 22, he began a four-month course and completed it with a grade of 9.8/10.
D2.zh: 他22岁时开始一个四个月的课程，并以9.8/10的成绩完成。

D19.en: At 15, he made his first balance: 40 shares of Banamex worth 6,600 pesos.
D19.zh: 他15岁时做了第一份资产负债表：40股Banamex股票，价值6600比索。

D15.en: At 20, he faced a bull that charged at him three times without touching him.
D15.zh: 20岁时，他面对一头公牛，公牛向他冲了三次都没碰到他。

D13b.en: At 17, he enrolled in the Faculty of Civil Engineering in 1957.
D13b.zh: 17岁时，他于1957年进入土木工程学院。

M1.en: At 15-16, he had duplicated his capital by May.
M1.zh: 15到16岁时，他到五月时已经使本金翻倍。

D5.en: At 20, he faced a bull that charged at him three times without touching him.
D5.zh: 20岁时，他面对一头公牛，公牛向他冲了三次都没碰到他。

D13b.en: She attended P.S. 14 from kindergarten in 1913 to 8th grade in 1921.
D13b.zh: 她1913年上幼儿园，1921年从P.S. 14小学毕业。

D2.en: At 16-25, she peddled beauty products and learned hands-on facial massage from him.
D2.zh: 16到25岁间，她推销美容产品并向他学习手部面部按摩技巧。

D14.en: As a child, she loved making people beautiful, brushing her mother's hair and combing her sister-in-law's hair.
D14.zh: 小时候，她喜欢让人变美，给妈妈梳头，给嫂子梳头。

D20.en: As a teenager, she served as publicity director for several school clubs.
D20.zh: 十几岁时，她担任了几个学校俱乐部的宣传主任。

D4.en: In May 1929, at 24, he went to London to buy a plane after getting his flying license.
D4.zh: 1929年5月，24岁的他拿到飞行执照后去伦敦买飞机。

D3.en: Before 1929, as a young man, he read books on aviation and was fascinated by Aero-Postale.
D3.zh: 1929年前，他读过几本航空书，被法国航空公司的故事迷住。

D18.en: At 21, in 1925, he persuaded his father to invest in a blue Bugatti.
D18.zh: 1925年21岁时，他说服父亲投资一辆蓝色布加迪。

D13b.en: At 20, in 1924, as a French citizen, he had to enlist in the French Army for at least a year.
D13b.zh: 1924年20岁时，作为法国公民，他必须参军至少一年。

D6.en: As a small child, he watched carriages roll by at the Café de la Paix.
D6.zh: 幼年时，他在和平咖啡馆看着马车驶过。

D2.en: In his late teens, his first poems were mostly in French, featuring Baudelaire and others.
D2.zh: 十几岁时，他的早期诗作大多是法语，以波德莱尔等人为主。

D14.en: Between 15 and 20, he took pleasure in writing and may have shown talent.
D14.zh: 15到20岁间，他喜欢写作，可能显露了一些才华。

M2.en: He spent his post-war years in France with his mother due to his father's decision.
M2.zh: 他因父亲的决定，战后在法国陪伴母亲。

D5.en: He saw a prize announcement for an England-India flight in the London Times.
D5.zh: 他在《伦敦泰晤士报》上看到一则英格兰-印度飞行比赛的公告。

D11.en: He complained to his mother that if she married earlier, he could have been a fighter pilot.
D11.zh: 他向母亲抱怨，如果她早结婚五六年，他就能当战斗机飞行员了。

D15.en: He was very interested in sports like hockey and football.
D15.zh: 他对曲棍球、足球等各种运动非常感兴趣。

D10.en: He glared at men who stared at his mother, feeling they insulted her.
D10.zh: 他怒视那些盯着他母亲看的男人，觉得他们在侮辱她。

D13a.en: He had hoped to return to Cambridge after a spell in India, but now the die was cast.
D13a.zh: 他原本希望先在印度待一段时间再回剑桥，但现在木已成舟。

M1.en: He was keen to study Engineering at Gonville and Caius College, Cambridge.
M1.zh: 他渴望在剑桥的冈维尔与凯斯学院学习工程学。

D9.en: His father suggested he spend a year in Jamshedpur to learn the steel company.
D9.zh: 他父亲建议他在贾姆谢德布尔待一年，了解钢铁公司的运作。

D1.en: He saw a prize announcement for an England-India flight in the London Times.
D1.zh: 他在《伦敦泰晤士报》上看到一则关于英印飞行比赛的公告。

D12.en: At age 2, her father fell ill with tuberculosis.
D12.zh: 2岁时，她父亲患上了肺结核。

D2.en: After school, she cleaned the house before doing homework.
D2.zh: 放学后，她先打扫房子再做作业。

M1.en: She worked hard to get straight A's or sell the most Girl Scout cookies.
M1.zh: 她努力拿全A或卖最多的女童子军饼干。

D14.en: In junior high, she won the trophy for best typist.
D14.zh: 初中时，她赢得了最佳打字员奖杯。

D6.en: Two school friends, including Tillie Bass, shaped her life.
D6.zh: 两个校友，包括蒂莉·巴斯，影响了她的生活。

D5.en: At age 17, she got married.
D5.zh: 17岁时，她结婚了。

D9.en: She called parents of Sunday School kids and sold all ten sets in a day and a half.
D9.zh: 她打电话给主日学孩子的家长，一天半卖掉了十套。

D1.en: At the next convention, she was crowned Queen of Sales.
D1.zh: 下一次大会上，她被加冕为销售女王。

D4.en: At 17, he sat behind the wheel of a newly finished car in the factory yard.
D4.zh: 17岁时，他坐在工厂院子里刚完工的汽车驾驶座上。

D15.en: At 16-17, he first fell in love with motor racing and speed thrills.
D15.zh: 16到17岁时，他首次爱上赛车和速度刺激。

D9.en: At 23, he persuaded Ugo Sivocci to leave CMN and join Alfa's test department.
D9.zh: 23岁时，他说服乌戈·西沃奇离开CMN加入阿尔法测试部。

D5.en: At age 22, he drove a prewar Alfa Romeo Tipo 40-60 at the Targa Florio on October 20, 1920.
D5.zh: 22岁时，他于1920年10月20日驾驶战前阿尔法·罗密欧Tipo 40-60参加塔加·弗洛里奥比赛。

D7.en: He betrayed Alfa Romeo, but it didn't improve his 1922 racing record.
D7.zh: 他背叛了阿尔法·罗密欧，但这并没有改善他1922年的比赛记录。

D8.en: He dismounted and chased down Florio to complain about unfair treatment, leading to a shouting match.
D8.zh: 他下车追赶弗洛里奥，抱怨受到不公平对待，引发了一场争吵。

D18.en: At 24, he returned to Modena and founded Carrozzeria Emilia, Enzo Ferrari & Company in a rented garage on Via Emilia.
D18.zh: 24岁时，他回到摩德纳，在艾米利亚大道租了一个小车库，创办了艾米利亚车身厂、恩佐·法拉利公司。

M1.en: He and Ramponi won the Circuito del Savio at Ravenna, a 25-lap, 359-kilometer race.
M1.zh: 他和兰波尼赢得了拉文纳的萨维奥赛道比赛，共25圈359公里。

D13b.en: He and Laura went to Turin and got married on April 28, 1923.
D13b.zh: 他和劳拉去了都灵，并于1923年4月28日结婚。

D14.en: His name appeared in La Gazzetta dello Sport for the first time.
D14.zh: 他的名字首次出现在《米兰体育报》上。

D16.en: At 16, he began to dedicate himself to semi-professional journalism.
D16.zh: 16岁时，他开始投身半职业新闻工作。

M3.en: He was captivated by Luciano Zuccoli's dual careers as journalist and author.
M3.zh: 他被卢恰诺·祖科利的记者和作家双重职业所吸引。

D19.en: He kept a journal recording every move, meeting, activity, and name of people he met.
D19.zh: 他开始记日记，记录每一次行动、会议、活动以及遇到的所有人的名字。

D10.en: His first reaction was anger and frustration, with tears coming to his eyes.
D10.zh: 他的第一反应是愤怒和沮丧，眼泪涌上眼眶。

D11.en: At 16, he wrote a letter chronicling a soccer match between Modena and Inter.
D11.zh: 16岁时，他写了一封信，记录了一场摩德纳对国际米兰的足球赛。

D17.en: At 25, he did everything he could to console Ugo's wife and son.
D17.zh: 25岁时，他竭尽全力安慰乌戈的妻子和儿子。

D12.en: She learned about the country and had a few years of happiness.
D12.zh: 她了解了乡村，享受了几年的快乐。

D11.en: At 23, she learned makeup, dance, and singing, living in the wings of her dream.
D11.zh: 23岁时，她学习化妆、跳舞和唱歌，生活在梦想的边缘。

D4.en: She decided to design her own hats.
D4.zh: 她决定自己设计帽子。

D13a.en: At 21, she escaped surveillance and moved to a place of her own choosing.
D13a.zh: 21岁时，她逃离监视，搬到了自己选择的地方。

D10.en: At 12, she held an undying grudge against her family.
D10.zh: 12岁时，她对家人怀有永久的怨恨。

D5.en: She took the initiative that led to a year's contract at La Rotonde.
D5.zh: 她主动争取到了在La Rotonde的一年合同。

D15.en: At 23, she applied to the municipal baths and was hired as a water giver.
D15.zh: 23岁时，她申请了市立浴场，被聘为供水员。

D14.en: At 24, she wore an outfit made by a humble tailor of La Croix St Ouen.
D14.zh: 24岁时，她穿着拉克鲁瓦圣旺一位普通裁缝做的衣服。

M8.en: She was living in the wings of her dream.
M8.zh: 她生活在梦想的翅膀里。

M1.en: Her only ambition was to end a condition of manifest inferiority.
M1.zh: 她唯一的抱负是结束一种明显的劣势状态。

D17.en: At 23, she applied to the municipal baths and was hired as a water giver at the Grande Grille.
D17.zh: 23岁时，她向市立浴场申请，被聘为大格里尔浴场的水源供应员。

D13b.en: At 23, she applied to the municipal baths and was hired as a water giver at the Grande Grille.
D13b.zh: 23岁时，她向市立浴场申请，被聘为格兰德格里尔的水源供应员。

D1.en: She tried again, failed, and gave up.
D1.zh: 她又试了一次，没用，然后放弃了。

D3.en: Being curious about everything, she faithfully attended the gatherings at Maud's house.
D3.zh: 她对一切都很好奇，忠实地参加了莫德家的聚会。

D16.en: At 23, she was learning how to use makeup, dance, and sing.
D16.zh: 23岁时，她在学习如何使用化妆品、跳舞和唱歌。

D8.en: At 23, she stuck it out and couldn't see herself anywhere else.
D8.zh: 23岁时，她坚持了下来，看不到自己还能去别的地方。

M2.en: At 23, she wanted to follow in the footsteps of Zulma Bouffar and meet Offenbach.
M2.zh: 23岁时，她想追随祖尔玛·布法尔的脚步，遇见奥芬巴赫。

M6.en: At 23, she was living in the wings of her dream.
M6.zh: 23岁时，她生活在梦想的边缘。

D19.en: At 23, she applied to the municipal baths and was hired as a water giver at the Grande Grille.
D19.zh: 23岁时，她向市立浴场申请，被聘为Grande Grille的供水员。

M7.en: At 23, she longed to go, but believed all idols had lived in poverty before reaching the top.
M7.zh: 23岁时，她渴望去，但相信所有偶像在成功前都经历过极度贫困。

M5.en: At 23, she longed to go, but believed all idols had lived in poverty before reaching the top.
M5.zh: 23岁时，她渴望去，但相信所有偶像在成功前都经历过极度贫困。

M3.en: At 23, she wanted to follow in the footsteps of Zulma Bouffar at Ems.
M3.zh: 23岁时，她想在埃姆斯追随祖尔玛·布法尔的脚步。

D20X.en: At 23, she lost the match and success would not come.
D20X.zh: 23岁时，她输掉了比赛，成功不会到来。

D12.en: As a small child, he found it difficult to escape an emotional desert without lasting damage.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他很难从情感荒漠中逃脱而不受持久伤害。

D6.en: At 16, he went to a lecture and was greatly enthused by a real seducer.
D6.zh: 16岁时，他去听了一场讲座，被一个真正的煽动者深深打动。

D11.en: As a small child, he lived with two imaginary Indians who shadowed him.
D11.zh: 幼年时，他生活在两个想象中的印第安人陪伴下，他们如影随形。

D15.en: He is still proud that he can milk a cow by hand and mow a meadow with a scythe.
D15.zh: 他仍然自豪自己能用手挤牛奶、用镰刀割草。

D9.en: For the first ten years, he ran IKEA as a one-man company with some family members.
D9.zh: 头十年，他经营宜家，这是一家只有他和一些家族成员的一人公司。

D5.en: In the 1950s, he was smuggled into a fair in the back of a Volvo Duett.
D5.zh: 20世纪50年代，他被藏在沃尔沃Duett的后备箱里偷运进展会。

D3.en: As a boy, he had leafed through it but never read it.
D3.zh: 他小时候翻过它，但从未读过。

D10.en: He worried about a new manager, took him to the farm, made fish soup, talked, then lay awake all night.
D10.zh: 他担心新经理，带他去农场，做鱼汤，聊天，然后整夜睡不着。

D4.en: He biked to the village and put some up at age 11-12.
D4.zh: 他11到12岁时骑车到村里贴了一些。

M4.en: He admired both Hitler and Lindholm at age 11-12.
M4.zh: 他11到12岁时崇拜希特勒和林德霍尔姆。

M3.en: At 11-12, he called Lindholm his first idol on his hopeless ideological journey.
M3.zh: 11到12岁时，他称林德霍尔姆是他绝望的意识形态之旅上的第一个偶像。

D13b.en: He discussed with his fiancée whether to invite Engdahl to their wedding.
D13b.zh: 他和未婚妻讨论是否邀请恩达尔参加婚礼。

D18.en: At 10, he and his friends offered to carry shoppers' bags for tips outside a new supermarket.
D18.zh: 10岁时，他和朋友们在新超市外帮顾客提袋子赚小费。

D9.en: At 21-22, he ran tests, completed his thesis, and had it bound in leather.
D9.zh: 21到22岁间，他做了测试，完成论文并用皮革装订。

D2.en: At 21-22, he finished his thesis in two semesters instead of three.
D2.zh: 21到22岁间，他用两个学期完成了论文，而不是三个。

D13b.en: As a child, he was never allowed to have a credit card or charge anything.
D13b.zh: 小时候，他从不被允许拥有信用卡或赊账。

D6.en: At 21-22, he roomed and drank beer with 51 guys from different colleges.
D6.zh: 21到22岁间，他和来自不同大学的51个家伙同住、喝啤酒。

M1.en: At 21-22, he finished his thesis in two semesters, eager to work at Ford.
M1.zh: 21到22岁间，他两个学期完成论文，急于去福特工作。

D7.en: At 22-23, he wanted to stay at Ford but in marketing or sales, not engineering.
D7.zh: 22到23岁间，他想留在福特，但做营销或销售，而不是工程。

D16.en: At 14, he joined the debating team sponsored by his Latin teacher.
D16.zh: 14岁时，他加入了由拉丁语老师赞助的辩论队。

D11.en: In seventh grade, he developed a passion for jazz and swing.
D11.zh: 七年级时，他迷上了爵士乐和摇摆乐。

D14.en: In high school, he got pretty good at doing the shag and the lindy hop.
D14.zh: 高中时，他跳沙格舞和林迪舞跳得相当不错。

D1.en: At 22-23, he insisted on a compromise, saying his Princeton master's was equivalent to the second nine months of training.
D1.zh: 22到23岁时，他坚持达成妥协，说他的普林斯顿硕士学位相当于培训的后九个月。

D5.en: At 17-18, he was classified 4F, a medical deferment, so he couldn't join the Air Corps.
D5.zh: 17到18岁时，他被列为4F类（医疗延期），因此无法加入空军参战。

M4.en: At 22-23, he wanted to stay at Ford but not in engineering; he was eager for marketing or sales.
M4.zh: 22到23岁时，他想留在福特但不想做工程，渴望做营销或销售。

M2.en: In college, he felt pressure from his father, typical of immigrant families, to compensate for his parents' lack of education.
M2.zh: 大学期间，他感受到父亲的压力，这在移民家庭中很典型，要弥补父母教育不足。

D2.en: At 25, he logged twelve-hour days, six days a week.
D2.zh: 25岁时，他每周工作六天，每天十二小时。

D6.en: At 24, he gave a formal presentation of his paper to classmates, who reacted with formal boredom.
D6.zh: 24岁时，他向同学正式展示论文，同学们反应冷淡。

D9.en: At 25-26, he sent two pairs of shoes to his old track coach Bill Bowerman.
D9.zh: 25到26岁间，他寄了两双鞋给前田径教练比尔·鲍尔曼。

D3.en: At 24, he read in his guidebook that Michelangelo was miserable while painting his masterpiece.
D3.zh: 24岁时，他在旅行指南上读到米开朗基罗画杰作时很痛苦。

D5.en: At 24, he went to Vietnam, where streets bristled with American soldiers and thrummed with fear.
D5.zh: 24岁时，他去了越南，街上满是美国士兵，弥漫着恐惧。

D13a.en: At 25, he quickly enrolled in three accounting classes at Portland State.
D13a.zh: 25岁时，他迅速在波特兰州立大学注册了三门会计课。

M1.en: At 24, he argued in his paper that Japanese running shoes might do the same thing, an idea that captivated him.
M1.zh: 24岁时，他在论文中提出日本跑鞋可能也有同样效果，这个想法吸引并迷住了他。

M6.en: At 24, he told himself to feel the presence of God, for want of a better word.
M6.zh: 24岁时，他告诉自己感受上帝的存在，虽然找不到更好的词。

D12.en: As a small child, he essentially had servants in the servants' quarters.
D12.zh: 幼年时，他家里有仆人，住在仆人区。

D7.en: At 24, he took a trip as a way to become someone less respectable.
D7.zh: 24岁时，他通过一次旅行来成为一个不那么体面的人。

D15.en: He lost count of how many races he ran in shoes modified by Bowerman.
D15.zh: 他记不清穿着鲍尔曼改装的鞋跑了多少场比赛。

D18.en: He knelt before a kid and measured his foot.
D18.zh: 他跪在孩子面前量他的脚。

D8.en: At 24, imagined a scene but omitted a crucial element.
D8.zh: 24岁时，他想象一个场景但遗漏了关键元素。

D11.en: At age 24, he had a clear thought of having been somewhere before.
D11.zh: 24岁时，他清楚感到自己曾来过这里。

D14.en: He showed off his speed, and everyone bowed to him as he crossed the finish line first.
D14.zh: 他炫耀速度，冲过终点线时大家都向他鞠躬。

D1.en: He couldn't give up, so he flew back to Japan.
D1.zh: 他不能放弃，于是飞回日本。

D10.en: He went into a deep funk and became such a grouch that his girlfriend left.
D10.zh: 他陷入深深的沮丧，变得非常暴躁，女友离开了他。

M2.en: At about 10, he played Ping-Pong with his father and felt pain from never beating him.
M2.zh: 大约10岁时，他和父亲打乒乓球，因从未赢过而感到痛苦。
